Hebrew Trans: Blessed is the man who has not walked in the counsel of the wicked, nor stood in the way of sinners, nor sat in the seat of scoffers.
Septuagint Trans: Blessed is the man who has not walked in the counsel of the ungodly, and has not stood in the way of sinners, and has not sat upon the seat of the pestilent.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Blessed is the man who has not walked in the counsel of the ungodly, and has not stood in the way of sinners, and has not sat in the seat of pestilence.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Blessed is the man who has not walked in the counsel of the ungodly, and has not stood in the way of sinners, and has not sat in the seat of the scornful.

Hebrew Trans: But rather in the law of the LORD is his delight, and on His law he meditates day and night.
Septuagint Trans: But rather his delight is in the law of the Lord, and in his law he shall meditate day and night.
Gallican Psalter Trans: But his will is in the law of the Lord, and in his law he shall meditate day and night.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: But his delight is in the law of the Lord, and in his law he shall meditate day and night.

Hebrew Trans: And he shall be like a tree planted by streams of water, which yields its fruit in its season, and whose leaf does not wither; and whatever he does shall prosper.
Septuagint Trans: And he shall be as the tree planted by the streams of waters, which shall yield its fruit in its season, and its leaf shall not fall off, and all things whatsoever he may do shall prosper.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And he shall be as a tree which is planted near the streams of waters, which shall yield its fruit in its season, and its leaf shall not fall off, and all things whatsoever he shall do shall prosper.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And he shall be like a tree transplanted beside streams of waters, which shall give its fruit in its season, and its leaf shall not wither, and all that he shall do shall prosper.

Hebrew Trans: Not so the wicked, but rather they are like the chaff which the wind drives away.
Septuagint Trans: Not so the ungodly, not so; but rather they are as the chaff which the wind casts away from the face of the earth.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Not so the ungodly, not so: but they are as the dust which the wind casts away from the face of the earth.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Not so the ungodly: but they are like the dust which the wind drives away.

Hebrew Trans: Therefore the wicked shall not stand in the judgment, nor sinners in the congregation of the righteous.
Septuagint Trans: Therefore the ungodly shall not rise up in judgment, nor sinners in the counsel of the righteous.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Therefore the ungodly shall not rise again in judgment, nor sinners in the counsel of the just.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Therefore the ungodly shall not rise in judgment, nor sinners in the congregation of the just.

Hebrew Trans: For the LORD knows the way of the righteous, but the way of the wicked shall perish.
Septuagint Trans: For the Lord knows the way of the righteous, and the way of the ungodly shall perish.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For the Lord knows the way of the just, and the way of the ungodly shall perish.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For the Lord knows the way of the just, and the way of the ungodly shall perish.

Hebrew Trans: Why do the nations rage, and the peoples meditate on a vain thing?
Septuagint Trans: Why did the nations rage, and the peoples meditate on vain things?
Gallican Psalter Trans: A psalm of David. Why have the nations raged, and the peoples meditated on vain things?
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Why will the nations be troubled, and the tribes meditate on vain things?

Hebrew Trans: The kings of the earth set themselves, and the rulers take counsel together, against the LORD and against His anointed:
Septuagint Trans: The kings of the earth stood ready, and the rulers were gathered together against the Lord and against His anointed. *Interlude*
Gallican Psalter Trans: The kings of the earth have stood up, and the princes have gathered together as one, against the Lord and against his anointed. Selah.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The kings of the earth will rise up, and the princes will take counsel together against the Lord and against his anointed one.

Hebrew Trans: "Let us break their bonds asunder, and cast away their cords from us."
Septuagint Trans: Let us break their bonds asunder, and let us cast off their yoke from us.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Let us break their bonds asunder, and let us cast away their yoke from us.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Let us break their bonds asunder, and let us cast away their snares from us.

Hebrew Trans: He who sits in the heavens shall laugh; the Lord shall mock them.
Septuagint Trans: He who dwells in the heavens shall laugh at them, and the Lord shall mock them.
Gallican Psalter Trans: He who dwells in the heavens shall laugh at them, and the Lord shall mock them.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: He who dwells in heaven will laugh; the Lord will mock them.

Hebrew Trans: Then He shall speak to them in His wrath, and in His fierce displeasure He shall terrify them:
Septuagint Trans: Then He shall speak to them in His anger, and in His wrath He shall trouble them.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Then he shall speak to them in his anger, and in his fury he shall trouble them.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Then he will speak to them in his anger, and in his fury he will trouble them.

Hebrew Trans: "But I have set My king upon Zion, My holy hill."
Septuagint Trans: But I have been established as king by Him upon Zion, His holy mountain.
Gallican Psalter Trans: But I have been appointed king by him over Zion, his holy mountain, proclaiming his decree.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: But I have established my king upon Zion, his holy mountain; I will declare the decree of God.

Hebrew Trans: I will declare the decree: the LORD said to me, "You are My son; this day have I begotten you.
Septuagint Trans: Declaring the decree of the Lord: the Lord said to me, "You are My son; today I have begotten you.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The Lord said to me: You are my son; today I have begotten you.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The Lord said to me: You are my son; today I have begotten you.

Hebrew Trans: Ask of Me, and I will give the nations as your inheritance, and the ends of the earth as your possession.
Septuagint Trans: Ask of me, and I will give you the nations as your inheritance, and the ends of the earth as your possession.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Ask of me, and I will give you the nations as your inheritance, and the ends of the earth as your possession.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Ask of me, and I will give you the nations as your inheritance, and the ends of the earth as your possession.

Hebrew Trans: You shall break them with a rod of iron; you shall dash them in pieces like a potter's vessel."
Septuagint Trans: You shall shepherd them with an iron rod; like a potter's vessel you shall shatter them."
Gallican Psalter Trans: You shall rule them with a rod of iron; like a potter's vessel you shall shatter them.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: You will rule them with a rod of iron; like a potter's vessel you will shatter them.

Hebrew Trans: And now, O kings, be wise; be instructed, you judges of the earth.
Septuagint Trans: And now, O kings, understand; be instructed, all you who judge the earth.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And now, O kings, understand; be instructed, you who judge the earth.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Now therefore, O kings, understand; be instructed, you judges of the earth.

Hebrew Trans: Serve the LORD with fear, and rejoice with trembling.
Septuagint Trans: Serve the Lord with fear, and rejoice in Him with trembling.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Serve the Lord in fear, and rejoice in him with trembling.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Serve the Lord in fear, and rejoice in trembling.

Hebrew Trans: Kiss the son, lest He be angry and you perish in the way, for His wrath may blaze up in a moment. Blessed are all who take refuge in Him.
Septuagint Trans: Lay hold of discipline, lest the Lord be angry and you perish from the righteous way, when His wrath is quickly kindled. Blessed are all who put their trust in Him.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Lay hold of discipline, lest at any time the Lord be angry and you perish from the just way, when his wrath has been kindled in a short time. Blessed are all who trust in him.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Worship purely, lest he become angry and you perish from the way, when his fury is kindled after a little while. Blessed are all who hope in him.

Hebrew Trans: A psalm of David, when he fled from before Absalom his son.
Septuagint Trans: A psalm of David, when he was fleeing from the face of Absalom his son.
Gallican Psalter Trans: A psalm of David, when he fled from the face of Absalom his son.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: A song of David, when he fled from the face of Absalom his son.

Hebrew Trans: O LORD, how many are my adversaries! Many are those who rise up against me.
Septuagint Trans: Lord, why have those who afflict me multiplied? Many rise up against me.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Lord, how are they multiplied who trouble me! Many rise up against me.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: O Lord, why are my enemies multiplied? Many rise up against me.

Hebrew Trans: Many are saying of my soul, "There is no salvation for him in God." Selah.
Septuagint Trans: Many say to my soul, "There is no salvation for him in his God." Selah.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Many say to my soul, "There is no salvation for him in his God." Diapsalma.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Many say to my soul, "There is no salvation for this one in God." Selah.

Hebrew Trans: But you, O LORD, are a shield about me, my glory, and the lifter of my head.
Septuagint Trans: But you, Lord, are my protector, my glory, and the one who lifts up my head.
Gallican Psalter Trans: But you, O Lord, are my protector, my glory, and the lifter up of my head.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: But you, O Lord, are a shield around me, my glory, and the one who lifts up my head.

Hebrew Trans: With my voice I cry out to the LORD, and he answers me from his holy mountain. Selah.
Septuagint Trans: With my voice I cried out to the Lord, and he heard me from his holy mountain. Selah.
Gallican Psalter Trans: With my voice I cried to the Lord, and he heard me from his holy mountain. Diapsalma.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: With my voice I will cry out to the Lord, and he will hear me from his holy mountain. Selah.

Hebrew Trans: I lay down and slept; I awoke, for the LORD sustains me.
Septuagint Trans: I lay down and slept; I awoke, for the Lord will sustain me.
Gallican Psalter Trans: I lay down and slept; I rose again, for the Lord will sustain me.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: I lay down and slept; I awoke, for the Lord sustained me.

Hebrew Trans: I will not be afraid of ten thousands of people who have set themselves against me all around.
Septuagint Trans: I will not be afraid of myriads of people who set themselves against me all around.
Gallican Psalter Trans: I will not fear the thousands of people surrounding me.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: I will not fear the thousands of people who have surrounded me on every side.

Hebrew Trans: Arise, O LORD! Save me, O my God! For you have struck all my enemies on the cheek; the teeth of the wicked you have broken.
Septuagint Trans: Arise, Lord; save me, O my God, for you have struck all those who oppose me without cause; you have shattered the teeth of sinners.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Arise, O Lord; save me, O my God: for you have struck all who oppose me without cause; you have broken the teeth of sinners.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Arise, O Lord; save me, O my God; for you have struck the cheek of all my enemies; you have broken the teeth of the wicked.

Hebrew Trans: Salvation belongs to the LORD; upon your people is your blessing. Selah.
Septuagint Trans: Salvation belongs to the Lord, and your blessing is upon your people.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Salvation is of the Lord, and your blessing is upon your people.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Salvation is the Lord's; upon your people be your blessing forever.

Hebrew Trans: For the choirmaster, with stringed instruments, a psalm of David.
Septuagint Trans: For the end, in psalms; a song of David.
Gallican Psalter Trans: To the end, in songs, a psalm of David.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: To the victor, in psalms, a song of David.

Hebrew Trans: When I call, answer me, O God of my righteousness; in distress You have made room for me; be gracious to me and hear my prayer.
Septuagint Trans: When I called upon him, the God of my righteousness heard me; in affliction you made room for me. Have mercy on me and hear my prayer.
Gallican Psalter Trans: When I called upon him, the God of my justice heard me: in tribulation you have made room for me. Have mercy on me and hear my prayer.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: When I call upon you, hear me, O God of my justice; in tribulation you have given me room. Have mercy on me and hear my prayer.

Hebrew Trans: O sons of men, how long shall my glory be turned to shame? How long will you love vanity and seek after falsehood? Selah.
Septuagint Trans: O sons of men, how long will you be heavy of heart? Why do you love vanity and seek after falsehood? Selah.
Gallican Psalter Trans: O sons of men, how long will you be heavy of heart? Why do you love vanity and seek after falsehood? Diapsalma.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: O sons of men, how long will you be heavy of heart? You love vanity and seek after falsehood always.

Hebrew Trans: And know that the LORD has set apart the godly one for Himself; the LORD will hear when I call to Him.
Septuagint Trans: And know that the Lord has made wondrous his holy one; the Lord will hear me when I cry out to him.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And know that the Lord has made his holy one wonderful: the Lord will hear me when I cry out to him.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And know that the Lord has made his holy one wonderful; the Lord will hear when I cry out to him.

Hebrew Trans: Tremble and do not sin; speak in your hearts upon your beds, and be still. Selah.
Septuagint Trans: Be angry, and do not sin; speak in your hearts and be pierced with compunction upon your beds. Selah.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Be angry, and do not sin: the things you say in your hearts, upon your beds be pierced with compunction. Diapsalma.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Be angry and do not sin; speak in your hearts upon your beds, and be silent always.

Hebrew Trans: Offer sacrifices of righteousness, and put your trust in the LORD.
Septuagint Trans: Offer a sacrifice of righteousness and put your hope in the Lord.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Offer the sacrifice of justice, and hope in the Lord.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Offer the sacrifice of justice and trust in the Lord.

Hebrew Trans: Many are saying, "Who will show us good?" Lift up upon us the light of Your countenance, O LORD.
Septuagint Trans: Many say, "Who will show us good things?" The light of your face, O Lord, has been stamped upon us.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Many say, "Who will show us good things?" The light of your countenance, O Lord, is signed upon us.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Many say, "Who will show us good things?" Lift up the light of your countenance upon us, O Lord.

Hebrew Trans: You have put gladness in my heart, more than in the time when their grain and their new wine abound.
Septuagint Trans: You have given gladness to my heart; from the season of their grain and wine and oil they were multiplied.
Gallican Psalter Trans: You have given gladness in my heart: from the fruit of their grain and wine and oil they have been multiplied.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: You have given gladness in my heart; in their season their grain and wine have been multiplied.

Hebrew Trans: In peace, together, I will both lie down and sleep; for You alone, O LORD, make me dwell in safety.
Septuagint Trans: In peace, at once I shall lie down and sleep, for you alone, O Lord, have made me dwell in hope.
Gallican Psalter Trans: In peace, in this very thing, I will sleep and I will rest: for you, O Lord, singularly in hope have established me.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: In peace, together, I will rest and sleep, for you, O Lord, have uniquely made me dwell in safety.

Hebrew Trans: For the choirmaster, upon the flutes, a psalm of David.
Septuagint Trans: To the end, on behalf of the one who inherits, a psalm of David.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Unto the end, for her who obtains the inheritance, a psalm of David.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: To the victor, for the inheritances, a song of David.

Hebrew Trans: Give ear to my words, O LORD; consider my meditation.
Septuagint Trans: Give ear to my words, O Lord; understand my cry.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Receive my words with your ears, O Lord; understand my cry.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Hear my words, O Lord; understand my murmuring.

Hebrew Trans: Hearken to the voice of my cry, my King and my God, for unto You do I pray.
Septuagint Trans: Attend to the voice of my supplication, my King and my God, for to you I will pray, O Lord.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Attend to the voice of my prayer, my King and my God, for to you I will pray.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: My king and my God, for to you I pray.

Hebrew Trans: O LORD, in the morning You shall hear my voice; in the morning I will set my prayer before You, and will watch.
Septuagint Trans: In the morning you shall hear my voice; in the morning I will stand before you and watch.
Gallican Psalter Trans: O Lord, in the morning you will hear my voice; in the morning I will stand before you and will see.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: O Lord, in the morning you will hear my voice; in the morning I will prepare myself before you, and I will watch.

Hebrew Trans: For You are not a God who takes pleasure in wickedness; evil shall not sojourn with You.
Septuagint Trans: For you are not a god who wills lawlessness, nor shall the evildoer dwell with you.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For you are not a god who wills iniquity, nor shall the evildoer dwell beside you.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For you are not a god who wills iniquity, nor shall the wicked dwell beside you.

Hebrew Trans: The boastful shall not stand before Your eyes; You hate all workers of iniquity.
Septuagint Trans: The lawless shall not remain before your eyes; you have hated all who work iniquity.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Nor shall the unjust endure before your eyes; you have hated all who work iniquity.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The unjust shall not stand in the sight of your eyes; you have hated all who work iniquity.

Hebrew Trans: You shall destroy those who speak falsehood; the man of blood and deceit the LORD abhors.
Septuagint Trans: You will destroy all who speak falsehood; the Lord abhors the man of blood and deceit.
Gallican Psalter Trans: You will destroy all who speak falsehood; the man of blood and deceit the Lord will abominate.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: You will destroy those who speak falsehood; the man of blood and deceit the Lord will abominate.

Hebrew Trans: But as for me, in the abundance of Your lovingkindness I will come into Your house; I will bow down toward Your holy temple in reverence of You.
Septuagint Trans: But I, in the abundance of your mercy, will enter into your house; I will worship toward your holy temple in fear of you.
Gallican Psalter Trans: But I, in the multitude of your mercy, will enter into your house; I will worship at your holy temple in your fear.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: But I, in the multitude of your mercy, will enter into your house; I will worship in your holy temple, in fear of you.

Hebrew Trans: O LORD, lead me in Your righteousness because of my enemies; make Your way straight before me.
Septuagint Trans: O Lord, guide me in your righteousness; because of my enemies, make straight your way before me.
Gallican Psalter Trans: O Lord, lead me in your justice; because of my enemies, direct your way in my sight.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: O Lord, lead me in your justice; because of those who lie in wait for me, direct your way before my face.

Hebrew Trans: For there is no faithfulness in their mouth; their inward part is destruction; their throat is an open grave; they flatter with their tongue.
Septuagint Trans: For there is no truth in their mouth; their heart is vain; their throat is an open tomb; with their tongues they have dealt deceitfully.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For there is no truth in their mouth; their heart is vain; their throat is an open sepulchre; with their tongues they acted deceitfully.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For there is nothing right in their mouth; their inward parts are snares; their throat is an open sepulcher; they make smooth their tongue.

Hebrew Trans: Hold them guilty, O God; let them fall by their own counsels; cast them out in the multitude of their transgressions, for they have rebelled against You.
Septuagint Trans: Judge them, O God; let them fall from their own counsels; according to the multitude of their ungodliness, drive them out, for they have embittered you, O Lord.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Judge them, O God; let them fall from their own designs; according to the multitude of their impieties, cast them out, for they have provoked you, O Lord.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Condemn them, O God; let them fall from their own counsels; according to the multitude of their crimes, cast them out, for they have provoked you.

Hebrew Trans: And let all who take refuge in You rejoice; let them ever shout for joy, and You shall shelter them; and let those who love Your name exult in You.
Septuagint Trans: And let all who hope in you rejoice; they shall exult forever, and you shall dwell among them; and all who love your name shall glory in you.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And let all who hope in you rejoice; they shall exult forever, and you will dwell in them; and all who love your name shall glory in you.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And let all who hope in you rejoice; they shall praise forever, and you will protect them; and let those who love your name be glad in you.

Hebrew Trans: For You, O LORD, will bless the righteous; with favor as with a shield You will surround him.
Septuagint Trans: For you, O Lord, will bless the righteous; as with a shield of favor you have crowned us.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For you will bless the just, O Lord; as with a shield of good will you have crowned us.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For you will bless the just, O Lord; as with a shield of favor you will crown him.

Hebrew Trans: For the choirmaster, with stringed instruments, upon the Sheminith. A psalm of David.
Septuagint Trans: For the end, among hymns, on behalf of the eighth, a psalm of David.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Unto the end, in songs for the octave, a psalm of David.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: To the victor, in psalms, upon the octave: a song of David.

Hebrew Trans: O LORD, do not rebuke me in your anger, and do not chasten me in your wrath.
Septuagint Trans: O Lord, do not rebuke me in your anger, nor chasten me in your wrath.
Gallican Psalter Trans: O Lord, rebuke me not in your fury, nor chastise me in your wrath.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: O Lord, do not rebuke me in your fury, nor chastise me in your wrath.

Hebrew Trans: Be gracious to me, O LORD, for I am languishing; heal me, O LORD, for my bones are terrified.
Septuagint Trans: Have mercy on me, O Lord, for I am weak; heal me, O Lord, for my bones are troubled.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Have mercy on me, O Lord, for I am weak; heal me, O Lord, for my bones are troubled.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Have mercy on me, O Lord, for I am weak; heal me, O Lord, for my bones are troubled.

Hebrew Trans: And my soul is greatly terrified; and you, O LORD—how long?
Septuagint Trans: And my soul is troubled greatly; and you, O Lord, how long?
Gallican Psalter Trans: And my soul is troubled greatly; and you, O Lord, how long?
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And my soul is greatly troubled: and you, O Lord, how long?

Hebrew Trans: Return, O LORD, deliver my soul; save me for the sake of your lovingkindness.
Septuagint Trans: Turn back, O Lord, deliver my soul; save me for the sake of your mercy.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Turn to me, O Lord, and deliver my soul; save me for the sake of your mercy.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Return, O Lord, rescue my soul: save me for the sake of your mercy.

Hebrew Trans: For in death there is no remembrance of you; in Sheol, who shall give thanks to you?
Septuagint Trans: For there is no one in death who remembers you; and in Hades, who shall give thanks to you?
Gallican Psalter Trans: For in death there is no one who remembers you; and in hell, who shall confess to you?
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For in death there is no remembrance of you: in hell, who shall confess to you?

Hebrew Trans: I am weary with my groaning; every night I flood my bed; with my tears I drench my couch.
Septuagint Trans: I grew weary in my groaning; every night I shall wash my bed, with my tears I shall drench my couch.
Gallican Psalter Trans: I have labored in my groaning; every night I will wash my bed; with my tears I will drench my couch.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: I have labored in my groaning; every night I shall make my bed swim; with my tears I shall drench my couch.

Hebrew Trans: My eye wastes away from grief; it grows old because of all my adversaries.
Septuagint Trans: My eye is troubled from anger; I have grown old among all my enemies.
Gallican Psalter Trans: My eye is troubled with fury; I have grown old among all my enemies.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: My eye has grown dim from bitterness; I am worn out by all my enemies.

Hebrew Trans: Depart from me, all you workers of iniquity, for the LORD has heard the voice of my weeping.
Septuagint Trans: Depart from me, all you who work lawlessness, for the Lord has heard the voice of my weeping.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Depart from me, all you who work iniquity, for the Lord has heard the voice of my weeping.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Depart from me, all you who work iniquity, for the Lord has heard the voice of my weeping.

Hebrew Trans: The LORD has heard my supplication; the LORD will accept my prayer.
Septuagint Trans: The Lord has heard my supplication; the Lord has accepted my prayer.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The Lord has heard my supplication; the Lord has received my prayer.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The Lord has heard my supplication; the Lord will receive my prayer.

Hebrew Trans: Let all my enemies be ashamed and greatly terrified; let them turn back and be put to shame in a moment.
Septuagint Trans: Let all my enemies be ashamed and be greatly troubled; let them be turned back and put to shame greatly with speed.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Let all my enemies be ashamed and greatly troubled; let them be turned back and ashamed very swiftly.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Let all my enemies be ashamed and greatly troubled: let them turn back and be put to shame suddenly.

Hebrew Trans: A Shiggaion of David, which he sang to the LORD, concerning the words of Cush, a Benjaminite.
Septuagint Trans: A psalm of David, which he sang to the Lord concerning the words of Cush the son of Jemeni.
Gallican Psalter Trans: A psalm of David, which he sang to the Lord concerning the words of Cush, the son of Jemini.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For the ignorance of David, which he sang to the Lord concerning the words of the Ethiopian, the son of Jemini.

Hebrew Trans: O LORD my God, in You I have taken refuge; save me from all who pursue me, and deliver me,
Septuagint Trans: O Lord my God, in you I have hoped; save me from all who pursue me, and deliver me,
Gallican Psalter Trans: O Lord my God, in you I have hoped: save me from all who persecute me, and deliver me.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: O Lord my God, in you I have hoped: save me from all who persecute me, and deliver me.

Hebrew Trans: lest he tear my soul like a lion, rending it in pieces, and there is none to deliver.
Septuagint Trans: lest he seize like a lion my soul, with no one to redeem or to save.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Lest at any time he seize my soul like a lion, while there is none to redeem, nor any to save.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Lest perhaps he seize my soul like a lion, and tear it apart, and there be none to rescue.

Hebrew Trans: O LORD my God, if I have done this, if there is iniquity in my hands,
Septuagint Trans: O Lord my God, if I have done this, if there is injustice in my hands,
Gallican Psalter Trans: O Lord my God, if I have done this thing, if there is iniquity in my hands,
Hebrew Psalter Trans: O Lord my God, if I have done this thing, if there is iniquity in my hands,

Hebrew Trans: if I have repaid my friend with evil, or plundered my adversary without cause,
Septuagint Trans: if I have repaid those who repay me with evils, then let me fall empty before my enemies.
Gallican Psalter Trans: if I have repaid those who rendered evil to me, let me deservedly fall empty before my enemies.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: if I have repaid those who rendered evil to me, and have let my enemies go empty,

Hebrew Trans: let the enemy pursue my soul and overtake it, and let him trample my life to the ground, and lay my glory in the dust. Selah.
Septuagint Trans: Let the enemy pursue my soul and overtake it, and let him trample my life to the ground, and let him make my glory dwell in the dust. Selah.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Let the enemy pursue my soul and overtake it, and let him trample my life upon the ground, and bring down my glory into the dust. Selah.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: let the enemy pursue my soul and overtake it, and trample my life upon the ground, and lay my glory in the dust forever.

Hebrew Trans: Arise, O LORD, in Your anger; lift Yourself up against the fury of my adversaries, and awake for me; You have commanded judgment.
Septuagint Trans: Arise, O Lord, in your anger; be exalted in the borders of my enemies. Awake, O Lord my God, with the decree that you have commanded.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Arise, O Lord, in your wrath; be exalted in the borders of my enemies; and arise, O Lord my God, in the commandment which you have commanded.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Arise, O Lord, in your fury; be lifted up in indignation against my enemies, and rise up for me in the judgment which you have commanded.

Hebrew Trans: And let the congregation of the peoples surround You, and over it return on high.
Septuagint Trans: And the assembly of peoples shall surround you, and on account of this return on high.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And the congregation of peoples shall surround you, and for this cause return on high.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And let the assembly of the peoples surround you, and for this return on high.

Hebrew Trans: The LORD judges the peoples; judge me, O LORD, according to my righteousness and according to my integrity that is upon me.
Septuagint Trans: The Lord judges the peoples; judge me, O Lord, according to my righteousness and according to my innocence that is upon me.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The Lord judges the peoples. Judge me, O Lord, according to my righteousness, and according to my innocence that is upon me.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The Lord will judge the peoples. Judge me, O Lord, according to my righteousness and according to my innocence that is in me.

Hebrew Trans: Let the evil of the wicked come to an end, but establish the righteous; for the righteous God tests the hearts and the minds.
Septuagint Trans: Let the wickedness of sinners be brought to an end, and you will direct the righteous, O God who examines hearts and minds.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Let the wickedness of sinners be consumed, and you shall direct the just one, O God who searches hearts and reins.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Let the wickedness of the unrighteous be consumed, and let the righteous be established. The tester of hearts and minds is God the just.

Hebrew Trans: My shield is with God, who saves the upright in heart.
Septuagint Trans: Righteous is my help from God, who saves the upright in heart.
Gallican Psalter Trans: My just help is from God, who saves the upright of heart.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: My shield is in God, who saves the upright of heart.

Hebrew Trans: God is a righteous judge, and a God who has indignation every day.
Septuagint Trans: God is a righteous and strong judge, and long-suffering, not bringing wrath every day.
Gallican Psalter Trans: God is a just judge, and strong and patient. Does he grow angry every day?
Hebrew Psalter Trans: God is a just judge, and strong, threatening every day.

Hebrew Trans: If one does not repent, He will sharpen His sword; He has bent His bow and made it ready.
Septuagint Trans: If you do not turn back, he will make his sword flash; he has bent his bow and made it ready.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Unless you shall have been converted, he will brandish his sword; he has bent his bow and made it ready.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: If one does not turn back, he will sharpen his sword; he has bent his bow and made it ready.

Hebrew Trans: And for him He has prepared instruments of death; He makes His arrows into fiery shafts.
Septuagint Trans: And in it he has prepared instruments of death; he has fashioned his arrows for those who burn.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And in it he has prepared the instruments of death; he has made his arrows for those who burn.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And in it he has prepared vessels of death; he has fashioned his arrows for burning.

Hebrew Trans: Behold, he travails with iniquity, and conceives mischief, and brings forth falsehood.
Septuagint Trans: Behold, he was in labor with injustice, he conceived toil, and brought forth lawlessness.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Behold, he has been in labor with injustice, and has conceived sorrow, and brought forth iniquity.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Behold, he labors with iniquity, and having conceived sorrow, he has brought forth falsehood.

Hebrew Trans: He has dug a pit and hollowed it out, and has fallen into the ditch which he made.
Septuagint Trans: He dug a pit and excavated it, and he shall fall into the hole that he made.
Gallican Psalter Trans: He has opened a pit and dug it out, and he shall fall into the hole which he has made.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: He has opened a pit and dug it out, and he will fall into the ruin which he has wrought.

Hebrew Trans: His mischief shall return upon his own head, and his violence shall come down upon his own crown.
Septuagint Trans: His toil shall return upon his own head, and upon his own crown his injustice shall descend.
Gallican Psalter Trans: His sorrow shall be turned upon his own head, and his iniquity shall descend upon the crown of his own head.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: His sorrow shall return upon his own head, and his iniquity shall descend upon his own crown.

Hebrew Trans: I will give thanks to the LORD according to His righteousness, and will sing praise to the name of the LORD Most High.
Septuagint Trans: I will give thanks to the Lord according to his righteousness, and I will sing praise to the name of the Lord Most High.
Gallican Psalter Trans: I will give praise to the Lord according to his righteousness, and I will sing to the name of the Lord most high.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: I will praise the Lord according to his righteousness, and I will sing to the name of the Lord most high.

Hebrew Trans: For the choirmaster, upon the Gittith, a psalm of David.
Septuagint Trans: For the end, concerning the winepresses; a psalm of David.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Unto the end, for the winepresses, a psalm of David.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: To the victor, for the winepresses, a song of David.

Hebrew Trans: O LORD, our Lord, how majestic is your name in all the earth, who has set your splendor upon the heavens.
Septuagint Trans: O Lord, our Lord, how wondrous is your name in all the earth! For your magnificence has been raised above the heavens.
Gallican Psalter Trans: O Lord, our Lord, how admirable is your name in all the earth! For your magnificence is elevated above the heavens.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: O Lord, our Lord, how great is your name in all the earth, you who have set your glory above the heavens.

Hebrew Trans: Out of the mouth of babes and sucklings you have established strength, on account of your adversaries, to silence the enemy and the avenger.
Septuagint Trans: Out of the mouth of babes and sucklings you have prepared praise, on account of your enemies, to destroy the enemy and the avenger.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Out of the mouth of infants and nurslings you have perfected praise, because of your enemies, that you may destroy the enemy and the avenger.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: From the mouth of infants and nursing babes you have perfected praise, because of your adversaries, that the enemy and the avenger may be silenced.

Hebrew Trans: When I behold your heavens, the works of your fingers, the moon and the stars which you have established,
Septuagint Trans: For I shall behold the heavens, the works of your fingers, the moon and the stars which you established.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For I shall see your heavens, the works of your fingers, the moon and the stars which you have established.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For I will see your heavens, the works of your fingers, the moon and the stars which you have established.

Hebrew Trans: What is man that you are mindful of him, and the son of man that you visit him?
Septuagint Trans: What is man, that you are mindful of him? Or the son of man, that you visit him?
Gallican Psalter Trans: What is man, that you are mindful of him? Or the son of man, that you visit him?
Hebrew Psalter Trans: What is man, that you are mindful of him, or the son of man, that you visit him?

Hebrew Trans: Yet you have made him a little lower than God, and with glory and honor you crown him.
Septuagint Trans: You made him a little lower than the angels; with glory and honor you crowned him.
Gallican Psalter Trans: You have made him a little less than the angels; with glory and honor you have crowned him.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: You will make him a little less than God; with glory and honor you will crown him.

Hebrew Trans: You make him to rule over the works of your hands; all things you have put under his feet:
Septuagint Trans: And you set him over the works of your hands; you subjected all things under his feet:
Gallican Psalter Trans: And you have set him over the works of your hands; you have subjected all things under his feet:
Hebrew Psalter Trans: You will give him power over the works of your hands; all things you have placed under his feet:

Hebrew Trans: Sheep and oxen, all of them, and also the beasts of the field,
Septuagint Trans: Sheep and oxen, all of them, and moreover the cattle of the field,
Gallican Psalter Trans: Sheep and oxen, all of them, and moreover the cattle of the field,
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Sheep and all cattle, moreover also the animals of the field,

Hebrew Trans: The birds of the heavens and the fish of the sea, whatever passes through the paths of the seas.
Septuagint Trans: The birds of the heaven and the fish of the sea, the things passing through the paths of the seas.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The birds of the sky and the fish of the sea, which traverse the paths of the sea.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The birds of the sky and the fish of the sea, which pass through the paths of the deep.

Hebrew Trans: O LORD, our Lord, how majestic is your name in all the earth.
Septuagint Trans: O Lord, our Lord, how wondrous is your name in all the earth!
Gallican Psalter Trans: O Lord, our Lord, how admirable is your name in all the earth!
Hebrew Psalter Trans: O Lord, our Lord, how great is your name in all the earth.

Hebrew Trans: For the choirmaster, upon Alamoth, of Ben, a psalm of David.
Septuagint Trans: For the end, concerning the hidden things of the son. A psalm of David.
Gallican Psalter Trans: To the end, for the hidden things of the son: a psalm of David.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: To the victor, for the death of the son, a song of David.

Hebrew Trans: I will give thanks to the LORD with all my heart; I will recount all your wondrous deeds.
Septuagint Trans: I will give thanks to you, O Lord, with my whole heart; I will declare all your wonders.
Gallican Psalter Trans: I will confess to you, O Lord, with my whole heart; I will declare all your wondrous works.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: I will confess to the Lord with my whole heart; I will declare all your wonders.

Hebrew Trans: I will be glad and exult in you; I will sing praise to your name, O Most High.
Septuagint Trans: I will be glad and rejoice in you; I will sing praises to your name, O Most High.
Gallican Psalter Trans: I will be glad and rejoice in you; I will sing praise to your name, O Most High.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: I will be glad and rejoice in you; I will sing to your name, O Most High.

Hebrew Trans: When my enemies turn back, they shall stumble and perish before you.
Septuagint Trans: When my enemy is turned back, they shall be weakened and shall perish before your face.
Gallican Psalter Trans: When my enemy is turned back, they shall be weakened and shall perish before your face.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: When my enemies shall fall backward, and shall stumble and perish before your face.

Hebrew Trans: For you have maintained my justice and my cause; you sat on the throne judging righteously.
Septuagint Trans: For you have upheld my judgment and my cause; you sat upon the throne, you who judge righteousness.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For you have upheld my judgment and my cause; you have sat upon the throne, you who judge justice.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For you have made my judgment and my cause; you have sat upon the throne, a judge of justice.

Hebrew Trans: You have rebuked the nations, you have destroyed the wicked; you have blotted out their name forever and ever.
Septuagint Trans: You have rebuked the nations, and the ungodly one has perished; you have blotted out their name forever and ever and ever.
Gallican Psalter Trans: You have rebuked the nations, and the wicked one has perished; you have blotted out their name forever and ever.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: You have rebuked the nations; the wicked one has perished; you have blotted out their name forever and ever.

Hebrew Trans: The enemy — their ruins are ended forever; and the cities you have uprooted — their very memory has perished.
Septuagint Trans: The swords of the enemy have utterly failed, and you have destroyed cities; their memorial has perished with a noise.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The swords of the enemy have failed utterly, and you have destroyed their cities; their memory has perished with a noise.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The desolations are completed unto the end, and you have overthrown cities; their memory has perished with them.

Hebrew Trans: But the LORD sits enthroned forever; he has established his throne for judgment.
Septuagint Trans: And the Lord endures forever; he has prepared his throne for judgment.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And the Lord endures forever; he has prepared his throne for judgment.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: But the Lord shall sit forever; he has established his throne for judgment.

Hebrew Trans: And he shall judge the world in righteousness; he shall govern the peoples with equity.
Septuagint Trans: And he shall judge the world in righteousness; he shall judge the peoples in uprightness.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And he shall judge the world in equity; he shall judge the peoples in justice.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And he himself judges the world in justice; he judges the peoples in equity.

Hebrew Trans: And the LORD shall be a stronghold for the oppressed, a stronghold in times of trouble.
Septuagint Trans: And the Lord became a refuge for the poor, a helper in due times, in affliction.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And the Lord has become a refuge for the poor, a helper in times of opportunity, in tribulation.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And the Lord shall be a refuge for the oppressed, a refuge in due time in tribulation.

Hebrew Trans: And those who know your name will put their trust in you, for you have not forsaken those who seek you, O LORD.
Septuagint Trans: And let those who know your name hope in you, for you have not forsaken those who seek you, O Lord.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And let them hope in you who know your name, for you have not forsaken those who seek you, O Lord.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And let those who know your name trust in you, for you have not forsaken those who seek you, O Lord.

Hebrew Trans: Sing praises to the LORD, who dwells in Zion; declare among the peoples his deeds.
Septuagint Trans: Sing praises to the Lord who dwells in Zion; declare his deeds among the nations.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Sing praises to the Lord, who dwells in Zion; declare his deeds among the nations.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Sing to the Lord who dwells in Zion; declare his deeds among the peoples.

Hebrew Trans: For he who avenges blood remembers them; he does not forget the cry of the humble.
Septuagint Trans: For he who seeks out blood has remembered them; he has not forgotten the cry of the poor.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For he who requires their blood has remembered; he has not forgotten the cry of the poor.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For he who seeks their blood has remembered; he has not forgotten the cry of the poor.

Hebrew Trans: Be gracious to me, O LORD; see my affliction from those who hate me, you who lift me up from the gates of death,
Septuagint Trans: Have mercy on me, O Lord; see my humiliation from my enemies, you who lift me up from the gates of death,
Gallican Psalter Trans: Have mercy on me, O Lord; see my humiliation at the hands of my enemies, you who lift me up from the gates of death,
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The Lord has had mercy on me; he has seen my affliction from my enemies, he who lifts me up from the gates of death.

Hebrew Trans: that I may recount all your praises; in the gates of the daughter of Zion I will rejoice in your salvation.
Septuagint Trans: so that I may declare all your praises in the gates of the daughter of Zion; I will rejoice in your salvation.
Gallican Psalter Trans: That I may declare all your praises in the gates of the daughter of Zion; I will rejoice in your salvation.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: That I may declare all your praises in the gates of the daughter of Zion; I will exult in your salvation.

Hebrew Trans: The nations have sunk in the pit they made; in the net which they hid, their own foot has been caught.
Septuagint Trans: The nations have sunk in the destruction they made; in this snare which they hid, their foot has been caught.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The nations have sunk down in the destruction which they made; in this snare which they hid, their foot has been caught.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The nations have sunk down in the destruction which they made; in the net which they hid, their foot has been caught.

Hebrew Trans: The LORD has made himself known; he has executed judgment; by the work of his own hands the wicked is snared. Higgaion. Selah.
Septuagint Trans: The Lord is known executing judgments; by the works of his own hands the sinner has been caught. A song of a pause.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The Lord is known by executing judgments; in the works of his own hands the sinner has been caught. A song of diapsalma.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The Lord is known, executing judgment; by the work of his own hands the wicked one has fallen, with an everlasting sound.

Hebrew Trans: The wicked shall return to Sheol, all the nations that forget God.
Septuagint Trans: Let the sinners be turned back into Hades, all the nations that forget God.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Let sinners be turned into hell, all the nations that forget God.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Let the wicked be turned into hell, all the nations that have forgotten God.

Hebrew Trans: For the needy shall not always be forgotten; the hope of the afflicted shall not perish forever.
Septuagint Trans: For the poor shall not be forgotten forever; the patience of the needy shall not perish for ever.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For the poor shall not be forgotten to the end; the patience of the poor shall not perish forever.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For the poor shall not be forgotten forever; the expectation of the poor shall not perish in perpetuity.

Hebrew Trans: Arise, O LORD! Let not man prevail; let the nations be judged before your face.
Septuagint Trans: Arise, O Lord, let not man prevail; let the nations be judged before you.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Arise, O Lord, let not man be strengthened; let the nations be judged in your sight.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Arise, O Lord, let not man be strengthened; let the nations be judged before your face.

Hebrew Trans: Set fear over them, O LORD; let the nations know that they are but mortal. Selah.
Septuagint Trans: Appoint, O Lord, a lawgiver over them; let the nations know that they are but men. A pause.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Appoint, O Lord, a lawgiver over them; let the nations know that they are but men. Diapsalma.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Set terror over them, O Lord; let the nations know that they are but men, always.

Hebrew Trans: Why, O LORD, do You stand afar off? Why do You hide Yourself in times of trouble?
Septuagint Trans: Why, O Lord, do you stand far off? Why do you overlook in times of prosperity, in tribulation?
Gallican Psalter Trans: Why, O Lord, have You withdrawn afar? Why do You look away in times of need, in tribulation?
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Why, O Lord, do You stand far off? Why do You look away in times of distress?

Hebrew Trans: In the pride of the wicked he hotly pursues the poor; let them be caught in the schemes that they have devised.
Septuagint Trans: When the ungodly is arrogant, the poor is set on fire; they are caught in the schemes which they devise.
Gallican Psalter Trans: While the ungodly is proud, the poor is set on fire; they are caught in the schemes which they devise.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: In the pride of the wicked, the poor man burns; let them be caught in the schemes which they have devised.

Hebrew Trans: For the wicked boasts of the desire of his soul, and the one greedy for gain curses and spurns the LORD.
Septuagint Trans: For the sinner is praised in the desires of his soul, and the one who does wrong is blessed.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For the sinner is praised in the desires of his soul, and the unjust one is blessed.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For the wicked has praised the desire of his soul, and the greedy man, applauding himself,

Hebrew Trans: The wicked, in the haughtiness of his countenance, does not seek Him; "There is no God" is all his thoughts.
Septuagint Trans: The sinner has provoked the Lord; according to the multitude of his wrath he will not seek him out. God is not before him.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The sinner has provoked the Lord; according to the multitude of his wrath he will not seek God; God is not before his sight.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: has blasphemed the Lord. The wicked, according to the height of his fury, will not seek Him; neither is God in any of his thoughts.

Hebrew Trans: His ways prosper at all times; Your judgments are far above, out of his sight; as for all his adversaries, he puffs at them.
Septuagint Trans: His ways are profaned at every time; your judgments are removed from before him; he will lord it over all his enemies.
Gallican Psalter Trans: His ways are defiled at all times; Your judgments are removed from his face; he shall dominate all his enemies.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: His ways bring forth fruit at all times; Your judgments are far from his sight; he looks down upon all his enemies.

Hebrew Trans: He says in his heart, "I shall not be moved; from generation to generation I shall not be in adversity."
Septuagint Trans: For he said in his heart, "I shall not be shaken, from generation to generation without evil."
Gallican Psalter Trans: For he has said in his heart: I shall not be moved from generation to generation, without evil.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: He speaks in his heart: I shall not be moved; from generation to generation I shall be without evil.

Hebrew Trans: His mouth is full of cursing and deceit and oppression; under his tongue is mischief and iniquity.
Septuagint Trans: His mouth is full of cursing and bitterness and deceit; under his tongue are trouble and pain.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Whose mouth is full of cursing and bitterness and deceit; under his tongue are labor and sorrow.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: His mouth is full of cursing and deceit and greed; under his tongue are pain and iniquity.

Hebrew Trans: He sits in the ambush of the courtyards; in secret places he slays the innocent; his eyes watch stealthily for the helpless.
Septuagint Trans: He sits in ambush with the rich, in secret places to slay the innocent; his eyes look out against the poor.
Gallican Psalter Trans: He sits in ambush with the rich in secret places, that he may kill the innocent; his eyes look upon the poor.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: He sits in ambush near the doorways, in hidden places, so that he may kill the innocent; his eyes watch for your strong ones.

Hebrew Trans: He lurks in secret like a lion in his thicket; he lies in wait to catch the poor; he catches the poor when he draws him into his net.
Septuagint Trans: He lies in wait in secret like a lion in his den; he lies in wait to seize the poor, to seize the poor by dragging him away.
Gallican Psalter Trans: He lies in wait in secret like a lion in his den; he lies in wait to seize the poor; to seize the poor when he draws him into his snare; he shall humble him.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: He lies in wait in secret like a lion in his den; he lies in wait to seize the poor man; he seizes the poor man when he draws him into his net.

Hebrew Trans: He crushes, he crouches down, and the helpless fall by his mighty ones.
Septuagint Trans: In his trap he will humble him; he will crouch and fall when he has mastered the poor.
Gallican Psalter Trans: He shall bow down and fall, when he has gained dominion over the poor.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And he will cast him down broken, and will rush upon him mightily with his strength.

Hebrew Trans: He says in his heart, "God has forgotten; He has hidden His face; He will never see it."
Septuagint Trans: For he said in his heart, "God has forgotten; he has turned away his face so as never to see."
Gallican Psalter Trans: For he has said in his heart: God has forgotten; He has turned away His face, that He may never see.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: He has said in his heart: God has forgotten; He has hidden His face; He will never look upon it.

Hebrew Trans: Arise, O LORD! O God, lift up Your hand; do not forget the afflicted.
Septuagint Trans: Arise, O Lord God; let your hand be lifted up; do not forget the poor.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Arise, O Lord God; let Your hand be lifted up; do not forget the poor.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Arise, O Lord God, lift up Your hand; do not forget the poor.

Hebrew Trans: Why does the wicked spurn God? He says in his heart, "You will not call to account."
Septuagint Trans: For what reason has the ungodly provoked God? For he said in his heart, "He will not seek it out."
Gallican Psalter Trans: For what reason has the ungodly provoked God? For he has said in his heart: He will not require it.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Why does the wicked man blaspheme God, saying in his heart that He will not require it?

Hebrew Trans: You have seen, for You behold mischief and vexation, to repay it with Your hand. Upon You the helpless commits himself; You have been the helper of the fatherless.
Septuagint Trans: You see, for you observe pain and wrath, to deliver them into your hands. To you the poor has been left; you have been a helper to the orphan.
Gallican Psalter Trans: You see, for You consider labor and sorrow, that You may deliver them into Your hands. To You the poor has been left; to the orphan You were a helper.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: You see, for You behold labor and fury, so that it may be given into Your hand; to You Your strong ones are left; You have been made the helper of the orphan.

Hebrew Trans: Break the arm of the wicked and the evildoer; seek out his wickedness until You find no more.
Septuagint Trans: Break the arm of the sinner and wicked one; his sin shall be sought out and shall not be found on account of it.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Break the arm of the sinner and the wicked one; his sin shall be sought out and shall not be found.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Break the arm of the wicked and the evil man; You shall seek out his wickedness and shall not find it.

Hebrew Trans: The LORD is King forever and ever; the nations have perished from His land.
Septuagint Trans: The Lord shall reign forever and ever and ever; you nations shall perish from his land.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The Lord shall reign forever and unto the age of ages; you nations shall perish from His land.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The Lord is King of the age and of eternity; the nations have perished from His land.

Hebrew Trans: The desire of the humble You have heard, O LORD; You will establish their heart; You will incline Your ear,
Septuagint Trans: The Lord has heard the desire of the poor; your ear has attended to the readiness of their heart,
Gallican Psalter Trans: The Lord has heard the desire of the poor; Your ear has heard the preparation of their heart.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The Lord hears the desire of the poor; You have prepared their heart so that Your ear may hear,

Hebrew Trans: To judge the fatherless and the oppressed, that man who is of the earth may terrify no more.
Septuagint Trans: To judge for the orphan and the humble, so that man may no longer boast upon the earth.
Gallican Psalter Trans: To judge for the fatherless and the humble, so that man may no longer presume to magnify himself upon the earth.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: to judge the orphan and the oppressed, so that man of the earth may no longer exalt himself.

Hebrew Trans: For the chief musician. Of David. In the LORD I have taken refuge; how can you say to my soul, "Flee to your mountain like a bird"?
Septuagint Trans: For the end, a psalm of David. In the Lord I have trusted; how will you say to my soul, "Flee to the mountains like a sparrow"?
Gallican Psalter Trans: Unto the end, a psalm of David. In the Lord I trust; how do you say to my soul, "Flee to the mountains like a sparrow"?
Hebrew Psalter Trans: To the victor. Of David. In the Lord I have trusted; how do you say to my soul, "Fly away to the mountain like a bird"?

Hebrew Trans: For behold, the wicked bend the bow, they have fitted their arrow upon the string, to shoot in darkness at the upright in heart.
Septuagint Trans: For behold, the sinners have bent their bow, they have prepared arrows in a quiver, to shoot in the dark of the moon at the upright in heart.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For behold, the sinners have bent their bow; they have prepared their arrows in the quiver, to shoot in darkness at the upright of heart.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For behold, the wicked have bent their bow; they have placed their arrow upon the string, to shoot in secret at the upright of heart.

Hebrew Trans: When the foundations are destroyed, what can the righteous do?
Septuagint Trans: For what you established, they have torn down; but the righteous one, what has he done?
Gallican Psalter Trans: For what you had established, they have destroyed; but the just man, what has he done?
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For the laws have been destroyed; what has the just man accomplished?

Hebrew Trans: The LORD is in His holy temple; the LORD, His throne is in heaven. His eyes behold, His eyelids test the children of man.
Septuagint Trans: The Lord is in his holy temple; the Lord, his throne is in heaven. His eyes look upon the poor; his eyelids examine the sons of men.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The Lord is in his holy temple; the Lord, his seat is in heaven. His eyes look upon the poor; his eyelids examine the sons of men.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The Lord is in his holy temple; the Lord's throne is in heaven. His eyes see, his eyelids test the sons of men.

Hebrew Trans: The LORD tests the righteous, but the wicked and the one who loves violence His soul hates.
Septuagint Trans: The Lord examines the righteous and the ungodly, and he who loves unrighteousness hates his own soul.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The Lord examines the just and the impious; but he who loves iniquity hates his own soul.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The Lord tests the just man, but the wicked and the one who loves iniquity, his soul hates.

Hebrew Trans: He shall rain upon the wicked snares, fire and brimstone, and a scorching wind shall be the portion of their cup.
Septuagint Trans: He shall rain upon sinners snares, fire and brimstone and a spirit of tempest are the portion of their cup.
Gallican Psalter Trans: He shall rain upon sinners snares, fire and brimstone, and a wind of storms shall be the portion of their cup.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: He shall rain upon sinners snares, fire and brimstone, and a spirit of tempests shall be the portion of their cup.

Hebrew Trans: For the LORD is righteous; He loves righteous deeds; the upright shall behold His face.
Septuagint Trans: For the Lord is righteous, and he has loved righteousness; his face has seen uprightness.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For the Lord is just, and he has loved justice; his countenance has beheld uprightness.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For the Lord is just; he has loved justice; the upright shall behold his face.

Hebrew Trans: For the choirmaster, upon the Sheminith; a psalm of David.
Septuagint Trans: To the end, concerning the eighth, a psalm of David.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Unto the end, for the octave, a psalm of David.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: To the victor, for the octave, a song of David.

Hebrew Trans: Save, O LORD, for the godly man ceases; for the faithful vanish from among the sons of men.
Septuagint Trans: Save me, Lord, for the holy one has failed, for truths have been diminished from the sons of men.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Save me, O Lord, for the holy one has failed, for truths have been diminished among the sons of men.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Save, O Lord, for the holy one has failed, for the faithful have diminished among the sons of men.

Hebrew Trans: They speak falsehood each one with his neighbor; with flattering lips and a double heart they speak.
Septuagint Trans: Each one spoke vain things to his neighbor; deceitful lips spoke with a double heart.
Gallican Psalter Trans: They have spoken vain things, each one to his neighbor: deceitful lips, with a heart and a heart they have spoken.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: They speak vain things each one to his neighbor: with deceitful lips, in heart and heart they have spoken.

Hebrew Trans: May the LORD cut off all flattering lips, the tongue that speaks great things,
Septuagint Trans: May the Lord destroy all deceitful lips and the boastful tongue,
Gallican Psalter Trans: May the Lord destroy all deceitful lips, and the boastful tongue.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: May the Lord destroy all deceitful lips, and the tongue that speaks great things.

Hebrew Trans: who have said, "With our tongue we will prevail; our lips are with us; who is lord over us?"
Septuagint Trans: those who said, "We will magnify our tongue, our lips are our own; who is lord over us?"
Gallican Psalter Trans: Who have said: We will magnify our tongue; our lips are our own; who is our lord?
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Who have said: Let us strengthen our tongue; our lips are with us: who is our lord?

Hebrew Trans: "Because of the plundering of the poor, because of the groaning of the needy, now I will arise," says the LORD; "I will set him in the safety for which he longs."
Septuagint Trans: "Because of the misery of the poor and because of the groaning of the needy, now I will arise," says the Lord; "I will place them in safety; I will speak boldly in him."
Gallican Psalter Trans: Because of the misery of the needy and the groaning of the poor, now I will arise, says the Lord: I will place him in safety; I will act confidently in him.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Because of the desolation of the needy and the groaning of the poor, now I will arise, says the Lord; I will place them in the salvation of their help.

Hebrew Trans: The words of the LORD are pure words, as silver refined in a furnace on the earth, purified seven times.
Septuagint Trans: The words of the Lord are pure words, silver tried by fire, a tested thing for the earth, purified seven times.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The words of the Lord are pure words: silver tested by fire, proven in the earth, purified sevenfold.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The words of the Lord are pure words: silver tested by fire, separated from the earth, refined sevenfold.

Hebrew Trans: You, O LORD, will keep them; you will preserve us from this generation forever.
Septuagint Trans: You, Lord, will guard us and preserve us from this generation and forever.
Gallican Psalter Trans: You, O Lord, will preserve us, and you will guard us from this generation forever.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: You, O Lord, will keep them: you will preserve us from this generation forever.

Hebrew Trans: The wicked walk on every side, when vileness is exalted among the sons of men.
Septuagint Trans: The ungodly walk in circles; according to your exaltation you have regarded the sons of men.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The wicked walk round about: according to your highness you have multiplied the sons of men.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The wicked walk round about: when the vilest of the sons of men are exalted.

Hebrew Trans: To the choirmaster, a psalm of David.
Septuagint Trans: For the end, a psalm of David.
Gallican Psalter Trans: To the end, a psalm of David.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: To the victor, a song of David.

Hebrew Trans: How long, O LORD, will you forget me forever? How long will you hide your face from me?
Septuagint Trans: How long, O Lord, will you forget me unto the end? How long will you turn your face away from me?
Gallican Psalter Trans: How long, O Lord, will you forget me to the end? How long do you turn your face from me?
Hebrew Psalter Trans: How long, O Lord, will you forget me utterly? How long will you hide your face from me?

Hebrew Trans: How long shall I take counsel in my soul, having sorrow in my heart by day? How long shall my enemy be exalted over me?
Septuagint Trans: How long shall I place counsels in my soul, sorrows in my heart by day? How long shall my enemy be exalted over me?
Gallican Psalter Trans: How long shall I take counsels in my soul, sorrow in my heart through the day? How long shall my enemy be exalted over me?
Hebrew Psalter Trans: How long shall I hold counsels in my soul, sorrow in my heart through the day? How long shall my enemy be exalted over me?

Hebrew Trans: Look upon me, answer me, O LORD my God; give light to my eyes, lest I sleep the sleep of death,
Septuagint Trans: Look upon me, hear me, O Lord my God; enlighten my eyes, lest I sleep unto death;
Gallican Psalter Trans: Look upon me, hear me, O Lord my God. Enlighten my eyes, lest I ever sleep in death.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Turn, hear me, O Lord my God; enlighten my eyes, lest I ever sleep in death.

Hebrew Trans: lest my enemy say, "I have prevailed against him," lest my adversaries rejoice when I am shaken.
Septuagint Trans: Lest my enemy say, "I have prevailed against him;" those who afflict me will rejoice if I am shaken.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Lest at any time my enemy say, "I have prevailed against him." Those who trouble me will rejoice if I am moved.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Lest my enemy should say, "I have prevailed against him"; my foes will rejoice when I am shaken.

Hebrew Trans: But I have trusted in your steadfast love; my heart shall rejoice in your salvation. I will sing to the LORD, for he has dealt bountifully with me.
Septuagint Trans: But I have hoped in your mercy; my heart shall rejoice in your salvation. I will sing to the Lord who has dealt bountifully with me, and I will sing praise to the name of the Lord Most High.
Gallican Psalter Trans: But I have hoped in your mercy. My heart shall rejoice in your salvation. I will sing to the Lord, who has given good things to me, and I will sing psalms to the name of the Lord most high.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: But I trust in your mercy; my heart shall rejoice in your salvation. I will sing to the Lord, because he has rendered to me.

Hebrew Trans: For the choirmaster, of David. The fool has said in his heart, "There is no God." They have acted corruptly, they have done abominable deeds; there is none who does good.
Septuagint Trans: For the end, a psalm of David. The fool has said in his heart, "There is no God." They have corrupted themselves and become abominable in their practices; there is none who does good, there is not even one.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Unto the end, a psalm of David. The fool has said in his heart: There is no God. They are corrupt and have become abominable in their pursuits; there is none who does good, there is not even one.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: To the victor. Of David. The fool has said in his heart: "There is no God." They are corrupt and have become abominable in their pursuits; there is none who does good.

Hebrew Trans: The LORD looked down from heaven upon the children of men, to see if there is any who understands, who seeks God.
Septuagint Trans: The Lord looked down from heaven upon the sons of men, to see if there is anyone who understands or seeks after God.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The Lord has looked down from heaven upon the sons of men, to see if there is one who understands or who seeks God.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The Lord looked down from heaven upon the sons of men, to see if there were any who understood, seeking God.

Hebrew Trans: They have all turned aside, together they have become corrupt; there is none who does good, not even one.
Septuagint Trans: They have all turned aside, together they have become worthless; there is none who does good, there is not even one. Their throat is an opened tomb; with their tongues they have practiced deceit; the venom of asps is under their lips; whose mouth is full of cursing and bitterness. Their feet are swift to shed blood; ruin and misery are in their ways, and the way of peace they have not known. There is no fear of God before their eyes.
Gallican Psalter Trans: They have all turned aside; together they have become worthless. There is none who does good, there is not even one. Their throat is an open sepulchre; with their tongues they acted deceitfully; the venom of asps is under their lips; whose mouth is full of cursing and bitterness. Their feet are swift to shed blood; destruction and misery are in their ways, and the way of peace they have not known. There is no fear of God before their eyes.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: All have turned aside; together they have become worthless. There is none who does good, not even one.

Hebrew Trans: Do not all the workers of iniquity know, who devour my people as they eat bread? They do not call upon the LORD.
Septuagint Trans: Will not all the workers of lawlessness come to know, those who devour my people as they eat bread? They have not called upon the Lord.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Shall not all who work iniquity come to know, who devour my people as they eat bread? They have not called upon the Lord.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Shall they not know, all who work iniquity, who devour my people as though eating bread? They have not called upon the Lord.

Hebrew Trans: There they were in great dread, for God is with the generation of the righteous.
Septuagint Trans: There they were seized with fear, where there was no fear, for God is among the righteous generation.
Gallican Psalter Trans: There they trembled with fear where there was no fear, for God is with the just generation.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: There they shall tremble with fear, for God is among the righteous generation.

Hebrew Trans: You would put to shame the counsel of the poor, but the LORD is his refuge.
Septuagint Trans: You have put to shame the counsel of the poor, because the Lord is his hope.
Gallican Psalter Trans: You have confounded the counsel of the poor, because the Lord is his hope.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: You have put to shame the counsel of the poor, because the Lord is his hope.

Hebrew Trans: Oh that out of Zion would come the salvation of Israel! When the LORD restores the captivity of His people, Jacob shall rejoice, Israel shall be glad.
Septuagint Trans: Who will give out of Zion the salvation of Israel? When the Lord restores the captivity of his people, let Jacob rejoice and let Israel be glad.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Who shall give out of Zion the salvation of Israel? When the Lord shall have turned back the captivity of his people, Jacob shall rejoice and Israel shall be glad.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Who will give from Zion the salvation of Israel? When the Lord shall bring back the captivity of his people, Jacob shall rejoice, and Israel shall be glad.

Hebrew Trans: A psalm of David. O LORD, who shall sojourn in your tent? Who shall dwell on your holy hill?
Septuagint Trans: A psalm of David. Lord, who shall sojourn in your tabernacle, and who shall dwell upon your holy mountain?
Gallican Psalter Trans: A psalm of David. Lord, who shall dwell in your tabernacle, or who shall rest upon your holy mountain?
Hebrew Psalter Trans: A song of David. Lord, who shall sojourn in your tent, and who shall dwell on your holy mountain?

Hebrew Trans: He who walks blamelessly and does what is righteous, and speaks truth in his heart.
Septuagint Trans: He who walks blamelessly and works righteousness, speaking truth in his heart.
Gallican Psalter Trans: He who walks without blemish and works justice; who speaks truth in his heart.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: He who walks without blemish and works justice, and speaks truth in his heart.

Hebrew Trans: He has not slandered with his tongue, he has not done evil to his neighbor, and he has not taken up reproach against his kinsman.
Septuagint Trans: He who has not deceived with his tongue, nor done evil to his neighbor, and has not taken up reproach against those nearest to him.
Gallican Psalter Trans: He who has not practiced deceit with his tongue, nor done evil to his neighbor, and has not taken up reproach against his neighbors.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: He who is not loose with his tongue, nor has done evil to his friend, and has not taken up reproach against his neighbor.

Hebrew Trans: In whose eyes a vile person is despised, but he honors those who fear the LORD; he swears to his own hurt and does not change.
Septuagint Trans: In his sight the evildoer is despised, but he glorifies those who fear the Lord; he who swears to his neighbor and does not break faith.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The malignant one is brought to nothing in his sight; but he glorifies those who fear the Lord; he who swears to his neighbor and does not deceive.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The wicked is despised in his eyes, but he glorifies those who fear the Lord; he swears so as to afflict himself and does not change.

Hebrew Trans: His money he has not given at interest, and a bribe against the innocent he has not taken. He who does these things shall never be moved.
Septuagint Trans: He has not given his money upon usury, and has not taken bribes against the innocent. He who does these things shall not be shaken forever.
Gallican Psalter Trans: He who has not given his money to usury, and has not accepted bribes against the innocent. He who does these things shall not be moved forever.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: He has not given his money to usury, and has not accepted bribes against the innocent. He who does these things shall not be moved forever.

Hebrew Trans: A Miktam of David. Keep me, O God, for I have taken refuge in You.
Septuagint Trans: A pillar inscription of David. Preserve me, Lord, for in you I have hoped.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The inscription of the title, of David himself. Preserve me, O Lord, for in you I have hoped.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Of the humble and simple David. Guard me, O God, for I have hoped in you.

Hebrew Trans: I have said to the LORD, "You are my Lord; my goodness is not apart from You."
Septuagint Trans: I said to the Lord, "You are my Lord, for you have no need of my good things."
Gallican Psalter Trans: I have said to the Lord: You are my Lord, for you have no need of my goods.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: I said to God: You are my Lord; my good is nothing without you.

Hebrew Trans: As for the holy ones who are in the earth, they are the noble ones in whom is all my delight.
Septuagint Trans: To the holy ones in his land he has made all his desires wonderful among them.
Gallican Psalter Trans: To the saints who are in his land, he has made wonderful all my desires among them.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: To the saints who are in his land, and to his magnificent ones, all my will is in them.

Hebrew Trans: Their sorrows shall be multiplied who hasten after another; I will not pour out their drink offerings of blood, nor will I take their names upon my lips.
Septuagint Trans: Their infirmities were multiplied; after these things they hastened. I will by no means gather their assemblies of blood, nor will I remember their names upon my lips.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Their infirmities have been multiplied; afterwards they hastened. I will not gather together their assemblies of blood, nor will I be mindful of their names upon my lips.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Their idols shall be multiplied; of those who follow after them I will not pour out their libations of blood, nor will I take up their names upon my lips.

Hebrew Trans: The LORD is the portion of my inheritance and my cup; You maintain my lot.
Septuagint Trans: The Lord is the portion of my inheritance and of my cup; you are the one who restores my inheritance to me.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The Lord is the portion of my inheritance and of my cup: it is you who will restore my inheritance to me.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The Lord is the portion of my inheritance and of my cup; you are the possessor of my lot.

Hebrew Trans: The boundary lines have fallen for me in pleasant places; indeed, my heritage is beautiful to me.
Septuagint Trans: Measuring lines have fallen to me among the finest places, for indeed my inheritance is most excellent to me.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The lines have fallen to me in excellent places; for indeed my inheritance is excellent to me.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The lines have fallen for me in the most beautiful places, and my inheritance is most splendid.

Hebrew Trans: I will bless the LORD who has counseled me; indeed, in the nights my inward parts instruct me.
Septuagint Trans: I will bless the Lord who has given me understanding; moreover, even until night my inward parts have instructed me.
Gallican Psalter Trans: I will bless the Lord who has given me understanding; moreover, even through the night my reins have corrected me.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: I will bless the Lord who has given me counsel; moreover, even in the nights my reins have instructed me.

Hebrew Trans: I have set the LORD always before me; because He is at my right hand, I shall not be moved.
Septuagint Trans: I foresaw the Lord before me continually, for he is at my right hand, so that I may not be shaken.
Gallican Psalter Trans: I set the Lord always in my sight; for he is at my right hand, that I may not be moved.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: I set the Lord before my sight always, for he is at my right hand, that I be not moved.

Hebrew Trans: Therefore my heart is glad, and my glory rejoices; my flesh also shall dwell in safety.
Septuagint Trans: Because of this my heart was gladdened and my tongue rejoiced; moreover also my flesh shall dwell in hope,
Gallican Psalter Trans: Because of this my heart has been glad, and my tongue has rejoiced; moreover my flesh also shall rest in hope.
