Hebrew Psalter Trans: Therefore my heart has been glad, and my glory has rejoiced, and my flesh has dwelt in confidence.

Hebrew Trans: For You will not abandon my soul to Sheol, nor will You allow Your holy one to see the pit.
Septuagint Trans: for you will not abandon my soul to Hades, nor will you allow your holy one to see corruption.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For you will not abandon my soul in hell, nor will you allow your holy one to see corruption.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For you will not abandon my soul in hell, nor will you allow your holy one to see corruption.

Hebrew Trans: You will make known to me the path of life; in Your presence is fullness of joys; at Your right hand are pleasures forevermore.
Septuagint Trans: You have made known to me the paths of life; you will fill me with gladness in your presence; pleasures are at your right hand forevermore.
Gallican Psalter Trans: You have made known to me the ways of life; you will fill me with joy with your countenance: delight is at your right hand even unto the end.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: You will show me the path of life, the fullness of joys before your face, delights at your right hand forevermore.

Hebrew Trans: A prayer of David. Hear, O LORD, what is right; attend to my cry; give ear to my prayer, which is not from lips of deceit.
Septuagint Trans: A prayer of David. Hear, O Lord, my righteousness; attend to my supplication; give ear to my prayer, not made with deceitful lips.
Gallican Psalter Trans: A prayer of David. Hear, O Lord, my justice; attend to my supplication. Perceive with your ears my prayer, not from deceitful lips.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: A prayer of David. Hear, O God, the just one; attend to my supplication. Perceive with your ears my prayer, which is not from lips of deceit.

Hebrew Trans: From before You let my judgment come forth; let Your eyes behold uprightness.
Septuagint Trans: From your presence let my judgment come forth; let my eyes behold what is upright.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Let my judgment come forth from your countenance; let your eyes behold what is equitable.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: From your face let my judgment come forth; let your eyes behold what is equitable.

Hebrew Trans: You have tested my heart, You have visited me in the night; You have refined me — You shall find no evil purpose in me; my mouth does not transgress.
Septuagint Trans: You have tested my heart; you have visited me by night; you have tried me by fire, and no injustice was found in me.
Gallican Psalter Trans: You have tested my heart, you have visited me by night; you have examined me by fire, and no iniquity has been found in me.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: You have tested my heart, you have visited me by night; you have refined me by fire and have not found iniquity.

Hebrew Trans: As for the deeds of men, by the word of Your lips I have kept myself from the paths of the violent.
Septuagint Trans: So that my mouth might not speak of the works of men, on account of the words of your lips I have kept to hard ways.
Gallican Psalter Trans: That my mouth may not speak the works of men: for the sake of the words of your lips, I have kept to hard ways.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: That my thoughts should not pass beyond my mouth: in the works of men, on account of the words of your lips, I have kept myself from the ways of the robber.

Hebrew Trans: Uphold my steps in Your paths, so that my footsteps do not slip.
Septuagint Trans: Set firm my steps in your paths, so that my footsteps may not be shaken.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Perfect my steps in your paths, so that my footsteps may not be moved.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Sustain my steps in your paths, and my footsteps shall not slip.

Hebrew Trans: I have called upon You, for You will answer me, O God; incline Your ear to me, hear my speech.
Septuagint Trans: I have cried out, for you have heard me, O God; incline your ear to me and hear my words.
Gallican Psalter Trans: I have cried out, for you have heard me, O God; incline your ear to me and hear my words.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: I have called upon you, for you will hear me, O God; incline your ear to me and hear my speech.

Hebrew Trans: Show wondrously Your lovingkindnesses, O Savior of those who take refuge from those who rise up against them, by Your right hand.
Septuagint Trans: Make your mercies marvelous, O you who save those who hope in you from those who resist your right hand.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Make wonderful your mercies, you who save those who hope in you from those who resist your right hand.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Make your mercy wonderful, O savior of those who hope, against those who resist your right hand.

Hebrew Trans: Keep me as the apple of the eye; hide me in the shadow of Your wings,
Septuagint Trans: Guard me as the pupil of an eye; in the shelter of your wings you shall cover me,
Gallican Psalter Trans: Guard me as the pupil of the eye; under the shadow of your wings you will protect me,
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Guard me as the pupil within the eye; in the shadow of your wings protect me,

Hebrew Trans: From the wicked who despoil me, my deadly enemies who surround me.
Septuagint Trans: from the face of the ungodly who have afflicted me. My enemies have surrounded my soul;
Gallican Psalter Trans: from the face of the wicked who have afflicted me. My enemies have surrounded my soul on every side.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: from the face of the wicked who lay waste to me. My enemies have surrounded my soul;

Hebrew Trans: They have closed their fat hearts; with their mouths they speak proudly.
Septuagint Trans: they have enclosed their fat; their mouth has spoken arrogance.
Gallican Psalter Trans: They have enclosed their fat; their mouth has spoken with pride.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: they have enclosed themselves in their own fat, and with their mouth they have spoken proudly.

Hebrew Trans: Our steps they have now surrounded; they set their eyes to cast us down to the earth.
Septuagint Trans: Casting me out, they have now encircled me; they have set their eyes to turn me down to the ground.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Casting me out, they have now surrounded me; they have set their eyes to turn down to the earth.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Advancing against me, they have now surrounded me; they have set their eyes to cast down to the earth.

Hebrew Trans: His likeness is as a lion that longs to tear, and as a young lion lurking in secret places.
Septuagint Trans: They took me up like a lion ready for prey, and like a young lion dwelling in hidden places.
Gallican Psalter Trans: They have taken me up like a lion ready for prey, and like a young lion dwelling in hidden places.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: His likeness is as a lion desiring prey, and as a young lion sitting in hidden places.

Hebrew Trans: Arise, O LORD, confront him, bring him down; deliver my soul from the wicked by Your sword,
Septuagint Trans: Arise, O Lord, confront them and bring them down; deliver my soul from the ungodly—your sword from the enemies of your hand.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Arise, O Lord, confront him and overthrow him; rescue my soul from the wicked one, your sword—
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Arise, O Lord, confront his face, bring him low; save my soul from the wicked one, who is your sword,

Hebrew Trans: From men, by Your hand, O LORD, from men of the world whose portion is in this life, and whose belly You fill with Your treasure; their children are satisfied, and they leave their abundance to their little ones.
Septuagint Trans: O Lord, from the few of the earth, divide them in their life; and their belly has been filled with your hidden treasures; they were satisfied with sons and left what remained to their infants.
Gallican Psalter Trans: from the enemies of your hand. O Lord, divide them from the few of the earth in their life; their belly has been filled from your hidden stores; they are satisfied with children, and they have left their remnants to their little ones.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: from the men of your hand, O Lord, from those who have died in the depths, whose portion is in this life, and whose belly you have filled from your hidden stores; who shall be satisfied with sons, and shall leave their remains to their little ones.

Hebrew Trans: As for me, in righteousness I shall behold Your face; I shall be satisfied, when I awake, with Your likeness.
Septuagint Trans: But I in righteousness shall behold your face; I shall be satisfied when your glory appears.
Gallican Psalter Trans: But I in justice shall appear before your sight; I shall be satisfied when your glory shall appear.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: I, in justice, shall see your face; I shall be filled, when I awake, with your likeness.

Hebrew Trans: For the choirmaster. Of the servant of the LORD, of David, who spoke to the LORD the words of this song on the day the LORD delivered him from the hand of all his enemies and from the hand of Saul.
Septuagint Trans: For the end; a psalm of the servant of the Lord, of David, which he spoke to the Lord—the words of this song—in the day the Lord delivered him from the hand of all his enemies and from the hand of Saul.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Unto the end, for the servant of the Lord, David, who spoke to the Lord the words of this canticle on the day that the Lord delivered him from the hand of all his enemies and from the hand of Saul.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: To the victor, a servant of the Lord, of David, who spoke to the Lord the words of this song on the day when the Lord delivered him from the hand of all his enemies and from the hand of Saul.

Hebrew Trans: And he said: I love you, O LORD, my strength.
Septuagint Trans: And he said: I will love you, O Lord, my strength.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And he said: I will love you, O Lord, my strength.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And he said: I will love you, O Lord, my strength.

Hebrew Trans: The LORD is my rock and my fortress and my deliverer, my God, my rock in whom I take refuge, my shield and the horn of my salvation, my stronghold.
Septuagint Trans: The Lord is my firm foundation and my refuge and my deliverer; my God is my helper, and I will hope in him; my shield and the horn of my salvation, my upholder.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The Lord is my firmament, and my refuge, and my deliverer. My God is my helper, and I will hope in him. My protector, and the horn of my salvation, and my upholder.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: O Lord, my rock and my fortress and my savior; my God, my strong one, I will hope in him; my shield and the horn of my salvation, my protector.

Hebrew Trans: I call upon the LORD, who is worthy of praise, and from my enemies I am saved.
Septuagint Trans: Praising, I will call upon the Lord, and I shall be saved from my enemies.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Praising, I will call upon the Lord, and I shall be saved from my enemies.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: I will call upon the Lord, who is worthy of praise, and I shall be saved from my enemies.

Hebrew Trans: The cords of death encompassed me, and the torrents of destruction terrified me.
Septuagint Trans: The pangs of death encompassed me, and torrents of lawlessness troubled me greatly.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The sorrows of death surrounded me, and the torrents of iniquity troubled me.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The cords of death surrounded me, and the torrents of the devil terrified me.

Hebrew Trans: The cords of Sheol surrounded me; the snares of death confronted me.
Septuagint Trans: The pangs of Hades encircled me; the snares of death came upon me beforehand.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The sorrows of hell surrounded me, and the snares of death overtook me.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The cords of hell surrounded me; the snares of death came upon me.

Hebrew Trans: In my distress I called upon the LORD, and to my God I cried out. He heard my voice from His temple, and my cry before Him came into His ears.
Septuagint Trans: And in my being afflicted I called upon the Lord, and to my God I cried out. He heard my voice from his holy temple, and my cry before him shall enter into his ears.
Gallican Psalter Trans: When I was in tribulation, I called upon the Lord, and I cried out to my God. He heard my voice from his holy temple, and my cry before him entered into his ears.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: In my tribulation I will call upon the Lord, and to my God I will cry out; he will hear my voice from his temple, and my cry before his face shall come into his ears.

Hebrew Trans: Then the earth shook and trembled, and the foundations of the mountains quaked and shook, for He was angry.
Septuagint Trans: And the earth was shaken and trembled, and the foundations of the mountains were troubled and were shaken, because God was angry with them.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And the earth was moved and trembled, and the foundations of the mountains were disturbed and shaken, because he was angry with them.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The earth was moved and trembled, and the foundations of the mountains were shaken and were shattered, because he was angry.

Hebrew Trans: Smoke rose from His nostrils, and fire from His mouth devoured; coals blazed forth from Him.
Septuagint Trans: Smoke went up in his wrath, and fire from his face consumed; coals were kindled from him.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Smoke ascended in his wrath, and fire blazed forth from his face; coals were kindled by him.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Smoke ascended from his fury, and a devouring fire from his mouth; coals were kindled by him.

Hebrew Trans: And He bowed the heavens and came down, and thick darkness was under His feet.
Septuagint Trans: And he bowed the heaven and came down, and thick darkness was under his feet.
Gallican Psalter Trans: He bowed the heavens and descended, and darkness was under his feet.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: He bowed the heavens and descended, and darkness was under his feet.

Hebrew Trans: And He rode upon a cherub and flew, and He soared upon the wings of the wind.
Septuagint Trans: And he mounted upon cherubim and flew; he flew upon the wings of the winds.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And he ascended upon the cherubim and flew; he flew upon the wings of the winds.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And he ascended upon a cherub and flew upon the wings of the wind.

Hebrew Trans: He made darkness His hiding place, His canopy around Him, dark waters and thick clouds of the skies.
Septuagint Trans: And he made darkness his hiding place; round about him was his tent—dark water in the clouds of the air.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And he made darkness his hiding place; his tabernacle round about him, dark water in the clouds of the air.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: He made darkness his hiding place; around him was his tabernacle, dark waters in the clouds of the sky.

Hebrew Trans: From the brightness before Him His clouds passed away, hail and coals of fire.
Septuagint Trans: From the brightness before him the clouds passed through—hail and coals of fire.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Before the brightness in his sight, his clouds passed away, hail and coals of fire.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Before the brightness in his sight the clouds passed away, hail and coals of fire.

Hebrew Trans: And the LORD thundered in the heavens, and the Most High gave forth His voice, hail and coals of fire.
Septuagint Trans: And the Lord thundered from heaven, and the Most High gave forth his voice.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And the Lord thundered from heaven, and the Most High gave forth his voice: hail and coals of fire.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And the Lord thundered from heaven, and the Most High gave forth his voice: hail and coals of fire.

Hebrew Trans: And He sent out His arrows and scattered them, and lightning bolts in abundance, and He routed them.
Septuagint Trans: And he sent out arrows and scattered them, and he multiplied lightnings and threw them into confusion.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And he sent forth his arrows and scattered them, and he multiplied lightnings and troubled them.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And he sent forth his arrows and scattered them; he multiplied lightnings and disturbed them.

Hebrew Trans: And the channels of waters were seen, and the foundations of the world were laid bare, at Your rebuke, O LORD, at the blast of the breath of Your nostrils.
Septuagint Trans: And the springs of the waters were seen, and the foundations of the world were uncovered, at your rebuke, O Lord, at the blast of the breath of your wrath.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And the fountains of the waters appeared, and the foundations of the world were revealed, at your rebuke, O Lord, at the blast of the spirit of your wrath.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And the outpourings of waters appeared, and the foundations of the world were revealed, at your rebuke, O Lord, at the blast of the spirit of your fury.

Hebrew Trans: He reached from on high, He took me; He drew me out of many waters.
Septuagint Trans: He sent from on high and took me; he drew me out of many waters.
Gallican Psalter Trans: He sent from on high and took me; he drew me out of many waters.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: He sent from on high and took me; he drew me out of many waters.

Hebrew Trans: He delivered me from my strong enemy, and from those who hated me, for they were too mighty for me.
Septuagint Trans: He shall deliver me from my mighty enemies and from those who hate me, for they were stronger than I.
Gallican Psalter Trans: He will deliver me from my strongest enemies, and from those who hated me, for they were too mighty for me.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: He delivered me from my most powerful enemies, and from those who hated me, for they were stronger than I.

Hebrew Trans: They confronted me in the day of my calamity, but the LORD was a support to me.
Septuagint Trans: They came upon me in the day of my affliction, and the Lord became my support.
Gallican Psalter Trans: They came upon me in the day of my affliction, and the Lord became my protector.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: They came upon me in the day of my affliction, and the Lord became my support.

Hebrew Trans: And He brought me out into a broad place; He rescued me because He delighted in me.
Septuagint Trans: And he brought me out into a broad place; he will deliver me because he delighted in me. He will deliver me from my mighty enemies and from those who hate me.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And he brought me forth into a broad place; he will save me, because he willed me.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And he led me forth into a broad place; he delivered me because he was pleased with me.

Hebrew Trans: The LORD rewarded me according to my righteousness; according to the cleanness of my hands He repaid me.
Septuagint Trans: And the Lord will repay me according to my righteousness, and according to the cleanness of my hands he will repay me.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And the Lord will reward me according to my justice, and according to the purity of my hands he will reward me.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The Lord rewarded me according to my righteousness, and according to the cleanness of my hands he repaid me.

Hebrew Trans: For I have kept the ways of the LORD, and I have not wickedly departed from my God.
Septuagint Trans: For I have kept the ways of the Lord and have not acted impiously against my God.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Because I have kept the ways of the Lord, and have not acted impiously against my God.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For I have kept the ways of the Lord, and I have not acted wickedly against my God.

Hebrew Trans: For all His judgments were before me, and His statutes I did not put away from me.
Septuagint Trans: For all his judgments are before me, and his statutes I have not put away from me.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For all his judgments are in my sight, and his statutes I have not put away from me.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For all his judgments are in my sight, and his precepts I have not removed from me.

Hebrew Trans: And I was blameless with Him, and I kept myself from my iniquity.
Septuagint Trans: And I shall be blameless with him, and I shall guard myself from my lawlessness.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And I shall be spotless with him, and I shall keep myself from my iniquity.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And I was blameless with him, and I kept myself from my iniquity.

Hebrew Trans: And the LORD repaid me according to my righteousness, according to the cleanness of my hands before His eyes.
Septuagint Trans: And the Lord will repay me according to my righteousness and according to the cleanness of my hands before his eyes.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And the Lord will reward me according to my justice, and according to the purity of my hands in the sight of his eyes.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And the Lord restored to me according to my righteousness, and according to the cleanness of my hands in the sight of his eyes.

Hebrew Trans: With the faithful You show Yourself faithful; with the blameless man You show Yourself blameless.
Septuagint Trans: With the holy one you will be holy, and with an innocent man you will be innocent.
Gallican Psalter Trans: With the holy one you will be holy, and with the innocent man you will be innocent.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: With the holy one you will be holy; with the innocent man you will deal innocently.

Hebrew Trans: With the pure You show Yourself pure, and with the crooked You show Yourself shrewd.
Septuagint Trans: And with the chosen one you will be chosen, and with the crooked you will deal perversely.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And with the elect you will be elect, and with the perverse you will be perverted.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: With the chosen one you will be chosen, and with the perverse you will be perverted.

Hebrew Trans: For You save a humble people, but haughty eyes You bring low.
Septuagint Trans: For you will save a humble people, and the eyes of the proud you will humble.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For you will save the humble people, and you will bring down the eyes of the proud.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For you will save the humble people, and you will humble the haughty eyes.

Hebrew Trans: For You light my lamp; the LORD my God illumines my darkness.
Septuagint Trans: For you will light my lamp, O Lord; my God, you will illuminate my darkness.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For you illuminate my lamp, O Lord; my God, you illuminate my darkness.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For you will light my lamp, O Lord my God; you will illuminate my darkness.

Hebrew Trans: For by You I can run against a troop, and by my God I can leap over a wall.
Septuagint Trans: For by you I shall be delivered from a trial, and by my God I shall leap over a wall.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For in you I shall be delivered from temptation, and in my God I shall leap over a wall.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For in you I will run, girded, and in my God I will leap over a wall.

Hebrew Trans: As for God, His way is perfect; the word of the LORD is refined; He is a shield to all who take refuge in Him.
Septuagint Trans: As for my God, blameless is his way; the words of the Lord are tested by fire; he is a shield to all who hope in him.
Gallican Psalter Trans: My God, his way is undefiled; the words of the Lord are tested by fire; he is the protector of all who hope in him.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: God—blameless is his way; the word of the Lord is tested by fire; he is a shield of all who hope in him.

Hebrew Trans: For who is God besides the LORD, and who is a rock except our God?
Septuagint Trans: For who is God except the Lord? And who is God except our God?
Gallican Psalter Trans: For who is God besides the Lord, and who is God besides our God?
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For who is god besides the Lord, and who is strong besides our God?

Hebrew Trans: The God who girds me with strength and makes my way perfect,
Septuagint Trans: The God who girds me with strength and has made my way blameless;
Gallican Psalter Trans: God, who girds me with strength, and has made my way spotless.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: God, who girds me with strength and has made my way blameless,

Hebrew Trans: Who makes my feet like the deer's, and upon my high places He sets me,
Septuagint Trans: Who makes my feet like a deer's and sets me upon the heights;
Gallican Psalter Trans: Who has made my feet perfect like the feet of deer, and sets me upon high places.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Making my feet equal to the deer's and setting me upon the heights,

Hebrew Trans: Who trains my hands for battle, so that my arms can bend a bow of bronze.
Septuagint Trans: Who teaches my hands for war; and you have made my arms a bow of bronze.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Who teaches my hands for battle, and you have made my arms like a bow of bronze.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Teaching my hands for battle, and fitting my arms like a bow of bronze.

Hebrew Trans: And You have given me the shield of Your salvation, and Your right hand upholds me, and Your gentleness makes me great.
Septuagint Trans: And you have given me the shield of your salvation, and your right hand has upheld me, and your discipline has set me upright to the end, and your discipline itself shall teach me.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And you have given me the protection of your salvation, and your right hand has upheld me, and your discipline has corrected me unto the end, and your discipline itself will teach me.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: You have given me the shield of your salvation, and your right hand has strengthened me, and your gentleness has multiplied me.

Hebrew Trans: You enlarge my steps beneath me, and my ankles have not slipped.
Septuagint Trans: You broadened my steps beneath me, and my footsteps did not grow weak.
Gallican Psalter Trans: You have enlarged my steps beneath me, and my footsteps have not been weakened.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: You will widen my steps beneath me, and my ankles will not falter.

Hebrew Trans: I pursued my enemies and overtook them, and I did not turn back until they were consumed.
Septuagint Trans: I shall pursue my enemies and overtake them, and I shall not turn back until they are consumed.
Gallican Psalter Trans: I will pursue my enemies and overtake them, and I will not turn back until they fail.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: I will pursue my enemies and seize them, and I will not turn back until I consume them.

Hebrew Trans: I struck them down and they were not able to rise; they fell under my feet.
Septuagint Trans: I shall crush them, and they shall not be able to stand; they shall fall under my feet.
Gallican Psalter Trans: I will break them, and they will not be able to stand; they will fall beneath my feet.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: I will strike them down and they will not be able to rise; they will fall under my feet.

Hebrew Trans: And You girded me with strength for battle; You subdued my adversaries beneath me.
Septuagint Trans: And you girded me with strength for war; you tripped up all who rose against me beneath me.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And you have girded me with strength for war; you have subdued those who rose up against me beneath me.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: You have girded me with strength for battle; you will bend down those who resist me beneath me.

Hebrew Trans: And my enemies You made turn their backs to me, and those who hated me I destroyed.
Septuagint Trans: And you gave me the backs of my enemies, and those who hated me you utterly destroyed.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And you have given me the backs of my enemies, and those who hated me you have destroyed.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: You have given me the backs of my enemies, and those who hate me you have destroyed.

Hebrew Trans: They cried out, but there was no savior—to the LORD, but He did not answer them.
Septuagint Trans: They cried out, and there was none to save; to the Lord, and he did not hear them.
Gallican Psalter Trans: They cried out, and there was no one to save them; to the Lord, and he did not hear them.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: They will cry out and there will be no one to save them; to the Lord, and he will not hear them.

Hebrew Trans: And I ground them fine as dust before the wind; like the mud of the streets I poured them out.
Septuagint Trans: And I shall grind them fine as dust before the wind; like mud of the streets I shall smooth them away.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And I will grind them as dust before the face of the wind; like the mud of the streets I will destroy them.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: I will crush them like dust before the face of the wind; like the mud of the streets I will cast them out.

Hebrew Trans: You delivered me from the strife of the people; You made me head of the nations; a people I had not known served me.
Septuagint Trans: You shall deliver me from the contradictions of the people; you shall set me as head of nations. A people whom I did not know served me.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Deliver me from the contradictions of the people; you will establish me as the head of the nations. A people whom I did not know has served me.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: You will save me from the contradictions of the people; you will place me as head of nations; a people whom I did not know will serve me.

Hebrew Trans: At the hearing of the ear they obeyed me; foreigners submitted to me.
Septuagint Trans: At the hearing of the ear they obeyed me; foreign sons lied to me.
Gallican Psalter Trans: At the hearing of the ear they obeyed me; foreign sons have lied to me.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: At the hearing of the ear they will obey me; foreign sons will lie to me.

Hebrew Trans: Foreigners withered away and came trembling out of their strongholds.
Septuagint Trans: Foreign sons grew old and limped away from their paths.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Foreign sons have grown old and have limped from their paths.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Foreign sons will wither away and will be confined in their straits.

Hebrew Trans: The LORD lives, and blessed be my Rock, and exalted be the God of my salvation,
Septuagint Trans: The Lord lives, and blessed be my God, and let the God of my salvation be exalted.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The Lord lives, and blessed is my God, and let the God of my salvation be exalted.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The Lord lives, and blessed is my God, and the God of my salvation will be exalted.

Hebrew Trans: The God who grants me vengeance and subdues peoples under me,
Septuagint Trans: The God who gives vengeances to me and has subjected peoples under me;
Gallican Psalter Trans: God, who gives vengeance to me and subdues peoples under me.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: God, who gives vengeances to me and gathers peoples under me,

Hebrew Trans: My deliverer from my enemies; indeed You lift me above those who rise against me; from the man of violence You rescue me.
Septuagint Trans: My deliverer from my wrathful enemies—from those who rise up against me you will exalt me; from the unjust man you will deliver me.
Gallican Psalter Trans: My deliverer from the wrathful nations; and from those who rise up against me you will exalt me; from the unjust man you will rescue me.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Who saves me from my enemies, and from those who resist me you raise me up; from the wicked man deliver me.

Hebrew Trans: Therefore I will give thanks to You among the nations, O LORD, and I will sing praises to Your name.
Septuagint Trans: Therefore I will confess you among the nations, O Lord, and I will sing to your name.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Therefore I will confess to you among the nations, O Lord, and I will sing a psalm to your name.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Therefore I will confess to you among the nations, O Lord, and I will sing to your name,

Hebrew Trans: He gives great salvation to His king, and shows steadfast love to His anointed, to David and to his offspring forever.
Septuagint Trans: He who magnifies the salvations of his king and shows mercy to his anointed one, to David and to his seed forever.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Magnifying the salvation of his king, and showing mercy to his anointed, to David and to his seed forever.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: To him who magnifies the salvations of his king, and shows mercy to his anointed, to David and to his seed forever.

Hebrew Trans: For the choirmaster, a psalm of David.
Septuagint Trans: For the end, a psalm of David.
Gallican Psalter Trans: To the end, a psalm of David.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: To the victor, a song of David.

Hebrew Trans: The heavens declare the glory of God, and the firmament proclaims the work of His hands.
Septuagint Trans: The heavens declare the glory of God, and the firmament proclaims the work of his hands.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The heavens declare the glory of God, and the firmament announces the works of his hands.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The heavens declare the glory of God, and the firmament announces the work of his hands.

Hebrew Trans: Day unto day pours forth speech, and night unto night reveals knowledge.
Septuagint Trans: Day unto day pours forth speech, and night unto night declares knowledge.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Day unto day pours forth speech, and night unto night reveals knowledge.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Day unto day pours forth speech, and night unto night reveals knowledge.

Hebrew Trans: There is no speech and there are no words; their voice is not heard.
Septuagint Trans: There are no speeches nor words whose voices are not heard.
Gallican Psalter Trans: There are no speeches nor words whose voices are not heard.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: There is no speech and there are no words whose voice is not heard.

Hebrew Trans: In all the earth their line goes out, and at the end of the world their words; for the sun He has set a tent among them.
Septuagint Trans: Their sound has gone out into all the earth, and their words to the ends of the inhabited world. In the sun he has set his tabernacle.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Their sound has gone out into all the earth, and their words unto the ends of the world.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Their sound has gone out into all the earth, and their words to the ends of the world. He has placed a tabernacle for the sun among them.

Hebrew Trans: And he is like a bridegroom coming out of his chamber, he rejoices like a mighty man to run his course.
Septuagint Trans: And he is as a bridegroom coming forth from his chamber; he shall rejoice as a giant to run his course.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And he, as a bridegroom coming forth from his chamber, rejoiced as a giant to run his course; in the sun he has set his tabernacle.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And he, like a bridegroom proceeding from his chamber, rejoiced as a strong man to run his course.

Hebrew Trans: From the end of the heavens is his going forth, and his circuit is unto their ends, and nothing is hidden from his heat.
Septuagint Trans: From the end of heaven is his going forth, and his circuit reaches to the end of heaven, and there is no one who shall be hidden from his heat.
Gallican Psalter Trans: His going forth is from the height of heaven, and his circuit even to the height thereof; nor is there anyone who can hide himself from his heat.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: His going forth is from the height of heaven, and his circuit even to the height thereof; nor is there anyone who can hide himself from his heat.

Hebrew Trans: The law of the LORD is perfect, restoring the soul; the testimony of the LORD is sure, making wise the simple.
Septuagint Trans: The law of the Lord is blameless, restoring souls; the testimony of the Lord is faithful, making wise the simple.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The law of the Lord is immaculate, converting souls; the testimony of the Lord is faithful, bestowing wisdom upon the little ones.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The law of the Lord is undefiled, converting the soul; the testimony of the Lord is faithful, bestowing wisdom upon the little ones.

Hebrew Trans: The precepts of the LORD are right, rejoicing the heart; the commandment of the LORD is pure, enlightening the eyes.
Septuagint Trans: The ordinances of the Lord are upright, gladdening the heart; the commandment of the Lord is radiant, enlightening the eyes.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The justices of the Lord are right, gladdening hearts; the commandment of the Lord is bright, enlightening the eyes.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The precepts of the Lord are right, gladdening the heart; the commandment of the Lord is bright, illuminating the eyes.

Hebrew Trans: The fear of the LORD is clean, enduring forever; the judgments of the LORD are true, they are righteous altogether.
Septuagint Trans: The fear of the Lord is pure, enduring forever and ever; the judgments of the Lord are true, justified altogether.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The fear of the Lord is holy, enduring forever and ever; the judgments of the Lord are true, justified in themselves.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The fear of the Lord is clean, enduring forever; the judgments of the Lord are true, justified in themselves.

Hebrew Trans: More to be desired are they than gold, even than much fine gold; and sweeter than honey and the drippings of the honeycomb.
Septuagint Trans: More to be desired than gold and much precious stone, and sweeter than honey and the honeycomb.
Gallican Psalter Trans: More to be desired than gold and many precious stones, and sweeter than honey and the honeycomb.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: More to be desired than gold and many a precious stone, and sweeter than honey and the overflowing honeycomb.

Hebrew Trans: Moreover by them is your servant warned; in keeping them there is great reward.
Septuagint Trans: For indeed your servant keeps them; in keeping them there is great reward.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For indeed your servant keeps them; in keeping them there is great reward.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For indeed your servant shall be taught by them; in keeping them there is much fruit.

Hebrew Trans: Who can discern errors? From hidden faults cleanse me.
Septuagint Trans: Who shall understand transgressions? From my hidden ones cleanse me.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Who can understand transgressions? From my hidden ones cleanse me.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Who can understand errors? From hidden things cleanse me.

Hebrew Trans: Also from presumptuous sins keep back your servant; let them not have dominion over me; then I shall be blameless, and I shall be innocent of great transgression.
Septuagint Trans: And from strange sins spare your servant; if they do not have dominion over me, then I shall be blameless and I shall be cleansed from great sin.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And from those of others spare your servant; if they shall not have dominion over me, then I shall be immaculate, and I shall be cleansed from the greatest transgression.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: From the proud also deliver your servant; if they shall not have dominion over me, then I shall be undefiled, and I shall be cleansed from the greatest transgression.

Hebrew Trans: Let the words of my mouth and the meditation of my heart be acceptable before you, O LORD, my rock and my redeemer.
Septuagint Trans: And let the words of my mouth and the meditation of my heart be acceptable before you always, O Lord, my helper and my redeemer.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And the words of my mouth shall be such as to please, and the meditation of my heart always in your sight, O Lord, my helper and my redeemer.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Let the words of my mouth be pleasing, and the meditation of my heart in your sight, O Lord, my strength and my redeemer.

Hebrew Trans: For the chief musician, a psalm of David.
Septuagint Trans: For the end, a psalm of David.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Unto the end, a psalm of David.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: To the victor, a song of David.

Hebrew Trans: May the LORD answer you in the day of trouble; may the name of the God of Jacob set you on high.
Septuagint Trans: May the Lord hear you in the day of affliction; may the name of the God of Jacob shield you.
Gallican Psalter Trans: May the Lord hear you in the day of tribulation; may the name of the God of Jacob protect you.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: May the Lord hear you in the day of tribulation; may the name of the God of Jacob protect you.

Hebrew Trans: May he send your help from the sanctuary, and from Zion may he sustain you.
Septuagint Trans: May he send you help from the sanctuary, and from Zion may he support you.
Gallican Psalter Trans: May he send you help from the sanctuary, and from Zion may he defend you.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: May he send you help from the sanctuary, and from Zion may he strengthen you.

Hebrew Trans: May he remember all your offerings, and may he accept your burnt offering. Selah.
Septuagint Trans: May he remember all your sacrifice, and may your whole burnt offering be made rich. Interlude.
Gallican Psalter Trans: May he be mindful of all your sacrifice, and may your burnt offering be made fat. Diapsalma.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: May he be mindful of all your sacrifice, and may your burnt offering always be made rich.

Hebrew Trans: May he grant you according to your heart, and fulfill all your counsel.
Septuagint Trans: May he grant you according to your heart, and fulfill all your counsel.
Gallican Psalter Trans: May he grant you according to your heart, and confirm all your counsel.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: May he give to you according to your heart, and fulfill all your will.

Hebrew Trans: We will shout for joy in your salvation, and in the name of our God we will set up our banners. May the LORD fulfill all your petitions.
Septuagint Trans: We will rejoice in your salvation, and in the name of our God we will be magnified. May the Lord fulfill all your requests.
Gallican Psalter Trans: We will rejoice in your salvation, and in the name of our God we will be magnified. May the Lord fulfill all your petitions.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: We will praise in your salvation, and in the name of our God we will lead choruses; may the Lord fulfill all your petitions.

Hebrew Trans: Now I know that the LORD has saved his anointed; he will answer him from his holy heavens with the mighty saving deeds of his right hand.
Septuagint Trans: Now I know that the Lord has saved his anointed one; he will hear him from his holy heaven; the salvation of his right hand is with mighty deeds.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Now I have known that the Lord has saved his anointed one; he will hear him from his holy heaven; salvation is in the powers of his right hand.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Now I know that the Lord will save his anointed one; he will hear him from his holy heaven, in the strength of the salvation of his right hand.

Hebrew Trans: Some trust in chariots and some in horses, but we will make mention of the name of the LORD our God.
Septuagint Trans: Some trust in chariots, and some in horses, but we will be magnified in the name of the Lord our God.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Some in chariots and some in horses, but we will call upon the name of the Lord our God.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: These trust in chariots, and these in horses, but we will remember the name of the Lord our God.

Hebrew Trans: They have bowed down and fallen, but we have risen and stood upright.
Septuagint Trans: They were tripped up and fell, but we rose up and were set upright.
Gallican Psalter Trans: They have been bound and have fallen, but we have risen and have been set upright.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: They have been bowed down and have fallen, but we have risen again and stand upright.

Hebrew Trans: O LORD, save! May the King answer us in the day we call.
Septuagint Trans: Lord, save your king, and hear us in whatever day we call upon you.
Gallican Psalter Trans: O Lord, save the king, and hear us in the day on which we shall call upon you.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: O Lord, save; may the king hear us in the day when we shall call upon him.

Hebrew Trans: For the choirmaster, a psalm of David.
Septuagint Trans: For the end, a psalm of David.
Gallican Psalter Trans: To the end, a psalm of David.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: To the victor, a song of David.

Hebrew Trans: O LORD, in Your strength the king rejoices, and in Your salvation how greatly he exults!
Septuagint Trans: O Lord, in your strength the king shall rejoice, and in your salvation he shall greatly exult.
Gallican Psalter Trans: O Lord, in your strength the king shall rejoice, and in your salvation he shall exult exceedingly.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: O Lord, in your strength the king shall rejoice, and in your salvation he shall exult greatly.

Hebrew Trans: The desire of his heart You have given him, and the request of his lips You have not withheld. Selah.
Septuagint Trans: You have given him the desire of his soul, and you have not denied him the wish of his lips. Selah.
Gallican Psalter Trans: You have granted him the desire of his soul, and you have not defrauded him of the will of his lips. Diapsalma.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: You have given him the desire of his heart, and you have not defrauded him of the will of his lips forever.

Hebrew Trans: For You meet him with blessings of goodness; You set upon his head a crown of fine gold.
Septuagint Trans: For you came before him with blessings of goodness; you set upon his head a crown of precious stone.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For you have anticipated him with blessings of sweetness; you have set upon his head a crown of precious stone.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For you will meet him with blessings of goodness; you will place on his head a crown of precious stone.

Hebrew Trans: Life he asked of You; You gave it to him—length of days forever and ever.
Septuagint Trans: He asked life of you, and you gave him length of days forever and ever.
Gallican Psalter Trans: He asked life of you, and you have given him length of days forever and into the age of ages.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: He asked life of you, and you gave him length of days forever and ever.

Hebrew Trans: Great is his glory through Your salvation; majesty and splendor You bestow upon him.
Septuagint Trans: Great is his glory in your salvation; glory and majesty you shall place upon him.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Great is his glory in your salvation; glory and great beauty you shall lay upon him.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Great is his glory in your salvation; glory and beauty you will place upon him.

Hebrew Trans: For You make him blessed forever; You gladden him with joy in Your presence.
Septuagint Trans: For you shall give him blessing forever and ever; you shall gladden him with joy in the presence of your face.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For you shall give him to be a blessing forever and ever; you shall make him glad with joy in your countenance.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For you will make him a blessing forever, and you will gladden him with joy before your face.

Hebrew Trans: For the king trusts in the LORD, and through the lovingkindness of the Most High he shall not be shaken.
Septuagint Trans: For the king hopes in the Lord, and in the mercy of the Most High he shall not be shaken.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For the king hopes in the Lord, and in the mercy of the Most High he shall not be moved.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For the king trusts in the Lord, and in the mercy of the Most High he shall not be deceived.

Hebrew Trans: Your hand shall find all your enemies; your right hand shall find those who hate you.
Septuagint Trans: May your hand find all your enemies; may your right hand find all who hate you.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Let your hand be found by all your enemies; let your right hand find all those who hate you.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Your hand shall find all your enemies; your right hand shall find those who hate you.

Hebrew Trans: You shall make them as a fiery furnace at the time of Your appearing; the LORD in His wrath shall swallow them up, and fire shall consume them.
Septuagint Trans: You shall make them as a fiery furnace in the time of your presence; the Lord in his wrath shall throw them into confusion, and fire shall devour them.
Gallican Psalter Trans: You shall make them as an oven of fire in the time of your countenance; the Lord shall trouble them in his wrath, and fire shall devour them.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: You will make them like a furnace of fire in the time of your countenance; the Lord in his fury will cast them down, and fire shall devour them.

Hebrew Trans: Their offspring You shall destroy from the earth, and their seed from among the children of men.
Septuagint Trans: Their fruit you shall destroy from the earth, and their seed from among the sons of men.
Gallican Psalter Trans: You shall destroy their fruit from the earth, and their seed from among the children of men.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: You will destroy their fruit from the earth, and their seed from among the children of men.

Hebrew Trans: For they intended evil against You; they devised a scheme—they shall not prevail.
Septuagint Trans: For they inclined evils against you; they devised a plan which they shall by no means be able to establish.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For they have turned evils against you; they have devised counsels which they were not able to establish.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For they have inclined evil against you; they devised a wickedness which they were not able to accomplish.

Hebrew Trans: For You shall make them turn their back; with Your bowstrings You shall aim at their faces.
Septuagint Trans: For you shall make them turn their back; among your remnants you shall prepare against their face.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For you shall set them as a back; among your remnants you shall prepare their face.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For you will set them as a shoulder; you will aim your bowstrings against their faces.

Hebrew Trans: Be exalted, O LORD, in Your strength! We will sing and make music of Your might.
Septuagint Trans: Be exalted, O Lord, in your strength; we shall sing and praise your mighty deeds.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Be exalted, O Lord, in your strength; we will sing and praise your mighty deeds.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Be exalted, O Lord, in your strength; we will sing and praise your mighty deeds.

Hebrew Trans: For the choirmaster, upon Aijeleth HaShachar [the doe of the dawn], a psalm of David.
Septuagint Trans: For the end, concerning the help of the dawn, a psalm of David.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Unto the end, for the assumption of the morning, a psalm of David.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: To the victor, for the morning deer, a song.

Hebrew Trans: My God, my God, why have you forsaken me? Far from my salvation are the words of my groaning.
Septuagint Trans: O God, my God, attend to me; why have you forsaken me? Far from my salvation are the words of my transgressions.
Gallican Psalter Trans: God, my God, look upon me: why have you forsaken me? Far from my salvation are the words of my transgressions.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: God, my God, why have you forsaken me? Far from my salvation are the words of my roaring.

Hebrew Trans: My God, I call by day, but you do not answer; and by night, but there is no rest for me.
Septuagint Trans: My God, I will cry by day, and you will not hear; and by night, and it shall not be counted as folly to me.
Gallican Psalter Trans: My God, I will cry out by day, and you will not hear; and by night, and it is not unto foolishness for me.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: My God, I will cry out by day, and you will not hear; and by night, nor is there silence for me.

Hebrew Trans: Yet you are holy, enthroned upon the praises of Israel.
Septuagint Trans: But you dwell among the holy ones, O praise of Israel.
Gallican Psalter Trans: But you dwell in the holy place, O Praise of Israel.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And you, Holy One, who dwells in the praises of Israel.

Hebrew Trans: In you our fathers trusted; they trusted, and you delivered them.
Septuagint Trans: In you our fathers hoped; they hoped, and you delivered them.
Gallican Psalter Trans: In you our fathers hoped; they hoped, and you delivered them.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: In you our fathers trusted; they trusted and you saved them.

Hebrew Trans: To you they cried out and were rescued; in you they trusted and were not put to shame.
Septuagint Trans: To you they cried out and were saved; in you they hoped and were not put to shame.
Gallican Psalter Trans: To you they cried out, and they were saved; in you they hoped, and they were not confounded.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: To you they cried out and were saved; in you they trusted and were not confounded.

Hebrew Trans: But I am a worm and not a man, a reproach of men and despised by the people.
Septuagint Trans: But I am a worm and not a man, a reproach of men and a despised thing of the people.
Gallican Psalter Trans: But I am a worm, and not a man; a reproach of men, and an outcast of the people.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: But I am a worm and not a man, a reproach of men and the despised of the people.

Hebrew Trans: All who see me mock me; they open wide their lips, they shake their head.
Septuagint Trans: All who looked upon me mocked me; they spoke with their lips, they shook their head:
Gallican Psalter Trans: All who see me have derided me; they spoke with their lips, they shook their head.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: All who see me mock me; they open their lips, they shake their head.

Hebrew Trans: "He committed himself to the LORD; let Him deliver him; let Him rescue him, for He delights in him."
Septuagint Trans: "He hoped in the Lord; let him deliver him; let him save him, for he desires him."
Gallican Psalter Trans: He hoped in the Lord; let him deliver him; let him save him, since he wills him.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: He fled to the Lord; let him save him, let him deliver him, since he desires him.

Hebrew Trans: For you are the one who drew me from the womb, making me secure upon my mother's breasts.
Septuagint Trans: For you are the one who drew me out of the womb, my hope from my mother's breasts.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For you are he who drew me out from the womb; my hope from the breasts of my mother.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: But you are my defender from the womb, my trust from my mother's breasts.

Hebrew Trans: Upon you I was cast from birth; from my mother's womb you have been my God.
Septuagint Trans: Upon you I was cast from the womb; from my mother's belly you are my God.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Upon you I was cast from the womb; from my mother's belly, you are my God.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Upon you I was cast from the womb; from my mother's belly you are my God.

Hebrew Trans: Be not far from me, for trouble is near, for there is none to help.
Septuagint Trans: Do not depart from me, for affliction is near, for there is none to help.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Depart not from me, for tribulation is near, for there is none to help.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Do not be far from me, for tribulation is near, for there is no helper.

Hebrew Trans: Many bulls have surrounded me; strong bulls of Bashan have encircled me.
Septuagint Trans: Many calves surrounded me; fat bulls enclosed me.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Many calves have surrounded me; fat bulls have besieged me.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Many calves have surrounded me; fat bulls have enclosed me.

Hebrew Trans: They open wide their mouths against me, like a lion tearing and roaring.
Septuagint Trans: They opened their mouth against me, like a lion seizing and roaring.
Gallican Psalter Trans: They opened their mouth against me, like a lion seizing and roaring.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: They opened their mouth against me, like a lion seizing and roaring.

Hebrew Trans: I am poured out like water, and all my bones are disjointed; my heart has become like wax, melting within my innards.
Septuagint Trans: I was poured out like water, and all my bones were scattered; my heart became like wax melting in the midst of my belly.
Gallican Psalter Trans: I am poured out like water, and all my bones are scattered; my heart has become like wax melting in the midst of my belly.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: I am poured out like water, and all my bones are separated; my heart has become like wax melted in the midst of my belly.

Hebrew Trans: My strength is dried up like a potsherd, and my tongue clings to my jaws; and to the dust of death you lay me down.
Septuagint Trans: My strength was dried up like a potsherd, and my tongue has been glued to my throat, and you have brought me down to the dust of death.
Gallican Psalter Trans: My strength has dried up like a potsherd, and my tongue has clung to my jaws; and you have brought me down into the dust of death.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: My strength has dried up like a potsherd, and my tongue has clung to my palate, and you have dragged me down into the dust of death.

Hebrew Trans: For dogs have surrounded me; a company of evildoers has enclosed me; like a lion, my hands and my feet.
Septuagint Trans: For many dogs surrounded me; a congregation of evildoers enclosed me; they pierced my hands and my feet.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For many dogs have surrounded me; a council of the malignant has besieged me. They have pierced my hands and my feet.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Hunters have surrounded me; a council of the wicked has enclosed me; they have pierced my hands and my feet.

Hebrew Trans: I can count all my bones; they look, they stare at me.
Septuagint Trans: I counted all my bones; but they observed and looked upon me.
Gallican Psalter Trans: They have numbered all my bones; and they themselves have looked and stared upon me.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: I have numbered all my bones, which they themselves, looking on, have gazed upon me.

Hebrew Trans: They divide my garments among them, and for my clothing they cast lots.
Septuagint Trans: They divided my garments among themselves, and upon my clothing they cast a lot.
Gallican Psalter Trans: They divided my garments among them, and upon my vesture they cast lots.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: They divided my garments among themselves, and upon my vesture they cast lots.

Hebrew Trans: But you, O LORD, be not far off; O my strength, hasten to my aid.
Septuagint Trans: But you, O Lord, do not keep your help far off; attend to my defense.
Gallican Psalter Trans: But you, O Lord, do not remove your help far off; look to my defense.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: But you, O Lord, do not be far away; my strength, hasten to my aid.

Hebrew Trans: Deliver my soul from the sword, my only one from the power of the dog.
Septuagint Trans: Deliver my soul from the sword, and my only one from the hand of the dog.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Deliver my soul from the sword, and my only one from the hand of the dog.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Deliver my soul from the sword, my lonely one from the hand of the dog.

Hebrew Trans: Save me from the lion's mouth, and from the horns of wild oxen you have answered me.
Septuagint Trans: Save me from the mouth of the lion, and my lowliness from the horns of the unicorns.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Save me from the lion's mouth, and my lowliness from the horns of the unicorns.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Save me from the mouth of the lion, and from the horns of the unicorns hear me.

Hebrew Trans: I will declare your name to my brothers; in the midst of the assembly I will praise you.
Septuagint Trans: I will declare your name to my brothers; in the midst of the assembly I will hymn you.
Gallican Psalter Trans: I will declare your name to my brethren; in the midst of the assembly I will praise you.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: I will declare your name to my brothers; in the midst of the assembly I will praise you.

Hebrew Trans: You who fear the LORD, praise Him; all the seed of Jacob, glorify Him; and stand in awe of Him, all the seed of Israel.
Septuagint Trans: You who fear the Lord, praise him; all the seed of Jacob, glorify him; let all the seed of Israel fear him.
Gallican Psalter Trans: You who fear the Lord, praise him; all the seed of Jacob, magnify him; let all the seed of Israel fear him.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: You who fear the Lord, praise him; all the seed of Jacob, glorify him, and fear him, all the seed of Israel.

Hebrew Trans: For He has not despised nor abhorred the affliction of the afflicted, nor has He hidden His face from him; and when he cried to Him, He heard.
Septuagint Trans: For he did not despise nor abhor the supplication of the poor, nor did he turn his face from me, and when I cried to him he heard me.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For he has not despised nor disdained the supplication of the poor; nor has he turned his face from me; and when I cried to him, he heard me.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For he has not despised nor contemned the lowliness of the poor, nor has he hidden his face from him, and when he cried out to him, he heard.

Hebrew Trans: From you comes my praise in the great assembly; my vows I will pay before those who fear Him.
Septuagint Trans: From you comes my praise in the great assembly; I will pay my vows before those who fear him.
Gallican Psalter Trans: With you is my praise in the great assembly; I will pay my vows in the sight of those who fear him.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: From you comes my praise in the great assembly; I will pay my vows in the sight of those who fear him.

Hebrew Trans: The humble shall eat and be satisfied; those who seek Him shall praise the LORD; may your hearts live forever.
Septuagint Trans: The poor shall eat and be satisfied, and those who seek the Lord shall praise him; their hearts shall live forever and ever.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The poor shall eat and shall be satisfied, and they shall praise the Lord who seek him; their hearts shall live forever and ever.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The meek shall eat and be satisfied; those who seek him shall praise the Lord; your heart shall live forever.

Hebrew Trans: All the ends of the earth shall remember and turn to the LORD, and all the families of the nations shall bow down before you.
Septuagint Trans: All the ends of the earth shall remember and turn to the Lord, and all the families of the nations shall worship before you.
Gallican Psalter Trans: All the ends of the earth shall remember and shall be converted to the Lord, and all the families of the nations shall worship in his sight.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: All the ends of the earth shall remember and turn to the Lord, and all the kindreds of the nations shall worship before him.

Hebrew Trans: For the kingship belongs to the LORD, and He rules over the nations.
Septuagint Trans: For the kingdom is the Lord's, and he rules over the nations.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For the kingdom is the Lord's, and he shall have dominion over the nations.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For the kingdom is the Lord's, and he shall rule over the nations.

Hebrew Trans: All the fat ones of the earth shall eat and worship; before Him shall bow all who go down to the dust, and he who cannot keep his soul alive.
Septuagint Trans: All the fat ones of the earth ate and worshipped; before him shall fall down all who descend into the earth, and my soul lives for him.
Gallican Psalter Trans: All the fat ones of the earth have eaten and have worshiped; in his sight shall fall all who descend into the earth.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: All the fat ones of the earth have eaten and worshipped; before his face all who descend into the dust shall bend the knee.

Hebrew Trans: A seed shall serve Him; it shall be told of the Lord to the coming generation.
Septuagint Trans: And my seed shall serve him; the coming generation shall be announced to the Lord.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And my soul shall live for him, and my seed shall serve him.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And his soul shall live for him; his seed shall serve him.

Hebrew Trans: They shall come and declare His righteousness to a people yet to be born, that He has done it.
Septuagint Trans: And they shall announce his righteousness to a people that shall be born, that the Lord has done it.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The generation to come shall be announced to the Lord, and they shall announce his righteousness to the people that shall be born, whom the Lord has made.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: It shall be told of the Lord to a generation to come; and they shall announce his righteousness to a people that shall be born, which he has done.

Hebrew Trans: A psalm of David. The LORD is my shepherd; I shall not want.
Septuagint Trans: A psalm of David. The Lord shepherds me, and nothing shall I lack.
Gallican Psalter Trans: A psalm of David. The Lord will rule me, and nothing shall be lacking to me.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: A song of David. The Lord shepherds me; nothing shall I lack.

Hebrew Trans: He makes me lie down in green pastures; he leads me beside the still waters.
Septuagint Trans: In a place of green pasture, there he made me dwell; beside the water of rest he nourished me.
Gallican Psalter Trans: In a place of pasture, there he has placed me; beside the water of refreshment he has brought me up.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: In pastures of green grass he has laid me down; beside waters of refreshment he has nourished me.

Hebrew Trans: He restores my soul; he leads me in paths of righteousness for his name's sake.
Septuagint Trans: He restored my soul; he guided me upon paths of righteousness for the sake of his name.
Gallican Psalter Trans: He has converted my soul. He has led me upon the paths of justice, for the sake of his name.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: He has restored my soul; he has led me along the paths of justice for the sake of his name.

Hebrew Trans: Even though I walk through the valley of the shadow of death, I will fear no evil, for you are with me; your rod and your staff, they comfort me.
Septuagint Trans: For even if I walk in the midst of the shadow of death, I shall not fear evils, for you are with me; your rod and your staff, these have comforted me.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For even if I shall walk in the midst of the shadow of death, I will fear no evils, for you are with me. Your rod and your staff, they have consoled me.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: But even if I should walk in the valley of death, I shall not fear evil, for you are with me; your rod and your staff, they shall comfort me.

Hebrew Trans: You prepare a table before me in the presence of my enemies; you anoint my head with oil; my cup runs over.
Septuagint Trans: You prepared a table before me in the presence of those who afflict me; you anointed my head with oil, and your cup intoxicates like the finest.
Gallican Psalter Trans: You have prepared a table before me, against those who trouble me. You have anointed my head with oil; and my cup that inebriates, how excellent it is!
Hebrew Psalter Trans: You set before me a table in the presence of my enemies; you have anointed my head with oil; my cup is overflowing.

Hebrew Trans: Surely goodness and mercy shall follow me all the days of my life, and I shall dwell in the house of the LORD for length of days.
Septuagint Trans: And your mercy shall pursue me all the days of my life, and my dwelling shall be in the house of the Lord for length of days.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And your mercy shall follow me all the days of my life, and that I may dwell in the house of the Lord for the length of days.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: But also kindness and mercy shall follow me all the days of my life, and I shall dwell in the house of the Lord for the length of days.

Hebrew Trans: Of David, a psalm. To the LORD belongs the earth and its fullness, the world and those who dwell in it.
Septuagint Trans: A psalm of David, for the first day of the week. The earth is the Lord's, and its fullness; the inhabited world and all who dwell in it.
Gallican Psalter Trans: A psalm of David, on the first day of the week. The earth is the Lord's, and the fullness thereof: the world of lands, and all who dwell in it.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Of David. A song. The Lord's is the earth and its fullness, the world and those who dwell in it.

Hebrew Trans: For He founded it upon the seas, and upon the rivers He established it.
Septuagint Trans: He founded it upon the seas and established it upon the rivers.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For He Himself founded it upon the seas, and prepared it upon the rivers.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For He Himself founded it upon the seas, and established it upon the rivers.

Hebrew Trans: Who shall ascend the hill of the LORD, and who shall stand in His holy place?
Septuagint Trans: Who shall ascend to the mountain of the Lord, and who shall stand in his holy place?
Gallican Psalter Trans: Who shall ascend into the mountain of the Lord? Or who shall stand in His holy place?
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Who shall ascend the mountain of the Lord, and who shall stand in His holy place?

Hebrew Trans: He who has clean hands and a pure heart, who has not lifted up his soul to falsehood, and has not sworn deceitfully.
Septuagint Trans: The one innocent in hands and pure in heart, who has not taken his soul upon vanity and has not sworn deceitfully to his neighbor.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The innocent in hands and clean of heart, who has not received his soul in vain, nor sworn in deceit to his neighbor.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The one innocent in hands and clean of heart, who has not lifted up his soul in vain, and has not sworn deceitfully.

Hebrew Trans: He shall receive a blessing from the LORD, and righteousness from the God of his salvation.
Septuagint Trans: This one shall receive a blessing from the Lord and mercy from God his savior.
Gallican Psalter Trans: He shall receive a blessing from the Lord, and mercy from God his savior.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: He shall receive a blessing from the Lord, and righteousness from God his salvation.

Hebrew Trans: This is the generation of those who seek Him, who seek Your face, O Jacob. Selah.
Septuagint Trans: This is the generation of those who seek him, who seek the face of the God of Jacob. Selah.
Gallican Psalter Trans: This is the generation of those who seek Him, of those who seek the face of the God of Jacob. Diapsalma.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: This is the generation of those who seek Him, of those who seek your face, O Jacob, forever.

Hebrew Trans: Lift up your heads, O gates, and be lifted up, O everlasting doors, that the King of glory may come in.
Septuagint Trans: Lift up your gates, you rulers, and be lifted up, you everlasting gates, and the King of glory shall enter in.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Lift up your gates, O princes, and be lifted up, you eternal gates, and the King of Glory shall enter in.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Lift up your heads, O gates, and be raised up, O everlasting doors, and the king of glory shall enter in.

Hebrew Trans: Who is this King of glory? The LORD, strong and mighty, the LORD mighty in battle.
Septuagint Trans: Who is this King of glory? The Lord strong and mighty, the Lord mighty in battle.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Who is this King of Glory? The Lord, strong and mighty; the Lord, mighty in battle.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Who is this king of glory? The Lord, strong and mighty, the Lord mighty in battle.

Hebrew Trans: Lift up your heads, O gates, and lift up, O everlasting doors, that the King of glory may come in.
Septuagint Trans: Lift up your gates, you rulers, and be lifted up, you everlasting gates, and the King of glory shall enter in.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Lift up your gates, O princes, and be lifted up, you eternal gates, and the King of Glory shall enter in.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Lift up your heads, O gates, and rise up, O everlasting doors, and the king of glory shall enter in.

Hebrew Trans: Who is He, this King of glory? The LORD of hosts, He is the King of glory. Selah.
Septuagint Trans: Who is this King of glory? The Lord of hosts, he is the King of glory.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Who is this King of Glory? The Lord of hosts, He Himself is the King of Glory. Diapsalma.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Who is this king of glory? The Lord of hosts, He is the king of glory, forever.

Hebrew Trans: Of David. To you, O LORD, I lift up my soul.
Septuagint Trans: A psalm of David. To you, O Lord, I have lifted up my soul, O my God.
Gallican Psalter Trans: A psalm of David. To you, O Lord, I have lifted up my soul.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Of David. To you, O Lord, I will lift up my soul.

Hebrew Trans: My God, in you I have trusted; let me not be put to shame; let not my enemies exult over me.
Septuagint Trans: In you I have trusted; let me not be put to shame, nor let my enemies laugh at me.
Gallican Psalter Trans: My God, in you I trust; let me not be put to shame, nor let my enemies mock me.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: My God, in you I have trusted; let me not be put to shame; let not my enemies rejoice over me.

Hebrew Trans: Indeed, none who wait for you shall be put to shame; let those who deal treacherously be ashamed empty-handed.
Septuagint Trans: For indeed, all who wait upon you shall not be put to shame; let all who act lawlessly without cause be ashamed.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For indeed, all who wait for you shall not be confounded; let all who act wickedly without cause be confounded.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: But also let all who hope in you not be confounded; let those who do wickedness in vain be confounded.

Hebrew Trans: Your ways, O LORD, make known to me; your paths, teach me.
Septuagint Trans: Make your ways known to me, O Lord, and teach me your paths.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Make known your ways to me, O Lord, and teach me your paths.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Your ways, O Lord, show to me; your paths, teach me.

Hebrew Trans: Lead me in your truth and teach me, for you are the God of my salvation; for you I have waited all the day.
Septuagint Trans: Guide me in your truth and teach me, for you are the God of my salvation, and I have waited for you all the day long.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Direct me in your truth and teach me, for you are the God of my salvation, and I have waited for you all the day long.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Lead me in your truth and teach me, for you are God my savior; I have waited for you the whole day.

Hebrew Trans: Remember your mercies, O LORD, and your lovingkindnesses, for they are from of old.
Septuagint Trans: Remember your compassions, O Lord, and your mercies, for they are from of old.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Remember your compassions, O Lord, and your mercies, for they are from of old.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Remember your compassions, O Lord, and your mercies, for they are from everlasting.

Hebrew Trans: The sins of my youth and my transgressions, do not remember; according to your lovingkindness, remember me — you — for the sake of your goodness, O LORD.
Septuagint Trans: Do not remember the sins of my youth and my ignorance; according to your mercy remember me, you yourself, for the sake of your goodness, O Lord.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The sins of my youth and my ignorances do not remember; according to your mercy remember me, you, for the sake of your goodness, O Lord.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The sins of my youth and my transgressions, do not remember; according to your mercy, remember me, for the sake of your goodness, O Lord.

Hebrew Trans: Good and upright is the LORD; therefore he instructs sinners in the way.
Septuagint Trans: Good and upright is the Lord; therefore he will instruct sinners in the way.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The Lord is sweet and upright; therefore he will give a law to those who go astray in the way.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Good and upright is the Lord; therefore he will teach sinners in the way.

Hebrew Trans: He guides the humble in justice, and teaches the humble his way.
Septuagint Trans: He will guide the meek in judgment; he will teach the meek his ways.
Gallican Psalter Trans: He will direct the meek in judgment; he will teach the gentle his ways.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: He will guide the meek in judgment, and he will teach the humble his way.

Hebrew Trans: All the paths of the LORD are lovingkindness and truth, to those who keep his covenant and his testimonies.
Septuagint Trans: All the ways of the Lord are mercy and truth to those who seek his covenant and his testimonies.
Gallican Psalter Trans: All the ways of the Lord are mercy and truth, to those who seek his covenant and his testimonies.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: All the paths of the Lord are mercy and truth to those who keep his covenant and his testimony.

Hebrew Trans: For the sake of your name, O LORD, pardon my iniquity, for it is great.
Septuagint Trans: For the sake of your name, O Lord, forgive my sin, for it is great.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For the sake of your name, O Lord, you will be merciful to my sin, for it is great.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For the sake of your name, be merciful to my iniquity, for it is great.

Hebrew Trans: Who is the man that fears the LORD? He will instruct him in the way he should choose.
Septuagint Trans: Who is the man that fears the Lord? He will instruct him in the way that he has chosen.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Who is the man that fears the Lord? He will establish for him a law in the way that he has chosen.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Who is this man that fears the Lord? He will teach him in the way that he has chosen.

Hebrew Trans: His soul shall dwell in goodness, and his seed shall inherit the land.
Septuagint Trans: His soul shall dwell in good things, and his seed shall inherit the land.
Gallican Psalter Trans: His soul shall dwell in good things, and his seed shall inherit the earth.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: His soul shall dwell in goodness, and his seed shall inherit the earth.

Hebrew Trans: The counsel of the LORD is for those who fear him, and his covenant, to make it known to them.
Septuagint Trans: The Lord is a stronghold of those who fear him, and the name of the Lord belongs to those who fear him, and his covenant is to make it known to them.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The Lord is a stronghold to those who fear him, and his covenant is that it may be made known to them.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The secret of the Lord is for those who fear him, and his covenant he will show to them.

Hebrew Trans: My eyes are continually toward the LORD, for he will bring my feet out of the net.
Septuagint Trans: My eyes are continually toward the Lord, for he shall draw my feet out of the snare.
Gallican Psalter Trans: My eyes are ever toward the Lord, for he will pluck my feet out of the snare.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: My eyes are always toward the Lord, for he will bring my feet out of the net.

Hebrew Trans: Turn to me and be gracious to me, for I am lonely and afflicted.
Septuagint Trans: Look upon me and have mercy on me, for I am alone and poor.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Look upon me and have mercy on me, for I am alone and poor.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Look upon me and have mercy on me, for I am alone and poor.

Hebrew Trans: The troubles of my heart are enlarged; bring me out of my distresses.
Septuagint Trans: The afflictions of my heart have been enlarged; bring me out of my distresses.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The troubles of my heart are multiplied; from my necessities deliver me.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The tribulations of my heart have been multiplied; from my distresses bring me out.

Hebrew Trans: Look upon my affliction and my toil, and forgive all my sins.
Septuagint Trans: Look upon my humiliation and my toil, and forgive all my sins.
Gallican Psalter Trans: See my humility and my toil, and forgive all my sins.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: See my affliction and my toil, and bear away all my sins.

Hebrew Trans: Look upon my enemies, for they are many, and with violent hatred they hate me.
Septuagint Trans: Look upon my enemies, for they have multiplied, and with unjust hatred they have hated me.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Look upon my enemies, for they are multiplied, and they have hated me with unjust hatred.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: See my enemies, for they have been multiplied, and with unjust hatred they have hated me.

Hebrew Trans: Guard my soul and deliver me; let me not be put to shame, for I take refuge in you.
Septuagint Trans: Guard my soul and deliver me; let me not be put to shame, for I have hoped in you.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Guard my soul and deliver me; let me not be put to shame, for I have hoped in you.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Guard my soul and deliver me; let me not be put to shame, for I have hoped in you.
