Hebrew Trans: Let integrity and uprightness preserve me, for I wait for you.
Septuagint Trans: The innocent and the upright clung to me, for I waited for you, O Lord.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The innocent and the upright have clung to me, because I have waited for you.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Simplicity and uprightness shall preserve me, for I have waited for you.

Hebrew Trans: Redeem, O God, Israel out of all his troubles.
Septuagint Trans: Redeem, O God, Israel out of all his afflictions.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Deliver, O God, Israel from all his tribulations.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Redeem, O God, Israel from all his distresses.

Hebrew Trans: Of David. Judge me, O LORD, for I have walked in my integrity, and in the LORD I have trusted; I shall not slip.
Septuagint Trans: Of David. Judge me, O Lord, for I have walked in my innocence, and hoping in the Lord I shall not weaken.
Gallican Psalter Trans: A psalm of David. Judge me, O Lord, for I have walked in my innocence: and trusting in the Lord, I shall not be weakened.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Of David. Judge me, O God, for I have walked in my simplicity, and trusting in the Lord I shall not falter.

Hebrew Trans: Examine me, O LORD, and test me; refine my kidneys and my heart.
Septuagint Trans: Examine me, O Lord, and test me; refine my kidneys and my heart.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Prove me, O Lord, and test me; burn my kidneys and my heart.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Prove me, O Lord, and test me; try by fire my kidneys and my heart.

Hebrew Trans: For your lovingkindness is before my eyes, and I have walked in your truth.
Septuagint Trans: For your mercy is before my eyes, and I have been well-pleased in your truth.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For your mercy is before my eyes, and I have been well pleased in your truth.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For your mercy is before the sight of my eyes, and I will walk in your truth.

Hebrew Trans: I have not sat with men of falsehood, and with the concealed I will not come.
Septuagint Trans: I have not sat with the council of vanity, and I shall not enter in with those who transgress the law.
Gallican Psalter Trans: I have not sat with the council of vanity, and I will not enter in with those who do wicked things.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: I have not sat with vain men, and I will not enter in with the proud.

Hebrew Trans: I have hated the assembly of evildoers, and with the wicked I will not sit.
Septuagint Trans: I have hated the assembly of evildoers, and I shall not sit with the ungodly.
Gallican Psalter Trans: I have hated the assembly of the malignant, and with the impious I will not sit.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: I have hated the assembly of the wicked, and I will not sit with the iniquitous.

Hebrew Trans: I will wash my hands in innocence, and I will go around your altar, O LORD,
Septuagint Trans: I will wash my hands among the innocent and I will go around your altar, O Lord,
Gallican Psalter Trans: I will wash my hands among the innocent, and I will surround your altar, O Lord.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: I will wash my hands in innocence, and I will go around your altar, O Lord.

Hebrew Trans: to make heard the voice of thanksgiving, and to tell of all your wondrous works.
Septuagint Trans: to hear the voice of praise and to declare all your wonders.
Gallican Psalter Trans: That I may hear the voice of praise, and tell of all your wondrous works.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: That I may proclaim praise with a clear voice, and tell of all your wondrous works.

Hebrew Trans: O LORD, I have loved the habitation of your house, and the place where your glory dwells.
Septuagint Trans: O Lord, I have loved the beauty of your house and the place of the dwelling of your glory.
Gallican Psalter Trans: O Lord, I have loved the beauty of your house, and the place where your glory dwells.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: O Lord, I have loved the dwelling place of your house, and the place of the tabernacle of your glory.

Hebrew Trans: Do not gather my soul with sinners, nor my life with men of blood,
Septuagint Trans: Do not destroy my soul together with the ungodly, nor my life with men of blood,
Gallican Psalter Trans: Do not destroy my soul with the impious, nor my life with men of blood,
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Do not take away my soul with sinners, nor my life with men of blood.

Hebrew Trans: in whose hands is wickedness, and whose right hand is full of bribes.
Septuagint Trans: in whose hands are iniquities; their right hand is filled with bribes.
Gallican Psalter Trans: In whose hands are iniquities; their right hand is filled with bribes.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: In whose hands is wickedness, and whose right hand is filled with bribes.

Hebrew Trans: But as for me, I will walk in my integrity; redeem me and be gracious to me.
Septuagint Trans: But I have walked in my innocence; redeem me and have mercy on me.
Gallican Psalter Trans: But I have walked in my innocence: redeem me and have mercy on me.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: But I will walk in my simplicity; redeem me and have mercy on me.

Hebrew Trans: My foot stands on level ground; in the assemblies I will bless the LORD.
Septuagint Trans: For my foot has stood in uprightness; in the assemblies I will bless you, O Lord.
Gallican Psalter Trans: My foot has stood on the straight path: in the assemblies I will bless you, O Lord.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: My foot has stood on level ground; in the assemblies I will bless the Lord.

Hebrew Trans: Of David. The LORD is my light and my salvation; whom shall I fear? The LORD is the stronghold of my life; of whom shall I be afraid?
Septuagint Trans: Of David, before he was anointed. The Lord is my light and my savior; whom shall I fear? The Lord is the defender of my life; of whom shall I be afraid?
Gallican Psalter Trans: Of David, before he was anointed. The Lord is my illumination and my salvation; whom shall I fear? The Lord is the protector of my life; of whom shall I tremble?
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Of David. The Lord is my light and my salvation; whom shall I fear? The Lord is the strength of my life; whom shall I dread?

Hebrew Trans: When evildoers drew near against me to devour my flesh, my adversaries and my enemies against me, they stumbled and fell.
Septuagint Trans: When those who do evil drew near against me to devour my flesh, those who afflict me and my enemies—they themselves grew weak and fell.
Gallican Psalter Trans: While the wicked draw near against me, to eat my flesh, those who trouble me and my enemies, they themselves were weakened and fell.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: When the wicked drew near to me to devour my flesh, my enemies and my foes, they themselves stumbled and fell.

Hebrew Trans: If an army should encamp against me, my heart shall not fear; if war should rise against me, in this I trust.
Septuagint Trans: If an army should encamp against me, my heart shall not fear; if war should rise up against me, in this I hope.
Gallican Psalter Trans: If armies should stand against me, my heart shall not fear; if battle should rise against me, in this I will hope.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: If armies should stand against me, my heart shall not fear; if war should rise against me, in this I shall be confident.

Hebrew Trans: One thing I have asked of the LORD, that I shall seek: that I may dwell in the house of the LORD all the days of my life, to behold the beauty of the LORD and to inquire in his temple.
Septuagint Trans: One thing I asked of the Lord, this I will seek: that I may dwell in the house of the Lord all the days of my life, to behold the delight of the Lord and to visit his temple.
Gallican Psalter Trans: One thing I have asked of the Lord, this I will seek: that I may dwell in the house of the Lord all the days of my life, that I may see the will of the Lord and visit his temple.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: One thing I have asked of the Lord, this I shall seek: that I may dwell in the house of the Lord all the days of my life, that I may behold the beauty of the Lord and attend to his temple.

Hebrew Trans: For he will hide me in his shelter in the day of trouble; he will conceal me in the secret of his tent; upon a rock he will lift me up.
Septuagint Trans: For he hid me in his tent in the day of my troubles; he sheltered me in the secret place of his tabernacle; upon a rock he raised me up.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For he has hidden me in his tabernacle; in the day of evils he protected me in the secret place of his tabernacle; upon a rock he exalted me.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For he will hide me in his shadow in the evil day; he will conceal me in the secret of his tabernacle; upon a rock he will exalt me. Now

Hebrew Trans: And now my head shall be lifted up above my enemies round about me, and I will offer in his tent sacrifices of shouting; I will sing and make music to the LORD.
Septuagint Trans: And now, behold, he has lifted up my head above my enemies. I went around and offered in his tabernacle a sacrifice of shouting; I will sing and make music to the Lord.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And now he has exalted my head above my enemies. I went about and offered in his tabernacle a sacrifice of shouting; I will sing and I will speak a psalm to the Lord.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: also he will lift up my head above my enemies who are round about me, and I will offer in his tabernacle sacrifices of jubilation; I will sing and make music to the Lord.

Hebrew Trans: Hear, O LORD, my voice when I call; be gracious to me and answer me.
Septuagint Trans: Hear, O Lord, my voice with which I cried out; have mercy on me and hear me.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Hear, O Lord, my voice with which I have cried; have mercy on me and hear me.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Hear, O Lord, my voice as I call; have mercy on me and hear me.

Hebrew Trans: To you my heart has said, "Seek my face." Your face, O LORD, I will seek.
Septuagint Trans: To you my heart said, "My face has sought you"; your face, O Lord, I will seek.
Gallican Psalter Trans: To you my heart has spoken; my face has sought you; your face, O Lord, I will seek.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: My heart said to you, my face has sought your face, O Lord, and I will seek it.

Hebrew Trans: Do not hide your face from me; do not turn your servant away in anger. You have been my help; do not abandon me and do not forsake me, O God of my salvation.
Septuagint Trans: Do not turn your face away from me; do not turn aside in anger from your servant. Be my helper; do not cast me off and do not forsake me, O God my savior.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Turn not your face from me; do not turn aside in anger from your servant. Be my helper; do not forsake me, nor despise me, O God my savior.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Do not hide your face from me; do not turn away in your anger from your servant. You have been my help; do not abandon me and do not forsake me, O God my savior.

Hebrew Trans: For my father and my mother have forsaken me, but the LORD will gather me up.
Septuagint Trans: For my father and my mother have forsaken me, but the Lord has taken me up.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For my father and my mother have forsaken me, but the Lord has taken me up.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For my father and my mother have forsaken me, but the Lord has gathered me up.

Hebrew Trans: Teach me, O LORD, your way, and lead me on a level path, because of my enemies.
Septuagint Trans: Teach me your way, O Lord, and guide me in a straight path, because of my enemies.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Set before me a law, O Lord, in your way, and direct me in the right path, because of my enemies.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Show me, O Lord, your way, and lead me on a straight path, because of those who lie in wait for me.

Hebrew Trans: Do not give me over to the desire of my adversaries, for false witnesses have risen against me, and they breathe out violence.
Septuagint Trans: Do not deliver me to the souls of those who afflict me, for unjust witnesses have risen up against me, and injustice has lied to itself.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Do not deliver me over to the souls of those who trouble me, for unjust witnesses have risen up against me, and iniquity has lied to itself.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Do not deliver me, O Lord, to the will of those who afflict me, for false witnesses have risen against me, and open falsehood.

Hebrew Trans: Had I not believed to see the goodness of the LORD in the land of the living—
Septuagint Trans: I believe that I shall see the good things of the Lord in the land of the living.
Gallican Psalter Trans: I believe I shall see the good things of the Lord in the land of the living.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: But I believe that I shall see the good things of the Lord in the land of the living.

Hebrew Trans: Wait for the LORD; be strong, and let your heart take courage; and wait for the LORD.
Septuagint Trans: Wait upon the Lord; be courageous, and let your heart be strengthened, and wait upon the Lord.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Wait for the Lord; act manfully, and let your heart be strengthened, and endure the Lord.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Wait for the Lord; be strong, and let your heart take courage, and wait for the Lord.

Hebrew Trans: Of David. To you, O LORD, I call; my rock, be not deaf to me, lest, if you be silent to me, I become like those who go down to the pit.
Septuagint Trans: Of David. To you, O Lord, I cried out; my God, do not be silent toward me, lest you be silent toward me and I become like those who go down into the pit.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Of David. To you, O Lord, I will cry out. My God, be not silent from me; lest if you be silent from me, I become like those who descend into the pit.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Of David. To you, O Lord, I will cry out. My Strength, do not be deaf to me, lest, if you be silent to me, I be compared to those who descend into the pit.

Hebrew Trans: Hear the voice of my supplications when I cry to you, when I lift up my hands toward the inner sanctuary of your holiness.
Septuagint Trans: Hear the voice of my supplication when I pray to you, when I lift up my hands toward your holy temple.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Hear the voice of my supplication, while I pray to you, while I lift up my hands to your holy temple.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Hear, O Lord, my supplication, when I cry out to you, when I lift up my hands to your holy oracle.

Hebrew Trans: Do not drag me away with the wicked and with the workers of iniquity, who speak peace with their neighbors while evil is in their hearts.
Septuagint Trans: Do not drag my soul away with sinners, and do not destroy me with workers of iniquity, who speak peace with their neighbors but have evil in their hearts.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Do not drag me away together with sinners, and do not destroy me with those who work iniquity, who speak peace with their neighbor, but evil is in their hearts.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Do not drag me away with the wicked and with the workers of iniquity, who speak peace with their friends, but evil is in their hearts.

Hebrew Trans: Give to them according to their deeds and according to the evil of their doings; according to the work of their hands give to them; repay to them their recompense.
Septuagint Trans: Give to them according to their works and according to the wickedness of their deeds; according to the works of their hands give to them; repay to them their recompense.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Give to them according to their works, and according to the wickedness of their devices; according to the works of their hands give to them; render their recompense to them.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Give to them according to their work and according to the wickedness of their inventions; according to the work of their hands give to them; render to them their recompense.

Hebrew Trans: For they do not regard the works of the LORD nor the deeds of his hands; he will tear them down and not build them up.
Septuagint Trans: For they have not understood the works of the Lord and the works of his hands; you will tear them down and will not build them up.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Because they have not understood the works of the Lord, and the works of his hands: you shall destroy them, and shall not build them up.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Because they do not understand the works of the Lord and the work of his hands, you will destroy them and not build them up.

Hebrew Trans: Blessed be the LORD, for he has heard the voice of my supplications.
Septuagint Trans: Blessed be the Lord, for he has heard the voice of my supplication.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Blessed be the Lord, for he has heard the voice of my supplication.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Blessed be the Lord, who has heard the voice of my supplication.

Hebrew Trans: The LORD is my strength and my shield; in him my heart trusted, and I was helped; and my heart exults, and with my song I will praise him.
Septuagint Trans: The Lord is my helper and my defender; in him my heart has hoped, and I was helped, and my flesh has flourished again, and of my own will I shall praise him.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The Lord is my helper and my protector; in him my heart has hoped, and I have been helped, and my flesh has flourished again; and of my own will I shall give praise to him.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The Lord is my strength and my shield; in him my heart has trusted, and I have been helped; my heart has rejoiced, and in my song I will praise him.

Hebrew Trans: The LORD is strength to them, and he is the stronghold of salvation for his anointed.
Septuagint Trans: The Lord is the strength of his people and the defender of the salvation of his anointed one.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The Lord is the strength of his people, and the protector of the salvation of his anointed one.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The Lord is my strength and the stronghold of the salvation of his anointed one.

Hebrew Trans: Save your people and bless your inheritance; shepherd them and carry them forever.
Septuagint Trans: Save your people and bless your inheritance, and shepherd them and lift them up forever.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Save your people, and bless your inheritance; and rule them, and lift them up forever.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Save your people and bless your inheritance, and shepherd them and lift them up forever.

Hebrew Trans: A psalm of David. Ascribe to the LORD, O sons of the mighty, ascribe to the LORD glory and strength.
Septuagint Trans: A psalm of David, at the departure from the tabernacle. Bring to the Lord, O sons of God, bring to the Lord the sons of rams; bring to the Lord glory and honor.
Gallican Psalter Trans: A psalm of David, at the completion of the tabernacle. Bring to the Lord, O sons of God, bring to the Lord the sons of rams.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: A song of David. Bring to the Lord the sons of rams.

Hebrew Trans: Ascribe to the LORD the glory of His name; worship the LORD in the splendor of holiness.
Septuagint Trans: Bring to the Lord the glory of his name; worship the Lord in his holy court.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Bring to the Lord glory and honor; bring to the Lord glory to His name; worship the Lord in His holy court.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Bring to the Lord glory and power; bring to the Lord the glory of his name; worship the Lord in holy beauty.

Hebrew Trans: The voice of the LORD is upon the waters; the God of glory thunders; the LORD is upon many waters.
Septuagint Trans: The voice of the Lord is upon the waters; the God of glory has thundered; the Lord is upon many waters.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The voice of the Lord is upon the waters; the God of majesty has thundered; the Lord is upon many waters.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The voice of the Lord is upon the waters; the God of glory has thundered; the Lord is upon many waters.

Hebrew Trans: The voice of the LORD is in power; the voice of the LORD is in majesty.
Septuagint Trans: The voice of the Lord is in strength; the voice of the Lord is in majesty.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The voice of the Lord is in power; the voice of the Lord is in magnificence.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The voice of the Lord is in power; the voice of the Lord is in beauty.

Hebrew Trans: The voice of the LORD breaks the cedars; and the LORD breaks the cedars of Lebanon.
Septuagint Trans: The voice of the Lord breaks the cedars; and the Lord shall shatter the cedars of Lebanon.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The voice of the Lord breaking the cedars; and the Lord shall break the cedars of Lebanon.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The voice of the Lord breaking the cedars; and the Lord shall break the cedars of Lebanon.

Hebrew Trans: And He makes them skip like a calf, Lebanon and Sirion like a young wild ox.
Septuagint Trans: And he shall make them thin as a calf—Lebanon itself—and the beloved one as a son of unicorns.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And He shall shatter them like a calf of Lebanon, and the beloved one like the son of unicorns.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And he shall scatter them like a calf of Lebanon, and Sarion like the son of a rhinoceros.

Hebrew Trans: The voice of the LORD hews out flames of fire.
Septuagint Trans: The voice of the Lord cuts through a flame of fire.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The voice of the Lord striking through the flame of fire.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The voice of the Lord dividing the flames of fire.

Hebrew Trans: The voice of the LORD shakes the wilderness; the LORD shakes the wilderness of Kadesh.
Septuagint Trans: The voice of the Lord shakes the wilderness; and the Lord shall shake the wilderness of Kadesh.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The voice of the Lord shaking the desert; and the Lord shall shake the desert of Kadesh.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The voice of the Lord making the desert of Cades to bring forth.

Hebrew Trans: The voice of the LORD makes the deer to calve and strips the forests bare; and in His temple all of it says, "Glory!"
Septuagint Trans: The voice of the Lord prepares the deer, and shall uncover the thickets; and in his temple everyone speaks of glory.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The voice of the Lord preparing the deer, and He shall reveal the thick places; and in His temple all shall speak of His glory.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The voice of the Lord acting as midwife to the deer, and revealing the thickets; and in his temple all shall speak of his glory.

Hebrew Trans: The LORD sat enthroned at the flood; and the LORD sits as king forever.
Septuagint Trans: The Lord shall inhabit the flood; and the Lord shall sit as king forever.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The Lord makes the flood to be inhabited, and the Lord shall sit as king forever.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The Lord inhabits the flood, and the Lord shall sit as king forever.

Hebrew Trans: The LORD will give strength to His people; the LORD will bless His people with peace.
Septuagint Trans: The Lord shall give strength to his people; the Lord shall bless his people with peace.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The Lord will give strength to His people; the Lord will bless His people in peace.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The Lord will give strength to his people; the Lord will bless his people in peace.

Hebrew Trans: A psalm, a song for the dedication of the house, of David.
Septuagint Trans: For the end, a psalm of a song of the dedication of the house, to David.
Gallican Psalter Trans: A psalm of a canticle at the dedication of the house of David.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: A psalm of a canticle for the dedication of the house of David.

Hebrew Trans: I will exalt you, O LORD, for you have drawn me up, and have not let my enemies rejoice over me.
Septuagint Trans: I will exalt you, O Lord, for you have lifted me up, and have not let my enemies rejoice over me.
Gallican Psalter Trans: I will extol you, O Lord, for you have lifted me up, and you have not let my enemies rejoice over me.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: I will exalt you, O Lord, for you have saved me, and you have not delighted my enemies over me.

Hebrew Trans: O LORD my God, I cried out to you, and you healed me.
Septuagint Trans: O Lord my God, I cried to you, and you healed me.
Gallican Psalter Trans: O Lord my God, I cried to you, and you healed me.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: O Lord my God, I cried to you, and you healed me.

Hebrew Trans: O LORD, you brought up my soul from Sheol; you kept me alive from among those who go down to the pit.
Septuagint Trans: O Lord, you brought up my soul from Hades; you saved me from those who go down into the pit.
Gallican Psalter Trans: O Lord, you have brought up my soul from hell; you have saved me from those descending into the pit.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: O Lord, you have brought up my soul from hell; you have given me life, lest I descend into the pit.

Hebrew Trans: Sing praise to the LORD, O you his faithful ones, and give thanks to the remembrance of his holiness.
Septuagint Trans: Sing to the Lord, you his holy ones, and give thanks at the remembrance of his holiness.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Sing to the Lord, you his saints, and give praise to the memory of his holiness.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Sing to the Lord, you his saints, and give praise to the memory of his holiness.

Hebrew Trans: For his anger is but for a moment; life is in his favor; weeping may lodge in the evening, but joy comes in the morning.
Septuagint Trans: For there is wrath in his anger, and life in his will; weeping shall lodge in the evening, and joy comes in the morning.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For wrath is in his indignation, and life in his good will: in the evening weeping shall linger, and in the morning gladness.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For wrath is in his indignation, and life in his good will: in the evening weeping shall linger, and in the morning, praise.

Hebrew Trans: And I said in my prosperity, I shall never be moved.
Septuagint Trans: And I said in my prosperity, I shall never be moved forever.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And I said in my abundance: I shall never be moved.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: But I said in my abundance: I shall never be moved.

Hebrew Trans: O LORD, by your favor you made my mountain stand strong; you hid your face, and I was dismayed.
Septuagint Trans: O Lord, in your will you granted strength to my beauty; but you turned away your face, and I became troubled.
Gallican Psalter Trans: O Lord, in your good will you gave strength to my beauty. You turned your face from me, and I became troubled.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: O Lord, in your good will you gave strength to my mountain; you hid your face, and I became troubled.

Hebrew Trans: To you, O LORD, I called, and to my Lord I made supplication.
Septuagint Trans: To you, O Lord, I will cry, and to my God I will make supplication.
Gallican Psalter Trans: To you, O Lord, I will cry, and to my God I will make supplication.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: To you, O Lord, I will cry, and to the Lord I will make supplication.

Hebrew Trans: What profit is there in my blood, in my going down to the pit? Shall the dust praise you? Shall it declare your truth?
Septuagint Trans: What profit is there in my blood, when I go down to corruption? Shall the dust confess to you? Or shall it declare your truth?
Gallican Psalter Trans: What profit is there in my blood, while I descend into corruption? Shall the dust confess to you, or shall it declare your truth?
Hebrew Psalter Trans: What profit is there in my blood, when I shall descend into corruption? Shall the dust confess to you, or shall it declare your truth?

Hebrew Trans: Hear, O LORD, and be gracious to me; O LORD, be a helper to me.
Septuagint Trans: The Lord heard and had mercy on me; the Lord became my helper.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The Lord heard and had mercy on me; the Lord became my helper.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Hear, O Lord, and have mercy on me; O Lord, be my helper.

Hebrew Trans: You have turned my mourning into dancing for me; you have loosened my sackcloth and girded me with gladness,
Septuagint Trans: You turned my mourning into dancing for me; you tore off my sackcloth and girded me with gladness,
Gallican Psalter Trans: You have turned my mourning into joy for me; you have torn my sackcloth and surrounded me with gladness.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: You have turned my mourning into dancing for me; you have loosed my sackcloth and girded me with gladness.

Hebrew Trans: So that my glory may sing praise to you and not be silent. O LORD my God, I will give thanks to you forever.
Septuagint Trans: So that my glory may sing to you and I may not be pierced with sorrow. O Lord my God, I will give thanks to you forever.
Gallican Psalter Trans: So that my glory may sing to you and I may not be pierced with sorrow. O Lord my God, I will give thanks to you forever.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: So that my glory may sing praise to you and not be silent: O Lord my God, I will confess to you forever.

Hebrew Trans: For the choirmaster, a psalm of David.
Septuagint Trans: To the end, a psalm of David, of ecstasy.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Unto the end, a psalm of David.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: To the victor, a song of David.

Hebrew Trans: In you, O LORD, I have taken refuge; let me never be put to shame; in your righteousness deliver me.
Septuagint Trans: In you, O Lord, I have hoped; let me never be put to shame. In your righteousness deliver me and rescue me.
Gallican Psalter Trans: In you, O Lord, I have hoped; let me never be confounded: in your justice deliver me.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: In you, O Lord, I have hoped; let me not be confounded forever; in your justice, save me.

Hebrew Trans: Incline your ear to me; rescue me speedily; be to me a rock of strength, a house of fortresses, to save me.
Septuagint Trans: Incline your ear to me; hasten to rescue me. Be to me a God who shields, and a house of refuge, to save me.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Incline your ear to me; hasten to rescue me. Be to me a God, a protector, and a house of refuge, that you may save me.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Incline your ear to me; swiftly deliver me. Be to me a stone most strong and a fortified house, that you may save me.

Hebrew Trans: For you are my rock and my fortress, and for your name's sake you will lead me and guide me.
Septuagint Trans: For you are my strength and my refuge, and for the sake of your name you will guide me and nourish me.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For you are my strength and my refuge; and for your name's sake you will lead me and nourish me.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For you are my rock and my fortress, and for your name's sake you will be my guide and will nourish me.

Hebrew Trans: You will bring me out of the net that they have secretly laid for me, for you are my stronghold.
Septuagint Trans: You will bring me out of this snare which they have hidden for me, for you are my protector.
Gallican Psalter Trans: You will bring me out of this snare which they have hidden for me, for you are my protector.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: You will lead me out of the net which they have hidden for me, for you are my strength.

Hebrew Trans: Into your hand I commit my spirit; you have redeemed me, O LORD, God of truth.
Septuagint Trans: Into your hands I will commit my spirit; you have redeemed me, O Lord, the God of truth.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Into your hands I will commend my spirit: you have redeemed me, O Lord, God of truth.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Into your hand I will commend my spirit; you have redeemed me, O Lord, God of truth.

Hebrew Trans: I hate those who cling to worthless vanities, but I trust in the LORD.
Septuagint Trans: You have hated those who cling to empty vanities, but I have hoped in the Lord.
Gallican Psalter Trans: You have hated those who observe vain things to no purpose; but I have hoped in the Lord.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: You have hated those who guard vanities in vain; but I have trusted in the Lord.

Hebrew Trans: I will rejoice and be glad in your steadfast love, for you have seen my affliction; you have known the distresses of my soul.
Septuagint Trans: I will rejoice and be glad in your mercy, for you have looked upon my humiliation; you have saved my soul from distresses.
Gallican Psalter Trans: I will exult and rejoice in your mercy, for you have regarded my lowliness; you have saved my soul from distresses.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: I will exult and rejoice in your mercy, for you have seen my affliction; you have known the tribulations of my soul.

Hebrew Trans: And you have not delivered me into the hand of the enemy; you have set my feet in a broad place.
Septuagint Trans: And you did not shut me up into the hands of the enemy; you set my feet in a broad place.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Nor have you shut me up in the hands of the enemy; you have set my feet in a spacious place.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And you have not shut me up in the hands of the enemy; you have set my feet in a broad place.

Hebrew Trans: Be gracious to me, O LORD, for I am in distress; my eye wastes away from grief, my soul and my body also.
Septuagint Trans: Have mercy on me, O Lord, for I am afflicted; my eye is troubled with anger, my soul and my belly.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Have mercy on me, O Lord, for I am troubled: my eye is disturbed with anger, my soul and my belly.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Have mercy on me, O Lord, for I am troubled; my eye has grown dim with fury, my soul and my belly.

Hebrew Trans: For my life is consumed with sorrow, and my years with sighing; my strength fails because of my iniquity, and my bones waste away.
Septuagint Trans: For my life has failed in pain, and my years in groaning; my strength has weakened in poverty, and my bones are troubled.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For my life has failed in sorrow, and my years in groaning. My strength is weakened in poverty, and my bones are troubled.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For my life has been consumed in sorrow, and my years in groaning; my strength has been weakened in iniquity, and my bones have wasted away.

Hebrew Trans: Because of all my adversaries I have become a reproach, and greatly so to my neighbors, and a dread to my acquaintances; those who see me in the street flee from me.
Septuagint Trans: I have become a reproach beyond all my enemies, and exceedingly so to my neighbors, and a fear to my acquaintances; those who saw me outside fled from me.
Gallican Psalter Trans: I have become a reproach above all my enemies, and exceedingly so to my neighbors, and a fear to my acquaintances. Those who saw me fled away from me outside.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Among all my enemies I have become a reproach, and exceedingly so to my neighbors, and a fear to my acquaintances; those who saw me in the streets fled from me.

Hebrew Trans: I am forgotten like a dead man, out of mind; I have become like a broken vessel.
Septuagint Trans: I have been forgotten like a dead man from the heart; I have become like a vessel that is destroyed.
Gallican Psalter Trans: I have been given to oblivion, as one dead from the heart; I have become as a vessel that is destroyed.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: I have been handed over to oblivion, like one dead from the heart; I have become like a ruined vessel.

Hebrew Trans: For I have heard the whispering of many, terror on every side; as they conspired together against me, they schemed to take my life.
Septuagint Trans: For I have heard the blame of many dwelling round about; when they gathered together against me, they plotted to take my life.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For I have heard the blame of many dwelling round about; while they assembled together against me, they took counsel to take away my soul.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For I heard the reproach of many, a gathering round about, when they entered into counsel against me, and plotted to take away my life.

Hebrew Trans: But I trust in you, O LORD; I say, "You are my God."
Septuagint Trans: But I have hoped in you, O Lord; I said, "You are my God."
Gallican Psalter Trans: But I have hoped in you, O Lord; I said: You are my God.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: But I have hoped in you, O Lord; I said, "You are my God."

Hebrew Trans: My times are in your hand; deliver me from the hand of my enemies and from my pursuers.
Septuagint Trans: My times are in your hands; deliver me from the hand of my enemies and from those who pursue me.
Gallican Psalter Trans: My lots are in your hands: deliver me from the hand of my enemies, and from those who persecute me.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: In your hand are my times; deliver me from the hand of my enemies and from those who persecute me.

Hebrew Trans: Make your face shine upon your servant; save me in your steadfast love.
Septuagint Trans: Make your face shine upon your servant; save me in your mercy.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Make your face to shine upon your servant; save me in your mercy.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Show your face upon your servant; save me in your mercy.

Hebrew Trans: O LORD, let me not be put to shame, for I have called upon you; let the wicked be put to shame; let them be silenced in Sheol.
Septuagint Trans: O Lord, let me not be put to shame, for I have called upon you; let the ungodly be ashamed and brought down to Hades.
Gallican Psalter Trans: O Lord, let me not be confounded, for I have called upon you. Let the wicked be ashamed, and let them be led down to hell.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: O Lord, let me not be confounded, for I have called upon you; let the wicked be confounded; let them be silent in hell.

Hebrew Trans: Let lying lips be mute, which speak arrogantly against the righteous with pride and contempt.
Septuagint Trans: Let the deceitful lips be made speechless, which speak lawlessness against the righteous with pride and contempt.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Let deceitful lips be made mute, which speak iniquity against the just, in pride and in contempt.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Let the lips of falsehood be made mute, which speak against the just one old things in pride and contempt.

Hebrew Trans: How great is your goodness, which you have stored up for those who fear you, which you have wrought for those who take refuge in you, before the sons of men.
Septuagint Trans: How great is the abundance of your goodness, O Lord, which you have hidden for those who fear you, which you have wrought for those who hope in you before the sons of men.
Gallican Psalter Trans: How great is the multitude of your sweetness, O Lord, which you have hidden for those who fear you, which you have perfected for those who hope in you, in the sight of the sons of men.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: How great is your goodness, which you have hidden for those who fear you; you have wrought it for those who hope in you in the sight of the sons of men.

Hebrew Trans: You hide them in the secret of your presence from the conspiracies of man; you shelter them in a booth from the strife of tongues.
Septuagint Trans: You will hide them in the secret of your presence from the disturbance of men; you will shelter them in a tabernacle from the contradiction of tongues.
Gallican Psalter Trans: You will hide them in the secret of your face from the disturbance of men; you will protect them in your tabernacle from the contradiction of tongues.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: You will hide them in the protection of your countenance from the harshness of man; you will hide them in a shelter from the contradiction of tongues.

Hebrew Trans: Blessed be the LORD, for he has wondrously shown his steadfast love to me in a besieged city.
Septuagint Trans: Blessed be the Lord, for he has made his mercy wondrous in a fortified city.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Blessed be the Lord, for he has made his mercy wonderful to me in a fortified city.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Blessed be the Lord, who has made his mercy wondrous to me in a fortified city.

Hebrew Trans: And I said in my alarm, "I am cut off from before your eyes." But indeed you heard the voice of my supplications when I cried out to you.
Septuagint Trans: But I said in my ecstasy, "I am cast away from before your eyes." Therefore you heard the voice of my supplication when I cried out to you.
Gallican Psalter Trans: But I said in the excess of my mind: I am cast away from the sight of your eyes. Therefore you heard the voice of my prayer, when I cried out to you.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: But I said in my astonishment, "I am cast away from the sight of his eyes." Yet you heard the voice of my supplication when I cried out to you.

Hebrew Trans: Love the LORD, all you his faithful ones. The LORD preserves the faithful, and abundantly repays the one who acts in pride.
Septuagint Trans: Love the Lord, all you his holy ones, for the Lord seeks out truth, and repays those who act with exceeding pride.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Love the Lord, all you his saints, for the Lord seeks out truths, and repays abundantly those who act with pride.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Love the Lord, all you his saints; the Lord preserves the faithful, and repays abundantly those who work pride.

Hebrew Trans: Be strong, and let your heart take courage, all you who wait for the LORD.
Septuagint Trans: Be courageous, and let your heart be strengthened, all you who hope in the Lord.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Act manfully, and let your heart be strengthened, all you who hope in the Lord.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Be strong, and let your heart be strengthened, all you who wait for the Lord.

Hebrew Trans: Of David, a Maskil. Blessed is he whose transgression is forgiven, whose sin is covered.
Septuagint Trans: Of David; a psalm of understanding. Blessed are those whose lawless deeds have been forgiven and whose sins have been covered over.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Of David, a psalm of understanding. Blessed are those whose iniquities have been forgiven and whose sins have been covered.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Of David, a skillful psalm. Blessed is he whose iniquity is forgiven and whose sin is covered.

Hebrew Trans: Blessed is the man to whom the LORD does not impute iniquity, and in whose spirit there is no deceit.
Septuagint Trans: Blessed is the man against whom the Lord will by no means reckon sin, nor is there deceit in his mouth.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Blessed is the man to whom the Lord will not impute sin, nor is there deceit in his spirit.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Blessed is the man to whom the Lord will not impute iniquity, nor is there deceit in his spirit.

Hebrew Trans: When I kept silent, my bones wasted away through my groaning all the day.
Septuagint Trans: Because I kept silent, my bones grew old from my crying out all the day long.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Because I kept silent, my bones grew old, while I cried out all the day long.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Because I kept silent, my bones were worn away in my groaning all the day long.

Hebrew Trans: For day and night your hand was heavy upon me; my moisture was turned into the drought of summer. Selah.
Septuagint Trans: Because day and night your hand was heavy upon me; I was turned to misery when a thorn was driven in. Selah.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Because day and night your hand was heavy upon me, I was turned in my anguish while the thorn was fixed in me. Selah.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For day and night your hand was heavy upon me; I was turned in my misery while the thorn continually burned hot.

Hebrew Trans: I acknowledged my sin to you, and my iniquity I did not hide; I said, "I will confess my transgressions to the LORD," and you forgave the iniquity of my sin. Selah.
Septuagint Trans: I made known my sin, and my lawlessness I did not cover. I said, "I will confess against myself my lawlessness to the Lord," and you forgave the ungodliness of my sin. Selah.
Gallican Psalter Trans: I made my transgression known to you, and my injustice I did not hide. I said: I will confess my injustice against myself to the Lord, and you forgave the wickedness of my sin. Selah.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: I make my sin known to you, and my iniquity I do not hide. I said: I will confess my transgression to the Lord, and you forgave the iniquity of my sin forever.

Hebrew Trans: Therefore let every godly one pray to you at a time of finding; surely when the great waters flood, they shall not reach him.
Septuagint Trans: For this, every holy one shall pray to you in a fitting time; yet in a flood of many waters, they shall not come near to him.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For this shall every holy one pray to you in a fitting time. Yet in a flood of many waters, they shall not draw near to him.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For this, every holy one shall pray to you in a time of finding, so that when many waters overflow, they shall not draw near to him.

Hebrew Trans: You are a hiding place for me; from distress you preserve me; with songs of deliverance you surround me. Selah.
Septuagint Trans: You are my refuge from the affliction that surrounds me; my joy, deliver me from those who have encircled me. Selah.
Gallican Psalter Trans: You are my refuge from the tribulation that has surrounded me; my exultation, deliver me from those who surround me. Selah.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: You are my protection; from the enemy you will guard me. O my praise, saving me, you will surround me always.

Hebrew Trans: I will instruct you and teach you in the way which you should go; I will counsel you with my eye upon you.
Septuagint Trans: I will give you understanding and instruct you in this way in which you shall go; I will fix my eyes upon you.
Gallican Psalter Trans: I will give you understanding, and I will instruct you in this way in which you shall walk; I will fix my eyes upon you.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: I will teach you and show you the way in which you should walk; I will fix my eye upon you.

Hebrew Trans: Do not be like a horse, like a mule, without understanding, whose trappings are bit and bridle to hold them in, lest they come near to you.
Septuagint Trans: Do not become like horse and mule, which have no understanding; with bit and bridle you must constrain their jaws, of those who do not draw near to you.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Do not become like the horse and the mule, which have no understanding. With bit and bridle bind the jaws of those who do not draw near to you.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Do not become like the horse and the mule, which have no understanding; with bit and bridle bind the jaws of those who do not draw near to you.

Hebrew Trans: Many are the sorrows of the wicked, but he who trusts in the LORD, lovingkindness shall surround him.
Septuagint Trans: Many are the scourges of the sinner, but mercy shall surround the one who hopes in the Lord.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Many are the scourges of the sinner, but mercy shall surround the one who hopes in the Lord.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Many are the sorrows of the wicked, but the one trusting in the Lord, mercy shall surround him.

Hebrew Trans: Be glad in the LORD and rejoice, you righteous ones; and shout for joy, all you who are upright in heart.
Septuagint Trans: Be glad in the Lord and rejoice, O righteous ones, and boast, all you who are upright in heart.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Be glad in the Lord and rejoice, you just, and glory, all you upright of heart.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Be glad in the Lord and rejoice, you just, and praise him, all you upright of heart.

Hebrew Trans: Rejoice in the LORD, O you righteous; for the upright, praise is fitting.
Septuagint Trans: Of David. Rejoice, O righteous, in the Lord; praise is fitting for the upright.
Gallican Psalter Trans: A psalm of David. Rejoice in the Lord, O you righteous: praise befits the upright.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Praise the Lord, you righteous; praise is fitting for the upright.

Hebrew Trans: Give thanks to the LORD with the harp; with the ten-stringed lute sing praises to him.
Septuagint Trans: Give thanks to the Lord with the harp; with a ten-stringed psaltery sing praises to him.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Give thanks to the Lord on the harp: sing to him on the psaltery of ten strings.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Give thanks to the Lord on the harp; on the ten-stringed psaltery sing to him.

Hebrew Trans: Sing to him a new song; play skillfully with a shout of joy.
Septuagint Trans: Sing to him a new song; play skillfully with a shout of joy.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Sing to him a new song: play skillfully with a loud noise.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Sing to him a new song; play skillfully with a shout of joy.

Hebrew Trans: For the word of the LORD is upright, and all his work is done in faithfulness.
Septuagint Trans: For the word of the Lord is upright, and all his works are done in faithfulness.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For the word of the Lord is right: and all his works are done in faithfulness.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For the word of the Lord is right, and all his work is done in faithfulness.

Hebrew Trans: He loves righteousness and justice; the earth is full of the lovingkindness of the LORD.
Septuagint Trans: He loves mercy and judgment; the earth is full of the mercy of the Lord.
Gallican Psalter Trans: He loves mercy and judgment: the earth is full of the mercy of the Lord.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: He loves justice and judgment; the earth is full of the mercy of the Lord.

Hebrew Trans: By the word of the LORD the heavens were made, and by the breath of his mouth all their host.
Septuagint Trans: By the word of the Lord the heavens were established, and by the breath of his mouth all their power.
Gallican Psalter Trans: By the word of the Lord the heavens were established: and all their power by the breath of his mouth.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: By the word of the Lord the heavens were made, and by the breath of his mouth all their adornment.

Hebrew Trans: He gathers the waters of the sea together as a heap; he puts the deeps in storehouses.
Septuagint Trans: Gathering the waters of the sea as in a wineskin, placing the depths in storehouses.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Gathering together the waters of the sea as in a wineskin: laying up the depths in storehouses.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Gathering together the waters of the sea as in a wineskin, placing the depths in storehouses.

Hebrew Trans: Let all the earth fear the LORD; let all the inhabitants of the world stand in awe of him.
Septuagint Trans: Let all the earth fear the Lord, and let all the inhabitants of the world be shaken before him.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Let all the earth fear the Lord: and let all the inhabitants of the world be moved by him.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Let all the earth fear the Lord; let all the inhabitants of the world stand in awe of him.

Hebrew Trans: For he spoke, and it came to be; he commanded, and it stood firm.
Septuagint Trans: For he spoke and they came into being; he commanded and they were created.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For he spoke, and they were made: he commanded, and they were created.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For he commanded, and it was made; at his ordering, it stood firm.

Hebrew Trans: The LORD brings the counsel of the nations to nothing; he frustrates the plans of the peoples.
Septuagint Trans: The Lord scatters the counsels of nations, and he sets aside the reasonings of peoples and sets aside the counsels of rulers.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The Lord scatters the counsels of the nations: he rejects the thoughts of the peoples and rejects the counsels of the princes.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The Lord dissolves the counsel of the nations; he has made void the thoughts of the peoples.

Hebrew Trans: The counsel of the LORD stands forever, the thoughts of his heart to all generations.
Septuagint Trans: But the counsel of the Lord endures forever; the thoughts of his heart from generation to generation.
Gallican Psalter Trans: But the counsel of the Lord stands forever: the thoughts of his heart from generation to generation.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The counsel of the Lord shall stand forever; the thoughts of his heart from generation to generation.

Hebrew Trans: Blessed is the nation whose God is the LORD, the people whom he has chosen as his inheritance.
Septuagint Trans: Blessed is the nation whose God is the Lord, the people whom he chose as an inheritance for himself.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Blessed is the nation whose God is the Lord: the people whom he has chosen for his own inheritance.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Blessed is the nation whose God is the Lord; the people whom he has chosen as his inheritance.

Hebrew Trans: From heaven the LORD looks down; he sees all the sons of men.
Septuagint Trans: From heaven the Lord looked down; he saw all the sons of men.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The Lord looked down from heaven: he saw all the sons of men.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: From heaven the Lord looked down; he saw all the sons of Adam.

Hebrew Trans: From his dwelling place he gazes upon all the inhabitants of the earth—
Septuagint Trans: From his prepared dwelling place he looked upon all those inhabiting the earth.
Gallican Psalter Trans: From his prepared dwelling place he looked out upon all who inhabit the earth.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: From his most firm throne he looked out upon all the inhabitants of the earth.

Hebrew Trans: he who fashions the hearts of them all, who discerns all their works.
Septuagint Trans: He who fashioned their hearts individually, he who understands all their works.
Gallican Psalter Trans: He who formed their hearts one by one: who understands all their works.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: He who fashioned their hearts alike, who understands all their works.

Hebrew Trans: The king is not saved by a great army; a warrior is not delivered by great strength.
Septuagint Trans: A king is not saved by a great army, and a giant shall not be saved by the abundance of his strength.
Gallican Psalter Trans: A king is not saved by great strength: nor shall a giant be saved by the abundance of his strength.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The king is not saved by the multitude of his army, nor shall the strong man be delivered by the greatness of his strength.

Hebrew Trans: The horse is a vain hope for salvation, and by its great might it cannot deliver.
Septuagint Trans: A horse is vain for salvation, and by the abundance of its power it shall not be saved.
Gallican Psalter Trans: A horse is deceitful for safety: nor shall he be saved by the abundance of his strength.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The horse is a vain hope for salvation, and by the abundance of its strength it shall not save.

Hebrew Trans: Behold, the eye of the LORD is upon those who fear him, upon those who hope in his lovingkindness,
Septuagint Trans: Behold, the eyes of the Lord are upon those who fear him, those who hope in his mercy,
Gallican Psalter Trans: Behold, the eyes of the Lord are upon those who fear him: who hope in his mercy.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Behold, the eye of the Lord is upon those who fear him, and upon those who hope in his mercy,

Hebrew Trans: to deliver their soul from death, and to keep them alive in famine.
Septuagint Trans: to deliver their souls from death and to nourish them in famine.
Gallican Psalter Trans: That he may deliver their souls from death: and nourish them in famine.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: To deliver their soul from death, and to keep them alive in famine.

Hebrew Trans: Our soul waits for the LORD; he is our help and our shield.
Septuagint Trans: Our soul waits upon the Lord, for he is our helper and our shield.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Our soul waits for the Lord: for he is our helper and protector.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Our soul has waited for the Lord; he is our help and our shield.

Hebrew Trans: For in him our heart rejoices, because in his holy name we have trusted.
Septuagint Trans: For in him our heart shall rejoice, and in his holy name we have hoped.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For in him our heart shall rejoice: and in his holy name we have hoped.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For in him our heart shall rejoice, because we have hoped in his holy name.

Hebrew Trans: Let your lovingkindness, O LORD, be upon us, even as we have hoped in you.
Septuagint Trans: May your mercy, O Lord, be upon us, even as we have hoped in you.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Let your mercy, O Lord, be upon us: even as we have hoped in you.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Let your mercy, O Lord, be upon us, even as we have hoped in you.

Hebrew Trans: Of David, when he changed his behavior before Abimelech, who drove him away, and he departed.
Septuagint Trans: Of David, when he changed his countenance before Abimelech, and he released him, and he departed.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Of David, when he changed his countenance before Abimelech, and he dismissed him, and he departed.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Of David, when he changed his countenance before Abimelech, and he dismissed him, and he departed.

Hebrew Trans: I will bless the LORD at all times; his praise shall continually be in my mouth.
Septuagint Trans: I will bless the Lord at all times; continually his praise shall be in my mouth.
Gallican Psalter Trans: I will bless the Lord at all times; his praise shall always be in my mouth.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: I will bless the Lord at all times; his praise shall always be in my mouth.

Hebrew Trans: In the LORD my soul shall boast; the humble shall hear and be glad.
Septuagint Trans: In the Lord my soul shall be praised; let the meek hear and be glad.
Gallican Psalter Trans: In the Lord my soul shall be praised; let the meek hear and be glad.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: In the Lord my soul shall rejoice; let the meek hear and be glad.

Hebrew Trans: Magnify the LORD with me, and let us exalt his name together.
Septuagint Trans: Magnify the Lord with me, and let us exalt his name together.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Magnify the Lord with me, and let us exalt his name together.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Magnify the Lord with me, and let us exalt his name together.

Hebrew Trans: I sought the LORD, and he answered me, and from all my fears he delivered me.
Septuagint Trans: I sought the Lord, and he heard me, and from all my sojournings he delivered me.
Gallican Psalter Trans: I sought the Lord, and he heard me, and he delivered me from all my tribulations.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: I sought the Lord, and he heard me, and delivered me from all my distresses.

Hebrew Trans: They looked to him and were radiant, and their faces shall not be ashamed.
Septuagint Trans: Come to him and be enlightened, and your faces shall not be put to shame.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Draw near to him and be enlightened, and your faces shall not be confounded.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Look to him and come together, and your faces shall not be confounded.

Hebrew Trans: This poor man cried, and the LORD heard, and saved him out of all his troubles.
Septuagint Trans: This poor man cried out, and the Lord heard him, and saved him out of all his afflictions.
Gallican Psalter Trans: This poor man cried out, and the Lord heard him, and saved him from all his tribulations.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: This poor man cried, and the Lord heard him, and will save him from all his tribulations.

Hebrew Trans: The angel of the LORD encamps around those who fear him, and delivers them.
Septuagint Trans: The angel of the Lord shall encamp around those who fear him, and shall deliver them.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The angel of the Lord shall encamp around those who fear him, and shall deliver them.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The angel of the Lord encamps around those who fear him, and will rescue them.

Hebrew Trans: Taste and see that the LORD is good; blessed is the man who takes refuge in him.
Septuagint Trans: Taste and see that the Lord is good; blessed is the man who hopes in him.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Taste and see that the Lord is sweet; blessed is the man who hopes in him.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Taste and see that the Lord is good; blessed is the man who hopes in him.

Hebrew Trans: Fear the LORD, you his holy ones, for there is no want to those who fear him.
Septuagint Trans: Fear the Lord, you his holy ones, for there is no want to those who fear him.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Fear the Lord, all you his saints, for there is no want to those who fear him.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Fear the Lord, you his saints, for there is no want to those who fear him.

Hebrew Trans: Young lions suffer lack and hunger, but those who seek the LORD shall not lack any good thing.
Septuagint Trans: The rich have become poor and hungered, but those who seek the Lord shall not be diminished of any good thing. Interlude.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The rich have been in need and have hungered, but those who seek the Lord shall not be deprived of any good thing. Selah.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Lions have been in need and have hungered, but those who seek the Lord shall not lack any good thing.

Hebrew Trans: Come, children, listen to me; I will teach you the fear of the LORD.
Septuagint Trans: Come, children, hear me; I will teach you the fear of the Lord.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Come, children, hear me; I will teach you the fear of the Lord.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Come, children, hear me; I will teach you the fear of the Lord.

Hebrew Trans: Who is the man who desires life, who loves days to see good?
Septuagint Trans: Who is the man who desires life, who loves to see good days?
Gallican Psalter Trans: Who is the man who desires life, who wishes to see good days?
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Who is the man that desires life, loving to see good days?

Hebrew Trans: Keep your tongue from evil, and your lips from speaking deceit.
Septuagint Trans: Keep your tongue from evil, and your lips from speaking deceit.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Keep your tongue from evil, and your lips from speaking deceit.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Keep your tongue from evil, and your lips from speaking deceit.

Hebrew Trans: Turn away from evil and do good; seek peace and pursue it.
Septuagint Trans: Turn away from evil and do good; seek peace and pursue it.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Turn away from evil and do good; seek peace and pursue it.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Turn away from evil and do good; seek peace and pursue it.

Hebrew Trans: The eyes of the LORD are toward the righteous, and his ears toward their cry.
Septuagint Trans: The eyes of the Lord are upon the righteous, and his ears are toward their supplication.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The eyes of the Lord are upon the just, and his ears are toward their prayer.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The eyes of the Lord are upon the just, and his ears toward their cry.

Hebrew Trans: The face of the LORD is against those who do evil, to cut off the memory of them from the earth.
Septuagint Trans: But the face of the Lord is against those who do evil, to destroy the remembrance of them from the earth.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The face of the Lord is against those who do evil, to destroy the remembrance of them from the earth.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The face of the Lord is against those who do evil, to destroy the memory of them from the earth.

Hebrew Trans: They cried out, and the LORD heard, and delivered them from all their troubles.
Septuagint Trans: The righteous cried out, and the Lord heard them, and delivered them out of all their afflictions.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The just cried out, and the Lord heard, and delivered them from all their tribulations.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: They cried out, and the Lord heard, and delivered them from all their tribulations.

Hebrew Trans: The LORD is near to the brokenhearted, and saves the crushed in spirit.
Septuagint Trans: The Lord is near to those who are broken in heart, and he will save the humble in spirit.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The Lord is near to those who are troubled in heart, and he will save the humble in spirit.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The Lord is near to the brokenhearted, and he will save the crushed in spirit.

Hebrew Trans: Many are the afflictions of the righteous, but out of them all the LORD delivers him.
Septuagint Trans: Many are the afflictions of the righteous, and out of them all he shall deliver them.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Many are the tribulations of the just, and from all these he has delivered them.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Many are the tribulations of the just, and the Lord will deliver him from all of them.

Hebrew Trans: He keeps all his bones; not one of them is broken.
Septuagint Trans: The Lord guards all their bones; not one of them shall be broken.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The Lord guards all their bones; not one of them shall be broken.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: He guards all his bones; not one of them shall be broken.

Hebrew Trans: Evil shall slay the wicked, and those who hate the righteous shall be condemned.
Septuagint Trans: The death of sinners is evil, and those who hate the righteous shall offend.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The death of sinners is the worst, and those who hate the just shall offend.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Wickedness shall slay the wicked, and those who hate the just shall be overcome.

Hebrew Trans: The LORD redeems the soul of his servants, and none of those who take refuge in him shall be condemned.
Septuagint Trans: The Lord shall redeem the souls of his servants, and none of those who hope in him shall offend.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The Lord shall redeem the souls of his servants, and none of those who hope in him shall offend.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The Lord redeems the soul of his servants, and none of those who hope in him shall be found guilty.

Hebrew Trans: Of David. Contend, O LORD, with those who contend with me; fight against those who fight against me.
Septuagint Trans: Of David. Judge, O Lord, those who wrong me; fight against those who fight against me.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Of David. Judge, O Lord, those who harm me; fight against those who fight against me.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Of David. Judge, O Lord, my adversaries; fight against those who fight against me.

Hebrew Trans: Take hold of shield and buckler, and rise up for my help.
Septuagint Trans: Take hold of shield and buckler, and rise up for my help.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Take hold of arms and shield, and rise up in help to me.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Take hold of shield and spear, and rise up in my aid.

Hebrew Trans: And draw out the spear and close up against my pursuers; say to my soul, "I am your salvation."
Septuagint Trans: Draw out the sword and close up the way against those who pursue me; say to my soul, "I am your salvation."
Gallican Psalter Trans: Pour out the spear and close the way against those who pursue me; say to my soul: "I am your salvation."
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Draw out the sword and block the way against those who pursue me; say to my soul: "I am your salvation."

Hebrew Trans: Let them be ashamed and brought to dishonor who seek my soul; let them be turned back and confounded who devise my hurt.
Septuagint Trans: Let them be ashamed and put to shame who seek my soul; let them be turned back and confounded who devise evil against me.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Let them be confounded and put to shame who seek my soul; let them be turned back and confounded who devise evil against me.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Let them be confounded and put to shame who seek my soul; let them be turned back and confounded who devise evil against me.

Hebrew Trans: Let them be as chaff before the wind, and the angel of the LORD driving them.
Septuagint Trans: Let them be as dust before the wind, and let the angel of the Lord afflict them.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Let them become like dust before the face of the wind, and let the angel of the Lord press them hard.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Let them become like dust before the face of the wind, and let the angel of the Lord drive them away.

Hebrew Trans: Let their way be dark and slippery, and the angel of the LORD pursuing them.
Septuagint Trans: Let their way be darkness and slipperiness, and let the angel of the Lord pursue them.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Let their way be darkness and slippery, and let the angel of the Lord pursue them.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Let their way be darkness and slippery, and let the angel of the Lord pursue them.

Hebrew Trans: For without cause they have hidden their pit of net for me; without cause they have dug for my soul.
Septuagint Trans: For without cause they hid for me the destruction of their snare; in vain they reproached my soul.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For without cause they have hidden for me the destruction of their snare; without reason they have reviled my soul.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For without cause they have hidden for me the snares of their net; without reason they have dug a pit for my soul.

Hebrew Trans: Let destruction come upon him unawares, and let his net which he has hidden catch him; let him fall into that very destruction.
Septuagint Trans: Let a snare which they do not know come upon them, and let the trap which they hid catch them, and let them fall into that very snare.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Let the snare which he knows not come upon him, and let the trap which he has hidden seize him, and let him fall into that very snare.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Let calamity come upon him unawares, and let his net which he has hidden catch him, and let him fall into the snare.

Hebrew Trans: And my soul shall rejoice in the LORD; it shall exult in his salvation.
Septuagint Trans: But my soul shall rejoice in the Lord; it shall delight in his salvation.
Gallican Psalter Trans: But my soul shall rejoice in the Lord; it shall delight in his salvation.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: But my soul shall exult in the Lord, and shall rejoice in His salvation.

Hebrew Trans: All my bones shall say, "LORD, who is like you, delivering the poor from one stronger than he, and the poor and needy from one who robs him?"
Septuagint Trans: All my bones shall say, "Lord, who is like you, delivering the poor from the hand of those stronger than him, and the poor and needy from those who plunder him?"
Gallican Psalter Trans: All my bones shall say: "Lord, who is like you, delivering the helpless from the hand of those stronger than him, the needy and the poor from those who plunder him?"
Hebrew Psalter Trans: All my bones shall say: "Lord, who is like You, delivering the helpless from one stronger than he, and the poor and needy from the violent?"

Hebrew Trans: Violent witnesses rise up; they ask me of things I know not.
Septuagint Trans: Unjust witnesses rose up; they questioned me about things I did not know.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Unjust witnesses rising up questioned me about things I did not know.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Unjust witnesses rising up asked me things I did not know.

Hebrew Trans: They repay me evil for good, bereavement to my soul.
Septuagint Trans: They repaid me evil for good, and childlessness to my soul.
Gallican Psalter Trans: They repaid me evil for good, barrenness to my soul.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: They repaid me evil for good, bringing barrenness to my soul.

Hebrew Trans: But as for me, when they were sick, my clothing was sackcloth; I afflicted my soul with fasting, and my prayer returned upon my bosom.
Septuagint Trans: But when they troubled me, I put on sackcloth and humbled my soul with fasting, and my prayer shall return into my bosom.
Gallican Psalter Trans: But I, when they were troublesome to me, clothed myself in sackcloth; I humbled my soul with fasting, and my prayer shall return into my own bosom.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: But I, when they were sick, clothed myself in sackcloth; I humbled my soul with fasting, and my prayer shall return to my own bosom.

Hebrew Trans: I walked about as though it were my friend or brother; I bowed down mourning, as one who mourns for a mother.
Septuagint Trans: As though it were a neighbor, as though our own brother, so I was pleasing; as one mourning and downcast, so I was humbled.
Gallican Psalter Trans: As though for a neighbor, as for our brother, so I was pleasing; as one mourning and sorrowful, so I was humbled.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: As though toward a friend, as though toward my own brother, so I walked; as a mother mourning, I was bowed down in sorrow.

Hebrew Trans: But at my stumbling they rejoiced and gathered together; the smiters gathered against me, and I knew it not; they tore at me and did not cease.
Septuagint Trans: But against me they rejoiced and gathered together; scourges gathered against me, and I did not know it; they were torn apart and were not pricked with remorse.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And against me they rejoiced and gathered together; scourges were heaped upon me, and I knew it not.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: But in my infirmity they rejoiced and gathered together; they assembled against me, striking me, and I did not know it.

Hebrew Trans: With godless mockers at a feast, they gnashed their teeth against me.
Septuagint Trans: They tested me, they mocked me with mockery, they gnashed their teeth against me.
Gallican Psalter Trans: They were scattered yet not repentant; they tested me, they mocked me with mockery; they gnashed their teeth against me.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Tearing at me and not being silent, with feigned and fictitious words they gnashed their teeth against me.

Hebrew Trans: O Lord, how long will you look on? Rescue my soul from their destructions, my only one from the young lions.
Septuagint Trans: Lord, when will you look upon this? Restore my soul from their malice, my only one from the lions.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Lord, when will you look upon me? Restore my soul from their malice, my only one from the lions.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Lord, how long will You look on? Rescue my soul from their calamities, my only one from the lions.

Hebrew Trans: I will give you thanks in the great assembly; among a mighty people I will praise you.
Septuagint Trans: I will give thanks to you, O Lord, in a great assembly; among a weighty people I will praise you.
Gallican Psalter Trans: I will confess to you in the great assembly; among a weighty people I will praise you.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: I will give thanks to You in the great assembly; among a mighty people I will praise You.

Hebrew Trans: Let not those who are wrongfully my enemies rejoice over me; let not those who hate me without cause wink the eye.
Septuagint Trans: Let not those who wrongfully are my enemies rejoice over me; let not those who hate me without cause wink with their eyes.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Let not those who oppose me unjustly rejoice over me, those who hate me without cause and wink with their eyes.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Let not my enemies who are liars rejoice over me, those who hate me without cause, winking with their eye.

Hebrew Trans: For they do not speak peace, and against the quiet ones of the land they devise deceitful words.
Septuagint Trans: For to me indeed they spoke peaceable words, but upon anger they devised deceits.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For indeed they spoke peacefully to me, and speaking in the wrath of the earth, they devised deceits.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For they do not speak peace, but against the quiet of the land they devise deceitful words.

Hebrew Trans: And they opened wide their mouth against me; they said, "Aha! Aha! Our eyes have seen it!"
Septuagint Trans: And they opened wide their mouth against me; they said, "Aha, aha! Our eyes have seen it."
Gallican Psalter Trans: And they opened wide their mouth against me; they said: "Aha, aha! Our eyes have seen it."
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And they opened wide their mouth against me; they said: "Aha, aha! Our eye has seen it."

Hebrew Trans: You have seen it, O LORD; be not silent. O Lord, be not far from me.
Septuagint Trans: You have seen, O Lord; do not be silent. O Lord, do not depart from me.
Gallican Psalter Trans: You have seen, O Lord; be not silent. Lord, do not depart from me.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: You have seen, O Lord; be not silent. O Lord, be not far from me.

Hebrew Trans: Stir up yourself and awake to my judgment, my God and my Lord, to my cause.
Septuagint Trans: Awake, O Lord, and attend to my judgment, my God and my Lord, to my cause.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Rise up and attend to my judgment, my God and my Lord, to my cause.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Rise up and awaken to my judgment, my God, and my Lord, to my cause.

Hebrew Trans: Judge me according to your righteousness, O LORD my God, and let them not rejoice over me.
Septuagint Trans: Judge me according to your righteousness, O Lord my God, and let them not rejoice over me.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Judge me according to your justice, O Lord my God, and let them not rejoice over me.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Judge me according to Your justice, O Lord my God, and let them not exult over me.

Hebrew Trans: Let them not say in their hearts, "Aha! Our desire!" Let them not say, "We have swallowed him up."
Septuagint Trans: Let them not say in their hearts, "Aha, aha, for our soul!" Nor let them say, "We have swallowed him up."
Gallican Psalter Trans: Let them not say in their hearts: "Aha, aha, for our soul!" Nor let them say: "We have devoured him."
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Let them not say in their heart: "Aha, aha, it is well for our soul!" Let them not say: "We have swallowed him up."

Hebrew Trans: Let them be ashamed and confounded together who rejoice at my hurt; let them be clothed with shame and dishonor who magnify themselves against me.
Septuagint Trans: Let them be ashamed and confounded together who rejoice at my misfortunes; let them be clothed with shame and disgrace who boast greatly against me.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Let them blush and be ashamed together who rejoice at my misfortunes; let them be clothed with confusion and shame who speak great things against me.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Let them be confounded and put to shame together who rejoice in my affliction; let them be clothed in confusion and shame who magnify themselves over me.

Hebrew Trans: Let them shout for joy and be glad who delight in my righteousness; and let them say continually, "Great is the LORD, who delights in the peace of his servant."
Septuagint Trans: Let those who desire my righteousness shout for joy and be glad, and let them say continually, "Let the Lord be magnified," those who desire the peace of his servant.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Let them exult and be glad who desire my justice, and let them say always: "The Lord be magnified," who desire the peace of his servant.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Let them praise and be glad who desire my justice, and let them say always: "Magnified be the Lord, who wills the peace of His servant."

Hebrew Trans: And my tongue shall speak of your righteousness, all the day long of your praise.
Septuagint Trans: And my tongue shall meditate on your righteousness, all the day long on your praise.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And my tongue shall meditate on your justice, all the day long on your praise.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And my tongue shall meditate on Your justice, all the day long on Your praise.

Hebrew Trans: For the choirmaster; of the servant of the LORD, of David.
Septuagint Trans: To the end, of the servant of the Lord, of David.
Gallican Psalter Trans: To the end, for the servant of the Lord, David.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For the victory of the servant of the Lord, David.

Hebrew Trans: Transgression speaks to the wicked in the midst of his heart; there is no fear of God before his eyes.
Septuagint Trans: The transgressor declares to himself that he will sin; there is no fear of God before his eyes.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The unjust one said within himself that he should transgress; there is no fear of God before his eyes.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The wickedness of the impious one speaks in the midst of his heart: there is no fear of God before his eyes.

Hebrew Trans: For he flatters himself in his own eyes, that his iniquity should be found out and hated.
Septuagint Trans: For he has dealt deceitfully before him, to discover his iniquity and to hate it.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For he has acted deceitfully in His sight, so that his iniquity might be found unto hatred.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For he has dealt deceitfully against him in his own eyes, so that his iniquity might be found unto hatred.

Hebrew Trans: The words of his mouth are iniquity and deceit; he has ceased to act wisely, to do good.
Septuagint Trans: The words of his mouth are lawlessness and deceit; he has refused to understand how to do good.
