Gallican Psalter Trans: The words of his mouth are iniquity and deceit; he has refused to understand so as to do good.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The words of his mouth are iniquity and deceit; he has ceased to think of doing good.

Hebrew Trans: He devises iniquity upon his bed; he sets himself on a path that is not good; evil he does not reject.
Septuagint Trans: He devised lawlessness upon his bed; he set himself upon every way that is not good, and he did not abhor evil.
Gallican Psalter Trans: He has devised iniquity upon his bed; he has set himself upon every way that is not good; moreover, he has not hated evil.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: He devises iniquity upon his bed; he shall stand in a way that is not good; he will not cast away evil.

Hebrew Trans: O LORD, your steadfast love is in the heavens; your faithfulness reaches to the skies.
Septuagint Trans: O Lord, in the heaven is your mercy, and your truth reaches unto the clouds.
Gallican Psalter Trans: O Lord, Your mercy is in heaven, and Your truth reaches unto the clouds.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: O Lord, your mercy is in heaven; your faithfulness reaches unto the clouds.

Hebrew Trans: Your righteousness is like the mighty mountains; your judgments are a great deep; man and beast you save, O LORD.
Septuagint Trans: Your righteousness is like the mountains of God; your judgments are a great abyss. You will save men and beasts, O Lord.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Your justice is like the mountains of God; Your judgments are a great deep. Men and beasts You will save, O Lord.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Your justice is like the mountains, O Lord; your judgment is a great deep. You will save both men and beasts, O Lord.

Hebrew Trans: How precious is your steadfast love, O God! The children of men take refuge in the shadow of your wings.
Septuagint Trans: How you have multiplied your mercy, O God! And the sons of men shall hope in the shelter of your wings.
Gallican Psalter Trans: How You have multiplied Your mercy, O God! And the children of men shall hope under the covering of Your wings.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: How precious is your mercy, O Lord! And the children of Adam shall hope in the shadow of your wings.

Hebrew Trans: They feast on the abundance of your house, and from the river of your delights you give them drink.
Septuagint Trans: They shall be made drunk from the fatness of your house, and you shall give them drink from the torrent of your delight.
Gallican Psalter Trans: They shall be inebriated with the abundance of Your house, and with the torrent of Your pleasure You shall give them drink.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: They shall be inebriated with the richness of your house, and you shall give them to drink from the torrent of your delights.

Hebrew Trans: For with you is the fountain of life; in your light we see light.
Septuagint Trans: For with you is the fountain of life; in your light we shall see light.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For with You is the fountain of life; in Your light we shall see light.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For with you is the fountain of life; in your light we shall see light.

Hebrew Trans: Extend your steadfast love to those who know you, and your righteousness to the upright in heart.
Septuagint Trans: Extend your mercy to those who know you, and your righteousness to the upright in heart.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Extend Your mercy to those who know You, and Your justice to those who are upright of heart.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Extend your mercy to those who know you, and your justice to the upright of heart.

Hebrew Trans: Let not the foot of pride come upon me, and let not the hand of the wicked drive me away.
Septuagint Trans: Let not the foot of pride come to me, and let not the hand of sinners shake me.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Let not the foot of pride come to me, and let not the hand of the sinner move me.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Let not the foot of pride come to me, and let not the hand of the wicked move me.

Hebrew Trans: There the workers of iniquity have fallen; they are thrust down and are not able to rise.
Septuagint Trans: There the workers of lawlessness have fallen; they were cast out and shall not be able to stand.
Gallican Psalter Trans: There they have fallen, those who work iniquity; they have been cast out, and were not able to stand.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: There the workers of iniquity have fallen; they have been cast out and were not able to rise.

Hebrew Trans: Of David. Do not fret because of evildoers; do not be envious of those who do iniquity.
Septuagint Trans: Of David. Do not be envious of evildoers, nor be jealous of those who practice lawlessness.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Of David himself. Do not emulate those who act in malice, nor be jealous of those who work iniquity.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Of David. Do not contend with the wicked, nor be envious of those who do iniquity.

Hebrew Trans: For like grass they shall quickly wither, and like green herbage they shall fade.
Septuagint Trans: For like grass they will quickly be dried up, and like green herbs they will quickly fall away.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For like hay they will quickly wither, and like green herbs they will swiftly fall.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For like grass they shall quickly be cut down, and like green herbs they shall wither.

Hebrew Trans: Trust in the LORD and do good; dwell in the land and feed on faithfulness.
Septuagint Trans: Hope in the Lord and do goodness, and dwell in the land, and you shall be shepherded upon its wealth.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Hope in the Lord and do goodness, and dwell in the land, and you shall be fed by its riches.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Hope in the Lord and do good; dwell in the land and feed on faith.

Hebrew Trans: And delight yourself in the LORD, and He shall give you the desires of your heart.
Septuagint Trans: Delight yourself in the Lord, and he will give you the requests of your heart.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Delight in the Lord, and he will give you the petitions of your heart.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And delight yourself in the Lord, and He shall give you the petitions of your heart.

Hebrew Trans: Roll your way upon the LORD, and trust in Him, and He shall bring it to pass.
Septuagint Trans: Reveal your way to the Lord and hope in him, and he himself will act.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Reveal your way to the Lord and hope in him, and he himself will act.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Roll your way upon the Lord, and trust in Him, and He Himself shall act.

Hebrew Trans: And He shall bring forth your righteousness as the light, and your justice as the noonday.
Septuagint Trans: And he will bring forth your righteousness as the light, and your judgment as the noonday.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And he will bring forth your justice as a light, and your judgment as the noonday.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And He shall bring forth your justice as the light, and your judgment as the noonday.

Hebrew Trans: Be still before the LORD and wait patiently for Him; do not fret because of him who prospers in his way, because of the man who carries out evil schemes.
Septuagint Trans: Be subject to the Lord and entreat him. Do not be envious of the one who prospers in his way, of a man who commits lawless deeds.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Be subject to the Lord and pray to him. Do not emulate the one who prospers in his way, the man who commits injustices.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Be silent before the Lord and wait for Him; do not contend against him who prospers in his way, against the man who carries out his schemes.

Hebrew Trans: Cease from anger and forsake wrath; do not fret—it leads only to evil.
Septuagint Trans: Cease from anger and forsake wrath; do not be envious so as to do evil.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Cease from anger and forsake fury; do not emulate so as to do evil.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Let go of anger and abandon fury; do not contend so as to do evil.

Hebrew Trans: For evildoers shall be cut off, but those who wait upon the LORD, they shall inherit the land.
Septuagint Trans: For evildoers shall be utterly destroyed, but those who wait upon the Lord, they shall inherit the land.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For those who act in malice shall be destroyed, but those who wait upon the Lord, they shall inherit the land.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For those who do evil shall perish, but those who wait upon the Lord, they shall inherit the land.

Hebrew Trans: And yet a little while and the wicked shall be no more; and you shall look carefully at his place, and he is not there.
Septuagint Trans: And yet a little while and the sinner shall not exist, and you will seek his place and shall not find it.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And yet a little while, and the sinner shall not be, and you shall seek his place and shall not find it.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For yet a little while and the wicked shall not be, and you shall consider his place and he shall not remain.

Hebrew Trans: But the meek shall inherit the land and shall delight themselves in the abundance of peace.
Septuagint Trans: But the meek shall inherit the land and shall delight themselves in an abundance of peace.
Gallican Psalter Trans: But the meek shall inherit the land and shall delight in the abundance of peace.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: But the meek shall inherit the land and shall delight themselves in the abundance of peace.

Hebrew Trans: The wicked plots against the righteous and gnashes his teeth at him.
Septuagint Trans: The sinner will watch against the righteous one and will gnash his teeth against him.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The sinner will watch the just man and will gnash his teeth upon him.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The wicked plots against the just and gnashes his teeth against him.

Hebrew Trans: The Lord shall laugh at him, for He sees that his day is coming.
Septuagint Trans: But the Lord will laugh at him, for he foresees that his day will come.
Gallican Psalter Trans: But the Lord will laugh at him, for he foresees that his day will come.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The Lord shall laugh at him, seeing that his day is coming.

Hebrew Trans: The wicked have drawn the sword and have bent their bow, to cast down the poor and needy, to slay those who are upright in way.
Septuagint Trans: Sinners have drawn a sword; they have bent their bow to cast down the poor and needy, to slay the upright in heart.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Sinners have drawn the sword; they have bent their bow, to cast down the poor and needy, to slaughter the upright of heart.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The wicked have drawn the sword; they have bent their bow, to strike down the needy and the poor, and to slay those who are upright in the way.

Hebrew Trans: Their sword shall enter into their own heart, and their bows shall be broken.
Septuagint Trans: May their sword enter into their own heart, and may their bows be shattered.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Let their sword enter into their own hearts, and let their bows be broken.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Let their sword enter into their own heart, and let their bows be broken.

Hebrew Trans: Better is the little of the righteous than the abundance of many wicked.
Septuagint Trans: Better is a little to the righteous one than the great wealth of sinners.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Better is a little to the just man than the great riches of sinners.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Better is the little of the just man than the great riches of the wicked.

Hebrew Trans: For the arms of the wicked shall be broken, but the LORD upholds the righteous.
Septuagint Trans: For the arms of sinners shall be broken, but the Lord upholds the righteous.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For the arms of sinners shall be broken, but the Lord strengthens the just.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For the arms of the wicked shall be broken, but the Lord upholds the just.

Hebrew Trans: The LORD knows the days of the blameless, and their inheritance shall be forever.
Septuagint Trans: The Lord knows the ways of the blameless, and their inheritance shall be forever.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The Lord knows the days of the undefiled, and their inheritance shall be forever.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The Lord knows the days of the blameless, and their inheritance shall be eternal.

Hebrew Trans: They shall not be ashamed in the time of evil, and in the days of famine they shall be satisfied.
Septuagint Trans: They shall not be put to shame in an evil time, and in days of famine they shall be satisfied.
Gallican Psalter Trans: They shall not be confounded in the evil time, and in the days of famine they shall be satisfied.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: They shall not be confounded in the evil time, and in the days of famine they shall be satisfied.

Hebrew Trans: For the wicked shall perish, and the enemies of the LORD shall be as the glory of the pastures; they are consumed—in smoke they are consumed.
Septuagint Trans: For sinners shall perish, and the enemies of the Lord, at the moment of their being glorified and exalted, failing like smoke they have failed.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For sinners shall perish; but the enemies of the Lord, as soon as they have been honored and exalted, failing, like smoke they have failed.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For the wicked shall perish, and the enemies of the Lord, glorying like wild oxen, shall be consumed; like smoke they shall be consumed.

Hebrew Trans: The wicked borrows and does not repay, but the righteous is gracious and gives.
Septuagint Trans: The sinner borrows and will not repay, but the righteous one has compassion and gives.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The sinner shall borrow and shall not repay, but the just man shows mercy and gives.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The wicked borrows and does not repay, but the just man gives and bestows.

Hebrew Trans: For those blessed by Him shall inherit the land, and those cursed by Him shall be cut off.
Septuagint Trans: For those who bless him shall inherit the land, but those who curse him shall be utterly destroyed.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For those who bless him shall inherit the land, but those who curse him shall utterly perish.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For those who are blessed by Him shall inherit the land, and those who are cursed shall perish.

Hebrew Trans: By the LORD the steps of a man are established, and He delights in his way.
Septuagint Trans: By the Lord the steps of a man are directed, and he will delight in his way.
Gallican Psalter Trans: By the Lord the steps of a man shall be directed, and he shall will his way.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: By the Lord the steps of a man are made firm, and He shall delight in his way.

Hebrew Trans: Though he fall, he shall not be utterly cast down, for the LORD upholds his hand.
Septuagint Trans: When he falls he shall not be dashed down, for the Lord supports his hand.
Gallican Psalter Trans: When he falls, he shall not be dashed down, for the Lord places his hand beneath him.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: When he falls, he shall not be dashed down, for the Lord sustains his hand.

Hebrew Trans: I have been young and now am old, yet I have not seen the righteous forsaken, nor his seed begging bread.
Septuagint Trans: I was young and indeed I have grown old, and I have not seen a righteous man forsaken, nor his seed begging for bread.
Gallican Psalter Trans: I was young and have grown old, and I have not seen the just man forsaken, nor his seed seeking bread.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: I was young and indeed have grown old, and I have not seen the just man forsaken, nor his seed seeking bread.

Hebrew Trans: All the day long he is gracious and lends, and his seed is a blessing.
Septuagint Trans: All the day long he shows mercy and lends, and his seed shall be for a blessing.
Gallican Psalter Trans: All the day long he shows mercy and lends, and his seed shall be in blessing.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: All the day long he gives and lends, and his seed shall be in blessing.

Hebrew Trans: Depart from evil and do good, and dwell forevermore.
Septuagint Trans: Turn away from evil and do good, and dwell forevermore.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Turn away from evil and do good, and dwell forever and ever.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Depart from evil and do good, and dwell forevermore.

Hebrew Trans: For the LORD loves justice and does not forsake His faithful ones; they are preserved forever, but the seed of the wicked is cut off.
Septuagint Trans: For the Lord loves justice and will not forsake his holy ones; they shall be guarded forever. But the lawless shall be driven out, and the seed of the ungodly shall be utterly destroyed.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For the Lord loves judgment and will not forsake his saints; they shall be preserved forever. The unjust shall be punished, and the seed of the wicked shall perish.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For the Lord loves justice and shall not forsake His saints; they are preserved forever, but the seed of the wicked shall perish.

Hebrew Trans: The righteous shall inherit the land and dwell upon it forever.
Septuagint Trans: But the righteous shall inherit the land and shall dwell upon it forevermore.
Gallican Psalter Trans: But the just shall inherit the land and shall dwell upon it forever and ever.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The just shall inherit the land and shall dwell upon it forever.

Hebrew Trans: The mouth of the righteous utters wisdom, and his tongue speaks justice.
Septuagint Trans: The mouth of the righteous shall meditate wisdom, and his tongue shall speak justice.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The mouth of the just man shall meditate wisdom, and his tongue shall speak judgment.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The mouth of the just shall meditate wisdom, and his tongue shall speak judgment.

Hebrew Trans: The law of his God is in his heart; his steps shall not slide.
Septuagint Trans: The law of his God is in his heart, and his steps shall not be tripped.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The law of his God is in his heart, and his steps shall not be supplanted.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The law of his God is in his heart; his steps shall not falter.

Hebrew Trans: The wicked watches the righteous and seeks to put him to death.
Septuagint Trans: The sinner observes the righteous one and seeks to put him to death.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The sinner considers the just man and seeks to put him to death.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The wicked watches the just man and seeks to slay him.

Hebrew Trans: The LORD will not leave him in his hand, nor condemn him when he is judged.
Septuagint Trans: But the Lord will not abandon him into his hands, nor condemn him when he is judged.
Gallican Psalter Trans: But the Lord will not leave him in his hands, nor condemn him when he is judged.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The Lord shall not leave him in his hand, nor condemn him when he is judged.

Hebrew Trans: Wait for the LORD and keep His way, and He shall exalt you to inherit the land; when the wicked are cut off, you shall see it.
Septuagint Trans: Wait upon the Lord and keep his way, and he will exalt you to inherit the land; when sinners are destroyed, you shall see it.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Wait for the Lord and keep his way, and he will exalt you to inherit the land; when sinners perish, you shall see it.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Wait upon the Lord and keep His way, and He shall exalt you to possess the land; when the wicked perish, you shall see it.

Hebrew Trans: I have seen the wicked ruthless, spreading himself like a luxuriant native tree.
Septuagint Trans: I saw an ungodly man greatly exalted and lifting himself up like the cedars of Lebanon.
Gallican Psalter Trans: I saw the wicked greatly exalted and lifted up like the cedars of Lebanon.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: I have seen the wicked, mighty and most powerful, like a native tree flourishing.

Hebrew Trans: But he passed away, and behold, he was no more; and I sought him, but he could not be found.
Septuagint Trans: And I passed by, and behold he was not, and I sought him and his place was not found.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And I passed by, and behold he was not, and I sought him and his place was not found.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And I passed by, and behold he was no more, and I sought him and he was not found.

Hebrew Trans: Mark the blameless man and behold the upright, for there is a future for the man of peace.
Septuagint Trans: Guard innocence and behold uprightness, for there is a remnant for the peaceful man.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Guard innocence and behold uprightness, for there are remnants for the peaceful man.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Guard innocence and behold uprightness, for there shall be peace at the last for the man.

Hebrew Trans: But transgressors shall be destroyed together; the future of the wicked shall be cut off.
Septuagint Trans: But the transgressors shall be utterly destroyed together; the remnants of the ungodly shall be utterly destroyed.
Gallican Psalter Trans: But the unjust shall utterly perish; the remnants of the wicked shall perish together.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: But the unjust shall be destroyed together, and the end of the wicked shall perish.

Hebrew Trans: And the salvation of the righteous is from the LORD; He is their stronghold in the time of trouble.
Septuagint Trans: But the salvation of the righteous is from the Lord, and he is their defender in a time of affliction.
Gallican Psalter Trans: But the salvation of the just is from the Lord, and he is their protector in the time of tribulation.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The salvation of the just is from the Lord, their strength in the time of tribulation.

Hebrew Trans: And the LORD helps them and delivers them; He delivers them from the wicked and saves them, because they take refuge in Him.
Septuagint Trans: And the Lord will help them and deliver them, and he will rescue them from sinners and save them, because they have hoped in him.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And the Lord will help them and deliver them, and he will rescue them from sinners and save them, because they have hoped in him.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And the Lord shall help them and shall save them from the wicked, because they have hoped in Him.

Hebrew Trans: A psalm of David, to bring to remembrance.
Septuagint Trans: A psalm of David, for remembrance, concerning the Sabbath.
Gallican Psalter Trans: A psalm of David, in remembrance, concerning the sabbath.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: A psalm of David, for a remembrance.

Hebrew Trans: O LORD, rebuke me not in your wrath, and chasten me not in your hot displeasure.
Septuagint Trans: O Lord, do not rebuke me in your anger, nor discipline me in your wrath.
Gallican Psalter Trans: O Lord, do not rebuke me in your fury, nor chastise me in your wrath.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: O Lord, do not rebuke me in your anger, nor chastise me in your fury.

Hebrew Trans: For your arrows have sunk into me, and your hand has come down upon me.
Septuagint Trans: For your arrows have been fixed in me, and you have laid your hand upon me.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For your arrows have been driven into me, and you have laid your hand heavily upon me.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For your arrows have been driven into me, and your hand has pressed upon me.

Hebrew Trans: There is no soundness in my flesh because of your indignation; there is no peace in my bones because of my sin.
Septuagint Trans: There is no healing in my flesh because of your anger; there is no peace in my bones because of my sins.
Gallican Psalter Trans: There is no health in my flesh because of your anger; there is no peace in my bones because of my sins.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: There is no health in my flesh before the face of your indignation; there is no peace in my bones before the face of my sin.

Hebrew Trans: For my iniquities have gone over my head; like a heavy burden they are too heavy for me.
Septuagint Trans: For my iniquities have risen above my head; like a heavy burden they have weighed heavily upon me.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For my iniquities have risen above my head; like a heavy burden they weigh heavily upon me.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For my iniquities have gone over my head; like a heavy burden they have been weighted upon me.

Hebrew Trans: My wounds stink and fester because of my foolishness.
Septuagint Trans: My wounds have grown foul and festered because of my foolishness.
Gallican Psalter Trans: My wounds have festered and corrupted, because of my foolishness.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: My wounds have rotted and festered before the face of my foolishness.

Hebrew Trans: I am bent and bowed down greatly; all the day I go about mourning.
Septuagint Trans: I was wretched and utterly bowed down; all the day long I went about mourning.
Gallican Psalter Trans: I am made wretched and bent over utterly; all the day long I walked about in sorrow.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: I am afflicted and bowed down exceedingly; all the day long I walked in mourning.

Hebrew Trans: For my loins are filled with burning, and there is no soundness in my flesh.
Septuagint Trans: For my loins were filled with mockings, and there is no healing in my flesh.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For my loins are filled with mockeries, and there is no health in my flesh.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For my loins are filled with shame, and there is no health in my flesh.

Hebrew Trans: I am benumbed and crushed exceedingly; I roar from the groaning of my heart.
Septuagint Trans: I was afflicted and humbled exceedingly; I roared from the groaning of my heart.
Gallican Psalter Trans: I am afflicted and humbled exceedingly; I roared from the groaning of my heart.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: I was awakened and afflicted exceedingly; I roared from the groaning of my heart.

Hebrew Trans: O Lord, all my desire is before you, and my sighing is not hidden from you.
Septuagint Trans: O Lord, before you is all my desire, and my groaning has not been hidden from you.
Gallican Psalter Trans: O Lord, before you is all my desire, and my groaning is not hidden from you.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: O Lord, all my desire is before your sight, and my groaning is not hidden from you.

Hebrew Trans: My heart throbs, my strength fails me; and the light of my eyes, even they are not with me.
Septuagint Trans: My heart was troubled; my strength has forsaken me, and the light of my eyes—even that is not with me.
Gallican Psalter Trans: My heart is troubled; my strength has forsaken me, and the light of my eyes—even that is not with me.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: My heart was in turmoil; my strength has forsaken me, and the light of my eyes, even that itself is not with me.

Hebrew Trans: My lovers and my friends stand aloof from my affliction, and my kinsmen stand afar off.
Septuagint Trans: My friends and my neighbors drew near and stood against me, and my nearest kin stood far off.
Gallican Psalter Trans: My friends and my neighbors drew near and stood against me, and those who were close to me stood far off.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: My dear ones and my friends stood as if against my plague, and my neighbors stood far off.

Hebrew Trans: And those who seek my life lay snares, and those who seek my hurt speak destruction and meditate deceits all the day long.
Septuagint Trans: And those who sought my life used violence, and those who sought evils against me spoke vanities and meditated on deceits all the day long.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And those who sought my life used violence, and those who sought evil against me spoke vanities, and meditated deceits all the day long.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And those seeking my life rushed upon me, and those investigating evils against me spoke treacheries, and meditated deceits all the day long.

Hebrew Trans: But I am like a deaf man, I do not hear; and like a mute man who does not open his mouth.
Septuagint Trans: But I, like a deaf man, did not hear, and like a mute man not opening his mouth.
Gallican Psalter Trans: But I, like a deaf man, did not hear, and was like a mute man not opening his mouth.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: But I, as though deaf, did not hear, and as one mute I did not open my mouth.

Hebrew Trans: And I am as a man who does not hear, and in whose mouth are no reproofs.
Septuagint Trans: And I became like a man who does not hear and who has no reproofs in his mouth.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And I became like a man who does not hear, and who has no reproofs in his mouth.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And I was as a man not hearing, nor having reproofs in his mouth.

Hebrew Trans: For in you, O LORD, I hope; you will answer, O Lord my God.
Septuagint Trans: For in you, O Lord, I have hoped; you will hear me, O Lord my God.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For in you, O Lord, I have hoped; you will hear me, O Lord my God.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For in you, O Lord, I waited; you will hear me, O Lord my God.

Hebrew Trans: For I said, lest they rejoice over me; when my foot slips, they magnify themselves against me.
Septuagint Trans: For I said, "Lest my enemies rejoice over me," and when my feet were shaken, they spoke boastfully against me.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For I said: lest at any time my enemies rejoice over me; and while my feet are shaken, they spoke great things against me.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For I said: lest perhaps they exult over me, and when my feet have wavered, they magnify themselves against me.

Hebrew Trans: For I am ready to stumble, and my pain is continually before me.
Septuagint Trans: For I am ready for scourges, and my pain is before me continually.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For I am prepared for scourges, and my sorrow is before me always.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For I am prepared for scourges, and my sorrow is before me always.

Hebrew Trans: For I will declare my iniquity; I am anxious because of my sin.
Septuagint Trans: For I will declare my iniquity, and I will be anxious about my sin.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For I will declare my iniquity, and I will think upon my sin.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For I will declare my iniquity; I will be anxious for my sin.

Hebrew Trans: And my enemies are lively and strong, and those who hate me wrongfully are multiplied.
Septuagint Trans: But my enemies live and have been strengthened over me, and those who hate me unjustly have been multiplied.
Gallican Psalter Trans: But my enemies live and are strengthened against me, and those who hate me unjustly are multiplied.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: But my enemies, living, are strengthened, and those hating me falsely are multiplied.

Hebrew Trans: And those who repay evil for good are adversaries to me, because I pursue what is good.
Septuagint Trans: Those who repay evil for good slandered me, because I pursued righteousness; and they cast me away, the beloved one, like a loathsome corpse.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Those who repay evil for good slandered me, because I followed goodness.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And those who repay evil for good opposed me, because I followed what is good.

Hebrew Trans: Do not forsake me, O LORD; O my God, be not far from me.
Septuagint Trans: Do not forsake me, O Lord my God; do not depart from me.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Do not forsake me, O Lord my God; do not depart from me.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Do not forsake me, O Lord my God; do not go far from me.

Hebrew Trans: Make haste to help me, O Lord, my salvation.
Septuagint Trans: Give heed to my help, O Lord of my salvation.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Attend to my help, O Lord of my salvation.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Hasten to my help, O Lord of my salvation.

Hebrew Trans: For the choirmaster, for Jeduthun, a psalm of David.
Septuagint Trans: To the end, for Idithun, a song of David.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Unto the end, for Idithun, a canticle of David.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For the victory; of Idithun; a canticle of David.

Hebrew Trans: I said, I will guard my ways, from sinning with my tongue; I will keep a muzzle on my mouth, while the wicked is before me.
Septuagint Trans: I said, I will guard my ways, so as not to sin with my tongue; I set a guard upon my mouth while the sinner stood before me.
Gallican Psalter Trans: I said: I will guard my ways, so that I do not offend with my tongue. I set a guard over my mouth, when the sinner stood against me.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: I said: I will guard my ways, that I may not sin with my tongue. I will guard my mouth with silence, while the wicked is before me.

Hebrew Trans: I was struck dumb in silence, I held my peace from good, and my pain was stirred.
Septuagint Trans: I was struck dumb and was humbled, and I kept silent even from good things, and my pain was renewed.
Gallican Psalter Trans: I became mute and was humbled, and I kept silence from good things, and my sorrow was renewed.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: I was mute with silence, I held my peace from good; and my sorrow was stirred up.

Hebrew Trans: My heart grew hot within me; while I was musing, the fire burned; I spoke with my tongue:
Septuagint Trans: My heart grew hot within me, and in my meditation a fire shall be kindled; I spoke with my tongue:
Gallican Psalter Trans: My heart grew hot within me, and in my meditation a fire shall blaze forth. I spoke with my tongue:
Hebrew Psalter Trans: My heart grew hot within me; in my meditation I was kindled with fire: I spoke with my tongue.

Hebrew Trans: Make me to know, O LORD, my end, and the measure of my days, what it is; let me know how fleeting I am.
Septuagint Trans: Make known to me, O Lord, my end, and the number of my days, what it is, that I may know what I lack.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Make known to me, O Lord, my end, and what is the number of my days, that I may know what is lacking to me.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Show me, O Lord, my end, and the measure of my days, what it may be, that I may know what I lack.

Hebrew Trans: Behold, you have made my days as handbreadths, and my lifetime is as nothing before you; surely all vanity is every man who stands. Selah.
Septuagint Trans: Behold, you have made my days as handbreadths, and my existence is as nothing before you; surely all things are vanity—every man living. Selah.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Behold, you have made my days measurable, and my substance is as nothing before you. Yet surely all is vanity: every man living. Selah.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Behold, you have made my days brief, and my life is as nothing in your sight. For all things are vanity: every man standing always.

Hebrew Trans: Surely as a shadow man walks about; surely in vain they are in turmoil; he heaps up and does not know who will gather them.
Septuagint Trans: Surely man walks about in a mere image; surely they are troubled in vain; he heaps up treasures and does not know for whom he shall gather them.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Surely man passes through as an image; moreover he is troubled in vain: he stores up treasures and knows not for whom he shall gather them.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Surely man walks in an image; surely he is troubled in vain. He heaps up, and knows not for whom he shall leave these things.

Hebrew Trans: And now, what do I wait for, O Lord? My hope is in you.
Septuagint Trans: And now, what is my expectation? Is it not the Lord? And my substance is from you.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And now what is my expectation? Is it not the Lord? And my substance is with you.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And now, what do I wait for, O Lord? My expectation is you.

Hebrew Trans: From all my transgressions deliver me; do not make me the reproach of the fool.
Septuagint Trans: Deliver me from all my transgressions; you have made me a reproach to the fool.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Deliver me from all my iniquities: you have made me a reproach to the fool.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Deliver me from all my iniquities; do not make me a reproach to the fool.

Hebrew Trans: I was struck dumb, I will not open my mouth, for it is you who have done it.
Septuagint Trans: I was struck dumb and did not open my mouth, because you are the one who made me.
Gallican Psalter Trans: I became mute and did not open my mouth, because you have done it.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: I was mute; I will not open my mouth, because you have done it.

Hebrew Trans: Remove from me your plague; from the blow of your hand I am consumed.
Septuagint Trans: Remove your scourges from me; from the strength of your hand I have fainted.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Remove your plagues from me.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Take away your plagues from me.

Hebrew Trans: With rebukes for iniquity you discipline a man, and you consume like a moth what is dear to him; surely all mankind is vanity. Selah.
Septuagint Trans: With rebukes for iniquity you disciplined man, and you made his soul waste away like a spider's web; surely every man is troubled in vain. Selah.
Gallican Psalter Trans: From the strength of your hand I have fainted. With rebukes you have corrected man for iniquity, and you have made his soul waste away like a spider. Yet surely every man is troubled in vain. Selah.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: From the striving of your hand I am consumed. With rebukes you have corrected a man for iniquity, and you have made his desirable things waste away like a moth. Surely every man is always vanity.

Hebrew Trans: Hear my prayer, O LORD, and give ear to my cry; be not silent at my tears; for I am a stranger with you, a sojourner, like all my fathers.
Septuagint Trans: Hear my prayer, O Lord, and give ear to my supplication; do not be silent at my tears, for I am a sojourner with you and a pilgrim, as all my fathers were.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Hear my prayer, O Lord, and my supplication; give ear to my tears. Be not silent, for I am a stranger with you, and a pilgrim, as all my fathers were.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Hear my prayer, O Lord, and give ear to my cry; be not deaf to my tears. For I am a stranger with you, and a pilgrim, as all my fathers were.

Hebrew Trans: Look away from me, that I may brighten up, before I depart and am no more.
Septuagint Trans: Spare me, that I may recover strength, before I depart and exist no more.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Forgive me, that I may be refreshed, before I depart, and shall be no more.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Spare me, that I may smile, before I go away and shall be no more.

Hebrew Trans: For the choirmaster. Of David. A psalm.
Septuagint Trans: For the end/director, of David, a psalm.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Unto the end, a psalm of David.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For the victory: a song of David.

Hebrew Trans: I waited earnestly for the LORD, and He inclined to me and heard my cry.
Septuagint Trans: Waiting I waited for the Lord, and he attended to me and heard my supplication.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Waiting I waited for the Lord, and he attended to me, and he heard my prayers.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Waiting, I waited for the Lord, and He inclined to me and heard my cry.

Hebrew Trans: And He brought me up out of the pit of destruction, out of the miry clay, and He set my feet upon a rock, making my steps firm.
Septuagint Trans: And he brought me up out of a pit of misery and out of miry clay, and he set my feet upon a rock and directed my steps.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And he brought me out of the pit of misery, and from the mire of dregs, and he set my feet upon a rock, and directed my steps.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And He brought me out of the pit of mire, out of the mud of filth, and He set my feet upon a rock and made firm my steps.

Hebrew Trans: And He put in my mouth a new song, a praise to our God; many shall see and fear, and shall trust in the LORD.
Septuagint Trans: And he put into my mouth a new song, a hymn to our God. Many shall see and shall fear and shall hope in the Lord.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And he put a new song into my mouth, a hymn to our God. Many shall see and shall fear, and they shall hope in the Lord.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And He put in my mouth a new song, a praise to our God. Many shall see and shall fear, and shall hope in the Lord.

Hebrew Trans: Blessed is the man who makes the LORD his trust, and does not turn to the proud, nor to those who go astray after falsehood.
Septuagint Trans: Blessed is the man whose hope is the name of the Lord, and who has not looked upon vanities and false frenzies.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Blessed is the man whose hope is the name of the Lord, and who has not looked upon vanities and false madnesses.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Blessed is the man who has made the Lord his trust, and has not turned aside to prides and the pomps of falsehood.

Hebrew Trans: Many things You have done, You, O LORD my God—Your wondrous deeds and Your thoughts toward us; none can compare to You. Were I to declare and speak of them, they are too numerous to count.
Septuagint Trans: Many things you have done, you, O Lord my God — your wonders — and in your thoughts there is none who shall be likened to you. I declared and I spoke; they are multiplied beyond number.
Gallican Psalter Trans: You have done many things, O Lord my God, your wonders, and in your thoughts there is none who is like you. I have declared and spoken: they are multiplied beyond number.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: You have done many things, O Lord my God: your wonders and your thoughts for us — I cannot find order before you. If I wished to declare and number them, they are more than can be told.

Hebrew Trans: Sacrifice and offering You did not desire; ears You have dug for me; burnt offering and sin offering You did not require.
Septuagint Trans: Sacrifice and offering you did not desire, but ears you prepared for me; burnt offering and offering for sin you did not require.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Sacrifice and offering you did not desire, but ears you have perfected for me; burnt offering and sin offering you did not require.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Sacrifice and offering you do not require; you have dug open ears for me. Burnt offering and sin offering you have not asked.

Hebrew Trans: Then I said, "Behold, I come; in the scroll of the book it is written concerning me.
Septuagint Trans: Then I said, "Behold, I come; in the scroll of the book it is written concerning me:
Gallican Psalter Trans: Then I said: Behold, I come. In the head of the book it is written of me,
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Then I said: Behold, I come. In the scroll of the book it is written of me,

Hebrew Trans: To do Your will, O my God, I have delighted, and Your law is within my inward parts."
Septuagint Trans: to do your will, O my God, I desired, and your law is in the midst of my belly."
Gallican Psalter Trans: that I should do your will. My God, I have willed it, and your law is in the midst of my heart.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: that I should do your pleasure, O my God, I have willed it, and your law is in the midst of my belly.

Hebrew Trans: I have proclaimed righteousness in the great assembly; behold, I do not restrain my lips—O LORD, You know.
Septuagint Trans: I proclaimed glad tidings of righteousness in the great assembly. Behold, I shall not restrain my lips — Lord, you know.
Gallican Psalter Trans: I have proclaimed justice in the great assembly. Behold, I will not restrain my lips: O Lord, you have known it.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: I have proclaimed righteousness in the great assembly; behold, I will not restrain my lips — Lord, you know.

Hebrew Trans: Your righteousness I have not hidden within my heart; Your faithfulness and Your salvation I have declared; I have not concealed Your lovingkindness and Your truth from the great assembly.
Septuagint Trans: Your righteousness I did not hide in my heart; your truth and your salvation I declared. I did not conceal your mercy and your truth from a great congregation.
Gallican Psalter Trans: I have not hidden your justice within my heart; your truth and your salvation I have declared. I have not concealed your mercy and your truth from the great council.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: I have not hidden your righteousness in the midst of my heart; I have declared your faithfulness and your salvation. I have not concealed your mercy and your truth in the great assembly.

Hebrew Trans: You, O LORD, will not withhold Your compassion from me; Your lovingkindness and Your truth will continually preserve me.
Septuagint Trans: But you, O Lord, do not withhold your compassions from me; your mercy and your truth have continually upheld me.
Gallican Psalter Trans: But you, O Lord, do not remove your compassions far from me; your mercy and your truth have always sustained me.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: You, O Lord, will not withhold your mercies from me; your mercy and your truth will continually preserve me.

Hebrew Trans: For evils have encompassed me beyond number; my iniquities have overtaken me, and I am not able to see; they are more numerous than the hairs of my head, and my heart has forsaken me.
Septuagint Trans: For evils surrounded me of which there is no number; my iniquities overtook me, and I was not able to see; they were multiplied beyond the hairs of my head, and my heart forsook me.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For evils without number have surrounded me; my iniquities have overtaken me, and I was not able to see. They are multiplied above the hairs of my head, and my heart has forsaken me.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For evils have surrounded me, of which there is no number; my iniquities have overtaken me, and I was not able to see. They have become more numerous than the hairs of my head, and my heart has forsaken me.

Hebrew Trans: Be pleased, O LORD, to deliver me; O LORD, make haste to help me.
Septuagint Trans: Be pleased, O Lord, to deliver me; O Lord, to help me, give heed.
Gallican Psalter Trans: May it please you, O Lord, to deliver me; O Lord, look to my aid.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Be pleased, O Lord, to deliver me; O Lord, make haste to help me.

Hebrew Trans: Let them be ashamed and confounded together who seek my soul to destroy it; let them be driven backward and put to shame who desire my hurt.
Septuagint Trans: Let them be ashamed and put to shame together who seek my soul to destroy it; let them be turned back and put to shame who desire evils against me.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Let them be confounded and ashamed together who seek my soul to take it away; let them be turned back and put to shame who wish me evil.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Let them be confounded and put to shame together who seek my soul to take it away; let them be turned back and confounded who wish me evil.

Hebrew Trans: Let them be appalled on account of their shame, those who say to me, "Aha! Aha!"
Septuagint Trans: Let them receive immediately their shame, those who say to me, "Well done, well done!"
Gallican Psalter Trans: Let them immediately bear their confusion, who say to me: "Well done, well done!"
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Let them perish after their confusion, those who say to me, "Aha! Aha!"

Hebrew Trans: Let all who seek You rejoice and be glad in You; let those who love Your salvation say continually, "The LORD be magnified!"
Septuagint Trans: Let all who seek you, O Lord, rejoice and be glad in you, and let those who love your salvation say continually, "Let the Lord be magnified!"
Gallican Psalter Trans: Let all who seek you exult and rejoice in you, and let those who love your salvation always say: The Lord be magnified.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Let all who seek you rejoice and be glad in you; let those who love your salvation say continually, "The Lord be magnified."

Hebrew Trans: But I am poor and needy; the Lord thinks upon me. You are my help and my deliverer; O my God, do not delay.
Septuagint Trans: But I am poor and needy; the Lord takes thought for me. My helper and my defender you are, O my God; do not delay.
Gallican Psalter Trans: But I am a beggar and poor; the Lord is solicitous for me. You are my helper and my protector; O my God, do not delay.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: But I am needy and poor; the Lord will be solicitous for me. You are my help and my salvation; O my God, do not delay.

Hebrew Trans: For the choirmaster, a psalm of David.
Septuagint Trans: For the end, a psalm of David.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Unto the end, a psalm of David.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For the victory, a song of David.

Hebrew Trans: Blessed is he who considers the poor; in the day of trouble the LORD will deliver him.
Septuagint Trans: Blessed is he who understands concerning the poor and needy; in the evil day the Lord shall deliver him.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Blessed is he who understands concerning the needy and the poor; in the evil day the Lord will deliver him.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Blessed is he who considers the poor; in the evil day the Lord will save him.

Hebrew Trans: The LORD will preserve him and keep him alive; he shall be called blessed upon the earth; and do not deliver him to the will of his enemies.
Septuagint Trans: May the Lord preserve him and give him life and make him blessed upon the earth, and may he not deliver him into the hands of his enemy.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The Lord preserve him and give him life, and make him blessed upon the earth, and deliver him not into the will of his enemies.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The Lord will keep him and give him life, and he shall be blessed upon the earth, and He will not deliver him into the soul of his enemies.

Hebrew Trans: The LORD will sustain him upon the bed of illness; all his lying place you have turned in his sickness.
Septuagint Trans: May the Lord help him upon the bed of his pain; you have turned all his bed in his sickness.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The Lord bring him help upon the bed of his sorrow; you have turned over all his bedding in his infirmity.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The Lord will strengthen him upon the bed of his infirmity; you have turned all his couch in his sickness.

Hebrew Trans: I said, "LORD, be gracious to me; heal my soul, for I have sinned against you."
Septuagint Trans: I said, "Lord, have mercy on me; heal my soul, for I have sinned against you."
Gallican Psalter Trans: I said: Lord, have mercy on me; heal my soul, for I have sinned against you.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: I said: Lord, have mercy on me; heal my soul, for I have sinned against you.

Hebrew Trans: My enemies speak evil of me: "When will he die, and his name perish?"
Septuagint Trans: My enemies spoke evil things against me: "When will he die, and his name perish?"
Gallican Psalter Trans: My enemies have spoken evil against me: when shall he die, and his name perish?
Hebrew Psalter Trans: My enemies will speak evil against me: when will he die, and his name perish?

Hebrew Trans: And if one comes to see me, he speaks falsehood; his heart gathers iniquity to itself; he goes out, and speaks of it.
Septuagint Trans: And if he came to see me, his heart spoke vanity; he gathered iniquity to himself; he went out and spoke.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And if he came in to see me, he spoke vain things; his heart gathered iniquity to itself. He went out and spoke it abroad.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And if he came to visit, he spoke vain things; his heart gathered iniquity to itself, and going out he would slander.

Hebrew Trans: Together all who hate me whisper against me; against me they devise evil for me.
Septuagint Trans: Together all my enemies whispered against me; against me they devised evil things for me.
Gallican Psalter Trans: All my enemies whispered together against me; against me they devised evil things for me.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Together all who hate me whispered against me; against me they devised evil.

Hebrew Trans: A wicked thing is poured out upon him, and now that he lies down, he shall rise up no more.
Septuagint Trans: They laid a lawless word against me: "Shall not he who sleeps no longer rise again?"
Gallican Psalter Trans: They set an unjust word against me: shall he who sleeps not rise again?
Hebrew Psalter Trans: They poured upon him a word of the devil: he who has slept, shall he not rise again?

Hebrew Trans: Even my close friend, in whom I trusted, who ate my bread, has lifted up his heel against me.
Septuagint Trans: For even the man of my peace, in whom I hoped, who ate my bread, lifted up his heel against me.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For even the man of my peace, in whom I trusted, who ate my bread, has greatly lifted up his heel against me.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: But even my peaceful man, in whom I trusted, who ate my bread, has lifted up his heel against me.

Hebrew Trans: But you, O LORD, be gracious to me and raise me up, that I may repay them.
Septuagint Trans: But you, Lord, have mercy on me and raise me up, and I shall repay them.
Gallican Psalter Trans: But you, O Lord, have mercy on me, and raise me up, and I shall repay them.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: But you, O Lord, have mercy on me, and raise me up, that I may repay them.

Hebrew Trans: By this I know that you delight in me, that my enemy does not shout in triumph over me.
Septuagint Trans: By this I have known that you have been pleased with me, because my enemy shall not rejoice over me.
Gallican Psalter Trans: By this I know that you have willed well for me, because my enemy shall not rejoice over me.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: By this I have known that you favor me, because my enemy shall not triumph over me.

Hebrew Trans: And as for me, in my integrity you have upheld me, and set me before your face forever.
Septuagint Trans: And because of my innocence you have upheld me and established me before you forever.
Gallican Psalter Trans: But me you have upheld because of my innocence, and you have established me in your sight forever.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: But as for me, in my innocence I have been helped by you, and you will establish me before your face forever.

Hebrew Trans: Blessed be the LORD, the God of Israel, from everlasting to everlasting. Amen and Amen.
Septuagint Trans: Blessed be the Lord God of Israel, from everlasting and to everlasting. So be it, so be it.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Blessed be the Lord God of Israel, from age to age. So be it. So be it.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Blessed be the Lord God of Israel, from age to age, amen, amen.

Hebrew Trans: For the choirmaster, a maskil of the sons of Korah.
Septuagint Trans: For the end, for understanding, for the sons of Korah.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Unto the end, for understanding, for the sons of Korah.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For the victory, a psalm of the most learned of the sons of Korah.

Hebrew Trans: As a deer longs for the water brooks, so my soul longs for you, O God.
Septuagint Trans: As the deer longs for the springs of waters, so my soul longs for you, O God.
Gallican Psalter Trans: As the deer longs for the springs of waters, so my soul longs for you, O God.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: As a garden bed prepared for the irrigation of waters, so my soul is prepared for you, O God.

Hebrew Trans: My soul thirsts for God, for the living God; when shall I come and appear before the face of God?
Septuagint Trans: My soul has thirsted for the living God; when shall I come and appear before the face of God?
Gallican Psalter Trans: My soul has thirsted for the strong, living God: when shall I come and appear before the face of God?
Hebrew Psalter Trans: My soul has thirsted for God, the strong, the living God: when shall I come and appear before your face?

Hebrew Trans: My tears have been my bread day and night, while they say to me all day long, "Where is your God?"
Septuagint Trans: My tears became my bread day and night, while it was said to me each day, "Where is your God?"
Gallican Psalter Trans: My tears have been my bread day and night, while it is said to me daily: "Where is your God?"
Hebrew Psalter Trans: My tears have been my bread day and night, while it was said to me all day long: "Where is your God?"

Hebrew Trans: These things I remember, and I pour out my soul within me: how I would pass along with the throng, leading them in procession to the house of God, with the voice of joy and thanksgiving, a multitude keeping festival.
Septuagint Trans: These things I remembered, and I poured out my soul upon me, for I shall pass through to the place of the wondrous tabernacle, even to the house of God, with a voice of rejoicing and thanksgiving, the sound of one keeping festival.
Gallican Psalter Trans: These things I remembered, and I poured out my soul within me: for I shall pass into the place of the wondrous tabernacle, even unto the house of God, with the voice of exultation and confession, the sound of one feasting.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: These things I have remembered, and I poured out my soul within me: for I shall come to the tabernacle, I shall be silent until I reach the house of God, amid the voice of praise and confession of the multitude celebrating the feast.

Hebrew Trans: Why are you cast down, O my soul, and why do you groan within me? Hope in God, for I shall yet praise him, the salvation of his countenance.
Septuagint Trans: Why are you deeply grieved, O soul, and why do you trouble me? Hope in God, for I shall give thanks to him; he is the salvation of my face, my God.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Why are you sad, O my soul, and why do you trouble me? Hope in God, for I shall confess to him, the salvation of my countenance.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Why are you bowed down, my soul, and why do you trouble me? Wait for the Lord, for I shall yet praise him, the salvation of his countenance.

Hebrew Trans: My God, my soul is cast down within me; therefore I remember you from the land of the Jordan and the Hermons, from Mount Mizar.
Septuagint Trans: My soul was troubled within myself; therefore I will remember you from the land of Jordan and the Hermons, from the small mountain.
Gallican Psalter Trans: O my God, my soul is troubled within myself: therefore I will remember you from the land of the Jordan and the Hermonites, from the small mountain.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: My God, within my very self my soul is bowed down; therefore I will remember you from the land of the Jordan and the Hermons, from the little mountain.

Hebrew Trans: Deep calls unto deep at the sound of your waterfalls; all your breakers and your waves have passed over me.
Septuagint Trans: Deep calls to deep at the sound of your cataracts; all your surges and your waves have passed over me.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Deep calls unto deep in the voice of your cataracts: all your heights and your waves have passed over me.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Deep calls unto deep at the voice of your cataracts; all your torrents and your waves have passed over me.

Hebrew Trans: By day the LORD commands his steadfast love, and at night his song is with me, a prayer to the God of my life.
Septuagint Trans: By day the Lord will command his mercy, and by night a song shall be with me, a prayer to the God of my life.
Gallican Psalter Trans: In the day the Lord commanded his mercy, and in the night his song is with me, a prayer to the God of my life.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: By day the Lord has commanded his mercy, and in the night his song is with me, a prayer to the God of my life.

Hebrew Trans: I will say to God, my rock, "Why have you forgotten me? Why do I go about mourning under the oppression of the enemy?"
Septuagint Trans: I will say to God, "You are my protector; why have you forgotten me? Why do I go about with a gloomy face while the enemy oppresses me?"
Gallican Psalter Trans: I will say to God: You are my protector; why have you forgotten me? Why do I go in sadness, while the enemy afflicts me?
Hebrew Psalter Trans: I will say to God, my rock: "Why have you forgotten me? Why do I walk in sorrow while the enemy afflicts me?"

Hebrew Trans: As with a crushing in my bones, my adversaries taunt me, while they say to me all day long, "Where is your God?"
Septuagint Trans: While they crushed my bones, those who afflicted me reproached me, while they said to me each day, "Where is your God?"
Gallican Psalter Trans: While my bones are broken, those who trouble me have reproached me, while they say to me each day: "Where is your God?"
Hebrew Psalter Trans: While they were slaying me in my bones, my enemies reproached me, saying all day long: "Where is your God?"

Hebrew Trans: Why are you cast down, O my soul, and why do you groan within me? Hope in God, for I shall yet praise him, the salvation of my countenance and my God.
Septuagint Trans: Why are you deeply grieved, O soul, and why do you trouble me? Hope in God, for I shall give thanks to him; he is the salvation of my face, my God.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Why are you sad, O my soul, and why do you trouble me? Hope in God, for I shall yet confess to him, the salvation of my countenance, and my God.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Why are you bowed down, my soul, and why do you trouble me? Wait for the Lord, for I shall yet praise him, the salvation of my countenance, and my God.

Hebrew Trans: Judge me, O God, and plead my cause against an ungodly nation; deliver me from the man of deceit and injustice.
Septuagint Trans: A psalm of David. Judge me, O God, and plead my cause against an unholy nation; deliver me from the unjust and deceitful man.
Gallican Psalter Trans: A psalm of David. Judge me, O God, and distinguish my cause from an unholy nation: from the unjust and deceitful man, deliver me.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Judge me, O God, and distinguish my cause from an unholy nation: from the deceitful and unjust man, save me.

Hebrew Trans: For You are the God of my strength; why have You cast me off? Why do I go about mourning under the oppression of the enemy?
Septuagint Trans: For you are the God of my strength; why have you cast me off? And why do I go about in gloom while the enemy oppresses me?
Gallican Psalter Trans: For you are the God of my strength: why have you cast me off? Why do I go about in sadness, while the enemy afflicts me?
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For you, O God, are my strength: why have you cast me off? Why do I go about in sadness, while the enemy afflicts me?

Hebrew Trans: Send out Your light and Your truth; let them lead me, let them bring me to Your holy mountain and to Your dwelling places.
Septuagint Trans: Send out your light and your truth; they have guided me and led me to your holy mountain and to your dwelling places.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Send forth your light and your truth: they have led me and brought me to your holy mountain and into your tabernacles.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Send forth your light and your truth: they themselves will lead me and bring me to your holy mountain and to your tabernacle.

Hebrew Trans: And I will go to the altar of God, to God, the gladness of my joy, and I will praise You upon the harp, O God, my God.
Septuagint Trans: And I shall go in to the altar of God, to the God who gladdens my youth; I shall give thanks to you on the harp, O God, my God.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And I will go in to the altar of God, to God who gives joy to my youth. I will praise you on the harp, O God, my God.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And I will go in to your altar, to the God of my gladness and my exultation: and I will praise you upon the harp, O God, my God.

Hebrew Trans: Why are you cast down, O my soul, and why do you moan within me? Hope in God, for I shall yet praise Him, the salvation of my countenance and my God.
Septuagint Trans: Why are you so sorrowful, O soul, and why do you trouble me? Hope in God, for I shall give thanks to him, the salvation of my countenance, my God.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Why are you sad, O my soul, and why do you trouble me? Hope in God, for I shall yet praise him, the salvation of my countenance, and my God.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Why are you bowed down, O my soul, and why do you trouble me? Hope in the Lord, for I shall yet praise him, the salvation of my countenance, and my God.

Hebrew Trans: For the choirmaster; of the sons of Korah; a Maskil.
Septuagint Trans: For the end; for the sons of Korah, for understanding; a psalm.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Unto the end, for the sons of Korah, for understanding.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For the victory of the sons of Korah, a song of instruction.

Hebrew Trans: O God, we have heard with our ears, our fathers have told us, the work You wrought in their days, in the days of old.
Septuagint Trans: O God, we have heard with our ears; our fathers have declared to us the work which you wrought in their days, in the days of old.
Gallican Psalter Trans: O God, with our ears we have heard: our fathers have declared to us the work that you wrought in their days, in the days of old.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: O God, with our ears we have heard; our fathers have told us the work that you performed in their days, in the days of old.

Hebrew Trans: You, with Your hand, dispossessed nations and planted them; You afflicted peoples and cast them out.
Septuagint Trans: Your hand destroyed nations, and you planted them; you afflicted peoples and drove them out.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Your hand destroyed the nations, and you planted them; you afflicted the peoples and drove them out.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: You with your hand destroyed the nations and planted them; you afflicted the peoples and drove them out.

Hebrew Trans: For not by their sword did they possess the land, and their own arm did not save them; but Your right hand and Your arm and the light of Your countenance, for You favored them.
Septuagint Trans: For not by their own sword did they inherit the land, and their own arm did not save them, but your right hand and your arm and the light of your countenance, because you were well pleased with them.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For not by their own sword did they possess the land, and their own arm did not save them; but your right hand and your arm and the light of your countenance, because you were well pleased in them.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For not by their own sword did they possess the land, nor did their own arm save them, but your right hand and your arm and the light of your countenance, because you were pleased with them.

Hebrew Trans: You are my King, O God; command the deliverances of Jacob.
Septuagint Trans: You are yourself my King and my God, the one who commands the salvations of Jacob.
Gallican Psalter Trans: You are yourself my king and my God, who commands the salvation of Jacob.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: You are my king, O God; command victories for Jacob.

Hebrew Trans: Through You we shall gore our adversaries; in Your name we shall trample those who rise up against us.
Septuagint Trans: Through you we shall gore our enemies, and in your name we shall bring to nothing those who rise up against us.
Gallican Psalter Trans: In you we shall scatter our enemies with the horn, and in your name we shall despise those who rise up against us.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Through you we shall scatter our enemies; in your name we shall trample down our adversaries.

Hebrew Trans: For I do not trust in my bow, and my sword will not save me.
Septuagint Trans: For I will not trust in my bow, and my sword shall not save me.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For I will not hope in my bow, and my sword will not save me.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For I will not trust in my bow, nor will my sword save me.

Hebrew Trans: For You have saved us from our foes, and those who hate us You have put to shame.
Septuagint Trans: For you have saved us from those who afflict us, and those who hate us you have put to shame.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For you have saved us from those who afflict us, and those who hate us you have put to shame.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For you have saved us from our enemies, and those who hated us you have put to shame.

Hebrew Trans: In God we have gloried all the day, and Your name we will praise forever. Selah.
Septuagint Trans: In God we shall be praised all the day long, and in your name we shall give thanks forever. Selah.
Gallican Psalter Trans: In God we shall be praised all the day long, and in your name we shall give thanks forever. Selah.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: In the Lord we shall rejoice all day long, and in your name we shall give thanks forever and always.

Hebrew Trans: Yet You have rejected and humiliated us, and You do not go out with our armies.
Septuagint Trans: But now you have cast us off and put us to shame, and you will not go out with our armies.
Gallican Psalter Trans: But now you have rejected and put us to shame, and you will not go out with our armies.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: But you have cast us off and put us to shame, and you will not go out with our armies.

Hebrew Trans: You cause us to retreat before the adversary, and those who hate us have plundered for themselves.
Septuagint Trans: You have turned us back before our enemies, and those who hate us plundered for themselves.
Gallican Psalter Trans: You have turned us back behind our enemies, and those who hate us plundered for themselves.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: You have turned our backs to the enemy, and those who hated us have plundered us.

Hebrew Trans: You give us like sheep for food, and among the nations You have scattered us.
Septuagint Trans: You have given us like sheep for food, and among the nations you have scattered us.
Gallican Psalter Trans: You have given us up like sheep for food, and among the nations you have scattered us.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: You have given us up like sheep to be devoured, and among the nations you have scattered us.

Hebrew Trans: You sell Your people for no wealth, and You have not increased Your profit by their price.
Septuagint Trans: You sold your people for nothing, and there was no abundance in the price paid for them.
Gallican Psalter Trans: You have sold your people without a price, and there was no great sum in the exchange for them.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: You have sold your people for no price, nor was the exchange of them great.

Hebrew Trans: You make us a reproach to our neighbors, a scorn and a derision to those around us.
Septuagint Trans: You have made us a reproach to our neighbors, a mockery and derision to those around us.
Gallican Psalter Trans: You have made us a reproach to our neighbors, a mockery and a derision to those who are around us.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: You have made us a reproach to our neighbors, a mockery and a derision to those who were around us.

Hebrew Trans: You make us a byword among the nations, a shaking of the head among the peoples.
Septuagint Trans: You have made us a byword among the nations, a shaking of the head among the peoples.
Gallican Psalter Trans: You have made us a byword among the nations, a shaking of the head among the peoples.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: You have made us a byword among the nations, a shaking of the head among the peoples.

Hebrew Trans: All the day my disgrace is before me, and the shame of my face has covered me,
Septuagint Trans: All the day long my shame is before me, and the disgrace of my face has covered me,
Gallican Psalter Trans: All the day long my shame is before me, and the confusion of my face has covered me,
Hebrew Psalter Trans: All day long my disgrace is before me, and the shame of my face has covered me,

Hebrew Trans: at the voice of the taunter and the blasphemer, because of the enemy and the avenger.
Septuagint Trans: at the voice of the one who reproaches and reviles, at the face of the enemy and the pursuer.
Gallican Psalter Trans: At the voice of him who reproaches and reviles, at the face of the enemy and the persecutor.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: At the voice of the one who reproaches and blasphemes, at the face of the enemy and the avenger.

Hebrew Trans: All this has come upon us, yet we have not forgotten You, and we have not been false to Your covenant.
Septuagint Trans: All these things have come upon us, and we have not forgotten you, and we have not dealt unjustly in your covenant.
Gallican Psalter Trans: All these things have come upon us, yet we have not forgotten you, and we have not dealt wickedly against your covenant.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: All these things have come upon us, yet we have not forgotten you, nor have we been false to your covenant.

Hebrew Trans: Our heart has not turned back, nor have our steps turned aside from Your path,
Septuagint Trans: And our heart has not turned back, and you have turned aside our paths from your way.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And our heart has not turned back, and you have turned aside our paths from your way.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Our heart has not turned back, nor have our steps turned aside from your path,

Hebrew Trans: though You have crushed us in a place of jackals and covered us over with the shadow of death.
Septuagint Trans: For you have humbled us in a place of affliction, and the shadow of death has covered us.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For you have humbled us in a place of affliction, and the shadow of death has covered us.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Though you have cast us down in the place of dragons and covered us with the shadow of death.

Hebrew Trans: If we had forgotten the name of our God and spread out our hands to a foreign god,
Septuagint Trans: If we had forgotten the name of our God, and if we had spread out our hands to a foreign god,
Gallican Psalter Trans: If we have forgotten the name of our God, and if we have stretched out our hands to a strange god,
Hebrew Psalter Trans: If we have forgotten the name of our God and stretched out our hands to a foreign god,

Hebrew Trans: would not God search this out? For He knows the secrets of the heart.
Septuagint Trans: would not God search this out? For he himself knows the hidden things of the heart.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Shall not God search this out? For he himself knows the hidden things of the heart.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Would not God search this out? For he himself knows the thoughts of the heart.

Hebrew Trans: For on account of You we are slain all the day; we are accounted as sheep for the slaughter.
Septuagint Trans: For on account of you we are put to death all the day long; we have been reckoned as sheep for slaughter.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For on account of you we are put to death all the day long; we are reckoned as sheep for the slaughter.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For on account of you we are put to death all day long; we are counted as sheep for the slaughter.

Hebrew Trans: Awake! Why do You sleep, O Lord? Arise! Do not reject us forever.
Septuagint Trans: Awake! Why do you sleep, O Lord? Arise, and do not cast us off forever.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Arise, why do you sleep, O Lord? Arise, and do not reject us to the end.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Arise! Why do you sleep, O Lord? Awake! Why do you cast us off forever?

Hebrew Trans: Why do You hide Your face? Why do You forget our affliction and our oppression?
Septuagint Trans: Why do you turn away your face? Do you forget our poverty and our affliction?
Gallican Psalter Trans: Why do you turn away your face? Why do you forget our need and our tribulation?
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Why do you hide your face? Why do you forget our afflictions and our distresses?

Hebrew Trans: For our soul is bowed down to the dust; our belly clings to the earth.
Septuagint Trans: For our soul has been humbled to the dust; our belly has cleaved to the earth.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For our soul has been humbled in the dust; our belly has been fastened to the earth.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For our soul is bowed down to the dust; our belly clings to the earth.

Hebrew Trans: Rise up, be our help, and redeem us for the sake of Your lovingkindness.
Septuagint Trans: Arise, O Lord, help us and redeem us for the sake of your name.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Arise, help us, and redeem us for the sake of your name.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Rise up, come to our aid, and redeem us for the sake of your mercy.

Hebrew Trans: For the choirmaster, upon Shoshannim, for the sons of Korah, a Maskil, a song of love.
Septuagint Trans: For the end, concerning the things that shall be changed, for the sons of Korah, for understanding; a song concerning the beloved.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Unto the end, for those who shall be changed, for the sons of Korah, for understanding; a song for the beloved.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: To the victor, for the lilies, of the sons of Korah, a song of instruction, of the most beloved.

Hebrew Trans: My heart overflows with a good word; I speak my works to the king; my tongue is the pen of a skilled scribe.
Septuagint Trans: My heart has uttered a good word; I speak of my works to the king; my tongue is the pen of a swift writer.
Gallican Psalter Trans: My heart has uttered a good word; I speak my works to the king; my tongue is the pen of a scribe writing swiftly.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: My heart has poured forth a good word; I speak my works to the king; my tongue is the pen of a swift scribe.

Hebrew Trans: You are fairer than the sons of men; grace is poured upon your lips; therefore God has blessed you forever.
Septuagint Trans: Beautiful in loveliness beyond the sons of men; grace has been poured out upon your lips; therefore God has blessed you forever.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Beautiful in form above the sons of men, grace is poured forth upon your lips; therefore God has blessed you forever.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: You are fairer in beauty than the sons of men; grace is poured out upon your lips; therefore God has blessed you forever.

Hebrew Trans: Gird your sword upon your thigh, O mighty one, your majesty and your splendor.
Septuagint Trans: Gird your sword upon your thigh, O mighty one, in your comeliness and your beauty.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Gird your sword upon your thigh, O most mighty one.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Gird your sword upon your thigh, O most mighty one.

Hebrew Trans: And in your splendor ride forth prosperously, on behalf of truth and meekness of righteousness; and let your right hand teach you awesome things.
Septuagint Trans: And bend your bow, and prosper, and reign, for the sake of truth and meekness and righteousness; and your right hand shall guide you wonderfully.
Gallican Psalter Trans: In your splendor and your beauty, both attend, proceed prosperously, and reign, on account of truth and meekness and justice; and your right hand shall lead you wonderfully.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: In your glory and your beauty, in your beauty ride forth prosperously, on behalf of truth and meekness and justice; and your right hand shall teach you terrible things.

Hebrew Trans: Your arrows are sharp—peoples fall beneath you—in the heart of the enemies of the king.
Septuagint Trans: Your arrows are sharpened, O mighty one; peoples shall fall beneath you, into the heart of the enemies of the king.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Your arrows are sharp; peoples shall fall under you, into the heart of the enemies of the king.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Your arrows are sharp; peoples shall fall beneath you, in the heart of the enemies of the king.

Hebrew Trans: Your throne, O God, is forever and ever; a scepter of uprightness is the scepter of your kingdom.
Septuagint Trans: Your throne, O God, is forever and ever; the scepter of uprightness is the scepter of your kingdom.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Your throne, O God, is forever and ever; the rod of direction is the rod of your kingdom.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Your throne, O God, is forever and ever; the scepter of equity is the scepter of your kingdom.

Hebrew Trans: You have loved righteousness and hated wickedness; therefore God, your God, has anointed you with the oil of gladness above your companions.
Septuagint Trans: You have loved righteousness and hated lawlessness; therefore God, your God, has anointed you with the oil of gladness beyond your companions.
Gallican Psalter Trans: You have loved justice and hated iniquity; therefore God, your God, has anointed you with the oil of gladness above your companions.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: You have loved justice and hated iniquity; therefore God, your God, has anointed you with the oil of exultation above your companions.

Hebrew Trans: Myrrh and aloes and cassia are all your garments; from ivory palaces stringed instruments have made you glad.
Septuagint Trans: Myrrh and drops of cassia from your garments, from ivory palaces, out of which they have gladdened you.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Myrrh and drop and cassia from your garments, from the ivory palaces, out of which they have delighted you.
