Hebrew Psalter Trans: Myrrh and stacte and cassia are in all your garments, from the ivory palaces, from which they have gladdened you.

Hebrew Trans: Daughters of kings are among your precious ones; the queen stands at your right hand in gold of Ophir.
Septuagint Trans: Daughters of kings are in your honor; the queen stood at your right hand, clothed in a gold-woven garment, adorned in varied colors.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The daughters of kings in your honor; the queen has stood at your right hand, in gilded clothing, surrounded with variety.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Daughters of kings are in your honor; the consort stood at your right hand, in a golden diadem.

Hebrew Trans: Hear, O daughter, and see, and incline your ear; and forget your people and your father's house.
Septuagint Trans: Hear, O daughter, and see, and incline your ear, and forget your people and your father's house.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Hear, O daughter, and see, and incline your ear; and forget your people and your father's house.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Hear, O daughter, and see, and incline your ear, and forget your people and your father's house.

Hebrew Trans: And the king shall desire your beauty, for he is your lord; and bow down to him.
Septuagint Trans: For the king has desired your beauty, for he is your lord.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And the king shall desire your beauty, for he is your lord, and they shall worship him.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And the king shall desire your beauty, for he is your lord, and worship him.

Hebrew Trans: And the daughter of Tyre with a gift—the rich among the people shall entreat your favor.
Septuagint Trans: And the daughters of Tyre shall worship him with gifts; the rich of the people shall entreat your face.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And the daughters of Tyre with gifts shall entreat your face; the rich ones of the people.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And O daughter of the most mighty, the rich of the people shall entreat your face with gifts.

Hebrew Trans: All glorious is the king's daughter within; her clothing is of settings of gold.
Septuagint Trans: All the glory of her, the daughter of the king, is within, clothed in golden fringes, adorned in varied colors.
Gallican Psalter Trans: All the glory of the king's daughter is from within, in golden fringes,
Hebrew Psalter Trans: All the glory of the king's daughter is within; she is clothed in golden fringes.

Hebrew Trans: In embroidered garments she shall be brought to the king; the virgins behind her, her companions, are brought to you.
Septuagint Trans: Virgins shall be brought to the king after her; those near her shall be brought to you.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Clothed about with varieties; virgins shall be brought to the king after her; her companions shall be brought to you.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: In embroidered garments she shall be led to the king; the virgins following her, her companions, shall be brought there.

Hebrew Trans: They shall be brought with gladness and rejoicing; they shall enter the palace of the king.
Septuagint Trans: They shall be brought with gladness and rejoicing; they shall be led into the temple of the king.
Gallican Psalter Trans: They shall be brought with gladness and exultation; they shall be led into the temple of the king.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: They shall be brought with gladness and exultation; they shall enter the chamber of the king.

Hebrew Trans: In place of your fathers shall be your sons; you shall make them princes in all the earth.
Septuagint Trans: In place of your fathers, sons have been born to you; you shall appoint them as rulers over all the earth.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Instead of your fathers, sons have been born to you; you shall establish them as princes over all the earth.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: In place of your fathers, sons shall be born to you; you shall make them princes over all the earth.

Hebrew Trans: I will cause your name to be remembered in every generation and generation; therefore the peoples shall praise you forever and ever.
Septuagint Trans: They shall remember your name in every generation and generation; therefore peoples shall give thanks to you forever and unto the age of the age.
Gallican Psalter Trans: I will be mindful of your name in every generation and generation; therefore the peoples shall praise you forever and unto the age of ages.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: I shall remember your name in every generation and generation; therefore the peoples shall praise you forever and ever.

Hebrew Trans: For the choirmaster, of the sons of Korah; upon Alamoth, a song.
Septuagint Trans: For the end, on behalf of the sons of Korah, concerning the hidden things, a psalm.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Unto the end, for the sons of Korah, for the hidden things, a psalm.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For the victor, of the sons of Korah, for the young women, a song.

Hebrew Trans: God is our refuge and strength, a help in troubles, found exceedingly.
Septuagint Trans: Our God is a refuge and strength, a helper in the afflictions that have found us exceedingly.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Our God is our refuge and strength, a helper in the tribulations that have found us exceedingly.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: God is our hope and strength; you have been found a mighty help in tribulations.

Hebrew Trans: Therefore we will not fear when the earth changes, and when the mountains totter into the heart of the seas.
Septuagint Trans: Therefore we will not fear when the earth is troubled and mountains are moved into the hearts of the seas.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Therefore we will not fear while the earth is troubled and the mountains are transferred into the heart of the sea.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Therefore we will not fear when the earth has been moved and the mountains shaken into the heart of the sea.

Hebrew Trans: Let its waters roar and foam; let the mountains tremble at its swelling. Selah.
Septuagint Trans: Their waters roared and were troubled; the mountains were shaken by his might. Selah.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Their waters roared and were troubled; the mountains were shaken by his strength. Diapsalma.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Though its waters roar and swell, and the mountains be shaken by its power forever.

Hebrew Trans: A river—its streams make glad the city of God, the holy dwelling place of the Most High.
Septuagint Trans: The rushings of the river gladden the city of God; the Most High has sanctified his dwelling.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The rush of the river makes glad the city of God; the Most High has sanctified his tabernacle.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The streams of the river make glad the city of God, the holy tabernacle of the Most High.

Hebrew Trans: God is in her midst; she shall not be moved. God will help her at the turn of morning.
Septuagint Trans: God is in the midst of her; she shall not be shaken. God will help her at the turning of the morning.
Gallican Psalter Trans: God is in the midst of it; it shall not be moved: God will help it in the morning, at daybreak.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The Lord is in the midst of her; she shall not be moved. God will help her at the very break of morning.

Hebrew Trans: Nations raged, kingdoms tottered; He uttered His voice, the earth melts.
Septuagint Trans: Nations were troubled, kingdoms tottered; he uttered his voice, the earth was shaken.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The nations were troubled, the kingdoms were bowed down; he uttered his voice, the earth was moved.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The nations were troubled, the kingdoms were shaken; he uttered his voice, the earth was laid low.

Hebrew Trans: The LORD of hosts is with us; the God of Jacob is a stronghold for us. Selah.
Septuagint Trans: The Lord of hosts is with us; our protector is the God of Jacob. Selah.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The Lord of hosts is with us; our protector is the God of Jacob. Diapsalma.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The Lord of hosts is with us; our protector is the God of Jacob forever.

Hebrew Trans: Come, behold the works of the LORD, who has wrought desolations in the earth.
Septuagint Trans: Come, behold the works of the Lord, what wonders he has set upon the earth.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Come and see the works of the Lord, what wonders he has set upon the earth.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Come and see the works of the Lord, what desolations he has made in the earth.

Hebrew Trans: He makes wars to cease to the end of the earth; He breaks the bow and cuts the spear in two; the chariots He burns with fire.
Septuagint Trans: Removing wars to the ends of the earth; he will shatter the bow and break the weapon, and will burn shields with fire.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Taking away wars even to the end of the earth; he shall break the bow and shall shatter weapons, and shields he shall burn in fire.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: He has made wars to cease unto the end of the earth; he will break the bow and shatter the spear; he will burn the chariots with fire.

Hebrew Trans: Be still, and know that I am God; I will be exalted among the nations, I will be exalted in the earth.
Septuagint Trans: Be still and know that I am God; I will be exalted among the nations, I will be exalted in the earth.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Be still and see that I am God; I will be exalted among the nations, I will be exalted in the earth.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Be still and know that I am God; I will be exalted among the nations, I will be exalted in the earth.

Hebrew Trans: The LORD of hosts is with us; the God of Jacob is a stronghold for us. Selah.
Septuagint Trans: The Lord of hosts is with us; our protector is the God of Jacob.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The Lord of hosts is with us; our protector is the God of Jacob.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The Lord of hosts is with us; our strength is the God of Jacob forever.

Hebrew Trans: For the choirmaster; of the sons of Korah, a psalm.
Septuagint Trans: To the end, on behalf of the sons of Korah, a psalm.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Unto the end, for the sons of Korah, a psalm.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: To the victor, of the sons of Korah, a canticle.

Hebrew Trans: All you peoples, clap your hands; shout unto God with a voice of joyful singing.
Septuagint Trans: All you nations, clap your hands; shout aloud to God with a voice of exultation.
Gallican Psalter Trans: All you nations, clap your hands; shout unto God with a voice of exultation.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: All you peoples, clap your hands; shout joyfully to God with a voice of praise.

Hebrew Trans: For the LORD Most High is awesome, a great king over all the earth.
Septuagint Trans: For the Lord Most High is fearful, a great king over all the earth.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For the Lord is most high, terrible, a great king over all the earth.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For the Lord most high is terrible, a great king over all the earth.

Hebrew Trans: He subdues peoples under us, and nations under our feet.
Septuagint Trans: He subdued peoples under us, and nations under our feet.
Gallican Psalter Trans: He has subdued peoples under us, and nations under our feet.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: He has gathered the peoples beneath us, and the tribes under our feet.

Hebrew Trans: He chooses for us our inheritance, the pride of Jacob whom he loves. Selah.
Septuagint Trans: He chose for us his inheritance, the beauty of Jacob which he loved. Selah.
Gallican Psalter Trans: He has chosen for us his inheritance, the beauty of Jacob which he loved. Selah.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: He chose for us our inheritance, the glory of Jacob which he loved always.

Hebrew Trans: God has gone up with a shout, the LORD with the sound of a trumpet.
Septuagint Trans: God has gone up with a shout; the Lord with the sound of a trumpet.
Gallican Psalter Trans: God has ascended with jubilation, the Lord with the sound of the trumpet.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: God ascends amid jubilation, God with the sound of the trumpet.

Hebrew Trans: Sing praises to God, sing praises; sing praises to our King, sing praises.
Septuagint Trans: Sing praises to our God, sing praises; sing praises to our King, sing praises.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Sing praises to our God, sing praises; sing praises to our king, sing praises.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Sing to God, sing; sing to our king, sing.

Hebrew Trans: For God is king of all the earth; sing praises with understanding.
Septuagint Trans: For God is king of all the earth; sing praises with understanding.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For God is king of all the earth; sing praises wisely.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For God is king of all the earth; sing wisely.

Hebrew Trans: God reigns over the nations; God sits upon his holy throne.
Septuagint Trans: God has reigned over the nations; God sits upon his holy throne.
Gallican Psalter Trans: God has reigned over the nations; God has sat upon his holy throne.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: God reigns over the nations; God sits upon his holy throne.

Hebrew Trans: The nobles of the peoples are gathered together, the people of the God of Abraham; for the shields of the earth belong to God; he is greatly exalted.
Septuagint Trans: The rulers of the peoples were gathered together with the God of Abraham, for the mighty ones of the earth belong to God; they were greatly exalted.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The princes of the peoples have gathered together with the God of Abraham, for the mighty ones of God belong to the earth; they are exceedingly exalted.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The princes of the peoples are gathered together, the people of the God of Abraham; for the shields of the earth belong to God, and they are greatly exalted.

Hebrew Trans: A song, a psalm of the sons of Korah.
Septuagint Trans: A psalm of song for the sons of Korah, for the second day of the Sabbath.
Gallican Psalter Trans: A canticle psalm for the sons of Korah, for the second day of the sabbath.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: A canticle psalm of the sons of Korah.

Hebrew Trans: Great is the LORD, and greatly to be praised, in the city of our God, His holy mountain.
Septuagint Trans: Great is the Lord and greatly to be praised, in the city of our God, on His holy mountain.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Great is the Lord, and exceedingly worthy of praise, in the city of our God, in his holy mountain.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Great is the Lord, and exceedingly to be praised, in the city of our God, on his holy mountain.

Hebrew Trans: Beautiful in elevation, the joy of all the earth, is Mount Zion, on the sides of the north, the city of the great King.
Septuagint Trans: Well-rooted in the joy of all the earth; the mountains of Zion, the sides of the north, the city of the great King.
Gallican Psalter Trans: It is founded with the joy of the whole earth; Mount Zion, on the sides of the north, the city of the great king.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: With beautiful growth, the joy of the whole earth, Mount Zion, on the sides of the north, the little city of the great king.

Hebrew Trans: God in her palaces has made Himself known as a stronghold.
Septuagint Trans: God is known in her citadels, when He takes hold of her.
Gallican Psalter Trans: God shall be known in her houses, when he shall receive her.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: God in her houses has made himself known as a defender.

Hebrew Trans: For behold, the kings assembled; they passed by together.
Septuagint Trans: For behold, the kings assembled; they came together.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For behold, the kings were assembled together; they gathered into one.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For behold, the kings assembled together, they came as one.

Hebrew Trans: They saw, and so they were astounded; they were terrified, they hastened away.
Septuagint Trans: They, having seen, were thus amazed; they were troubled, they were shaken.
Gallican Psalter Trans: They themselves, seeing it, were so amazed; they were troubled, they were shaken.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: They, seeing thus, were astounded; they were troubled, they were amazed.

Hebrew Trans: Trembling seized them there, anguish as of a woman in travail.
Septuagint Trans: Trembling seized them there, pangs as of a woman in labor.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Trembling seized them there, pains as of a woman in labor.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Horror seized them there, pain as of a woman in labor.

Hebrew Trans: With an east wind You shatter the ships of Tarshish.
Septuagint Trans: With a violent wind You will shatter the ships of Tarshish.
Gallican Psalter Trans: With a vehement wind you shall shatter the ships of Tarshish.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: With a scorching wind you will shatter the ships of the sea.

Hebrew Trans: As we have heard, so have we seen, in the city of the LORD of hosts, in the city of our God; God will establish it forever. Selah.
Septuagint Trans: Just as we have heard, so we have seen, in the city of the Lord of hosts, in the city of our God; God has founded her forever. Selah.
Gallican Psalter Trans: As we have heard, so we have seen, in the city of the Lord of hosts, in the city of our God; God has founded her forever. Selah.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: As we have heard, so we have seen, in the city of the Lord of hosts, in the city of our God; God has founded her forever and ever.

Hebrew Trans: We have pondered, O God, Your lovingkindness, in the midst of Your temple.
Septuagint Trans: We have considered, O God, Your mercy in the midst of Your temple.
Gallican Psalter Trans: We have received, O God, your mercy, in the midst of your temple.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: We have contemplated, O God, your mercy in the midst of your temple.

Hebrew Trans: As is Your name, O God, so is Your praise, unto the ends of the earth; Your right hand is full of righteousness.
Septuagint Trans: According to Your name, O God, so also is Your praise unto the ends of the earth; Your right hand is full of righteousness.
Gallican Psalter Trans: According to your name, O God, so also is your praise unto the ends of the earth; your right hand is full of justice.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: According to your name, O God, so does your praise reach to the ends of the earth; your right hand is filled with justice.

Hebrew Trans: Let Mount Zion rejoice, let the daughters of Judah be glad, because of Your judgments.
Septuagint Trans: Let Mount Zion rejoice; let the daughters of Judea be glad, because of Your judgments, O Lord.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Let Mount Zion rejoice, let the daughters of Judah exult, because of your judgments, O Lord.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Let Mount Zion rejoice, let the daughters of Judah exult, because of your judgments.

Hebrew Trans: Walk about Zion and go around her; count her towers.
Septuagint Trans: Go around Zion and encompass her; recount her towers.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Surround Zion and encompass her; recount her towers.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Surround Zion and go around her; count her towers.

Hebrew Trans: Set your heart upon her ramparts; go through her palaces, that you may tell it to the generation following.
Septuagint Trans: Set your hearts upon her strength, and examine her citadels, so that you may recount it to another generation.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Set your hearts upon her strength, and examine her houses, that you may declare it to the next generation.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Set your hearts upon her ramparts; examine her palaces, that you may tell of it to the next generation.

Hebrew Trans: For this God is our God forever and ever; He will guide us beyond death.
Septuagint Trans: For this is God, our God, forever and unto the age of ages; He Himself shall shepherd us unto the ages.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For this is God, our God, forever and unto the age of ages; he himself shall rule us for all ages.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For this God is our God forever and in perpetuity; he himself shall be our guide even unto death.

Hebrew Trans: For the choirmaster; of the sons of Korah; a psalm.
Septuagint Trans: For the end; a psalm for the sons of Korah.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Unto the end, a psalm for the sons of Korah.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: To the victor; of the sons of Korah, a song.

Hebrew Trans: Hear this, all you peoples; give ear, all you inhabitants of the world,
Septuagint Trans: Hear these things, all you nations; give ear, all you who inhabit the world,
Gallican Psalter Trans: Hear these things, all nations; perceive with your ears, all you who inhabit the world:
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Hear this, all you peoples; perceive with your ears, all you inhabitants of the west.

Hebrew Trans: Both low-born and high-born, rich and poor together.
Septuagint Trans: both the earth-born and the sons of men, rich and poor together.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Both earthborn and sons of men, rich and poor together.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Both the sons of Adam and the sons of each one, together rich and poor.

Hebrew Trans: My mouth shall speak wisdom, and the meditation of my heart shall be of understanding.
Septuagint Trans: My mouth shall speak wisdom, and the meditation of my heart shall speak understanding.
Gallican Psalter Trans: My mouth shall speak wisdom, and the meditation of my heart shall speak understanding.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: My mouth shall speak wisdoms, and the meditation of my heart shall speak prudences.

Hebrew Trans: I will incline my ear to a parable; I will open my riddle upon the harp.
Septuagint Trans: I will incline my ear to a parable; I will open my riddle upon the psaltery.
Gallican Psalter Trans: I will incline my ear to a parable; I will open my proposition upon the psaltery.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: I will incline my ear to a parable; I will open my riddle upon the harp.

Hebrew Trans: Why should I fear in days of evil, when the iniquity of those who dog my heels surrounds me?
Septuagint Trans: Why should I fear in the evil day? The iniquity of my heel shall surround me.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Why shall I fear in the evil day? The iniquity of my heel shall surround me.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Why shall I fear in the days of evil? The iniquity of my heel shall surround me.

Hebrew Trans: Those who trust in their wealth and boast in the abundance of their riches—
Septuagint Trans: Those who trust in their own strength and boast in the abundance of their wealth—
Gallican Psalter Trans: They who trust in their own strength and glory in the multitude of their riches—
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Those who have trust in their own strength and boast in the multitude of their riches—

Hebrew Trans: No man can by any means redeem his brother, nor give to God a ransom for him—
Septuagint Trans: A brother does not redeem; shall a man redeem? He shall not give to God his ransom,
Gallican Psalter Trans: A brother does not redeem; shall a man redeem? He shall not give to God his appeasement,
Hebrew Psalter Trans: A man redeeming shall not redeem a brother, nor shall he give to God a propitiation for him,

Hebrew Trans: For the redemption of their soul is costly, and it fails forever—
Septuagint Trans: nor the price of the redemption of his soul.
Gallican Psalter Trans: nor the price of the redemption of his soul; and he shall labor forever,
Hebrew Psalter Trans: nor the price of the redemption of their souls; but he shall rest forever,

Hebrew Trans: That he should live on forever and not see the pit.
Septuagint Trans: And he has ceased forever—and shall he live to the end? For he shall not see destruction when he sees the wise dying.
Gallican Psalter Trans: and shall he yet live to the end? He shall not see destruction.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: and he shall live on in perpetuity and shall not see destruction,

Hebrew Trans: For he sees that wise men die; together the fool and the brutish perish and leave their wealth to others.
Septuagint Trans: Together the fool and the senseless shall perish, and they shall leave their wealth to strangers.
Gallican Psalter Trans: When he shall see wise men dying, the fool and the senseless shall likewise perish, and they shall leave their riches to strangers.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: when he shall see the wise dying. Together the fool and the unlearned shall perish and shall leave their riches to strangers.

Hebrew Trans: Their inward thought is that their houses shall endure forever, their dwelling places to all generations; they call their lands after their own names.
Septuagint Trans: And their graves are their houses forever, their dwelling places to generation and generation; they have called their lands after their own names.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And their sepulchres shall be their houses forever, their dwellings from generation to generation; they have called their names upon their lands.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Their inward thought is that their houses shall endure forever, their dwellings from generation to generation; they have called their lands by their own names.

Hebrew Trans: But man in his pomp will not endure; he is like the beasts that perish.
Septuagint Trans: And man, being in honor, did not understand; he was compared to the senseless beasts and was made like them.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And man, when he was in honor, did not understand; he is compared to the senseless beasts, and is made like unto them.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And man, though he be in honor, shall not abide; he is likened to the beasts and is made equal to them.

Hebrew Trans: This is the way of those who are foolish, and of those after them who approve their sayings. Selah.
Septuagint Trans: This way of theirs is a stumbling block to them, and afterward they will take pleasure in their own mouth. Selah.
Gallican Psalter Trans: This way of theirs is a stumbling block to them, and afterward they shall take pleasure in their own mouth. Selah.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: This is the way of their foolishness, and after them they shall ever run according to their mouth.

Hebrew Trans: Like sheep they are appointed for Sheol; death shall be their shepherd; and the upright shall have dominion over them in the morning, and their form shall be consumed in Sheol, far from their dwelling.
Septuagint Trans: Like sheep they are laid in Hades; death shall shepherd them, and the upright shall have dominion over them in the morning, and their help shall grow old in Hades, far from their glory.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Like sheep they are placed in hell; death shall feed upon them, and the just shall have dominion over them in the morning; and their help shall grow old in hell, away from their glory.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Like a flock they are placed in hell; death shall feed upon them, and the upright shall have dominion over them in the morning, and their form shall be consumed in hell after their dwelling place.

Hebrew Trans: But God will redeem my soul from the hand of Sheol, for he will receive me. Selah.
Septuagint Trans: But God shall redeem my soul from the hand of Hades, when he receives me. Selah.
Gallican Psalter Trans: But indeed God shall redeem my soul from the hand of hell, when he shall receive me. Selah.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: But truly God shall redeem my soul from the hand of hell, when he shall receive me forever.

Hebrew Trans: Do not be afraid when a man becomes rich, when the glory of his house increases;
Septuagint Trans: Do not fear when a man becomes rich, and when the glory of his house is multiplied;
Gallican Psalter Trans: Fear not when a man shall be made rich, and when the glory of his house shall be multiplied.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Do not fear when a man is made rich, when the glory of his house is multiplied.

Hebrew Trans: For when he dies he shall carry nothing away; his glory shall not descend after him.
Septuagint Trans: for when he dies he shall take nothing, nor shall his glory descend with him.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For when he shall die, he shall take nothing away, nor shall his glory descend with him.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For when he dies he shall take nothing away, nor shall his glory descend after him.

Hebrew Trans: For while he lives he blesses his soul, and men will praise you when you do well for yourself.
Septuagint Trans: For his soul shall be blessed in his lifetime; he will praise you when you do good to him.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For his soul shall be blessed in his lifetime; he will confess to you when you shall do well unto him.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For he shall bless his own soul in his lifetime; they shall praise you, they will say, when you do well for yourself.

Hebrew Trans: He shall go to the generation of his fathers; they shall never see light.
Septuagint Trans: He shall enter unto the generation of his fathers; unto eternity he shall not see light.
Gallican Psalter Trans: He shall enter even unto the generations of his fathers; he shall never see light unto eternity.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: He shall enter even unto the generations of his fathers; unto the end they shall not see the light.

Hebrew Trans: Man in his pomp, yet without understanding, is like the beasts that perish.
Septuagint Trans: Man, being in honor, did not understand; he was compared to the senseless beasts and was made like them.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Man, when he was in honor, did not understand; he is compared to the senseless beasts, and is made like unto them.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Man, when he was in honor, did not understand; he compared himself to the beasts and shall be silenced.

Hebrew Trans: A psalm of Asaph. God, God, the LORD, has spoken and called the earth, from the rising of the sun to its setting.
Septuagint Trans: A psalm of Asaph. The God of gods, the Lord, has spoken and called the earth from the rising of the sun even to its setting.
Gallican Psalter Trans: A psalm of Asaph. The God of gods, the Lord, has spoken and called the earth from the rising of the sun to its setting.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: A song of Asaph. The mighty God, the Lord, has spoken and called the earth from the rising of the sun to its setting.

Hebrew Trans: Out of Zion, the perfection of beauty, God has shone forth.
Septuagint Trans: Out of Zion is the beauty of his splendor. God shall come manifestly.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Out of Zion, the beauty of his comeliness. God manifestly
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Out of Zion, perfect in beauty, God has appeared.

Hebrew Trans: Let our God come and not keep silence; fire devours before Him, and around Him it storms greatly.
Septuagint Trans: Our God, and he shall not keep silence; a fire shall burn before him, and around him a mighty tempest.
Gallican Psalter Trans: shall come; our God shall not be silent. Fire shall blaze forth in his sight, and around him a mighty tempest.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Our God shall come and shall not keep silence; fire shall devour before him, and around him a mighty tempest.

Hebrew Trans: He calls to the heavens above and to the earth, to judge His people.
Septuagint Trans: He shall summon the heaven above and the earth to judge his people.
Gallican Psalter Trans: He shall call the heavens from above, and the earth, to discern his people.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: He shall call the heavens from above, and the earth, that he may judge his people.

Hebrew Trans: "Gather My faithful ones to Me, those who have made a covenant with Me by sacrifice."
Septuagint Trans: Gather to him his holy ones, those who have established his covenant upon sacrifices.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Gather to him his saints, who order his covenant above sacrifices.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Gather to me my saints, who make a covenant with me by sacrifice.

Hebrew Trans: And the heavens declare His righteousness, for God Himself is judge. Selah.
Septuagint Trans: And the heavens shall declare his righteousness, for God is judge. Selah.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And the heavens shall declare his justice, for God is the judge. Selah.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And the heavens shall declare his righteousness, for God is judge forever.

Hebrew Trans: "Hear, O My people, and I will speak; O Israel, and I will testify against you: God, your God, am I.
Septuagint Trans: Hear, my people, and I will speak to you; Israel, and I will testify to you: I am God, your God.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Hear, my people, and I will speak to you; Israel, and I will testify to you: I am God, your God.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Hear, O my people, and I will speak; O Israel, and I will testify against you: I am God, your God.

Hebrew Trans: Not for your sacrifices do I rebuke you, and your burnt offerings are continually before Me.
Septuagint Trans: I will not rebuke you for your sacrifices, and your burnt offerings are before me continually.
Gallican Psalter Trans: I will not rebuke you for your sacrifices, for your burnt offerings are always in my sight.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: I will not rebuke you for your sacrifices, and your burnt offerings are before me always.

Hebrew Trans: I will not take a bull from your house, nor male goats from your pens.
Septuagint Trans: I will not accept calves from your house, nor he-goats from your flocks.
Gallican Psalter Trans: I will not accept calves from your house, nor he-goats from your flocks.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: I will not accept a calf from your house, nor he-goats from your flocks.

Hebrew Trans: For every beast of the forest is Mine, the cattle on a thousand hills.
Septuagint Trans: For all the beasts of the forest are mine, the cattle on the mountains and the oxen.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For all the wild beasts of the forests are mine, the cattle on the mountains and the oxen.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For all the beasts of the forests are mine, the cattle on the mountains of thousands.

Hebrew Trans: I know every bird of the mountains, and the creatures of the field are with Me.
Septuagint Trans: I have known all the birds of the heaven, and the beauty of the field is with me.
Gallican Psalter Trans: I know all the birds of the sky, and the beauty of the field is with me.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: I know all the birds of the mountains, and the fullness of the field is with me.

Hebrew Trans: If I were hungry, I would not tell you, for the world is Mine and all that fills it.
Septuagint Trans: If I should hunger, I would not tell you, for the world is mine and its fullness.
Gallican Psalter Trans: If I should hunger, I will not tell you, for the world is mine, and its fullness.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: If I were hungry, I would not tell you, for the world is mine and its fullness.

Hebrew Trans: Do I eat the flesh of bulls, or drink the blood of male goats?
Septuagint Trans: Shall I eat the flesh of bulls, or shall I drink the blood of goats?
Gallican Psalter Trans: Shall I eat the flesh of bulls, or shall I drink the blood of goats?
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Shall I eat the flesh of bulls, or drink the blood of goats?

Hebrew Trans: Offer to God a sacrifice of thanksgiving, and pay your vows to the Most High.
Septuagint Trans: Sacrifice to God a sacrifice of praise, and pay your vows to the Most High.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Offer to God a sacrifice of praise, and pay your vows to the Most High.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Sacrifice to God praise, and pay your vows to the Most High.

Hebrew Trans: And call upon Me in the day of trouble; I will deliver you, and you shall glorify Me."
Septuagint Trans: And call upon me in the day of affliction, and I will deliver you, and you shall glorify me. Selah.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And call upon me in the day of tribulation, and I will deliver you, and you shall honor me. Selah.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And call upon me in the day of tribulation; I will deliver you, and you shall glorify me.

Hebrew Trans: But to the wicked God says: "What right have you to recite My statutes, and to take My covenant upon your lips?
Septuagint Trans: But to the sinner God said: Why do you declare my ordinances and take up my covenant in your mouth?
Gallican Psalter Trans: But to the sinner God said: Why do you declare my statutes and take up my covenant through your mouth?
Hebrew Psalter Trans: But to the wicked God has said: What is it to you to declare my precepts, and to take my covenant in your mouth?

Hebrew Trans: For you hate instruction and cast My words behind you.
Septuagint Trans: But you have hated instruction and cast my words behind you.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For you have hated discipline and have cast my words behind you.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: You who have hated discipline and cast my words behind you.

Hebrew Trans: When you see a thief, you run with him, and with adulterers is your portion.
Septuagint Trans: If you saw a thief, you ran together with him, and with adulterers you set your portion.
Gallican Psalter Trans: If you saw a thief, you ran with him, and with adulterers you placed your portion.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: If you saw a thief, you consented with him, and with adulterers was your portion.

Hebrew Trans: You let loose your mouth in evil, and your tongue frames deceit.
Septuagint Trans: Your mouth abounded in evil, and your tongue wove deceit.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Your mouth abounded with malice, and your tongue fashioned deceits.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: You let loose your mouth to malice, and your tongue fashioned deceit.

Hebrew Trans: You sit and speak against your brother; against your own mother's son you give slander.
Septuagint Trans: Sitting down, you spoke against your brother, and against the son of your mother you set a stumbling block.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Sitting, you spoke against your brother, and against the son of your mother you set a stumbling block.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Sitting, you spoke against your brother, and against the son of your mother you devised reproach.

Hebrew Trans: These things you have done, and I kept silence; you thought that I was altogether like you. I will rebuke you and set it in order before your eyes.
Septuagint Trans: These things you did, and I kept silence; you supposed lawlessness, that I would be like you. I will rebuke you and set them before your face.
Gallican Psalter Trans: These things you did, and I was silent. You thought, wicked one, that I would be like you. I will rebuke you and set it before your face.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: These things you did, and I was silent; you thought that I would be like you. I will rebuke you and set it before your eyes.

Hebrew Trans: Consider this now, you who forget God, lest I tear you apart and there be none to deliver.
Septuagint Trans: Understand now these things, you who forget God, lest he seize you and there be no one to deliver.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Understand now these things, you who forget God, lest he seize you and there be none to deliver.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Understand this, you who forget God, lest I seize you and there be none to deliver.

Hebrew Trans: He who offers a sacrifice of thanksgiving honors Me, and to him who orders his way aright I will show the salvation of God."
Septuagint Trans: A sacrifice of praise shall glorify me, and there is the way by which I will show him the salvation of God.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The sacrifice of praise shall honor me, and there is the way by which I shall show him the salvation of God.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: He who sacrifices a confession of praise glorifies me, and he who walks in right order, I will show him the salvation of God.

Hebrew Trans: For the choirmaster, a psalm of David.
Septuagint Trans: For the end, a psalm of David.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Unto the end, a psalm of David.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: To the victor, a song of David.

Hebrew Trans: When Nathan the prophet came to him, after he had gone in to Bathsheba.
Septuagint Trans: When Nathan the prophet came to him, when he had gone in to Bathsheba.
Gallican Psalter Trans: When Nathan the prophet came to him, after he had gone in to Bathsheba.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: When Nathan the prophet had come to him, after he had gone in to Bathsheba.

Hebrew Trans: Be gracious to me, O God, according to your lovingkindness; according to the multitude of your mercies, blot out my transgressions.
Septuagint Trans: Have mercy on me, O God, according to your great mercy, and according to the multitude of your compassions, blot out my transgression.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Have mercy on me, O God, according to your great mercy; and according to the multitude of your compassions, blot out my iniquity.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Have mercy on me, O God, according to your mercy; according to the multitude of your compassions, blot out my iniquities.

Hebrew Trans: Wash me thoroughly from my iniquity, and cleanse me from my sin.
Septuagint Trans: Wash me yet more from my lawlessness, and cleanse me from my sin.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Wash me more fully from my iniquity, and cleanse me from my sin.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Wash me thoroughly from my iniquity, and cleanse me from my sin.

Hebrew Trans: For my transgressions I know, and my sin is before me continually.
Septuagint Trans: For I know my lawlessness, and my sin is before me continually.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For I acknowledge my iniquity, and my sin is always before me.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For I know my iniquities, and my sin is ever before me.

Hebrew Trans: Against you, you alone, have I sinned, and done what is evil in your sight, so that you are justified in your speaking, and blameless in your judging.
Septuagint Trans: Against you alone have I sinned, and done evil before you, so that you may be justified in your words and prevail when you are judged.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Against you alone have I sinned, and I have done evil before you, so that you may be justified in your words and may prevail when you are judged.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Against you alone have I sinned, and done evil before you, that you may be justified in your words and may prevail when you judge.

Hebrew Trans: Behold, in iniquity I was brought forth, and in sin my mother conceived me.
Septuagint Trans: For behold, I was conceived in iniquities, and in sins my mother bore me.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For behold, I was conceived in iniquities, and in sins did my mother conceive me.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Behold, in iniquity I was conceived, and in sin my mother bore me.

Hebrew Trans: Behold, you desire truth in the inward parts, and in the hidden part you make me know wisdom.
Septuagint Trans: For behold, you have loved truth; the hidden and secret things of your wisdom you have made known to me.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For behold, you have loved truth; the uncertain and hidden things of your wisdom you have made manifest to me.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Behold, for you love truth; the hidden and secret things of your wisdom you have made manifest to me.

Hebrew Trans: Purge me with hyssop, and I shall be clean; wash me, and I shall be whiter than snow.
Septuagint Trans: You shall sprinkle me with hyssop, and I shall be cleansed; you shall wash me, and I shall be made whiter than snow.
Gallican Psalter Trans: You will sprinkle me with hyssop, and I shall be cleansed; you will wash me, and I shall be made whiter than snow.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: You shall sprinkle me with hyssop, and I shall be cleansed; you shall wash me, and I shall be made whiter than snow.

Hebrew Trans: Make me hear joy and gladness; let the bones you have crushed rejoice.
Septuagint Trans: You shall make me hear joy and gladness; the bones that have been humbled shall rejoice.
Gallican Psalter Trans: To my hearing you will give joy and gladness; my humbled bones shall exult.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: You shall make me hear joy and gladness, that the bones which you have broken may rejoice.

Hebrew Trans: Hide your face from my sins, and blot out all my iniquities.
Septuagint Trans: Turn your face away from my sins, and blot out all my iniquities.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Turn your face away from my sins, and blot out all my iniquities.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Hide your face from my sins, and blot out all my iniquities.

Hebrew Trans: Create in me a clean heart, O God, and renew a steadfast spirit within me.
Septuagint Trans: Create in me a clean heart, O God, and renew a right spirit within my inward parts.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Create a clean heart in me, O God, and renew a right spirit within my inmost being.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Create in me a clean heart, O God, and renew a steadfast spirit within my inmost being.

Hebrew Trans: Do not cast me away from your presence, and do not take your Holy Spirit from me.
Septuagint Trans: Do not cast me away from your presence, and do not take your Holy Spirit from me.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Cast me not away from your face, and take not your holy spirit from me.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Cast me not away from your face, and take not your holy spirit from me.

Hebrew Trans: Restore to me the joy of your salvation, and let a willing spirit sustain me.
Septuagint Trans: Restore to me the joy of your salvation, and establish me with a governing spirit.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Restore to me the joy of your salvation, and strengthen me with a sovereign spirit.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Restore to me the joy of your Jesus, and strengthen me with a powerful spirit.

Hebrew Trans: I will teach transgressors your ways, and sinners shall return to you.
Septuagint Trans: I will teach the lawless your ways, and the ungodly shall turn back to you.
Gallican Psalter Trans: I will teach the unjust your ways, and the wicked shall be converted to you.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: I will teach the wicked your ways, and sinners shall return to you.

Hebrew Trans: Deliver me from bloodguilt, O God, God of my salvation, and my tongue shall sing aloud of your righteousness.
Septuagint Trans: Deliver me from bloodguilt, O God, the God of my salvation; my tongue shall rejoice in your righteousness.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Deliver me from blood-guilt, O God, the God of my salvation; my tongue shall exult in your justice.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Deliver me from bloodshed, O God, God of my salvation; my tongue shall praise your righteousness.

Hebrew Trans: O Lord, open my lips, and my mouth shall declare your praise.
Septuagint Trans: O Lord, you shall open my lips, and my mouth shall declare your praise.
Gallican Psalter Trans: O Lord, you will open my lips, and my mouth shall proclaim your praise.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: O Lord, you shall open my lips, and my mouth shall declare your praise.

Hebrew Trans: For you do not desire sacrifice, or I would give it; a burnt offering you do not delight in.
Septuagint Trans: For if you had desired sacrifice, I would have given it; in whole burnt offerings you will not take pleasure.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For if you had desired sacrifice, I would indeed have given it; with burnt offerings you will not be delighted.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For you do not desire that I should offer a victim, nor does a holocaust please you.

Hebrew Trans: The sacrifices of God are a broken spirit; a broken and contrite heart, O God, you will not despise.
Septuagint Trans: A sacrifice to God is a broken spirit; a broken and humbled heart God will not despise.
Gallican Psalter Trans: A sacrifice to God is a troubled spirit; a contrite and humbled heart, O God, you will not despise.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The sacrifice of God is a troubled spirit; a contrite and humbled heart, O God, you will not despise.

Hebrew Trans: Do good in your favor unto Zion; build the walls of Jerusalem.
Septuagint Trans: Do good, O Lord, in your good pleasure to Zion, and let the walls of Jerusalem be built up.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Deal favorably, O Lord, in your good will with Zion, and let the walls of Jerusalem be built up.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Deal favorably, O Lord, in your good will with Zion, and let the walls of Jerusalem be built up.

Hebrew Trans: Then you shall delight in sacrifices of righteousness, burnt offering and whole offering; then bulls shall be offered upon your altar.
Septuagint Trans: Then you shall be pleased with a sacrifice of righteousness, with offering and whole burnt offerings; then they shall lay calves upon your altar.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Then you will accept the sacrifice of justice, oblations and burnt offerings; then they shall lay calves upon your altar.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Then you shall accept the sacrifice of righteousness, offerings and holocausts; then they shall lay calves upon your altar.

Hebrew Trans: For the chief musician, a maskil of David.
Septuagint Trans: For the end, a psalm of understanding, of David.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Unto the end, a psalm of understanding of David.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: To the victor, by the learned David.

Hebrew Trans: When Doeg the Edomite came and told Saul, and said to him, "David has come to the house of Ahimelech."
Septuagint Trans: When Doek the Edomite came and reported to Saul and said to him, "David has come to the house of Abimelech."
Gallican Psalter Trans: When Doeg the Edomite came and announced to Saul, and said: David has come into the house of Ahimelech.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: When Doeg the Edomite had come and had reported to Saul, saying to him: David has come into the house of Ahimelech.

Hebrew Trans: Why do you boast in evil, O mighty man? The steadfast love of God endures all the day.
Septuagint Trans: Why do you boast in wickedness, O mighty one? Lawlessness all the day long.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Why do you glory in malice, you who are mighty in iniquity?
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Why do you glory in malice, you who are mighty? The mercy of God endures all the day.

Hebrew Trans: Your tongue devises destruction, like a sharpened razor, working deceit.
Septuagint Trans: Your tongue has devised injustice; like a sharpened razor you have wrought deceit.
Gallican Psalter Trans: All day long your tongue has devised injustice: like a sharp razor, you have wrought deceit.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Your tongue devises treacheries, like a sharp razor, working deceit.

Hebrew Trans: You love evil more than good, falsehood more than speaking righteousness. Selah.
Septuagint Trans: You have loved evil more than goodness, injustice more than speaking righteousness. Selah.
Gallican Psalter Trans: You have loved malice above goodness, iniquity more than speaking equity. Selah.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: You have loved evil more than good, falsehood more than speaking justice always.

Hebrew Trans: You love all devouring words, O deceitful tongue.
Septuagint Trans: You have loved all words of destruction, a deceitful tongue.
Gallican Psalter Trans: You have loved all words of ruin, a deceitful tongue.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: You have loved all devouring words, O deceitful tongue.

Hebrew Trans: God will also tear you down forever; He will snatch you up and pluck you from your tent, and uproot you from the land of the living. Selah.
Septuagint Trans: Therefore God will pull you down to the end; he will pluck you out and uproot you from your dwelling, and your root from the land of the living. Selah.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Therefore God will destroy you unto the end; he will pluck you out and remove you from your dwelling, and your root from the land of the living. Selah.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: But God will destroy you forever; He will terrify you and pluck you out of your dwelling, and will uproot you from the land of the living forever.

Hebrew Trans: And the righteous shall see and fear, and shall laugh at him:
Septuagint Trans: And the righteous shall see and shall fear, and shall laugh at him and shall say:
Gallican Psalter Trans: The just shall see and shall fear, and they shall laugh over him and say:
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The just shall see and shall fear, and shall laugh over him:

Hebrew Trans: "Behold the man who would not make God his stronghold, but trusted in the abundance of his riches, and strengthened himself in his destruction."
Septuagint Trans: "Behold, the man who did not make God his helper, but trusted in the abundance of his wealth and strengthened himself in his vanity."
Gallican Psalter Trans: Behold the man who did not make God his helper, but trusted in the abundance of his riches, and prevailed in his vanity.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Behold the man who did not make God his strength, but trusted in the abundance of his riches and strengthened himself in his treacheries.

Hebrew Trans: But I am like a green olive tree in the house of God; I trust in the lovingkindness of God forever and ever.
Septuagint Trans: But I am like a fruitful olive tree in the house of God; I have hoped in the mercy of God forever and unto the age of ages.
Gallican Psalter Trans: But I, like a fruitful olive tree in the house of God, have trusted in the mercy of God forever and unto the age of ages.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: But I, like a flourishing olive tree in the house of God, have trusted in the mercy of God for ever and ever.

Hebrew Trans: I will give thanks to you forever, because you have done it; and I will hope in your name, for it is good, in the presence of your faithful ones.
Septuagint Trans: I will give thanks to you forever, because you have acted; and I will wait upon your name, for it is good, in the presence of your holy ones.
Gallican Psalter Trans: I will give thanks to you forever, because you have done it; and I will wait upon your name, for it is good in the sight of your saints.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: I will give thanks to you forever, because you have done it; and I will wait upon your name, for it is good in the sight of your saints.

Hebrew Trans: For the chief musician, upon Mahalath, a Maskil of David.
Septuagint Trans: For the end, concerning Maeleth, of understanding, belonging to David.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Unto the end, for Melech, of the understanding of David.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: To the victor, through the choir, of the learned David.

Hebrew Trans: The fool has said in his heart, "There is no God." They have corrupted themselves and have done abominable iniquity; there is none who does good.
Septuagint Trans: The fool said in his heart, "There is no God." They were corrupted and became abominable in their lawlessness; there is none who does good.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The fool has said in his heart: There is no God. They are corrupt and have become abominable in their iniquities; there is none who does good.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The fool has said in his heart: There is no God. They are corrupt and have become abominable in iniquity; there is none who does good.

Hebrew Trans: God looked down from heaven upon the children of men, to see if there were any who understood, who sought God.
Septuagint Trans: God looked down from heaven upon the sons of men, to see if there is one who understands or seeks after God.
Gallican Psalter Trans: God looked down from heaven upon the children of men, to see if there is any who understands, or who seeks God.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: God looked down from heaven upon the children of men, to see if there were any who understood, seeking God.

Hebrew Trans: They have all turned aside; together they have become corrupt; there is none who does good, not even one.
Septuagint Trans: All have turned aside; together they have become worthless. There is none who does good, there is not even one.
Gallican Psalter Trans: All have turned aside; together they have become useless. There is none who does good, there is not even one.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: All have turned aside; together they have become worthless. There is none who does good, there is not even one.

Hebrew Trans: Do the workers of iniquity not know, who eat up my people as they eat bread? They have not called upon God.
Septuagint Trans: Will not all the workers of lawlessness come to know — those who devour my people as they eat bread? They did not call upon God.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Shall not all those who work iniquity know, who devour my people as the food of bread? They have not called upon God.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Do they not know, those who work iniquity, who eat up my people as they eat bread? They have not called upon God.

Hebrew Trans: There they were in great fear, where no fear had been; for God has scattered the bones of him who encamps against you; you have put them to shame, for God has rejected them.
Septuagint Trans: There they shall fear with a fear where there was no fear, for God has scattered the bones of those who please men; they were put to shame, for God has brought them to nothing.
Gallican Psalter Trans: There they shall tremble with fear, where there was no fear; for God has scattered the bones of those who please men; they have been confounded, for God has despised them.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: There they trembled with fear where there was no fear; for God has scattered the bones of those who encamped against you; you were put to shame, because God has rejected them.

Hebrew Trans: Oh that the salvations of Israel would come out of Zion! When God brings back the captivity of His people, let Jacob rejoice, let Israel be glad.
Septuagint Trans: Who will give out of Zion the salvation of Israel? When the Lord brings back the captivity of his people, Jacob shall rejoice and Israel shall be glad.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Who shall give out of Zion the salvation of Israel? When God shall turn back the captivity of his people, Jacob shall rejoice, and Israel shall be glad.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Who will give out of Zion the salvation of Israel? When God brings back the captivity of his people, Jacob shall exult, Israel shall be glad.

Hebrew Trans: For the choirmaster, with stringed instruments, a Maskil of David.
Septuagint Trans: For the end, in hymns, of understanding, by David.
Gallican Psalter Trans: To the end, in songs of understanding, of David.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: To the victor, in psalms, of the learned David.

Hebrew Trans: When the Ziphites came and said to Saul, "Is not David hiding among us?"
Septuagint Trans: When the Ziphites came and said to Saul, "Is not David hiding among us?"
Gallican Psalter Trans: When the Ziphites had come and said to Saul, "Is not David hidden among us?"
Hebrew Psalter Trans: When the Ziphites came and said to Saul, "Is not David hidden among us?"

Hebrew Trans: O God, by Your name save me, and by Your might vindicate me.
Septuagint Trans: O God, in your name save me, and in your power judge me.
Gallican Psalter Trans: O God, in your name save me, and in your strength judge me.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: O God, in your name save me, and in your strength avenge me.

Hebrew Trans: O God, hear my prayer; give ear to the words of my mouth.
Septuagint Trans: O God, hear my prayer; give ear to the words of my mouth.
Gallican Psalter Trans: O God, hear my prayer; with your ears perceive the words of my mouth.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: O God, hear my prayer; with your ears perceive the words of my mouth.

Hebrew Trans: For strangers have risen up against me, and ruthless men have sought my life; they have not set God before them. Selah.
Septuagint Trans: For strangers have risen up against me, and mighty men have sought my soul; they have not set God before them. Selah.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For strangers have risen up against me, and the mighty have sought my soul; they have not set God before their sight. Selah.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For strangers have risen up against me, and the mighty have sought my soul, and they have not set God before their sight always.

Hebrew Trans: Behold, God is my helper; the Lord is among those who sustain my soul.
Septuagint Trans: For behold, God helps me, and the Lord is the supporter of my soul.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For behold, God helps me, and the Lord is the upholder of my soul.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Behold, God is my helper; the Lord is the sustainer of my soul.

Hebrew Trans: He will repay the evil to my adversaries; in Your faithfulness, cut them off.
Septuagint Trans: He shall turn back the evils upon my enemies; in your truth destroy them.
Gallican Psalter Trans: He shall turn back evils upon my enemies; in your truth destroy them.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: He shall repay evil to my enemies; in your truth destroy them.

Hebrew Trans: With a freewill offering I will sacrifice to You; I will give thanks to Your name, O LORD, for it is good.
Septuagint Trans: Willingly I will sacrifice to you; I will give thanks to your name, O Lord, for it is good.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Willingly I shall sacrifice to you; I shall confess your name, O Lord, for it is good.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Willingly I will sacrifice to you; I will praise your name, O Lord, for it is good.

Hebrew Trans: For from every trouble He has delivered me, and upon my enemies my eye has looked.
Septuagint Trans: For out of all affliction you have delivered me, and my eye has looked upon my enemies.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For out of all tribulation you have delivered me, and my eye has looked down upon my enemies.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For from every tribulation he has delivered me, and my eye has looked down upon my enemies.

Hebrew Trans: For the choirmaster, with stringed instruments. A Maskil of David.
Septuagint Trans: For the end, in hymns, of understanding, of David.
Gallican Psalter Trans: To the end, in songs of understanding, of David.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: To the victor, in psalms, of the learned David.

Hebrew Trans: Give ear, O God, to my prayer, and do not hide yourself from my supplication.
Septuagint Trans: Give ear, O God, to my prayer, and do not overlook my supplication.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Hear my prayer, O God, and do not despise my supplication.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Hear, O God, my prayer, and do not despise my supplication.

Hebrew Trans: Attend to me and answer me; I am restless in my complaint and I moan,
Septuagint Trans: Attend to me and hear me; I was grieved in my meditation and I was troubled
Gallican Psalter Trans: Attend to me and hear me; I am grieved in my distress and I am troubled,
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Attend to me and hear me; I am brought low in my meditation and I am troubled,

Hebrew Trans: Because of the voice of the enemy, because of the oppression of the wicked; for they bring down trouble upon me, and in anger they bear a grudge against me.
Septuagint Trans: at the voice of the enemy and at the oppression of the sinner, for they turned iniquity upon me, and in anger they bore a grudge against me.
Gallican Psalter Trans: by the voice of the enemy and by the tribulation of the sinner, for they have turned iniquity upon me, and in anger they were hostile to me.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: by the voice of the enemy, by the face of the persecuting wicked one; for they have cast iniquity upon me, and in fury they opposed me.

Hebrew Trans: My heart writhes within me, and the terrors of death have fallen upon me.
Septuagint Trans: My heart was troubled within me, and the fear of death fell upon me.
Gallican Psalter Trans: My heart is troubled within me, and the fear of death has fallen upon me.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: My heart was pained within my vitals, and the terrors of death fell upon me.

Hebrew Trans: Fear and trembling come upon me, and horror has overwhelmed me.
Septuagint Trans: Fear and trembling came upon me, and darkness covered me.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Fear and trembling came upon me, and darkness covered me.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Fear and trembling came upon me, and darkness covered me.

Hebrew Trans: And I said, "Oh that I had wings like a dove! I would fly away and be at rest.
Septuagint Trans: And I said, "Who will give me wings like a dove? And I will fly away and find rest."
Gallican Psalter Trans: And I said: "Who will give me wings like a dove, and I will fly away and find rest?"
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And I said: "Who will give me wings like a dove, that I may fly away and be at rest?"

Hebrew Trans: Behold, I would wander far off; I would lodge in the wilderness. Selah.
Septuagint Trans: Behold, I went far away in flight, and I lodged in the wilderness. Selah.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Behold, I fled far away and remained in the wilderness. Selah.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: "That I may go far off and dwell in the wilderness forever."

Hebrew Trans: I would hasten to my refuge from the raging wind and tempest."
Septuagint Trans: I waited for him who saves me from faintheartedness and from the tempest.
Gallican Psalter Trans: I waited for him who saved me from faintheartedness of spirit and from the tempest.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: "I will hasten that I may be saved from the spirit of tempest and storm."

Hebrew Trans: Confuse, O Lord, and divide their tongues, for I have seen violence and strife in the city.
Septuagint Trans: Drown them, O Lord, and divide their tongues, for I have seen lawlessness and strife in the city.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Cast them down, O Lord, divide their tongues, for I have seen iniquity and strife in the city.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Cast them down, O Lord, divide their tongues; for I have seen iniquity and strife in the city.

Hebrew Trans: Day and night they go around it upon its walls; and iniquity and trouble are in its midst.
Septuagint Trans: Day and night it shall surround her upon her walls; iniquity and trouble are in the midst of her, and injustice.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Day and night it shall surround it upon its walls, and iniquity and toil are in the midst of it,
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Day and night they go around its walls; wickedness and sorrow are in the midst of it.

Hebrew Trans: Destruction is in its midst; oppression and deceit do not depart from its square.
Septuagint Trans: And usury and deceit have not departed from her streets.
Gallican Psalter Trans: and injustice; and usury and deceit have not departed from its streets.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Treachery is in its vitals, and harm and deceit do not depart from its streets.

Hebrew Trans: For it is not an enemy who taunts me—then I could bear it; it is not one who hates me who magnifies himself against me—then I could hide from him.
Septuagint Trans: For if an enemy had reproached me, I would have endured it; and if he who hates me had spoken boastfully against me, I would have hidden myself from him.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For if an enemy had cursed me, I would indeed have endured it; and if he who hated me had spoken great things against me, I would perhaps have hidden myself from him.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For it was not an enemy who reproached me, that I might endure it; nor was it one who hated me who magnified himself over me, that I might hide from him.

Hebrew Trans: But it is you, a man my equal, my companion and my close friend.
Septuagint Trans: But it was you, O man, my equal, my leader and my familiar friend,
Gallican Psalter Trans: But it was you, a man of one mind with me, my guide and my close friend,
Hebrew Psalter Trans: But it was you, a man of one mind with me, my guide and my close friend,

Hebrew Trans: We who together made sweet counsel; in the house of God we walked in the throng.
Septuagint Trans: who together with me made sweet the food; in the house of God we walked in harmony.
Gallican Psalter Trans: who together with me took sweet food; in the house of God we walked in agreement.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: who together with me shared sweet counsel; in the house of God we walked in dread.

Hebrew Trans: Let death come upon them; let them go down alive to Sheol, for evil is in their dwelling, in their midst.
Septuagint Trans: Let death come upon them, and let them go down alive into Hades, for wickedness is in their dwellings, in the midst of them.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Let death come upon them, and let them descend into hell alive, for there are wickednesses in their dwellings, in the midst of them.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Let death come upon them; let them go down alive into hell, for there is wickedness in their gathering and in the midst of them.

Hebrew Trans: As for me, I will call upon God, and the LORD shall save me.
Septuagint Trans: But I cried out to God, and the Lord heard me.
Gallican Psalter Trans: But I cried out to God, and the Lord will save me.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: I will cry out to God, and the Lord will save me.

Hebrew Trans: Evening and morning and at noon I will complain and moan, and He shall hear my voice.
Septuagint Trans: Evening and morning and at noon I will recount, I will declare, and he shall hear my voice.
Gallican Psalter Trans: In the evening and in the morning and at midday I will speak and declare, and he will hear my voice.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: In the evening and in the morning and at midday I will speak and resound, and He will hear my voice.

Hebrew Trans: He has redeemed my soul in peace from the battle against me, for they were many who strove with me.
Septuagint Trans: He shall redeem my soul in peace from those who draw near to me, for among many they were with me.
Gallican Psalter Trans: He will redeem my soul in peace from those who draw near to me, for among many they were with me.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: He will redeem my soul in peace from those who draw near to me, for many they were against me.

Hebrew Trans: God will hear and will answer them—He who is enthroned from of old—Selah—those who have no changes and do not fear God.
Septuagint Trans: God shall hear and shall humble them, he who exists before the ages. Selah. For there is no exchange for them, and they did not fear God.
Gallican Psalter Trans: God will hear and will humble them, he who exists before the ages. Selah. For there is no change in them, and they have not feared God.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: God will hear and will humble them, He who is judge from the beginning forever; for they do not change, nor do they fear God.

Hebrew Trans: He has stretched out his hands against those at peace with him; he has violated his covenant.
Septuagint Trans: He stretched out his hand in recompense; they profaned his covenant.
Gallican Psalter Trans: He stretched out his hand in retribution; they defiled his covenant.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: He stretched out his hand against those at peace with him; he defiled his covenant.

Hebrew Trans: Smoother than butter were the words of his mouth, but war was in his heart; his words were softer than oil, yet they were drawn swords.
Septuagint Trans: They were divided by the anger of his countenance, yet his heart drew near; his words were made softer than oil, and yet they are arrows.
Gallican Psalter Trans: They were divided by the wrath of his countenance, yet his heart drew near; his words were made softer than oil, and yet they are javelins.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Smoother than butter was his mouth, yet his heart makes war; softer were his words than oil, though they be spears.

Hebrew Trans: Cast your burden upon the LORD, and He will sustain you; He will never allow the righteous to be shaken.
Septuagint Trans: Cast your care upon the Lord, and he shall nourish you; he shall never permit the righteous to be shaken.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Cast your care upon the Lord, and he himself will nourish you; he will not allow the righteous to be shaken forever.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Cast your care upon the Lord, and He Himself will nourish you; He will not give to the just an eternal wavering.

Hebrew Trans: But You, O God, will bring them down to the pit of destruction; men of bloodshed and deceit shall not live out half their days. But as for me, I will trust in You.
Septuagint Trans: But you, O God, shall bring them down into the pit of destruction; men of blood and deceit shall not live out half their days. But I will hope in you, O Lord.
Gallican Psalter Trans: But you, O God, will lead them down into the pit of destruction; men of blood and deceit shall not live out half their days. But I will hope in you, O Lord.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: But You, O God, will lead them down into the pit of destruction; men of blood and deceit shall not live out half their days; but I will put my trust in You.

Hebrew Trans: For the choirmaster, according to "The Silent Dove of Far-off Places." Of David, a Miktam, when the Philistines seized him in Gath.
Septuagint Trans: For the end, concerning the people who have been removed far from the holy places; by David, for a pillar-inscription, when the foreigners seized him in Gath.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Unto the end, for the people who have been made far from the holy ones; of David, in the inscription of the title, when the foreigners held him in Gath.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: To the victor, concerning the silent dove, because she has gone far away. Of David, humble and simple, when the Philistines seized him in Gath.

Hebrew Trans: Be gracious to me, O God, for man pants after me; all day long a warrior presses upon me.
Septuagint Trans: Have mercy on me, Lord, for man has trampled me; all day long making war he has oppressed me.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Have mercy on me, O God, for man has trampled upon me; all day long he fighting has afflicted me.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Have mercy on me, O God, for man has trampled upon me; fighting all day long, he has afflicted me.

Hebrew Trans: My enemies pant after me all day long, for many fight against me, O Most High.
Septuagint Trans: My enemies have trampled me all day long, for many are those who war against me from on high.
Gallican Psalter Trans: My enemies have trampled upon me all day long; for many are they who wage war against me.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: My enemies have trampled upon me all day long, for many are those who fight against me, O Most High.

Hebrew Trans: The day I am afraid, I will trust in You.
Septuagint Trans: In the day I am afraid, yet I will hope in you.
Gallican Psalter Trans: From the height of the day I shall fear; but I will hope in you.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: In whatever day I shall be frightened, I will trust in you.

Hebrew Trans: In God I will praise His word; in God I have trusted, I will not fear—what can flesh do to me?
Septuagint Trans: In God I will praise my words; all day long I have hoped in God; I will not fear—what will flesh do to me?
Gallican Psalter Trans: In God I will praise my words; in God I have hoped. I will not fear what flesh may do to me.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: In God I have praised his word; in God I have hoped. I will not fear what flesh may do to me.

Hebrew Trans: All day long they twist my words; all their thoughts are against me for evil.
Septuagint Trans: All day long they abhorred my words; all their thoughts are against me for evil.
Gallican Psalter Trans: All day long they execrated my words; against me all their counsels are for evil.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: All day long they afflicted me with their words; all their thoughts are against me for evil.

Hebrew Trans: They stir up strife, they lurk, they watch my steps, as they have waited for my soul.
Septuagint Trans: They will sojourn and hide themselves; they will watch my heel, even as they have waited for my soul.
Gallican Psalter Trans: They will dwell and hide themselves; they will observe my heel, as they have waited for my soul.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: They will gather together; they will hide; they will watch my steps, lying in wait for my soul.

Hebrew Trans: For their iniquity, cast them out; in anger bring down the peoples, O God.
Septuagint Trans: On account of their worthlessness, will you save them? In wrath you will bring down the peoples, O God.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For nothing shall you save them; in anger you shall shatter the peoples, O God.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For there is no safety in them; in fury God will cast down the peoples.

Hebrew Trans: My wanderings You have counted; You put my tears in Your bottle—are they not in Your record?
Septuagint Trans: I have declared my life to you; you have placed my tears before you, as also in your promise.
Gallican Psalter Trans: I have declared my life to you; you have placed my tears in your sight, even as in your promise.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: You have numbered my secret wanderings; place my tear in your sight, but is it not also in your record?

Hebrew Trans: Then my enemies will turn back in the day I call; this I know, that God is for me.
Septuagint Trans: My enemies will turn back, on whatever day I call upon you; behold, I have known that you are my God.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Then my enemies shall be turned back, on whatever day I shall call upon you. Behold, I have known that you are my God.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Then my enemies will be turned back, on whatever day I shall call upon you. This I know, that you are my God.

Hebrew Trans: In God I will praise His word; in the LORD I will praise His word.
Septuagint Trans: In God I will praise a word; in the Lord I will praise a saying.
Gallican Psalter Trans: In God I will praise the word; in the Lord I will praise the speech.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: In God I will praise his word; in the Lord I will proclaim his speech.

Hebrew Trans: In God I have trusted; I will not fear—what can man do to me?
Septuagint Trans: In God I have hoped; I will not fear—what will man do to me?
Gallican Psalter Trans: In God I have hoped; I will not fear what man may do to me.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: In God I have hoped; I will not fear what man may do to me.

Hebrew Trans: Upon me, O God, are Your vows; I will render thank offerings to You.
Septuagint Trans: In me, O God, are the vows which I will render of praise to you.
Gallican Psalter Trans: In me, O God, are your vows, which I shall repay as praises to you.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: In me, O God, are your vows; I will render thanksgivings to you.

Hebrew Trans: For You have delivered my soul from death—have You not kept my feet from stumbling?—that I may walk before God in the light of the living.
Septuagint Trans: For you have delivered my soul from death and my feet from slipping, so as to be pleasing before God in the light of the living.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For you have delivered my soul from death and my feet from falling, that I may be pleasing before God in the light of the living.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For you have delivered my soul from death, and my feet from falling, that I may walk before God in the light of the living.

Hebrew Trans: For the chief musician; "Do not destroy." Of David, a miktam, when he fled from Saul in the cave.
Septuagint Trans: For the end; do not destroy; by David, for a pillar-inscription, when he was fleeing from the face of Saul into the cave.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Unto the end, do not destroy. Of David, in the inscription of a title, when he fled from the face of Saul into the cave.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For victory, that you may not destroy. Of David, humble and simple, when he fled from the face of Saul into the cave.

Hebrew Trans: Be gracious to me, O God, be gracious to me, for in you my soul takes refuge; and in the shadow of your wings I will take refuge, until destruction passes by.
Septuagint Trans: Have mercy on me, O God, have mercy on me, for in you my soul has trusted, and in the shadow of your wings I will hope, until the lawlessness passes away.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Have mercy on me, O God, have mercy on me: for in you my soul trusts, and in the shadow of your wings I will hope, until iniquity passes away.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Have mercy on me, O God, have mercy on me, for in you my soul hopes; in the shadow of your wings I will hope, until the snares pass away.

Hebrew Trans: I will cry out to God Most High, to God who accomplishes all things for me.
Septuagint Trans: I will cry out to God the Most High, to God who has been my benefactor.
Gallican Psalter Trans: I will cry out to God most high, to God who has done good to me.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: I will call upon God most high, God my avenger.

Hebrew Trans: He will send from heaven and save me; he rebukes the one who pants after me. Selah. God will send forth his steadfast love and his truth.
Septuagint Trans: He sent forth from heaven and saved me; he gave over to reproach those who trample upon me. Selah. God sent forth his mercy and his truth.
Gallican Psalter Trans: He sent from heaven and delivered me; he gave over to reproach those who trample upon me. Selah. God sent his mercy and his truth.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: He will send from heaven and save me; he will reproach those who trample upon me always. God will send his mercy and his truth.
