Hebrew Trans: My soul is in the midst of lions; I lie down among those who are aflame, the sons of men whose teeth are spears and arrows, and whose tongue is a sharp sword.
Septuagint Trans: And he rescued my soul from the midst of young lions; I lay down to sleep, greatly troubled. The sons of men—their teeth are weapons and arrows, and their tongue is a sharp sword.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And he rescued my soul from the midst of the young lions; I slept troubled. The sons of men, their teeth are weapons and arrows, and their tongue is a sharp sword.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: My soul has slept in the midst of lions, of fierce ones; the sons of men, whose teeth are a lance and arrows, and whose tongue is a sharp sword.

Hebrew Trans: Be exalted above the heavens, O God; let your glory be over all the earth.
Septuagint Trans: Be exalted above the heavens, O God, and let your glory be over all the earth.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Be exalted above the heavens, O God, and your glory over all the earth.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Be exalted above the heavens, O God; over all the earth be your glory.

Hebrew Trans: They prepared a net for my steps; my soul was bowed down. They dug a pit before me; they have fallen into the midst of it themselves. Selah.
Septuagint Trans: They prepared a snare for my feet and bowed down my soul; they dug a pit before my face, and they fell into it themselves. Selah.
Gallican Psalter Trans: They prepared a snare for my feet, and they bowed down my soul; they dug a pit before my face, and they fell into it themselves. Selah.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: They prepared a net for my steps, to bow down my soul; they dug a pit before me; they have fallen into the midst of it always.

Hebrew Trans: My heart is steadfast, O God, my heart is steadfast; I will sing and make music.
Septuagint Trans: My heart is ready, O God, my heart is ready; I will sing and make music.
Gallican Psalter Trans: My heart is ready, O God, my heart is ready; I will sing and I will recite a psalm.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: My heart is ready, O God, my heart is ready; I will sing and I will make music.

Hebrew Trans: Awake, my glory! Awake, O harp and lyre! I will awaken the dawn.
Septuagint Trans: Awake, O my glory! Awake, O psaltery and harp! I will awake at dawn.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Arise, my glory; arise, psaltery and harp; I will arise at the break of day.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Arise, my glory; arise, psaltery and harp; I will arise in the morning.

Hebrew Trans: I will give thanks to you among the peoples, O Lord; I will sing praises to you among the nations.
Septuagint Trans: I will give thanks to you among the peoples, O Lord; I will sing praises to you among the nations.
Gallican Psalter Trans: I will praise you among the peoples, O Lord; I will sing a psalm to you among the nations.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: I will praise you among the peoples, O Lord; I will sing to you among the nations.

Hebrew Trans: For your steadfast love is great unto the heavens, and your truth unto the skies.
Septuagint Trans: For your mercy has been magnified up to the heavens, and your truth up to the clouds.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For your mercy is magnified even unto the heavens, and your truth even unto the clouds.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For your mercy is great even to the heavens, and your truth even to the clouds.

Hebrew Trans: Be exalted above the heavens, O God; let your glory be over all the earth.
Septuagint Trans: Be exalted above the heavens, O God, and let your glory be over all the earth.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Be exalted above the heavens, O God, and your glory over all the earth.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Be exalted above the heavens, O God; over all the earth be your glory.

Hebrew Trans: For the choirmaster. "Do Not Destroy." Of David. A miktam.
Septuagint Trans: To the end; do not destroy. Of David, for a pillar-inscription.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Unto the end, do not destroy. Of David, in the inscription of a title.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: To the victor: do not destroy. Of David, humble and simple.

Hebrew Trans: Do you indeed speak righteousness in silence? Do you judge uprightly, O sons of men?
Septuagint Trans: If you truly speak righteousness, judge uprightly, O sons of men.
Gallican Psalter Trans: If truly indeed you speak justice, judge rightly, O sons of men.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: If truly indeed you speak justice, judge rightly, O sons of men.

Hebrew Trans: Yea, in heart you work wickedness; in the land you weigh out the violence of your hands.
Septuagint Trans: For indeed in your heart you work lawlessness; in the earth your hands weave together injustice.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For indeed in your heart you work iniquities; in the earth your hands fashion injustice.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For indeed in your heart you work iniquities; on the earth you weigh out the injustice of your hands.

Hebrew Trans: The wicked are estranged from the womb; they go astray from birth, speaking lies.
Septuagint Trans: The sinners were estranged from the womb; they went astray from birth, they spoke lies.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Sinners are alienated from the womb; they have gone astray from birth, they have spoken false things.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The sinners are alienated from the womb; they have gone astray from birth, speaking falsehood.

Hebrew Trans: Their venom is like the likeness of a serpent's venom, like a deaf adder that stops its ear,
Septuagint Trans: Their fury is in the likeness of a serpent, like a deaf asp that stops its ears,
Gallican Psalter Trans: Their fury is according to the likeness of a serpent, like a deaf asp that stops its ears,
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Their fury is like the fury of a serpent, like a deaf asp stopping its ear,

Hebrew Trans: which does not hear the voice of charmers, a binder of spells, however skillful.
Septuagint Trans: which will not hear the voice of enchanters, nor of a potion prepared by a wise man.
Gallican Psalter Trans: which will not hear the voice of enchanters, and of the sorcerer who charms wisely.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: so that it may not hear the voice of those who murmur, nor the cunning charms of the enchanter.

Hebrew Trans: O God, break their teeth in their mouth; tear out the fangs of the young lions, O LORD.
Septuagint Trans: God has shattered their teeth in their mouth; the Lord has crushed the fangs of the lions.
Gallican Psalter Trans: God will shatter their teeth in their mouth; the Lord will break the jaws of the lions.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: O God, shatter their teeth in their mouth; break the molars of the lions, O Lord.

Hebrew Trans: Let them melt away like water that flows off; when he aims his arrows, let them be as though cut off.
Septuagint Trans: They shall be utterly scorned like water flowing away; he shall bend his bow until they are weakened.
Gallican Psalter Trans: They shall come to nothing like water running down; he has bent his bow until they be weakened.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Let them be dissolved like waters that flow away; he shall bend his bow until they are crushed.

Hebrew Trans: Like a snail that melts away as it goes, like a woman's stillbirth, let them not see the sun.
Septuagint Trans: Like wax that melts they shall be destroyed; fire has fallen upon them, and they did not see the sun.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Like wax that melts they shall be taken away; fire has fallen upon them and they have not seen the sun.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Let them pass away like a melting worm, like the miscarriage of a woman that has not seen the sun.

Hebrew Trans: Before your thorns can feel the bramble, whether green or ablaze, he shall sweep them away.
Septuagint Trans: Before your thorns perceive the bramble, as though alive, as though in wrath, he shall swallow you up.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Before your thorns could perceive the bramble, as though living, as though in wrath, he swallows you up.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Before your thorns grow into a bramble, as though living, as in wrath, the tempest shall seize them.

Hebrew Trans: The righteous shall rejoice when he sees the vengeance; he shall wash his feet in the blood of the wicked.
Septuagint Trans: The righteous shall rejoice when he sees the vengeance of the ungodly; he shall wash his hands in the blood of the sinner.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The just man shall rejoice when he shall see the vengeance; he shall wash his hands in the blood of the sinner.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The just man shall rejoice when he has seen vengeance; he shall wash his feet in the blood of the wicked.

Hebrew Trans: And a man shall say, "Surely there is fruit for the righteous; surely there is a God who judges in the earth."
Septuagint Trans: And a man shall say, "If indeed there is fruit for the righteous, then indeed there is a God who judges them in the earth."
Gallican Psalter Trans: And a man shall say: If indeed there is fruit for the just, indeed there is a God judging them on the earth.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And a man shall say: truly there is fruit for the just; truly there is a God judging on the earth.

Hebrew Trans: For the choirmaster. "Do Not Destroy." Of David. A miktam. When Saul sent and they watched the house to kill him.
Septuagint Trans: For the end; do not destroy; by David, for a pillar-inscription, when Saul sent and watched his house in order to kill him.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Unto the end, do not destroy. Of David, in an inscription of a title, when Saul sent and watched his house in order to kill him.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: To the victor: do not destroy. Of David, humble and simple, when Saul sent and they watched the house to kill him.

Hebrew Trans: Deliver me from my enemies, O my God; set me on high from those who rise up against me.
Septuagint Trans: Deliver me from my enemies, O God, and from those who rise up against me, redeem me.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Deliver me from my enemies, O God, and free me from those who rise up against me.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Deliver me from my enemies, O my God, and protect me from those who rise up against me.

Hebrew Trans: Deliver me from the workers of iniquity, and save me from men of blood.
Septuagint Trans: Rescue me from the workers of lawlessness, and from men of blood save me.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Deliver me from the workers of iniquity, and save me from men of blood.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Free me from the workers of iniquity, and save me from men of blood.

Hebrew Trans: For behold, they lie in wait for my soul; fierce ones gather against me—not for my transgression and not for my sin, O LORD.
Septuagint Trans: For behold, they have hunted my soul; the mighty have set upon me — neither for my transgression nor for my sin, O Lord.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For behold, they have seized my soul; the strong have rushed upon me: neither is it my iniquity, nor my sin, O Lord.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For behold, they have laid in wait for my soul; the mightiest gather together against me, without my iniquity and without my sin, O Lord.

Hebrew Trans: Without iniquity on my part they run and prepare themselves. Awake to meet me, and see!
Septuagint Trans: Without iniquity I ran and kept a straight course. Arise to meet me, and see.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Without iniquity I have run and directed my course: arise to meet me and see.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: I have done no wrong, yet they run and prepare themselves; arise to meet me and see.

Hebrew Trans: And You, O LORD God of hosts, God of Israel, rouse Yourself to punish all the nations; do not be gracious to any treacherous workers of iniquity. Selah.
Septuagint Trans: And you, Lord, the God of hosts, the God of Israel, attend to visit all the nations; do not have mercy on any who work lawlessness. Selah.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And you, O Lord God of hosts, God of Israel, attend to visiting all the nations; have no mercy on all those who work iniquity. Selah.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And you, O Lord God of hosts, God of Israel, awake to visit all the nations; do not have mercy on any who work iniquity forever.

Hebrew Trans: They return at evening, they howl like a dog, and go around the city.
Septuagint Trans: They will return at evening and hunger like a dog, and go around the city.
Gallican Psalter Trans: They shall return at evening, and shall suffer hunger like dogs, and shall go around the city.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Let them return at evening, and let them bark like a dog, and go around the city.

Hebrew Trans: Behold, they pour forth with their mouths; swords are in their lips, for "Who hears?"
Septuagint Trans: Behold, they will speak forth with their mouth, and a sword is on their lips — for "Who has heard?"
Gallican Psalter Trans: Behold, they shall speak with their mouth, and a sword is in their lips: for who, they say, has heard?
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Behold, they speak with their mouth; swords are in their lips, as though no one hears.

Hebrew Trans: But You, O LORD, shall laugh at them; You shall mock all the nations.
Septuagint Trans: But you, Lord, will laugh at them; you will bring to nothing all the nations.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And you, O Lord, shall deride them; you shall bring all the nations to nothing.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: But you, O Lord, shall laugh at them; you shall mock all the nations.

Hebrew Trans: His strength—unto You I will watch, for God is my high fortress.
Septuagint Trans: My strength I will guard for you, for God, you are my defender.
Gallican Psalter Trans: My strength I will keep toward you, for God is my protector.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: My strength I will keep for you, for you, O God, are my lifter up.

Hebrew Trans: The God of my steadfast love will come to meet me; God will let me look upon my adversaries.
Septuagint Trans: My God — his mercy will come before me; God will show me triumph among my enemies.
Gallican Psalter Trans: My God, his mercy shall come to meet me; God shall show me triumph over my enemies.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The mercy of my God shall go before me; God shall show me over my enemies who lie in wait.

Hebrew Trans: Do not slay them, lest my people forget; scatter them by Your power and bring them down, O Lord our shield.
Septuagint Trans: Do not slay them, lest my people forget; scatter them by your power and bring them down, O my protector, Lord.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Do not slay them, lest at any time my people forget; scatter them by your power and bring them down, O Lord my protector.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Do not slay them, lest my people forget; scatter them by your strength and bring them down, O Lord, our protector.

Hebrew Trans: The sin of their mouth, the word of their lips—let them be caught in their pride, and for the cursing and lying they recount.
Septuagint Trans: For the sin of their mouth, the word of their lips — let them be caught in their pride; and because of cursing and falsehood, the destructions will be proclaimed.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The sin of their mouth, the speech of their lips: and let them be caught in their pride; and for their cursing and lying they shall be denounced.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For the sin of their mouth, for the speech of their lips, let them be caught in their pride, and for the cursing and lying which they tell.

Hebrew Trans: Consume them in wrath, consume them that they be no more; and let them know that God rules in Jacob to the ends of the earth. Selah.
Septuagint Trans: In the wrath of consummation, and they shall be no more; and they shall know that God rules over Jacob, to the ends of the earth. Selah.
Gallican Psalter Trans: In the consummation, in the wrath of consummation, and they shall be no more; and they shall know that God shall rule Jacob unto the ends of the earth. Selah.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Consume them in fury, consume them so that they may not stand, and let them know that God rules over Jacob to the ends of the earth forever.

Hebrew Trans: And they return at evening, they howl like a dog, and go around the city.
Septuagint Trans: They will return at evening and hunger like a dog, and go around the city.
Gallican Psalter Trans: They shall return at evening, and shall suffer hunger like dogs, and shall go around the city.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And let them return at evening, and let them bark like a dog, and go around the city.

Hebrew Trans: They wander about for food; if they are not satisfied, they growl through the night.
Septuagint Trans: They will be scattered abroad to eat, and if they are not satisfied, they will grumble.
Gallican Psalter Trans: They shall be scattered abroad to eat, and indeed if they shall not be satisfied, they shall murmur.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: They shall wander about to eat, and when they are not satisfied, they shall murmur.

Hebrew Trans: But I will sing of Your strength, and I will shout for joy in the morning of Your steadfast love, for You have been a high fortress for me and a refuge in the day of my distress.
Septuagint Trans: But I will sing of your power, and in the morning I will rejoice in your mercy, for you have been my defender and a refuge in the day of my affliction.
Gallican Psalter Trans: But I will sing of your strength, and will exult in the morning in your mercy; for you have been my protector and my refuge in the day of my tribulation.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: But I will sing of your power, and I will praise your mercy in the morning, for you have been my strength and a refuge in the day of my tribulation.

Hebrew Trans: O my strength, unto You I will sing praises, for God is my high fortress, the God of my steadfast love.
Septuagint Trans: My helper, to you I will sing praise, for God, you are my defender — my God, my mercy.
Gallican Psalter Trans: My helper, to you will I sing praises, for God is my protector; my God, my mercy.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: To you I will sing, for God is my helper, my strength, God is my mercy.

Hebrew Trans: For the choirmaster, upon Shushan Eduth, a Michtam of David, to teach.
Septuagint Trans: To the end, for those who shall be changed, still for an inscription on a pillar, by David, for instruction.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Unto the end, for those who shall be changed, in the inscription of a title, of David, for instruction.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For the victor, concerning the lilies, a testimony of the humble and perfect David, for teaching.

Hebrew Trans: When he contended with Aram-Naharaim and with Aram-Zobah, and Joab returned and struck Edom in the Valley of Salt, twelve thousand.
Septuagint Trans: When he set fire to Mesopotamia of Syria and Syria of Sobah, and Joab returned and struck the Valley of Salt—twelve thousand.
Gallican Psalter Trans: When he set fire to Syria of Mesopotamia and Syria of Zobah, and Joab returned and struck the Valley of Salt, twelve thousand.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: When he fought against Syria of Mesopotamia and against Syria of Zobah, and Joab returned and struck Edom in the Valley of Salt, twelve thousand.

Hebrew Trans: O God, You have rejected us, You have broken through us; You were angry — restore us!
Septuagint Trans: O God, you have rejected us and torn us down; you were angry, yet you had compassion on us.
Gallican Psalter Trans: O God, you have cast us off and destroyed us; you were angry and have had mercy on us.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: O God, you have cast us off and broken us apart; you were angry; turn back to us.

Hebrew Trans: You have made the earth tremble, You have split it open; heal its breaches, for it totters.
Septuagint Trans: You shook the earth and troubled it; heal its fractures, for it has been shaken.
Gallican Psalter Trans: You have shaken the earth and disturbed it; heal its breaches, for it has been moved.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: You have shaken the earth and torn it open; heal its fractures, for it has been shaken.

Hebrew Trans: You have shown Your people hard things; You have made us drink the wine of staggering.
Septuagint Trans: You showed your people hard things; you gave us the wine of stupefaction to drink.
Gallican Psalter Trans: You have shown your people hard things; you have made us drink the wine of compunction.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: You have shown your people hardship; you have given us wine that makes us stagger.

Hebrew Trans: You have given a banner to those who fear You, that it may be displayed because of truth. Selah.
Septuagint Trans: You gave to those who fear you a signal to flee from before the bow. Selah.
Gallican Psalter Trans: You have given to those who fear you a sign, that they may flee from the face of the bow. Selah.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: You have given to those who fear you a sign, that they may flee from the face of the bow forever.

Hebrew Trans: That Your beloved ones may be delivered, save with Your right hand and answer me.
Septuagint Trans: So that your beloved ones may be delivered, save with your right hand and hear me.
Gallican Psalter Trans: That your beloved ones may be delivered, save with your right hand and hear me.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: That your beloved ones may be delivered, save with your right hand and hear me.

Hebrew Trans: God has spoken in His holiness: I will exult, I will divide Shechem, and the Valley of Succoth I will measure out.
Septuagint Trans: God has spoken in his sanctuary: I will rejoice and divide Shechem, and I will measure out the Valley of Tents.
Gallican Psalter Trans: God has spoken in his holy place: I will rejoice and divide Shechem, and I will measure out the valley of tabernacles.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: God has spoken in his sanctuary: I will rejoice; I will divide Shechem and measure out the valley of Succoth.

Hebrew Trans: Gilead is mine, and Manasseh is mine; Ephraim is the strength of my head; Judah is my lawgiver.
Septuagint Trans: Gilead is mine and Manasseh is mine; and Ephraim is the strength of my head; Judah is my king.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Gilead is mine, and Manasseh is mine, and Ephraim is the strength of my head; Judah is my king.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Gilead is mine, and Manasseh is mine, and Ephraim is the strength of my head; Judah is my lawgiver.

Hebrew Trans: Moab is my washbasin; upon Edom I will cast my sandal; over Philistia, shout in triumph!
Septuagint Trans: Moab is the basin of my hope; upon Edom I will stretch out my sandal; the foreigners have been subjected to me.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Moab is the pot of my hope; into Edom I will stretch out my sandal; to me the foreigners are subjected.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Moab is the basin of my washing; upon Edom I will tread with my sandal; Philistia has been allied to me.

Hebrew Trans: Who will bring me to the fortified city? Who will lead me to Edom?
Septuagint Trans: Who will bring me into the fortified city? Who will lead me as far as Edom?
Gallican Psalter Trans: Who will lead me into the fortified city? Who will lead me as far as Edom?
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Who will lead me into the fortified city? Who will lead me as far as Edom?

Hebrew Trans: Have not You, O God, rejected us? And You do not go forth, O God, with our armies.
Septuagint Trans: Is it not you, O God, who have rejected us? And will you not go out, O God, with our armies?
Gallican Psalter Trans: Is it not you, O God, who have cast us off? And will you not go out, O God, with our armies?
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Is it not you, O God, who have cast us off? And will you not go out, O God, with our armies?

Hebrew Trans: Grant us help against the adversary, for vain is the salvation of man.
Septuagint Trans: Grant us help from affliction, for vain is the salvation of man.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Give us help from tribulation, for vain is the salvation of man.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Give us help in tribulation, for vain is the salvation that comes from man.

Hebrew Trans: Through God we shall do valiantly, and He shall tread down our enemies.
Septuagint Trans: In God we shall do valiantly, and he himself will bring to nothing those who afflict us.
Gallican Psalter Trans: In God we shall do valiantly, and he himself will bring to nothing those who trouble us.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: In God we shall do valiantly, and he himself will trample down those who afflict us.

Hebrew Trans: For the chief musician, upon stringed instruments. Of David.
Septuagint Trans: For the end, in hymns, by David.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Unto the end, in hymns, of David.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: To the victor, in psalms, of David.

Hebrew Trans: Hear my cry, O God; attend unto my prayer.
Septuagint Trans: Hear, O God, my supplication; attend to my prayer.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Hear, O God, my supplication; attend to my prayer.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Hear, O God, my praise; attend to my prayer.

Hebrew Trans: From the end of the earth I call unto You, when my heart grows faint; lead me to the rock that is higher than I.
Septuagint Trans: From the ends of the earth I cried out to you when my heart was in distress; upon a rock you raised me up.
Gallican Psalter Trans: From the ends of the earth I have cried to you, while my heart was in anguish; upon a rock you have exalted me, you have led me.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: From the ends of the earth I will cry out to you, when my heart shall be sorrowful; when the enemy shall be strengthened against me, you will be my guide.

Hebrew Trans: For You have been a refuge for me, a tower of strength in the face of the enemy.
Septuagint Trans: You guided me, for you became my hope, a tower of strength before the face of the enemy.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For you have been my hope, a tower of strength against the face of the enemy.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: You have been my hope, a most fortified tower from the face of the enemy.

Hebrew Trans: I will dwell in Your tent forever; I will take shelter in the cover of Your wings. Selah.
Septuagint Trans: I shall dwell in your tabernacle forever; I shall be sheltered under the cover of your wings. Selah.
Gallican Psalter Trans: I shall dwell in your tabernacle forever; I shall be protected under the covering of your wings. Selah.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: I will dwell in your tabernacle continually; I will hope in the protection of your wings forever.

Hebrew Trans: For You, O God, have heard my vows; You have granted the heritage of those who fear Your name.
Septuagint Trans: For you, O God, have heard my prayers; you have given an inheritance to those who fear your name.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For you, my God, have heard my prayer; you have given an inheritance to those who fear your name.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For you, O God, have heard my prayer; you have given an inheritance to those who fear your name.

Hebrew Trans: May You add days upon the days of the king; may his years be as generation upon generation.
Septuagint Trans: You will add days upon days to the king; his years unto the generation of generations.
Gallican Psalter Trans: You will add days upon days to the king; his years even unto the day of generation and generation.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: You will add days upon the days of the king; his years until generation and generation.

Hebrew Trans: May he be enthroned forever before God; appoint lovingkindness and truth that they may preserve him.
Septuagint Trans: He abides forever before God; his mercy and truth, who shall seek them out?
Gallican Psalter Trans: He abides forever in the sight of God; his mercy and truth, who shall seek them out?
Hebrew Psalter Trans: He will sit forever before the face of God; mercy and truth will preserve him.

Hebrew Trans: So will I sing praise unto Your name forever, that I may fulfill my vows day by day.
Septuagint Trans: Thus I will sing praises to your name forever and ever, so that I may fulfill my vows day after day.
Gallican Psalter Trans: So I will sing a psalm to your name forever and ever, that I may render my vows from day to day.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: So will I sing to your name continually, rendering my vows day by day.

Hebrew Trans: For the choirmaster, upon Jeduthun, a psalm of David.
Septuagint Trans: To the end, on behalf of Jedithun, a psalm of David.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Unto the end, for Idithun, a psalm of David.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: To the victor, through Jedithun, a canticle of David.

Hebrew Trans: Truly, unto God alone my soul waits in silence; from Him is my salvation.
Septuagint Trans: Shall not my soul be subject to God? For from him is my salvation.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Shall not my soul be subject to God? For from him is my salvation.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Truly before God my soul shall be silent; from him is my salvation.

Hebrew Trans: He alone is my rock and my salvation, my high fortress; I shall not be greatly moved.
Septuagint Trans: For he indeed is my God and my savior, my helper; I shall not be greatly shaken.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For he indeed is my God and my savior; he is my protector, I shall be moved no more.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Truly he is my shield and my salvation, my strength; I shall be moved no more.

Hebrew Trans: How long will you assail a man? You would murder him, all of you, like a leaning wall, a tottering fence.
Septuagint Trans: How long will you set upon a man? You all murder, like a leaning wall and a pushed-down fence.
Gallican Psalter Trans: How long do you rush upon a man? You all kill, as though you were a leaning wall and a broken-down fence.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: How long do you lie in wait against a man? You all kill, like a leaning wall and a tottering fence.

Hebrew Trans: They only consult to thrust him down from his eminence; they delight in lies; with their mouth they bless, but inwardly they curse—Selah.
Septuagint Trans: Yet they plotted to thrust away my honor; they ran in falsehood; with their mouth they blessed, but in their heart they cursed. Selah.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Yet they thought to cast down my worth; I ran in thirst; with their mouth they blessed, and with their heart they cursed. Selah.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For they have plotted to drive out his portion; they delighted in lies; with their mouth each one blesses, but in their heart they always curse.

Hebrew Trans: Truly, my soul, wait in silence for God alone, for from Him is my hope.
Septuagint Trans: Yet be subject to God alone, O my soul, for from him is my patience.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Yet be subject to God, O my soul, for from him is my patience.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Nevertheless, be silent to God, O my soul, for from him is my expectation.

Hebrew Trans: He alone is my rock and my salvation, my high fortress; I shall not be moved.
Septuagint Trans: For he is my God and my savior, my helper; I shall not be moved.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For he is my God and my savior, my helper; I shall not be moved away.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: He is my strength and my salvation, my protector; I shall not fear.

Hebrew Trans: Upon God rests my salvation and my glory; the rock of my strength, my refuge is in God.
Septuagint Trans: Upon God is my salvation and my glory; the God of my help, and my hope is in God.
Gallican Psalter Trans: In God is my salvation and my glory; the God of my help, and my hope is in God.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: In God is my salvation and my glory; the rock of my strength, my salvation is in God.

Hebrew Trans: Trust in Him at all times, O people; pour out your heart before Him; God is a refuge for us—Selah.
Septuagint Trans: Hope in him, all you congregation of the people; pour out your hearts before him. God is our helper. Selah.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Hope in him, all you congregation of the people; pour out your hearts before him. God is our helper forever.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Hope in him at all times, O peoples; pour out your heart before him; God is our hope forever.

Hebrew Trans: Surely men of low degree are but a breath, men of high degree are a lie; in the balances they go up; they are together lighter than a breath.
Septuagint Trans: Yet the sons of men are vain, the sons of men are false; in the balances they are for wrongdoing—they are altogether from vanity.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Yet the sons of men are vain, the sons of men are liars; in the balances they are deceitful, drawn together from vanity.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Nevertheless, the sons of Adam are vanity, the sons of men are a lie; in deceitful balances they act fraudulently together.

Hebrew Trans: Trust not in oppression, and in robbery do not become vain; if wealth increases, do not set your heart upon it.
Septuagint Trans: Do not hope in injustice, and do not long for plunder; if wealth should flow, do not set your heart upon it.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Do not hope in iniquity, and do not covet robberies; if riches abound, do not set your heart upon them.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Do not trust in calumny and in robbery, lest you be frustrated; if riches flow in, do not set your heart upon them.

Hebrew Trans: One thing God has spoken, two things have I heard: that power belongs to God,
Septuagint Trans: Once God has spoken; these two things I have heard:
Gallican Psalter Trans: God has spoken once; these two things have I heard: that power belongs to God,
Hebrew Psalter Trans: God has spoken one thing; these two things have I heard: that power belongs to God,

Hebrew Trans: and to You, O Lord, belongs steadfast love; for You repay a man according to his deeds.
Septuagint Trans: That power belongs to God, and to you, O Lord, belongs mercy; for you will repay each one according to his works.
Gallican Psalter Trans: and to you, O Lord, belongs mercy; for you will render to each one according to his works.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: and to you, O Lord, belongs mercy; for you will render to each one according to his work.

Hebrew Trans: A psalm of David, when he was in the wilderness of Judah.
Septuagint Trans: A psalm of David, when he was in the wilderness of Judea.
Gallican Psalter Trans: A psalm of David, when he was in the desert of Judea.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: A canticle of David, when he was in the desert of Judah.

Hebrew Trans: O God, You are my God; earnestly I seek You. My soul thirsts for You; my flesh longs for You in a dry and weary land without water.
Septuagint Trans: O God, my God, to you I rise early; my soul has thirsted for you, how greatly my flesh longs for you, in a land desert and trackless and waterless.
Gallican Psalter Trans: O God, my God, to you I watch from the light of dawn. My soul has thirsted for you; how manifoldly does my flesh long for you, in a desert and pathless and waterless land.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: O God, my strength, you are; from the light I shall rise to you. My soul has thirsted for you; my flesh has longed for you, in a land pathless and parching and without water.

Hebrew Trans: So in the sanctuary I have beheld You, to see Your power and Your glory.
Septuagint Trans: Thus in the sanctuary I appeared before you, to see your power and your glory.
Gallican Psalter Trans: So in the sanctuary I appeared before you, that I might see your power and your glory.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: So in the sanctuary I have appeared before you, that I might see your strength and your glory.

Hebrew Trans: For Your lovingkindness is better than life; my lips shall praise You.
Septuagint Trans: For your mercy is better than life; my lips shall praise you.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For your mercy is better than lives; my lips shall praise you.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For your mercy is better than lives; my lips shall praise you.

Hebrew Trans: So I will bless You while I live; in Your name I will lift up my hands.
Septuagint Trans: Thus I will bless you in my life; in your name I will lift up my hands.
Gallican Psalter Trans: So I will bless you in my life; in your name I will lift up my hands.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: So I will bless you in my life; in your name I will lift up my hands.

Hebrew Trans: As with fat and richness my soul shall be satisfied, and with joyful lips my mouth shall praise You.
Septuagint Trans: As with fatness and richness may my soul be filled, and with lips of rejoicing my mouth shall praise you.
Gallican Psalter Trans: As with fat and richness let my soul be filled, and with lips of exultation my mouth shall praise you.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: As with fat and richness my soul shall be filled, and with praising lips my mouth shall sing.

Hebrew Trans: When I remember You upon my bed, in the night watches I meditate on You.
Septuagint Trans: If I remembered you upon my bed, in the early morning hours I would meditate on you.
Gallican Psalter Trans: If I remembered you upon my bed, in the morning hours I would meditate on you.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Remembering you upon my bed, through each of the night watches I shall meditate on you.

Hebrew Trans: For You have been my help, and in the shadow of Your wings I will sing for joy.
Septuagint Trans: For you have been my helper, and in the shelter of your wings I will rejoice.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For you have been my helper, and in the shelter of your wings I will exult.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For you have been my help; in the shadow of your wings I shall praise.

Hebrew Trans: My soul clings after You; Your right hand upholds me.
Septuagint Trans: My soul has clung close behind you; your right hand has upheld me.
Gallican Psalter Trans: My soul has clung close after you; your right hand has upheld me.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: My soul has clung close after you; your right hand has upheld me.

Hebrew Trans: But those who seek my soul for destruction, they shall go into the depths of the earth.
Septuagint Trans: But they in vain sought my soul; they shall go into the lowest parts of the earth.
Gallican Psalter Trans: But they in vain have sought my soul; they shall go into the lower parts of the earth.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: But those very ones who seek to destroy my soul, let them go into the depths of the earth.

Hebrew Trans: They shall be delivered over to the power of the sword; they shall be a portion for foxes.
Septuagint Trans: They shall be delivered over to the hands of the sword; they shall be portions for foxes.
Gallican Psalter Trans: They shall be delivered into the hands of the sword; they shall be the portions of foxes.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Let them be gathered into the hands of the sword; they shall be the portion of foxes.

Hebrew Trans: But the king shall rejoice in God; all who swear by Him shall glory, for the mouth of those who speak lies shall be stopped.
Septuagint Trans: But the king shall rejoice in God; everyone who swears by him shall be praised, for the mouth of those who speak unjust things has been stopped.
Gallican Psalter Trans: But the king shall rejoice in God; all who swear by him shall be praised, for the mouth of those who speak wicked things has been stopped.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: But the king shall rejoice in God; all who swear by him shall be praised, for the mouth of those who speak lies shall be stopped.

Hebrew Trans: To the choirmaster, a psalm of David.
Septuagint Trans: To the end, a psalm of David.
Gallican Psalter Trans: To the end, a psalm of David.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: To the victor, a song of David.

Hebrew Trans: Hear, O God, my voice in my complaint; from the dread of the enemy preserve my life.
Septuagint Trans: Hear my voice, O God, when I pray to you; deliver my soul from the fear of the enemy.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Hear, O God, my prayer when I make supplication; from the fear of the enemy deliver my soul.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Hear, O God, my voice as I speak; from the fear of the enemy preserve my life.

Hebrew Trans: Hide me from the council of evildoers, from the tumult of the workers of iniquity,
Septuagint Trans: You have sheltered me from the conspiracy of evildoers, from the multitude of those who work iniquity.
Gallican Psalter Trans: You have protected me from the assembly of the malignant, from the multitude of those who work iniquity.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Hide me from the counsel of the wicked, from the tumult of those who work iniquity.

Hebrew Trans: who have sharpened their tongue like a sword, who have aimed their arrow, a bitter word,
Septuagint Trans: Who have sharpened their tongues like a sword; they have bent their bow, a bitter thing,
Gallican Psalter Trans: For they have sharpened their tongues like a sword; they have bent their bow, a bitter thing,
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Who have sharpened their tongue like a sword; they have drawn their arrow, a most bitter word,

Hebrew Trans: to shoot in secret at the blameless; suddenly they shoot at him and do not fear.
Septuagint Trans: to shoot in secret at the blameless; suddenly they will shoot at him and will not fear.
Gallican Psalter Trans: that they may shoot in secret at the undefiled; suddenly they shall shoot at him and shall not fear.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: that they might shoot in secret at the innocent; suddenly they will shoot at him and will not fear.

Hebrew Trans: They strengthen for themselves an evil purpose; they talk of laying snares secretly; they say, "Who will see them?"
Septuagint Trans: They have strengthened for themselves an evil word; they have plotted to hide snares; they said, "Who will see them?"
Gallican Psalter Trans: They have strengthened for themselves an evil word; they have spoken to hide snares; they said, "Who shall see them?"
Hebrew Psalter Trans: They have strengthened for themselves an evil purpose; they have plotted to hide snares; they said, "Who will see us?"

Hebrew Trans: They search out iniquities; "We have completed a thorough search!" And the inward thought of a man, and the heart, is deep.
Septuagint Trans: They have searched out iniquities; they have exhausted themselves searching thoroughly. A man shall come forward with deep searching and a deep heart.
Gallican Psalter Trans: They have searched out iniquities; they failed in their searching scrutiny. Man shall come forward, and the heart shall be deep.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: They have searched out iniquities; they have failed in their searching scrutiny, in the thoughts of each one and in the deep heart.

Hebrew Trans: But God will shoot an arrow at them suddenly; they will be struck with wounds.
Septuagint Trans: And God shall be exalted; their wounds have become the arrows of infants.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And God shall be exalted; the arrows of little ones their wounds have become.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Therefore God will shoot them with a sudden arrow; their wounds will be inflicted.

Hebrew Trans: And they will be made to stumble, their own tongue being against them; all who see them will flee away.
Septuagint Trans: And their own tongues have been weakened against them; all who see them have been troubled.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And their tongues have been weakened against them; all who saw them were troubled.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And they will stumble over themselves by their own tongues; all who see them will flee.

Hebrew Trans: And all men will fear, and will declare the work of God, and will wisely consider His deed.
Septuagint Trans: And every man feared, and they declared the works of God, and understood his deeds.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And every man feared, and they declared the works of God, and understood his deeds.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And all men will fear, and they will declare the work of God, and will understand His deeds.

Hebrew Trans: The righteous shall rejoice in the LORD and take refuge in Him, and all the upright in heart shall glory.
Septuagint Trans: The righteous shall rejoice in the Lord and shall hope in him, and all the upright in heart shall be praised.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The just shall rejoice in the Lord and shall hope in him, and all the upright of heart shall be praised.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The just one will rejoice in the Lord and will hope in Him, and all the upright of heart will exult.

Hebrew Trans: For the choirmaster, a psalm of David, a song.
Septuagint Trans: To the end, a psalm of David, a song of Jeremiah and Ezekiel, from the word of the sojourning, when they were about to go forth.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Unto the end, a psalm of David, a canticle of Jeremiah and Haggai, concerning the word of the sojourning, when they were beginning to depart.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: A song of victory of David, a canticle.

Hebrew Trans: To you silence is praise, O God, in Zion, and to you shall the vow be fulfilled.
Septuagint Trans: To you a hymn is fitting, O God, in Zion, and to you a vow shall be rendered in Jerusalem.
Gallican Psalter Trans: A hymn befits you, O God, in Zion, and to you a vow shall be rendered in Jerusalem.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: To you, silence is praise, O God, in Zion, and to you shall a vow be rendered.

Hebrew Trans: O you who hears prayer, to you all flesh shall come.
Septuagint Trans: Hear my prayer; to you all flesh shall come.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Hear my prayer; to you all flesh shall come.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Hear my prayer; until all flesh shall come to you.

Hebrew Trans: Matters of iniquities have prevailed over me; our transgressions—you shall atone for them.
Septuagint Trans: Words of lawless ones have overpowered us, and our impieties—you will forgive them.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The words of the wicked have prevailed over us, and you will be merciful to our impieties.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Words of iniquities have prevailed against me; our transgressions, you will pardon.

Hebrew Trans: Blessed is the one whom you choose and bring near, that he may dwell in your courts; we shall be satisfied with the goodness of your house, the holy place of your temple.
Septuagint Trans: Blessed is he whom you have chosen and taken to yourself; he shall dwell in your courts. We shall be filled with the good things of your house; holy is your temple, wondrous in righteousness.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Blessed is he whom you have chosen and taken up; he shall dwell in your courts. We shall be filled with the good things of your house; holy is your temple.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Blessed is he whom you have chosen and taken up; he shall dwell in your courts. We shall be filled with the good things of your house, with the holiness of your temple.

Hebrew Trans: With awesome deeds in righteousness you answer us, O God of our salvation, the confidence of all the ends of the earth and of the distant seas.
Septuagint Trans: Hearken to us, O God our savior, the hope of all the ends of the earth and of those far off at sea.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Wonderful in equity, hear us, O God our savior, the hope of all the ends of the earth and of those far off at sea.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Terrible in justice, hear us, O God our savior, the confidence of all the ends of the earth and of the distant sea.

Hebrew Trans: Who establishes the mountains by his strength, being girded with might.
Septuagint Trans: He who establishes the mountains by his strength, girded with power;
Gallican Psalter Trans: You who prepare the mountains in your strength, girded with power;
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Preparing the mountains in your strength, girded with power.

Hebrew Trans: Who stills the roaring of the seas, the roaring of their waves, and the tumult of the peoples.
Septuagint Trans: He who troubles the depths of the sea, the sounds of its waves—the nations shall be troubled.
Gallican Psalter Trans: You who trouble the depths of the sea, the sound of its waves. The nations shall be troubled,
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Calming the roar of the sea, the tumult of its waves, and the multitude of nations.

Hebrew Trans: And those who dwell at the ends of the earth are in awe of your signs; the goings forth of morning and evening you cause to sing for joy.
Septuagint Trans: And those who inhabit the ends of the earth shall fear because of your signs; the goings forth of morning and evening you shall make to rejoice.
Gallican Psalter Trans: and those who inhabit the borders shall fear at your signs. The risings of the morning and the evening you shall make joyful.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And those who dwell in the uttermost parts shall fear at your signs; you make the goings out of the morning and the evening to sing praise.

Hebrew Trans: You visit the earth and make it overflow; greatly you enrich it. The stream of God is full of water; you prepare their grain, for so you prepare it.
Septuagint Trans: You visited the earth and made it drunk; you multiplied it so as to enrich it. The river of God was filled with waters; you prepared their food, for thus is your preparation.
Gallican Psalter Trans: You have visited the earth and watered it abundantly; you have greatly enriched it. The river of God is filled with waters; you have prepared their food, for thus is its preparation.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Visit the earth and water it; enrich it with abundance. The river of God is full of water; you will prepare their grain, for so you have established it.

Hebrew Trans: Its furrows you water abundantly; you settle its ridges; with showers you soften it; its growth you bless.
Septuagint Trans: Drench its furrows, multiply its crops; in its drops of rain it shall rejoice as it springs up.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Drench its furrows, multiply its fruits; in its showers that which springs forth shall rejoice.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Drench its furrows, multiply its fruits; water it with rains and bless its growth.

Hebrew Trans: You crown the year of your bounty, and your paths drip with fatness.
Septuagint Trans: You shall bless the crown of the year of your goodness, and your fields shall be filled with fatness.
Gallican Psalter Trans: You shall bless the crown of the year of your goodness, and your fields shall be filled with abundance.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The year shall be crowned with your goodness, and your paths shall drip with richness.

Hebrew Trans: The pastures of the wilderness drip, and the hills gird themselves with rejoicing.
Septuagint Trans: The beautiful places of the wilderness shall grow fat, and the hills shall gird themselves with rejoicing.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The beautiful places of the wilderness shall grow fat, and the hills shall be girded with exultation.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The pastures of the wilderness shall grow fat, and the hills shall be girded with rejoicing.

Hebrew Trans: The meadows are clothed with flocks, and the valleys are covered with grain; they shout for joy, indeed they sing.
Septuagint Trans: The rams of the flocks have clothed themselves, and the valleys abound with grain; they shall cry out, and indeed they shall sing hymns.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The rams of the flock are clothed, and the valleys shall abound with grain; they shall cry out, and indeed they shall sing a hymn.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The flocks shall be clothed with lambs, and the valleys shall abound with grain; they shall overflow and sing.

Hebrew Trans: To the choirmaster. A song. A psalm. Shout joyfully to God, all the earth!
Septuagint Trans: For the end; a song of a psalm of resurrection. Make a joyful noise to God, all the earth!
Gallican Psalter Trans: To the end, a canticle psalm of the resurrection. Shout joyfully to God, all the earth.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: To the victor, a song of a psalm. Shout joyfully to God, all the earth.

Hebrew Trans: Sing the glory of His name; make His praise glorious.
Septuagint Trans: Sing praise to his name; give glory to his praise.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Sing a psalm to His name; give glory to His praise.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Sing the glory of His name; give glory to His praise.

Hebrew Trans: Say to God, "How awesome are Your works! Through the greatness of Your power Your enemies shall submit themselves to You.
Septuagint Trans: Say to God, "How fearful are your works! In the greatness of your power your enemies shall cringe before you."
Gallican Psalter Trans: Say to God: How terrible are Your works, O Lord! In the multitude of Your power, Your enemies shall lie to You.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Say to God: How terrible is Your work! In the multitude of Your strength, Your enemies shall lie to You.

Hebrew Trans: All the earth shall worship You and shall sing praises to You; they shall sing praises to Your name." Selah.
Septuagint Trans: Let all the earth worship you and sing praise to you; let them sing praise to your name. Selah.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Let all the earth adore You, and sing to You; let them sing a psalm to Your name. Diapsalma.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Let all the earth adore You and sing to You; let it sing to Your name forever.

Hebrew Trans: Come and see the works of God; He is awesome in His deeds toward the children of men.
Septuagint Trans: Come and see the works of God; he is fearful in his counsels beyond the sons of men.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Come and see the works of God: terrible in His counsels over the sons of men.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Come and see the works of God: terrible in His counsels over the sons of men.

Hebrew Trans: He turned the sea into dry land; they crossed through the river on foot; there let us rejoice in Him.
Septuagint Trans: He who turns the sea into dry land—through the river they shall pass on foot; there we shall rejoice in him.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Who turned the sea into dry land; in the river they shall pass through on foot: there we shall rejoice in Him.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: He turned the sea into dry land; through the river they shall pass on foot: there we shall rejoice in Him.

Hebrew Trans: He rules by His might forever; His eyes watch over the nations; let not the rebellious exalt themselves. Selah.
Septuagint Trans: He who rules by his power forever—his eyes look upon the nations; let not those who provoke him exalt themselves. Selah.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Who rules by His power forever; His eyes look upon the nations: let not those who provoke Him be exalted in themselves. Diapsalma.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Who rules in His strength forever; His eyes behold the nations: let not those who are unbelieving be exalted in themselves forever.

Hebrew Trans: Bless our God, O peoples, and make the voice of His praise to be heard,
Septuagint Trans: Bless our God, O nations, and make the voice of his praise to be heard,
Gallican Psalter Trans: Bless our God, O you nations, and make the voice of His praise to be heard.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Bless our God, O peoples, and make the voice of His praise to be heard.

Hebrew Trans: who sets our soul among the living, and does not allow our feet to be moved.
Septuagint Trans: who has set my soul in life and has not allowed my feet to be shaken.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Who has set my soul to life, and has not allowed my feet to be moved.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Who has set our soul in life, and has not given our feet to be moved.

Hebrew Trans: For You, O God, have tested us; You have refined us as silver is refined.
Septuagint Trans: For you have tested us, O God; you have refined us as silver is refined.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For You have tested us, O God; You have tried us by fire, as silver is tried.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For You have proved us, O God; You have refined us by fire, as silver is refined.

Hebrew Trans: You brought us into the net; You laid affliction upon our loins.
Septuagint Trans: You brought us into the snare; you laid afflictions upon our back.
Gallican Psalter Trans: You brought us into the snare; You laid tribulations upon our back.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: You brought us into a siege; You placed a burden upon our back.

Hebrew Trans: You caused men to ride over our heads; we went through fire and through water, but You brought us out to abundance.
Septuagint Trans: You caused men to ride over our heads; we passed through fire and water, and you brought us out into refreshment.
Gallican Psalter Trans: You set men over our heads; we passed through fire and water, and You brought us out into refreshment.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: You set men over our head; we passed through fire and water, and You brought us out into refreshment.

Hebrew Trans: I will come into Your house with burnt offerings; I will pay You my vows,
Septuagint Trans: I will enter your house with whole burnt offerings; I will pay you my vows,
Gallican Psalter Trans: I will go into Your house with burnt offerings; I will pay You my vows,
Hebrew Psalter Trans: I will enter Your house with burnt offerings; I will pay You my vows,

Hebrew Trans: which my lips uttered and my mouth spoke when I was in distress.
Septuagint Trans: which my lips distinguished and my mouth spoke in my affliction.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Which my lips distinguished, and my mouth spoke, in my tribulation.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: which my lips promised, and my mouth spoke, when I was in tribulation.

Hebrew Trans: Burnt offerings of fatlings I will offer to You, with the incense of rams; I will offer bulls with goats. Selah.
Septuagint Trans: I will offer up to you whole burnt offerings full of marrow, with incense and rams; I will sacrifice to you oxen with goats. Selah.
Gallican Psalter Trans: I will offer to You burnt offerings full of marrow, with the incense of rams; I will offer to You oxen with goats. Diapsalma.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: I will offer to You burnt offerings full of marrow, with the incense of rams; I will offer oxen with goats forever.

Hebrew Trans: Come, hear, all you who fear God, and I will declare what He has done for my soul.
Septuagint Trans: Come, hear, and I will recount, all you who fear God, what great things he has done for my soul.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Come, hear, and I will tell, all you who fear God, how great things He has done for my soul.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Come, hear, and I will tell, all you who fear God, how great things He has done for my soul.

Hebrew Trans: I cried to Him with my mouth, and high praise was under my tongue.
Septuagint Trans: I cried to him with my mouth, and I exalted him with my tongue.
Gallican Psalter Trans: I cried to Him with my mouth, and I exalted Him under my tongue.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: I cried to Him with my mouth, and I exalted Him with my tongue.

Hebrew Trans: If I had regarded iniquity in my heart, the Lord would not have heard.
Septuagint Trans: If I regarded iniquity in my heart, let the Lord not hear me.
Gallican Psalter Trans: If I have regarded iniquity in my heart, let the Lord not hear me.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: If I have regarded iniquity in my heart, let the Lord not hear me.

Hebrew Trans: But surely God has heard; He has attended to the voice of my prayer.
Septuagint Trans: Therefore God has heard me; he has attended to the voice of my supplication.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Therefore God has heard me; He has attended to the voice of my supplication.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Therefore God has heard, and has attended to the voice of my supplication.

Hebrew Trans: Blessed be God, who has not turned away my prayer, nor His lovingkindness from me.
Septuagint Trans: Blessed be God, who has not turned away my prayer, nor his mercy from me.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Blessed be God, who has not removed my prayer, nor His mercy from me.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Blessed be God, who has not removed my prayer, nor His mercy from me.

Hebrew Trans: For the chief musician, on stringed instruments, a psalm, a song.
Septuagint Trans: For the end, among hymns, a psalm of a song.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Unto the end, in hymns, a psalm of a canticle.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: To the victor, in psalms, a song of a poem.

Hebrew Trans: May God be gracious unto us and bless us; may He cause His face to shine upon us. Selah.
Septuagint Trans: May God have mercy on us and bless us; may he cause his face to shine upon us. Selah.
Gallican Psalter Trans: May God have mercy on us and bless us; may He cause His face to shine upon us, and have mercy on us. Diapsalma.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: May God have mercy on us and bless us; may He cause His face to shine upon us always.

Hebrew Trans: That Your way may be known upon the earth, Your salvation among all nations.
Septuagint Trans: That your way may be known upon the earth, your salvation among all nations.
Gallican Psalter Trans: That we may know Your way upon the earth, Your salvation among all nations.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: That Your way may be known on earth, Your salvation among all nations.

Hebrew Trans: Let the peoples praise You, O God; let the peoples praise You, all of them.
Septuagint Trans: Let the peoples give thanks to you, O God; let all the peoples give thanks to you.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Let the peoples confess to You, O God; let all the peoples confess to You.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Let the peoples praise You, O God; let all the peoples praise You.

Hebrew Trans: Let the nations be glad and sing for joy, for You judge the peoples with equity, and the nations upon the earth, You guide them. Selah.
Septuagint Trans: Let the nations be glad and rejoice, for you judge the peoples with uprightness, and you guide the nations upon the earth. Selah.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Let the nations be glad and rejoice, for You judge the peoples with equity, and You direct the nations upon the earth. Diapsalma.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Let the nations be glad and sing praise, for You judge the peoples with equity, and You are the everlasting guide of the nations that are on the earth.

Hebrew Trans: Let the peoples praise You, O God; let the peoples praise You, all of them.
Septuagint Trans: Let the peoples give thanks to you, O God; let all the peoples give thanks to you.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Let the peoples confess to You, O God; let all the peoples confess to You.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Let the peoples praise You, O God; let all the peoples praise You.

Hebrew Trans: The earth has yielded her increase; may God, our God, bless us.
Septuagint Trans: The earth has yielded its fruit; may God, our God, bless us.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The earth has yielded its fruit; may God, our God, bless us.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The earth has yielded its fruit; may God, our God, bless us.

Hebrew Trans: May God bless us, and let all the ends of the earth fear Him.
Septuagint Trans: May God bless us, and let all the ends of the earth fear him.
Gallican Psalter Trans: May God bless us, and may all the ends of the earth fear Him.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: May God bless us, and may all the ends of the earth fear Him.

Hebrew Trans: For the choirmaster. Of David. A psalm, a song.
Septuagint Trans: For the end; by David, a psalm of a song.
Gallican Psalter Trans: To the end. A psalm of a canticle by David.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: To the victor, a psalm of David, a song.

Hebrew Trans: Let God arise, let His enemies be scattered, and let those who hate Him flee before Him.
Septuagint Trans: Let God arise, and let his enemies be scattered, and let those who hate him flee from his face.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Let God arise, and let his enemies be scattered: and let those who hate him flee from his face.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Let God arise, and let his enemies be scattered, and let those who hate him flee from his face.

Hebrew Trans: As smoke is driven away, so drive them away; as wax melts before fire, so let the wicked perish before God.
Septuagint Trans: As smoke vanishes, let them vanish; as wax melts before the face of fire, so let the sinners perish from the face of God.
Gallican Psalter Trans: As smoke vanishes, let them vanish; as wax melts before the face of fire, so let sinners perish before the face of God.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: As smoke vanishes, let them vanish; as wax melts before the fire, let the wicked perish before the face of God.

Hebrew Trans: But let the righteous be glad; let them exult before God, and let them rejoice with gladness.
Septuagint Trans: And let the righteous be glad; let them rejoice before God; let them be delighted with gladness.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And let the just feast; let them exult in the sight of God; let them delight in gladness.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: But let the just rejoice; let them exult in the sight of God and be glad in joy.

Hebrew Trans: Sing to God, make music to His name; raise up a highway for Him who rides through the deserts; by Yah is His name, and exult before Him.
Septuagint Trans: Sing to God, make music to his name; make a way for him who rides upon the west — Lord is his name — and rejoice before him. They shall be troubled before his face,
Gallican Psalter Trans: Sing to God, sing a psalm to his name: make a way for him who ascends over the west. The Lord is his name, and exult in his sight; they shall be troubled before his face.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Sing to God, chant to his name; prepare a way for him who ascends through the deserts—the Lord is his name—and exult before him.

Hebrew Trans: A father of orphans and a judge of widows is God in His holy dwelling.
Septuagint Trans: the father of the orphans and the judge of the widows; God is in his holy place.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The father of orphans and the judge of widows: God in his holy place.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: A father of orphans and a defender of widows is God in his holy dwelling place.

Hebrew Trans: God settles the solitary in a home; He brings out prisoners into prosperity; but the rebellious dwell in a parched land.
Septuagint Trans: God settles the solitary in a house, leading out those who are bound in courage; likewise the rebellious, those who dwell in tombs.
Gallican Psalter Trans: God makes those of one manner to dwell in a house; he leads out the bound in strength, likewise those who provoke, who dwell in sepulchres.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: God makes the solitary to dwell in a house; he leads forth the bound in strength; but the unbelieving have dwelt in parched lands.

Hebrew Trans: O God, when You went out before Your people, when You marched through the wilderness, Selah,
Septuagint Trans: O God, when you went forth before your people, when you passed through the wilderness — Selah —
Gallican Psalter Trans: O God, when you went forth in the sight of your people, when you passed through the desert. Selah.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: O God, when you went forth before your people and walked through the wilderness forever,

Hebrew Trans: the earth trembled, the heavens also dropped rain before God; this Sinai trembled before God, the God of Israel.
Septuagint Trans: the earth quaked, and indeed the heavens dropped rain before the face of God — this Sinai — before the face of the God of Israel.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The earth was moved, for the heavens distilled at the face of the God of Sinai, at the face of the God of Israel.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: the earth was moved, and the heavens dripped at your presence, O God—this is Sinai—at the presence of God, the God of Israel.

Hebrew Trans: A bountiful rain You shed abroad, O God; Your inheritance, when it was weary, You established it.
Septuagint Trans: O God, you will set apart a willing rain for your inheritance, and it was weakened, but you restored it.
Gallican Psalter Trans: You shall set apart a willing rain, O God, for your inheritance: and it was weakened, but you yourself perfected it.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: You lifted up a willing rain, O God; your inheritance was weary, and you strengthened it.

Hebrew Trans: Your congregation dwelt in it; You provided in Your goodness for the poor, O God.
Septuagint Trans: Your living creatures dwell in it; you prepared in your goodness for the poor, O God.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Your creatures shall dwell in it; you have prepared in your sweetness for the poor, O God.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Your creatures dwelt in it; you prepared in your goodness for the poor, O God.

Hebrew Trans: The Lord gives the word; the women who proclaim the tidings are a great host.
Septuagint Trans: The Lord shall give a word to those who proclaim good tidings with great power.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The Lord shall give the word to those who evangelize with great power.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The Lord will give the word to those women who announce it with great strength.

Hebrew Trans: Kings of armies flee, they flee, and she who stays at home divides the spoil.
Septuagint Trans: The king of the powers of the beloved — and for the beauty of the house — to divide the spoils.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The king of powers of the beloved, of the beloved, and of the beauty of the house, to divide the spoils.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Kings of armies shall be allied, shall be allied, and the beauty of the house shall divide the spoils.

Hebrew Trans: Though you lie down among the sheepfolds, the wings of a dove are covered with silver, and her feathers with shimmering gold.
Septuagint Trans: If you should sleep between the lots, the wings of a dove covered with silver, and her back with the greenness of gold. Selah.
Gallican Psalter Trans: If you sleep among the midst of the lots, the wings of a dove covered with silver, and the hinder parts of her back in the paleness of gold.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: If you sleep among the midst of the borders, the wings of a dove covered with silver, and its back feathers in the greenness of gold.

Hebrew Trans: When the Almighty scattered kings in it, it snowed on Zalmon.
Septuagint Trans: When the Heavenly One separates kings upon it, they shall be made white as snow in Selmon.
Gallican Psalter Trans: When the Heavenly One discerns kings over her, they shall be made white as snow in Selmon.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: When the Almighty scattered kings in it, it was made white as snow in Selmon.

Hebrew Trans: The mountain of God is the mountain of Bashan; a mountain of many peaks is the mountain of Bashan.
Septuagint Trans: The mountain of God is a rich mountain, a curdled mountain, a rich mountain.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The mountain of God is a fat mountain; a curdled mountain, a fat mountain.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The mountain of God is a rich mountain, a lofty mountain, a rich mountain.

Hebrew Trans: Why do you look with envy, O mountains of many peaks, at the mountain God desired for His abode? Indeed, the LORD will dwell there forever.
Septuagint Trans: Why do you suppose, O curdled mountains? This is the mountain in which God was pleased to dwell; indeed the Lord shall tabernacle in it forever.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Why do you look with envy, you curdled mountains? The mountain in which it has pleased God to dwell in it; for indeed the Lord will dwell there to the end.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Why do you contend, O lofty mountains, against the mountain which God has chosen to dwell in? Indeed the Lord will dwell there forever.

Hebrew Trans: The chariots of God are myriads, thousands upon thousands; the Lord is among them, Sinai is in the holy place.
Septuagint Trans: The chariot of God is ten-thousandfold, thousands of prospering ones; the Lord is among them, in Sinai, in the holy place.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The chariot of God is ten-thousandfold, thousands of those rejoicing: the Lord is among them, in Sinai, in the holy place.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The chariots of God are innumerable, thousands upon thousands abounding; the Lord is among them, in Sinai, in the holy place.

Hebrew Trans: You have ascended on high, You have led captivity captive; You have received gifts among men, and even among the rebellious, that the LORD God might dwell there.
Septuagint Trans: You ascended on high; you led captivity captive; you received gifts among men, for even the disobedient, that the Lord God might dwell among them. Blessed be the Lord.
Gallican Psalter Trans: You have ascended on high; you have led captivity captive; you have received gifts among men; for indeed those not believing, that the Lord God might dwell there.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: You have ascended on high; you have led captivity captive; you have received gifts among men, even those who do not believe, that the Lord God might dwell there.

Hebrew Trans: Blessed be the Lord, day by day He bears our burden, the God of our salvation. Selah.
Septuagint Trans: Blessed be the Lord day by day; the God of our salvation shall prosper us. Selah.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Blessed be the Lord day by day; the God of our salvation shall make our journey prosperous. Selah.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Blessed be the Lord day by day; the God of our salvation will bear us forever.

Hebrew Trans: God is to us a God of deliverances, and to the LORD, the Lord, belong the escapes from death.
Septuagint Trans: Our God is a God of salvation, and to the Lord, the Lord, belong the exits of death.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Our God is the God of salvation: and to the Lord, the Lord belong the exits of death.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Our God is the God of salvation, and to the Lord God belongs the escape from death.

Hebrew Trans: Surely God will crush the head of His enemies, the hairy scalp of him who walks about in his guilt.
Septuagint Trans: But God shall crush the heads of his enemies, the hairy crown of those who walk in their trespasses.
Gallican Psalter Trans: But surely God shall crush the heads of his enemies, the crown of the hair of those who walk about in their sins.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: But truly God shall crush the heads of his enemies, the crown of hair of those who walk in their sins.

Hebrew Trans: The Lord said, "I will bring back from Bashan, I will bring them back from the depths of the sea,
Septuagint Trans: The Lord said: I will bring back from Bashan, I will bring back from the depths of the sea,
Gallican Psalter Trans: The Lord said: I will turn them from Bashan, I will turn them into the depths of the sea,
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The Lord said: From Bashan I will turn, I will turn from the depths of the sea,

Hebrew Trans: that your foot may shatter them in blood, that the tongue of your dogs may have its portion from the enemies."
Septuagint Trans: so that your foot may be dipped in blood, the tongue of your dogs in the blood of enemies, from him.
Gallican Psalter Trans: That your foot may be dipped in the blood, the tongue of your dogs from the enemies, from him.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: that your foot may be dipped in blood, that the tongue of your dogs may have its portion from the enemies, from himself.

Hebrew Trans: They have seen Your processions, O God, the processions of my God, my King, into the sanctuary.
Septuagint Trans: Your processions were seen, O God, the processions of my God, my King, who is in the holy place.
Gallican Psalter Trans: They have seen your processions, O God, the processions of my God, my king, who is in the holy place.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: They have seen your processions, O God, the processions of my God, my king, into the holy place.

Hebrew Trans: The singers went before, the musicians after, in the midst of maidens playing timbrels.
Septuagint Trans: The rulers went before, joined with those playing instruments, in the midst of young women beating drums.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The princes went before, joined with those singing psalms, in the midst of young women playing timbrels.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The singers went before, those who played instruments followed after, in the midst of young women playing timbrels.

Hebrew Trans: In the assemblies bless God, the LORD, you who are from the fountain of Israel.
Septuagint Trans: In the congregations bless God, the Lord, from the fountains of Israel.
Gallican Psalter Trans: In the assemblies bless God the Lord, you who are from the fountains of Israel.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: In the assemblies bless God, the Lord, from the fountains of Israel.

Hebrew Trans: There is Benjamin, the youngest, ruling them, the princes of Judah in their throng, the princes of Zebulun, the princes of Naphtali.
Septuagint Trans: There is Benjamin the youngest in ecstasy, the rulers of Judah their leaders, the rulers of Zebulun, the rulers of Naphtali.
Gallican Psalter Trans: There is Benjamin, the youngest, in ecstasy of mind; the princes of Judah, their leaders; the princes of Zebulun, the princes of Naphtali.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: There is Benjamin, the youngest, leading them; the princes of Judah in their purple, the princes of Zebulun, the princes of Naphtali.

Hebrew Trans: Your God has commanded your strength; show strength, O God, You who have acted on our behalf.
Septuagint Trans: Command, O God, with your strength; strengthen, O God, this which you have wrought for us.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Command, O God, your strength; confirm, O God, this which you have wrought in us.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Your God has commanded your strength; strengthen, O God, that which you have wrought for us.

Hebrew Trans: From Your temple above Jerusalem, kings shall bring gifts to You.
Septuagint Trans: From your temple at Jerusalem, kings shall bring gifts to you.
Gallican Psalter Trans: From your temple in Jerusalem, kings shall bring gifts to you.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: From your temple which is in Jerusalem, kings shall offer gifts to you.

Hebrew Trans: Rebuke the beast of the reeds, the herd of bulls among the calves of the peoples, trampling underfoot those with pieces of silver; He has scattered the peoples who delight in wars.
Septuagint Trans: Rebuke the beasts of the reed, the congregation of bulls among the heifers of the peoples, so that those tested by silver may not be shut out. Scatter the nations that desire wars.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Rebuke the wild beasts of the reeds, the congregation of bulls among the cows of the peoples, so that they may shut out those who have been tested with silver. Scatter the nations that desire wars.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Rebuke the beast of the reed, the congregation of the strong among the calves of the peoples, kicking against the wheels of silver; scatter the peoples who desire wars.

Hebrew Trans: Nobles shall come from Egypt; Cush shall hasten to stretch out her hands to God.
Septuagint Trans: Ambassadors shall come from Egypt; Ethiopia shall stretch out her hand to God.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Ambassadors shall come from Egypt; Ethiopia shall stretch out her hands to God.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Let them offer swiftly from Egypt; let Ethiopia hasten to stretch out its hands to God.

Hebrew Trans: O kingdoms of the earth, sing to God; make music to the Lord, Selah,
Septuagint Trans: O kingdoms of the earth, sing to God, make music to the Lord. Selah.
Gallican Psalter Trans: O kingdoms of the earth, sing to God; sing psalms to the Lord. Selah. Sing psalms to God,
Hebrew Psalter Trans: O kingdoms of the earth, sing to God; chant to the Lord forever,

Hebrew Trans: to Him who rides upon the heavens of heavens of old; behold, He utters His voice, a mighty voice.
Septuagint Trans: Make music to God, who rides upon the heaven of heavens toward the east; behold, he shall give in his voice a voice of power.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Who ascends above the heaven of heavens, to the east. Behold, he shall give to his voice a voice of power.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: who ascends above the heaven of heavens from the beginning. Behold, he will give to his voice a voice of strength.

Hebrew Trans: Ascribe strength to God; His majesty is over Israel, and His strength is in the skies.
Septuagint Trans: Give glory to God; his magnificence is over Israel, and his power is in the clouds.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Give glory to God over Israel; his magnificence and his power are in the clouds.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Give glory to God over Israel; his magnificence and his strength are in the heavens.

Hebrew Trans: Awesome is God from Your sanctuaries; the God of Israel, He gives strength and power to the people. Blessed be God.
Septuagint Trans: Wondrous is God in his holy ones; the God of Israel, he himself shall give power and strength to his people. Blessed be God.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Wondrous is God in his holy ones; the God of Israel, he himself shall give power and strength to his people. Blessed be God.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Terrible is God from his sanctuary; the God of Israel, he himself will give strength and power to the people. Blessed be God.

Hebrew Trans: For the choirmaster, upon Shoshannim [lilies], of David.
Septuagint Trans: For the end, concerning those who shall be changed; by David.
