Gallican Psalter Trans: Unto the end, for those who shall be changed. Of David.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: To the victor, for the lilies. Of David.

Hebrew Trans: Save me, O God, for the waters have come up to my soul.
Septuagint Trans: Save me, O God, for the waters have come in unto my soul.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Save me, O God, for the waters have entered even unto my soul.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Save me, O God, for the waters have come in even unto my soul.

Hebrew Trans: I have sunk in deep mire, and there is no foothold; I have come into the depths of waters, and the flood sweeps over me.
Septuagint Trans: I am stuck fast in the mire of the deep, and there is no foothold. I have come into the depths of the sea, and a storm has overwhelmed me.
Gallican Psalter Trans: I am stuck fast in the mire of the deep, and there is no firm ground. I have come into the depths of the sea, and the tempest has overwhelmed me.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: I am stuck fast in the mire of the deep, and I cannot stand firm. I have come into the depth of the waters, and the flood has overwhelmed me.

Hebrew Trans: I am weary with my crying; my throat is parched; my eyes fail while I wait for my God.
Septuagint Trans: I have labored with crying; my throat has become hoarse; my eyes have failed from hoping in my God.
Gallican Psalter Trans: I have labored with crying; my jaws have become hoarse; my eyes have failed while I hope in my God.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: I have labored with crying; my throat has become hoarse; my eyes have failed while waiting for my God.

Hebrew Trans: More numerous than the hairs of my head are those who hate me without cause; mighty are those who would destroy me, my enemies falsely; what I did not steal, I must then restore.
Septuagint Trans: Those who hate me without cause have been multiplied beyond the hairs of my head; my enemies who persecute me unjustly have been strengthened; what I did not seize, then I was made to repay.
Gallican Psalter Trans: They who hate me without cause are multiplied above the hairs of my head; they who have persecuted me, my enemies, are strengthened unjustly; what I did not seize, then I was paying back.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: They who hate me without cause are multiplied above the hairs of my head; those who persecute me, my enemies unjustly, are strengthened. What I did not take by robbery, I was then repaying.

Hebrew Trans: O God, You know my foolishness, and my guilt is not hidden from You.
Septuagint Trans: O God, you have known my foolishness, and my transgressions have not been hidden from you.
Gallican Psalter Trans: O God, you know my foolishness, and my offenses are not hidden from you.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: O God, you know my foolishness, and my sins are not hidden from you.

Hebrew Trans: Let not those who wait for You be ashamed through me, O Lord GOD of hosts; let not those who seek You be put to shame through me, O God of Israel.
Septuagint Trans: Let not those who wait on you be ashamed on my account, O Lord, Lord of hosts; let not those who seek you be put to disgrace on my account, O God of Israel.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Let not those who wait for you be ashamed in me, O Lord, Lord of hosts; let not those who seek you be confounded on my account, O God of Israel.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Let not those who wait for you be confounded in me, O Lord, God of hosts; let not those who seek you be confounded in me, O God of Israel.

Hebrew Trans: For on account of You I have borne reproach; shame has covered my face.
Septuagint Trans: For on your account I have endured reproach; shame has covered my face.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For because of you I have endured reproach; confusion has covered my face.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For because of you I have borne reproach; confusion has covered my face.

Hebrew Trans: I have become a stranger to my brothers, and a foreigner to the sons of my mother.
Septuagint Trans: I became estranged to my brothers, and a stranger to the sons of my mother.
Gallican Psalter Trans: I have become a stranger to my brothers, and a pilgrim to the sons of my mother.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: I have become a stranger to my brothers, and a foreigner to the sons of my mother.

Hebrew Trans: For the zeal of Your house has consumed me, and the reproaches of those who reproach You have fallen upon me.
Septuagint Trans: For the zeal of your house has consumed me, and the reproaches of those who reproach you have fallen upon me.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For the zeal of your house has consumed me, and the reproaches of those who reproached you have fallen upon me.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For the zeal of your house has consumed me, and the reproaches of those who reproach you have fallen upon me.

Hebrew Trans: And I wept with fasting of my soul, and it became a reproach to me.
Septuagint Trans: And I bowed down my soul with fasting, and it became a reproach to me.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And I covered my soul in fasting, and it was made a reproach to me.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And I wept over my soul with fasting, and it was made a reproach to me.

Hebrew Trans: And I made sackcloth my garment, and I became a byword to them.
Septuagint Trans: And I made sackcloth my garment, and I became a proverb to them.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And I made sackcloth my garment, and I became a parable to them.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And I made sackcloth my garment, and I became a parable to them.

Hebrew Trans: Those who sit in the gate talk about me, and I am the song of the drunkards.
Septuagint Trans: Those who sit at the gate talked against me, and those who drink wine sang songs about me.
Gallican Psalter Trans: They who sat in the gate spoke against me, and they who drank wine sang psalms against me.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Those who sat in the gate spoke against me, and those who drank wine sang about me.

Hebrew Trans: But as for me, my prayer is to You, O LORD, in an acceptable time; O God, in the abundance of Your lovingkindness, answer me in the truth of Your salvation.
Septuagint Trans: But as for me, my prayer is to you, O Lord; it is a time of good pleasure. O God, in the abundance of your mercy, hear me in the truth of your salvation.
Gallican Psalter Trans: But as for me, my prayer is to you, O Lord; it is the time of your good pleasure. O God, in the multitude of your mercy, hear me in the truth of your salvation.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: But as for me, my prayer is to you, O Lord; it is the time of reconciliation. O God, in the multitude of your mercy, hear me, in the truth of your salvation.

Hebrew Trans: Deliver me from the mire, and let me not sink; let me be delivered from those who hate me and from the depths of waters.
Septuagint Trans: Save me from the mire, that I may not be stuck fast; let me be delivered from those who hate me and from the depths of the waters.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Rescue me from the mire, that I may not be stuck fast; deliver me from those who hate me and from the deep waters.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Rescue me from the mire, that I may not be stuck fast; deliver me from those who hate me and from the deep waters.

Hebrew Trans: Let not the flood of waters sweep over me, and let not the deep swallow me up, and let not the pit shut its mouth upon me.
Septuagint Trans: Let not the storm of water overwhelm me, nor let the deep swallow me up, nor let the pit close its mouth over me.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Let not the tempest of water overwhelm me, nor let the deep swallow me up, nor let the pit shut its mouth upon me.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Let not the flood of water overwhelm me, nor let the deep swallow me up, and let not the pit close its mouth over me.

Hebrew Trans: Answer me, O LORD, for Your lovingkindness is good; according to the abundance of Your mercies, turn to me.
Septuagint Trans: Hear me, O Lord, for your mercy is good; according to the multitude of your compassions, look upon me.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Hear me, O Lord, for your mercy is kind; according to the multitude of your compassions, look upon me.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Hear me, O Lord, for your mercy is good; according to the multitude of your compassions, look upon me.

Hebrew Trans: And do not hide Your face from Your servant, for I am in distress; answer me speedily.
Septuagint Trans: Do not turn your face away from your servant, for I am afflicted; quickly hear me.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And turn not your face away from your servant, for I am in trouble; hear me speedily.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And do not hide your face from your servant, for I am in tribulation; hear me speedily.

Hebrew Trans: Draw near to my soul, redeem it; on account of my enemies, ransom me.
Septuagint Trans: Draw near to my soul and redeem it; on account of my enemies, deliver me.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Attend to my soul and deliver it; because of my enemies, rescue me.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Draw near to my soul; redeem it. Because of my enemies, deliver me.

Hebrew Trans: You know my reproach and my shame and my disgrace; before You are all my adversaries.
Septuagint Trans: For you know my reproach and my shame and my disgrace; before you are all who afflict me.
Gallican Psalter Trans: You know my reproach, and my confusion, and my shame; in your sight are all who trouble me.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: You know my reproach and my confusion and my shame; before you are all my adversaries.

Hebrew Trans: Reproach has broken my heart, and I am sick; and I looked for sympathy, but there was none, and for comforters, but I found none.
Septuagint Trans: My soul expected reproach and misery; and I waited for someone to grieve with me, and there was none, and for comforters, and I found none.
Gallican Psalter Trans: My heart has expected reproach and misery; and I waited for one who would grieve together with me, and there was none, and for one who would comfort me, and I found none.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: My heart has been broken by reproach, and I am in despair; and I waited for one who would grieve with me, and there was none, and for one who would console me, and I found none.

Hebrew Trans: And they gave me gall for my food, and for my thirst they gave me vinegar to drink.
Septuagint Trans: And they gave gall for my food, and for my thirst they gave me vinegar to drink.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And they gave gall for my food, and in my thirst they gave me vinegar to drink.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And they gave gall for my food, and in my thirst they gave me vinegar to drink.

Hebrew Trans: Let their table before them become a snare, and their peace offerings a trap.
Septuagint Trans: Let their table become a snare before them, and a recompense, and a stumbling block.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Let their table become a snare before them, and a retribution, and a stumbling block.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Let their table become a snare before them, and their retributions a cause of stumbling.

Hebrew Trans: Let their eyes be darkened so that they cannot see, and make their loins tremble continually.
Septuagint Trans: Let their eyes be darkened so as not to see, and bow down their back continually.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Let their eyes be darkened that they may not see, and bow down their back always.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Let their eyes be darkened so that they may not see, and bow down their back always.

Hebrew Trans: Pour out Your indignation upon them, and let the fierceness of Your anger overtake them.
Septuagint Trans: Pour out your anger upon them, and let the fury of your wrath overtake them.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Pour out your wrath upon them, and let the fury of your anger take hold of them.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Pour out your indignation upon them, and let the wrath of your fury seize them.

Hebrew Trans: Let their encampment be desolate; in their tents let there be no one dwelling.
Septuagint Trans: Let their dwelling place be made desolate, and let there be no one dwelling in their tents.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Let their habitation be made desolate, and let there be none to dwell in their tents.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Let their dwelling place be made desolate; in their tents let there be none to dwell.

Hebrew Trans: For they persecute him whom You have struck, and they recount the pain of those You have wounded.
Septuagint Trans: For him whom you have struck, they have persecuted, and they have added to the pain of your wounded ones.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For him whom you have struck, they have persecuted, and they have added to the grief of my wounds.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For they persecuted him whom you had struck, and they recounted the sufferings of your wounded ones.

Hebrew Trans: Add iniquity upon their iniquity, and let them not come into Your righteousness.
Septuagint Trans: Add iniquity upon their iniquity, and let them not enter into your righteousness.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Add iniquity upon their iniquity, and let them not enter into your justice.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Add iniquity upon their iniquity, and let them not come into your justice.

Hebrew Trans: Let them be blotted out of the book of the living, and let them not be written with the righteous.
Septuagint Trans: Let them be blotted out of the book of the living, and let them not be written with the righteous.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Let them be blotted out of the book of the living, and let them not be written with the just.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Let them be blotted out of the book of the living, and let them not be written with the just.

Hebrew Trans: But I am afflicted and in pain; Your salvation, O God, shall set me on high.
Septuagint Trans: I am poor and in pain; and the salvation of your face, O God, has upheld me.
Gallican Psalter Trans: I am poor and sorrowful; your salvation, O God, has lifted me up.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: But I am poor and sorrowful; your salvation, O God, will lift me up.

Hebrew Trans: I will praise the name of God with a song, and I will magnify Him with thanksgiving.
Septuagint Trans: I will praise the name of God with a song; I will magnify him with praise.
Gallican Psalter Trans: I will praise the name of God with a canticle; I will magnify him in praise.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: I will praise the name of God with a song, and I will magnify him with thanksgiving.

Hebrew Trans: And it shall please the LORD more than an ox, a bull with horns and hooves.
Septuagint Trans: And it shall please God more than a young calf putting forth horns and hooves.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And it shall please God more than a young calf producing horns and hooves.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And it shall please the Lord more than a young bull putting forth horns and hooves.

Hebrew Trans: The humble have seen it and are glad; you who seek God, let your hearts live.
Septuagint Trans: Let the poor see and be glad; seek God, and your soul shall live.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Let the poor see and rejoice; seek God, and your soul shall live.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The meek shall see and be glad; you who seek God, your soul shall live.

Hebrew Trans: For the LORD hears the needy, and His prisoners He does not despise.
Septuagint Trans: For the Lord has heard the needy, and has not despised his prisoners.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For the Lord has heard the poor, and has not despised his prisoners.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For the Lord has heard the poor, and has not despised his prisoners.

Hebrew Trans: Let heaven and earth praise Him, the seas and everything that moves in them.
Septuagint Trans: Let the heavens and the earth praise him, the sea and all things that creep in them.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Let the heavens and the earth praise him, the sea and all creeping things in them.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Let the heavens and the earth praise him, the seas and everything that moves in them.

Hebrew Trans: For God will save Zion and will build the cities of Judah, and they shall dwell there and possess it.
Septuagint Trans: For God shall save Zion, and the cities of Judea shall be built up, and they shall dwell there and inherit it.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For God will save Zion, and the cities of Judah shall be built up, and they shall dwell there, and they shall acquire it by inheritance.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For God will save Zion and will build the cities of Judah, and they shall dwell there and possess it.

Hebrew Trans: And the seed of His servants shall inherit it, and those who love His name shall dwell in it.
Septuagint Trans: And the seed of his servants shall possess it, and those who love his name shall dwell in it.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And the seed of his servants shall possess it, and they who love
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And the seed of his servants shall possess it, and those who love his name shall dwell in it.

Hebrew Trans: For the chief musician, [a psalm] of David, to bring to remembrance.
Septuagint Trans: For the end, by David, for remembrance.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Unto the end, of David, in remembrance, because the Lord has saved me.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: To the victor, of David, for remembrance.

Hebrew Trans: O God, deliver me; O LORD, make haste to help me.
Septuagint Trans: O Lord, to save me; O God, attend to my help.
Gallican Psalter Trans: O God, attend to my help; O Lord, make haste to help me.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: O God, that you may deliver me; O Lord, that you may help me, make haste.

Hebrew Trans: Let them be ashamed and confounded who seek my soul; let them be turned backward and put to shame who desire my hurt.
Septuagint Trans: Let them be ashamed and confounded who seek my soul; let them be turned back and put to shame who wish me evil.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Let them be confounded and put to shame who seek my soul; let them be turned back and blush who wish me evil.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Let them be confounded and put to shame who seek my soul; let them be turned back and blush who wish evil upon me.

Hebrew Trans: Let them be turned back because of their shame, those who say, "Aha, aha!"
Septuagint Trans: Let them be turned back immediately, being ashamed, who say to me, "Well done! Well done!"
Gallican Psalter Trans: Let them be turned back immediately, blushing with shame, who say to me: "Well done, well done!"
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Let them be turned back to the mark of their confusion who say "Aha, aha!"

Hebrew Trans: Let all who seek You rejoice and be glad in You; and let those who love Your salvation say continually, "Let God be magnified."
Septuagint Trans: Let all who seek you rejoice and be glad in you, and let those who love your salvation say continually, "Let God be magnified!"
Gallican Psalter Trans: Let all who seek you exult and rejoice in you, and let those who love your salvation always say: "Let God be magnified."
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Let all who seek you rejoice and be glad in you, and let those who love your salvation always say, "Let God be magnified."

Hebrew Trans: But I am poor and needy; O God, make haste to me. You are my help and my deliverer; O LORD, do not delay.
Septuagint Trans: But I am poor and needy; O God, help me. You are my helper and my deliverer; O Lord, do not delay.
Gallican Psalter Trans: But I am needy and poor; O God, help me. You are my helper and my deliverer; O Lord, do not delay.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: But I am needy and poor; O God, make haste for me. You are my help and my deliverer; O Lord, do not delay.

Hebrew Trans: In you, O LORD, I have taken refuge; let me never be put to shame.
Septuagint Trans: Of David, of the sons of Jonadab and of those first taken captive. O God, in you I have hoped; let me not be put to shame forever.
Gallican Psalter Trans: A Psalm of David, of the sons of Jonadab and of the first captives. In you, O Lord, I have hoped; let me not be confounded forever.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: In you, O Lord, I have hoped; let me never be confounded.

Hebrew Trans: In your righteousness deliver me and rescue me; incline your ear to me and save me.
Septuagint Trans: In your righteousness deliver me and rescue me; incline your ear to me and save me.
Gallican Psalter Trans: In your justice deliver me and rescue me; incline your ear to me and save me.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: In your justice rescue me and deliver me; incline your ear to me and save me.

Hebrew Trans: Be to me a rock of dwelling, to come to continually; you have commanded to save me, for you are my rock and my fortress.
Septuagint Trans: Be to me a God who shields, and a fortified place to save me, for you are my stronghold and my refuge.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Be to me a God, a protector, and a fortified place, that you may save me; for you are my firmament and my refuge.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Be to me a strong dwelling place, that I may enter in continually; you have commanded that you would save me, for you are my rock and my fortress.

Hebrew Trans: My God, rescue me from the hand of the wicked, from the grasp of the unjust and the ruthless.
Septuagint Trans: My God, deliver me from the hand of the sinner, from the hand of the lawbreaker and the unjust.
Gallican Psalter Trans: My God, rescue me from the hand of the sinner, from the hand of the one acting against the law and of the unjust.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: My God, save me from the hand of the wicked, from the hand of the unjust and the harmful.

Hebrew Trans: For you are my hope, O Lord GOD, my trust from my youth.
Septuagint Trans: For you are my endurance, O Lord; the Lord is my hope from my youth.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For you are my patience, O Lord; O Lord, my hope from my youth.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For you are my expectation, O God; O Lord, my trust from my youth.

Hebrew Trans: Upon you I have leaned from the womb; from my mother's bowels you are the one who severed me; in you is my praise continually.
Septuagint Trans: Upon you I have been supported from the womb; from my mother's belly you are my protector; in you is my song of praise continually.
Gallican Psalter Trans: In you I have been confirmed from the womb; from the belly of my mother you are my protector; in you is my song always.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: By you I have been sustained from the womb; from my mother's belly you are my protector; in you is my praise always.

Hebrew Trans: I have been as a wonder to many, but you are my strong refuge.
Septuagint Trans: I have become as a wonder to many, and you are my mighty helper.
Gallican Psalter Trans: I have become as a wonder to many, and you are my strong helper.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: I have become as a wonder to many, and you are my strongest hope.

Hebrew Trans: Let my mouth be filled with your praise, all the day with your glory.
Septuagint Trans: Let my mouth be filled with praise, so that I may sing of your glory all the day long, and of your magnificence.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Let my mouth be filled with praise, that I may sing your glory, all the day long your greatness.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Let my mouth be filled with your praise, all the day long with your greatness.

Hebrew Trans: Do not cast me off in the time of old age; when my strength fails, do not forsake me.
Septuagint Trans: Do not cast me off in the time of old age; when my strength fails, do not forsake me.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Do not cast me off in the time of old age; when my strength shall fail, do not forsake me.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Do not cast me off in the time of old age; when my strength fails, do not forsake me.

Hebrew Trans: For my enemies speak against me, and those who watch for my life take counsel together,
Septuagint Trans: For my enemies have spoken against me, and those who watch for my soul have taken counsel together,
Gallican Psalter Trans: For my enemies have spoken against me, and those who watched my soul have taken counsel together,
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For my enemies have spoken against me, and those who watched for my soul have taken counsel together,

Hebrew Trans: Saying, "God has forsaken him; pursue and seize him, for there is none to deliver."
Septuagint Trans: saying, "God has forsaken him; pursue and seize him, for there is none to deliver him."
Gallican Psalter Trans: Saying: God has forsaken him; pursue and seize him, for there is none to deliver him.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Saying: "God has forsaken him; pursue and seize him, for there is no one to deliver him."

Hebrew Trans: O God, be not far from me; my God, hasten to my help.
Septuagint Trans: O God, be not far from me; O my God, attend to my help.
Gallican Psalter Trans: O God, be not far from me; my God, look to my help.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: O God, be not far from me; my God, hasten to help me.

Hebrew Trans: Let them be put to shame and consumed who are adversaries of my soul; let them be covered with reproach and dishonor who seek my harm.
Septuagint Trans: Let those who slander my soul be put to shame and fail; let those who seek evils against me be clothed in shame and disgrace.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Let them be confounded and fail who detract from my soul; let them be covered with confusion and shame who seek evils against me.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Let the adversaries of my soul be confounded and consumed; let them be covered with reproach and confusion who seek evil against me.

Hebrew Trans: But I will hope continually, and will add to all your praise.
Septuagint Trans: But I will always hope, and I will add to all your praise.
Gallican Psalter Trans: But I will always hope, and will add upon all your praise.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: But I will continually hope, and will add upon all your praises.

Hebrew Trans: My mouth shall tell of your righteousness, all the day of your salvation, for I do not know their numbers.
Septuagint Trans: My mouth will declare your righteousness all the day long, and your salvation, for I have not known letters.
Gallican Psalter Trans: My mouth shall declare your justice, all the day long your salvation, for I have not known learning.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: My mouth shall declare your justice, all the day long your salvation, for I have not known letters.

Hebrew Trans: I will come in the mighty deeds of the Lord GOD; I will make mention of your righteousness, yours alone.
Septuagint Trans: I will enter in the might of the Lord; O Lord, I will remember your righteousness alone.
Gallican Psalter Trans: I will enter into the powers of the Lord; O Lord, I will remember your justice alone.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: I shall enter into the strength of the Lord God; I will remember your justice alone.

Hebrew Trans: O God, you have taught me from my youth, and until now I declare your wondrous works.
Septuagint Trans: You have taught me, O God, from my youth, and until now I will declare your wondrous works.
Gallican Psalter Trans: O God, you have taught me from my youth, and until now I will declare your wonders.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: O God, you have taught me from my youth, and until now I will declare your wondrous works.

Hebrew Trans: And also unto old age and gray hairs, O God, do not forsake me, until I declare your arm to the generation, to all who are to come, your might.
Septuagint Trans: And even unto old age and advanced years, O God, do not forsake me, until I declare your arm to every generation that is to come—your power and your righteousness,
Gallican Psalter Trans: And even unto old age and gray hairs, O God, do not forsake me, until I declare your arm to every generation that is to come, your power
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Moreover, even unto old age and gray hairs, O God, do not forsake me, until I declare your arm to this generation, and your mighty deeds to all who are to come.

Hebrew Trans: And your righteousness, O God, reaches to the heights, you who have done great things—O God, who is like you?
Septuagint Trans: O God, even to the highest heights, the great things you have done. O God, who is like you?
Gallican Psalter Trans: And your justice, O God, even to the highest things, the great things you have done; O God, who is like you?
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And your justice, O God, even to the highest; how great things you have done, O God, who is like unto you?

Hebrew Trans: You who have shown me many and grievous troubles will revive me again, and from the depths of the earth you will bring me up again.
Septuagint Trans: How many afflictions, many and grievous, you have shown me! And turning back, you gave me life, and from the depths of the earth you brought me up again.
Gallican Psalter Trans: How many tribulations, many and grievous, you have shown me, and turning you have brought me to life, and from the depths of the earth you have brought me back again.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: You who have shown me many tribulations and afflictions; turning back, you will give us life, and from the depths of the earth you will bring us up again.

Hebrew Trans: You will increase my greatness and turn and comfort me.
Septuagint Trans: You increased your greatness, and turning back you comforted me, and from the depths of the earth you brought me up again.
Gallican Psalter Trans: You have multiplied your magnificence, and turning you have comforted me.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: You will multiply my greatness, and turning, you will comfort me.

Hebrew Trans: I also will praise you with the instrument of the harp for your faithfulness, O my God; I will sing praises to you with the lyre, O Holy One of Israel.
Septuagint Trans: For indeed I will give thanks to you with an instrument of psalmody for your truth, O God; I will sing to you with a harp, O Holy One of Israel.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For I also will praise you with the instruments of the psalm for your truth, O God; I will sing to you on the harp, O Holy One of Israel.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And I will praise you with the instruments of the psaltery for your truth, my God; I will sing to you on the harp, O Holy One of Israel.

Hebrew Trans: My lips will shout for joy when I sing praises to you, and my soul, which you have redeemed.
Septuagint Trans: My lips will rejoice when I sing to you, and my soul which you have redeemed.
Gallican Psalter Trans: My lips shall rejoice when I sing to you, and my soul which you have redeemed.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: My lips shall rejoice when I sing to you, and my soul which you have redeemed.

Hebrew Trans: My tongue also shall speak of your righteousness all the day long, for they are put to shame, for they are confounded, those who seek my harm.
Septuagint Trans: Moreover also my tongue will meditate on your righteousness all the day long, when those who seek evils against me are put to shame and confounded.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Moreover my tongue shall meditate on your justice all the day long, when those who seek evils against me shall have been confounded and put to shame.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Moreover, my tongue also shall meditate on your justice all the day long, for they are confounded and put to shame who seek evil against me.

Hebrew Trans: For Solomon. O God, give Your judgments to the king, and Your righteousness to the king's son.
Septuagint Trans: For Solomon. O God, give your judgment to the king, and your righteousness to the son of the king.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For Solomon. O God, give your judgment to the king, and your justice to the son of the king.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Of Solomon. O God, give judgment to the king, and your justice to the son of the king.

Hebrew Trans: May he judge Your people with righteousness, and Your poor with justice.
Septuagint Trans: To judge your people in righteousness, and your poor in justice.
Gallican Psalter Trans: To judge your people in justice, and your poor in judgment.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: He shall judge your people in justice, and your poor in judgment.

Hebrew Trans: Let the mountains bear peace to the people, and the hills, in righteousness.
Septuagint Trans: Let the mountains take up peace for your people, and the hills in righteousness.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Let the mountains receive peace for the people, and the hills justice.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The mountains shall bring peace to the people, and the hills justice.

Hebrew Trans: May he judge the poor of the people, save the children of the needy, and crush the oppressor.
Septuagint Trans: He shall judge the poor of the people and save the children of the needy, and shall bring low the oppressor.
Gallican Psalter Trans: He shall judge the poor of the people, and he shall save the sons of the poor, and he shall humble the slanderer.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: He shall judge the poor of the people, he shall save the children of the needy, and he shall crush the oppressor.

Hebrew Trans: May they fear You as long as the sun endures, and before the moon, generation of generations.
Septuagint Trans: And he shall endure alongside the sun, and before the moon, for generations of generations.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And he shall endure with the sun, and before the moon, through generations of generations.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And they shall fear you as long as the sun endures, and beyond the moon, through generation of generations.

Hebrew Trans: May he come down like rain upon mown grass, like showers that water the earth.
Septuagint Trans: And he shall come down like rain upon a fleece, and like drops falling upon the earth.
Gallican Psalter Trans: He shall descend like rain upon a fleece, and like drops falling upon the earth.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: He shall descend like rain upon a fleece, like drops watering the earth.

Hebrew Trans: In his days may the righteous flourish, and abundance of peace, until the moon is no more.
Septuagint Trans: In his days righteousness shall arise, and abundance of peace, until the moon be taken away.
Gallican Psalter Trans: In his days shall justice arise, and abundance of peace, until the moon be taken away.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: In his days justice shall spring forth, and abundance of peace, until the moon shall be no more.

Hebrew Trans: And may he rule from sea to sea, and from the river to the ends of the earth.
Septuagint Trans: And he shall have dominion from sea to sea, and from the river to the ends of the inhabited world.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And he shall have dominion from sea to sea, and from the river to the ends of the world.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And he shall rule from sea to sea, and from the river to the ends of the earth.

Hebrew Trans: Before him the desert-dwellers shall bow down, and his enemies shall lick the dust.
Septuagint Trans: Before him the Ethiopians shall fall down, and his enemies shall lick the dust.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Before him the Ethiopians shall fall down, and his enemies shall lick the earth.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Before him the Ethiopians shall fall down, and his enemies shall lick the dust.

Hebrew Trans: The kings of Tarshish and the islands shall bring tribute; the kings of Sheba and Seba shall offer gifts.
Septuagint Trans: The kings of Tharsis and the islands shall bring gifts; the kings of Arabia and Saba shall offer presents.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The kings of Tarshish and the islands shall offer gifts; the kings of Arabia and Sheba shall bring presents.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The kings of Tarshish and the islands shall offer gifts; the kings of Arabia and Sheba shall bring tribute.

Hebrew Trans: And all kings shall bow down to him; all nations shall serve him.
Septuagint Trans: And all kings shall worship him; all nations shall serve him.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And all kings shall worship him; all nations shall serve him.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And all kings shall worship him; all nations shall serve him.

Hebrew Trans: For he shall deliver the needy when he cries out, and the poor who has no helper.
Septuagint Trans: For he delivered the poor from the hand of the mighty, and the needy who had no helper.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For he has delivered the poor from the mighty, and the poor man who had no helper.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For he shall deliver the poor from the mighty, and the needy who has no helper.

Hebrew Trans: He shall have pity on the lowly and the needy, and the souls of the needy he shall save.
Septuagint Trans: He shall spare the poor and needy, and shall save the souls of the needy.
Gallican Psalter Trans: He shall spare the poor and the needy, and he shall save the souls of the poor.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: He shall spare the lowly and the poor, and he shall save the souls of the poor.

Hebrew Trans: From oppression and from violence he shall redeem their souls, and their blood shall be precious in his eyes.
Septuagint Trans: From usury and from injustice he shall redeem their souls, and their name shall be precious before him.
Gallican Psalter Trans: From usury and iniquity he shall redeem their souls, and honorable shall their name be before him.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: From usury and iniquity he shall redeem their souls, and precious shall their blood be before his eyes.

Hebrew Trans: And may he live, and may he give him of the gold of Sheba; and may he pray for him continually, all the day may he bless him.
Septuagint Trans: And he shall live, and there shall be given to him of the gold of Arabia, and they shall pray concerning him continually; all the day long they shall bless him.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And he shall live, and to him shall be given of the gold of Arabia, and they shall pray for him always; all the day long they shall bless him.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And he shall live, and to him shall be given of the gold of Sheba, and they shall pray for him continually; all the day long they shall bless him.

Hebrew Trans: Let there be an abundance of grain in the land; on the top of the mountains may its fruit rustle like Lebanon, and may they blossom from the city like the grass of the earth.
Septuagint Trans: There shall be a firm foundation in the earth upon the tops of the mountains; its fruit shall be exalted above Lebanon, and they shall flourish out of the city like grass of the earth.
Gallican Psalter Trans: There shall be a foundation in the earth on the tops of the mountains; its fruit shall be exalted above Lebanon, and they shall flourish from the city like the grass of the earth.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: There shall be an abundance of grain in the land; on the tops of the mountains its fruit shall be raised up like Lebanon, and they shall flourish from the city like the grass of the earth.

Hebrew Trans: May his name endure forever; before the sun may his name propagate, and may all nations be blessed in him; may they call him blessed.
Septuagint Trans: Let his name be blessed forever; his name endures before the sun, and all the tribes of the earth shall be blessed in him; all nations shall call him blessed.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Let his name be blessed forever; before the sun his name endures, and in him shall all the tribes of the earth be blessed; all nations shall call him blessed.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: His name shall be forever; beyond the sun his name shall endure, and in him all nations shall be blessed, and they shall call him blessed.

Hebrew Trans: Blessed be the LORD God, the God of Israel, who alone does wondrous things.
Septuagint Trans: Blessed be the Lord God, the God of Israel, who alone does wondrous things.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Blessed be the Lord God, the God of Israel, who alone does wondrous things.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Blessed be the Lord God, the God of Israel, who alone does wondrous things.

Hebrew Trans: And blessed be His glorious name forever, and may His glory fill all the earth. Amen and Amen.
Septuagint Trans: And blessed be the name of his glory forever and unto the age of ages, and all the earth shall be filled with his glory. So be it, so be it.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And blessed be the name of his majesty forever, and all the earth shall be filled with his majesty. So be it, so be it.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And blessed be the name of his glory forever, and all the earth shall be filled with his glory. Amen, amen.

Hebrew Trans: The prayers of David the son of Jesse are ended.
Septuagint Trans: The hymns of David, the son of Jesse, have come to an end.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The praises of David, the son of Jesse, are ended.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The prayers of David, the son of Jesse, are ended.

Hebrew Trans: A psalm of Asaph. Surely God is good to Israel, to those who are pure of heart.
Septuagint Trans: A psalm of Asaph. How good is God to Israel, to those who are upright in heart.
Gallican Psalter Trans: A psalm of Asaph. How good is the God of Israel to those who are upright in heart.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: A song of Asaph. Truly God is good to Israel, to those who are pure in heart.

Hebrew Trans: But as for me, my feet had almost slipped; my steps had nearly been poured out.
Septuagint Trans: But as for me, my feet were almost shaken; my steps were very nearly poured out.
Gallican Psalter Trans: But my feet were almost moved; my steps were nearly poured out.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: But my feet had almost stumbled; my steps had nearly slipped.

Hebrew Trans: For I was envious of the boastful; I saw the peace of the wicked.
Septuagint Trans: For I was jealous of the lawless, observing the peace of sinners.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Because I was zealous over the wicked, seeing the peace of sinners.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For I was envious of the wicked, seeing the peace of the ungodly.

Hebrew Trans: For there are no bonds in their death, and their body is fat.
Septuagint Trans: For there is no refusal of their death, and no firmness in their affliction.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Because there is no regard for their death, and no firmness in their affliction.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For they have no thought of their death, and their bodies are firm.

Hebrew Trans: They are not in the toil of mortals, and with mankind they are not plagued.
Septuagint Trans: They are not among the toils of men, and they shall not be scourged with men.
Gallican Psalter Trans: They are not in the labor of men, and they shall not be scourged with men.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: They are not in the toil of men, and they shall not be scourged with other men.

Hebrew Trans: Therefore pride is as a necklace to them; violence covers them as a garment.
Septuagint Trans: Therefore pride has taken hold of them; they have clothed themselves in their injustice and ungodliness.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Therefore pride has held them; they are covered with their iniquity and impiety.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Therefore pride has nourished them; they have clothed themselves with iniquity.

Hebrew Trans: Their eyes bulge out from fatness; the imaginations of their heart overflow.
Septuagint Trans: Their injustice shall come forth as from fatness; they have passed into the disposition of the heart.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Their iniquity shall come forth as if from fat; they have passed into the affection of the heart.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Their eyes have swelled out from fatness; the thoughts of their heart have overflowed.

Hebrew Trans: They mock and speak wickedly of oppression; from on high they speak.
Septuagint Trans: They deliberated and spoke wickedness; they spoke injustice on high.
Gallican Psalter Trans: They have thought and spoken in wickedness; they have spoken iniquity on high.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: They have scoffed and spoken in malice; they speak calumny from on high.

Hebrew Trans: They set their mouth against the heavens, and their tongue walks through the earth.
Septuagint Trans: They set their mouth against heaven, and their tongue passed over the earth.
Gallican Psalter Trans: They have set their mouth against heaven, and their tongue has passed through the earth.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: They have set their mouth against heaven, and their tongue has walked through the earth.

Hebrew Trans: Therefore his people return here, and waters of fullness are drained by them.
Septuagint Trans: Therefore my people shall turn here, and full days shall be found for them.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Therefore my people shall be turned back here, and full days shall be found in them.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Therefore his people will turn here, and full days shall be found among them.

Hebrew Trans: And they say, "How does God know? And is there knowledge in the Most High?"
Septuagint Trans: And they said, "How does God know? And is there knowledge in the Most High?"
Gallican Psalter Trans: And they said: How does God know? And is there knowledge in the Most High?
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And they said: "How does God know? And is there knowledge in the Most High?"

Hebrew Trans: Behold, these are the wicked, and those at ease in the world—they increase in wealth.
Septuagint Trans: Behold, these are sinners, and they prosper forever; they have obtained wealth.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Behold, these are sinners, and abounding in the world, they have obtained riches.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Behold, these are the ungodly, and they who prosper in the world have multiplied riches.

Hebrew Trans: Surely in vain I have cleansed my heart and washed my hands in innocence.
Septuagint Trans: And I said, "Then in vain have I justified my heart, and washed my hands among the innocent."
Gallican Psalter Trans: And I said: Then without cause have I justified my heart and washed my hands among the innocent.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Then have I cleansed my heart in vain and washed my hands in innocence?

Hebrew Trans: And I have been stricken all day long, and my chastisement comes every morning.
Septuagint Trans: And I was scourged all the day long, and my reproof came every morning.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And I have been scourged all the day, and my chastisement was in the morning.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And I have been scourged all day long, and my chastisement came in the mornings.

Hebrew Trans: If I had said, "I will speak thus," behold, I would have betrayed the generation of your children.
Septuagint Trans: If I said, "I will speak thus," behold, I would have been false to the generation of your children.
Gallican Psalter Trans: If I said: I will speak thus; behold, I have rejected the nation of your children.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: I said: If I shall speak thus, behold, I have abandoned the generation of your children.

Hebrew Trans: And I thought to understand this, but it was troublesome in my eyes,
Septuagint Trans: And I undertook to understand this — it is a labor before me —
Gallican Psalter Trans: And I thought to understand this; it is a labor before me.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And I thought that I might understand this—it is a labor in my eyes—

Hebrew Trans: Until I came into the sanctuaries of God; then I discerned their end.
Septuagint Trans: until I enter into the sanctuary of God and understand their final end.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Until I enter into the sanctuary of God and understand concerning their last ends.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Until I enter into the sanctuaries of God and understand their end.

Hebrew Trans: Surely on slippery places you set them; you cast them down to ruins.
Septuagint Trans: Surely, because of their deceitfulness you set things for them; you cast them down when they were lifted up.
Gallican Psalter Trans: But truly, on account of deceits you have set things for them; you cast them down while they were being lifted up.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Yet truly you have set them on slippery places; you have cast them down to destruction.

Hebrew Trans: How they have become a desolation in a moment! They are utterly consumed by terrors.
Septuagint Trans: How they came to desolation! Suddenly they failed; they perished because of their lawlessness.
Gallican Psalter Trans: How they have been brought to desolation! They have suddenly failed; they have perished because of their iniquity.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: How they have been laid waste! Suddenly they have failed; they are consumed as though they were not.

Hebrew Trans: As a dream upon awakening, O Lord, in the city you will despise their image.
Septuagint Trans: As a dream of one waking, O Lord, in your city you shall despise their image.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Like the dream of those who awake, O Lord, in your city you shall reduce their image to nothing.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Like the dream of one who awakens, O Lord, in your city you shall reduce their image to nothing.

Hebrew Trans: For my heart was embittered, and I was pierced in my kidneys.
Septuagint Trans: For my heart was inflamed, and my inward parts were changed.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Because my heart was inflamed and my reins were changed,
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For my heart was embittered, and my loins were like a smoldering fire.

Hebrew Trans: And I was brutish and did not know; I was as a beast before you.
Septuagint Trans: And I was brought to nothing and did not know; I became like a beast before you.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And I was brought to nothing and I did not know; I was made as a beast before you.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And I was foolish and ignorant; I was made as a beast before you.

Hebrew Trans: Yet I am continually with you; you have held my right hand.
Septuagint Trans: Yet I am continually with you; you held my right hand.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And I am always with you; you have held my right hand.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And I was always with you, and you held my right hand.

Hebrew Trans: With your counsel you will guide me, and afterward you will take me to glory.
Septuagint Trans: With your counsel you guided me, and with glory you received me.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And in your will you have led me, and with glory you have received me.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: By your counsel you shall guide me, and afterward you shall receive me in glory.

Hebrew Trans: Whom have I in heaven? And besides you I desire nothing on earth.
Septuagint Trans: For what do I have in heaven? And besides you, what have I desired upon the earth?
Gallican Psalter Trans: For what is there for me in heaven, and what have I desired from you upon earth?
Hebrew Psalter Trans: What is there for me in heaven? And with you I have desired nothing on earth.

Hebrew Trans: My flesh and my heart fail, but God is the rock of my heart and my portion forever.
Septuagint Trans: My heart and my flesh have failed; God of my heart and my portion is God forever.
Gallican Psalter Trans: My flesh and my heart have failed; God of my heart and God my portion forever.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: My flesh and my heart are consumed; the strength of my heart and my portion is God forever.

Hebrew Trans: For behold, those who are far from you shall perish; you destroy all who are unfaithful to you.
Septuagint Trans: For behold, those who distance themselves from you shall perish; you have destroyed all who go whoring from you.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For behold, those who make themselves far from you shall perish; you have destroyed all who go astray from you.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For behold, those who distance themselves from you shall perish; you have destroyed all who go whoring from you.

Hebrew Trans: But as for me, the nearness of God is my good; I have made the Lord GOD my refuge, to declare all your works.
Septuagint Trans: But for me, to cling to God is good — to place my hope in the Lord, so as to proclaim all your praises in the gates of the daughter of Zion.
Gallican Psalter Trans: But for me it is good to cling to God, to place my hope in the Lord God, that I may declare all your praises in the gates of the daughter of Sion.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: But for me, to draw near to God is good; I have placed my hope in the Lord God, that I may declare all your proclamations.

Hebrew Trans: A Maskil of Asaph. Why, O God, have you cast off forever? Why does your anger smoke against the flock of your pasture?
Septuagint Trans: An understanding psalm of Asaph. Why have you rejected us, O God, forever? Why has your anger burned against the sheep of your pasture?
Gallican Psalter Trans: The understanding of Asaph. Why, O God, have you cast us off to the end? Your fury is kindled against the sheep of your pasture.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: A lesson of Asaph. Why, O God, have you cast us off to the end? Your fury has smoked against the flock of your pasture.

Hebrew Trans: Remember your congregation, which you acquired of old, which you redeemed as the tribe of your inheritance, Mount Zion, in which you have dwelt.
Septuagint Trans: Remember your congregation, which you acquired from the beginning; you redeemed the rod of your inheritance, this Mount Zion in which you dwelt.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Remember your congregation, which you possessed from the beginning. You redeemed the rod of your inheritance, Mount Zion, in which you have dwelt.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Remember your congregation, which you possessed from the beginning, and redeemed the rod of your inheritance, Mount Zion in which you dwelt.

Hebrew Trans: Lift up your steps to the perpetual ruins; all the evil the enemy has done in the sanctuary.
Septuagint Trans: Lift up your hands against their arrogance forever — all the evil the enemy has done in your holy places.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Lift up your hands against their pride to the end. How much the enemy has wrought evil in the sanctuary!
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The height of your feet has been destroyed utterly; the enemy has done all evil things in the sanctuary.

Hebrew Trans: Your adversaries have roared in the midst of your meeting place; they have set up their signs as signs.
Septuagint Trans: And those who hate you have boasted in the midst of your feast; they set up their signs as signs, and they did not acknowledge it.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And those who hate you have gloried in the midst of your solemnity. They set up their signs as signs,
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Your enemies have roared in the midst of your covenant; they set up their signs as a trophy.

Hebrew Trans: It is known as one bringing axes upward into a thicket of trees.
Septuagint Trans: As though at the entrance above —
Gallican Psalter Trans: And they did not recognize it, as at the entrance above the highest, as though in a forest of trees with axes.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Manifest at the entrance above, in the thicket of trees, with axes.

Hebrew Trans: And now its carved works altogether with hatchet and hammers they smash.
Septuagint Trans: As in a thicket of trees with axes they cut down its doors; together with hatchet and chisel they smashed it.
Gallican Psalter Trans: They cut down its gates all together; with axe and hatchet they have brought it down.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And now its carvings together with hatchet and chisels they have scraped away.

Hebrew Trans: They have sent your sanctuary into fire; to the ground they have profaned the dwelling place of your name.
Septuagint Trans: They set fire to your sanctuary; they profaned the dwelling place of your name to the ground.
Gallican Psalter Trans: They set fire to your sanctuary; they polluted the tabernacle of your name on the earth.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: They have set fire to your sanctuary; they have defiled the dwelling place of your name to the ground.

Hebrew Trans: They said in their heart, "Let us crush them altogether." They have burned all the meeting places of God in the land.
Septuagint Trans: They said in their heart — they and their kindred together — "Come, let us burn all the feast days of God from the land."
Gallican Psalter Trans: They said in their heart, their kindred together: "Let us cause all the feast days of God to cease from the land."
Hebrew Psalter Trans: They said in their hearts, their posterity together: they burned all the solemn feasts of God in the land.

Hebrew Trans: Our signs we do not see; there is no longer a prophet, and there is none among us who knows how long.
Septuagint Trans: Our signs we do not see; there is no longer a prophet, and he will not know us anymore.
Gallican Psalter Trans: We do not see our signs; there is no longer a prophet, and he will not know us anymore.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Our signs we do not see; there is no longer a prophet, and there is none among us who knows how long.

Hebrew Trans: How long, O God, shall the adversary reproach? Shall the enemy blaspheme your name forever?
Septuagint Trans: How long, O God, will the enemy reproach? Will the adversary provoke your name forever?
Gallican Psalter Trans: How long, O God, will the enemy reproach? Does the adversary provoke your name to the end?
Hebrew Psalter Trans: How long, O God, shall the adversary reproach? Shall the enemy blaspheme your name forever?

Hebrew Trans: Why do you withdraw your hand, even your right hand? From the midst of your bosom, consume them!
Septuagint Trans: Why do you turn away your hand, and your right hand from the midst of your bosom, forever?
Gallican Psalter Trans: Why do you turn away your hand, and your right hand from the midst of your bosom to the end?
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Why do you turn away your hand, and your right hand from the midst of your bosom? Consume them!

Hebrew Trans: Yet God is my King from of old, working salvation in the midst of the earth.
Septuagint Trans: But God is our king before the ages; he has worked salvation in the midst of the earth.
Gallican Psalter Trans: But God is our king before the ages; he has worked salvation in the midst of the earth.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: But God is my king from the beginning; he works salvation in the midst of the earth.

Hebrew Trans: You divided the sea by your strength; you broke the heads of the sea-monsters upon the waters.
Septuagint Trans: You strengthened the sea by your power; you shattered the heads of the dragons upon the water.
Gallican Psalter Trans: You established the sea by your strength; you crushed the heads of the dragons in the waters.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: You scattered the sea by your strength; you crushed the heads of the dragons in the waters.

Hebrew Trans: You crushed the heads of Leviathan; you gave him as food to the people of the desert creatures.
Septuagint Trans: You crushed the heads of the dragon; you gave him as food to the peoples, the Ethiopians.
Gallican Psalter Trans: You broke the heads of the dragon; you gave him as food to the peoples of the Ethiopians.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: You broke the heads of Leviathan; you gave him as food to the people of the Ethiopians.

Hebrew Trans: You split open spring and torrent; you dried up ever-flowing rivers.
Septuagint Trans: You broke open springs and torrents; you dried up the rivers of Etham.
Gallican Psalter Trans: You broke open the fountain and the torrents; you dried up the rivers of Etham.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: You burst open fountain and torrent; you dried up mighty rivers.

Hebrew Trans: Yours is the day, yours also is the night; you established the light and the sun.
Septuagint Trans: Yours is the day and yours is the night; you fashioned the light and the sun.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Yours is the day and yours is the night; you fashioned the dawn and the sun.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Yours is the day and yours is the night; you established the luminaries and the sun.

Hebrew Trans: You set all the boundaries of the earth; summer and winter, you formed them.
Septuagint Trans: You made all the boundaries of the earth; summer and spring — you formed them.
Gallican Psalter Trans: You made all the boundaries of the earth; summer and spring, you formed them.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: You set all the boundaries of the earth; summer and winter you formed.

Hebrew Trans: Remember this: the enemy has reproached the LORD, and a foolish people has blasphemed your name.
Septuagint Trans: Remember this: the enemy has reproached the Lord, and a foolish people has provoked your name.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Remember this: the enemy has reproached the Lord, and a foolish people has provoked your name.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Remember this: the enemy has reproached the Lord, and a foolish people has blasphemed your name.

Hebrew Trans: Do not give to the beast the soul of your turtledove; do not forget the life of your afflicted ones forever.
Septuagint Trans: Do not deliver to the beasts the soul that confesses you; do not forget the souls of your poor ones forever.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Do not deliver to beasts the soul that confesses to you; do not forget the souls of your poor ones to the end.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Do not deliver to the beasts the soul instructed in your law; do not forget the lives of your poor ones forever.

Hebrew Trans: Look upon the covenant, for the dark places of the earth are full of habitations of violence.
Septuagint Trans: Look upon your covenant, for the dark places of the earth have been filled with houses of lawlessness.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Look upon your covenant, for those who have been darkened of the earth are filled with houses of iniquities.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Look upon the covenant, for the dark places of the earth are filled with habitations of iniquity, overthrown.

Hebrew Trans: Let not the oppressed return ashamed; let the poor and needy praise your name.
Septuagint Trans: Let not the humbled one be turned away in shame; the poor and the needy shall praise your name.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Let not the humble be turned away in confusion; the poor and the needy shall praise your name.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Let not the broken and confused be turned back; the needy and the poor shall praise your name.

Hebrew Trans: Arise, O God, plead your cause; remember your reproach from the fool all the day.
Septuagint Trans: Arise, O God, plead your cause; remember your reproaches from the fool all the day long.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Arise, O God, judge your own cause; remember your reproaches, those that come from the foolish all the day long.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Arise, O God, judge your own cause; remember your reproach from the foolish one all day long.

Hebrew Trans: Do not forget the voice of your adversaries; the tumult of those who rise against you ascends continually.
Septuagint Trans: Do not forget the voice of your suppliants; the pride of those who hate you ascends continually before you.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Do not forget the voices of your enemies; the pride of those who hate you ascends continually.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Do not forget the voice of your enemies; the tumult of your adversaries ascends continually.

Hebrew Trans: For the choirmaster; "Do Not Destroy"; a psalm of Asaph, a song.
Septuagint Trans: To the end; do not destroy; a psalm of Asaph; a song.
Gallican Psalter Trans: To the end; do not corrupt. A psalm of Asaph, of a canticle.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: To the victor; do not destroy; a psalm of Asaph, of a canticle.

Hebrew Trans: We give thanks to You, O God, we give thanks, and Your name is near; they recount Your wondrous deeds.
Septuagint Trans: We will give thanks to you, O God, we will give thanks and call upon your name.
Gallican Psalter Trans: We will confess to you, O God, we will confess, and we will call upon your name. We will declare your wondrous works.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: We will give thanks to you, O God, we will give thanks, and those near to your name will declare your wondrous deeds.

Hebrew Trans: "When I seize the appointed time, I myself will judge with equity.
Septuagint Trans: I will recount all your wonders. When I find the right time, I will judge with uprightness.
Gallican Psalter Trans: When I shall have received the time, I will judge justly.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: When I shall have received the appointed time, I will judge with justice.

Hebrew Trans: The earth melts and all its inhabitants; it is I who have set firm its pillars. Selah.
Septuagint Trans: The earth melted, and all who dwell in it; I strengthened its pillars. Selah.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The earth is melted, and all who dwell in it: I have established its pillars. Selah.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The earth shall be dissolved with all its inhabitants; I have steadied its pillars forever.

Hebrew Trans: I said to the boastful, 'Do not boast,' and to the wicked, 'Do not lift up the horn.'
Septuagint Trans: I said to the transgressors, do not transgress; and to the sinners, do not raise up the horn.
Gallican Psalter Trans: I said to the wicked: Do not act wickedly; and to the transgressors: Do not exalt the horn.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: I said to those who act wickedly: do not act wickedly; and to the impious: do not exalt the horn.

Hebrew Trans: Do not lift up your horn on high; do not speak with an arrogant neck.
Septuagint Trans: Do not lift up your horn on high; do not speak injustice against God.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Do not lift up your horn on high; do not speak iniquity against God.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Do not exalt your horn on high, speaking with a stiff neck.

Hebrew Trans: For not from the east nor from the west, nor from the wilderness comes exaltation.
Septuagint Trans: For neither from the east nor from the west, nor from the desert mountains—
Gallican Psalter Trans: For neither from the east, nor from the west, nor from the desert mountains—
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For neither from the east, nor from the west, nor from the wilderness of the mountains—

Hebrew Trans: For God is the judge; He brings down one and lifts up another.
Septuagint Trans: For God is the judge; he humbles this one and exalts that one.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For God is the judge: He humbles one and exalts another.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: But God is the judge: he will humble this one and exalt that one.

Hebrew Trans: For there is a cup in the hand of the LORD, and the wine foams, it is full of mixture, and He pours out from it; surely all the wicked of the earth shall drain and drink its dregs.
Septuagint Trans: For there is a cup in the hand of the Lord, full of unmixed wine, full of mixture; and he poured out from this to that, but its dregs were not emptied; all the sinners of the earth shall drink them.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For in the hand of the Lord there is a cup of unmixed wine, full of mixture, and He has poured out from this to that; yet its dregs are not emptied: all the sinners of the earth shall drink.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For a cup is in the hand of the Lord, and of strong wine full to the brim, mixed; and he will pour out from it; yet all the wicked of the earth shall drink, draining its dregs.

Hebrew Trans: But as for me, I will declare forever; I will sing praises to the God of Jacob.
Septuagint Trans: But I will rejoice forever; I will sing praises to the God of Jacob.
Gallican Psalter Trans: But I will declare forever; I will sing to the God of Jacob.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: But I will proclaim forever; I will sing to the God of Jacob.

Hebrew Trans: And all the horns of the wicked I will cut off; the horns of the righteous shall be exalted."
Septuagint Trans: And all the horns of the sinners I will break, and the horns of the righteous one shall be exalted.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And all the horns of sinners I will break off, and the horns of the righteous shall be exalted.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And all the horns of the wicked I will break off; the horns of the righteous shall be exalted.

Hebrew Trans: For the choirmaster, on stringed instruments, a psalm of Asaph, a song.
Septuagint Trans: For the end, in hymns, a psalm for Asaph; a song regarding the Assyrian.
Gallican Psalter Trans: To the end, in praises, a psalm of Asaph, a canticle to the Assyrian.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: To the victor, in psalms, a song of Asaph, of a canticle.

Hebrew Trans: God is known in Judah; in Israel his name is great.
Septuagint Trans: God is known in Judea; in Israel his name is great.
Gallican Psalter Trans: God is known in Judea; in Israel his name is great.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: God shall be known in Judea; in Israel his name is great.

Hebrew Trans: And his tabernacle was in Salem, and his dwelling place in Zion.
Septuagint Trans: And his place was established in peace, and his dwelling place in Zion.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And his place has been made in peace, and his dwelling in Zion.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And his tabernacle shall be in Salem, and his dwelling place in Zion.

Hebrew Trans: There he broke the flashing arrows of the bow, the shield and the sword and the battle. Selah.
Septuagint Trans: There he shattered the powers of the bows, the shield and the sword and the war. Selah.
Gallican Psalter Trans: There he broke the powers of bows, the shield, and the sword, and the battle. Selah.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: There he shall break the flying shafts of the bow, the shield and the sword and war forever.

Hebrew Trans: Radiant are you, majestic from the mountains of prey.
Septuagint Trans: You shine wonderfully from the everlasting mountains.
Gallican Psalter Trans: You illuminate wonderfully from the eternal mountains.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: You are light, O Magnificent One, from the mountains of captivity.

Hebrew Trans: The stout-hearted were stripped of their spoil; they sank into sleep, and none of the men of valor could find their hands.
Septuagint Trans: All the senseless in heart were troubled; they slept their sleep, and all the men of wealth found nothing in their hands.
Gallican Psalter Trans: All the foolish of heart were troubled; they slept their sleep, and all the men of riches found nothing in their hands.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The proud of heart have been despoiled; they have slept their sleep, and all the men of the army have not found their hands.

Hebrew Trans: At your rebuke, O God of Jacob, both chariot and horse fell into a dead sleep.
Septuagint Trans: At your rebuke, O God of Jacob, those who were mounted on horses fell into slumber.
Gallican Psalter Trans: At your rebuke, O God of Jacob, those who mounted horses fell asleep.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: At your rebuke, O God of Jacob, both chariot and horse have fallen into sleep.

Hebrew Trans: You, you are to be feared, and who can stand before you once your anger is kindled?
Septuagint Trans: You are fearsome, and who shall withstand you from the time of your wrath?
Gallican Psalter Trans: You are terrible, and who shall resist you? From that time your wrath.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: You are terrible, and who shall stand against you? From that time your wrath comes.

Hebrew Trans: From the heavens you pronounced judgment; the earth feared and was still,
Septuagint Trans: From heaven you caused judgment to be heard; the earth feared and was still,
Gallican Psalter Trans: From heaven you made your judgment heard; the earth feared and was still,
Hebrew Psalter Trans: From heaven you shall announce judgment; the earth, fearing, shall be silent.

Hebrew Trans: when God arose to judgment, to save all the humble of the earth. Selah.
Septuagint Trans: when God arose to judgment, to save all the meek of the earth. Selah.
Gallican Psalter Trans: when God arose in judgment, to save all the meek of the earth. Selah.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: When God shall rise up to judge, so that he may save all the meek of the earth forever.

Hebrew Trans: For the wrath of man shall praise you; the remnant of wrath you will gird upon yourself.
Septuagint Trans: For the thought of man shall give praise to you, and the remnant of thought shall keep festival to you.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For the thought of man shall give praise to you, and the remainders of thought shall keep a feast day to you.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For the wrath of man shall confess to you; with the remnants of wrath you shall gird yourself.

Hebrew Trans: Make vows and fulfill them to the LORD your God; let all who are around him bring gifts to the one who is to be feared.
Septuagint Trans: Make your vows and pay them to the Lord your God; all who are around him shall bring gifts
Gallican Psalter Trans: Make vows and render them to the Lord your God, all you who are round about him; bring gifts to the terrible one,
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Make vows and render them to the Lord your God; all who are round about him shall offer gifts to the terrible one,

Hebrew Trans: He cuts off the spirit of princes; he is fearsome to the kings of the earth.
Septuagint Trans: to the fearsome one who takes away the spirits of rulers, fearsome among the kings of the earth.
Gallican Psalter Trans: even to him who takes away the spirits of princes, who is terrible among the kings of the earth.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: To him who takes away the spirit of princes, to him who is terrible to the kings of the earth.

Hebrew Trans: For the choirmaster, according to Jeduthun, of Asaph, a psalm.
Septuagint Trans: For the end, concerning Jedithun, a psalm of Asaph.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Unto the end, for Idithun, a psalm of Asaph.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: To the victor, through Idithun, a psalm of Asaph.

Hebrew Trans: My voice is unto God, and I will cry out; my voice is unto God, and He will give ear to me.
Septuagint Trans: My voice was to the Lord; I cried out, my voice was to God, and he gave heed to me.
Gallican Psalter Trans: I cried to the Lord with my voice; with my voice to God, and he attended to me.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: With my voice I cried to the Lord; with my voice I cried to the Lord, and He heard me.

Hebrew Trans: In the day of my trouble I sought the Lord; my hand was stretched out in the night and did not cease; my soul refused to be comforted.
Septuagint Trans: In the day of my affliction I sought God; with my hands by night before him, and I was not deceived. My soul refused to be comforted.
Gallican Psalter Trans: In the day of my tribulation I sought God with my hands; by night before him, and I was not deceived. My soul refused to be comforted.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: In the day of my tribulation I sought the Lord; my hand was stretched out by night and did not rest; my soul refused to be consoled.

Hebrew Trans: I remember God and I moan; I meditate and my spirit faints. Selah.
Septuagint Trans: I remembered God and was gladdened; I mused and my spirit grew faint. Selah.
Gallican Psalter Trans: I remembered God and was delighted; I meditated and my spirit fainted. Diapsalma.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Remembering God, I was troubled; I spoke within myself, and my spirit always grew faint.

Hebrew Trans: You held open the watches of my eyes; I was troubled and could not speak.
Septuagint Trans: My eyes kept watch before sleep; I was troubled and did not speak.
Gallican Psalter Trans: My eyes anticipated the watches; I was troubled and I did not speak.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: I held up the gaze of my eyes; I was stunned and did not speak.

Hebrew Trans: I considered the days of old, the years of ancient times.
Septuagint Trans: I considered the days of old, and I remembered the eternal years and I meditated.
Gallican Psalter Trans: I thought upon the days of old, and I had the eternal years in my mind.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: I thought back upon the ancient days, the years of old.

Hebrew Trans: I remember my song in the night; I commune with my heart, and my spirit searches diligently.
Septuagint Trans: By night I mused with my heart, and my spirit was searching.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And I meditated in the night with my heart; I exercised myself and I swept my spirit.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: I remembered my psalms in the night; with my heart I spoke, and I searched my spirit.

Hebrew Trans: Will the Lord cast off forever, and will He not be favorable anymore?
Septuagint Trans: Will the Lord cast off forever, and will he no longer be favorable?
Gallican Psalter Trans: Will God cast off forever, and will he not be favorable any more?
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Will the Lord then cast off forever, and will He not be favorable again?

Hebrew Trans: Has His lovingkindness ceased forever? Has His promise come to an end for generation after generation?
Septuagint Trans: Or will he cut off his mercy to the end, from generation to generation?
Gallican Psalter Trans: Or will he cut off his mercy forever, from generation to generation?
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Will He then bring His mercy to an end forever? Will He conclude His word from generation to generation?

Hebrew Trans: Has God forgotten to be gracious? Has He in anger shut up His compassions? Selah.
Septuagint Trans: Or will God forget to have compassion, or will he shut up his mercies in his anger? Selah.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Or will God forget to be merciful, or will he hold back his mercies in his anger? Diapsalma.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Has God forgotten to have mercy, or will He forever shut up His mercies in His fury?

Hebrew Trans: And I said, "This is my affliction: the years of the right hand of the Most High."
Septuagint Trans: And I said, "Now I have begun; this is the change of the right hand of the Most High."
Gallican Psalter Trans: And I said: Now I have begun; this is the change of the right hand of the Most High.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And I said: this is my weakness, this change of the right hand of the Most High.

Hebrew Trans: I will remember the deeds of the LORD, for I will remember Your wonders of old.
Septuagint Trans: I remembered the works of the Lord, for I will remember your wonders from the beginning.
Gallican Psalter Trans: I remembered the works of the Lord, for I will be mindful from the beginning of your wonders.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: I will remember the thoughts of the Lord, recalling Your ancient wonders.

Hebrew Trans: And I will meditate on all Your work, and on Your deeds I will muse.
Septuagint Trans: And I will meditate on all your works, and I will muse on your deeds.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And I will meditate on all your works, and I will be occupied with your inventions.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And I will meditate on all Your work, and I will speak of Your inventions.

Hebrew Trans: O God, Your way is in holiness; who is a god as great as God?
Septuagint Trans: O God, your way is in the holy place; what god is great like our God?
Gallican Psalter Trans: O God, your way is in the holy place; what god is great like our God?
Hebrew Psalter Trans: O God, Your way is in the sanctuary; what god is as great as God?

Hebrew Trans: You are the God who does wonders; You have made known Your strength among the peoples.
Septuagint Trans: You are the God who does wonders; you have made known your power among the peoples.
Gallican Psalter Trans: You are the God who does wonders; you have made known your power among the peoples.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: You are the God who does wonders; You have made known Your power among the peoples.

Hebrew Trans: You have redeemed with Your arm Your people, the sons of Jacob and Joseph. Selah.
Septuagint Trans: You redeemed your people with your arm, the sons of Jacob and Joseph. Selah.
Gallican Psalter Trans: You have redeemed with your arm your people, the children of Jacob and Joseph. Diapsalma.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: You redeemed Your people with Your arm, the sons of Jacob and Joseph, forever.

Hebrew Trans: The waters saw You, O God, the waters saw You and trembled; the depths also were troubled.
Septuagint Trans: The waters saw you, O God, the waters saw you and were afraid, and the depths were troubled, a multitude of the sound of waters.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The waters saw you, O God, the waters saw you and they feared, and the depths were troubled.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The waters saw You, O God, the waters saw You and were in anguish, and the depths were stirred.

Hebrew Trans: The clouds poured out water; the skies gave forth thunder; Your arrows also went abroad.
Septuagint Trans: The clouds gave forth a voice, for indeed your arrows pass through.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The multitude of the sound of waters; the clouds gave forth a voice, for your arrows pass through.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The clouds poured out waters; the skies gave forth a voice, and Your arrows ran about.

Hebrew Trans: The voice of Your thunder was in the whirlwind; the lightnings illumined the world; the earth trembled and shook.
Septuagint Trans: The voice of your thunder was in the whirlwind; your lightnings illumined the world; the earth shook and trembled.
