Gallican Psalter Trans: The voice of your thunder was in the wheel; your lightnings illuminated the world; the earth was moved and trembled.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The voice of Your thunder was in the whirlwind; Your lightnings appeared to the world; the earth was shaken and trembled.

Hebrew Trans: Your way was in the sea, and Your path in the great waters, and Your footsteps were not known.
Septuagint Trans: Your way is in the sea, and your paths in many waters, and your footsteps shall not be known.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Your way is in the sea, and your paths in many waters, and your footsteps shall not be known.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Your way is in the sea, and Your paths in many waters, and Your footsteps are not known.

Hebrew Trans: You led Your people like a flock by the hand of Moses and Aaron.
Septuagint Trans: You led your people like sheep by the hand of Moses and Aaron.
Gallican Psalter Trans: You led your people like sheep, by the hand of Moses and Aaron.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: You led Your people like a flock, by the hand of Moses and Aaron.

Hebrew Trans: A Maskil of Asaph. Give ear, O my people, to my teaching; incline your ears to the words of my mouth.
Septuagint Trans: Of understanding, by Asaph. Give heed, my people, to my law; incline your ear to the words of my mouth.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The understanding of Asaph. Attend, my people, to my law; incline your ear to the words of my mouth.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: A lesson of Asaph. Give ear, my people, to my law; incline your ear to the words of my mouth.

Hebrew Trans: I will open my mouth in a parable; I will utter riddles from of old,
Septuagint Trans: I will open my mouth in parables; I will utter dark sayings from the beginning.
Gallican Psalter Trans: I will open my mouth in a parable; I will speak forth propositions from the beginning.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: I will open my mouth in a parable; I will speak riddles of old.

Hebrew Trans: which we have heard and known, and our fathers have told us.
Septuagint Trans: All that we have heard and known, and our fathers have told us.
Gallican Psalter Trans: How great things we have heard and known, and our fathers have narrated them to us.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Which we have heard and known, and our fathers have told us.

Hebrew Trans: We will not hide them from their children, telling to the generation to come the praises of the LORD, and his strength, and his wondrous works that he has done.
Septuagint Trans: They were not hidden from their children, declaring to another generation the praises of the Lord, and his mighty deeds, and his wonders which he performed.
Gallican Psalter Trans: They have not been hidden from their children, in another generation declaring the praises of the Lord, and his powers, and his wonderful things which he did.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: They are not hidden from their children, from the following generation telling the praises of the Lord, and his power, and his wondrous works which he did.

Hebrew Trans: And he established a testimony in Jacob, and set a law in Israel, which he commanded our fathers to make them known to their children,
Septuagint Trans: And he raised up a testimony in Jacob, and appointed a law in Israel, which he commanded our fathers, to make them known to their children;
Gallican Psalter Trans: And he raised up a testimony in Jacob, and set a law in Israel, how great things he commanded our fathers to make them known to their sons.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: He established a testimony in Jacob, and set a law in Israel, which he commanded our fathers, that they should teach their children.

Hebrew Trans: that the generation to come might know them, children yet to be born, that they might arise and tell them to their children,
Septuagint Trans: So that another generation might know them, sons yet to be born, and they would arise and declare them to their children;
Gallican Psalter Trans: That another generation might know them, the sons who shall be born, and they shall rise up and narrate them to their sons.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: That the generation to come might know them, the children yet to be born; they shall arise and tell their children,

Hebrew Trans: and that they might set their confidence in God, and not forget the works of God, but keep his commandments,
Septuagint Trans: That they might set their hope upon God, and not forget the works of God, and would seek out his commandments;
Gallican Psalter Trans: That they may set their hope in God, and not forget the works of God, and seek out his commandments.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: That they might set their hope in God, and not forget his works, and keep his commandments;

Hebrew Trans: and not be like their fathers, a stubborn and rebellious generation, a generation that did not set its heart aright, and whose spirit was not faithful to God.
Septuagint Trans: That they might not become like their fathers, a crooked and embittering generation, a generation that did not direct its heart aright, and whose spirit was not faithful with God.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Lest they become like their fathers, a perverse and exasperating generation; a generation that did not direct its heart aright, and whose spirit was not faithful with God.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: That they might not be like their fathers, a stubborn and provoking generation; a generation that did not prepare its heart, and whose spirit did not believe in God.

Hebrew Trans: The children of Ephraim, armed bowmen, turned back in the day of battle.
Septuagint Trans: The sons of Ephraim, bending and shooting with bows, turned back in the day of battle.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The sons of Ephraim, bending and shooting bows, were turned back in the day of battle.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The children of Ephraim, bending and shooting the bow, turned their backs in the day of battle.

Hebrew Trans: They did not keep the covenant of God and refused to walk in his law.
Septuagint Trans: They did not keep the covenant of God, and in his law they did not wish to walk.
Gallican Psalter Trans: They did not keep the covenant of God, and in his law they would not walk.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: They did not keep the covenant of their God, and refused to walk in his law;

Hebrew Trans: And they forgot his deeds and his wonders that he had shown them.
Septuagint Trans: And they forgot his benefits and his wonders which he had shown them.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And they forgot his benefactions and his wonderful things which he showed them.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And they forgot his deeds, and his wonders which he had shown them.

Hebrew Trans: Before their fathers he did marvels in the land of Egypt, in the field of Zoan.
Septuagint Trans: Before their fathers he performed wonders in the land of Egypt, in the plain of Tanis.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Before their fathers he did wonders in the land of Egypt, in the field of Tanis.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Before their fathers he did marvelous things in the land of Egypt, in the region of Tanis.

Hebrew Trans: He split the sea and caused them to pass through, and he made the waters stand as a heap.
Septuagint Trans: He broke open the sea and led them through; he made the waters stand as in a wineskin.
Gallican Psalter Trans: He broke open the sea and led them through; he made the waters stand as in a wineskin.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: He divided the sea and led them through, and he made the waters stand as a heap.

Hebrew Trans: And he led them with a cloud by day, and all the night with a light of fire.
Septuagint Trans: And he guided them with a cloud by day, and all the night with a light of fire.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And he led them with a cloud by day, and all the night with a light of fire.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And he led them with a cloud by day, and all the night with a light of fire.

Hebrew Trans: He split rocks in the wilderness and gave them drink abundantly as from the great depths.
Septuagint Trans: He broke open a rock in the wilderness and gave them drink as from a great abyss.
Gallican Psalter Trans: He broke open the rock in the wilderness and gave them drink as from a great abyss.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: He split the rock in the desert, and gave them drink as from great depths.

Hebrew Trans: And he brought streams out of the rock and caused waters to flow down like rivers.
Septuagint Trans: And he brought forth water out of the rock and brought down waters like rivers.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And he brought forth water from the rock and led down waters like rivers.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And he brought streams out of the rock, and caused waters to flow down like rivers.

Hebrew Trans: Yet they continued still to sin against him, to rebel against the Most High in the dry land.
Septuagint Trans: And they continued still to sin against him; they provoked the Most High in the waterless land.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And they continued still to sin against him; in anger they provoked the Most High in the waterless place.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And they continued still to sin against him, to provoke the Most High in the waterless place.

Hebrew Trans: And they tested God in their heart by asking food for their appetite.
Septuagint Trans: And they tested God in their hearts, by asking food for their souls.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And they tempted God in their hearts, by asking food for their souls.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And they tested God in their hearts, asking food for their souls.

Hebrew Trans: And they spoke against God; they said, "Can God prepare a table in the wilderness?"
Septuagint Trans: And they spoke against God and said, "Can God prepare a table in the wilderness?"
Gallican Psalter Trans: And they spoke ill of God; they said: Can God prepare a table in the desert?
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And speaking against God, they said: Can God prepare a table in the wilderness?

Hebrew Trans: Behold, he struck the rock and waters gushed out, and streams overflowed; can he also give bread, or provide meat for his people?
Septuagint Trans: Since he struck a rock and waters flowed, and torrents were flooded — can he also give bread, or prepare a table for his people?
Gallican Psalter Trans: Because he struck the rock and waters flowed and torrents flooded forth—can he also give bread, or prepare a table for his people?
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Behold, he struck the rock and waters flowed, and torrents overflowed; can he also give bread, or prepare meat for his people?

Hebrew Trans: Therefore the LORD heard and was wrathful, and a fire was kindled against Jacob, and anger also rose against Israel,
Septuagint Trans: Therefore the Lord heard and delayed, and a fire was kindled in Jacob, and wrath went up against Israel;
Gallican Psalter Trans: Therefore the Lord heard and delayed, and a fire was kindled in Jacob, and anger ascended against Israel.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Therefore the Lord heard and did not delay, and a fire was kindled in Jacob, and anger rose against Israel;

Hebrew Trans: because they did not believe in God and did not trust in his salvation.
Septuagint Trans: Because they did not believe in God, nor did they hope in his salvation.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Because they did not believe in God, nor hope in his salvation.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Because they did not believe in God, nor trust in his salvation.

Hebrew Trans: Yet he commanded the skies above and opened the doors of heaven,
Septuagint Trans: And he commanded the clouds from above, and opened the doors of heaven.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And he commanded the clouds from above, and opened the doors of heaven.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And he commanded the clouds above, and opened the doors of heaven,

Hebrew Trans: and he rained down manna upon them to eat and gave them the grain of heaven.
Septuagint Trans: And he rained down manna upon them to eat, and gave them the bread of heaven.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And he rained down manna upon them to eat, and gave them the bread of heaven.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And rained down manna upon them to eat, and gave them the grain of heaven.

Hebrew Trans: Man ate the bread of the mighty; he sent them provision to the full.
Septuagint Trans: Man ate the bread of angels; he sent them provisions to fullness.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Man ate the bread of angels; he sent them provisions in abundance.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Man ate the bread of the mighty; he sent them provisions to satiety.

Hebrew Trans: He caused the east wind to blow in the heavens, and by his power he guided the south wind.
Septuagint Trans: He removed the south wind from heaven, and by his power he brought on the southwest wind.
Gallican Psalter Trans: He removed the south wind from heaven, and by his power brought in the southwest wind.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: He took away the east wind from heaven, and by his power brought in the south wind.

Hebrew Trans: And he rained meat upon them like dust, and winged birds like the sand of the seas.
Septuagint Trans: And he rained upon them flesh like dust, and winged birds like the sand of the seas.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And he rained upon them flesh like dust, and winged birds like the sand of the sea.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And he rained flesh upon them like dust, and feathered birds like the sand of the sea.

Hebrew Trans: And he let them fall in the midst of their camp, all around their dwellings.
Septuagint Trans: And they fell in the midst of their camp, round about their dwellings.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And they fell in the midst of their camp, around their tents.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And they fell in the midst of their camp, round about their tents.

Hebrew Trans: And they ate and were well filled, for he brought them what they craved.
Septuagint Trans: And they ate and were filled exceedingly, and he brought them their desire.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And they ate and were exceedingly satisfied, and he brought them their desire.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And they ate and were greatly satisfied, and he brought them their desire.

Hebrew Trans: They had not turned away from their craving; their food was still in their mouths,
Septuagint Trans: They were not deprived of their desire; while their food was still in their mouths,
Gallican Psalter Trans: They were not deprived of their desire; while their food was still in their mouths,
Hebrew Psalter Trans: They were not deprived of their craving; while the food was still in their mouths,

Hebrew Trans: when the anger of God rose against them, and he slew the stoutest of them and struck down the young men of Israel.
Septuagint Trans: The wrath of God came upon them, and slew among the fattest of them, and brought down the chosen men of Israel.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The anger of God ascended upon them, and he slew the fat ones among them, and brought down the chosen of Israel.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The anger of God rose against them, and he slew the fattest of them, and struck down the chosen of Israel.

Hebrew Trans: In spite of all this they sinned still and did not believe in his wonders.
Septuagint Trans: In all these things they sinned still, and did not believe in his wonders.
Gallican Psalter Trans: In all these things they sinned still, and did not believe in his wonders.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: In all this they sinned still more, and did not believe in his wonders.

Hebrew Trans: So he consumed their days in vanity and their years in terror.
Septuagint Trans: And their days were consumed in vanity, and their years in haste.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And their days failed in vanity, and their years in haste.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And he consumed their days in vanity, and their years in haste.

Hebrew Trans: When he slew them, then they sought him; they returned and sought earnestly for God.
Septuagint Trans: When he slew them, they sought him, and they turned back and sought God early.
Gallican Psalter Trans: When he slew them, they sought him, and they returned and came early to God.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: When he slew them, then they sought him, and turned back, and rose early to God.

Hebrew Trans: And they remembered that God was their rock and the Most High God their redeemer.
Septuagint Trans: And they remembered that God is their helper, and God the Most High is their redeemer.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And they remembered that God is their helper, and the most high God is their redeemer.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And they remembered that God was their strength, and the Most High God their redeemer.

Hebrew Trans: But they flattered him with their mouths and lied to him with their tongues.
Septuagint Trans: But they deceived him with their mouth, and with their tongue they lied to him.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And they loved him with their mouth, and with their tongue they lied to him.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: But they flattered him with their mouth, and with their tongue they lied to him.

Hebrew Trans: For their heart was not steadfast with him, and they were not faithful to his covenant.
Septuagint Trans: But their heart was not right with him, nor were they faithful in his covenant.
Gallican Psalter Trans: But their heart was not right with him, nor were they held faithful in his covenant.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: But their heart was not steadfast with him, nor did they remain faithful in his covenant.

Hebrew Trans: But he, being compassionate, atoned for iniquity and did not destroy them; and many a time he turned his anger away and did not stir up all his wrath.
Septuagint Trans: But he is compassionate and will forgive their sins, and will not destroy them; and he will often turn away his anger, and will not kindle all his wrath.
Gallican Psalter Trans: But he is merciful and will be propitious to their sins, and will not destroy them; and he will abound in turning away his anger, and will not kindle all his wrath.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: But he, being merciful, will forgive iniquity and will not destroy; and many times he turned away his anger, and did not stir up all his wrath.

Hebrew Trans: And he remembered that they were flesh, a wind that passes and does not return.
Septuagint Trans: And he remembered that they are flesh, a spirit that goes and does not return.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And he remembered that they are flesh, a spirit that goes and does not return.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: But he remembered that they were flesh, a breath that goes and does not return.

Hebrew Trans: How often they rebelled against him in the wilderness and grieved him in the desert!
Septuagint Trans: How often they provoked him in the wilderness, and angered him in the waterless land!
Gallican Psalter Trans: How often they provoked him in the desert, stirred him to anger in the waterless place!
Hebrew Psalter Trans: How often they provoked him in the desert, and grieved him in the wilderness!

Hebrew Trans: And they turned again and tested God and provoked the Holy One of Israel.
Septuagint Trans: And they turned back and tested God, and provoked the Holy One of Israel.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And they turned back and tempted God, and provoked the Holy One of Israel.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And they turned back and tested God, and provoked the Holy One of Israel.

Hebrew Trans: They did not remember his hand, the day when he redeemed them from the adversary,
Septuagint Trans: They did not remember his hand, the day when he redeemed them from the hand of the oppressor;
Gallican Psalter Trans: They did not remember his hand, the day when he redeemed them from the hand of the oppressor.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: They did not remember his hand, the day when he redeemed them from the oppressor;

Hebrew Trans: when he set his signs in Egypt and his wonders in the field of Zoan.
Septuagint Trans: How he set his signs in Egypt, and his wonders in the plain of Tanis.
Gallican Psalter Trans: As he set his signs in Egypt, and his prodigies in the field of Tanis.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Who performed his signs in Egypt, and his portents in the region of Tanis;

Hebrew Trans: And he turned their rivers to blood, and their streams so that they could not drink.
Septuagint Trans: And he turned their rivers to blood, and their streams so that they could not drink.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And he turned their rivers into blood, and their showers so that they could not drink.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Who turned their rivers into blood, and their streams so that they could not drink;

Hebrew Trans: He sent among them swarms of flies, which devoured them, and frogs, which destroyed them.
Septuagint Trans: He sent among them the dog-fly and it devoured them, and the frog and it destroyed them.
Gallican Psalter Trans: He sent among them the dog-fly, and it devoured them, and the frog, and it destroyed them.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Who sent among them every kind of fly to devour them, and frogs to destroy them;

Hebrew Trans: And he gave their crops to the caterpillar and their labor to the locust.
Septuagint Trans: And he gave their fruit to the blight, and their labors to the locust.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And he gave their fruits to the caterpillar, and their labors to the locust.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Who gave their crops to the locust-grub, and their labor to the locust;

Hebrew Trans: He destroyed their vines with hail and their sycamores with frost.
Septuagint Trans: He killed their vines with hail, and their sycamore trees with frost.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And he killed their vines with hail, and their mulberry trees with frost.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Who killed their vines with hail, and their sycamores with frost;

Hebrew Trans: And he gave over their cattle to the hail and their livestock to bolts of lightning.
Septuagint Trans: And he gave over their cattle to hail, and their possessions to fire.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And he delivered their cattle to the hail, and their possessions to fire.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Who delivered their cattle to the hail, and their livestock to the thunderbolts;

Hebrew Trans: He sent upon them the fierceness of his anger, wrath and indignation and distress, a band of messengers of evil.
Septuagint Trans: He sent upon them the fury of his wrath — anger and wrath and affliction, a mission by evil angels.
Gallican Psalter Trans: He sent upon them the wrath of his indignation: indignation, and anger, and tribulation, a sending through evil angels.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Who sent upon them the fury of his anger, indignation and wrath and anguish, a mission of evil angels.

Hebrew Trans: He made a path for his anger; he did not spare their soul from death but gave their life over to the pestilence.
Septuagint Trans: He made a path for his wrath; he did not spare their souls from death, and shut up their cattle to death.
Gallican Psalter Trans: He made a way for the path of his anger; he did not spare their souls from death, and he shut up their cattle in death.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: He made a path for his fury; he did not spare their souls from death, and delivered their animals to the pestilence.

Hebrew Trans: And he struck all the firstborn in Egypt, the firstfruits of their strength in the tents of Ham.
Septuagint Trans: And he struck every firstborn in Egypt, the firstfruits of their labors in the tents of Ham.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And he struck every firstborn in the land of Egypt, the firstfruits of their labors in the tents of Ham.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And he struck every firstborn in Egypt, the first fruits of labor in the tents of Ham.

Hebrew Trans: And he led forth his people like sheep and guided them like a flock in the wilderness.
Septuagint Trans: And he removed his people like sheep, and led them up like a flock in the wilderness.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And he took away his people like sheep, and led them through like a flock in the wilderness.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And he led forth his people like sheep, and guided them like a flock in the desert.

Hebrew Trans: And he led them safely so that they did not fear, and the sea covered their enemies.
Septuagint Trans: And he guided them in hope, and they did not fear, and the sea covered their enemies.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And he led them in hope, and they did not fear, and the sea covered their enemies.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And he led them in safety and without fear, but the sea covered their enemies.

Hebrew Trans: And he brought them to his holy border, this mountain which his right hand had acquired.
Septuagint Trans: And he brought them into the borders of his sanctuary, this mountain which his right hand acquired.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And he brought them into the mountain of his sanctification, the mountain which his right hand acquired.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And he brought them to his holy border, to this mountain which his right hand had possessed.

Hebrew Trans: And he drove out nations before them and allotted them by line as an inheritance, and he settled the tribes of Israel in their tents.
Septuagint Trans: And he drove out nations before them, and allotted them by the line of an inheritance, and made the tribes of Israel dwell in their tents.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And he cast out the nations before their face, and divided the land to them by lot with a line of distribution, and made the tribes of Israel to dwell in their tents.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And he cast out the nations before them, and made them possess an inheritance by measured line, and settled the tribes of Israel in their tents.

Hebrew Trans: Yet they tested and rebelled against God Most High and did not keep his testimonies.
Septuagint Trans: And they tested and provoked the Most High God, and did not keep his testimonies.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And they tempted and provoked the most high God, and did not keep his testimonies.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And they tested and provoked the Most High God, and did not keep his testimonies.

Hebrew Trans: And they turned back and acted treacherously like their fathers; they turned aside like a deceitful bow.
Septuagint Trans: And they turned away and dealt treacherously, just as their fathers did, and they were twisted like a crooked bow.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And they turned away and did not keep the covenant, just as their fathers; they were turned aside like a crooked bow.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And they turned away and dealt treacherously like their fathers; they were bent like a useless bow.

Hebrew Trans: And they provoked him to anger with their high places and moved him to jealousy with their graven images.
Septuagint Trans: And they provoked him with their high places, and with their graven images they moved him to jealousy.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And they provoked him to anger on their hills, and with their graven images they stirred him to jealousy.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And they provoked him with their high places, and with their graven images stirred him to jealousy.

Hebrew Trans: God heard and was wrathful, and he utterly rejected Israel.
Septuagint Trans: God heard and was disdainful, and greatly despised Israel.
Gallican Psalter Trans: God heard and spurned them, and reduced Israel greatly to nothing.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: God heard and did not delay, and he utterly cast off Israel.

Hebrew Trans: And he abandoned the tabernacle of Shiloh, the tent where he dwelt among men.
Septuagint Trans: And he rejected the tabernacle of Shiloh, his dwelling where he dwelt among men.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And he rejected the tabernacle of Shiloh, his tabernacle where he dwelt among men.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And he abandoned the tabernacle of Shiloh, the tent which he had placed among men.

Hebrew Trans: And he delivered his strength into captivity and his glory into the hand of the adversary.
Septuagint Trans: And he gave their strength into captivity, and their beauty into the hands of the enemy.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And he delivered their strength into captivity, and their beauty into the hands of the enemy.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: He delivered his glory into captivity, and his beauty into the hand of the enemy.

Hebrew Trans: And he gave his people over to the sword and was wrathful with his inheritance.
Septuagint Trans: And he shut up his people to the sword, and disregarded his inheritance.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And he shut up his people under the sword, and despised his inheritance.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And he shut up his people to the sword, and did not spare his inheritance.

Hebrew Trans: Fire consumed their young men, and their maidens had no wedding songs.
Septuagint Trans: Fire devoured their young men, and their virgins were not mourned.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Fire devoured their young men, and their virgins were not lamented.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Fire devoured their young men, and their maidens no one mourned.

Hebrew Trans: Their priests fell by the sword, and their widows did not weep.
Septuagint Trans: Their priests fell by the sword, and their widows shall not be wept for.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Their priests fell by the sword, and their widows were not mourned.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Their priests fell by the sword, and their widows were not wept for.

Hebrew Trans: Then the Lord awoke as from sleep, like a mighty man shouting because of wine.
Septuagint Trans: And the Lord was roused as one sleeping, as a mighty man reeling from wine.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And the Lord was aroused as one sleeping, as a mighty man overcome with wine.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And the Lord awoke as one sleeping, like a mighty man shouting after wine.

Hebrew Trans: And he struck his adversaries backward; he put upon them an everlasting reproach.
Septuagint Trans: And he struck his enemies backward; he gave them everlasting reproach.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And he struck his enemies in the rear; he gave them everlasting reproach.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And he struck his enemies backward; he gave them everlasting reproach.

Hebrew Trans: And he rejected the tent of Joseph and did not choose the tribe of Ephraim,
Septuagint Trans: And he rejected the tent of Joseph, and did not choose the tribe of Ephraim.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And he rejected the tabernacle of Joseph, and did not choose the tribe of Ephraim.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And he rejected the tabernacle of Joseph, and did not choose the tribe of Ephraim;

Hebrew Trans: but chose the tribe of Judah, Mount Zion, which he loved.
Septuagint Trans: But he chose the tribe of Judah, Mount Zion which he loved.
Gallican Psalter Trans: But he chose the tribe of Judah, Mount Zion which he loved.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: But he chose the tribe of Judah, Mount Zion which he loved.

Hebrew Trans: And he built his sanctuary like the heights, like the earth which he has founded forever.
Septuagint Trans: And he built his sanctuary like that of unicorns; in the earth he founded it forever.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And he built his sanctuary like that of unicorns, in the land which he founded forever.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And he built his sanctuary like the heights of the unicorn; like the earth he founded it forever.

Hebrew Trans: And he chose David his servant and took him from the sheepfolds;
Septuagint Trans: And he chose David his servant, and took him from the sheepfolds.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And he chose David his servant, and took him from the flocks of sheep.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And he chose David his servant, and took him from the sheepfolds;

Hebrew Trans: from following the nursing ewes he brought him to shepherd Jacob his people and Israel his inheritance.
Septuagint Trans: From behind the nursing ewes he took him, to shepherd Jacob his people and Israel his inheritance.
Gallican Psalter Trans: From following the nursing ewes he received him, to shepherd Jacob his servant and Israel his inheritance.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: From following the nursing ewes he brought him, to shepherd Jacob his people and Israel his inheritance.

Hebrew Trans: And he shepherded them according to the integrity of his heart and guided them by the skillfulness of his hands.
Septuagint Trans: And he shepherded them in the innocence of his heart, and by the skillfulness of his hands he guided them.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And he fed them in the innocence of his heart, and by the understanding of his hands he led them.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Who fed them in the simplicity of his heart, and by the skillfulness of his hands he was their guide.

Hebrew Trans: A psalm of Asaph. O God, the nations have come into your inheritance; they have defiled your holy temple; they have made Jerusalem into heaps of ruins.
Septuagint Trans: A psalm of Asaph. O God, the nations have come into your inheritance; they have defiled your holy temple; they have made Jerusalem into a storehouse for fruit-watchers.
Gallican Psalter Trans: A psalm of Asaph. O God, the nations have come into your inheritance; they have defiled your holy temple; they have made Jerusalem into a guardhouse of orchards.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: A song of Asaph. O God, the nations have come into your inheritance; they have defiled your holy temple; they have laid Jerusalem in heaps of stones.

Hebrew Trans: They have given the corpses of your servants as food to the birds of the heavens, the flesh of your faithful ones to the beasts of the earth.
Septuagint Trans: They have given the corpses of your servants as food to the birds of the heaven, the flesh of your holy ones to the beasts of the earth.
Gallican Psalter Trans: They have given the dead bodies of your servants as food to the birds of the sky, the flesh of your holy ones to the beasts of the earth.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: They have given the corpses of your servants as food to the birds of the sky, the flesh of your holy ones to the beasts of the earth.

Hebrew Trans: They have poured out their blood like water around Jerusalem, and there was none to bury them.
Septuagint Trans: They have poured out their blood like water around Jerusalem, and there was no one to bury them.
Gallican Psalter Trans: They have poured out their blood like water round about Jerusalem, and there was no one to bury them.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: They have poured out their blood like water around Jerusalem, and there was no one to bury them.

Hebrew Trans: We have become a reproach to our neighbors, a scorn and derision to those around us.
Septuagint Trans: We have become a reproach to our neighbors, a mockery and derision to those around us.
Gallican Psalter Trans: We have become a reproach to our neighbors, a scorn and derision to those who are around us.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: We have become a reproach to our neighbors, a mockery and derision to those who are around us.

Hebrew Trans: How long, O LORD? Will you be angry forever? Will your jealousy burn like fire?
Septuagint Trans: How long, O Lord? Will you be angry to the end? Will your jealousy burn like fire?
Gallican Psalter Trans: How long, O Lord? Will you be angry forever? Will your jealousy burn like fire?
Hebrew Psalter Trans: How long, O Lord? Will you be angry to the end? Will your jealousy burn like fire?

Hebrew Trans: Pour out your wrath upon the nations that do not know you, and upon the kingdoms that have not called upon your name.
Septuagint Trans: Pour out your wrath upon the nations that do not know you, and upon the kingdoms that have not called upon your name.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Pour out your wrath upon the nations that have not known you, and upon the kingdoms that have not called upon your name.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Pour out your fury upon the nations that have not known you, and upon the kingdoms that have not called upon your name.

Hebrew Trans: For they have devoured Jacob, and laid waste his dwelling place.
Septuagint Trans: For they have devoured Jacob and laid waste his place.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For they have devoured Jacob and laid waste his dwelling place.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For they have devoured Jacob and laid waste his beauty.

Hebrew Trans: Do not remember against us the iniquities of our forefathers; let your mercies come swiftly to meet us, for we have been brought very low.
Septuagint Trans: Do not remember our former iniquities; let your compassions quickly overtake us, for we have become exceedingly poor.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Do not remember our former iniquities; let your mercies swiftly come before us, for we have been made exceedingly poor.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Do not remember our former iniquities; let your mercies swiftly come upon us, for we have been brought very low.

Hebrew Trans: Help us, O God of our salvation, for the sake of the glory of your name; and deliver us, and atone for our sins, for your name's sake.
Septuagint Trans: Help us, O God our savior, for the sake of the glory of your name; O Lord, deliver us and be merciful regarding our sins, for the sake of your name.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Help us, O God of our salvation, for the glory of your name; O Lord, deliver us, and be merciful to our sins, for the sake of your name.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Help us, O God of our salvation, for the glory of your name; and deliver us, and be merciful to our sins, for your name's sake.

Hebrew Trans: Why should the nations say, "Where is their God?" Let the avenging of the blood of your servants that has been shed be known among the nations before our eyes.
Septuagint Trans: Lest the nations say, "Where is their God?" And let the avenging of the blood of your servants that has been poured out be made known among the nations before our eyes.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Lest perhaps they say among the nations, "Where is their God?" And let the avenging of the blood of your servants, which has been poured out, be made known among the nations before our eyes.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Why do the nations say, "Where is their God?" Let the avenging of the blood of your servants, which has been poured out, be made known among the nations before our eyes.

Hebrew Trans: Let the groaning of the prisoner come before you; according to the greatness of your arm, preserve the children of death.
Septuagint Trans: Let the groaning of those who are bound come before you; according to the greatness of your arm, preserve the sons of those who have been put to death.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Let the groaning of the prisoners come before your sight; according to the greatness of your arm, take possession of the children of those who have been put to death.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Let the groaning of the prisoners come before you; in the greatness of your arm, preserve the children of death.

Hebrew Trans: And return to our neighbors sevenfold into their bosom their reproach with which they have reproached you, O Lord.
Septuagint Trans: Repay to our neighbors sevenfold into their bosom the reproach with which they have reproached you, O Lord.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And repay our neighbors sevenfold into their bosom their reproach with which they have reproached you, O Lord.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And repay to our neighbors sevenfold into their bosom their reproach with which they have reproached you, O Lord.

Hebrew Trans: And we, your people and the flock of your pasture, will give thanks to you forever; from generation to generation we will recount your praise.
Septuagint Trans: But we, your people and the sheep of your pasture, will give thanks to you forever; from generation to generation we will declare your praise.
Gallican Psalter Trans: But we, your people and the sheep of your pasture, will give thanks to you forever; from generation to generation we will declare your praise.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For we, your people and the flock of your pasture, will give thanks to you forever; from generation to generation we will declare your praises.

Hebrew Trans: For the choirmaster, upon Shoshannim Eduth; a psalm of Asaph.
Septuagint Trans: For the end, concerning the things that shall be changed, a testimony for Asaph, a psalm concerning the Assyrian.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Unto the end, for those who shall be changed, a testimony of Asaph, a psalm.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: To the victor, for the lilies of the testimony. A canticle of Asaph.

Hebrew Trans: O Shepherd of Israel, give ear; you who lead Joseph like a flock, you who sit upon the cherubim, shine forth.
Septuagint Trans: O Shepherd of Israel, give heed, you who lead Joseph like sheep; you who sit upon the cherubim, manifest yourself.
Gallican Psalter Trans: You who rule Israel, give heed; you who lead Joseph like sheep, you who sit upon the cherubim, show yourself.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: You who shepherd Israel, give ear; you who lead Joseph like a flock, you who sit upon the cherubim, show yourself.

Hebrew Trans: Before Ephraim and Benjamin and Manasseh, stir up your might, and come to save us.
Septuagint Trans: Before Ephraim and Benjamin and Manasseh, stir up your power and come to save us.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Before Ephraim and Benjamin and Manasseh, stir up your power and come, that you may save us.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Before Ephraim and Benjamin and Manasseh, stir up your might, and come to save us.

Hebrew Trans: O God, restore us, and cause your face to shine, and we shall be saved.
Septuagint Trans: O God, restore us and cause your face to shine, and we shall be saved.
Gallican Psalter Trans: O God, turn us back, and show your face, and we shall be saved.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: O God, restore us, and show your face, and we shall be saved.

Hebrew Trans: O LORD God of hosts, how long will you be angry against the prayer of your people?
Septuagint Trans: O Lord, God of hosts, how long will you be angry at the prayer of your servant?
Gallican Psalter Trans: O Lord God of hosts, how long will you be angry over the prayer of your servant?
Hebrew Psalter Trans: O Lord God of hosts, how long will you smolder with anger at the prayer of your people?

Hebrew Trans: You have fed them with the bread of tears, and given them tears to drink in great measure.
Septuagint Trans: You will feed us with the bread of tears and give us tears to drink in measure.
Gallican Psalter Trans: You will feed us with the bread of tears, and you will give us drink in tears in measure.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: You have fed us with the bread of weeping, and you have given us to drink in tears threefold.

Hebrew Trans: You make us a strife to our neighbors, and our enemies mock among themselves.
Septuagint Trans: You have made us a contradiction to our neighbors, and our enemies have mocked us.
Gallican Psalter Trans: You have made us a contradiction to our neighbors, and our enemies have mocked us.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: You have made us a strife to our neighbors, and our enemies have mocked us.

Hebrew Trans: O God of hosts, restore us, and cause your face to shine, and we shall be saved.
Septuagint Trans: O Lord, God of hosts, restore us and cause your face to shine, and we shall be saved. Interlude.
Gallican Psalter Trans: O God of hosts, turn us back, and show your face, and we shall be saved.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: O God of hosts, restore us, and show your face, and we shall be saved.

Hebrew Trans: You brought a vine out of Egypt; you drove out the nations and planted it.
Septuagint Trans: You removed a vine out of Egypt; you cast out nations and planted it.
Gallican Psalter Trans: You transplanted a vine out of Egypt; you cast out the nations and planted it.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: You brought a vine out of Egypt; you cast out the nations and planted it.

Hebrew Trans: You cleared the ground before it, and it took deep root and filled the land.
Septuagint Trans: You prepared a way before it and planted its roots, and the land was filled.
Gallican Psalter Trans: You were the guide of its journey before it, and you planted its roots, and it filled the land.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: You prepared the way before its face, and you established its roots, and it filled the land.

Hebrew Trans: The mountains were covered with its shadow, and its branches were like the cedars of God.
Septuagint Trans: Its shadow covered the mountains, and its branches covered the cedars of God.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Its shadow covered the mountains, and its branches the cedars of God.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The mountains were covered with its shadow, and its branches were as the cedars of God.

Hebrew Trans: It sent out its boughs to the sea, and its shoots to the river.
Septuagint Trans: It stretched out its tendrils as far as the sea, and its shoots as far as the river.
Gallican Psalter Trans: It stretched out its boughs unto the sea, and its shoots unto the River.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: It spread its boughs as far as the sea, and its shoots as far as the River.

Hebrew Trans: Why have you broken down its walls, so that all who pass along the way pluck from it?
Septuagint Trans: Why have you broken down its fence, so that all who pass along the way pluck its fruit?
Gallican Psalter Trans: Why have you destroyed its wall, and all who pass along the way gather its grapes?
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Why have you broken down its wall, and all who pass along the way pluck its grapes?

Hebrew Trans: The boar from the forest ravages it, and the creatures of the field feed on it.
Septuagint Trans: The boar from the forest ravaged it, and the wild solitary beast devoured it.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The boar from the forest has laid it waste, and a singular wild beast has devoured it.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The boar from the forest has laid it waste, and every beast of the field has fed upon it.

Hebrew Trans: O God of hosts, turn again, we beseech you; look down from heaven and see, and visit this vine,
Septuagint Trans: O God of hosts, turn back now; look down from heaven and see, and visit this vine,
Gallican Psalter Trans: O God of hosts, turn back; look down from heaven and see, and visit this vine.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: O God of hosts, return, I beseech you; look down from heaven, and see, and visit this vine,

Hebrew Trans: and the stock which your right hand planted, and the son whom you made strong for yourself.
Septuagint Trans: and restore it which your right hand planted, and upon the son of man whom you strengthened for yourself.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And bring to completion that which your right hand has planted, and upon the son whom you have strengthened for yourself.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: and the root which your right hand has planted, and the son whom you confirmed for yourself.

Hebrew Trans: It is burned with fire, it is cut down; at the rebuke of your face they perish.
Septuagint Trans: It is burned with fire and dug up; from the rebuke of your face they shall perish.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Things burned with fire and dug up shall perish at the rebuke of your countenance.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Those who burned it with fire and cut its branches—at the rebuke of your face let them perish.

Hebrew Trans: Let your hand be upon the man of your right hand, upon the son of man whom you made strong for yourself.
Septuagint Trans: Let your hand be upon the man of your right hand, and upon the son of man whom you strengthened for yourself.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Let your hand be upon the man of your right hand, and upon the son of man whom you have strengthened for yourself.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Let your hand be upon the man of your right hand, and upon the son of man whom you confirmed for yourself.

Hebrew Trans: Then we will not turn back from you; revive us, and we will call upon your name.
Septuagint Trans: And we shall not depart from you; you will give us life, and we shall call upon your name.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And we will not depart from you; you will give us life, and we will call upon your name.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And we will not depart from you; you will give us life, and we will call upon your name.

Hebrew Trans: O LORD God of hosts, restore us; cause your face to shine, and we shall be saved.
Septuagint Trans: O Lord, God of hosts, restore us and cause your face to shine, and we shall be saved.
Gallican Psalter Trans: O Lord God of hosts, turn us back, and show your face, and we shall be saved.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: O Lord God of hosts, restore us, and show your face, and we shall be saved.

Hebrew Trans: For the choirmaster, upon the Gittith. Of Asaph.
Septuagint Trans: For the end, over the wine-presses. A psalm for Asaph.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Unto the end, for the wine presses. A Psalm of Asaph.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: To the victor, on the winepresses. Of Asaph.

Hebrew Trans: Sing aloud to God our strength; shout for joy to the God of Jacob.
Septuagint Trans: Rejoice in God our helper; shout aloud to the God of Jacob.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Rejoice in God our helper; sing joyfully to the God of Jacob.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Praise God, our strength; shout joyfully to the God of Jacob.

Hebrew Trans: Raise a song and sound the timbrel, the pleasant harp with the lute.
Septuagint Trans: Take up a psalm and bring forth the timbrel, the pleasant psaltery with the harp.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Take up a psalm, and bring forth the timbrel, the pleasant psaltery with the harp.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Take up a song and bring forth the timbrel, the beautiful harp with the psaltery.

Hebrew Trans: Blow the shofar at the new moon, at the full moon, for the day of our feast.
Septuagint Trans: Blow the trumpet at the new moon, with the trumpet on the notable day of our feast.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Blow the trumpet at the new moon, with the trumpet on the notable day of our solemnity.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Blow the trumpet at the new moon, and in the middle of the month, on the day of our solemnity.

Hebrew Trans: For it is a statute for Israel, an ordinance of the God of Jacob.
Septuagint Trans: For this is a statute for Israel, and a judgment of the God of Jacob.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For it is a precept of Israel, and a judgment of the God of Jacob.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For it is a statute for Israel, and a judgment of the God of Jacob.

Hebrew Trans: He appointed it as a testimony in Joseph, when he went out over the land of Egypt; a language I had not known I heard.
Septuagint Trans: He appointed it as a testimony in Joseph, when he came out of the land of Egypt; he heard a language he did not know.
Gallican Psalter Trans: He established it as a testimony in Joseph, when he came out of the land of Egypt; he heard a tongue which he knew not.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: He established it as a testimony in Joseph, when he went out from the land of Egypt; I heard a language that I did not know.

Hebrew Trans: I removed the burden from his shoulder; his hands were freed from the basket.
Septuagint Trans: He removed his back from the burdens; his hands had served in the basket.
Gallican Psalter Trans: He turned away his back from the burdens; his hands served in the basket.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: I removed his shoulder from the burden; his hands withdrew from the basket.

Hebrew Trans: In distress you called, and I delivered you; I answered you in the secret place of thunder; I tested you at the waters of Meribah. Selah.
Septuagint Trans: In affliction you called upon me, and I delivered you; I heard you in the secret place of the storm; I tested you at the water of contradiction. Selah.
Gallican Psalter Trans: In tribulation you called upon me, and I delivered you; I heard you in the hidden place of the tempest; I proved you at the waters of Contradiction. Selah.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: In tribulation you called, and I delivered you; I heard you in the secret place of thunder; I proved you at the waters of Contradiction. Selah.

Hebrew Trans: Hear, O my people, and I will testify against you; O Israel, if you would listen to me!
Septuagint Trans: Hear, O my people, and I will testify to you; O Israel, if you would listen to me:
Gallican Psalter Trans: Hear, O my people, and I will testify to you: O Israel, if you would listen to me:
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Hear, O my people, and I will testify to you: O Israel, if you will hear me:

Hebrew Trans: There shall be no strange god among you, nor shall you bow down to a foreign god.
Septuagint Trans: There shall be no new god in you, nor shall you worship a foreign god.
Gallican Psalter Trans: There shall be no new god in you, nor shall you adore a foreign god.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: There shall be no strange god in you, nor shall you worship a foreign god.

Hebrew Trans: I am the LORD your God, who brought you up out of the land of Egypt; open wide your mouth, and I will fill it.
Septuagint Trans: For I am the Lord your God, who brought you up out of the land of Egypt; open wide your mouth, and I will fill it.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For I am the Lord your God, who brought you out of the land of Egypt. Open wide your mouth, and I will fill it.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: I am the Lord your God, who brought you out of the land of Egypt; open wide your mouth, and I will fill it.

Hebrew Trans: But my people did not listen to my voice, and Israel would not submit to me.
Septuagint Trans: But my people did not hear my voice, and Israel gave no heed to me.
Gallican Psalter Trans: But my people did not hear my voice, and Israel did not attend to me.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: But my people did not hear my voice, and Israel did not believe in me.

Hebrew Trans: So I sent them away in the stubbornness of their heart; they walked in their own counsels.
Septuagint Trans: And I sent them away according to the devices of their hearts; they shall walk in their own devices.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And I let them go according to the desires of their heart; they shall walk in their own inventions.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: So I let them go in the perversity of their heart; they shall walk in their own counsels.

Hebrew Trans: If only my people would listen to me, if Israel would walk in my ways,
Septuagint Trans: If my people had listened to me, if Israel had walked in my ways,
Gallican Psalter Trans: If my people had listened to me, if Israel had walked in my ways,
Hebrew Psalter Trans: If only my people had heard me, if Israel had walked in my ways,

Hebrew Trans: I would soon subdue their enemies, and against their adversaries I would turn my hand.
Septuagint Trans: I would have humbled their enemies in no time, and I would have laid my hand upon those who afflict them.
Gallican Psalter Trans: I would perhaps have humbled their enemies for nothing, and upon those who troubled them I would have laid my hand.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: I would have humbled their enemies as nothing, and upon their adversaries I would have turned my hand.

Hebrew Trans: Those who hate the LORD would cringe before him, and their time of punishment would last forever.
Septuagint Trans: The enemies of the Lord have lied to him, and their time shall be forever.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The enemies of the Lord have lied to him, and their time shall be forever.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Those who hate the Lord would deny him, and their time would be forever.

Hebrew Trans: And he would feed him with the finest of wheat, and with honey from the rock I would satisfy you.
Septuagint Trans: And he fed them with the fat of wheat, and with honey from the rock he satisfied them.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And he fed them with the fat of wheat, and from the rock with honey he satisfied them.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And he fed them with the fat of wheat, and with honey from the rock he satisfied them.

Hebrew Trans: A psalm of Asaph. God stands in the congregation of the mighty; in the midst of the gods He judges.
Septuagint Trans: A psalm of Asaph. God stood in the assembly of gods, and in the midst he judges among gods.
Gallican Psalter Trans: A psalm of Asaph. God stood in the assembly of the gods; but in the midst God judges.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: A song of Asaph. God has stood in the assembly of God; in the midst He judges.

Hebrew Trans: How long will you judge unjustly and show favor to the wicked? Selah.
Septuagint Trans: How long will you judge unjustly and show partiality to sinners? Selah.
Gallican Psalter Trans: How long will you judge iniquity and show partiality to the faces of sinners? Diapsalma.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: How long will you judge iniquity and accept the faces of the wicked? Always?

Hebrew Trans: Judge the poor and the fatherless; do justice to the afflicted and the destitute.
Septuagint Trans: Judge for the orphan and the poor; vindicate the humble and the needy.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Judge for the needy and the orphan; justify the humble and the poor.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Judge for the poor and the orphan; do justice to the needy and the destitute.

Hebrew Trans: Rescue the poor and the needy; deliver them from the hand of the wicked.
Septuagint Trans: Rescue the needy and the poor; deliver them from the hand of the sinner.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Rescue the poor and the needy; deliver them from the hand of the sinner.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Save the helpless and the poor; deliver them from the hand of the wicked.

Hebrew Trans: They do not know, nor do they understand; they walk about in darkness; all the foundations of the earth are shaken.
Septuagint Trans: They did not know, nor did they understand; they walk in darkness. All the foundations of the earth shall be shaken.
Gallican Psalter Trans: They did not know, nor did they understand; they walk in darkness: all the foundations of the earth shall be moved.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: They do not know, nor do they understand; they walk in darkness; all the foundations of the earth shall be moved.

Hebrew Trans: I said, "You are gods, and all of you are sons of the Most High.
Septuagint Trans: I said, "You are gods, and all of you are sons of the Most High."
Gallican Psalter Trans: I said: You are gods, and all of you are sons of the Most High.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: I said: You are gods, and all of you are sons of the Most High.

Hebrew Trans: But indeed, like men you shall die, and like one of the princes you shall fall."
Septuagint Trans: But you shall die like men, and fall like one of the rulers.
Gallican Psalter Trans: But you shall die like men, and shall fall like one of the princes.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Therefore you shall die like Adam, and you shall fall like one of the princes.

Hebrew Trans: Arise, O God, judge the earth, for You shall inherit all the nations.
Septuagint Trans: Arise, O God, judge the earth, for you shall inherit among all the nations.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Arise, O God, judge the earth: for you shall inherit among all the nations.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Arise, O Lord, judge the earth, for You shall inherit all the nations.

Hebrew Trans: A song, a psalm of Asaph.
Septuagint Trans: A song of a psalm, for Asaph.
Gallican Psalter Trans: A canticle psalm of Asaph.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: A canticle psalm of Asaph.

Hebrew Trans: O God, do not keep silence; do not be still and do not be quiet, O God.
Septuagint Trans: O God, who shall be likened to you? Do not be silent, and do not be still, O God.
Gallican Psalter Trans: O God, who shall be like unto you? Do not be silent, neither be still, O God.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: O God, be not silent, do not be still and do not rest, O God.

Hebrew Trans: For behold, your enemies rage, and those who hate you have lifted up their head.
Septuagint Trans: For behold, your enemies have made a clamor, and those who hate you have lifted up their head.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For behold, your enemies have made a noise, and those who hate you have lifted up the head.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For behold, your enemies have made a tumult, and those who hate you have lifted up their head.

Hebrew Trans: Against your people they devise cunning counsel, and they conspire against your hidden ones.
Septuagint Trans: Against your people they have devised a crafty scheme, and they have plotted against your holy ones.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Against your people they have devised a malicious counsel, and have plotted against your holy ones.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Against your people they have dealt wickedly, and they have conspired against your hidden ones.

Hebrew Trans: They have said, "Come, and let us cut them off from being a nation, and let the name of Israel be remembered no more."
Septuagint Trans: They said, "Come, and let us utterly destroy them from being a nation, and let the name of Israel be remembered no more."
Gallican Psalter Trans: They have said: Come, and let us destroy them from among the nations, and let the name of Israel be remembered no more.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: They said: "Come, and let us destroy them from being a nation, and let the name of Israel be remembered no more."

Hebrew Trans: For they have consulted together with one heart; against you they make a covenant:
Septuagint Trans: For they have plotted together in one accord; against you they have made a covenant:
Gallican Psalter Trans: For they have plotted together with one mind; against you they have made a covenant:
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For they have plotted together with one accord; they have made a covenant against you:

Hebrew Trans: The tents of Edom and the Ishmaelites, Moab and the Hagrites,
Septuagint Trans: The tents of the Idumeans and the Ishmaelites, Moab and the Hagarenes,
Gallican Psalter Trans: The tabernacles of the Edomites and the Ishmaelites, Moab and the Hagarenes,
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The tabernacles of Edom and the Ishmaelites, Moab and the Hagarenes,

Hebrew Trans: Gebal and Ammon and Amalek, Philistia with the inhabitants of Tyre.
Septuagint Trans: Gebal and Ammon and Amalek, and the foreigners with the inhabitants of Tyre.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Gebal and Ammon and Amalek, the foreigners with the inhabitants of Tyre.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Gebal and Ammon and Amalek, Philistia with the inhabitants of Tyre.

Hebrew Trans: Assyria also has joined with them; they have been an arm to the children of Lot. Selah.
Septuagint Trans: And indeed Assyria also came along with them; they became a help to the sons of Lot. Selah.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For indeed Assyria has come with them; they have become a help to the children of Lot. Selah.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Assyria also has joined with them; they have become the arm of the children of Lot forever.

Hebrew Trans: Do to them as to Midian, as to Sisera, as to Jabin at the brook of Kishon,
Septuagint Trans: Do to them as to Midian and to Sisera, as to Jabin at the torrent of Kishon.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Do unto them as unto Midian and Sisera, as unto Jabin at the torrent of Kishon.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Do to them as to Midian and Sisera, as to Jabin at the brook of Kishon.

Hebrew Trans: who were destroyed at En-dor; they became as dung for the ground.
Septuagint Trans: They were utterly destroyed at En-dor; they became as dung for the earth.
Gallican Psalter Trans: They perished at Endor; they became as dung for the earth.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: They were destroyed at Endor; they became as dung for the earth.

Hebrew Trans: Make their nobles like Oreb and like Zeeb, and like Zebah and like Zalmunna all their princes,
Septuagint Trans: Make their rulers like Oreb and Zeeb, and Zebee and Salmana, all their rulers,
Gallican Psalter Trans: Make their princes like Oreb and Zeeb, and Zebah and Zalmunna, all their princes,
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Make their leaders like Oreb and Zeeb, and Zebah and Zalmunna, all their princes,

Hebrew Trans: who said, "Let us take possession for ourselves of the pastures of God."
Septuagint Trans: who said, "Let us take for ourselves the sanctuary of God as an inheritance."
Gallican Psalter Trans: who said: Let us possess for ourselves the sanctuary of God as an inheritance.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: who said: "Let us possess for ourselves the beauty of God."

Hebrew Trans: O my God, make them like the whirling dust, like chaff before the wind.
Septuagint Trans: O my God, make them like a wheel, like stubble before the face of the wind.
Gallican Psalter Trans: My God, make them like a wheel, like stubble before the face of the wind.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: My God, make them like a wheel, like stubble before the face of the wind.

Hebrew Trans: As fire burns the forest, and as the flame sets the mountains ablaze,
Septuagint Trans: As fire that shall burn through a forest, as a flame that shall consume mountains,
Gallican Psalter Trans: As a fire that burns the forest, as a flame that burns the mountains,
Hebrew Psalter Trans: As fire burns a forest, and as a flame devours the mountains,

Hebrew Trans: so pursue them with your tempest, and terrify them with your storm.
Septuagint Trans: so shall you pursue them with your tempest, and in your wrath you shall trouble them.
Gallican Psalter Trans: so shall you pursue them in your tempest, and in your wrath you shall trouble them.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: so pursue them with your tempest, and trouble them in your whirlwind.

Hebrew Trans: Fill their faces with shame, that they may seek your name, O LORD.
Septuagint Trans: Fill their faces with dishonor, and they shall seek your name, O Lord.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Fill their faces with shame, and they shall seek your name, O Lord.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Fill their faces with shame, and they shall seek your name, O Lord.

Hebrew Trans: Let them be ashamed and dismayed forever; let them be confounded and perish.
Septuagint Trans: Let them be ashamed and troubled forever and ever, and let them be confounded and perish.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Let them be ashamed and troubled forever and ever, and let them be confounded and perish.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Let them be confounded and troubled forever, and let them be ashamed and perish.

Hebrew Trans: And let them know that you alone, whose name is the LORD, are the Most High over all the earth.
Septuagint Trans: And let them know that your name is Lord; you alone are the Most High over all the earth.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And let them know that your name is the Lord; you alone are the Most High over all the earth.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And let them know that your name is the Lord; you alone are the Most High over all the earth.

Hebrew Trans: For the choirmaster, upon the Gittith; a psalm of the sons of Korah.
Septuagint Trans: For the end, concerning the winepresses; for the sons of Korah, a psalm.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Unto the end, for the winepresses, a psalm for the sons of Korah.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: To the victor, for the winepress, of the sons of Korah, a song.

Hebrew Trans: How lovely are your dwelling places, O LORD of hosts!
Septuagint Trans: How beloved are your dwelling places, O Lord of hosts!
Gallican Psalter Trans: How lovely are your tabernacles, O Lord of hosts!
Hebrew Psalter Trans: How lovely are your tabernacles, O Lord of hosts!

Hebrew Trans: My soul longs, yea, even faints for the courts of the LORD; my heart and my flesh cry out to the living God.
Septuagint Trans: My soul longs and faints for the courts of the Lord; my heart and my flesh rejoiced in the living God.
Gallican Psalter Trans: My soul longs and faints for the courts of the Lord; my heart and my flesh have rejoiced in the living God.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: My soul longs and faints for the courts of the Lord; my heart and my flesh shall praise the living God.

Hebrew Trans: Even the sparrow has found a house, and the swallow a nest for herself, where she may lay her young — your altars, O LORD of hosts, my King and my God.
Septuagint Trans: For even the sparrow has found herself a house, and the turtledove a nest for herself, where she may lay her young—your altars, O Lord of hosts, my King and my God.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For even the sparrow has found herself a home, and the turtledove a nest for herself, where she may lay her young—your altars, O Lord of hosts, my King and my God.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Indeed, the bird has found a home, and the sparrow a nest for herself, where she may lay her young—your altars, O Lord of hosts, my King and my God.

Hebrew Trans: Blessed are those who dwell in your house; they will ever praise you. Selah.
Septuagint Trans: Blessed are those who dwell in your house; unto the ages of ages they shall praise you. Selah.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Blessed are they who dwell in your house; they shall praise you forever and ever. Selah.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Blessed are they who dwell in your house; they shall praise you forever still.

Hebrew Trans: Blessed is the man whose strength is in you, in whose heart are the highways.
Septuagint Trans: Blessed is the man whose help is from you, O Lord; he has set ascents in his heart.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Blessed is the man whose help is from you; he has arranged ascents in his heart.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Blessed is the man whose strength is in you; paths are in his heart.

Hebrew Trans: Passing through the valley of Baca, they make it a place of springs; indeed, the early rain covers it with blessings.
Septuagint Trans: In the valley of weeping, to the place which he has set; for indeed the lawgiver shall give blessings.
Gallican Psalter Trans: In the valley of tears, in the place which he has set; for the lawgiver shall give blessings.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Passing through the valley of weeping, they shall make it a fountain; indeed, the teacher shall be clothed with blessing.

Hebrew Trans: They go from strength to strength; each one appears before God in Zion.
Septuagint Trans: They shall go from strength to strength; the God of gods shall be seen in Zion.
Gallican Psalter Trans: They shall go from strength to strength; the God of gods shall be seen in Zion.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: They shall go from strength to strength; they shall appear before God in Zion.

Hebrew Trans: O LORD God of hosts, hear my prayer; give ear, O God of Jacob. Selah.
Septuagint Trans: O Lord, the God of hosts, hear my prayer; give ear, O God of Jacob. Selah.
Gallican Psalter Trans: O Lord God of hosts, hear my prayer; give ear, O God of Jacob. Selah.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: O Lord God of hosts, hear my prayer; listen, O God of Jacob, always.

Hebrew Trans: Behold our shield, O God, and look upon the face of your anointed one.
Septuagint Trans: O God, our defender, behold, and look upon the face of your anointed one.
Gallican Psalter Trans: O God, our protector, look upon us, and regard the face of your anointed.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Behold our shield, O God, and look upon the face of your anointed one.

Hebrew Trans: For a day in your courts is better than a thousand; I would rather stand at the threshold of the house of my God than dwell in the tents of wickedness.
Septuagint Trans: For one day in your courts is better than thousands; I chose to be cast aside in the house of God rather than to dwell in the tents of sinners.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For one day in your courts is better than a thousand; I have chosen to be cast aside in the house of my God, rather than to dwell in the tabernacles of sinners.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For better is one day in your courts than a thousand; I have chosen to be cast aside in the house of my God rather than to dwell in the tents of wickedness.

Hebrew Trans: For the LORD God is a sun and a shield; the LORD gives grace and glory; no good thing does he withhold from those who walk in uprightness.
Septuagint Trans: For the Lord God loves mercy and truth; the Lord shall give grace and glory; he shall not deprive of good things those who walk in innocence.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For God loves mercy and truth; the Lord will give grace and glory; he will not deprive of good things those who walk in innocence.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For the Lord God is a sun and a shield; the Lord will give grace and glory, nor will he withhold good from those who walk in perfection.

Hebrew Trans: O LORD of hosts, blessed is the man who trusts in you.
Septuagint Trans: O Lord of hosts, blessed is the man who hopes in you.
Gallican Psalter Trans: O Lord of hosts, blessed is the man who hopes in you.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: O Lord of hosts, blessed is the man who trusts in you.

Hebrew Trans: For the chief musician; of the sons of Korah; a psalm.
Septuagint Trans: For the end; a psalm for the sons of Korah.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Unto the end, a psalm for the sons of Korah.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: To the victor; of the sons of Korah, a canticle.

Hebrew Trans: You showed favor, O LORD, to your land; you restored the captivity of Jacob.
Septuagint Trans: You have been well pleased, O Lord, with your land; you have turned back the captivity of Jacob.
Gallican Psalter Trans: You have blessed, O Lord, your land; you have turned back the captivity of Jacob.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: You have been pleased, O Lord, with your land; you have brought back the captivity of Jacob.

Hebrew Trans: You forgave the iniquity of your people; you covered all their sin. Selah.
Septuagint Trans: You have forgiven the lawless deeds of your people; you have covered all their sins. Interlude.
Gallican Psalter Trans: You have forgiven the iniquities of your people; you have covered all their sins. Diapsalma.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: You have forgiven the iniquity of your people; you have covered all their iniquities forever.

Hebrew Trans: You gathered all your wrath; you turned from the fierceness of your anger.
Septuagint Trans: You have put an end to all your wrath; you have turned away from the anger of your fury.
Gallican Psalter Trans: You have mitigated all your wrath; you have turned away from the anger of your indignation.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: You have restrained all your indignation; you have turned away from the wrath of your fury.

Hebrew Trans: Restore us, O God of our salvation, and put away your displeasure toward us.
Septuagint Trans: Restore us, O God of our salvation, and turn away your fury from us.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Convert us, O God of our salvation, and turn away your anger from us.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Convert us, O God our salvation, and turn away your anger from us.

Hebrew Trans: Will you be angry with us forever? Will you draw out your anger to generation after generation?
Septuagint Trans: Will you be angry with us forever, or will you extend your wrath from generation to generation?
Gallican Psalter Trans: Will you be angry with us forever, or will you extend your wrath from generation to generation?
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Do not be angry with us forever, extending your wrath from generation to generation.

Hebrew Trans: Will you not again revive us, that your people may rejoice in you?
Septuagint Trans: O God, you will turn and give us life, and your people will rejoice in you.
Gallican Psalter Trans: O God, you will turn and give us life, and your people will rejoice in you.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Will you not, turning again, give us life, and your people shall rejoice in you?

Hebrew Trans: Show us, O LORD, your steadfast love, and grant us your salvation.
Septuagint Trans: Show us, O Lord, your mercy, and grant us your salvation.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Show us, O Lord, your mercy, and grant us your salvation.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Show us, O Lord, your mercy, and grant us your salvation.

Hebrew Trans: I will hear what God the LORD will speak, for he will speak peace to his people and to his faithful ones, and let them not turn back to folly.
Septuagint Trans: I will hear what the Lord God will speak in me, for he will speak peace to his people, and to his holy ones, and to those who turn their heart toward him.
Gallican Psalter Trans: I will hear what the Lord God speaks in me, for he will speak peace unto his people, and upon his holy ones, and unto those who turn to the heart.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: I will hear what the Lord God will speak, for he will speak peace to his people and to his saints, that they may not turn back to foolishness.

Hebrew Trans: Surely his salvation is near to those who fear him, that glory may dwell in our land.
Septuagint Trans: Surely his salvation is near to those who fear him, so that glory may dwell in our land.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Surely his salvation is near to those who fear him, that glory may dwell in our land.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Surely his salvation is near to those who fear him, that glory may dwell in our land.

Hebrew Trans: Steadfast love and truth have met together; righteousness and peace have kissed each other.
Septuagint Trans: Mercy and truth have met together; righteousness and peace have kissed each other.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Mercy and truth have met each other; justice and peace have kissed.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Mercy and truth have met together; justice and peace have kissed.

Hebrew Trans: Truth springs up from the earth, and righteousness looks down from heaven.
Septuagint Trans: Truth has sprung up from the earth, and righteousness has looked down from heaven.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Truth has sprung from the earth, and justice has looked down from heaven.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Truth has sprung from the earth, and justice has looked down from heaven.

Hebrew Trans: Indeed, the LORD will give what is good, and our land will yield its increase.
Septuagint Trans: For indeed the Lord will give goodness, and our land will yield its fruit.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For indeed the Lord will give goodness, and our land will yield its fruit.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Indeed the Lord will give what is good, and our land will yield its fruit.

Hebrew Trans: Righteousness will go before him and will make his footsteps a way.
Septuagint Trans: Righteousness will go before him, and he will set his steps on the way.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Justice will walk before him, and will set his steps upon the way.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Justice shall walk before him, and shall set his steps upon the way.

Hebrew Trans: A prayer of David. Incline, O LORD, Your ear; answer me, for I am poor and needy.
Septuagint Trans: A prayer of David. Incline your ear, O Lord, and hear me, for I am poor and needy.
Gallican Psalter Trans: A prayer of David himself. Incline your ear, O Lord, and hear me, for I am needy and poor.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: A prayer of David. Incline your ear, O Lord, hear me, for I am needy and poor.

Hebrew Trans: Preserve my soul, for I am godly; save Your servant, O You my God, who trusts in You.
Septuagint Trans: Guard my soul, for I am holy; save your servant, O my God, the one who hopes in you.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Preserve my soul, for I am holy; save your servant, O my God, who hopes in you.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Guard my soul, for I am holy; save your servant, you my God, who trusts in you.

Hebrew Trans: Be gracious to me, O Lord, for to You I cry all the day.
Septuagint Trans: Have mercy on me, O Lord, for to you I will cry all the day long.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Have mercy on me, O Lord, for I will cry to you all the day long.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Have mercy on me, O Lord, for I will cry out to you all the day long.

Hebrew Trans: Gladden the soul of Your servant, for to You, O Lord, I lift up my soul.
Septuagint Trans: Gladden the soul of your servant, for to you, O Lord, I have lifted up my soul.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Gladden the soul of your servant, for to you, O Lord, I have lifted up my soul.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Gladden the soul of your servant, for to you I lift up my soul.

Hebrew Trans: For You, O Lord, are good and forgiving, and abundant in lovingkindness to all who call upon You.
Septuagint Trans: For you, O Lord, are kind and gentle, and abundant in mercy to all who call upon you.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For you, O Lord, are sweet and gentle, and of great mercy to all who call upon you.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For you, O Lord, are good and forgiving, and abundant in mercy to all who call upon you.

Hebrew Trans: Give ear, O LORD, to my prayer, and attend to the voice of my supplications.
Septuagint Trans: Give ear, O Lord, to my prayer, and attend to the voice of my supplication.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Receive with your ears, O Lord, my prayer, and attend to the voice of my supplication.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Hear, O Lord, my prayer, and listen to the voice of my supplications.
