Hebrew Trans: In the day of my trouble I will call upon You, for You will answer me.
Septuagint Trans: In the day of my affliction I cried out to you, for you heard me.
Gallican Psalter Trans: In the day of my tribulation I cried to you, for you heard me.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: In the day of my tribulation I will call upon you, for you will hear me.

Hebrew Trans: There is none like You among the gods, O Lord, and there are none like Your works.
Septuagint Trans: There is none like you among the gods, O Lord, and there is nothing according to your works.
Gallican Psalter Trans: There is none like you among the gods, O Lord, and there is nothing according to your works.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: There is none like you among the gods, O Lord, nor is there anything equal to your works.

Hebrew Trans: All nations whom You have made shall come and worship before You, O Lord, and shall glorify Your name.
Septuagint Trans: All the nations, as many as you have made, shall come and worship before you, O Lord, and shall glorify your name.
Gallican Psalter Trans: All the nations, whatsoever you have made, shall come and worship before you, O Lord, and shall glorify your name.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: All the nations which you have made shall come and worship before you, O Lord, and shall glorify your name.

Hebrew Trans: For You are great and do wondrous things; You alone are God.
Septuagint Trans: For you are great and do wondrous things; you alone are God, the great one.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For you are great and do wondrous things; you alone are God.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For you are great and do wondrous things; you alone are God.

Hebrew Trans: Teach me, O LORD, Your way; I will walk in Your truth; unite my heart to fear Your name.
Septuagint Trans: Guide me, O Lord, in your way, and I will walk in your truth; let my heart rejoice to fear your name.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Lead me, O Lord, in your way, and I will walk in your truth; let my heart rejoice that it may fear your name.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Teach me, O Lord, your way, that I may walk in your truth; make my heart undivided, that it may fear your name.

Hebrew Trans: I will praise You, O Lord my God, with all my heart, and I will glorify Your name forevermore.
Septuagint Trans: I will praise you, O Lord my God, with all my heart, and I will glorify your name forever.
Gallican Psalter Trans: I will praise you, O Lord my God, with my whole heart, and I will glorify your name forever.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: I will praise you, O Lord my God, with my whole heart, and I will glorify your name forever.

Hebrew Trans: For Your lovingkindness toward me is great, and You have delivered my soul from the depths of Sheol.
Septuagint Trans: For your mercy is great upon me, and you have delivered my soul from the lowest depths of Hades.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For your mercy is great upon me, and you have delivered my soul from the lowest hell.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For your mercy is great upon me, and you have delivered my soul from the lowest depths of hell.

Hebrew Trans: O God, the arrogant have risen against me, and a company of ruthless men have sought my life, and they have not set You before them.
Septuagint Trans: O God, transgressors have risen up against me, and an assembly of mighty ones have sought my soul, and they have not set you before them.
Gallican Psalter Trans: O God, the wicked have risen up against me, and the assembly of the mighty have sought my soul, and they have not set you before their sight.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: O God, the proud have risen up against me, and an assembly of violent men have sought my soul, and they have not set you before their sight.

Hebrew Trans: But You, O Lord, are a God merciful and gracious, slow to anger and abundant in lovingkindness and truth.
Septuagint Trans: And you, O Lord God, are compassionate and merciful, slow to anger and abundant in mercy and true.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And you, O Lord God, compassionate and merciful, patient and of great mercy and true,
Hebrew Psalter Trans: But you, O Lord God, are merciful and gracious, patient and of great mercy and true.

Hebrew Trans: Turn to me and be gracious to me; give Your strength to Your servant, and save the son of Your handmaid.
Septuagint Trans: Look upon me and have mercy on me; give your strength to your servant, and save the son of your handmaid.
Gallican Psalter Trans: look upon me and have mercy on me; give your strength to your servant, and save the son of your handmaid.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Look upon me and have mercy on me; give your strength to your servant, and save the son of your handmaid.

Hebrew Trans: Show me a sign for good, that those who hate me may see and be ashamed, for You, O LORD, have helped me and comforted me.
Septuagint Trans: Show me a sign for good, and let those who hate me see it and be put to shame, for you, O Lord, have helped me and comforted me.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Show me a sign for good, and let those who hate me see it and be confounded, for you, O Lord, have helped me and comforted me.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Show me a sign of your goodness, and let those who hate me see it and be confounded, for you, O Lord, have helped me and consoled me.

Hebrew Trans: Of the sons of Korah, a psalm, a song. His foundation is in the holy mountains.
Septuagint Trans: For the sons of Korah, a psalm of song. His foundations are in the holy mountains.
Gallican Psalter Trans: A psalm of a canticle for the sons of Korah. His foundations are in the holy mountains.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: A psalm of a canticle for the sons of Korah. His foundations are in the holy mountains.

Hebrew Trans: The LORD loves the gates of Zion more than all the dwellings of Jacob.
Septuagint Trans: The Lord loves the gates of Zion more than all the dwellings of Jacob.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The Lord loves the gates of Zion above all the tabernacles of Jacob.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The Lord loves the gates of Zion above all the tabernacles of Jacob.

Hebrew Trans: Glorious things are spoken of you, O city of God. Selah.
Septuagint Trans: Glorious things have been spoken concerning you, O city of God. Selah.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Glorious things are said of you, O city of God. Diapsalma.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Glorious things are said of you forever, O city of God.

Hebrew Trans: I will make mention of Rahab and Babylon among those who know me; behold Philistia and Tyre, with Cush — this one was born there.
Septuagint Trans: I will make mention of Rahab and Babylon to those who know me; and behold, the Philistines and Tyre and the people of the Ethiopians—these were born there.
Gallican Psalter Trans: I will be mindful of Rahab and Babylon among those who know me: behold the foreigners, and Tyre, and the people of the Ethiopians, these were there.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: I will remember the proud ones and Babylon among those who know me: behold Philistia and Tyre with Ethiopia — this one was born there.

Hebrew Trans: And of Zion it shall be said, "Man after man was born in her," and the Most High himself shall establish her.
Septuagint Trans: A man shall say "Mother" to Zion, and a man was born in her, and the Most High himself founded her.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Shall it not be said of Zion, "This man and that man was born in her," and the Most High himself has founded her?
Hebrew Psalter Trans: But of Zion it shall be said: man after man was born in her, and the Most High himself has founded her.

Hebrew Trans: The LORD shall count, when he records the peoples, "This one was born there." Selah.
Septuagint Trans: The Lord shall recount in the writing of peoples and of rulers those who were born in her. Selah.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The Lord shall tell in the writing of the peoples and of the princes of those who were in her. Diapsalma.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The Lord shall count when he writes up the peoples: this one was born in her forever.

Hebrew Trans: And singers as well as dancers — all my springs are in you.
Septuagint Trans: The dwelling of all who rejoice is in you.
Gallican Psalter Trans: As of all those rejoicing, their dwelling is in you.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And they shall sing as in choruses: all my springs are in you.

Hebrew Trans: A song, a psalm of the sons of Korah; for the choirmaster, upon Mahalath Leannoth; a Maskil of Heman the Ezrahite.
Septuagint Trans: An ode of a psalm for the sons of Korah, to the end, concerning Maeleth, for responding; of understanding, of Heman the Israelite.
Gallican Psalter Trans: A canticle of a psalm for the sons of Korah, unto the end, for Maeleth, to respond: an instruction of Heman the Ezrahite.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: A song of a poem of the sons of Korah, for the choirmaster, for the chorus, for singing; an instruction of Heman the Ezrahite.

Hebrew Trans: O LORD, God of my salvation, by day I have cried out, in the night before You.
Septuagint Trans: O Lord, the God of my salvation, by day I cried out and in the night before you.
Gallican Psalter Trans: O Lord, God of my salvation, by day I have cried out and by night before you.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: O Lord God of my salvation, by day I have cried out, in the night before you.

Hebrew Trans: Let my prayer come before You; incline Your ear to my cry.
Septuagint Trans: Let my prayer come before you; incline your ear to my supplication, O Lord.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Let my prayer enter into your sight; incline your ear to my supplication.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Let my prayer come before you; incline your ear to my supplication.

Hebrew Trans: For my soul is full of troubles, and my life draws near to Sheol.
Septuagint Trans: For my soul is filled with troubles, and my life has drawn near to Hades.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For my soul is filled with evils, and my life has drawn near to hell.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For my soul is filled with troubles, and my life has descended to the grave.

Hebrew Trans: I am counted with those who go down to the pit; I have become like a man without strength.
Septuagint Trans: I was reckoned among those who go down into the pit; I became as a man without help, free among the dead.
Gallican Psalter Trans: I have been reckoned with those who descend into the pit; I have become like a man without help.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: I have been reckoned with those descending into the pit; I have become as a man without strength.

Hebrew Trans: Among the dead, set free, like the slain who lie in the grave, whom You remember no more, and they are cut off from Your hand.
Septuagint Trans: Like the wounded cast aside, sleeping in the grave, whom you remembered no more, and they were thrust away from your hand.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Free among the dead, like the wounded sleeping in the sepulchres, of whom you are no longer mindful, and they have been cast off from your hand.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Free among the dead, like the slain and those sleeping in the tomb, whom you remember no more, and who have been cut off from your hand.

Hebrew Trans: You have put me in the lowest pit, in dark places, in the depths.
Septuagint Trans: They placed me in the lowest pit, in dark places and in the shadow of death.
Gallican Psalter Trans: They have laid me in the lower pit, in dark places and in the shadow of death.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: You have placed me in the lowest pit, in darkness, in the depths.

Hebrew Trans: Your wrath lies heavy upon me, and You have afflicted me with all Your waves. Selah.
Septuagint Trans: Your wrath was fixed upon me, and all your waves you brought upon me. Selah.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Your fury is confirmed upon me, and all your waves you have brought in upon me. Selah.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Your fury has been confirmed upon me, and with all your waves you have afflicted me continually.

Hebrew Trans: You have removed my acquaintances far from me; You have made me an abomination to them; I am shut in and cannot go out.
Septuagint Trans: You have put my acquaintances far from me; they have made me an abomination to themselves. I was delivered over and could not go out.
Gallican Psalter Trans: You have put my acquaintances far from me; they have made me an abomination to themselves. I have been delivered up, and I did not come forth.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: You have put my acquaintances far from me; you have made me an abomination to them; I am shut in and cannot go out.

Hebrew Trans: My eye wastes away from affliction; I call upon You, O LORD, every day; I spread out my hands to You.
Septuagint Trans: My eyes grew weak from affliction; I cried out to you, O Lord, all the day long; I spread out my hands to you.
Gallican Psalter Trans: My eyes have languished from affliction. I have cried to you, O Lord, all the day; I have stretched out my hands to you.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: My eye has grown weak from affliction; I have called upon you, O Lord, all the day; I have stretched out my hands to you.

Hebrew Trans: Will You work wonders for the dead? Shall the shades rise up and praise You? Selah.
Septuagint Trans: Will you work wonders for the dead? Or will healers raise them up, and they shall give thanks to you?
Gallican Psalter Trans: Will you work wonders for the dead? Or shall physicians raise them up, and they shall confess to you? Selah.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Will you work wonders for the dead? Or shall the giants rise and confess to you forever?

Hebrew Trans: Shall Your lovingkindness be declared in the grave, Your faithfulness in Abaddon?
Septuagint Trans: Will anyone recount your mercy in the grave, and your truth in destruction?
Gallican Psalter Trans: Shall anyone in the sepulchre recount your mercy, and your truth in destruction?
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Shall your mercy be declared in the tomb, and your truth in destruction?

Hebrew Trans: Shall Your wonders be known in the darkness, and Your righteousness in the land of forgetfulness?
Septuagint Trans: Will your wonders be known in the darkness, and your righteousness in a land of forgetfulness?
Gallican Psalter Trans: Shall your wonders be known in the darkness, and your justice in the land of oblivion?
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Shall your wonders be known in the darkness, and your justice in the land that has been given over to oblivion?

Hebrew Trans: But I, unto You, O LORD, I have cried out, and in the morning my prayer comes before You.
Septuagint Trans: But I have cried out to you, O Lord, and in the morning my prayer shall come before you.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And I have cried to you, O Lord, and in the morning my prayer shall come before you.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: But I have cried out to you, O Lord, and in the morning my prayer shall come before you.

Hebrew Trans: Why, O LORD, do You cast off my soul? Why do You hide Your face from me?
Septuagint Trans: Why, O Lord, do you reject my soul? Why do you turn your face away from me?
Gallican Psalter Trans: Why, O Lord, do you reject my prayer? Why do you turn your face from me?
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Why, O Lord, do you cast away my soul? Why do you hide your face from me?

Hebrew Trans: I am afflicted and near death from my youth; I have borne Your terrors, I am in despair.
Septuagint Trans: I am poor and in labors from my youth; and having been exalted, I was humbled and brought to despair.
Gallican Psalter Trans: I am poor and in labors from my youth; but having been exalted, I was humbled and troubled.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: I am poor and full of sorrows; from my youth I have borne your fury, and I am troubled.

Hebrew Trans: Your fierce wrath has passed over me; Your terrors have destroyed me.
Septuagint Trans: Your wraths passed over me, and your terrors utterly troubled me.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Your wraths have passed over me, and your terrors have disturbed me.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Your wraths have passed over me; your terrors have crushed me.

Hebrew Trans: They surround me like water all the day long; they close in upon me together.
Septuagint Trans: They surrounded me like water all the day long; they encompassed me altogether.
Gallican Psalter Trans: They have surrounded me like water all the day long; they have encompassed me together.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: They have surrounded me like waters all the day; they have enclosed me together.

Hebrew Trans: You have removed from me lover and friend; my acquaintances are darkness.
Septuagint Trans: You have put far from me friend and neighbor, and my acquaintances, because of misery.
Gallican Psalter Trans: You have put far from me friend and neighbor, and my acquaintances from my misery.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: You have put far from me friend and companion; my acquaintances you have taken away.

Hebrew Trans: A Maskil of Ethan the Ezrahite.
Septuagint Trans: A wisdom-psalm of Ethan the Israelite.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The understanding of Ethan the Ezrahite.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Of the instruction of Ethan the Ezrahite.

Hebrew Trans: The mercies of the LORD I will sing forever; to generation after generation I will make known your faithfulness with my mouth.
Septuagint Trans: Your mercies, O Lord, I will sing forever; to generation and generation I will proclaim your truth with my mouth.
Gallican Psalter Trans: I will sing of the mercies of the Lord forever; to generation and generation I will declare your truth with my mouth.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: I will sing of the mercies of the Lord forever; from generation to generation I will proclaim your truth with my mouth.

Hebrew Trans: For I have said, "Forever shall mercy be built up; the heavens—you establish your faithfulness in them."
Septuagint Trans: For you said: "Mercy shall be built up forever; in the heavens your truth shall be established."
Gallican Psalter Trans: For you have said: Mercy shall be built up forever; in the heavens your truth shall be established in them.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For you have said: mercy shall be built up forever; you will establish the heavens, and your truth in them.

Hebrew Trans: "I have cut a covenant with my chosen one; I have sworn to David my servant:
Septuagint Trans: "I made a covenant with my chosen ones; I swore to David my servant:
Gallican Psalter Trans: I have made a covenant with my chosen ones; I have sworn to David my servant:
Hebrew Psalter Trans: I have struck a covenant with my chosen one; I have sworn to David my servant:

Hebrew Trans: Forever I will establish your seed, and I will build up your throne to generation after generation." Selah.
Septuagint Trans: Forever I will establish your seed, and I will build up your throne to generation and generation." Selah.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Forever I will prepare your seed, and I will build up your throne to generation and generation. Selah.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Forever I will establish your seed, and I will build up your throne from generation to generation always.

Hebrew Trans: And the heavens shall praise your wonder, O LORD, your faithfulness also in the assembly of the holy ones.
Septuagint Trans: The heavens shall confess your wonders, O Lord, and your truth in the assembly of the holy ones.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The heavens shall confess your wonders, O Lord, and your truth in the assembly of the saints.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The heavens shall confess your wonders, O Lord, and your truth in the assembly of the saints.

Hebrew Trans: For who in the sky can be compared to the LORD? Who among the sons of the mighty is like the LORD?
Septuagint Trans: For who in the clouds shall be equal to the Lord, and who shall be likened to the Lord among the sons of God?
Gallican Psalter Trans: For who in the clouds shall be equal to the Lord? Who shall be like the Lord among the sons of God?
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For who in the clouds shall be equal to the Lord? Who among the sons of God shall be likened to the Lord?

Hebrew Trans: God is greatly feared in the council of the holy ones, and awesome above all who surround him.
Septuagint Trans: God is glorified in the council of the holy ones, great and fearful above all those around him.
Gallican Psalter Trans: God, who is glorified in the council of the saints, is great and terrible above all who are around him.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: God is glorious in the secret council of the saints exceedingly, and terrible above all who are around him.

Hebrew Trans: O LORD, God of hosts, who is like you, mighty LORD? And your faithfulness surrounds you.
Septuagint Trans: O Lord, God of hosts, who is like you? You are mighty, O Lord, and your truth is round about you.
Gallican Psalter Trans: O Lord God of hosts, who is like you? You are mighty, O Lord, and your truth is round about you.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: O Lord God of hosts, who is like you? Most mighty Lord, and your truth is round about you.

Hebrew Trans: You rule over the swelling of the sea; when its waves rise, you still them.
Septuagint Trans: You rule the power of the sea, and the surging of its waves you calm.
Gallican Psalter Trans: You rule the power of the sea; and the motion of its waves you calm.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: You rule the pride of the sea, and you suppress the surging of its waves.

Hebrew Trans: You crushed Rahab like one slain; with the arm of your strength you scattered your enemies.
Septuagint Trans: You humbled the proud one as one who is wounded, and with the arm of your power you scattered your enemies.
Gallican Psalter Trans: You have humbled the proud one as one that is wounded; with the arm of your strength you have scattered your enemies.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: You have crushed the proud one as one who is wounded; with your strong arm you have scattered your enemies.

Hebrew Trans: Yours are the heavens, yours also is the earth; the world and its fullness—you founded them.
Septuagint Trans: Yours are the heavens, and yours is the earth; the inhabited world and its fullness, you founded them.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Yours are the heavens, and yours is the earth; the world and its fullness you have founded.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Yours are the heavens and yours is the earth; the world and its fullness you have founded.

Hebrew Trans: The north and the south—you created them; Tabor and Hermon rejoice in your name.
Septuagint Trans: The north and the seas, you created them; Tabor and Hermon shall rejoice in your name.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The north and the sea you have created; Tabor and Hermon shall rejoice in your name.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The north and the south you have created; Tabor and Hermon shall praise your name.

Hebrew Trans: Yours is an arm with might; strong is your hand, exalted is your right hand.
Septuagint Trans: Yours is the arm with sovereignty; let your hand be strengthened, let your right hand be exalted.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Yours is the arm with power; let your hand be strengthened and your right hand be exalted.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Yours is the arm with might; let your hand be strengthened, let your right hand be exalted.

Hebrew Trans: Righteousness and justice are the foundation of your throne; mercy and truth go before your face.
Septuagint Trans: Righteousness and judgment are the foundation of your throne; mercy and truth shall go before your face.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Justice and judgment are the foundation of your throne; mercy and truth shall go before your face.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Justice and judgment are the foundation of your throne; mercy and truth shall go before your face.

Hebrew Trans: Blessed are the people who know the joyful shout; O LORD, in the light of your face they walk.
Septuagint Trans: Blessed is the people that knows the shout of praise; O Lord, in the light of your face they shall walk.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Blessed is the people that knows jubilation. O Lord, in the light of your countenance they shall walk,
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Blessed is the people that knows the joyful shout. O Lord, in the light of your countenance they shall walk.

Hebrew Trans: In your name they rejoice all the day, and in your righteousness they are exalted.
Septuagint Trans: And in your name they shall rejoice all the day, and in your righteousness they shall be exalted.
Gallican Psalter Trans: and in your name they shall rejoice all the day, and in your justice they shall be exalted.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: In your name they shall rejoice all the day, and in your justice they shall be exalted.

Hebrew Trans: For you are the glory of their strength, and by your favor our horn is exalted.
Septuagint Trans: For you are the boast of their strength, and in your good pleasure our horn shall be exalted.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For you are the glory of their strength, and in your good pleasure our horn shall be exalted.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For you are the glory of their strength, and in your mercy you will lift up our horn.

Hebrew Trans: For to the LORD belongs our shield, and to the Holy One of Israel our king.
Septuagint Trans: For our help belongs to the Lord, and to the Holy One of Israel, our king.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For our protection belongs to the Lord, and to the Holy One of Israel, our king.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For from the Lord is our protection, and from the Holy One of Israel, our king.

Hebrew Trans: Then you spoke in a vision to your faithful ones and said, "I have set help upon a mighty one; I have exalted a chosen one from among the people.
Septuagint Trans: Then you spoke in a vision to your holy ones and said: "I have placed help upon a mighty one; I have exalted a chosen one out of my people.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Then you spoke in a vision to your saints and said: I have laid help upon one who is mighty; I have exalted one chosen from my people.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Then you spoke in a vision to your saints and said: I have placed help upon one who is mighty; I have exalted a chosen one from the people.

Hebrew Trans: I have found David my servant; with my holy oil I have anointed him,
Septuagint Trans: I have found David my servant; with my holy oil I have anointed him.
Gallican Psalter Trans: I have found David my servant; with my holy oil I have anointed him.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: I have found David my servant; with my holy oil I have anointed him.

Hebrew Trans: with whom my hand shall be established; my arm also shall strengthen him.
Septuagint Trans: For my hand shall support him, and my arm shall strengthen him.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For my hand shall help him, and my arm shall strengthen him.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: With whom my hand shall be firm, and my arm shall strengthen him.

Hebrew Trans: No enemy shall exact tribute from him, and no son of wickedness shall afflict him.
Septuagint Trans: No enemy shall prevail against him, and a son of lawlessness shall not proceed to afflict him.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The enemy shall have no advantage over him, nor shall the son of iniquity succeed in harming him.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The enemy shall not deceive him, nor shall the son of iniquity afflict him.

Hebrew Trans: And I will crush his adversaries before him, and those who hate him I will strike down.
Septuagint Trans: And I will cut down his enemies before his face, and those who hate him I will put to flight.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And I will cut down his enemies before his face, and those who hate him I will put to flight.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: But I will cut down his foes before his face, and those who hate him I will strike.

Hebrew Trans: And my faithfulness and my mercy shall be with him, and in my name his horn shall be exalted.
Septuagint Trans: And my truth and my mercy shall be with him, and in my name his horn shall be exalted.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And my truth and my mercy shall be with him, and in my name his horn shall be exalted.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And my truth and my mercy shall be with him, and in my name his horn shall be exalted.

Hebrew Trans: And I will set his hand on the sea, and his right hand on the rivers.
Septuagint Trans: And I will set his hand upon the sea, and his right hand upon the rivers.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And I will set his hand upon the sea, and his right hand upon the rivers.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And I will set his hand upon the sea, and his right hand upon the rivers.

Hebrew Trans: He shall cry to me, 'You are my father, my God, and the rock of my salvation.'
Septuagint Trans: He shall call upon me: 'You are my father, my God, and the protector of my salvation.'
Gallican Psalter Trans: He shall cry to me: You are my father, my God, and the upholder of my salvation.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: He shall call upon me: You are my father, my God, and the rock of my salvation.

Hebrew Trans: I also will make him the firstborn, the highest of the kings of the earth.
Septuagint Trans: And I will make him the firstborn, high above the kings of the earth.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And I will make him the firstborn, exalted above the kings of the earth.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And I will make him the firstborn, exalted above the kings of the earth.

Hebrew Trans: Forever I will keep my mercy for him, and my covenant shall stand firm for him.
Septuagint Trans: Forever I will keep my mercy for him, and my covenant shall be faithful to him.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Forever I will keep my mercy for him, and my covenant shall be faithful to him.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Forever I will keep my mercy for him, and my covenant shall be faithful to him.

Hebrew Trans: And I will establish his seed forever, and his throne as the days of heaven.
Septuagint Trans: And I will establish his seed forever and ever, and his throne as the days of heaven.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And I will establish his seed for ever and ever, and his throne as the days of heaven.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And I will make his seed perpetual, and his throne as the days of heaven.

Hebrew Trans: If his children forsake my law and do not walk in my judgments,
Septuagint Trans: If his sons forsake my law and do not walk in my judgments,
Gallican Psalter Trans: If his sons forsake my law, and walk not in my judgments;
Hebrew Psalter Trans: But if his sons shall forsake my law, and shall not walk in my judgments;

Hebrew Trans: if they profane my statutes and do not keep my commandments,
Septuagint Trans: if they profane my statutes and do not keep my commandments,
Gallican Psalter Trans: if they profane my statutes, and keep not my commandments;
Hebrew Psalter Trans: If they shall profane my ceremonies, and shall not keep my commandments;

Hebrew Trans: then I will visit their transgression with the rod and their iniquity with stripes.
Septuagint Trans: I will visit their lawless deeds with a rod and their sins with scourges.
Gallican Psalter Trans: I will visit their iniquities with a rod, and their sins with stripes.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: I will visit their transgressions with the rod, and their iniquity with stripes.

Hebrew Trans: But my mercy I will not break off from him, nor will I be false in my faithfulness.
Septuagint Trans: But my mercy I will not utterly scatter from him, nor will I act unjustly in my truth.
Gallican Psalter Trans: But my mercy I will not take away from him, nor will I harm him in my truth.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: But my mercy I will not take away from him, nor will I be false in my truth.

Hebrew Trans: I will not profane my covenant, and that which has gone forth from my lips I will not alter.
Septuagint Trans: Nor will I profane my covenant, and the things proceeding from my lips I will not annul.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Neither will I profane my covenant, and the things that proceed from my lips I will not make void.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: I will not violate my covenant, and that which has gone forth from my lips I will not alter.

Hebrew Trans: Once I have sworn by my holiness—surely I will not lie to David—
Septuagint Trans: Once I swore by my holiness: I shall not lie to David.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Once I have sworn by my holy one: I shall not lie to David.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Once I have sworn by my holiness; I will not lie to David.

Hebrew Trans: his seed shall endure forever, and his throne as the sun before me,
Septuagint Trans: His seed shall remain forever, and his throne as the sun before me,
Gallican Psalter Trans: His seed shall endure forever, and his throne as the sun in my sight,
Hebrew Psalter Trans: His seed shall endure forever, and his throne as the sun in my sight.

Hebrew Trans: like the moon it shall be established forever, and the witness in the sky is faithful." Selah.
Septuagint Trans: and as the moon established forever, and the witness in heaven is faithful." Selah.
Gallican Psalter Trans: and as the perfect moon forever, and a faithful witness in heaven. Selah.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: As the moon it shall be established forever, and a faithful witness in heaven always.

Hebrew Trans: But you have cast off and rejected; you have been angry with your anointed.
Septuagint Trans: But you have rejected and despised; you have cast off your anointed one.
Gallican Psalter Trans: But you have rejected and despised; you have cast off your anointed one.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: But you have rejected and cast off; you have been angry against your anointed one.

Hebrew Trans: You have renounced the covenant of your servant; you have profaned his crown to the ground.
Septuagint Trans: You have overthrown the covenant of your servant; you have profaned his sanctuary to the ground.
Gallican Psalter Trans: You have overthrown the covenant of your servant; you have profaned his sanctuary on the ground.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: You have diminished the covenant of your servant; you have profaned his crown upon the earth.

Hebrew Trans: You have broken down all his walls; you have laid his strongholds in ruins.
Septuagint Trans: You have torn down all his walls; you have made his strongholds a thing of fear.
Gallican Psalter Trans: You have destroyed all his hedges; you have made his strongholds a terror.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: You have broken down all his walls; you have made his fortifications a terror.

Hebrew Trans: All who pass along the way plunder him; he has become a reproach to his neighbors.
Septuagint Trans: All who pass along the way have plundered him; he has become a reproach to his neighbors.
Gallican Psalter Trans: All who pass by the way have plundered him; he has become a reproach to his neighbors.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: All who pass by the way have plundered him; he has become a reproach to his neighbors.

Hebrew Trans: You have exalted the right hand of his adversaries; you have made all his enemies rejoice.
Septuagint Trans: You have exalted the right hand of his enemies; you have made glad all his foes.
Gallican Psalter Trans: You have exalted the right hand of those who oppress him; you have made all his enemies rejoice.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: You have lifted up the right hand of his enemies; you have made all his foes rejoice.

Hebrew Trans: You also turn back the edge of his sword and have not sustained him in battle.
Septuagint Trans: You have turned back the help of his sword, and you did not support him in battle.
Gallican Psalter Trans: You have turned away the help of his sword, and you have not assisted him in battle.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: You have turned back the edge of his sword, and have not sustained him in battle.

Hebrew Trans: You have made his splendor to cease and cast his throne to the ground.
Septuagint Trans: You have brought his cleansing to an end; his throne you have dashed to the ground.
Gallican Psalter Trans: You have destroyed him from cleansing; his throne you have cast down to the ground.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: You have made his splendor to cease, and his throne you have cast down to the earth.

Hebrew Trans: You have shortened the days of his youth; you have covered him with shame. Selah.
Septuagint Trans: You have shortened the days of his time; you have poured shame upon him. Selah.
Gallican Psalter Trans: You have shortened the days of his time; you have poured shame upon him. Selah.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: You have shortened the days of his youth; you have covered him with everlasting shame.

Hebrew Trans: How long, O LORD? Will you hide yourself forever? How long will your wrath burn like fire?
Septuagint Trans: How long, O Lord? Will you turn away forever? Shall your wrath burn like fire?
Gallican Psalter Trans: How long, O Lord? Will you turn away to the end? Shall your wrath burn like fire?
Hebrew Psalter Trans: How long, O Lord? Will you hide yourself to the end? Shall your indignation burn like fire?

Hebrew Trans: Remember how short my time is; for what vanity you have created all the children of men!
Septuagint Trans: Remember what my substance is; for surely you have not created all the sons of men in vain.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Remember what my substance is; for have you not established all the sons of men in vain?
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Remember me from the depths; otherwise, why have you created the sons of men in vain?

Hebrew Trans: What man shall live and not see death? Shall he deliver his soul from the hand of Sheol? Selah.
Septuagint Trans: What man is there who shall live and not see death? Shall he deliver his soul from the hand of Hades? Selah.
Gallican Psalter Trans: What man is he that shall live and not see death? Shall he deliver his soul from the hand of the grave? Selah.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: What man is he that shall live and not see death, saving his soul from the hand of the grave forever?

Hebrew Trans: Where are your former mercies, O Lord, which you swore to David in your faithfulness?
Septuagint Trans: Where are your ancient mercies, O Lord, which you swore to David in your truth?
Gallican Psalter Trans: Where are your ancient mercies, O Lord, as you swore to David in your truth?
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Where are your ancient mercies, O Lord, which you swore to David in your truth?

Hebrew Trans: Remember, O Lord, the reproach of your servants; how I bear in my bosom the insults of all the many peoples,
Septuagint Trans: Remember, O Lord, the reproach of your servants, which I have borne in my bosom from many nations,
Gallican Psalter Trans: Be mindful, O Lord, of the reproach of your servants, which I have borne in my bosom from many nations,
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Remember, O Lord, the reproach of your servants; for I have borne in my bosom all the iniquities of the peoples,

Hebrew Trans: with which your enemies have reproached, O LORD, with which they have reproached the footsteps of your anointed.
Septuagint Trans: with which your enemies have reproached, O Lord, with which they have reproached the recompense of your anointed one.
Gallican Psalter Trans: with which your enemies have reproached, O Lord, with which they have reproached the exchange of your anointed one.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: with which your enemies have reproached, O Lord, with which they have reproached the footsteps of your anointed one.

Hebrew Trans: Blessed be the LORD forevermore. Amen and Amen.
Septuagint Trans: Blessed be the Lord forever. So be it, so be it.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Blessed be the Lord forever. So be it, so be it.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Blessed be the Lord forevermore. Amen and amen.

Hebrew Trans: A prayer of Moses, the man of God. Lord, You have been a dwelling place for us in every generation.
Septuagint Trans: A prayer of Moses, the man of God. Lord, you have been our refuge from generation to generation.
Gallican Psalter Trans: A prayer of Moses, the man of God. Lord, you have been our refuge from generation to generation.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: A prayer of Moses, the man of God. Lord, you have been made a dwelling place for us in generation and generation.

Hebrew Trans: Before the mountains were born, and You brought forth the earth and the world, from everlasting to everlasting, You are God.
Septuagint Trans: Before the mountains were brought into being and the earth and the world were formed, and from everlasting to everlasting, you are.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Before the mountains were made, and the earth and the world were formed, from age to age, you are God.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Before the mountains were born, and the earth and the world were brought forth, from age even unto age, you are.

Hebrew Trans: You turn man back to dust, and say, "Return, O children of men."
Septuagint Trans: Do not turn man back to humiliation, for you said, "Return, sons of men."
Gallican Psalter Trans: Do not turn man back to lowliness; and you said: "Return, O children of men."
Hebrew Psalter Trans: You turn man back to destruction, and you say: "Return, O sons of Adam."

Hebrew Trans: For a thousand years in Your eyes are as yesterday when it passes, and as a watch in the night.
Septuagint Trans: For a thousand years in your eyes are as yesterday which has passed, and as a watch in the night.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For a thousand years before your eyes are as yesterday which has passed, and as a watch in the night.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For a thousand years in your eyes are as yesterday which has passed away, and as a watch in the night.

Hebrew Trans: You sweep them away; they are as a sleep; in the morning they are like grass that sprouts anew.
Septuagint Trans: Their years of nothingness shall be; in the morning let it pass away like grass.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Those things which are held as nothing—their years shall be; in the morning, like grass, let it pass away.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: When you strike them, they shall be as a dream; in the morning, like grass passing away.

Hebrew Trans: In the morning it blossoms and flourishes; by evening it withers and dries up.
Septuagint Trans: In the morning let it bloom and pass away; in the evening let it fall off, let it be hardened and let it be withered.
Gallican Psalter Trans: In the morning let it flourish and pass away; in the evening let it fall, harden, and wither.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: In the morning it flourished and passed away; by evening it shall be crushed and shall wither.

Hebrew Trans: For we are consumed by Your anger, and by Your wrath we are terrified.
Septuagint Trans: For we have been consumed in your anger, and in your wrath we have been troubled.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For we have fainted in your wrath, and in your fury we have been troubled.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For we are consumed in your fury, and in your indignation we are troubled.

Hebrew Trans: You have set our iniquities before You, our secret sins in the light of Your countenance.
Septuagint Trans: You have set our iniquities before you; our lifetime is in the light of your countenance.
Gallican Psalter Trans: You have set our iniquities before your sight, our lifetime in the light of your countenance.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: You have set our iniquities before you, our secret sins in the light of your countenance.

Hebrew Trans: For all our days decline in Your fury; we finish our years like a sigh.
Septuagint Trans: For all our days have failed, and in your wrath we have been consumed; our years were pondering as a spider's web.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For all our days have failed in your wrath; our years have failed—they were pondered like a spider's web.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For all our days have passed away in your fury; we have spent our years as a word being spoken.

Hebrew Trans: The days of our years among them are seventy years, and if in strength, eighty years; and their pride is but toil and sorrow, for it is soon cut off and we fly away.
Septuagint Trans: The days of our years among them are seventy years, but if in strength, eighty years; and the greater part of them is toil and pain, for gentleness came upon us, and we shall be chastened.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The days of our years in themselves are seventy years; but if in strength, eighty years, and what is more of them is labor and sorrow, for gentleness comes upon us and we shall be corrected.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The days of our years in themselves are seventy years; but if in abundance, eighty years, and what is more than these is labor and sorrow, for we have passed through quickly and have flown away.

Hebrew Trans: Who knows the power of Your anger, and Your wrath according to the fear due to You?
Septuagint Trans: Who knows the power of your anger, and from your fear, your wrath?
Gallican Psalter Trans: Who knows the power of your wrath, and for fear of you, who knows your anger?
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Who knows the strength of your wrath, and according to your fear, your indignation?

Hebrew Trans: So teach us to number our days, that we may acquire a heart of wisdom.
Septuagint Trans: So make known your right hand, and those who are shackled in heart with wisdom.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Make known your right hand thus, and those fettered in heart in wisdom.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: So make known that our days may be numbered, and we shall come with a wise heart.

Hebrew Trans: Return, O LORD—how long?—and have compassion upon Your servants.
Septuagint Trans: Return, O Lord! How long? And be comforted concerning your servants.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Return, O Lord, how long? And be merciful toward your servants.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Return, O Lord, how long? And be entreatable toward your servants.

Hebrew Trans: Satisfy us in the morning with Your lovingkindness, that we may sing for joy and be glad all our days.
Septuagint Trans: We were filled in the morning with your mercy, and we rejoiced and were glad in all our days.
Gallican Psalter Trans: We were filled in the morning with your mercy, and we rejoiced and were delighted in all our days.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Fill us with your morning mercy, and we shall praise and rejoice in all our days.

Hebrew Trans: Make us glad according to the days You have afflicted us, the years we have seen evil.
Septuagint Trans: We were glad for the days in which you humbled us, for the years in which we saw evil.
Gallican Psalter Trans: We were made glad for the days in which you humbled us, for the years in which we saw evil.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Make us glad for the days in which you afflicted us, and for the years in which we saw evil.

Hebrew Trans: Let Your work appear unto Your servants, and Your majesty upon their children.
Septuagint Trans: And look upon your servants and upon your works, and guide their children.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And look upon your servants and upon your works, and direct their children.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Let your work appear before your servants, and your glory upon their children.

Hebrew Trans: And let the pleasantness of the Lord our God be upon us; and establish the work of our hands upon us—the work of our hands, establish it.
Septuagint Trans: And let the splendor of the Lord our God be upon us, and the works of our hands direct upon us.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And let the splendor of the Lord our God be upon us, and direct the works of our hands upon us, and direct the work of our hands.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And let the beauty of the Lord our God be upon us, and the work of our hands make steadfast upon us; the work of our hands, confirm it.

Hebrew Trans: He who dwells in the secret place of the Most High shall abide under the shadow of the Almighty.
Septuagint Trans: A praise-song of David. He who dwells in the help of the Most High shall abide in the shelter of the God of heaven.
Gallican Psalter Trans: A praise of the song of David. He who dwells in the help of the Most High shall abide in the protection of the God of heaven.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: He who dwells in the secret place of the Most High shall abide in the shadow of the Lord.

Hebrew Trans: I will say of the LORD, He is my refuge and my fortress; my God, in Him I will trust.
Septuagint Trans: He shall say to the Lord, "You are my protector and my refuge; my God, I will hope in him."
Gallican Psalter Trans: He shall say to the Lord: You are my protector and my refuge; my God, I will hope in him.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Saying to the Lord: my hope and my strength, my God, I will trust in him.

Hebrew Trans: For He shall deliver you from the snare of the fowler, from the pestilence of destruction.
Septuagint Trans: For he will deliver me from the snare of hunters and from a troublesome word.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For he himself will deliver me from the snare of the hunters and from the harsh word.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For he himself will deliver you from the snare of the hunters, from the death of treacheries.

Hebrew Trans: With His pinions He shall cover you, and under His wings you shall take refuge; His truth shall be your shield and buckler.
Septuagint Trans: He will overshadow you with his shoulders, and under his wings you shall hope; his truth shall encircle you as a shield.
Gallican Psalter Trans: He will overshadow you with his shoulders, and under his wings you shall hope; his truth shall surround you as a shield.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: He will overshadow you with his shoulders, and under his wings you shall hope; his truth shall be a shield and protection.

Hebrew Trans: You shall not be afraid of the terror by night, nor of the arrow that flies by day,
Septuagint Trans: You shall not be afraid of the terror of the night, nor of the arrow that flies by day,
Gallican Psalter Trans: You shall not be afraid of the terror of the night, nor of the arrow that flies by day,
Hebrew Psalter Trans: You shall not fear the terror of the night, nor the arrow that flies by day,

Hebrew Trans: Nor of the pestilence that walks in darkness, nor of the destruction that wastes at noonday.
Septuagint Trans: nor of the thing that walks in darkness, nor of the calamity and the demon of noonday.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Nor of the pestilence that walks in darkness, nor of the assault and the noonday demon.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Nor the plague that walks in darkness, nor the bite of the one raging at midday.

Hebrew Trans: A thousand shall fall at your side, and ten thousand at your right hand; but it shall not come near you.
Septuagint Trans: A thousand shall fall at your side, and ten thousand at your right hand, but it shall not come near you.
Gallican Psalter Trans: A thousand shall fall at your side, and ten thousand at your right hand; but it shall not come near to you.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: A thousand shall fall at your side, and ten thousand at your right hand; but it shall not come near to you.

Hebrew Trans: Only with your eyes shall you look, and you shall see the recompense of the wicked.
Septuagint Trans: Only with your eyes shall you observe, and you shall see the recompense of sinners.
Gallican Psalter Trans: But you shall consider with your eyes, and you shall see the recompense of sinners.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Nevertheless with your eyes you shall behold, and you shall see the retribution of the wicked.

Hebrew Trans: For you, O LORD, are my refuge; you have made the Most High your dwelling place.
Septuagint Trans: For you, O Lord, are my hope; you have made the Most High your refuge.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For you, O Lord, are my hope; you have made the Most High your refuge.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For you, O Lord, are my hope; you have made the Most High your dwelling place.

Hebrew Trans: No evil shall befall you, nor shall any plague come near your tent.
Septuagint Trans: No evil shall approach you, and no scourge shall come near your dwelling.
Gallican Psalter Trans: No evils shall approach you, and no scourge shall come near your dwelling.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: No evil shall come near to you, nor shall leprosy approach your tabernacle.

Hebrew Trans: For He shall give His angels charge over you, to keep you in all your ways.
Septuagint Trans: For he shall command his angels concerning you, to guard you in all your ways.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For he shall give his angels charge over you, that they may guard you in all your ways.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For he shall command his angels concerning you, that they may guard you in all your ways.

Hebrew Trans: Upon their hands they shall bear you up, lest you strike your foot against a stone.
Septuagint Trans: Upon their hands they shall bear you up, lest you strike your foot against a stone.
Gallican Psalter Trans: In their hands they shall bear you up, lest perhaps you dash your foot against a stone.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: In their hands they shall bear you up, lest perhaps your foot strike against a stone.

Hebrew Trans: Upon the lion and the cobra you shall tread; you shall trample the young lion and the serpent.
Septuagint Trans: Upon the asp and the basilisk you shall tread, and you shall trample the lion and the serpent.
Gallican Psalter Trans: You shall walk upon the asp and the basilisk, and you shall trample upon the lion and the dragon.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Upon the asp and the basilisk you shall tread; you shall trample the lion and the dragon.

Hebrew Trans: Because he has set his love upon Me, I will deliver him; I will set him on high, because he has known My name.
Septuagint Trans: "Because he has hoped in me, I will deliver him; I will shelter him, because he has known my name.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Because he has hoped in me, I will deliver him; I will protect him because he has known my name.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Because he has clung to me, I will deliver him; I will exalt him, because he has known my name.

Hebrew Trans: He shall call upon Me, and I will answer him; I am with him in trouble; I will rescue him and honor him.
Septuagint Trans: He shall call upon me, and I will hear him; I am with him in affliction, and I will rescue him and glorify him.
Gallican Psalter Trans: He shall cry to me and I will hear him; I am with him in tribulation; I will rescue him and glorify him.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: He shall call upon me, and I will hear him; I will be with him in tribulation; I will rescue him and glorify him.

Hebrew Trans: With long life I will satisfy him, and I will show him My salvation.
Septuagint Trans: With length of days I will fill him, and I will show him my salvation."
Gallican Psalter Trans: With length of days I will fill him, and I will show him my salvation.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: With length of days I will fill him, and I will show him my salvation.

Hebrew Trans: A psalm, a song for the Sabbath day.
Septuagint Trans: A psalm of song for the day of the Sabbath.
Gallican Psalter Trans: A psalm of a canticle, on the sabbath day.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: A psalm of a song for the Sabbath day.

Hebrew Trans: It is good to give thanks unto the LORD, and to sing praises unto Thy name, O Most High;
Septuagint Trans: It is good to give thanks to the Lord and to sing praises to your name, O Most High;
Gallican Psalter Trans: It is good to confess to the Lord, and to sing psalms to your name, O Most High.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: It is good to give thanks to the Lord, and to sing praises to Your name, O Most High.

Hebrew Trans: To declare Thy lovingkindness in the morning, and Thy faithfulness in the nights;
Septuagint Trans: to proclaim your mercy in the morning and your truth by night,
Gallican Psalter Trans: To proclaim your mercy in the morning, and your truth through the night.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: To declare Your mercy in the morning, and Your faithfulness in the night,

Hebrew Trans: Upon a ten-stringed instrument, and upon the lute, upon the meditation of the harp.
Septuagint Trans: on a ten-stringed psaltery, with a song on the harp.
Gallican Psalter Trans: On a ten-stringed psaltery, with a canticle upon the harp.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Upon a ten-stringed instrument, and upon the psaltery, with a song upon the harp.

Hebrew Trans: For Thou, O LORD, hast made me glad through Thy work; I will exult in the works of Thy hands.
Septuagint Trans: For you have gladdened me, O Lord, by your work, and in the works of your hands I shall rejoice.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For you have delighted me, O Lord, in your making, and in the works of your hands I shall exult.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For You have made me glad, O Lord, through Your work; in the works of Your hands I will praise.

Hebrew Trans: How great are Thy works, O LORD! Thy thoughts are very deep.
Septuagint Trans: How great are your works, O Lord! Your thoughts are exceedingly deep.
Gallican Psalter Trans: How magnificent are your works, O Lord! Exceedingly deep are your thoughts.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: How great are Your works, O Lord! Exceedingly deep are Your thoughts.

Hebrew Trans: A brutish man does not know, and a fool does not understand this:
Septuagint Trans: A foolish man will not know, and a senseless man will not understand these things.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The foolish man shall not know, and the stupid man shall not understand these things.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The foolish man will not know, and the stupid man will not understand this.

Hebrew Trans: When the wicked spring up like grass, and all the workers of iniquity flourish, it is that they may be destroyed forever and ever.
Septuagint Trans: When sinners spring up like grass and all the workers of lawlessness peer forth, it is that they may be utterly destroyed forever and ever.
Gallican Psalter Trans: When sinners shall have sprung up like grass, and all who work iniquity shall have appeared, it is that they may perish forever and ever.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The wicked have sprouted like grass, and all who work iniquity have flourished, that they may be destroyed forever and ever.

Hebrew Trans: But Thou, O LORD, art on high forevermore.
Septuagint Trans: But you are the Most High forever, O Lord.
Gallican Psalter Trans: But you, O Lord, are the Most High for eternity.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: But You are the Most High forever, O Lord.

Hebrew Trans: For behold, Thine enemies, O LORD, for behold, Thine enemies shall perish; all the workers of iniquity shall be scattered.
Septuagint Trans: For behold, your enemies shall perish, and all the workers of lawlessness shall be scattered.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For behold, your enemies, O Lord, for behold, your enemies shall perish, and all who work iniquity shall be scattered.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For behold, Your enemies, O Lord, behold Your enemies shall perish, and all who work iniquity shall be scattered.

Hebrew Trans: But Thou hast exalted my horn like that of the wild ox; I am anointed with fresh oil.
Septuagint Trans: And my horn shall be exalted like that of the wild ox, and my old age shall be anointed with rich oil.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And my horn shall be exalted like that of the unicorn, and my old age shall be in rich mercy.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And my horn shall be exalted like that of the wild ox, and my old age shall be anointed with rich oil.

Hebrew Trans: And mine eye has gazed upon mine adversaries; mine ears have heard of the evil-doers that rise up against me.
Septuagint Trans: And my eye has looked upon my enemies, and my ear shall hear concerning those who rise up against me as evildoers.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And my eye has looked down upon my enemies, and my ear shall hear of the malignant who rise up against me.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And my eye shall look down upon those who lie in wait for me; of those who rise up against me as evildoers, my ear shall hear.

Hebrew Trans: The righteous shall flourish like the palm tree; he shall grow like a cedar in Lebanon.
Septuagint Trans: The righteous shall flourish like a palm tree; like a cedar in Lebanon he shall be multiplied.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The just man shall flourish like the palm tree; like the cedar of Lebanon he shall be multiplied.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The righteous shall flourish like a palm tree; like a cedar in Lebanon he shall be multiplied.

Hebrew Trans: Planted in the house of the LORD, they shall flourish in the courts of our God.
Septuagint Trans: Planted in the house of the Lord, in the courts of our God they shall blossom.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Planted in the house of the Lord, in the courts of our God they shall flourish.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Transplanted in the house of the Lord, in the courts of our God, they shall flourish.

Hebrew Trans: They shall still bring forth fruit in old age; they shall be full of sap and freshness;
Septuagint Trans: They shall still be multiplied in rich old age, and they shall be flourishing,
Gallican Psalter Trans: They shall still be multiplied in a rich old age, and shall be well-enduring,
Hebrew Psalter Trans: They shall still bear fruit in old age; they shall be fat and flourishing,

Hebrew Trans: To declare that the LORD is upright, my Rock, and there is no unrighteousness in Him.
Septuagint Trans: to declare that the Lord my God is upright, and there is no unrighteousness in him.
Gallican Psalter Trans: That they may proclaim that the Lord our God is upright, and there is no iniquity in him.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Declaring that the Lord is upright, my strength, and there is no unrighteousness in Him.

Hebrew Trans: The LORD reigns; He is robed in majesty; the LORD is robed, He has girded Himself with strength; indeed, the world is established, it shall not be moved.
Septuagint Trans: For the day before the Sabbath, when the land was inhabited. A psalm of song to David. The Lord has reigned; he has clothed himself with majesty; the Lord has clothed himself with strength and girded himself; for he has established the world, which shall not be shaken.
Gallican Psalter Trans: A psalm of a canticle of David, on the day before the sabbath, when the earth was inhabited. The Lord has reigned, he is clothed with beauty: the Lord is clothed with strength and has girded himself. For he has established the world of the earth, which shall not be moved.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The Lord has reigned, He is clothed in glory; the Lord is clothed in strength and has girded Himself; moreover He has established the world, which shall not be moved.

Hebrew Trans: Your throne is established from of old; You are from everlasting.
Septuagint Trans: Your throne is prepared from of old; you are from everlasting.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Your throne is prepared from of old: you are from everlasting.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Your throne is firm from of old; You are from eternity.

Hebrew Trans: The rivers have lifted up, O LORD, the rivers have lifted up their voice; the rivers lift up their crashing waves.
Septuagint Trans: The rivers have lifted up, O Lord, the rivers have lifted up their voices.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The rivers have lifted up, O Lord: the rivers have lifted up their voice; the rivers shall lift up their waves.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The rivers have lifted up, O Lord, the rivers have lifted up their voices; the rivers have lifted up their torrents.

Hebrew Trans: More than the sounds of many waters, mightier than the breakers of the sea, mighty on high is the LORD.
Septuagint Trans: Above the sounds of many waters, wonderful are the surges of the sea; wonderful on high is the Lord.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Above the voices of many waters, wonderful are the surges of the sea: wonderful is the Lord on high.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: From the voices of many waters, great are the waves of the sea; great on high is the Lord.

Hebrew Trans: Your testimonies are very sure; holiness befits Your house, O LORD, for length of days.
Septuagint Trans: Your testimonies have been made very sure; holiness becomes your house, O Lord, unto length of days.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Your testimonies are made exceedingly credible: holiness befits your house, O Lord, unto length of days.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Your testimonies are made exceedingly faithful; holiness befits Your house, O Lord, unto length of days.

Hebrew Trans: O God of vengeance, O LORD, O God of vengeance, shine forth!
Septuagint Trans: A psalm of David, for the fourth day of the week. The God of vengeance, the Lord, the God of vengeance, has spoken boldly.
Gallican Psalter Trans: A psalm of David, for the fourth day of the week. The Lord is the God of vengeance; the God of vengeance has acted freely.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: O Lord, God of vengeance; God of vengeance, show yourself.

Hebrew Trans: Rise up, O Judge of the earth; render recompense to the proud.
Septuagint Trans: Be exalted, O you who judge the earth; render recompense to the proud.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Rise up, you who judge the earth; render retribution to the proud.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Rise up, you who judge the earth; render recompense to the proud.

Hebrew Trans: How long shall the wicked, O LORD, how long shall the wicked exult?
Septuagint Trans: How long shall sinners, O Lord, how long shall sinners boast?
Gallican Psalter Trans: How long shall sinners, O Lord, how long shall sinners glory?
Hebrew Psalter Trans: How long shall the wicked, O Lord, how long shall the wicked exult?

Hebrew Trans: They pour forth speech, they speak arrogantly; all the workers of iniquity boast of themselves.
Septuagint Trans: They will utter and speak unrighteousness; all who work lawlessness will speak.
Gallican Psalter Trans: They shall speak and utter iniquity; all who work injustice shall speak.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: They pour forth speaking ancient things; all who work iniquity shall babble.

Hebrew Trans: Your people, O LORD, they crush, and your heritage they afflict.
Septuagint Trans: They humbled your people, O Lord, and afflicted your inheritance.
Gallican Psalter Trans: They have humbled your people, O Lord, and have troubled your inheritance.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: They shall crush your people, O Lord, and afflict your heritage.

Hebrew Trans: They slay the widow and the stranger, and they murder the fatherless.
Septuagint Trans: They slew the widow and the stranger, and murdered the orphans.
Gallican Psalter Trans: They have slain the widow and the stranger, and they have murdered the fatherless.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: They shall slay the widow and the stranger, and murder the fatherless.

Hebrew Trans: And they say, "The LORD does not see, neither does the God of Jacob perceive."
Septuagint Trans: And they said, "The Lord shall not see, nor shall the God of Jacob understand."
Gallican Psalter Trans: And they said: The Lord shall not see, nor shall the God of Jacob understand.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And they said: "The Lord shall not see, nor shall the God of Jacob understand."

Hebrew Trans: Understand, you brutish among the people; and you fools, when will you be wise?
Septuagint Trans: Understand now, you senseless ones among the people; and you fools, when will you be wise?
Gallican Psalter Trans: Understand, you who are foolish among the people; and you who are senseless, at length be wise.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Understand, you foolish among the people; and you senseless ones, learn at last.

Hebrew Trans: He who planted the ear, shall He not hear? He who formed the eye, shall He not see?
Septuagint Trans: He who planted the ear, does he not hear? Or he who formed the eye, does he not perceive?
Gallican Psalter Trans: He who planted the ear, shall he not hear? Or he who formed the eye, shall he not observe?
Hebrew Psalter Trans: He who planted the ear, shall he not hear? Or he who formed the eye, shall he not see?

Hebrew Trans: He who chastises the nations, shall He not reprove? He who teaches man knowledge—
Septuagint Trans: He who chastens nations, shall he not rebuke? He who teaches man knowledge?
Gallican Psalter Trans: He who corrects the nations, shall he not rebuke? He who teaches man knowledge?
Hebrew Psalter Trans: He who chastises the nations, shall he not rebuke? He who teaches man knowledge?

Hebrew Trans: The LORD knows the thoughts of man, that they are vanity.
Septuagint Trans: The Lord knows the thoughts of men, that they are vain.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The Lord knows the thoughts of men, that they are vain.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The Lord knows the thoughts of men, that they are vain.

Hebrew Trans: Blessed is the man whom You chasten, O LORD, and whom You teach out of Your law,
Septuagint Trans: Blessed is the man whom you chasten, O Lord, and teach him out of your law,
Gallican Psalter Trans: Blessed is the man whom you shall instruct, O Lord, and whom you shall teach out of your law.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Blessed is the man whom you shall instruct, O Lord, and whom you shall teach from your law,

Hebrew Trans: To give him rest from the days of evil, until the pit is dug for the wicked.
Septuagint Trans: To give him rest from evil days, until a pit be dug for the sinner.
Gallican Psalter Trans: That you may give him rest from evil days, until a pit be dug for the sinner.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: That he may rest from the days of affliction, until a pit be dug for the wicked unto destruction.

Hebrew Trans: For the LORD will not cast off His people, nor will He forsake His inheritance.
Septuagint Trans: For the Lord will not cast off his people, and he will not forsake his inheritance,
Gallican Psalter Trans: For the Lord will not cast off his people, and he will not forsake his inheritance.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For the Lord will not forsake his people, and he will not abandon his heritage.

Hebrew Trans: For judgment shall return unto righteousness, and all the upright in heart shall follow it.
Septuagint Trans: Until righteousness return to judgment, and all the upright in heart shall hold to it. Selah.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Until justice be turned into judgment, and they who are near it are all those who are upright in heart. Selah.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For judgment shall return to justice, and all who are upright in heart shall follow it.

Hebrew Trans: Who will rise up for me against the evildoers? Who will stand up for me against the workers of iniquity?
Septuagint Trans: Who will rise up for me against the evildoers? Or who will stand with me against the workers of lawlessness?
Gallican Psalter Trans: Who shall rise up for me against the evildoers? Or who shall stand with me against the workers of iniquity?
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Who shall stand up for me against the wicked? Who shall stand up for me against the workers of iniquity?

Hebrew Trans: Unless the LORD had been my help, my soul had almost dwelt in silence.
Septuagint Trans: If the Lord had not helped me, my soul would have nearly dwelt in Hades.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Unless the Lord had helped me, my soul had almost dwelt in hell.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Unless the Lord had been my helper, my soul would have nearly dwelt in hell.

Hebrew Trans: If I said, "My foot has slipped," Your lovingkindness, O LORD, held me up.
Septuagint Trans: If I said, "My foot has slipped," your mercy, O Lord, helped me.
Gallican Psalter Trans: If I said: My foot has slipped, your mercy, O Lord, was helping me.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: If I said, "My foot has slipped," your mercy, O Lord, sustained me.

Hebrew Trans: In the multitude of my anxious thoughts within me, Your consolations delight my soul.
Septuagint Trans: O Lord, according to the multitude of my sorrows in my heart, your consolations have delighted my soul.
Gallican Psalter Trans: According to the multitude of my sorrows in my heart, your consolations have gladdened my soul.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: In the multitude of my thoughts that are within me, your consolations shall delight my soul.

Hebrew Trans: Shall the throne of destruction have fellowship with You, which frames mischief by statute?
Septuagint Trans: Shall the throne of lawlessness be allied with you, which fashions trouble by decree?
Gallican Psalter Trans: Shall the seat of iniquity have fellowship with you, who fashions trouble in commandment?
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Shall the throne of wickedness have part with you, which fashions trouble under the guise of law?

Hebrew Trans: They band together against the soul of the righteous, and condemn innocent blood.
Septuagint Trans: They will hunt the soul of the righteous, and condemn innocent blood.
Gallican Psalter Trans: They shall hunt for the soul of the just, and shall condemn innocent blood.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: They shall band together against the soul of the just, and shall condemn innocent blood.

Hebrew Trans: But the LORD has been my high tower, and my God the rock of my refuge.
Septuagint Trans: And the Lord became my refuge, and my God the helper of my hope.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And the Lord has become my refuge, and my God the helper of my hope.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: But the Lord shall be my refuge, and my God shall be as the rock of my hope.

Hebrew Trans: And He shall bring back upon them their own iniquity, and shall cut them off in their own wickedness; the LORD our God shall cut them off.
Septuagint Trans: And he will render to them their lawlessness, and according to their wickedness the Lord our God will destroy them.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And he shall render to them their iniquity, and in their malice he shall destroy them; the Lord our God shall destroy them.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And he shall bring back upon them their own iniquity, and in their malice he shall destroy them; the Lord our God shall destroy them.

Hebrew Trans: Come, let us sing joyfully to the LORD; let us shout aloud to the rock of our salvation.
Septuagint Trans: A psalm of praise, a song of David. Come, let us rejoice in the Lord; let us shout aloud to God our Savior.
Gallican Psalter Trans: A psalm of praise of David. Come, let us exult in the Lord; let us sing joyfully to God our savior.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Come, let us praise the Lord; let us sing joyfully to the rock of our Jesus.

Hebrew Trans: Let us come before his presence with thanksgiving; with psalms let us shout joyfully to him.
Septuagint Trans: Let us come before his face with thanksgiving, and with psalms let us shout aloud to him.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Let us come before his face with thanksgiving, and let us sing joyfully to him with psalms.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Let us come before his face with thanksgiving; let us sing joyfully to him in songs.

Hebrew Trans: For the LORD is a great God, and a great king above all gods.
Septuagint Trans: For the Lord is a great God, and a great King over all the gods.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For the Lord is a great God, and a great king above all gods.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For the Lord is a strong and great God, and a great king above all gods.

Hebrew Trans: In whose hand are the deep places of the earth, and the heights of the mountains are his.
Septuagint Trans: For in his hand are the ends of the earth, and the heights of the mountains are his.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For in his hand are the depths of the earth, and the heights of the mountains are his.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: In whose hand are the foundations of the earth, and the heights of the mountains are his.

Hebrew Trans: The sea is his, for he made it, and the dry land his hands formed.
Septuagint Trans: For the sea is his, and he made it, and his hands formed the dry land.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For the sea is his, and he made it, and his hands formed the dry land.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Whose is the sea, for he himself made it, and his hands formed the dry land.

Hebrew Trans: Come, let us worship and bow down; let us kneel before the LORD our maker.
Septuagint Trans: Come, let us worship and fall down before him, and let us weep before the Lord who made us.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Come, let us adore and fall down, and let us weep before the Lord who made us.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Come, let us worship and bow down; let us bend our knees before the face of the Lord our maker.

Hebrew Trans: For he is our God, and we are the people of his pasture and the flock of his hand. Today, if you will hear his voice,
Septuagint Trans: For he is our God, and we are the people of his pasture and the sheep of his hand. Today, if you hear his voice,
Gallican Psalter Trans: For he is the Lord our God, and we are the people of his pasture and the sheep of his hand. Today, if you hear his voice,
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For he is our God, and we are the people of his pasture and the flock of his hand. Today, if you will hear his voice,

Hebrew Trans: Do not harden your hearts as at Meribah, as on the day of Massah in the wilderness,
Septuagint Trans: do not harden your hearts as in the provocation, according to the day of temptation in the wilderness,
Gallican Psalter Trans: do not harden your hearts, as in the provocation, according to the day of temptation in the wilderness,
Hebrew Psalter Trans: do not harden your hearts, as in the provocation, as in the day of temptation in the wilderness,

Hebrew Trans: When your fathers tested me, they tried me, even though they had seen my work.
Septuagint Trans: where your fathers tempted me, tested me, and saw my works.
Gallican Psalter Trans: where your fathers tempted me, they tested me and saw my works.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: where your fathers tempted me, tested me, and saw my work.

Hebrew Trans: Forty years I loathed that generation and said, "They are a people that err in their heart, and they have not known my ways."
Septuagint Trans: Forty years I was grieved with that generation, and I said: They always go astray in their heart, and they have not known my ways.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For forty years I was offended with that generation, and I said: They always err in heart, and they have not known my ways.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For forty years I was displeased with that generation, and I said: They are a people erring in heart, and they have not known my ways.

Hebrew Trans: So I swore in my wrath, "They shall not enter into my rest."
Septuagint Trans: So I swore in my wrath: They shall not enter into my rest.
Gallican Psalter Trans: So I swore in my wrath: They shall not enter into my rest.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And I swore in my fury that they shall not enter into my rest.

Hebrew Trans: Sing to the LORD a new song; sing to the LORD, all the earth.
Septuagint Trans: When the house was being built after the captivity. A song of David. Sing to the Lord a new song; sing to the Lord, all the earth.
Gallican Psalter Trans: When the house was being built after the captivity, a song for David. Sing to the Lord a new song; sing to the Lord, all the earth.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Sing to the Lord a new song; sing to the Lord, all the earth.

Hebrew Trans: Sing to the LORD, bless his name; proclaim from day to day his salvation.
Septuagint Trans: Sing to the Lord, bless his name; proclaim from day to day his salvation.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Sing to the Lord, bless his name; announce from day to day his salvation.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Sing to the Lord, bless his name; proclaim from day to day his salvation.

Hebrew Trans: Declare his glory among the nations, his wondrous deeds among all the peoples.
Septuagint Trans: Declare among the nations his glory, among all the peoples his wonders.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Announce his glory among the nations, his wonders among all peoples.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Declare his glory among the nations, his wonders among all peoples.

Hebrew Trans: For great is the LORD, and greatly to be praised; he is to be feared above all gods.
Septuagint Trans: For great is the Lord, and greatly to be praised; he is fearful above all the gods.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For the Lord is great and greatly to be praised; he is terrible above all gods.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For the Lord is great and exceedingly worthy of praise; he is terrible above all gods.

Hebrew Trans: For all the gods of the peoples are idols, but the LORD made the heavens.
Septuagint Trans: For all the gods of the nations are demons, but the Lord made the heavens.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For all the gods of the nations are demons, but the Lord made the heavens.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For all the gods of the peoples are graven images, but the Lord made the heavens.

Hebrew Trans: Splendor and majesty are before him; strength and beauty are in his sanctuary.
Septuagint Trans: Confession and beauty are before him; holiness and majesty are in his sanctuary.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Confession and beauty are in his sight; holiness and magnificence are in his sanctuary.
