Hebrew Psalter Trans: Glory and beauty are before his face; strength and exultation are in his sanctuary.

Hebrew Trans: Ascribe to the LORD, O families of the peoples, ascribe to the LORD glory and strength.
Septuagint Trans: Bring to the Lord, O families of the nations, bring to the Lord glory and honor.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Bring to the Lord, O families of the nations, bring to the Lord glory and honor.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Bring to the Lord, O families of peoples, bring to the Lord glory and strength.

Hebrew Trans: Ascribe to the LORD the glory of his name; bring an offering and come into his courts.
Septuagint Trans: Bring to the Lord glory due his name; take up sacrifices and enter into his courts.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Bring to the Lord the glory of his name; take up sacrifices and enter into his courts.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Bring to the Lord the glory of his name; lift up offerings and enter into his courts.

Hebrew Trans: Worship the LORD in the splendor of holiness; tremble before him, all the earth.
Septuagint Trans: Worship the Lord in his holy court; let all the earth be shaken before his face.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Worship the Lord in his holy court; let all the earth be moved before his face.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Worship the Lord in the beauty of the sanctuary; let all the earth tremble before his face.

Hebrew Trans: Say among the nations, "The LORD reigns!" Indeed, the world is established, it shall not be moved; he shall judge the peoples with equity.
Septuagint Trans: Say among the nations: The Lord has reigned; for he has established the world, which shall not be shaken; he will judge peoples in uprightness.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Say among the nations that the Lord has reigned; for he has established the world, which shall not be moved; he will judge the peoples in equity.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Say among the nations: The Lord has reigned; for he has established the world immovable; he will judge the peoples in equity.

Hebrew Trans: Let the heavens rejoice, and let the earth be glad; let the sea roar, and all that fills it.
Septuagint Trans: Let the heavens rejoice and let the earth be glad; let the sea and its fullness be shaken.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Let the heavens rejoice and let the earth exult; let the sea and its fullness be moved.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Let the heavens rejoice and let the earth exult; let the sea roar, and the fullness thereof.

Hebrew Trans: Let the field exult, and all that is in it; then shall all the trees of the forest sing for joy
Septuagint Trans: The fields shall rejoice, and all things in them; then all the trees of the forest shall exult,
Gallican Psalter Trans: The fields shall rejoice, and all things that are in them; then all the trees of the forests shall exult
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Let the field rejoice, and all things that are in it; then shall all the trees of the forest give praise,

Hebrew Trans: before the LORD, for he comes, for he comes to judge the earth. He shall judge the world in righteousness, and the peoples in his faithfulness.
Septuagint Trans: before the face of the Lord, for he comes, for he comes to judge the earth. He shall judge the world in righteousness, and the peoples in his truth.
Gallican Psalter Trans: before the face of the Lord, for he comes, for he comes to judge the earth. He will judge the world in equity, and the peoples in his truth.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: before the face of the Lord, for he comes, for he comes to judge the earth. He will judge the world in justice, and the peoples in his faithfulness.

Hebrew Trans: The LORD reigns; let the earth rejoice; let the many islands be glad.
Septuagint Trans: By David, when his land is established. The Lord has become king; let the earth rejoice, let many islands be glad.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For David, when his land was restored. The Lord has reigned: let the earth exult, let the many islands be glad.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The Lord has reigned; let the earth exult, let the many islands be glad.

Hebrew Trans: Cloud and thick darkness surround him; righteousness and justice are the foundation of his throne.
Septuagint Trans: Cloud and darkness are round about him; righteousness and judgment are the foundation of his throne.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Clouds and darkness are round about him: justice and judgment are the correction of his throne.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Clouds and darkness are round about him; justice and judgment are the foundation of his throne.

Hebrew Trans: Fire goes before him and consumes his adversaries round about.
Septuagint Trans: Fire goes before him and will burn up his enemies round about.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Fire shall go before him and shall burn his enemies round about.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Fire shall go before his face and shall burn up his enemies round about.

Hebrew Trans: His lightnings illumined the world; the earth saw and trembled.
Septuagint Trans: His lightnings illuminated the world; the earth saw and trembled.
Gallican Psalter Trans: His lightnings illuminated the world: the earth saw and was moved.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: His lightnings appeared to the world; the earth saw and trembled.

Hebrew Trans: The mountains melted like wax before the LORD, before the Lord of all the earth.
Septuagint Trans: The mountains melted like wax before the face of the Lord, before the face of the Lord of all the earth.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The mountains melted like wax before the face of the Lord, before the face of the Lord of all the earth.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The mountains melted like wax before the face of the Lord, before the face of the ruler of all the earth.

Hebrew Trans: The heavens declared his righteousness, and all the peoples saw his glory.
Septuagint Trans: The heavens declared his righteousness, and all the peoples saw his glory.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The heavens declared his justice, and all the peoples saw his glory.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The heavens declared his justice, and all the peoples saw his glory.

Hebrew Trans: Let all who serve graven images be ashamed, who boast themselves in idols; bow down before him, all you gods.
Septuagint Trans: Let all who worship graven images be put to shame, those who boast in their idols; worship him, all you his angels.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Let all who adore graven things be confounded, who glory in their idols. Adore him, all you his angels.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Let all who serve graven images be confounded, who glory in idols; worship him, all you gods.

Hebrew Trans: Zion heard and was glad, and the daughters of Judah rejoiced, because of your judgments, O LORD.
Septuagint Trans: Zion heard and was glad, and the daughters of Judea rejoiced because of your judgments, O Lord.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Zion heard and was glad, and the daughters of Judea rejoiced, because of your judgments, O Lord.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Zion heard and was glad, and the daughters of Judah rejoiced, because of your judgments, O Lord.

Hebrew Trans: For you, O LORD, are most high above all the earth; you are exalted far above all gods.
Septuagint Trans: For you are the Lord, the Most High over all the earth; you are greatly exalted above all gods.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For you, Lord, are the Most High over all the earth: you are exceedingly exalted above all gods.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For you, Lord, are the Most High over all the earth; you are exalted exceedingly above all gods.

Hebrew Trans: You who love the LORD, hate evil; he preserves the souls of his faithful ones; from the hand of the wicked he delivers them.
Septuagint Trans: You who love the Lord, hate evil; the Lord guards the souls of his holy ones, he will deliver them from the hand of sinners.
Gallican Psalter Trans: You who love the Lord, hate evil: he guards the souls of his saints, he shall deliver them from the hand of the sinner.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: You who love the Lord, hate evil; he guards the souls of his saints, he shall deliver them from the hand of the wicked.

Hebrew Trans: Light is sown for the righteous, and gladness for the upright in heart.
Septuagint Trans: Light has dawned for the righteous, and gladness for the upright in heart.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Light is risen for the just, and gladness for the right of heart.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Light has risen for the just, and gladness for the upright of heart.

Hebrew Trans: Rejoice in the LORD, you righteous, and give thanks to his holy name.
Septuagint Trans: Rejoice, O righteous, in the Lord, and give thanks to the remembrance of his holiness.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Rejoice, you just, in the Lord, and give praise to the memory of his holiness.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Rejoice, you just, in the Lord, and give praise to the memory of his holiness.

Hebrew Trans: A psalm. Sing to the LORD a new song, for He has done wondrous things; His right hand and His holy arm have wrought salvation for Him.
Septuagint Trans: A psalm of David. Sing to the Lord a new song, for the Lord has done wondrous things; his right hand and his holy arm have saved him.
Gallican Psalter Trans: A psalm of David. Sing to the Lord a new song, for He has done marvelous things; His right hand and His holy arm have won salvation for Him.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Sing to the Lord a new song, for he has done marvelous things: his right hand and his holy arm have wrought salvation for him.

Hebrew Trans: The LORD has made known His salvation; before the eyes of the nations He has revealed His righteousness.
Septuagint Trans: The Lord has made known his salvation; he has revealed his righteousness before the nations.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The Lord has made known His salvation; in the sight of the nations He has revealed His justice.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The Lord has made known his salvation: he has revealed his justice in the sight of the nations.

Hebrew Trans: He has remembered His lovingkindness and His faithfulness to the house of Israel; all the ends of the earth have seen the salvation of our God.
Septuagint Trans: He has remembered his mercy to Jacob and his truth to the house of Israel; all the ends of the earth have seen the salvation of our God.
Gallican Psalter Trans: He has remembered His mercy and His truth toward the house of Israel. All the ends of the earth have seen the salvation of our God.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: He has remembered his mercy and his truth toward the house of Jacob: all the ends of the earth have seen the salvation of our God.

Hebrew Trans: Shout joyfully to the LORD, all the earth; break forth and sing for joy and make melody.
Septuagint Trans: Shout joyfully to God, all the earth; sing and rejoice and make music.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Shout joyfully to the Lord, all the earth; sing and rejoice and make music.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Shout joyfully to the Lord, all the earth: cry out and praise and sing.

Hebrew Trans: Make melody to the LORD with the lyre, with the lyre and the voice of song.
Septuagint Trans: Make music to the Lord with a harp, with a harp and the sound of a psalm.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Make music to the Lord with the harp, with the harp and the voice of a psalm.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Sing to the Lord with the harp, with the harp and the voice of song.

Hebrew Trans: With trumpets and the sound of the horn, shout joyfully before the King, the LORD.
Septuagint Trans: With hammered trumpets and the sound of a horn of brass, shout joyfully before the King, the Lord.
Gallican Psalter Trans: With trumpets of wrought metal and with the sound of the horn trumpet, shout joyfully before the Lord, the King.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: With trumpets and the sound of the horn, shout joyfully before the Lord, the King.

Hebrew Trans: Let the sea roar, and all that fills it; the world, and those who dwell in it.
Septuagint Trans: Let the sea be shaken, and its fullness; the world and those who dwell in it.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Let the sea and its fullness be moved, the world and those who dwell in it.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Let the sea roar, and its fullness; the world, and those who dwell therein.

Hebrew Trans: Let the rivers clap their hands; let the mountains sing for joy together.
Septuagint Trans: The rivers shall clap their hands together; the mountains shall rejoice,
Gallican Psalter Trans: The rivers shall clap their hands; together the mountains shall rejoice,
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The rivers shall clap their hands; together the mountains shall praise.

Hebrew Trans: Before the LORD, for He comes to judge the earth; He shall judge the world with righteousness, and the peoples with equity.
Septuagint Trans: for he has come to judge the earth; he shall judge the world in righteousness and the peoples in uprightness.
Gallican Psalter Trans: before the Lord, for He comes to judge the earth. He shall judge the world in justice and the peoples in equity.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Before the Lord, for he comes to judge the earth: he shall judge the world in justice, and the peoples in equity.

Hebrew Trans: The LORD reigns; let the peoples tremble. He sits upon the cherubim; let the earth quake.
Septuagint Trans: A psalm of David. The Lord has reigned; let the peoples be angry. He who sits upon the cherubim—let the earth be shaken.
Gallican Psalter Trans: A psalm of David. The Lord has reigned; let the peoples be angry. He who sits upon the cherubim; let the earth be moved.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The Lord has reigned; let the peoples be moved: He who sits upon the cherubim; let the earth be shaken.

Hebrew Trans: The LORD is great in Zion, and He is exalted above all the peoples.
Septuagint Trans: The Lord is great in Zion and is exalted above all the peoples.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The Lord in Zion is great, and he is exalted above all peoples.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The Lord is great in Zion, and He is high above all peoples.

Hebrew Trans: Let them praise Your name, great and awesome; holy is He.
Septuagint Trans: Let them give thanks to your name, the great one, for it is fearful and holy.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Let them confess your great name, for it is terrible and holy.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Let them confess Your name, great and terrible, for it is holy.

Hebrew Trans: And the strength of the king loves justice; You have established equity; justice and righteousness in Jacob You have wrought.
Septuagint Trans: And the honor of the king loves judgment. You have prepared uprightness; judgment and righteousness in Jacob you have wrought.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And the honor of the king loves judgment. You have prepared directions; judgment and justice in Jacob you have made.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The authority of the king loves judgment: You have established equity; judgment and justice in Jacob You have wrought.

Hebrew Trans: Exalt the LORD our God, and bow down at His footstool; holy is He.
Septuagint Trans: Exalt the Lord our God, and worship at the footstool of his feet, for he is holy.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Exalt the Lord our God, and adore the footstool of his feet, for it is holy.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Exalt the Lord our God, and worship at His footstool, for He is holy.

Hebrew Trans: Moses and Aaron among His priests, and Samuel among those who call upon His name; they called upon the LORD, and He answered them.
Septuagint Trans: Moses and Aaron among his priests, and Samuel among those who call upon his name—they called upon the Lord, and he heard them.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Moses and Aaron among his priests, and Samuel among those who call upon his name: they called upon the Lord, and he heard them.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Moses and Aaron among His priests, and Samuel among those who call upon His name: they called upon the Lord, and He heard them.

Hebrew Trans: In a pillar of cloud He spoke to them; they kept His testimonies and the statute He gave them.
Septuagint Trans: In a pillar of cloud he spoke to them; they kept his testimonies and the ordinances that he gave them.
Gallican Psalter Trans: In a pillar of cloud he spoke to them; they kept his testimonies and the precept which he gave to them.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: He spoke to them in a pillar of cloud; they kept His testimonies and the precept that He gave them.

Hebrew Trans: O LORD our God, You answered them; a forgiving God You were to them, yet an avenger of their misdeeds.
Septuagint Trans: O Lord our God, you heard them; O God, you were merciful to them, yet exacting vengeance upon all their practices.
Gallican Psalter Trans: O Lord our God, you heard them; O God, you were merciful to them, and taking vengeance upon all their inventions.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: O Lord our God, You heard them; O Lord, You were merciful to them, and an avenger upon their transgressions.

Hebrew Trans: Exalt the LORD our God, and bow down at His holy mountain; for holy is the LORD our God.
Septuagint Trans: Exalt the Lord our God, and worship at his holy mountain, for the Lord our God is holy.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Exalt the Lord our God, and adore upon his holy mountain, for the Lord our God is holy.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Exalt the Lord our God, and worship at His holy mountain, for the Lord our God is holy.

Hebrew Trans: A psalm of thanksgiving. Shout joyfully to the LORD, all the earth.
Septuagint Trans: A psalm for thanksgiving. Shout joyfully to the Lord, all the earth!
Gallican Psalter Trans: A psalm of praise. Rejoice to the Lord, all the earth.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: A canticle in thanksgiving. Sing joyfully to the Lord, all the earth.

Hebrew Trans: Serve the LORD with gladness; come before him with singing.
Septuagint Trans: Serve the Lord with gladness; come before him with rejoicing.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Serve the Lord in gladness; enter into his presence in exultation.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Serve the Lord in gladness; enter before him in praise.

Hebrew Trans: Know that the LORD, he is God; he made us, and we are his, his people and the sheep of his pasture.
Septuagint Trans: Know that the Lord, he is God; he made us, and not we ourselves; we are his people and the sheep of his pasture.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Know that the Lord himself is God; he made us, and not we ourselves; we are his people and the sheep of his pasture.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Know that the Lord himself is God; he made us, and we are his own, his people and the flock of his pasture.

Hebrew Trans: Enter his gates with thanksgiving, his courts with praise; give thanks to him, bless his name.
Septuagint Trans: Enter into his gates with thanksgiving, into his courts with hymns; give thanks to him, praise his name.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Enter his gates in praise, his courts in hymns; give thanks to him, praise his name.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Enter his gates in thanksgiving, his courts in praise; give thanks to him, bless his name.

Hebrew Trans: For the LORD is good; his steadfast love endures forever, and his faithfulness to all generations.
Septuagint Trans: For the Lord is good; his mercy endures forever, and his truth unto generation and generation.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For the Lord is sweet; his mercy endures forever, and his truth unto generation and generation.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For the Lord is good; his mercy endures forever, and his faithfulness unto generation and generation.

Hebrew Trans: Of David, a psalm. Of mercy and justice I will sing; to you, O LORD, I will make music.
Septuagint Trans: A Psalm of David. I will sing of mercy and judgment to you, O Lord.
Gallican Psalter Trans: A psalm of David. I will sing of mercy and judgment: to you, O Lord, I will sing praise.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Of David. A song. I will sing of mercy and judgment; to you, O Lord, I will make music.

Hebrew Trans: I will give heed to the way of integrity—when will you come to me? I will walk with integrity of heart within my house.
Septuagint Trans: I will play music and give heed to a blameless way. When will you come to me? I walked in the innocence of my heart in the midst of my house.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And I will understand in the way of the undefiled: when will you come to me? I walked in the innocence of my heart, in the midst of my house.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: I will be instructed in the way of perfection — when will you come to me? I will walk in the simplicity of my heart in the midst of my house.

Hebrew Trans: I will not set before my eyes any worthless thing; the doing of those who turn aside I hate—it shall not cling to me.
Septuagint Trans: I did not set before my eyes any unlawful thing. I hated those who commit transgressions.
Gallican Psalter Trans: I did not set before my eyes any unjust thing: I hated those who committed transgressions; it did not cling to me.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: I will not set before my eyes any worthless thing; I have hated the doing of transgressions, nor has it clung to me.

Hebrew Trans: A perverse heart shall depart from me; evil I will not know.
Septuagint Trans: A crooked heart did not cling to me. When the wicked turned away from me, I did not acknowledge him.
Gallican Psalter Trans: A perverse heart departed from me; I did not know the malicious one.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: A perverse heart shall depart from me; I will not know evil.

Hebrew Trans: Whoever slanders his neighbor in secret, him I will destroy; one haughty of eyes and broad of heart, him I will not endure.
Septuagint Trans: The one who secretly slandered his neighbor, this one I drove out. The one with a proud eye and an insatiable heart — with this one I did not eat.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The one who secretly slandered his neighbor, him I pursued; the one with a proud eye and an insatiable heart, with such a one I would not eat.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Him who speaks in secret against his neighbor, him I will destroy; the one proud in eyes and lofty in heart — with such a one I will not be able to abide.

Hebrew Trans: My eyes are upon the faithful of the land, that they may dwell with me; he who walks in the way of integrity, he shall minister to me.
Septuagint Trans: My eyes were upon the faithful of the land, that they might sit with me. He who walks in a blameless way, this one ministered to me.
Gallican Psalter Trans: My eyes were upon the faithful of the land, that they might sit with me; he who walked in the way of the undefiled, he ministered to me.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: My eyes are upon the faithful of the land, that they may dwell with me; he who walks in the way with simplicity, he shall minister to me.

Hebrew Trans: He who practices deceit shall not dwell within my house; he who speaks lies shall not be established before my eyes.
Septuagint Trans: He who practices arrogance did not dwell in the midst of my house. He who speaks unjust things did not prosper before my eyes.
Gallican Psalter Trans: He who practiced pride did not dwell in the midst of my house; he who spoke unjust things did not prosper in the sight of my eyes.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: He who practices deceit shall not dwell in the midst of my house; he who speaks lies shall not find favor in the sight of my eyes.

Hebrew Trans: Every morning I will destroy all the wicked of the land, to cut off from the city of the LORD all the workers of iniquity.
Septuagint Trans: In the mornings I slew all the sinners of the land, to destroy out of the city of the Lord all those who work lawlessness.
Gallican Psalter Trans: In the morning I put to death all the sinners of the land, that I might destroy out of the city of the Lord all who worked iniquity.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: In the morning I will destroy all the wicked of the land, that I may cut off from the city of the Lord all who work iniquity.

Hebrew Trans: A prayer of the afflicted, when he is faint and pours out his complaint before the LORD.
Septuagint Trans: A prayer of the poor man when he is in despair and pours out his supplication before the Lord.
Gallican Psalter Trans: A prayer of the poor man when he was anxious and poured out his supplication before the Lord.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: A prayer of the poor man, when he was anxious and poured out his speech before the Lord.

Hebrew Trans: O LORD, hear my prayer, and let my cry come unto you.
Septuagint Trans: Hear, O Lord, my prayer, and let my cry come to you.
Gallican Psalter Trans: O Lord, hear my prayer, and let my cry come to you.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: O Lord, hear my prayer, and let my cry come to you.

Hebrew Trans: Hide not your face from me in the day of my distress; incline your ear to me; in the day I call, answer me speedily.
Septuagint Trans: Do not turn your face away from me; in whatever day I am afflicted, incline your ear to me; in whatever day I call upon you, quickly hear me.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Do not turn your face from me; in whatever day I am troubled, incline your ear to me; in whatever day I call upon you, quickly hear me.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Do not hide your face from me in the day of my tribulation: incline your ear to me; in whatever day I shall call upon you, quickly hear me.

Hebrew Trans: For my days are consumed in smoke, and my bones are burned as a firebrand.
Septuagint Trans: For my days have vanished like smoke, and my bones have been scorched like a dry stick.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For my days have vanished like smoke, and my bones have dried up like a hearth.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For my days have been consumed like smoke, and my bones have dried up as if scorched.

Hebrew Trans: My heart is struck like grass and withered, for I have forgotten to eat my bread.
Septuagint Trans: My heart has been struck like grass and has withered, for I forgot to eat my bread.
Gallican Psalter Trans: My heart has been struck like grass and has withered, for I forgot to eat my bread.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: My heart has been struck like grass and has withered, because I forgot to eat my bread.

Hebrew Trans: By reason of the sound of my groaning, my bones cling to my flesh.
Septuagint Trans: From the sound of my groaning, my bone has clung to my flesh.
Gallican Psalter Trans: From the voice of my groaning, my bone has clung to my flesh.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: From the voice of my groaning, my bone has clung to my flesh.

Hebrew Trans: I am like a pelican of the wilderness; I have become like an owl of the ruins.
Septuagint Trans: I have become like a pelican of the wilderness; I have become like a night-raven in a ruined place.
Gallican Psalter Trans: I have become like a pelican of the wilderness; I have become like a night-owl in a dwelling place.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: I have become like a pelican of the desert; I have become like an owl of the solitudes.

Hebrew Trans: I watch, and am become like a solitary bird upon a rooftop.
Septuagint Trans: I have kept watch and have become like a sparrow alone upon a rooftop.
Gallican Psalter Trans: I have kept watch and have become like a solitary sparrow on a rooftop.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: I kept watch and was like a solitary bird upon the roof.

Hebrew Trans: All the day my enemies reproach me; those who mock me swear by me.
Septuagint Trans: All day long my enemies reproached me, and those who praised me swore oaths against me.
Gallican Psalter Trans: All day long my enemies reproached me, and those who praised me swore against me.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: All day long my enemies reproached me, and those who exulted over me swore against me.

Hebrew Trans: For I have eaten ashes like bread, and mingled my drink with weeping,
Septuagint Trans: For I ate ashes as though they were bread, and I mixed my drink with weeping.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For I ate ashes as though they were bread, and I mingled my drink with weeping.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For I ate ashes like bread, and mingled my drink with weeping.

Hebrew Trans: Because of your indignation and your wrath, for you have lifted me up and cast me down.
Septuagint Trans: Because of your anger and your wrath, for having lifted me up, you dashed me down.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Because of the face of your wrath and indignation, for having lifted me up, you dashed me down.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Because of the face of your indignation and wrath, for you lifted me up and dashed me down.

Hebrew Trans: My days are like a lengthened shadow, and I wither like grass.
Septuagint Trans: My days have declined like a shadow, and I have withered like grass.
Gallican Psalter Trans: My days have declined like a shadow, and I have withered like grass.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: My days have declined like a shadow, and I have withered like grass.

Hebrew Trans: But you, O LORD, shall endure forever, and your remembrance unto all generations.
Septuagint Trans: But you, O Lord, endure forever, and your remembrance is from generation to generation.
Gallican Psalter Trans: But you, O Lord, endure forever, and your memorial endures from generation to generation.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: But you, O Lord, endure forever, and your memorial endures from generation to generation.

Hebrew Trans: You will arise and have mercy upon Zion, for it is time to be gracious to her, for the appointed time has come.
Septuagint Trans: You shall arise and have compassion on Zion, for it is time to have mercy on her, for the appointed time has come.
Gallican Psalter Trans: You, arising, will have mercy on Zion, for it is time to have mercy on her, for the time has come.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: You shall arise and have mercy on Zion, for it is time to have mercy on her, for the time has come, for the appointed time has come.

Hebrew Trans: For your servants take pleasure in her stones and have pity on her dust.
Septuagint Trans: For your servants have taken pleasure in her stones, and they shall have pity on her dust.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For her stones have pleased your servants, and they will have pity on her earth.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For your servants have been pleased with her stones, and shall have pity on her dust.

Hebrew Trans: So the nations shall fear the name of the LORD, and all the kings of the earth your glory.
Septuagint Trans: And the nations shall fear the name of the Lord, and all the kings of the earth your glory.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And the nations shall fear the name of the Lord, and all the kings of the earth your glory.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And the nations shall fear the name of the Lord, and all the kings of the earth your glory.

Hebrew Trans: For the LORD has built up Zion; he has appeared in his glory.
Septuagint Trans: For the Lord shall build up Zion, and he shall appear in his glory.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For the Lord will build up Zion, and he shall be seen in his glory.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For the Lord has built up Zion, and he has appeared in his glory.

Hebrew Trans: He has turned to the prayer of the destitute and has not despised their prayer.
Septuagint Trans: He has looked upon the prayer of the humble and has not despised their supplication.
Gallican Psalter Trans: He has looked upon the prayer of the humble and has not despised their supplication.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: He has looked upon the prayer of the destitute, and he has not despised their prayer.

Hebrew Trans: Let this be written for a future generation, and a people yet to be created shall praise the LORD.
Septuagint Trans: Let this be written for another generation, and the people being created shall praise the Lord.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Let these things be written for another generation, and the people who shall be created will praise the Lord.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Let this be written for the last generation, and the people that shall be created shall praise the Lord.

Hebrew Trans: For he has looked down from the height of his sanctuary; the LORD from heaven to earth has gazed,
Septuagint Trans: For the Lord has looked down from his holy height; from heaven he gazed upon the earth,
Gallican Psalter Trans: For the Lord has looked down from his holy height; from heaven he gazed upon the earth,
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For the Lord has looked down from his high sanctuary; from heaven he has gazed upon the earth.

Hebrew Trans: To hear the groaning of the prisoner, to loose those who are appointed to death,
Septuagint Trans: to hear the groaning of those who are bound, to loose the sons of those who have been put to death,
Gallican Psalter Trans: that he might hear the groaning of those in chains, that he might release the children of the slain,
Hebrew Psalter Trans: That he might hear the groaning of the prisoner, that he might release the children of death.

Hebrew Trans: To declare the name of the LORD in Zion and his praise in Jerusalem,
Septuagint Trans: to proclaim in Zion the name of the Lord and his praise in Jerusalem,
Gallican Psalter Trans: that they might proclaim the name of the Lord in Zion and his praise in Jerusalem,
Hebrew Psalter Trans: That the name of the Lord may be declared in Zion, and his praise in Jerusalem.

Hebrew Trans: When the peoples are gathered together, and the kingdoms, to serve the LORD.
Septuagint Trans: when the peoples are gathered together, and the kingdoms, to serve the Lord.
Gallican Psalter Trans: when the peoples gather together as one, and kings, to serve the Lord.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: When the peoples shall be gathered together, and the kingdoms, to serve the Lord.

Hebrew Trans: He has afflicted my strength in the way; he has shortened my days.
Septuagint Trans: He answered him in the way of his strength: "Declare to me the fewness of my days.
Gallican Psalter Trans: He answered him in the way of his strength: Declare to me the fewness of my days.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: He afflicted my strength in the way; he shortened my days.

Hebrew Trans: I say, O my God, take me not away in the midst of my days; your years are throughout all generations.
Septuagint Trans: Do not take me up in the midst of my days. Your years are throughout the generation of generations.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Do not call me back in the midst of my days; your years are from generation to generation.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: I will say: O my God, do not take me away in the midst of my days; your years are from generation to generation.

Hebrew Trans: Of old you laid the foundation of the earth, and the heavens are the work of your hands.
Septuagint Trans: In the beginning you, O Lord, founded the earth, and the heavens are the works of your hands.
Gallican Psalter Trans: In the beginning, you, O Lord, founded the earth, and the heavens are the works of your hands.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: In the beginning you founded the earth, and the heavens are the work of your hands.

Hebrew Trans: They shall perish, but you shall endure; and all of them shall wear out like a garment; like a vestment you shall change them, and they shall pass away.
Septuagint Trans: They shall perish, but you remain; and they shall all grow old like a garment, and like a cloak you shall change them, and they shall be changed.
Gallican Psalter Trans: They shall perish, but you remain; and they shall all grow old like a garment, and like a covering you shall change them, and they shall be changed.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: They shall perish, but you shall endure; and they shall all wear out like a garment, and like a cloak you shall change them, and they shall be changed.

Hebrew Trans: But you are the same, and your years shall have no end.
Septuagint Trans: But you are the same, and your years shall not fail.
Gallican Psalter Trans: But you are the selfsame, and your years shall not fail.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: But you are the same, and your years shall not fail.

Hebrew Trans: The children of your servants shall dwell securely, and their offspring shall be established before you.
Septuagint Trans: The sons of your servants shall dwell securely, and their seed shall be established forever."
Gallican Psalter Trans: The children of your servants shall dwell securely, and their seed shall be established forever.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The children of your servants shall dwell securely, and their seed shall be established before your face.

Hebrew Trans: Of David. Bless the LORD, O my soul, and all that is within me, bless His holy name.
Septuagint Trans: Of David. Bless the Lord, O my soul, and all that is within me, bless his holy name.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Of David himself. Bless the Lord, O my soul, and all that is within me, bless his holy name.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Of David. Bless the Lord, O my soul, and all my inward parts, bless his holy name.

Hebrew Trans: Bless the LORD, O my soul, and forget not all His benefits:
Septuagint Trans: Bless the Lord, O my soul, and do not forget all his recompenses:
Gallican Psalter Trans: Bless the Lord, O my soul, and do not forget all his rewards.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Bless the Lord, O my soul, and do not forget all his rewards.

Hebrew Trans: Who forgives all your iniquities, who heals all your diseases,
Septuagint Trans: who pardons all your iniquities, who heals all your diseases,
Gallican Psalter Trans: Who forgives all your iniquities, who heals all your infirmities.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Who forgives all your iniquities, and heals all your infirmities.

Hebrew Trans: Who redeems your life from the pit, who crowns you with lovingkindness and mercies,
Septuagint Trans: who redeems your life from corruption, who crowns you with mercy and compassions,
Gallican Psalter Trans: Who redeems your life from destruction, who crowns you with mercy and compassion.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Who redeems your life from corruption, and crowns you with mercy and compassions.

Hebrew Trans: Who satisfies your mouth with good things, so that your youth is renewed like the eagle's.
Septuagint Trans: who fills your desire with good things; your youth shall be renewed like an eagle's.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Who fills your desire with good things; your youth shall be renewed like the eagle's.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Who fills your adornment with good things; your youth shall be renewed like the eagle's.

Hebrew Trans: The LORD executes righteousness and judgments for all who are oppressed.
Septuagint Trans: The Lord performs acts of mercy and judgment for all who are wronged.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The Lord performs mercies and judgment for all who suffer injury.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The Lord performs acts of justice and judgments for all who suffer oppression.

Hebrew Trans: He made known His ways to Moses, His deeds to the children of Israel.
Septuagint Trans: He made known his ways to Moses, his will to the sons of Israel.
Gallican Psalter Trans: He made his ways known to Moses, his purposes to the children of Israel.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: He made known his ways to Moses, his thoughts to the children of Israel.

Hebrew Trans: The LORD is merciful and gracious, slow to anger and abounding in lovingkindness.
Septuagint Trans: The Lord is compassionate and merciful, long-suffering and abundant in mercy.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The Lord is compassionate and merciful, long-suffering and greatly merciful.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The Lord is merciful and gracious, patient and of great compassion.

Hebrew Trans: He will not contend forever, nor will He keep His anger forever.
Septuagint Trans: He will not be angry to the end, nor will he bear a grudge forever.
Gallican Psalter Trans: He will not be angry forever, nor will he threaten for eternity.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: He will not judge forever, nor will he be angry for eternity.

Hebrew Trans: He has not dealt with us according to our sins, nor repaid us according to our iniquities.
Septuagint Trans: He has not dealt with us according to our sins, nor repaid us according to our iniquities.
Gallican Psalter Trans: He has not dealt with us according to our sins, nor repaid us according to our iniquities.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: He has not dealt with us according to our sins, nor repaid us according to our iniquities.

Hebrew Trans: For as the heavens are high above the earth, so great is His lovingkindness toward those who fear Him.
Septuagint Trans: For as the height of heaven is from the earth, so the Lord has strengthened his mercy upon those who fear him.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For as the height of heaven is from the earth, so has he strengthened his mercy upon those who fear him.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For as much as the heaven is higher than the earth, so great is his mercy strengthened over those who fear him.

Hebrew Trans: As far as the east is from the west, so far has He removed our transgressions from us.
Septuagint Trans: As far as the east is distant from the west, so far has he removed our iniquities from us.
Gallican Psalter Trans: As far as the east is distant from the west, so far has he removed our iniquities from us.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: As far as the east is from the west, so far has he removed our transgressions from us.

Hebrew Trans: As a father has compassion on his children, so the LORD has compassion on those who fear Him.
Septuagint Trans: As a father has compassion on his children, so the Lord has had compassion on those who fear him.
Gallican Psalter Trans: As a father has pity on his children, so the Lord has had pity on those who fear him.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: As a father has pity on his children, so the Lord has had pity on those who fear him.

Hebrew Trans: For He knows our frame; He remembers that we are dust.
Septuagint Trans: For he knows our formation; remember that we are dust.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For he himself has known our frame; he has remembered that we are dust.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For he himself knows our formation; he has remembered that we are dust.

Hebrew Trans: As for man, his days are as grass; as the flower of the field, so he flourishes.
Septuagint Trans: As for man, his days are as grass; as a flower of the field, so shall he blossom.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Man, his days are like grass; as a flower of the field, so shall he bloom.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: As for man, his days are as grass; as a flower of the field, so shall he flourish.

Hebrew Trans: For the wind passes over it, and it is gone, and its place knows it no more.
Septuagint Trans: For the wind has passed through it, and it shall not be; and it shall no longer know its place.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For the wind has passed through him, and he shall not endure, and he shall know his place no more.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For the wind passes over him, and he shall not endure, and his place shall know him no more.

Hebrew Trans: But the lovingkindness of the LORD is from everlasting to everlasting upon those who fear Him, and His righteousness to children's children,
Septuagint Trans: But the mercy of the Lord is from everlasting to everlasting upon those who fear him, and his righteousness upon children's children,
Gallican Psalter Trans: But the mercy of the Lord is from everlasting and unto everlasting upon those who fear him, and his justice upon the children's children,
Hebrew Psalter Trans: But the mercy of the Lord is from everlasting and to everlasting upon those who fear him, and his justice unto the children's children,

Hebrew Trans: To those who keep His covenant and to those who remember His precepts to do them.
Septuagint Trans: to those who keep his covenant and remember his commandments to do them.
Gallican Psalter Trans: upon those who keep his covenant and are mindful of his commandments to do them.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: to those who keep his covenant and remember his precepts to do them.

Hebrew Trans: The LORD has established His throne in the heavens, and His kingdom rules over all.
Septuagint Trans: The Lord has prepared his throne in heaven, and his kingdom rules over all.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The Lord has prepared his throne in heaven, and his kingdom shall rule over all.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The Lord has established his throne in heaven, and his kingdom rules over all.

Hebrew Trans: Bless the LORD, you His angels, mighty in strength, who do His word, hearkening to the voice of His word.
Septuagint Trans: Bless the Lord, all you his angels, mighty in strength, who perform his word, to hear the voice of his words.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Bless the Lord, all you his angels, mighty in strength, who do his word, hearkening to the voice of his words.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Bless the Lord, all you his angels, mighty in strength, who do his word, obedient to the voice of his speech.

Hebrew Trans: Bless the LORD, all you His hosts, you ministers of His who do His will.
Septuagint Trans: Bless the Lord, all you his powers, his ministers who do his will.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Bless the Lord, all you his hosts, his ministers who do his will.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Bless the Lord, all you his hosts, his ministers who do his pleasure.

Hebrew Trans: Bless the LORD, all His works, in all places of His dominion. Bless the LORD, O my soul.
Septuagint Trans: Bless the Lord, all his works, in every place of his dominion. Bless the Lord, O my soul.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Bless the Lord, all his works, in every place of his dominion; bless the Lord, O my soul.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Bless the Lord, all his works, in all places of his dominion. Bless the Lord, O my soul.

Hebrew Trans: Bless the LORD, O my soul. O LORD my God, You are very great; You have clothed Yourself with majesty and splendor.
Septuagint Trans: Of David. Bless the Lord, O my soul. O Lord my God, you have been greatly magnified; you have clothed yourself with praise and splendor.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Of David himself. Bless the Lord, O my soul. O Lord my God, you are exceedingly magnified: you have put on confession and beauty.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Bless the Lord, O my soul. O Lord my God, you are exceedingly magnified: you are clothed with glory and beauty.

Hebrew Trans: Who covers Yourself with light as with a garment, who stretches out the heavens like a curtain.
Septuagint Trans: Wrapping yourself in light as a garment, stretching out the heaven like a curtain.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Clothed with light as with a garment, stretching out the heaven like a curtain.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Wrapped in light as with a garment, stretching out the heavens like a curtain.

Hebrew Trans: Who lays the beams of His upper chambers in the waters, who makes the clouds His chariot, who walks upon the wings of the wind.
Septuagint Trans: He who roofs his upper chambers with waters, who makes clouds his chariot, who walks upon the wings of the winds.
Gallican Psalter Trans: You who cover its upper parts with waters, who make the cloud your ascent, who walk upon the wings of the winds.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: You who cover its upper chambers with waters, who make the clouds your chariot, who walk upon the wings of the wind.

Hebrew Trans: Who makes His angels spirits, His ministers a flaming fire.
Septuagint Trans: He who makes his angels spirits, and his ministers a flaming fire.
Gallican Psalter Trans: You who make your angels spirits, and your ministers a burning fire.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: You who make your angels spirits, and your ministers a burning fire.

Hebrew Trans: He established the earth upon its foundations, so that it shall not be moved forever and ever.
Septuagint Trans: He founded the earth upon its stability; it shall not be moved forever and ever.
Gallican Psalter Trans: You who founded the earth upon its stability: it shall not be moved forever and ever.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: You who founded the earth upon its base: it shall not be moved forever and ever.

Hebrew Trans: You covered it with the deep as with a garment; the waters stood above the mountains.
Septuagint Trans: The deep is like a garment, his covering; upon the mountains the waters shall stand.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The deep is its covering like a garment: the waters shall stand above the mountains.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: You covered it with the deep as with a garment: the waters shall stand above the mountains.

Hebrew Trans: At Your rebuke they fled; at the voice of Your thunder they hastened away.
Septuagint Trans: At your rebuke they shall flee; at the voice of your thunder they shall be afraid.
Gallican Psalter Trans: At your rebuke they shall flee; at the voice of your thunder they shall tremble.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: At your rebuke they shall flee; at the voice of your thunder they shall tremble.

Hebrew Trans: The mountains rose, the valleys sank down, to the place which You established for them.
Septuagint Trans: Mountains rise and valleys sink down to the place which you established for them.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The mountains ascend, and the plains descend, to the place which you have founded for them.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The mountains ascend and the plains descend to the place which you founded for them.

Hebrew Trans: You set a boundary that they may not pass over, that they may not return to cover the earth.
Septuagint Trans: You set a boundary which they shall not pass over, nor shall they return to cover the earth.
Gallican Psalter Trans: You have set a boundary which they shall not cross over, nor shall they return to cover the earth.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: You have set a boundary which they shall not cross over, nor shall they return to cover the earth.

Hebrew Trans: Who sends forth springs into the valleys; they flow between the mountains.
Septuagint Trans: He who sends forth springs in the ravines; between the mountains the waters shall flow.
Gallican Psalter Trans: You who send forth springs in the valleys: the waters shall pass between the midst of the mountains.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: You who send forth springs in the valleys, that they may flow between the midst of the mountains.

Hebrew Trans: They give drink to every beast of the field; the wild donkeys quench their thirst.
Septuagint Trans: They shall give drink to all the beasts of the field; the wild donkeys shall receive drink for their thirst.
Gallican Psalter Trans: All the beasts of the field shall drink; the wild donkeys shall await them in their thirst.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: That all the beasts of the field may drink, and the wild donkey may quench its thirst.

Hebrew Trans: Beside them the birds of the heavens dwell; from among the branches they give forth their voice.
Septuagint Trans: Upon them the birds of the heaven shall dwell; from among the rocks they shall give forth their voice.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Above them the birds of the heaven shall dwell; from the midst of the rocks they shall give voice.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Above them the birds of the sky shall dwell; from the midst of the branches they shall give voice.

Hebrew Trans: He waters the mountains from His upper chambers; the earth is satisfied with the fruit of Your works.
Septuagint Trans: He who waters the mountains from his upper chambers; from the fruit of your works the earth shall be satisfied.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Watering the mountains from his upper chambers: from the fruit of your works the earth shall be satisfied.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: You who water the mountains from your upper chambers: the earth shall be filled from the fruit of your works.

Hebrew Trans: He causes grass to grow for the cattle, and vegetation for the labor of man, to bring forth bread from the earth.
Septuagint Trans: He who causes grass to spring up for the cattle, and green plants for the service of mankind, to bring forth bread from the earth.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Bringing forth grass for cattle, and herbs for the service of men, that you may bring bread out of the earth.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Bringing forth grass for the cattle, and hay for the service of men, that he may bring bread from the earth.

Hebrew Trans: And wine that gladdens the heart of man, to make the face shine with oil, and bread that sustains the heart of man.
Septuagint Trans: And wine gladdens the heart of man, to make the face cheerful with oil, and bread strengthens the heart of man.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And wine gladdens the heart of man, that he may make his face cheerful with oil, and bread strengthens the heart of man.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And wine gladdens the heart of man, to make the face cheerful with oil, and bread strengthens the heart of man.

Hebrew Trans: The trees of the LORD are satisfied, the cedars of Lebanon which He planted,
Septuagint Trans: The trees of the plain shall be satisfied, the cedars of Lebanon which he planted.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The trees of the field shall be saturated, and the cedars of Lebanon which he planted.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The trees of the Lord shall be satisfied, the cedars of Lebanon which you planted.

Hebrew Trans: Where the birds make their nests; as for the stork, the cypresses are her house.
Septuagint Trans: There the sparrows shall make their nests; the house of the heron leads them.
Gallican Psalter Trans: There the sparrows shall build their nests; the house of the heron is their leader.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: There the birds shall make their nests; the stork's home is the fir trees.

Hebrew Trans: The high mountains are for the wild goats; the rocks are a refuge for the rock badgers.
Septuagint Trans: The high mountains are for the deer; the rock is a refuge for the hyraxes.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The high mountains are for the deer; the rock is a refuge for the hedgehogs.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The high mountains are for the deer; the rock is a refuge for the hedgehogs.

Hebrew Trans: He made the moon for appointed seasons; the sun knows its setting.
Septuagint Trans: He made the moon for seasons; the sun knew its setting.
Gallican Psalter Trans: He made the moon for seasons; the sun knows its setting.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: He made the moon for the seasons; the sun knows its setting.

Hebrew Trans: You appoint darkness, and it is night, in which all the beasts of the forest creep about.
Septuagint Trans: You appointed darkness, and it became night; in it all the beasts of the forest shall pass through.
Gallican Psalter Trans: You appointed darkness, and night was made: in it all the beasts of the forest shall pass through.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: You appointed darkness, and night was made: in it all the beasts of the forest shall move about.

Hebrew Trans: The young lions roar for their prey, and seek their food from God.
Septuagint Trans: Young lions roaring to seize prey and to seek their food from God.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The young lions roaring to seize prey and to seek their food from God.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The lions roaring after their prey, and seeking their food from God.

Hebrew Trans: The sun rises, they gather themselves together and lie down in their dens.
Septuagint Trans: The sun arose, and they were gathered together, and in their dens they shall lie down.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The sun has risen, and they are gathered together, and in their dens they shall be placed.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: When the sun rises, they shall withdraw, and in their dens they shall lie down.

Hebrew Trans: Man goes out to his work and to his labor until the evening.
Septuagint Trans: Man shall go forth to his work and to his labor until evening.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Man shall go forth to his work, and to his labor until the evening.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Man goes forth to his work, and to his labor until the evening.

Hebrew Trans: How manifold are Your works, O LORD! In wisdom You have made them all; the earth is full of Your possessions.
Septuagint Trans: How great are your works, O Lord! You have made all things in wisdom; the earth is filled with your creation.
Gallican Psalter Trans: How magnificent are your works, O Lord! You have made all things in wisdom; the earth is filled with your possession.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: How manifold are your works, O Lord! You have made all things in wisdom; the earth is filled with your possession.

Hebrew Trans: This is the sea, great and wide of extent; there are creeping things without number, living creatures small together with great.
Septuagint Trans: This is the sea, great and wide; there are creeping things without number, living things small and great.
Gallican Psalter Trans: This sea, great and spacious in extent: there are creeping things without number, animals small with great.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: This sea, great and wide in its expanse: there are innumerable creeping things, small creatures together with great.

Hebrew Trans: There the ships go; there is Leviathan, which You formed to play in it.
Septuagint Trans: There ships travel; this dragon which you formed to sport in it.
Gallican Psalter Trans: There ships shall pass through; that dragon which you formed to play with him.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: There the ships pass through; that Leviathan which you formed to play in it.

Hebrew Trans: They all look to You to give them their food in its season.
Septuagint Trans: All things look to you to give them their food in due season.
Gallican Psalter Trans: All things look to you, that you may give them food in due season.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: All things hope in you, that you may give them food in due season.

Hebrew Trans: You give to them, they gather; You open Your hand, they are satisfied with good.
Septuagint Trans: When you give to them, they shall gather; when you open your hand, all things shall be filled with goodness.
Gallican Psalter Trans: When you give to them, they shall gather; when you open your hand, all things shall be filled with goodness.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: When you give it to them, they shall gather it; when you open your hand, they shall be filled with good.

Hebrew Trans: You hide Your face, they are troubled; You take away their breath, they perish and return to their dust.
Septuagint Trans: But when you turn away your face, they shall be troubled; you shall take away their spirit, and they shall fail and return to their dust.
Gallican Psalter Trans: But when you turn away your face, they shall be troubled; you shall take away their spirit, and they shall fail, and shall return to their dust.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: You shall hide your face, and they shall be troubled; you shall take away their spirit, and they shall fail, and shall return to their dust.

Hebrew Trans: You send forth Your spirit, they are created; and You renew the face of the ground.
Septuagint Trans: You shall send forth your spirit, and they shall be created, and you shall renew the face of the earth.
Gallican Psalter Trans: You shall send forth your spirit, and they shall be created, and you shall renew the face of the earth.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: You shall send forth your spirit, and they shall be created; and you shall renew the face of the earth.

Hebrew Trans: May the glory of the LORD endure forever; may the LORD rejoice in His works.
Septuagint Trans: Let the glory of the Lord be forever; the Lord shall rejoice in his works.
Gallican Psalter Trans: May the glory of the Lord endure forever; the Lord shall rejoice in his works.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: May the glory of the Lord endure forever; the Lord shall rejoice in his works.

Hebrew Trans: He looks at the earth and it trembles; He touches the mountains and they smoke.
Septuagint Trans: He who looks upon the earth and makes it tremble; he who touches the mountains and they smoke.
Gallican Psalter Trans: He who looks upon the earth and makes it tremble; who touches the mountains and they smoke.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: He who looks upon the earth and it trembles; he touches the mountains and they shall smoke.

Hebrew Trans: I will sing to the LORD as long as I live; I will sing praises to my God while I have my being.
Septuagint Trans: I will sing to the Lord in my life; I will sing praise to my God while I exist.
Gallican Psalter Trans: I will sing to the Lord in my life; I will sing praise to my God as long as I am.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: I will sing to the Lord in my life; I will sing praise to my God as long as I am.

Hebrew Trans: May my meditation be pleasing to Him; I will rejoice in the LORD.
Septuagint Trans: May my meditation be sweet to him; and I shall rejoice in the Lord.
Gallican Psalter Trans: May my speech be pleasing to him; I indeed shall delight in the Lord.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: May my speech be pleasing to him; but I shall rejoice in the Lord.

Hebrew Trans: Let sinners be consumed from the earth, and let the wicked be no more. Bless the LORD, O my soul. Praise the LORD!
Septuagint Trans: Let sinners fail from the earth, and the lawless, so that they exist no more. Bless the Lord, O my soul.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Let sinners perish from the earth, and the wicked, so that they be no more. Bless the Lord, O my soul.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Let sinners fail from the earth, and let the wicked be no more. Bless the Lord, O my soul.

Hebrew Trans: Give thanks to the LORD, call upon His name; make known His deeds among the peoples.
Septuagint Trans: Hallelujah! Give thanks to the Lord and call upon his name; declare his works among the nations.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Alleluia. Confess to the Lord and call upon His name: make known His works among the nations.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Alleluia. Give thanks to the Lord, call upon his name; make known his thoughts among the peoples.

Hebrew Trans: Sing to Him, make music to Him; speak of all His wondrous works.
Septuagint Trans: Sing to him and make music to him; recount all his wonders.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Sing to Him and make melody to Him: tell of all His wondrous works.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Sing to him and make music to him; speak of all his wondrous works.

Hebrew Trans: Glory in His holy name; let the heart of those who seek the LORD rejoice.
Septuagint Trans: Boast in his holy name; let the heart of those who seek the Lord rejoice.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Glory in His holy name: let the heart of those who seek the Lord rejoice.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Exult in his holy name; let the heart of those who seek the Lord rejoice.

Hebrew Trans: Seek the LORD and His strength; seek His face continually.
Septuagint Trans: Seek the Lord and be strengthened; seek his face continually.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Seek the Lord and be strengthened: seek His face always.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Seek the Lord and his strength; seek his face continually.

Hebrew Trans: Remember His wondrous works that He has done, His signs and the judgments of His mouth.
Septuagint Trans: Remember his wonders which he did, his portents and the judgments of his mouth.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Remember His marvelous works which He has done, His wonders and the judgments of His mouth.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Remember his wondrous works which he has done, his signs and the judgments of his mouth.

Hebrew Trans: O seed of Abraham His servant, children of Jacob, His chosen ones.
Septuagint Trans: O seed of Abraham, his servants, sons of Jacob, his chosen ones.
Gallican Psalter Trans: O seed of Abraham His servant, O sons of Jacob His chosen ones.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: O seed of Abraham his servant, sons of Jacob his chosen ones.

Hebrew Trans: He is the LORD our God; His judgments are in all the earth.
Septuagint Trans: He is the Lord our God; his judgments are in all the earth.
Gallican Psalter Trans: He is the Lord our God: His judgments are in all the earth.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: He is the Lord our God; his judgments are in all the earth.

Hebrew Trans: He remembers His covenant forever, the word He commanded to a thousand generations,
Septuagint Trans: He remembered his covenant forever, the word which he commanded to a thousand generations,
Gallican Psalter Trans: He has been mindful forever of His covenant, the word which He commanded to a thousand generations,
Hebrew Psalter Trans: He has remembered his covenant forever, the word which he commanded for a thousand generations,

Hebrew Trans: Which He made with Abraham, and His oath to Isaac,
Septuagint Trans: which he established with Abraham, and his oath to Isaac.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Which He disposed to Abraham, and of His oath to Isaac.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Which he made with Abraham, and his oath with Isaac,

Hebrew Trans: And He established it to Jacob as a statute, to Israel as an everlasting covenant,
Septuagint Trans: And he set it up for Jacob as a decree, and for Israel as an everlasting covenant,
Gallican Psalter Trans: And He established it to Jacob as a precept, and to Israel as an everlasting covenant,
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And he established it with Jacob as a law, with Israel as an everlasting covenant,

Hebrew Trans: Saying, "To you I will give the land of Canaan, the portion of your inheritance."
Septuagint Trans: saying, "To you I will give the land of Canaan, the portion of your inheritance."
Gallican Psalter Trans: Saying: To you I will give the land of Canaan, the portion of your inheritance.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Saying, "To you I will give the land of Canaan, the measuring line of your inheritance,"

Hebrew Trans: When they were few in number, very few, and strangers in it,
Septuagint Trans: When they were few in number, very few, and sojourners in it,
Gallican Psalter Trans: When they were few in number, very few, and sojourners in it.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: When they were few in number, very few, and strangers in it,

Hebrew Trans: And they wandered from nation to nation, from one kingdom to another people.
Septuagint Trans: and they passed from nation to nation, from one kingdom to another people.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And they passed from nation to nation, and from one kingdom to another people.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And they passed from nation to nation, from one kingdom to another people.

Hebrew Trans: He allowed no man to oppress them, and He rebuked kings on their account:
Septuagint Trans: He did not allow any man to wrong them, and he rebuked kings on their behalf:
Gallican Psalter Trans: He suffered no man to hurt them, and He reproved kings for their sake.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: He did not allow any man to harm them, and he rebuked kings on their behalf:

Hebrew Trans: "Do not touch My anointed ones, and do My prophets no harm."
Septuagint Trans: "Do not touch my anointed ones, and do no evil to my prophets."
Gallican Psalter Trans: Do not touch My anointed ones, and do not deal maliciously against My prophets.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: "Do not touch my anointed ones, and do not afflict my prophets."

Hebrew Trans: And He called a famine upon the land; He broke every staff of bread.
Septuagint Trans: And he called a famine upon the land; he broke every support of bread.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And He called a famine upon the land: He broke every staff of bread.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And he called a famine upon the land; he broke every staff of bread.

Hebrew Trans: He sent a man before them — Joseph was sold as a slave.
Septuagint Trans: He sent a man before them; Joseph was sold as a slave.
Gallican Psalter Trans: He sent a man before them: Joseph was sold as a slave.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: He sent before their face a man; Joseph was sold as a servant.

Hebrew Trans: They afflicted his feet with fetters; his soul came into iron,
Septuagint Trans: They humbled his feet with shackles; iron pierced his soul,
Gallican Psalter Trans: They humbled his feet in fetters: the iron pierced his soul,
Hebrew Psalter Trans: They afflicted his feet with shackles; his soul came into iron,

Hebrew Trans: Until the time that his word came to pass; the word of the LORD tested him.
Septuagint Trans: until his word came to pass; the oracle of the Lord tested him by fire.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Until his word came to pass: the utterance of the Lord inflamed him.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Until the time when his word came to pass; the utterance of the Lord proved him.

Hebrew Trans: The king sent and released him; the ruler of peoples set him free.
Septuagint Trans: The king sent and released him; the ruler of peoples set him free.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The king sent and released him, the ruler of peoples, and set him free.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The king sent and released him, the ruler of peoples, and set him free.

Hebrew Trans: He made him lord of his house, and ruler over all his possessions,
Septuagint Trans: He established him as lord of his house and ruler of all his possession,
Gallican Psalter Trans: He made him lord of his house, and ruler of all his possessions,
Hebrew Psalter Trans: He made him lord of his house and ruler over all his possessions,

Hebrew Trans: To bind his princes at his will, and to teach his elders wisdom.
Septuagint Trans: to instruct his rulers as he saw fit, and to make his elders wise.
Gallican Psalter Trans: That he might instruct his princes as he saw fit, and teach his elders wisdom.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: That he might instruct his princes according to his will and teach his elders wisdom.

Hebrew Trans: And Israel came to Egypt, and Jacob sojourned in the land of Ham.
Septuagint Trans: And Israel entered into Egypt, and Jacob sojourned in the land of Ham.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And Israel entered into Egypt, and Jacob sojourned in the land of Ham.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And Israel entered Egypt, and Jacob was a sojourner in the land of Ham.

Hebrew Trans: And He made His people very fruitful, and made them stronger than their adversaries.
Septuagint Trans: And he increased his people greatly and made them stronger than their enemies.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And He increased His people exceedingly, and made them stronger than their enemies.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And he made his people to increase greatly and strengthened them over their enemies.

Hebrew Trans: He turned their heart to hate His people, to deal craftily with His servants.
Septuagint Trans: He turned their heart to hate his people, to deal deceitfully with his servants.
Gallican Psalter Trans: He turned their heart to hate His people, to deal deceitfully with His servants.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: He turned their heart to hate his people, to deal craftily against his servants.

Hebrew Trans: He sent Moses His servant, and Aaron whom He had chosen.
Septuagint Trans: He sent forth Moses his servant, and Aaron whom he had chosen.
Gallican Psalter Trans: He sent Moses His servant, and Aaron whom He had chosen.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: He sent Moses his servant, and Aaron whom he had chosen for himself.

Hebrew Trans: They set among them His signs and wonders in the land of Ham.
Septuagint Trans: He set among them the words of his signs and wonders in the land of Ham.
Gallican Psalter Trans: He set among them the words of His signs and of His wonders in the land of Ham.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: He set among them the words of his signs and of his portents in the land of Ham.

Hebrew Trans: He sent darkness and made it dark, and they did not rebel against His word.
Septuagint Trans: He sent darkness and made it dark, and they rebelled against his words.
Gallican Psalter Trans: He sent darkness and made it dark, and they did not rebel against His words.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: He sent darkness and made it dark, and they were not disbelieving of his words.

Hebrew Trans: He turned their waters into blood and killed their fish.
Septuagint Trans: He turned their waters into blood and killed their fish.
Gallican Psalter Trans: He turned their waters into blood and killed their fish.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: He turned their waters into blood and killed their fish.

Hebrew Trans: Their land swarmed with frogs, even in the chambers of their kings.
Septuagint Trans: Their land swarmed with frogs, even in the chambers of their kings.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Their land brought forth frogs, even in the chambers of their kings.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Their land swarmed with frogs, even in the chambers of their kings.

Hebrew Trans: He spoke, and there came swarms of flies, and gnats in all their territory.
Septuagint Trans: He spoke, and the dog-fly came, and gnats in all their borders.
Gallican Psalter Trans: He spoke, and there came swarms of flies, and gnats in all their borders.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: He spoke, and there came swarms of flies of every kind, and gnats in all their territories.

Hebrew Trans: He gave them hail for rain, and flaming fire in their land.
Septuagint Trans: He made their rains into hail, with flaming fire in their land.
Gallican Psalter Trans: He gave them hail for rain, and burning fire in their land.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: He gave them hail for rain, and flaming fire in their land.

Hebrew Trans: And He struck their vines and their fig trees, and broke the trees of their territory.
Septuagint Trans: And he struck their vines and their fig trees, and shattered every tree of their territory.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And He struck their vines and their fig trees, and shattered the trees of their borders.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And he struck their vines and their fig trees, and broke the trees of their territories.

Hebrew Trans: He spoke, and locusts came, and young locusts without number,
Septuagint Trans: He spoke, and the locust came, and the young locust without number,
Gallican Psalter Trans: He spoke, and the locust came, and the caterpillar without number,
Hebrew Psalter Trans: He spoke, and the locust came, and the young locust without number,

Hebrew Trans: And they ate every plant in their land, and ate the fruit of their ground.
Septuagint Trans: and they devoured all the grass in their land and devoured the fruit of their ground.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And devoured all the grass in their land, and devoured all the fruit of their land.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And it ate all the grass of their land and devoured the fruit of their land.

Hebrew Trans: And He struck every firstborn in their land, the firstfruits of all their strength.
Septuagint Trans: And he struck every firstborn in their land, the firstfruits of all their labor.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And He struck every firstborn in their land, the first fruits of all their labor.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And he struck every firstborn in their land, the firstfruits of all their offspring.

Hebrew Trans: And He brought them out with silver and gold, and there was none among His tribes who stumbled.
Septuagint Trans: And he brought them out with silver and gold, and there was none among their tribes who was feeble.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And He brought them out with silver and gold, and there was not among their tribes one that was feeble.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And he brought them out with silver and gold, and there was not a feeble one among their tribes.

Hebrew Trans: Egypt was glad when they departed, for the fear of them had fallen upon them.
Septuagint Trans: Egypt rejoiced at their departure, for the fear of them had fallen upon them.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Egypt was glad at their departure, for the fear of them had fallen upon them.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Egypt was glad when they departed, for the terror of them had fallen upon them.

Hebrew Trans: He spread a cloud for a covering, and fire to give light by night.
Septuagint Trans: He spread out a cloud for a covering for them, and fire to give them light by night.
Gallican Psalter Trans: He spread a cloud for their protection, and fire to give them light through the night.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: He spread a cloud as a tent, and fire to give light by night.

Hebrew Trans: They asked, and He brought quail, and with bread of heaven He satisfied them.
Septuagint Trans: They asked, and the quail came, and he filled them with the bread of heaven.
Gallican Psalter Trans: They asked, and the quail came, and He satisfied them with the bread of heaven.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: They asked, and he brought quail, and with bread from heaven he satisfied them.

Hebrew Trans: He opened the rock, and waters gushed out; they flowed in the dry places like a river.
Septuagint Trans: He split open a rock and waters flowed; rivers ran in waterless places.
Gallican Psalter Trans: He split the rock and waters flowed: rivers ran in the dry places.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: He opened the rock and waters flowed; they ran in the dry places as rivers.

Hebrew Trans: For He remembered His holy word, and Abraham His servant.
Septuagint Trans: For he remembered his holy word, the one to Abraham his servant.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For He was mindful of His holy word, which He had spoken to Abraham His servant.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For he remembered his holy word with Abraham his servant.

Hebrew Trans: And He brought out His people with joy, His chosen ones with singing.
Septuagint Trans: And he brought out his people with rejoicing, and his chosen ones with gladness.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And He brought forth His people with rejoicing, and His chosen ones with gladness.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And he brought out his people with joy, his chosen ones with praise.

Hebrew Trans: And He gave them the lands of the nations, and they inherited the labor of the peoples,
Septuagint Trans: And he gave them the lands of nations, and they inherited the labors of peoples,
Gallican Psalter Trans: And He gave them the regions of the nations, and they possessed the labors of the peoples,
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And he gave them the lands of the nations, and they possessed the labor of the tribes,

Hebrew Trans: So that they might keep His statutes and observe His laws. Praise the LORD!
Septuagint Trans: so that they might keep his statutes and seek out his law.
Gallican Psalter Trans: That they might keep His statutes and seek His law.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: That they might keep his ceremonies and observe his laws. Alleluia.

Hebrew Trans: Hallelujah! Give thanks to the LORD, for he is good, for his steadfast love endures forever.
Septuagint Trans: Hallelujah! Give thanks to the Lord, for he is good, for his mercy endures forever.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Alleluia. Give thanks to the Lord, for he is good, for his mercy endures forever.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Alleluia. Give thanks to the Lord, for he is good, for his mercy endures forever.

Hebrew Trans: Who can utter the mighty acts of the LORD, or make heard all his praise?
Septuagint Trans: Who shall speak the mighty acts of the Lord? Who shall make all his praises heard?
Gallican Psalter Trans: Who shall speak the mighty acts of the Lord? Who shall make all his praises heard?
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Who shall speak the mighty deeds of the Lord? Who shall make all his praises heard?

Hebrew Trans: Blessed are those who keep justice, who do righteousness at all times.
Septuagint Trans: Blessed are those who keep justice and do righteousness at all times.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Blessed are they who keep judgment and do justice at all times.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Blessed are they who keep judgment and do justice at all times.

Hebrew Trans: Remember me, O LORD, with the favor of your people; visit me with your salvation,
Septuagint Trans: Remember us, O Lord, in the good pleasure of your people; visit us with your salvation,
Gallican Psalter Trans: Remember us, O Lord, in the good pleasure of your people; visit us with your salvation.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Remember me, O Lord, in the favor shown to your people; visit me with your salvation.

Hebrew Trans: That I may see the good of your chosen ones, that I may rejoice in the gladness of your nation, that I may glory with your inheritance.
Septuagint Trans: that we may see the goodness of your chosen ones, that we may rejoice in the gladness of your nation, that we may glory with your inheritance.
Gallican Psalter Trans: That we may see the goodness of your chosen ones, that we may rejoice in the gladness of your nation, and that you may be praised with your inheritance.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: That I may see the good things of your chosen ones, and rejoice in the joy of your nation, and exult with your inheritance.

Hebrew Trans: We have sinned with our fathers, we have committed iniquity, we have done wickedly.
Septuagint Trans: We have sinned with our fathers; we have committed lawlessness; we have done wrong.
Gallican Psalter Trans: We have sinned with our fathers; we have acted unjustly; we have committed iniquity.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: We have sinned with our fathers; we have acted unjustly; we have done wickedly.
