Hebrew Trans: Our fathers in Egypt did not consider your wonders; they did not remember the multitude of your mercies, but rebelled at the sea, at the Sea of Reeds.
Septuagint Trans: Our fathers in Egypt did not understand your wonders; they did not remember the abundance of your mercy, and they rebelled when going up at the Red Sea.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Our fathers in Egypt did not understand your wonders; they were not mindful of the multitude of your mercy, and they provoked you, going up into the sea, the Red Sea.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Our fathers in Egypt did not understand your wonders; they did not remember the multitude of your mercy, and they provoked to anger at the sea, at the Red Sea.

Hebrew Trans: Yet he saved them for his name's sake, to make known his mighty power.
Septuagint Trans: And he saved them for the sake of his name, to make known his mighty power.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And he saved them for the sake of his name, that he might make known his power.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: But he saved them for the sake of his name, that he might make known his power.

Hebrew Trans: And he rebuked the Sea of Reeds, and it dried up; and he led them through the depths as through a wilderness.
Septuagint Trans: And he rebuked the Red Sea, and it was dried up; and he led them through the deep as through a wilderness.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And he rebuked the Red Sea, and it was dried up; and he led them through the depths as through a desert.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And he rebuked the Red Sea, and it dried up; and he led them through the depths as through a desert.

Hebrew Trans: And he saved them from the hand of the one who hated them, and redeemed them from the hand of the enemy.
Septuagint Trans: And he saved them from the hand of those who hated them, and redeemed them from the hand of the enemy.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And he saved them from the hand of those who hated them, and he redeemed them from the hand of the enemy.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And he saved them from the hand of him who hated them, and he redeemed them from the hand of the enemy.

Hebrew Trans: And the waters covered their adversaries; not one of them was left.
Septuagint Trans: And water covered those who afflicted them; not one of them was left.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And the water covered those who afflicted them; not one of them remained.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And the water covered their adversaries; not one of them remained.

Hebrew Trans: Then they believed his words; they sang his praise.
Septuagint Trans: And they believed in his words, and they sang his praise.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And they believed his words, and they sang his praise.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And they believed his words, and they sang his praise.

Hebrew Trans: They quickly forgot his works; they did not wait for his counsel.
Septuagint Trans: They quickly forgot his works; they did not wait for his counsel.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Quickly they forgot; they forgot his works; they did not wait for his counsel.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Quickly they forgot his works, and they did not wait for his counsel.

Hebrew Trans: And they lusted exceedingly in the wilderness, and tested God in the desert.
Septuagint Trans: And they lusted with desire in the wilderness and tested God in the waterless land.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And they coveted with great craving in the desert, and they tempted God in the waterless place.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And they desired a craving in the desert, and they tempted God in the wilderness.

Hebrew Trans: And he gave them their request, but sent leanness into their soul.
Septuagint Trans: And he gave them their request, and sent fullness into their souls.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And he gave them their petition, and sent leanness into their souls.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And he gave them their request, and sent leanness into their souls.

Hebrew Trans: And they were jealous of Moses in the camp, and of Aaron, the holy one of the LORD.
Septuagint Trans: And they provoked Moses in the camp, and Aaron, the holy one of the Lord.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And they provoked Moses in the camp, and Aaron, the holy one of the Lord.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And they were jealous of Moses in the camp, and of Aaron, the holy one of the Lord.

Hebrew Trans: The earth opened and swallowed Dathan, and covered the company of Abiram.
Septuagint Trans: The earth opened and swallowed Dathan, and covered over the congregation of Abiram.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The earth opened and swallowed Dathan, and covered over the congregation of Abiron.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The earth opened and swallowed Dathan, and covered the assembly of Abiram.

Hebrew Trans: And a fire was kindled in their company; a flame consumed the wicked.
Septuagint Trans: And a fire was kindled in their congregation; a flame consumed the sinners.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And fire blazed up in their assembly; a flame consumed the sinners.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And a fire was kindled in their assembly; a flame consumed the wicked.

Hebrew Trans: They made a calf at Horeb, and bowed down to a molten image.
Septuagint Trans: And they made a calf in Horeb, and worshiped the graven image.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And they made a calf in Horeb, and they worshipped a graven image.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: They made a calf in Horeb, and they worshipped a molten image.

Hebrew Trans: And they exchanged their glory for the likeness of an ox that eats grass.
Septuagint Trans: And they exchanged their glory for the likeness of a calf that eats grass.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And they exchanged their glory for the likeness of a calf that eats hay.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And they exchanged their glory for the likeness of an ox that eats hay.

Hebrew Trans: They forgot God their savior, who had done great things in Egypt,
Septuagint Trans: They forgot God who saved them, who had done great things in Egypt,
Gallican Psalter Trans: They forgot God who saved them, who had done great things in Egypt,
Hebrew Psalter Trans: They forgot God their savior, who had done great things in Egypt,

Hebrew Trans: Wondrous works in the land of Ham, awesome things at the Sea of Reeds.
Septuagint Trans: wondrous things in the land of Ham, fearful things at the Red Sea.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Wondrous things in the land of Ham, terrible things at the Red Sea.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Wondrous things in the land of Ham, terrible things at the Red Sea.

Hebrew Trans: And he said he would destroy them, had not Moses his chosen one stood in the breach before him, to turn away his wrath from destroying them.
Septuagint Trans: And he said he would destroy them, had not Moses his chosen one stood in the breach before him, to turn back his wrath, so as not to destroy them.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And he said that he would destroy them, had not Moses his chosen one stood in the breach before him, to turn away his wrath, lest he destroy them.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And he said that he would destroy them, had not Moses, his chosen one, stood in the breach before his face, to turn away his indignation, lest he destroy them.

Hebrew Trans: And they despised the pleasant land; they did not believe his word.
Septuagint Trans: And they despised the desirable land; they did not believe his word.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And they counted as nothing the desirable land; they did not believe his word.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And they despised the desirable land; they did not believe his word.

Hebrew Trans: And they murmured in their tents; they did not listen to the voice of the LORD.
Septuagint Trans: And they murmured in their tents; they did not listen to the voice of the Lord.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And they murmured in their tents; they did not listen to the voice of the Lord.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And they murmured in their tents; they did not listen to the voice of the Lord.

Hebrew Trans: And he lifted up his hand to them, to make them fall in the wilderness,
Septuagint Trans: And he lifted up his hand against them, to cast them down in the wilderness,
Gallican Psalter Trans: And he lifted up his hand against them, to strike them down in the desert,
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And he lifted up his hand against them, to cast them down in the desert,

Hebrew Trans: And to make their seed fall among the nations, and to scatter them in the lands.
Septuagint Trans: and to cast down their seed among the nations, and to scatter them in the lands.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And to cast down their seed among the nations, and to scatter them throughout the lands.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And to cast down their seed among the nations, and to scatter them in the lands.

Hebrew Trans: And they yoked themselves to Baal-Peor, and ate sacrifices of the dead.
Septuagint Trans: And they were initiated to Baal-Peor, and ate sacrifices offered to the dead.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And they were initiated into the rites of Baal-Peor, and they ate the sacrifices of the dead.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And they consecrated themselves to Baal-Peor, and ate the sacrifices of the dead.

Hebrew Trans: And they provoked him with their deeds, and a plague broke out among them.
Septuagint Trans: And they provoked him with their practices, and a plague was multiplied among them.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And they provoked him with their inventions, and ruin was multiplied among them.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And they provoked him with their deeds, and a plague struck them.

Hebrew Trans: Then Phinehas stood up and intervened, and the plague was stayed.
Septuagint Trans: And Phinehas stood up and made atonement, and the plague was stopped.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And Phinehas stood up and made intercession, and the plague ceased.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: But Phinehas stood up and judged, and the plague was stayed.

Hebrew Trans: And it was counted to him as righteousness, to generation and generation forever.
Septuagint Trans: And it was reckoned to him as righteousness, to generation and generation forever.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And it was counted to him as righteousness, from generation to generation, forever.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And it was reckoned to him as righteousness, from generation to generation, forever.

Hebrew Trans: And they angered him at the waters of Meribah, and it went ill with Moses on their account,
Septuagint Trans: And they provoked him at the waters of strife, and Moses was afflicted on their account,
Gallican Psalter Trans: And they provoked him at the waters of Contradiction, and Moses was troubled on their account,
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And they provoked him at the waters of Contradiction, and Moses was afflicted on their account,

Hebrew Trans: For they embittered his spirit, and he spoke rashly with his lips.
Septuagint Trans: because they embittered his spirit, and he spoke rashly with his lips.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Because they embittered his spirit, and he spoke rashly with his lips.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Because they provoked his spirit, and he spoke rashly with his lips.

Hebrew Trans: They did not destroy the peoples, as the LORD had commanded them,
Septuagint Trans: They did not destroy the nations which the Lord had told them to destroy.
Gallican Psalter Trans: They did not destroy the nations which the Lord had commanded them.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: They did not destroy the peoples whom the Lord had commanded them.

Hebrew Trans: But they mingled with the nations and learned their works.
Septuagint Trans: And they mingled with the nations and learned their works.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And they were mingled among the nations, and they learned their works.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And they mingled with the nations and learned their works.

Hebrew Trans: And they served their idols, which became a snare to them.
Septuagint Trans: And they served their graven images, and it became a snare to them.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And they served their graven images, and it became a stumbling block to them.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And they served their graven images, and it became a snare to them.

Hebrew Trans: And they sacrificed their sons and their daughters to demons,
Septuagint Trans: And they sacrificed their sons and their daughters to demons.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And they sacrificed their sons and their daughters to demons.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And they sacrificed their sons and their daughters to demons.

Hebrew Trans: And they shed innocent blood, the blood of their sons and daughters, whom they sacrificed to the idols of Canaan; and the land was polluted with blood.
Septuagint Trans: And they poured out innocent blood, the blood of their sons and daughters, whom they sacrificed to the graven images of Canaan, and the land was polluted with blood.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And they poured out innocent blood, the blood of their sons and their daughters, which they sacrificed to the idols of Canaan; and the land was polluted with blood.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And they poured out innocent blood, the blood of their sons and their daughters, whom they sacrificed to the graven images of Canaan, and the land was polluted with blood.

Hebrew Trans: And they became unclean by their works, and played the harlot in their deeds.
Septuagint Trans: And it was defiled by their works, and they played the harlot in their practices.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And it was defiled by their works, and they went whoring in their inventions.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And they were defiled by their works, and they played the harlot in their pursuits.

Hebrew Trans: And the anger of the LORD was kindled against his people, and he abhorred his inheritance.
Septuagint Trans: And the Lord was angry in wrath against his people, and he abhorred his inheritance.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And the Lord was kindled with fury against his people, and he abhorred his inheritance.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And therefore the fury of the Lord was kindled against his people, and he abhorred his inheritance.

Hebrew Trans: And he gave them into the hand of the nations, and those who hated them ruled over them.
Septuagint Trans: And he delivered them into the hands of the nations, and those who hated them ruled over them.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And he delivered them into the hands of the nations, and those who hated them had dominion over them.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And he gave them into the hands of the nations, and those who hated them ruled over them.

Hebrew Trans: And their enemies oppressed them, and they were subdued under their hand.
Septuagint Trans: And their enemies afflicted them, and they were humbled under their hands.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And their enemies afflicted them, and they were humbled under their hands.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And their enemies afflicted them, and they were humbled under their hand.

Hebrew Trans: Many times he delivered them, but they rebelled in their counsel and sank low in their iniquity.
Septuagint Trans: Many times he delivered them, but they rebelled against him by their counsel, and they were brought low in their iniquities.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Many times he delivered them, but they provoked him with their counsel, and they were brought low in their iniquities.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Many times he delivered them, but they provoked him with their counsels, and they were brought low because of their iniquities.

Hebrew Trans: Yet he looked upon their distress, when he heard their cry.
Septuagint Trans: And he looked upon their affliction, when he heard their supplication.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And he saw when they were in tribulation, and he heard their prayer.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And he saw their tribulation, when he heard their cry.

Hebrew Trans: And he remembered for them his covenant, and relented according to the abundance of his steadfast love.
Septuagint Trans: And he remembered his covenant, and relented according to the abundance of his mercy.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And he was mindful of his covenant, and he repented according to the multitude of his mercy.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And he remembered his covenant with them, and he relented according to the multitude of his mercy.

Hebrew Trans: And he gave them compassion before all who had taken them captive.
Septuagint Trans: And he granted them compassion before all who had taken them captive.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And he gave them over to mercies in the sight of all who had captured them.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And he made them objects of pity before all who had taken them captive.

Hebrew Trans: Save us, O LORD our God, and gather us from among the nations, to give thanks to your holy name, to glory in your praise.
Septuagint Trans: Save us, O Lord our God, and gather us from among the nations, to give thanks to your holy name, to glory in your praise.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Save us, O Lord our God, and gather us from among the nations, that we may give thanks to your holy name and glory in your praise.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Save us, O Lord our God, and gather us from among the nations, that we may give thanks to your holy name, and sing in praising you.

Hebrew Trans: Blessed be the LORD, the God of Israel, from everlasting to everlasting; and let all the people say, "Amen." Hallelujah!
Septuagint Trans: Blessed be the Lord, the God of Israel, from everlasting to everlasting; and all the people shall say, "So be it! So be it!"
Gallican Psalter Trans: Blessed be the Lord God of Israel, from everlasting and to everlasting; and let all the people say: So be it, so be it.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Blessed be the Lord God of Israel, from everlasting and to everlasting; and let all the people say: Amen. Alleluia.

Hebrew Trans: Give thanks to the LORD, for He is good, for His steadfast love endures forever.
Septuagint Trans: Hallelujah! Give thanks to the Lord, for he is good, for his mercy endures forever.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Alleluia. Give thanks to the Lord, for he is good, for his mercy endures forever.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Give thanks to the Lord, for he is good, for his mercy endures forever.

Hebrew Trans: Let the redeemed of the LORD say so, whom He has redeemed from the hand of the adversary,
Septuagint Trans: Let the redeemed of the Lord say so, whom he redeemed from the hand of the enemy,
Gallican Psalter Trans: Let those who have been redeemed by the Lord say so, whom he redeemed from the hand of the enemy.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Let those who have been redeemed by the Lord say so, whom he redeemed from the hand of the enemy.

Hebrew Trans: And gathered them from the lands, from the east and from the west, from the north and from the sea.
Septuagint Trans: From the lands he gathered them, from the east and the west, and from the north and the sea.
Gallican Psalter Trans: From the regions he gathered them, from the rising and the setting of the sun, and from the north and the sea.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And he gathered them from the lands, from the east and from the west, from the north and from the sea.

Hebrew Trans: They wandered in the wilderness, in a desolate wasteland; they found no city to dwell in.
Septuagint Trans: They wandered in the wilderness, in a waterless land; they found no way to an inhabited city.
Gallican Psalter Trans: They wandered in the wilderness, in a waterless place; they found no way to a city of habitation.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: They wandered in the wilderness, in a desert way; they found no city to dwell in.

Hebrew Trans: Hungry and thirsty, their soul fainted within them.
Septuagint Trans: Hungering and thirsting, their soul within them fainted.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Hungry and thirsty, their soul fainted within them.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Hungry and thirsty, their soul fainted within them.

Hebrew Trans: Then they cried out to the LORD in their distress; He delivered them from their troubles.
Septuagint Trans: And they cried out to the Lord in their affliction, and he delivered them from their distresses.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And they cried out to the Lord when they were in distress, and he rescued them from their necessities.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And they cried out to the Lord in their tribulation, and he delivered them from their affliction.

Hebrew Trans: And He led them by a straight way, to go to a city of habitation.
Septuagint Trans: And he guided them in a straight way, to go to an inhabited city.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And he led them in a straight way, that they might go to a city of habitation.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And he led them by a straight way, that they might come to a habitable city.

Hebrew Trans: Let them give thanks to the LORD for His steadfast love, and His wondrous works to the children of men.
Septuagint Trans: Let them give thanks to the Lord for his mercies and his wonders to the sons of men.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Let them give thanks to the Lord for his mercies and his wondrous works to the children of men.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Let them confess to the Lord his mercy and his wonders to the children of men.

Hebrew Trans: For He has satisfied the longing soul, and the hungry soul He has filled with good.
Septuagint Trans: For he satisfied the empty soul, and the hungry soul he filled with good things.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For he satisfied the empty soul, and the hungry soul he filled with good things.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For he satisfied the empty soul and filled the hungry soul with good things.

Hebrew Trans: Those who sat in darkness and in the shadow of death, prisoners of affliction and iron,
Septuagint Trans: Those sitting in darkness and the shadow of death, bound in poverty and iron,
Gallican Psalter Trans: Those sitting in darkness and the shadow of death, bound in poverty and iron,
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Those dwelling in darkness and the shadow of death, bound in poverty and iron,

Hebrew Trans: Because they rebelled against the words of God, and despised the counsel of the Most High.
Septuagint Trans: Because they had embittered the words of God and provoked the counsel of the Most High.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Because they had provoked the words of God and irritated the counsel of the Most High,
Hebrew Psalter Trans: because they provoked the words of God and blasphemed the counsel of the Most High.

Hebrew Trans: So He humbled their heart with toil; they stumbled, and there was none to help.
Septuagint Trans: And their heart was humbled with toils; they grew weak and there was none to help.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And their heart was humbled with labors; they were weakened, and there was none to help.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And he humbled their heart with toil; they fell, and there was none to help.

Hebrew Trans: Then they cried out to the LORD in their distress; He saved them from their troubles.
Septuagint Trans: And they cried out to the Lord in their affliction, and he saved them from their distresses.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And they cried out to the Lord when they were in distress, and he delivered them from their necessities.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And they cried out to the Lord in their tribulation, and he saved them from their distresses.

Hebrew Trans: He brought them out of darkness and the shadow of death, and broke their bonds asunder.
Septuagint Trans: And he brought them out of darkness and the shadow of death, and burst their bonds asunder.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And he brought them out of darkness and the shadow of death, and broke their bonds asunder.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And he brought them out of darkness and the shadow of death, and broke apart their chains.

Hebrew Trans: Let them give thanks to the LORD for His steadfast love, and His wondrous works to the children of men.
Septuagint Trans: Let them give thanks to the Lord for his mercies and his wonders to the sons of men.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Let them give thanks to the Lord for his mercies and his wondrous works to the children of men.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Let them confess to the Lord his mercy and his wonders to the children of men.

Hebrew Trans: For He has shattered the doors of bronze, and cut through the bars of iron.
Septuagint Trans: For he shattered gates of bronze and broke bars of iron.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For he shattered the gates of bronze and broke the bars of iron.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For he shattered the gates of bronze and broke the bars of iron.

Hebrew Trans: Fools, because of the way of their transgression, and because of their iniquities, were afflicted.
Septuagint Trans: He took hold of them out of the way of their lawlessness, for on account of their lawlessness they were humbled.
Gallican Psalter Trans: He took them up from the way of their iniquity, for on account of their injustices they had been humbled.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Fools, on account of the way of their wickedness and on account of their iniquities, were afflicted.

Hebrew Trans: Their soul abhorred all manner of food, and they drew near to the gates of death.
Septuagint Trans: Their soul abhorred all food, and they drew near to the gates of death.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Their soul abhorred all food, and they drew near even to the gates of death.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Their soul abhorred all food, and they drew near to the gates of death.

Hebrew Trans: Then they cried out to the LORD in their distress; He saved them from their troubles.
Septuagint Trans: And they cried out to the Lord in their affliction, and he saved them from their distresses.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And they cried out to the Lord when they were in distress, and he delivered them from their necessities.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And they cried out to the Lord in their tribulation, and he saved them from their distresses.

Hebrew Trans: He sent His word and healed them, and delivered them from their destructions.
Septuagint Trans: He sent his word and healed them, and delivered them from their destructions.
Gallican Psalter Trans: He sent his word and healed them, and rescued them from their destructions.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: He sent his word and healed them, and saved them from destruction.

Hebrew Trans: Let them give thanks to the LORD for His steadfast love, and His wondrous works to the children of men.
Septuagint Trans: Let them give thanks to the Lord for his mercies and his wonders to the sons of men.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Let them give thanks to the Lord for his mercies and his wondrous works to the children of men.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Let them confess to the Lord his mercy and his wonders to the children of men.

Hebrew Trans: And let them offer sacrifices of thanksgiving, and recount His deeds with joyful singing.
Septuagint Trans: And let them offer a sacrifice of praise, and proclaim his works with rejoicing.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And let them sacrifice a sacrifice of praise, and declare his works with exultation.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And let them sacrifice sacrifices of thanksgiving, and declare his works with praise.

Hebrew Trans: Those who go down to the sea in ships, who do business on great waters,
Septuagint Trans: Those who go down to the sea in ships, doing business on many waters—
Gallican Psalter Trans: Those who go down to the sea in ships, doing business on the great waters,
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Those who go down to the sea in ships, doing their work in the great waters—

Hebrew Trans: They have seen the works of the LORD, and His wonders in the deep.
Septuagint Trans: They have seen the works of the Lord and his wonders in the deep.
Gallican Psalter Trans: They have seen the works of the Lord and his wonders in the deep.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: they themselves saw the works of the Lord and his wonders in the deep.

Hebrew Trans: For He spoke and raised up a stormy wind, and it lifted up its waves.
Septuagint Trans: He spoke and a stormy wind arose, and its waves were lifted high.
Gallican Psalter Trans: He spoke, and a stormy wind arose, and its waves were lifted high.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: He spoke, and a stormy wind arose, and lifted up its waves.

Hebrew Trans: They mounted up to the heavens, they went down to the depths; their soul melted in misery.
Septuagint Trans: They rise up to the heavens and go down to the depths; their soul melted away in misery.
Gallican Psalter Trans: They ascend up to the heavens and descend to the depths; their soul was melting away in misery.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: They ascend to the heavens and descend to the depths; their soul is consumed in affliction.

Hebrew Trans: They reeled and staggered like a drunkard, and all their wisdom was swallowed up.
Septuagint Trans: They were troubled, they staggered like a drunkard, and all their wisdom was swallowed up.
Gallican Psalter Trans: They were troubled and staggered like a drunkard, and all their wisdom was swallowed up.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: They were stunned and trembled like a drunkard, and all their wisdom was swallowed up.

Hebrew Trans: Then they cried out to the LORD in their distress, and He brought them out of their troubles.
Septuagint Trans: And they cried out to the Lord in their affliction, and he brought them out of their distresses.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And they cried out to the Lord when they were in distress, and he brought them out of their necessities.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: But they will cry out to the Lord in their tribulation, and he will lead them out of their distress.

Hebrew Trans: He stilled the storm to a calm, and the waves thereof were hushed.
Septuagint Trans: And he commanded the storm and it became a calm breeze, and its waves were silenced.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And he stilled the storm to a breeze, and its waves fell silent.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: He will turn the tempest into calm, and its waves will be silent.

Hebrew Trans: And they were glad because they grew quiet, and He guided them to the harbor of their desire.
Septuagint Trans: And they rejoiced because they were at rest, and he guided them to the harbor of their desire.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And they were glad because they were silenced, and he led them to the harbor of their desire.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: They will rejoice because they have been stilled, and he will lead them to the port they desired.

Hebrew Trans: Let them give thanks to the LORD for His steadfast love, and His wondrous works to the children of men.
Septuagint Trans: Let them give thanks to the Lord for his mercies and his wonders to the sons of men.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Let them give thanks to the Lord for his mercies and his wondrous works to the children of men.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Let them confess to the Lord his mercy and his wonders to the children of men.

Hebrew Trans: And let them exalt Him in the assembly of the people, and praise Him in the seat of the elders.
Septuagint Trans: Let them exalt him in the assembly of the people, and in the seat of the elders let them praise him.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Let them exalt him in the assembly of the people, and praise him in the seat of the elders.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And let them exalt him in the assembly of the people, and praise him in the seat of the elders.

Hebrew Trans: He turns rivers into a wilderness, and springs of water into thirsty ground,
Septuagint Trans: He turned rivers into a desert, and springs of water into thirsty ground,
Gallican Psalter Trans: He turned rivers into a desert, and springs of water into thirst,
Hebrew Psalter Trans: He turns rivers into a desert and springs of water into thirst,

Hebrew Trans: A fruitful land into a salt waste, because of the wickedness of those who dwell in it.
Septuagint Trans: A fruitful land into saltness, because of the wickedness of those dwelling in it.
Gallican Psalter Trans: A fruitful land into a salt waste, because of the wickedness of those dwelling in it.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: a fruitful land into a salt waste, because of the wickedness of its inhabitants.

Hebrew Trans: He turns a wilderness into a pool of water, and a dry land into springs of water.
Septuagint Trans: He turned a desert into pools of water, and a waterless land into springs of water.
Gallican Psalter Trans: He turned the desert into pools of water, and a waterless land into springs of water.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: He turns the desert into pools of water and a pathless land into springs of water.

Hebrew Trans: And He settles the hungry there, and they establish a city of habitation.
Septuagint Trans: And he settled the hungry there, and they established an inhabited city.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And he settled the hungry there, and they established a city of habitation.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And he will settle the hungry there, and they will found a city to dwell in.

Hebrew Trans: And they sow fields and plant vineyards, and they yield a fruitful harvest.
Septuagint Trans: And they sowed fields and planted vineyards, and they produced a fruitful harvest.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And they sowed fields and planted vineyards, and they yielded a fruitful harvest.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And they will sow fields and plant vineyards and produce fruitful harvests.

Hebrew Trans: And He blesses them, and they multiply greatly, and their cattle He does not diminish.
Septuagint Trans: And he blessed them, and they multiplied greatly, and their cattle he did not diminish.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And he blessed them, and they were multiplied greatly, and their cattle he did not diminish.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And he will bless them, and they will be multiplied greatly, and their cattle he will not diminish.

Hebrew Trans: Then they are diminished and brought low through oppression, misery, and sorrow.
Septuagint Trans: And they were diminished and brought low through the affliction of evils and pain.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And they became few and were afflicted by the tribulation of evils and sorrow.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: But they were diminished and afflicted through the anguish of evil and sorrow.

Hebrew Trans: He pours contempt upon princes, and causes them to wander in a trackless waste.
Septuagint Trans: Contempt was poured out upon princes, and he caused them to wander in a trackless waste and not a road.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Contempt was poured out upon princes, and he made them wander in a trackless waste and not on a road.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And he will pour contempt upon princes and cause them to wander in a trackless wilderness.

Hebrew Trans: But He lifts the needy out of affliction, and makes their families like a flock.
Septuagint Trans: And he helped the poor out of poverty, and made their families like flocks.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And he helped the poor man out of poverty, and made families like flocks.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And he will lift up the poor from poverty and establish families like a flock.

Hebrew Trans: The upright see it and rejoice, and all iniquity shuts its mouth.
Septuagint Trans: The upright shall see and shall rejoice, and all iniquity shall shut its mouth.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The righteous shall see and rejoice, and all iniquity shall shut its mouth.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The upright will see and rejoice, and all iniquity will shut its mouth.

Hebrew Trans: Whoever is wise, let him observe these things, and let them consider the steadfast lovingkindnesses of the LORD.
Septuagint Trans: Who is wise and will keep these things? And they shall understand the mercies of the Lord.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Who is wise and will keep these things, and they shall understand the mercies of the Lord.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Who is wise and will keep these things, and they will understand the mercies of the Lord?

Hebrew Trans: A song, a psalm of David.
Septuagint Trans: A song of a psalm, of David.
Gallican Psalter Trans: A canticle psalm of David.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: A canticle of a psalm of David.

Hebrew Trans: My heart is steadfast, O God; I will sing and make music, even with my glory.
Septuagint Trans: My heart is ready, O God, my heart is ready; I will sing and make music in my glory.
Gallican Psalter Trans: My heart is prepared, O God, my heart is prepared: I will sing and make melody in my glory.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: My heart is prepared, O God; I will sing and make music; yea, even my glory.

Hebrew Trans: Awake, O harp and lyre! I will awaken the dawn.
Septuagint Trans: Awake, psaltery and harp; I will awake at dawn.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Arise, O psaltery and harp: I will arise at the break of day.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Arise, O psaltery and harp; I will arise in the morning.

Hebrew Trans: I will give thanks to You among the peoples, O LORD, and I will sing praises to You among the nations.
Septuagint Trans: I will give thanks to you among the peoples, O Lord, and I will sing to you among the nations.
Gallican Psalter Trans: I will give thanks to you among the peoples, O Lord, and I will sing to you among the nations.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: I will praise you among the peoples, O Lord, and I will sing to you among the nations.

Hebrew Trans: For great above the heavens is Your lovingkindness, and unto the skies is Your faithfulness.
Septuagint Trans: For your mercy is great above the heavens, and your truth reaches to the clouds.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For your mercy is great above the heavens, and your truth reaches even to the clouds.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For your mercy is great above the heavens, and your truth reaches unto the skies.

Hebrew Trans: Be exalted above the heavens, O God, and Your glory above all the earth.
Septuagint Trans: Be exalted above the heavens, O God, and your glory above all the earth.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Be exalted above the heavens, O God, and your glory above all the earth.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Be exalted above the heavens, O God, and your glory above all the earth.

Hebrew Trans: That Your beloved ones may be delivered, save with Your right hand and answer me.
Septuagint Trans: So that your beloved ones may be delivered, save with your right hand and hear me.
Gallican Psalter Trans: That your beloved may be delivered, save with your right hand and hear me.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: That your beloved ones may be delivered, save with your right hand and hear me.

Hebrew Trans: God has spoken in His holiness: "I will exult, I will divide Shechem and measure out the Valley of Succoth.
Septuagint Trans: God has spoken in his sanctuary: I will be exalted and I will divide Shechem, and I will measure out the valley of tents.
Gallican Psalter Trans: God has spoken in his holy place: I will be exalted and I will divide Shechem, and I will measure out the valley of tabernacles.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: God has spoken in his sanctuary: I will rejoice; I will divide Shechem and measure out the valley of Succoth.

Hebrew Trans: Gilead is mine, Manasseh is mine; Ephraim is the strength of my head; Judah is my lawgiver.
Septuagint Trans: Gilead is mine, and Manasseh is mine, and Ephraim is the strength of my head; Judah is my king.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Gilead is mine, and Manasseh is mine, and Ephraim is the strength of my head; Judah is my king.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Gilead is mine, and Manasseh is mine, and Ephraim is the inheritance of my head; Judah is my ruler.

Hebrew Trans: Moab is my washbasin; upon Edom I will cast my sandal; over Philistia I will shout in triumph."
Septuagint Trans: Moab is the basin of my hope; upon Edom I will stretch out my sandal; the foreigners have been subjected to me.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Moab is the basin of my hope; into Edom I will stretch out my sandal; to me the foreigners have become friends.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Moab is the basin of my washing; upon Edom I will cast my sandal; with Philistia I will make a covenant.

Hebrew Trans: Who will bring me to the fortified city? Who will lead me to Edom?
Septuagint Trans: Who will bring me into the fortified city? Who will lead me as far as Edom?
Gallican Psalter Trans: Who will lead me into the fortified city? Who will lead me as far as Edom?
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Who will lead me into the fortified city? Who will lead me as far as Edom?

Hebrew Trans: Have You not rejected us, O God? And You do not go forth, O God, with our armies.
Septuagint Trans: Is it not you, O God, who have rejected us? And will you not go out, O God, with our armies?
Gallican Psalter Trans: Is it not you, O God, who have cast us off? And will you not go out, O God, with our armies?
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Is it not you, O God, who had cast us off, and did not go out, O God, with our armies?

Hebrew Trans: Grant us help against the adversary, for vain is the salvation of man.
Septuagint Trans: Grant us help from affliction, for vain is the salvation of man.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Give us help from tribulation, for vain is the salvation of man.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Give us aid in tribulation, for vain is the salvation from man.

Hebrew Trans: Through God we shall do valiantly, and He will tread down our enemies.
Septuagint Trans: In God we shall do mightily, and he himself will bring to nothing our enemies.
Gallican Psalter Trans: In God we shall do valiantly, and he himself will bring our enemies to nothing.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: In God we shall be mighty, and he himself will trample down our enemies.

Hebrew Trans: For the choirmaster, of David, a psalm. O God of my praise, do not be silent.
Septuagint Trans: For the end; of David, a psalm. O God, do not pass over my praise in silence.
Gallican Psalter Trans: To the end. A psalm of David. O God, be not silent about my praise.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For the victory. A song of David. O God of my praise, do not be silent.

Hebrew Trans: For the mouth of the wicked and the mouth of deceit have opened against me; they have spoken against me with a lying tongue.
Septuagint Trans: For the mouth of the sinner and the mouth of the deceitful have been opened against me; they have spoken against me with a deceitful tongue.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For the mouth of the sinner and the mouth of the deceitful one have been opened against me; they have spoken against me with a deceitful tongue.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For the mouth of the wicked and the mouth of the deceitful are opened against me; they have spoken against me with a lying tongue.

Hebrew Trans: And words of hatred have surrounded me, and they fight against me without cause.
Septuagint Trans: And with words of hatred they have surrounded me and warred against me without cause.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And with words of hatred they have surrounded me, and they have attacked me without cause.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: They have surrounded me with words of hatred, and they have fought against me without cause.

Hebrew Trans: In return for my love they accuse me, but I am all prayer.
Septuagint Trans: Instead of loving me, they slandered me; but I was praying.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Instead of loving me, they slandered me; but I prayed.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: In return for my love they opposed me, but I prayed.

Hebrew Trans: And they have set upon me evil in return for good, and hatred in return for my love.
Septuagint Trans: And they set evil against me in return for good, and hatred in return for my love.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And they set against me evil for good, and hatred for my love.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And they set against me evil for good, and hatred for my love.

Hebrew Trans: Appoint a wicked man over him, and let an accuser stand at his right hand.
Septuagint Trans: Set a sinner over him, and let an accuser stand at his right hand.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Set a sinner over him, and let the devil stand at his right hand.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Set a wicked man over him, and let Satan stand at his right hand.

Hebrew Trans: When he is judged, let him come forth guilty, and let his prayer become sin.
Septuagint Trans: When he is judged, let him come forth condemned, and let his prayer become sin.
Gallican Psalter Trans: When he is judged, let him go out condemned, and let his prayer become sin.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: When he is judged, let him go out condemned, and let his prayer become sin.

Hebrew Trans: Let his days be few; let another take his office.
Septuagint Trans: Let his days be few, and let another take his office.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Let his days be few, and let another take his office.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Let his days be few; let another take his office.

Hebrew Trans: Let his children be fatherless, and his wife a widow.
Septuagint Trans: Let his sons become orphans and his wife a widow.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Let his sons be orphans, and his wife a widow.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Let his sons be orphans, and his wife a widow.

Hebrew Trans: And let his children wander about and beg, and let them seek from their ruins.
Septuagint Trans: Let his sons be shaken and wander as refugees, and let them beg; let them be cast out from their homesteads.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Let his sons be carried away wandering, and let them beg; let them be cast out from their dwellings.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Let his children wander about unstably and beg, and let them be driven out from their ruins.

Hebrew Trans: Let the creditor ensnare all that he has, and let strangers plunder his labor.
Septuagint Trans: Let the creditor search out all that he has; let strangers plunder his labors.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Let the moneylender search out all his substance, and let strangers plunder his labors.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Let the creditor search out all that he has, and let strangers plunder his labor.

Hebrew Trans: Let there be none to extend kindness to him, and let there be none to show favor to his orphans.
Septuagint Trans: Let there be no helper for him, nor let there be anyone to have compassion on his orphans.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Let there be no helper for him, nor let there be anyone who has mercy on his fatherless children.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Let there be no one who has mercy on him, nor anyone who is kind to his orphans.

Hebrew Trans: Let his posterity be cut off; in the next generation let their name be blotted out.
Septuagint Trans: Let his children be given over to destruction; in a single generation let his name be blotted out.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Let his offspring be given over to destruction; in one generation let his name be blotted out.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Let his posterity be cut off; in the next generation let his name be blotted out.

Hebrew Trans: Let the iniquity of his fathers be remembered before the LORD, and let the sin of his mother not be blotted out.
Septuagint Trans: Let the iniquity of his fathers be remembered before the Lord, and let the sin of his mother not be blotted out.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Let the iniquity of his fathers be remembered in the sight of the Lord, and let the sin of his mother not be blotted out.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Let the iniquity of his fathers be remembered before God, and let the iniquity of his mother not be blotted out.

Hebrew Trans: Let them be before the LORD continually, and let Him cut off their memory from the earth.
Septuagint Trans: Let them be before the Lord continually, and let the memory of them be utterly destroyed from the earth.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Let them be before the Lord always, and let the memory of them perish from the earth.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Let them be before the Lord always, and let the memory of them perish from the earth.

Hebrew Trans: Because he did not remember to show kindness, but pursued the poor and needy man, and the broken-hearted, to put them to death.
Septuagint Trans: Because he did not remember to show mercy, and pursued a man who was needy and poor and pierced in heart, to put him to death.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Because he did not remember to show mercy, and persecuted the needy and poor man and the brokenhearted, to put them to death.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Because he did not remember to show mercy, and persecuted the needy and poor man, and the broken-hearted, to put him to death.

Hebrew Trans: And he loved cursing, and it came upon him; and he did not delight in blessing, and it was far from him.
Septuagint Trans: And he loved a curse, and it shall come upon him; and he did not desire blessing, and it shall be far from him.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And he loved cursing, and it shall come upon him; and he did not desire blessing, and it shall be far from him.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And he loved cursing, and it shall come to him; and he did not desire blessing, and it shall be far from him.

Hebrew Trans: And he clothed himself with cursing as his garment, and it entered like water into his inward parts, and like oil into his bones.
Septuagint Trans: And he put on cursing as a garment, and it entered like water into his inward parts, and like oil into his bones.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And he put on cursing like a garment, and it entered like water into his inward parts, and like oil into his bones.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And he clothed himself with cursing as with his garment, and it shall enter like water into his bowels, and like oil into his bones.

Hebrew Trans: Let it be to him like a garment that covers him, and as a belt with which he is always girded.
Septuagint Trans: Let it be to him like a garment in which he wraps himself, and like a belt with which he continually girds himself.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Let it be to him like a garment with which he is covered, and like a belt with which he is always girded.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Let it be to him like a cloak with which he is covered, and like a belt with which he is always girded.

Hebrew Trans: This is the reward of my accusers from the LORD, and of those who speak evil against my soul.
Septuagint Trans: This is the work of those who slander me before the Lord, and of those who speak evil against my soul.
Gallican Psalter Trans: This is the work of those who slander me before the Lord, and who speak evil against my soul.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: This is the recompense from the Lord to those who oppose me, and who speak evil against my soul.

Hebrew Trans: But You, O LORD, my Lord, deal with me for Your name's sake; because Your lovingkindness is good, deliver me.
Septuagint Trans: But you, O Lord, Lord, deal mercifully with me for your name's sake, for your mercy is good.
Gallican Psalter Trans: But you, O Lord, Lord, deal with me for your name's sake; because your mercy is sweet, deliver me.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: But you, O God the Lord, deal with me for your name's sake; for your mercy is good, deliver me.

Hebrew Trans: For I am poor and needy, and my heart is pierced within me.
Septuagint Trans: Deliver me, for I am poor and needy, and my heart is troubled within me.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For I am needy and poor, and my heart is troubled within me.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For I am needy and poor, and my heart is wounded within me.

Hebrew Trans: Like a shadow when it lengthens, I am gone; I am shaken off like a locust.
Septuagint Trans: Like a shadow when it declines, I have been taken away; I have been shaken off like locusts.
Gallican Psalter Trans: I have been taken away like a shadow when it declines; I have been shaken off like locusts.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: I am taken away like a shadow when it declines, and I am shaken off like a locust.

Hebrew Trans: My knees totter from fasting, and my flesh has grown lean, without fatness.
Septuagint Trans: My knees have grown weak from fasting, and my flesh has been changed for lack of oil.
Gallican Psalter Trans: My knees are weakened from fasting, and my flesh is changed for lack of oil.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: My knees have grown weak from fasting, and my flesh is changed for lack of oil.

Hebrew Trans: And I have become a reproach to them; they see me, they shake their heads.
Septuagint Trans: And I became a reproach to them; they saw me and shook their heads.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And I have become a reproach to them; they saw me and shook their heads.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And I have become a reproach to them; seeing me, they shook their heads.

Hebrew Trans: Help me, O LORD my God; save me according to Your lovingkindness.
Septuagint Trans: Help me, O Lord my God; save me according to your mercy.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Help me, O Lord my God; save me according to your mercy.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Help me, O Lord my God; save me according to your mercy.

Hebrew Trans: And let them know that this is Your hand; that You, O LORD, have done it.
Septuagint Trans: And let them know that this is your hand, and that you, O Lord, have done it.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And let them know that this is your hand; you, O Lord, have done it.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And let them know that this is your hand; you, O Lord, have done it.

Hebrew Trans: Let them curse, but You will bless; they arise and are put to shame, but Your servant shall rejoice.
Septuagint Trans: They will curse, but you will bless; let those who rise up against me be put to shame, but let your servant rejoice.
Gallican Psalter Trans: They will curse, but you will bless; let those who rise up against me be confounded, but your servant shall rejoice.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: They will curse, but you will bless; they have risen up and shall be confounded, but your servant shall rejoice.

Hebrew Trans: Let my accusers be clothed with disgrace, and let them wrap themselves in their shame as in a cloak.
Septuagint Trans: Let those who slander me be clothed with disgrace, and let them wrap themselves in their shame as in a cloak.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Let those who slander me be clothed with shame, and let them be covered with their confusion as with a cloak.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Let my adversaries be clothed with shame, and let them be covered with their confusion as with a garment.

Hebrew Trans: I will greatly praise the LORD with my mouth, and in the midst of many I will praise Him.
Septuagint Trans: I will give thanks to the Lord greatly with my mouth, and in the midst of many I will praise him.
Gallican Psalter Trans: I will give great thanks to the Lord with my mouth, and in the midst of many I will praise him.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: I will praise the Lord greatly with my mouth, and in the midst of the peoples I will praise him.

Hebrew Trans: For He stands at the right hand of the needy, to save him from those who judge his soul.
Septuagint Trans: For he stood at the right hand of the poor, to save my soul from those who persecute it.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For he stood at the right hand of the poor, to save my soul from those who persecute it.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For he shall stand at the right hand of the poor, to save his soul from those who judge him.

Hebrew Trans: Of David, a psalm. The declaration of the LORD to my lord: "Sit at my right hand, until I make your enemies a footstool for your feet."
Septuagint Trans: Of David, a psalm. The Lord said to my lord: Sit at my right hand, until I make your enemies a footstool for your feet.
Gallican Psalter Trans: A psalm of David. The Lord said to my Lord: Sit at my right hand, until I make your enemies a footstool for your feet.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Of David, a song. The Lord said to my Lord: "Sit at my right hand, until I make your enemies a footstool for your feet."

Hebrew Trans: The rod of your strength the LORD shall send from Zion; rule in the midst of your enemies.
Septuagint Trans: The Lord will send forth the rod of your strength out of Zion: and rule in the midst of your enemies.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The Lord will send forth the rod of your strength out of Zion: rule in the midst of your enemies.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The Lord will send forth the rod of your strength out of Zion: rule in the midst of your enemies.

Hebrew Trans: Your people are freewill offerings in the day of your power; in the splendors of holiness, from the womb of the dawn, yours is the dew of your youth.
Septuagint Trans: With you is dominion in the day of your power, in the splendors of the holy ones; from the womb, before the morning star, I begot you.
Gallican Psalter Trans: With you is the principality in the day of your strength, in the splendors of the saints: from the womb before the daystar I begot you.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Your people shall be willing in the day of your strength; in the holy mountains, as if from the womb, the dew of your youth shall be born to you.

Hebrew Trans: The LORD has sworn and will not repent: "You are a priest forever after the order of Melchizedek."
Septuagint Trans: The Lord has sworn and will not repent: You are a priest forever, according to the order of Melchizedek.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The Lord has sworn, and he will not repent: You are a priest forever according to the order of Melchizedek.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The Lord has sworn and will not repent: "You are a priest forever according to the order of Melchizedek."

Hebrew Trans: The Lord at your right hand shatters kings in the day of his wrath.
Septuagint Trans: The Lord at your right hand has shattered kings in the day of his wrath.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The Lord at your right hand has shattered kings in the day of his wrath.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The Lord at your right hand has struck down kings in the day of his wrath.

Hebrew Trans: He shall judge among the nations, filling them with corpses; he shatters the head over the wide earth.
Septuagint Trans: He will judge among the nations, he will fill them with corpses, he will shatter heads over a vast land.
Gallican Psalter Trans: He shall judge among the nations, he shall fill them with corpses, he shall crush heads in the land of many.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: He shall judge among the nations, he shall fill the valleys, he shall strike the head over a vast land.

Hebrew Trans: From the brook along the way he shall drink; therefore he shall lift up the head.
Septuagint Trans: From the torrent along the way he will drink; therefore he will lift up his head.
Gallican Psalter Trans: He shall drink from the torrent on the way; therefore he shall lift up his head.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: He shall drink from the torrent along the way; therefore he shall lift up his head.

Hebrew Trans: Praise Yah! I will give thanks to the LORD with all my heart, in the council of the upright and the congregation.
Septuagint Trans: Hallelujah! I will give thanks to you, O Lord, with my whole heart, in the council of the upright and in the congregation.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Alleluia, of the return of Haggai and Zechariah. I will praise you, O Lord, with my whole heart, in the council of the just and in the congregation.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Alleluia. I will confess to the Lord with my whole heart, in the council of the just and in the congregation.

Hebrew Trans: Great are the works of the LORD, sought out by all who delight in them.
Septuagint Trans: Great are the works of the Lord, sought out according to all his purposes.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Great are the works of the Lord, sought out according to all his desires.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Great are the works of the Lord, sought out according to all His purposes.

Hebrew Trans: Majesty and splendor are His work, and His righteousness endures forever.
Septuagint Trans: Thanksgiving and majesty are his work, and his righteousness endures forever and ever.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Praise and magnificence are his work, and his justice endures forever and ever.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Glory and beauty are His work, and His justice endures forever.

Hebrew Trans: He has made a memorial for His wondrous deeds; gracious and compassionate is the LORD.
Septuagint Trans: He has made a memorial of his wonders; the Lord is merciful and compassionate.
Gallican Psalter Trans: He has made a memorial of his wonders; the Lord is merciful and compassionate.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: He has made a memorial of His wonders; the Lord is gracious and merciful.

Hebrew Trans: Food He has given to those who fear Him; He remembers His covenant forever.
Septuagint Trans: He has given food to those who fear him; he will remember his covenant forever.
Gallican Psalter Trans: He has given food to those who fear him; he will be mindful forever of his covenant.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: He has given food to those who fear Him; He will be mindful forever of His covenant.

Hebrew Trans: The power of His works He has declared to His people, to give them the inheritance of the nations.
Septuagint Trans: He has declared the power of his works to his people, to give them the inheritance of the nations.
Gallican Psalter Trans: He will declare to his people the power of his works, that he may give them the inheritance of the nations.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: He will declare the strength of His works to His people, that He may give them the inheritance of the nations.

Hebrew Trans: The works of His hands are truth and justice; faithful are all His precepts.
Septuagint Trans: The works of his hands are truth and judgment; all his commandments are faithful,
Gallican Psalter Trans: The works of his hands are truth and judgment.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The works of His hands are truth and judgment; all His precepts are faithful,

Hebrew Trans: They are established forever and ever, wrought in truth and uprightness.
Septuagint Trans: established forever and ever, made in truth and uprightness.
Gallican Psalter Trans: All his commandments are faithful, established forever and ever, made in truth and equity.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: established forever and ever, made in truth and equity.

Hebrew Trans: Redemption He sent to His people; He has commanded His covenant forever; holy and awesome is His name.
Septuagint Trans: He sent redemption to his people; he has commanded his covenant forever. Holy and fearful is his name.
Gallican Psalter Trans: He sent redemption to his people; he commanded his covenant forever. Holy and terrible is his name.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: He sent redemption to His people; He commanded His covenant forever. Holy and terrible is His name.

Hebrew Trans: The beginning of wisdom is the fear of the LORD; good understanding have all who practice them; His praise endures forever.
Septuagint Trans: The fear of the Lord is the beginning of wisdom; a good understanding have all those who practice it. His praise endures forever and ever.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The fear of the Lord is the beginning of wisdom; a good understanding have all those who practice it. His praise endures forever and ever.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The fear of the Lord is the beginning of wisdom; a good understanding to all who practice it. His praise endures forever. Alleluia.

Hebrew Trans: Praise Yah! Blessed is the man who fears the LORD, who delights greatly in His commandments.
Septuagint Trans: Hallelujah! Blessed is the man who fears the Lord; in his commandments he shall delight greatly.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Alleluia, of the return of Haggai and Zechariah. Blessed is the man who fears the Lord: in his commandments he shall delight greatly.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Blessed is the man who fears the Lord: in his commandments he shall delight greatly.

Hebrew Trans: His seed shall be mighty upon the earth; the generation of the upright shall be blessed.
Septuagint Trans: His seed shall be mighty upon the earth; the generation of the upright shall be blessed.
Gallican Psalter Trans: His seed shall be mighty upon the earth: the generation of the upright shall be blessed.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: His seed shall be mighty upon the earth: the generation of the righteous shall be blessed.

Hebrew Trans: Wealth and riches are in his house, and his righteousness endures forever.
Septuagint Trans: Glory and wealth are in his house, and his righteousness endures forever and ever.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Glory and riches shall be in his house, and his justice endures forever and ever.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Substance and riches shall be in his house, and his justice endures forever.

Hebrew Trans: Light arises in the darkness for the upright; he is gracious, and compassionate, and righteous.
Septuagint Trans: Light has risen in the darkness for the upright; he is merciful and compassionate and righteous.
Gallican Psalter Trans: A light has risen in the darkness for the upright: he is merciful, and compassionate, and just.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: A light has risen in the darkness for the just: he is merciful, and compassionate, and righteous.

Hebrew Trans: Good is the man who is gracious and lends; he will guide his affairs with justice.
Septuagint Trans: Good is the man who shows mercy and lends; he will manage his affairs with judgment.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Happy is the man who shows mercy and lends; he shall order his words in judgment.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The good man is merciful and lends; he shall order his words with judgment.

Hebrew Trans: For he shall never be moved; the righteous shall be in everlasting remembrance.
Septuagint Trans: For he shall never be shaken; the righteous one shall be in everlasting remembrance.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For he shall never be moved; the just man shall be in everlasting remembrance.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For he shall never be moved: the just shall be in everlasting remembrance.

Hebrew Trans: He shall not be afraid of evil tidings; his heart is steadfast, trusting in the LORD.
Septuagint Trans: He shall not be afraid of an evil report; his heart is ready to hope in the Lord.
Gallican Psalter Trans: He shall not fear at evil tidings; his heart is prepared to hope in the Lord.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: He shall not be afraid of evil tidings: his heart is prepared, trusting in the Lord.

Hebrew Trans: His heart is upheld, he shall not fear, until he looks upon his adversaries.
Septuagint Trans: His heart is established; he shall not be afraid, until he looks upon his enemies.
Gallican Psalter Trans: His heart is confirmed; he shall not be moved until he looks down upon his enemies.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: His heart is firm; he shall not fear until he looks upon his enemies.

Hebrew Trans: He has scattered, he has given to the needy; his righteousness endures forever; his horn shall be exalted with honor.
Septuagint Trans: He has scattered abroad, he has given to the poor; his righteousness endures forever and ever; his horn shall be exalted in glory.
Gallican Psalter Trans: He has distributed, he has given to the poor; his justice endures forever and ever; his horn shall be exalted in glory.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: He has dispersed, he has given to the poor: his justice endures forever; his horn shall be exalted in glory.

Hebrew Trans: The wicked shall see it and be vexed; he shall gnash his teeth and melt away; the desire of the wicked shall perish.
Septuagint Trans: The sinner shall see it and be angered; he shall gnash his teeth and waste away; the desire of sinners shall perish.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The sinner shall see it and shall be angry; he shall gnash his teeth and waste away; the desire of sinners shall perish.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The wicked shall see and shall be angry; he shall gnash with his teeth and waste away: the desire of the wicked shall perish.

Hebrew Trans: Praise the LORD! Praise, O servants of the LORD, praise the name of the LORD.
Septuagint Trans: Hallelujah! Praise, O children, the Lord; praise the name of the Lord.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Alleluia. Praise the Lord, you children: praise the name of the Lord.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Alleluia. Praise the Lord, O servants; praise the name of the Lord.

Hebrew Trans: Let the name of the LORD be blessed from this time forth and forevermore.
Septuagint Trans: May the name of the Lord be blessed from now and unto the age.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Let the name of the Lord be blessed, from this time now and unto eternity.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Let the name of the Lord be blessed from this time forth and forevermore.

Hebrew Trans: From the rising of the sun to its setting, the name of the LORD is to be praised.
Septuagint Trans: From the risings of the sun until its settings, praise the name of the Lord.
Gallican Psalter Trans: From the rising of the sun unto its setting, praiseworthy is the name of the Lord.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: From the rising of the sun to its setting, the name of the Lord is worthy of praise.

Hebrew Trans: The LORD is high above all nations, and his glory above the heavens.
Septuagint Trans: The Lord is high above all the nations; his glory is above the heavens.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The Lord is exalted above all nations; his glory is above the heavens.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The Lord is exalted above all nations; his glory is above the heavens.

Hebrew Trans: Who is like the LORD our God, who is enthroned on high,
Septuagint Trans: Who is like the Lord our God, who dwells in the heights,
Gallican Psalter Trans: Who is like the Lord our God, who dwells in the heights,
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Who is like the Lord our God, who dwells on high,

Hebrew Trans: Who looks down low upon the heavens and the earth?
Septuagint Trans: and looks upon the lowly things in heaven and on earth?
Gallican Psalter Trans: and looks upon the lowly things in heaven and on earth?
Hebrew Psalter Trans: yet looks down upon the lowly things in heaven and on earth?

Hebrew Trans: He raises the poor from the dust, from the ash heap he lifts the needy,
Septuagint Trans: He who raises the poor from the earth and lifts the needy from the dunghill,
Gallican Psalter Trans: Raising up the needy from the earth, and lifting the poor from the dung,
Hebrew Psalter Trans: He raises the needy from the earth, and lifts the poor from the dung heap,

Hebrew Trans: To seat them with nobles, with the nobles of his people.
Septuagint Trans: to seat him with rulers, with the rulers of his people;
Gallican Psalter Trans: that he may place him with princes, with the princes of his people.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: that he may seat him with princes, with the princes of his people.

Hebrew Trans: He settles the barren woman of the house as a joyful mother of children. Praise the LORD!
Septuagint Trans: He who settles the barren woman in a house as a joyful mother of children.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Who makes the barren woman to dwell in a home, a joyful mother of children.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: He settles the barren woman in a home, a joyful mother of children. Alleluia.

Hebrew Trans: When Israel went out of Egypt, the house of Jacob from a people of foreign tongue,
Septuagint Trans: Hallelujah! When Israel went out of Egypt, the house of Jacob from a barbarous people,
Gallican Psalter Trans: Alleluia. In the going out of Israel from Egypt, the house of Jacob from a barbarous people,
Hebrew Psalter Trans: When Israel went out of Egypt, the house of Jacob from a barbarous people,

Hebrew Trans: Judah became His sanctuary, Israel His dominions.
Septuagint Trans: Judah became his sanctuary, Israel his dominion.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Judea was made his sanctification, Israel his power.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Judah became his sanctuary, Israel his dominion.

Hebrew Trans: The sea saw and fled; the Jordan turned back.
Septuagint Trans: The sea saw and fled; the Jordan turned back.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The sea saw and fled; the Jordan was turned back.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The sea saw and fled; the Jordan was turned back.

Hebrew Trans: The mountains skipped like rams, the hills like young sheep.
Septuagint Trans: The mountains skipped like rams, and the hills like lambs of the flock.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The mountains leaped like rams, the hills like lambs of the flock.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The mountains leaped like rams, the hills like the young of the flock.

Hebrew Trans: What ails you, O sea, that you flee? O Jordan, that you turn back?
Septuagint Trans: What ails you, O sea, that you fled? And you, O Jordan, that you turned back?
Gallican Psalter Trans: What ails you, O sea, that you fled? And you, O Jordan, that you were turned back?
Hebrew Psalter Trans: What ails you, O sea, that you fled? O Jordan, that you were turned back?

Hebrew Trans: O mountains, that you skip like rams? O hills, like young sheep?
Septuagint Trans: O mountains, that you skipped like rams? And O hills, like lambs of the flock?
Gallican Psalter Trans: O mountains, that you leaped like rams? And hills, like lambs of the flock?
Hebrew Psalter Trans: O mountains, that you leaped like rams? O hills, like the young of the flock?

Hebrew Trans: Tremble, O earth, before the Lord, before the God of Jacob,
Septuagint Trans: At the presence of the Lord the earth was shaken, at the presence of the God of Jacob,
Gallican Psalter Trans: At the face of the Lord the earth was moved, at the face of the God of Jacob,
Hebrew Psalter Trans: At the presence of the Lord tremble, O earth, at the presence of the God of Jacob,

Hebrew Trans: Who turns the rock into a pool of water, the flint into a fountain of water.
Septuagint Trans: Who turned the rock into pools of water, and the flint into springs of water.
Gallican Psalter Trans: who turned the rock into pools of waters, and the cliff into fountains of waters.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: who turned the rock into pools of waters, the flint into fountains of waters.

Hebrew Trans: Not unto us, O LORD, not unto us, but unto your name give glory, for your lovingkindness, for your truth.
Septuagint Trans: Not to us, Lord, not to us, but to your name give glory, for your mercy and your truth.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Not to us, O Lord, not to us, but to your name give glory, for your mercy and your truth.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Not to us, O Lord, not to us, but to your name give glory, on account of your mercy and your truth.

Hebrew Trans: Why should the nations say, "Where now is their God?"
Septuagint Trans: Lest the nations should say, "Where is their God?"
Gallican Psalter Trans: Lest the nations should say, "Where is their God?"
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Lest the nations say, "Where is their God?"

Hebrew Trans: But our God is in the heavens; all that he pleased, he has done.
Septuagint Trans: But our God is in heaven above, in the heavens and on the earth; all things whatsoever he willed, he did.
Gallican Psalter Trans: But our God is in heaven; he has done all things whatsoever he willed.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: But our God is in heaven; all things whatsoever he willed, he has done.

Hebrew Trans: Their idols are silver and gold, the work of the hands of man.
Septuagint Trans: The idols of the nations are silver and gold, works of the hands of men.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The idols of the nations are silver and gold, the works of the hands of men.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The idols of the nations are silver and gold, the work of the hands of men.

Hebrew Trans: They have mouths, but they do not speak; they have eyes, but they do not see.
Septuagint Trans: They have a mouth and will not speak; they have eyes and will not see.
Gallican Psalter Trans: They have mouths and shall not speak; they have eyes and shall not see.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: They have a mouth and shall not speak; they have eyes and shall not see.

Hebrew Trans: They have ears, but they do not hear; they have noses, but they do not smell.
Septuagint Trans: They have ears and will not hear; they have noses and will not smell.
Gallican Psalter Trans: They have ears and shall not hear; they have noses and shall not smell.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: They have ears and shall not hear; they have a nose and shall not smell.

Hebrew Trans: They have hands, but they do not feel; they have feet, but they do not walk; they do not utter a sound with their throat.
Septuagint Trans: They have hands and will not feel; they have feet and will not walk; they will not utter a sound in their throat.
Gallican Psalter Trans: They have hands and shall not feel; they have feet and shall not walk; they shall not cry out in their throat.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: They have hands and shall not feel; they have feet and shall not walk; nor shall they make a sound in their throat.

Hebrew Trans: Like them shall be those who make them, all who trust in them.
Septuagint Trans: May those who make them become like them, and all who trust in them.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Let those who make them become like them, and all who trust in them.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Let those who make them become like them, and all who trust in them.

Hebrew Trans: O Israel, trust in the LORD; he is their help and their shield.
Septuagint Trans: The house of Israel has hoped in the Lord; he is their helper and their protector.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The house of Israel has hoped in the Lord; he is their helper and their protector.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Israel trusts in the Lord; he is their helper and protector.

Hebrew Trans: O house of Aaron, trust in the LORD; he is their help and their shield.
Septuagint Trans: The house of Aaron has hoped in the Lord; he is their helper and their protector.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The house of Aaron has hoped in the Lord; he is their helper and their protector.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The house of Aaron trusts in the Lord; he is their helper and protector.

Hebrew Trans: You who fear the LORD, trust in the LORD; he is their help and their shield.
Septuagint Trans: Those who fear the Lord have hoped in the Lord; he is their helper and their protector.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Those who fear the Lord have hoped in the Lord; he is their helper and their protector.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Those who fear the Lord trust in the Lord; he is their helper and protector.

Hebrew Trans: The LORD has remembered us; he will bless; he will bless the house of Israel; he will bless the house of Aaron.
Septuagint Trans: The Lord has remembered us and has blessed us; he has blessed the house of Israel; he has blessed the house of Aaron.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The Lord has been mindful of us, and has blessed us. He has blessed the house of Israel; he has blessed the house of Aaron.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The Lord has been mindful of us and will bless us; he will bless the house of Israel; he will bless the house of Aaron.

Hebrew Trans: He will bless those who fear the LORD, the small together with the great.
Septuagint Trans: He has blessed those who fear the Lord, the small together with the great.
Gallican Psalter Trans: He has blessed all who fear the Lord, the small together with the great.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: He will bless those who fear the Lord, the small and the great.

Hebrew Trans: May the LORD increase you, you and your children.
Septuagint Trans: May the Lord add unto you, unto you and unto your children.
Gallican Psalter Trans: May the Lord add blessings upon you, upon you and upon your children.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: May the Lord add blessings upon you, upon you and upon your children.

Hebrew Trans: Blessed are you of the LORD, the maker of heaven and earth.
Septuagint Trans: Blessed are you by the Lord, who made heaven and earth.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Blessed are you by the Lord, who made heaven and earth.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Blessed are you by the Lord, who made heaven and earth.

Hebrew Trans: The heavens are the heavens of the LORD, but the earth he has given to the children of men.
Septuagint Trans: The heaven of heaven belongs to the Lord, but the earth he has given to the sons of men.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The heaven of heavens is the Lord's, but the earth he has given to the children of men.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The heaven of heavens belongs to the Lord, but the earth he has given to the children of men.

Hebrew Trans: The dead do not praise the LORD, nor any who go down into silence.
Septuagint Trans: The dead will not praise you, Lord, nor all those who go down into Hades.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The dead shall not praise you, O Lord, nor shall any who descend into hell.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The dead shall not praise the Lord, nor all those who descend into silence.

Hebrew Trans: But we will bless the LORD from this time forth and forevermore. Praise the LORD!
Septuagint Trans: But we, the living, will bless the Lord from now and unto the age.
Gallican Psalter Trans: But we who live bless the Lord, from this time now and forever.
