Hebrew Psalter Trans: But we bless the Lord, from this time forth and forever. Alleluia.

Hebrew Trans: I love [the LORD], for the LORD hears my voice, my supplications.
Septuagint Trans: Alleluia. I loved, because the Lord will hear the voice of my supplication.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Alleluia. I have loved, because the Lord will hear the voice of my prayer.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: I have loved, because the Lord will hear the voice of my supplication.

Hebrew Trans: For He has inclined His ear to me, and in my days I will call [upon Him].
Septuagint Trans: Because he inclined his ear to me, and in my days I will call upon him.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Because he has inclined his ear to me, and in my days I will call upon you.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: He inclined his ear to me, and in my days I will call upon him.

Hebrew Trans: The cords of death encompassed me, and the straits of Sheol found me; I found trouble and sorrow.
Septuagint Trans: The pangs of death encompassed me, the dangers of Hades found me; I found affliction and pain.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The sorrows of death surrounded me, the perils of hell found me; I found tribulation and sorrow.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The cords of death surrounded me, and the fortifications of hell found me; I found anguish and sorrow.

Hebrew Trans: And I called upon the name of the LORD: "O LORD, I beseech You, deliver my soul."
Septuagint Trans: And I called upon the name of the Lord: O Lord, deliver my soul.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And I called upon the name of the Lord: O Lord, deliver my soul.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And I called upon the name of the Lord: I beseech you, O Lord, save my soul.

Hebrew Trans: Gracious is the LORD, and righteous; and our God is merciful.
Septuagint Trans: Merciful is the Lord and righteous, and our God has compassion.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The Lord is merciful and just, and our God shows mercy.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The Lord is merciful and just, and our God is compassionate.

Hebrew Trans: The LORD preserves the simple; I was brought low, and He saved me.
Septuagint Trans: The Lord preserves the simple; I was brought low, and he saved me.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The Lord is the keeper of little ones: I was humbled, and he delivered me.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The Lord guards the little ones: I was brought low, and he saved me.

Hebrew Trans: Return, O my soul, to your rest; for the LORD has dealt bountifully with you.
Septuagint Trans: Return, O my soul, to your rest, for the Lord has dealt bountifully with you.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Return, O my soul, to your rest, for the Lord has been bountiful to you.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Return, my soul, to your rest, for the Lord will repay you.

Hebrew Trans: For You have delivered my soul from death, my eyes from tears, my feet from stumbling.
Septuagint Trans: For he delivered my soul from death, my eyes from tears, and my feet from stumbling.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For he has rescued my soul from death, my eyes from tears, my feet from falling.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For he has rescued my soul from death, my eyes from tears, my feet from stumbling.

Hebrew Trans: I shall walk before the LORD in the lands of the living.
Septuagint Trans: I shall be pleasing before the Lord in the land of the living.
Gallican Psalter Trans: I will please the Lord in the land of the living.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: I will walk before the Lord in the lands of the living.

Hebrew Trans: I believed, even when I spoke: "I am greatly afflicted."
Septuagint Trans: Alleluia. I believed, therefore I spoke; but I was greatly humbled.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Alleluia. I believed, for which reason I have spoken; but I have been humbled exceedingly.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: I believed, for which reason I spoke: I am afflicted exceedingly.

Hebrew Trans: I said in my haste: "All men are liars."
Septuagint Trans: I said in my alarm: every man is a liar.
Gallican Psalter Trans: I said in my excess: every man is a liar.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: I said in my alarm: every man is a liar.

Hebrew Trans: What shall I render to the LORD for all His benefits toward me?
Septuagint Trans: What shall I render to the Lord for all that he has rendered to me?
Gallican Psalter Trans: What shall I render to the Lord for all the things that he has rendered to me?
Hebrew Psalter Trans: What shall I render to the Lord for all the things he has bestowed upon me?

Hebrew Trans: I will lift up the cup of salvations, and call upon the name of the LORD.
Septuagint Trans: I will take up the cup of salvation and call upon the name of the Lord.
Gallican Psalter Trans: I will take the chalice of salvation, and I will call upon the name of the Lord.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: I will take the cup of salvation, and I will call upon the name of the Lord.

Hebrew Trans: My vows to the LORD I will pay, now in the presence of all His people.
Septuagint Trans: [dummy verse inserted by Amos]
Gallican Psalter Trans: I will pay my vows to the Lord before all his people.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: I will pay my vows to the Lord before all his people.

Hebrew Trans: Precious in the eyes of the LORD is the death of His faithful ones.
Septuagint Trans: Precious before the Lord is the death of his holy ones.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Precious in the sight of the Lord is the death of his saints.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Precious in the sight of the Lord is the death of his saints.

Hebrew Trans: O LORD, truly I am Your servant; I am Your servant, the son of Your handmaid; You have loosed my bonds.
Septuagint Trans: O Lord, I am your servant; I am your servant and the son of your handmaid. You have broken my bonds.
Gallican Psalter Trans: O Lord, for I am your servant; I am your servant and the son of your handmaid. You have broken my bonds.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: I beseech you, O Lord, for I am your servant: I am your servant, the son of your handmaid. You have loosed my bonds.

Hebrew Trans: To You I will offer a sacrifice of thanksgiving, and I will call upon the name of the LORD.
Septuagint Trans: To you I will offer a sacrifice of praise.
Gallican Psalter Trans: To you I will sacrifice a sacrifice of praise, and I will call upon the name of the Lord.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: To you I will sacrifice the offering of praise, and I will call upon the name of the Lord.

Hebrew Trans: My vows to the LORD I will pay, now in the presence of all His people.
Septuagint Trans: I will pay my vows to the Lord before all his people,
Gallican Psalter Trans: I will pay my vows to the Lord in the sight of all his people,
Hebrew Psalter Trans: I will pay my vows to the Lord in the sight of all his people,

Hebrew Trans: In the courts of the house of the LORD, in your midst, O Jerusalem. Praise the LORD!
Septuagint Trans: In the courts of the house of the Lord, in your midst, O Jerusalem.
Gallican Psalter Trans: in the courts of the house of the Lord, in your midst, O Jerusalem.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: in the courts of the house of the Lord, in the midst of you, O Jerusalem. Alleluia.

Hebrew Trans: Praise the LORD, all nations; laud Him, all peoples.
Septuagint Trans: Hallelujah! Praise the Lord, all nations; extol him, all peoples.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Alleluia. Praise the Lord, all nations; praise him, all peoples.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Praise the Lord, all nations; praise him together, all peoples.

Hebrew Trans: For His lovingkindness is mighty over us, and the truth of the LORD endures forever. Praise the LORD.
Septuagint Trans: For his mercy has been mighty upon us, and the truth of the Lord endures forever.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For his mercy is confirmed upon us, and the truth of the Lord endures forever.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For his mercy has been strengthened upon us, and the truth of the Lord endures forever. Alleluia.

Hebrew Trans: Give thanks to the LORD, for He is good, for His steadfast love endures forever.
Septuagint Trans: Hallelujah! Give thanks to the Lord, for he is good, for his mercy endures forever.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Alleluia. Give thanks to the Lord, for he is good, for his mercy endures forever.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Give thanks to the Lord, for He is good, for His mercy endures forever.

Hebrew Trans: Let Israel now say, for His steadfast love endures forever.
Septuagint Trans: Let the house of Israel now say that he is good, for his mercy endures forever.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Let Israel now say, for he is good, for his mercy endures forever.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Let Israel now say, for His mercy endures forever.

Hebrew Trans: Let the house of Aaron now say, for His steadfast love endures forever.
Septuagint Trans: Let the house of Aaron now say that he is good, for his mercy endures forever.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Let the house of Aaron now say, for his mercy endures forever.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Let the house of Aaron say, for His mercy endures forever.

Hebrew Trans: Let those who fear the LORD now say, for His steadfast love endures forever.
Septuagint Trans: Let all who fear the Lord now say that he is good, for his mercy endures forever.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Let those who fear the Lord now say, for his mercy endures forever.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Let those who fear the Lord say, for His mercy endures forever.

Hebrew Trans: Out of the straits I called upon the LORD; the LORD answered me in a broad place.
Septuagint Trans: In distress I called upon the Lord, and he heard me and brought me into a broad place.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Out of tribulation I called upon the Lord, and the Lord heard me in a broad place.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: In my tribulation I called upon the Lord, and the Lord heard me and set me in a broad place.

Hebrew Trans: The LORD is for me; I will not fear. What can man do to me?
Septuagint Trans: The Lord is my helper; I shall not fear. What shall man do to me?
Gallican Psalter Trans: The Lord is my helper; I will not fear what man may do to me.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The Lord is mine; I will not fear. What can man do to me?

Hebrew Trans: The LORD is for me among my helpers, and I shall look upon those who hate me.
Septuagint Trans: The Lord is my helper, and I shall look upon my enemies.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The Lord is my helper, and I shall look down upon my enemies.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The Lord is my helper, and I shall look down upon those who hate me.

Hebrew Trans: It is better to take refuge in the LORD than to trust in man.
Septuagint Trans: It is better to trust in the Lord than to trust in man.
Gallican Psalter Trans: It is better to trust in the Lord than to trust in man.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: It is better to trust in the Lord than to trust in man.

Hebrew Trans: It is better to take refuge in the LORD than to trust in princes.
Septuagint Trans: It is better to hope in the Lord than to hope in princes.
Gallican Psalter Trans: It is better to hope in the Lord than to hope in princes.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: It is better to trust in the Lord than to trust in princes.

Hebrew Trans: All nations surrounded me; in the name of the LORD I will cut them off.
Septuagint Trans: All the nations surrounded me, and in the name of the Lord I fended them off.
Gallican Psalter Trans: All the nations surrounded me, and in the name of the Lord I took vengeance upon them.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: All the nations surrounded me, and in the name of the Lord I took vengeance upon them.

Hebrew Trans: They surrounded me, yes, they surrounded me; in the name of the LORD I will cut them off.
Septuagint Trans: Surrounding me they surrounded me, and in the name of the Lord I fended them off.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Surrounding me, they encircled me, but in the name of the Lord I took vengeance upon them.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: They surrounded me and besieged me, but in the name of the Lord I took vengeance upon them.

Hebrew Trans: They surrounded me like bees; they were extinguished like a fire of thorns; in the name of the LORD I will cut them off.
Septuagint Trans: They surrounded me like bees around a honeycomb, and they were kindled like fire among thorns, and in the name of the Lord I fended them off.
Gallican Psalter Trans: They surrounded me like bees, and they blazed like fire among thorns, and in the name of the Lord I took vengeance upon them.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: They surrounded me like bees; they were extinguished like a fire of thorns; in the name of the Lord, for I took vengeance upon them.

Hebrew Trans: You pushed me hard so that I was falling, but the LORD helped me.
Septuagint Trans: I was pushed and overturned so as to fall, but the Lord upheld me.
Gallican Psalter Trans: I was pushed and overturned so that I might fall, but the Lord upheld me.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: I was pushed and driven so that I might fall, but the Lord sustained me.

Hebrew Trans: The LORD is my strength and my song, and He has become my salvation.
Septuagint Trans: The Lord is my strength and my song, and he has become my salvation.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The Lord is my strength and my praise, and he has become my salvation.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The Lord is my strength and my praise, and He has become my salvation.

Hebrew Trans: The sound of joyful singing and salvation is in the tents of the righteous; the right hand of the LORD does valiantly.
Septuagint Trans: The sound of rejoicing and salvation is in the tents of the righteous; the right hand of the Lord has done mightily.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The voice of rejoicing and salvation is in the tents of the righteous; the right hand of the Lord has done valiantly.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The voice of praise and salvation is in the tents of the righteous; the right hand of the Lord has wrought strength.

Hebrew Trans: The right hand of the LORD is exalted; the right hand of the LORD does valiantly.
Septuagint Trans: The right hand of the Lord has exalted me; the right hand of the Lord has done mightily.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The right hand of the Lord has exalted me; the right hand of the Lord has done valiantly.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The right hand of the Lord is exalted; the right hand of the Lord has wrought strength.

Hebrew Trans: I shall not die, but I shall live, and I shall recount the deeds of the LORD.
Septuagint Trans: I shall not die, but I shall live and recount the works of the Lord.
Gallican Psalter Trans: I shall not die, but I shall live, and I shall declare the works of the Lord.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: I shall not die, but I shall live, and I shall declare the works of the Lord.

Hebrew Trans: The LORD has surely chastened me, but He has not given me over to death.
Septuagint Trans: The Lord has surely chastened me, but he has not given me over to death.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Chastising, the Lord has chastised me, but he has not handed me over to death.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The Lord has chastened and corrected me, but He has not delivered me over to death.

Hebrew Trans: Open to me the gates of righteousness; I will enter through them and give thanks to the LORD.
Septuagint Trans: Open to me the gates of righteousness; entering through them I shall give thanks to the Lord.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Open to me the gates of justice; having entered through them, I shall give thanks to the Lord.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Open to me the gates of justice; having entered them, I will give thanks to the Lord.

Hebrew Trans: This is the gate of the LORD; the righteous shall enter through it.
Septuagint Trans: This is the gate of the Lord; the righteous shall enter through it.
Gallican Psalter Trans: This is the gate of the Lord; the righteous shall enter into it.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: This is the gate of the Lord; the just shall enter into it.

Hebrew Trans: I will give thanks to You, for You have answered me, and You have become my salvation.
Septuagint Trans: I shall give thanks to you, for you heard me and became my salvation.
Gallican Psalter Trans: I shall give thanks to you, for you have heard me, and you have become my salvation.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: I will give thanks to You, for You have heard me, and You have become my salvation.

Hebrew Trans: The stone which the builders rejected has become the chief cornerstone.
Septuagint Trans: The stone which the builders rejected, this has become the head of the corner.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The stone which the builders rejected, this has become the head of the corner.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The stone which the builders rejected has become the head of the corner.

Hebrew Trans: This is from the LORD; it is marvelous in our eyes.
Septuagint Trans: This came about from the Lord, and it is marvelous in our eyes.
Gallican Psalter Trans: By the Lord this was done; it is wonderful in our eyes.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: By the Lord has this been done, and it is wonderful in our eyes.

Hebrew Trans: This is the day that the LORD has made; let us rejoice and be glad in it.
Septuagint Trans: This is the day which the Lord has made; let us rejoice and be glad in it.
Gallican Psalter Trans: This is the day which the Lord has made; let us rejoice and be glad in it.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: This is the day which the Lord has made; let us exult and rejoice in it.

Hebrew Trans: Please, O LORD, save now! Please, O LORD, grant prosperity now!
Septuagint Trans: O Lord, save now! O Lord, grant prosperity now!
Gallican Psalter Trans: O Lord, save us; O Lord, grant prosperity.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: I beseech You, O Lord, save, I beseech; I beseech You, O Lord, grant prosperity, I beseech.

Hebrew Trans: Blessed is he who comes in the name of the LORD; we bless you from the house of the LORD.
Septuagint Trans: Blessed is he who comes in the name of the Lord; we have blessed you from the house of the Lord.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Blessed is he who is to come in the name of the Lord; we have blessed you from the house of the Lord.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Blessed is he who comes in the name of the Lord; we have blessed you from the house of the Lord.

Hebrew Trans: The LORD is God, and He has given us light; bind the festival offering with cords to the horns of the altar.
Septuagint Trans: God is the Lord, and he has shone upon us. Establish a feast with branches, up to the horns of the altar.
Gallican Psalter Trans: God is the Lord, and he has shone upon us. Appoint the solemn day with thick branches, even unto the horns of the altar.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: God is the Lord, and He has appeared to us. Celebrate the solemn feast with leafy branches, up to the horns of the altar.

Hebrew Trans: You are my God, and I will give thanks to You; You are my God, I will exalt You.
Septuagint Trans: You are my God, and I shall give thanks to you; you are my God, and I shall exalt you. I shall give thanks to you, for you heard me and became my salvation.
Gallican Psalter Trans: You are my God, and I shall give thanks to you; you are my God, and I shall exalt you. I shall give thanks to you, for you have heard me, and you have become my salvation.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: You are my God, and I will give thanks to You; You are my God, and I will exalt You.

Hebrew Trans: Give thanks to the LORD, for He is good, for His steadfast love endures forever.
Septuagint Trans: Give thanks to the Lord, for he is good, for his mercy endures forever.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Give thanks to the Lord, for he is good, for his mercy endures forever.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Give thanks to the Lord, for He is good, for His mercy endures forever.

Hebrew Trans: Happy are those whose way is blameless, who walk in the Torah of the LORD.
Septuagint Trans: Hallelujah! Blessed are the blameless in the way, who walk in the law of the Lord.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Alleluia. Blessed are the undefiled in the way, who walk in the law of the Lord.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Blessed are the undefiled in the way, who walk in the law of the Lord.

Hebrew Trans: Happy are those who keep His testimonies, who seek Him with all their heart.
Septuagint Trans: Blessed are those who search out his testimonies; with their whole heart they shall seek him.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Blessed are they who search his testimonies; with their whole heart they shall seek him.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Blessed are they who keep his testimonies, who seek him with their whole heart.

Hebrew Trans: Indeed, they do no iniquity; they walk in His ways.
Septuagint Trans: For those who work lawlessness have not walked in his ways.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For they who work iniquity have not walked in his ways.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For neither have they who work iniquity walked in his ways.

Hebrew Trans: You have commanded Your precepts to be kept diligently.
Septuagint Trans: You have commanded your commandments to be kept diligently.
Gallican Psalter Trans: You have commanded your precepts to be kept exceedingly.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: You have commanded your precepts to be kept diligently.

Hebrew Trans: Oh that my ways were established to keep Your statutes!
Septuagint Trans: Would that my ways were directed to keep your statutes!
Gallican Psalter Trans: O that my ways may be directed to keep your statutes!
Hebrew Psalter Trans: O that my ways may be directed to keep your precepts!

Hebrew Trans: Then I would not be ashamed, when I look upon all Your commandments.
Septuagint Trans: Then I shall not be put to shame, when I look upon all your commandments.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Then I shall not be confounded, when I shall look into all your commandments.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Then I shall not be confounded, when I shall look upon all your commandments.

Hebrew Trans: I will give thanks to You with uprightness of heart, when I learn Your righteous judgments.
Septuagint Trans: I will give thanks to you, O Lord, in uprightness of heart, when I have learned the judgments of your righteousness.
Gallican Psalter Trans: I will praise you with uprightness of heart, in that I have learned the judgments of your justice.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: I will praise you with uprightness of heart, when I shall have learned the judgments of your justice.

Hebrew Trans: I will keep Your statutes; do not forsake me utterly.
Septuagint Trans: I will keep your statutes; do not utterly forsake me.
Gallican Psalter Trans: I will keep your statutes; do not utterly forsake me.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: I will keep your precepts; do not utterly forsake me.

Hebrew Trans: How shall a young man keep his path pure? By guarding it according to Your word.
Septuagint Trans: By what shall the young man correct his way? By keeping your words.
Gallican Psalter Trans: By what shall the young man correct his way? By keeping your words.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: By what does a young man correct his path? By keeping your words.

Hebrew Trans: With all my heart I have sought You; do not let me stray from Your commandments.
Septuagint Trans: With my whole heart I have sought you; do not thrust me away from your commandments.
Gallican Psalter Trans: With my whole heart I have sought you; do not repel me from your commandments.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: With my whole heart I have sought you; do not let me stray from your commandments.

Hebrew Trans: In my heart I have treasured Your word, so that I might not sin against You.
Septuagint Trans: In my heart I have hidden your oracles, so that I might not sin against you.
Gallican Psalter Trans: In my heart I have hidden your words, that I may not sin against you.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: In my heart I have hidden your word, that I may not sin against you.

Hebrew Trans: Blessed are You, O LORD; teach me Your statutes.
Septuagint Trans: Blessed are you, O Lord; teach me your statutes.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Blessed are you, O Lord; teach me your statutes.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Blessed are you, O Lord; teach me your precepts.

Hebrew Trans: With my lips I have declared all the judgments of Your mouth.
Septuagint Trans: With my lips I have declared all the judgments of your mouth.
Gallican Psalter Trans: With my lips I have declared all the judgments of your mouth.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: With my lips I have declared all the judgments of your mouth.

Hebrew Trans: In the way of Your testimonies I have rejoiced, as over all wealth.
Septuagint Trans: In the way of your testimonies I have delighted, as in all riches.
Gallican Psalter Trans: In the way of your testimonies I have delighted, as in all riches.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: In the way of your testimonies I have rejoiced, as in all riches.

Hebrew Trans: I will meditate on Your precepts and gaze upon Your paths.
Septuagint Trans: I will meditate on your commandments and consider your ways.
Gallican Psalter Trans: I will be exercised in your commandments, and I will consider your ways.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: I will meditate on your precepts and contemplate your paths.

Hebrew Trans: I will delight myself in Your statutes; I will not forget Your word.
Septuagint Trans: I will study your statutes; I will not forget your words.
Gallican Psalter Trans: I will meditate on your statutes; I will not forget your words.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: I will delight in your statutes; I will not forget your words.

Hebrew Trans: Deal bountifully with Your servant, that I may live and keep Your word.
Septuagint Trans: Deal bountifully with your servant; I shall live and keep your words.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Repay your servant; give me life, and I shall keep your words.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Deal bountifully with your servant, that I may live and keep your words.

Hebrew Trans: Open my eyes, that I may behold wondrous things from Your Torah.
Septuagint Trans: Unveil my eyes, and I shall perceive the wonders from your law.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Unveil my eyes, and I shall consider the wondrous things of your law.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Open my eyes, and I will behold wondrous things in your law.

Hebrew Trans: I am a sojourner in the land; do not hide Your commandments from me.
Septuagint Trans: I am a sojourner in the earth; do not hide your commandments from me.
Gallican Psalter Trans: I am a sojourner on the earth; do not hide your commandments from me.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: I am a stranger in the land; do not hide your commandments from me.

Hebrew Trans: My soul is crushed with longing for Your judgments at all times.
Septuagint Trans: My soul has longed to desire your judgments at all times.
Gallican Psalter Trans: My soul has longed to desire your statutes at all times.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: My soul has longed to desire your judgments at all times.

Hebrew Trans: You have rebuked the proud, the accursed, who stray from Your commandments.
Septuagint Trans: You have rebuked the proud; cursed are those who turn aside from your commandments.
Gallican Psalter Trans: You have rebuked the proud; cursed are they who turn aside from your commandments.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: You have rebuked the proud; cursed are they who depart from your commandments.

Hebrew Trans: Remove from me reproach and contempt, for I have kept Your testimonies.
Septuagint Trans: Take away from me reproach and contempt, for I have sought out your testimonies.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Remove from me reproach and contempt, for I have sought your testimonies.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Remove from me reproach and contempt, for I have kept your testimonies.

Hebrew Trans: Even though princes sit and speak against me, Your servant meditates on Your statutes.
Septuagint Trans: For indeed rulers sat and spoke against me, but your servant was meditating on your statutes.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For indeed princes sat and spoke against me; but your servant was exercised in your statutes.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For even princes sat and spoke against me, but your servant meditated on your precepts.

Hebrew Trans: Your testimonies also are my delight, the men of my counsel.
Septuagint Trans: For indeed your testimonies are my meditation, and your statutes are my counselors.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For your testimonies are my meditation, and your statutes are my counsel.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For your testimonies are my delight, as men who are my closest friends.

Hebrew Trans: My soul clings to the dust; revive me according to Your word.
Septuagint Trans: My soul has cleaved to the ground; give me life according to your word.
Gallican Psalter Trans: My soul has cleaved to the pavement; give me life according to your word.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: My soul has clung to the dust; revive me according to your word.

Hebrew Trans: I have declared my ways and You answered me; teach me Your statutes.
Septuagint Trans: I declared my ways and you heard me; teach me your statutes.
Gallican Psalter Trans: I have declared my ways, and you have heard me; teach me your statutes.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: I declared my ways and you heard me; teach me your statutes.

Hebrew Trans: Make me understand the way of Your precepts, and I will meditate on Your wondrous works.
Septuagint Trans: Make me understand the way of your statutes, and I shall meditate on your wonders.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Make me understand the way of your statutes, and I shall be exercised in your wondrous works.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Make me understand the way of your precepts, and I will speak of your wondrous works.

Hebrew Trans: My soul weeps from grief; strengthen me according to Your word.
Septuagint Trans: My soul has dripped from weariness; strengthen me with your words.
Gallican Psalter Trans: My soul has slumbered from weariness; strengthen me in your words.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: My soul has melted away from sorrow; sustain me according to your word.

Hebrew Trans: Remove from me the way of falsehood, and graciously grant me Your Torah.
Septuagint Trans: Remove from me the way of unrighteousness, and by your law have mercy on me.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Remove from me the way of iniquity, and by your law have mercy on me.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Remove from me the way of falsehood, and graciously grant me your law.

Hebrew Trans: I have chosen the way of faithfulness; Your judgments I have set before me.
Septuagint Trans: I have chosen the way of truth; I have not forgotten your judgments.
Gallican Psalter Trans: I have chosen the way of truth; your judgments I have not forgotten.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: I have chosen the way of faithfulness; I have set your judgments before me.

Hebrew Trans: I cling to Your testimonies; O LORD, do not put me to shame.
Septuagint Trans: I have clung to your testimonies, O Lord; do not put me to shame.
Gallican Psalter Trans: I have clung to your testimonies, O Lord; do not put me to shame.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: I have clung to your testimonies, O Lord; do not put me to shame.

Hebrew Trans: I will run the way of Your commandments, for You will enlarge my heart.
Septuagint Trans: I ran the way of your commandments, when you enlarged my heart.
Gallican Psalter Trans: I have run the way of your commandments, when you enlarged my heart.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: I will run the way of your commandments, for you have enlarged my heart.

Hebrew Trans: Teach me, O LORD, the way of Your statutes, and I shall keep it to the end.
Septuagint Trans: Set before me as law, O Lord, the way of your statutes, and I shall seek it continually.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Set before me a law, O Lord, the way of your statutes, and I shall seek it always.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Show me, O Lord, the way of your precepts, and I will keep it to the very end.

Hebrew Trans: Give me understanding, and I shall keep Your Torah and observe it with all my heart.
Septuagint Trans: Give me understanding, and I shall search out your law and keep it with my whole heart.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Give me understanding, and I shall search your law, and I shall keep it with my whole heart.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Teach me and I will observe your law, and I will keep it with my whole heart.

Hebrew Trans: Make me walk in the path of Your commandments, for in it I delight.
Septuagint Trans: Guide me in the path of your commandments, for I have desired it.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Lead me in the path of your commandments, for I have desired it.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Lead me in the path of your commandments, for that is what I have desired.

Hebrew Trans: Incline my heart to Your testimonies, and not to unjust gain.
Septuagint Trans: Incline my heart to your testimonies and not to covetousness.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Incline my heart to your testimonies, and not to covetousness.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Incline my heart to your testimonies, and not to covetousness.

Hebrew Trans: Turn my eyes away from looking at vanity; revive me in Your way.
Septuagint Trans: Turn away my eyes from seeing vanity; give me life in your way.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Turn away my eyes, lest they see vanity; in your way give me life.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Turn away my eyes lest they see vanity; revive me in your way.

Hebrew Trans: Establish Your word to Your servant, which is for those who fear You.
Septuagint Trans: Establish your oracle to your servant, unto the fear of you.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Establish your word to your servant, in your fear.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Establish your word to your servant, in your fear.

Hebrew Trans: Take away my reproach which I dread, for Your judgments are good.
Septuagint Trans: Take away my reproach, which I have dreaded, for your judgments are good.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Cut away my reproach, which I have feared, for your judgments are delightful.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Turn away my reproach, which I have dreaded, for your judgments are good.

Hebrew Trans: Behold, I long for Your precepts; in Your righteousness revive me.
Septuagint Trans: Behold, I have desired your commandments; give me life in your righteousness.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Behold, I have longed for your commandments; in your equity give me life.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Behold, I have longed for your precepts; in your justice revive me.

Hebrew Trans: And let Your lovingkindness come to me, O LORD, Your salvation according to Your word.
Septuagint Trans: And let your mercy come upon me, O Lord, your salvation according to your oracle.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And let your mercy come upon me, O Lord, your salvation according to your word.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And let your mercies come to me, O Lord, and your salvation according to your word.

Hebrew Trans: So I will answer him who reproaches me a word, for I trust in Your word.
Septuagint Trans: And I shall answer those who reproach me with a word, for I have hoped in your words.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And I shall answer a word to those who reproach me, for I have trusted in your words.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And I will answer him who reproaches me with a word, for I have trusted in your word.

Hebrew Trans: And do not take the word of truth utterly from my mouth, for I wait for Your judgment.
Septuagint Trans: And do not utterly take the word of truth from my mouth, for I have hoped in your judgments.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And do not utterly take the word of truth from my mouth, for in your judgments I have greatly hoped.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And do not take utterly from my mouth the word of truth, for I have waited for your judgments.

Hebrew Trans: And I will keep Your Torah continually, forever and ever.
Septuagint Trans: And I shall keep your law continually, forever and unto the age of ages.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And I shall keep your law always, forever and ever.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And I will keep your law continually, forever and ever.

Hebrew Trans: And I will walk in a broad place, for I seek Your precepts.
Septuagint Trans: And I walked in a broad place, for I sought out your commandments.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And I walked in a broad place, for I sought your commandments.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And I will walk in a broad place, for I have sought your precepts.

Hebrew Trans: And I will speak of Your testimonies before kings and will not be ashamed.
Septuagint Trans: And I spoke of your testimonies before kings and was not ashamed.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And I spoke of your testimonies in the sight of kings, and I was not ashamed.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And I will speak of your testimonies before kings, and I will not be confounded.

Hebrew Trans: And I will delight myself in Your commandments, which I love.
Septuagint Trans: And I meditated on your commandments, which I loved exceedingly.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And I meditated on your commandments, which I loved.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And I will delight in your commandments, which I have loved.

Hebrew Trans: And I will lift up my hands to Your commandments, which I love, and I will meditate on Your statutes.
Septuagint Trans: And I lifted up my hands to your commandments, which I loved, and I meditated on your statutes.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And I lifted up my hands to your commandments, which I loved, and I was exercised in your statutes.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And I will lift up my hands to your commandments, which I have loved, and I will meditate on your precepts.

Hebrew Trans: Remember the word to Your servant, upon which You have caused me to hope.
Septuagint Trans: Remember your word to your servant, in which you gave me hope.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Remember your word to your servant, in which you have given me hope.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Remember the word to your servant, in which you have made me hope.

Hebrew Trans: This is my comfort in my affliction, that Your word has revived me.
Septuagint Trans: This comforted me in my humiliation, for your oracle gave me life.
Gallican Psalter Trans: This has comforted me in my humiliation, for your word has given me life.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: This is my consolation in my affliction, for your word has given me life.

Hebrew Trans: The proud have utterly scorned me; from Your Torah I have not turned aside.
Septuagint Trans: The proud transgressed exceedingly, but from your law I did not turn aside.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The proud acted utterly unjustly; but from your law I did not turn aside.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The proud derided me exceedingly; I have not turned aside from your law.

Hebrew Trans: I remembered Your judgments of old, O LORD, and I comforted myself.
Septuagint Trans: I remembered your judgments from of old, O Lord, and was comforted.
Gallican Psalter Trans: I remembered your judgments of old, O Lord, and I was consoled.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: I remembered your judgments of old, O Lord, and I was consoled.

Hebrew Trans: Burning indignation has seized me because of the wicked who forsake Your Torah.
Septuagint Trans: Despondency seized me because of sinners who forsake your law.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Faintness seized me because of the sinners who forsake your law.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Horror seized me because of the wicked who forsake your law.

Hebrew Trans: Your statutes have been my songs in the house of my sojourning.
Septuagint Trans: Your statutes were songs to me in the place of my sojourning.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Your statutes were songs to me in the place of my pilgrimage.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Your precepts were songs to me in the house of my pilgrimage.

Hebrew Trans: I have remembered Your name in the night, O LORD, and I have kept Your Torah.
Septuagint Trans: I remembered your name in the night, O Lord, and I kept your law.
Gallican Psalter Trans: I remembered your name in the night, O Lord, and I kept your law.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: I remembered your name in the night, O Lord, and I kept your law.

Hebrew Trans: This has been mine, for I have kept Your precepts.
Septuagint Trans: This came to be mine, because I sought out your statutes.
Gallican Psalter Trans: This happened to me because I sought your statutes.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: This has been done to me, because I kept your precepts.

Hebrew Trans: The LORD is my portion; I have said that I would keep Your words.
Septuagint Trans: You are my portion, O Lord; I said I would keep your law.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The Lord is my portion; I said I would keep your law.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: You are my portion, O Lord; I have said that I would keep your word.

Hebrew Trans: I have entreated Your face with all my heart; be gracious to me according to Your word.
Septuagint Trans: I entreated your face with my whole heart; have mercy on me according to your oracle.
Gallican Psalter Trans: I entreated your face with my whole heart; have mercy on me according to your word.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: I entreated your face with my whole heart; have mercy on me according to your word.

Hebrew Trans: I have considered my ways and turned my feet to Your testimonies.
Septuagint Trans: I considered my ways and turned my feet to your testimonies.
Gallican Psalter Trans: I considered my ways, and you turned my feet to your testimonies.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: I considered my ways, and turned my feet to your testimonies.

Hebrew Trans: I hastened and did not delay to keep Your commandments.
Septuagint Trans: I prepared myself and was not troubled, to keep your commandments.
Gallican Psalter Trans: I am prepared, and I am not troubled, to keep your commandments.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: I made haste and did not neglect to keep your commandments.

Hebrew Trans: The cords of the wicked have ensnared me; Your Torah I have not forgotten.
Septuagint Trans: The cords of sinners entangled me, and I did not forget your law.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The cords of sinners have entangled me, and I have not forgotten your law.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The cords of the wicked have entangled me; I have not forgotten your law.

Hebrew Trans: At midnight I rise to give thanks to You for Your righteous judgments.
Septuagint Trans: At midnight I arose to give thanks to you for the judgments of your righteousness.
Gallican Psalter Trans: At midnight I arose to give thanks to you for the judgments of your justice.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: At midnight I will rise to give thanks to you for the judgments of your righteousness.

Hebrew Trans: I am a companion of all who fear You and of those who keep Your precepts.
Septuagint Trans: I am a companion of all who fear you and of those who keep your commandments.
Gallican Psalter Trans: I am a partaker with all who fear you and who keep your commandments.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: I am a companion of all who fear you, and of those who keep your precepts.

Hebrew Trans: Your lovingkindness, O LORD, fills the earth; teach me Your statutes.
Septuagint Trans: Of your mercy, O Lord, the earth is full; teach me your statutes.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The earth is full of the mercy of the Lord; teach me your statutes.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The earth is full of your mercy; teach me your precepts.

Hebrew Trans: You have dealt well with Your servant, O LORD, according to Your word.
Septuagint Trans: You have dealt goodness with your servant, O Lord, according to your word.
Gallican Psalter Trans: You have dealt goodness with your servant, O Lord, according to your word.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: You have dealt well with your servant, O Lord, according to your word.

Hebrew Trans: Teach me good discernment and knowledge, for I believe in Your commandments.
Septuagint Trans: Teach me goodness and discipline and knowledge, for I have believed in your commandments.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Teach me goodness and discipline and knowledge, for I have believed your commandments.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Teach me good discernment and knowledge, for I have believed in your commandments.

Hebrew Trans: Before I was afflicted I went astray, but now I keep Your word.
Septuagint Trans: Before I was humbled, I transgressed; therefore I have kept your oracle.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Before I was humbled, I offended; therefore I have kept your word.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Before I heard, I went astray; but now I have kept your word.

Hebrew Trans: You are good and do good; teach me Your statutes.
Septuagint Trans: Good are you, O Lord, and in your goodness teach me your statutes.
Gallican Psalter Trans: You are good, and in your goodness teach me your statutes.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: You are good and beneficent; teach me your precepts.

Hebrew Trans: The proud have smeared a lie upon me; I with all my heart will keep Your precepts.
Septuagint Trans: The unrighteousness of the proud has been multiplied against me, but I with my whole heart will search out your commandments.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The iniquity of the proud has been multiplied over me; but I with my whole heart shall search your commandments.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The proud fabricated lies against me, but I with my whole heart kept your precepts.

Hebrew Trans: Their heart is gross like fat; I delight in Your Torah.
Septuagint Trans: Their heart has been curdled like milk, but I have meditated on your law.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Their heart has been curdled like milk; but I have meditated on your law.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Their heart has become gross like fat, but I delighted in your law.

Hebrew Trans: It is good for me that I was afflicted, so that I might learn Your statutes.
Septuagint Trans: It is good for me that you humbled me, so that I might learn your statutes.
Gallican Psalter Trans: It is good for me that you have humbled me, that I may learn your statutes.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: It is good for me that I was afflicted, that I might learn your precepts.

Hebrew Trans: The Torah of Your mouth is better for me than thousands of gold and silver.
Septuagint Trans: The law of your mouth is good to me, above thousands of gold and silver.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The law of your mouth is good to me, above thousands of gold and silver.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The law of your mouth is better to me than thousands of gold and silver.

Hebrew Trans: Your hands made me and established me; give me understanding that I may learn Your commandments.
Septuagint Trans: Your hands have made me and fashioned me; give me understanding, and I shall learn your commandments.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Your hands have made me and formed me; give me understanding, and I shall learn your commandments.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Your hands have made me and formed me; teach me, and I will learn your commandments.

Hebrew Trans: Those who fear You will see me and rejoice, for I have hoped in Your word.
Septuagint Trans: Those who fear you shall see me and be glad, for I have hoped in your words.
Gallican Psalter Trans: They who fear you shall see me and rejoice, for I have greatly hoped in your words.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Those who fear you will see me and rejoice, for I have waited for your word.

Hebrew Trans: I know, O LORD, that Your judgments are righteous, and in faithfulness You have afflicted me.
Septuagint Trans: I know, O Lord, that your judgments are righteousness, and in truth you humbled me.
Gallican Psalter Trans: I know, O Lord, that your judgments are equity, and in truth you have humbled me.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: I know, O Lord, that your judgment is just, and that in truth you have afflicted me.

Hebrew Trans: Let, I pray, Your lovingkindness comfort me, according to Your word to Your servant.
Septuagint Trans: Let your mercy now come to comfort me, according to your oracle to your servant.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Let your mercy be to comfort me, according to your word to your servant.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Let, I beseech you, your mercy be my consolation, as you have spoken to your servant.

Hebrew Trans: Let Your mercies come to me that I may live, for Your Torah is my delight.
Septuagint Trans: Let your compassions come to me, and I shall live, for your law is my meditation.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Let your compassions come to me, and I shall live, for your law is my meditation.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Let your mercies come to me, and I shall live, for your law is my delight.

Hebrew Trans: Let the proud be ashamed, for with falsehood they have perverted me; I will meditate on Your precepts.
Septuagint Trans: Let the proud be put to shame, for they have dealt unjustly and lawlessly against me; but I will meditate on your commandments.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Let the proud be confounded, for they have unjustly wrought iniquity against me; but I shall be exercised in your commandments.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Let the proud be confounded, for they have unjustly oppressed me; but I will speak of your precepts.

Hebrew Trans: Let those who fear You turn to me, and those who know Your testimonies.
Septuagint Trans: Let those who fear you turn to me, and those who know your testimonies.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Let those who fear you turn to me, and those who know your testimonies.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Let those who fear you turn to me, and those who know your testimony.

Hebrew Trans: Let my heart be blameless in Your statutes, so that I may not be ashamed.
Septuagint Trans: Let my heart be blameless in your statutes, so that I may not be put to shame.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Let my heart be undefiled in your statutes, that I may not be confounded.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Let my heart be perfect in your precepts, that I may not be confounded.

Hebrew Trans: My soul languishes for Your salvation; I hope in Your word.
Septuagint Trans: My soul faints for your salvation, and I have hoped in your word.
Gallican Psalter Trans: My soul has fainted for your salvation; in your word I have greatly hoped.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: My soul has fainted for your salvation; I have waited for your word.

Hebrew Trans: My eyes fail for Your word, saying, "When will You comfort me?"
Septuagint Trans: My eyes have failed for your oracle, saying, "When will you comfort me?"
Gallican Psalter Trans: My eyes have failed for your word, saying: When will you comfort me?
Hebrew Psalter Trans: My eyes have been consumed looking for your word, saying: when will you console me?

Hebrew Trans: For I have become like a wineskin in smoke; Your statutes I have not forgotten.
Septuagint Trans: For I have become like a wineskin in the frost; I have not forgotten your statutes.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For I have become like a wineskin in the frost; your statutes I have not forgotten.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And though I was like a wineskin in the frost, I have not forgotten your precepts.

Hebrew Trans: How many are the days of Your servant? When will You execute judgment on those who persecute me?
Septuagint Trans: How many are the days of your servant? When will you execute judgment on those who persecute me?
Gallican Psalter Trans: How many are the days of your servant? When will you execute judgment on those who persecute me?
Hebrew Psalter Trans: How many are the days of your servant? When will you execute judgment on those who persecute me?

Hebrew Trans: The proud have dug pits for me, which is not according to Your Torah.
Septuagint Trans: The lawless have told me idle tales, but not like your law, O Lord.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The wicked told me fables, but not as your law.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The proud have dug pits for me, which were not according to your law.

Hebrew Trans: All Your commandments are faithful; they persecute me with falsehood—help me!
Septuagint Trans: All your commandments are truth; unjustly they have persecuted me—help me!
Gallican Psalter Trans: All your commandments are truth; they have unjustly persecuted me—help me.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: All your commandments are true; they have persecuted me falsely; help me.

Hebrew Trans: They almost destroyed me on earth, but I did not forsake Your precepts.
Septuagint Trans: They nearly destroyed me in the earth, but I did not forsake your commandments.
Gallican Psalter Trans: They had almost consumed me upon the earth; but I did not forsake your commandments.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: They had almost consumed me upon earth, but I did not forsake your precepts.

Hebrew Trans: According to Your lovingkindness revive me, and I will keep the testimony of Your mouth.
Septuagint Trans: According to your mercy, give me life, and I shall keep the testimonies of your mouth.
Gallican Psalter Trans: According to your mercy give me life, and I shall keep the testimonies of your mouth.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: According to your mercy, revive me, and I will keep the testimony of your mouth.

Hebrew Trans: Forever, O LORD, Your word stands firm in the heavens.
Septuagint Trans: Forever, O Lord, your word endures in heaven.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Forever, O Lord, your word endures in heaven.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Forever, O Lord, your word endures in heaven.

Hebrew Trans: To generation and generation is Your faithfulness; You established the earth, and it stands.
Septuagint Trans: To generation and generation is your truth; you established the earth, and it endures.
Gallican Psalter Trans: From generation to generation your truth endures; you established the earth, and it stands firm.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: From generation to generation your faithfulness endures; you established the earth, and it stands.

Hebrew Trans: By Your judgments they stand this day, for all are Your servants.
Septuagint Trans: By your ordinance the day continues, for all things are your servants.
Gallican Psalter Trans: By your ordinance the day perseveres, for all things serve you.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: By your judgment they stand to this day, for all things serve you.

Hebrew Trans: If Your Torah had not been my delight, then I would have perished in my affliction.
Septuagint Trans: If your law had not been my meditation, then I would have perished in my humiliation.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Unless your law had been my meditation, then perhaps I would have perished in my humiliation.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Had not your law been my delight, I would have perished in my affliction.

Hebrew Trans: Forever I will not forget Your precepts, for by them You have given me life.
Septuagint Trans: I will never forget your statutes forever, for by them you gave me life, O Lord.
Gallican Psalter Trans: I will never forget your justifications, for by them you have given me life.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: I will never forget your precepts, for through them you have given me life.

Hebrew Trans: I am Yours—save me, for I have sought Your precepts.
Septuagint Trans: I am yours; save me, for I have sought out your statutes.
Gallican Psalter Trans: I am yours; save me, for I have sought out your justifications.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: I am yours; save me, for I have sought your precepts.

Hebrew Trans: The wicked have waited for me to destroy me, but I contemplate Your testimonies.
Septuagint Trans: Sinners lay in wait for me to destroy me, but I understood your testimonies.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Sinners waited for me to destroy me, but I understood your testimonies.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The wicked waited for me to destroy me, but I will consider your testimony.

Hebrew Trans: To all perfection I have seen an end; Your commandment is exceedingly broad.
Septuagint Trans: Of all perfection I have seen an end; your commandment is exceedingly broad.
Gallican Psalter Trans: I have seen an end to all perfection, but your commandment is exceedingly broad.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: To every perfection I have seen a limit; your commandment is exceedingly broad.

Hebrew Trans: Oh how I love Your Torah! All the day it is my meditation.
Septuagint Trans: How I have loved your law, O Lord! All the day long it is my meditation.
Gallican Psalter Trans: How I have loved your law! It is my meditation all the day.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: How I have loved your law! All day long it is my meditation.

Hebrew Trans: From my enemies Your commandments make me wiser, for it is mine forever.
Septuagint Trans: Beyond my enemies you have made me wise through your commandment, for it is mine forever.
Gallican Psalter Trans: You have made me wiser than my enemies by your commandment, for it is mine forever.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: You make me wiser than my enemies through your commandments, for they are mine forever.

Hebrew Trans: From all my teachers I have gained insight, for Your testimonies are my meditation.
Septuagint Trans: Beyond all who teach me I have gained understanding, for your testimonies are my meditation.
Gallican Psalter Trans: I have understood more than all my teachers, for your testimonies are my meditation.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: I have gained understanding beyond all who taught me, for your testimonies are my meditation.

Hebrew Trans: From the elders I gain understanding, for I have kept Your precepts.
Septuagint Trans: Beyond the elders I have gained understanding, for I have sought out your commandments.
Gallican Psalter Trans: I have understood more than the elders, for I have sought your commandments.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: I have understood more than the elders, for I have kept your precepts.

Hebrew Trans: From every evil path I have restrained my feet, in order that I may keep Your word.
Septuagint Trans: From every evil way I have restrained my feet, so that I might keep your words.
Gallican Psalter Trans: I have restrained my feet from every evil way, that I may keep your words.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: I have restrained my feet from every evil path, that I might keep your words.

Hebrew Trans: From Your judgments I have not turned aside, for You have instructed me.
Septuagint Trans: From your judgments I have not turned aside, for you have given me your law.
Gallican Psalter Trans: I have not turned aside from your judgments, for you have set the law before me.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: I have not departed from your judgments, for you have enlightened me.

Hebrew Trans: How sweet are Your words to my palate, more than honey to my mouth!
Septuagint Trans: How sweet are your oracles to my throat — beyond honey and honeycomb to my mouth!
Gallican Psalter Trans: How sweet are your words to my palate, sweeter than honey to my mouth!
Hebrew Psalter Trans: How sweet is your speech to my throat, sweeter than honey to my mouth!

Hebrew Trans: From Your precepts I gain understanding; therefore I hate every false path.
Septuagint Trans: From your commandments I gained understanding; therefore I have hated every way of injustice, for you have given me your law.
Gallican Psalter Trans: From your commandments I have gained understanding; therefore I have hated every way of iniquity.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: I considered your precepts; therefore I hated every path of falsehood.

Hebrew Trans: Your word is a lamp to my feet and a light to my path.
Septuagint Trans: Your word is a lamp to my feet and a light to my paths.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Your word is a lamp to my feet and a light to my paths.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Your word is a lamp to my foot and a light to my path.

Hebrew Trans: I have sworn and I will confirm it, to keep Your righteous judgments.
Septuagint Trans: I have sworn and resolved to keep the judgments of your righteousness.
Gallican Psalter Trans: I have sworn and resolved to keep the judgments of your justice.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: I have sworn and I will persevere, to keep the judgments of your righteousness.

Hebrew Trans: I am afflicted exceedingly; O LORD, give me life according to Your word.
Septuagint Trans: I was humbled exceedingly; O Lord, give me life according to your word.
Gallican Psalter Trans: I have been humbled exceedingly; O Lord, give me life according to your word.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: I am afflicted exceedingly, O Lord; give me life according to your word.

Hebrew Trans: Accept, I pray, the freewill offerings of my mouth, O LORD, and teach me Your judgments.
Septuagint Trans: Be pleased now with the voluntary offerings of my mouth, O Lord, and teach me your judgments.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Make the willing offerings of my mouth pleasing, O Lord, and teach me your judgments.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Let the free offerings of my mouth please you, O Lord, and teach me according to your judgments.

Hebrew Trans: My soul is continually in my hand, yet Your Torah I have not forgotten.
Septuagint Trans: My soul is in my hands continually, yet I have not forgotten your law.
Gallican Psalter Trans: My soul is continually in my hands, and I have not forgotten your law.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: My soul is ever in my hand, yet I have not forgotten your law.

Hebrew Trans: The wicked have set a snare for me, yet from Your precepts I have not strayed.
Septuagint Trans: Sinners set a snare for me, yet from your commandments I did not stray.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Sinners set a snare for me, yet I have not strayed from your commandments.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The wicked set a snare for me, yet I have not strayed from your precepts.

Hebrew Trans: I have inherited Your testimonies forever, for they are the joy of my heart.
Septuagint Trans: I have inherited your testimonies forever, for they are the joy of my heart.
Gallican Psalter Trans: I have acquired your testimonies as an inheritance forever, for they are the joy of my heart.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Your testimonies are my heritage forever, for they are the joy of my heart.

Hebrew Trans: I have inclined my heart to perform Your statutes forever, to the end.
Septuagint Trans: I inclined my heart to perform your statutes forever, for the sake of recompense.
Gallican Psalter Trans: I have inclined my heart to perform your justifications forever, for the sake of the reward.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: I have inclined my heart to perform your righteous deeds, for the sake of eternal reward.

Hebrew Trans: The double-minded I hate, but Your Torah I love.
Septuagint Trans: The lawless I have hated, but your law I have loved.
Gallican Psalter Trans: I have hated the wicked, and I have loved your law.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: I have hated the tumultuous, and I have loved your law.

Hebrew Trans: My hiding place and my shield are You; for Your word I hope.
Septuagint Trans: You are my helper and my protector; in your word I have hoped.
Gallican Psalter Trans: You are my helper and my protector; in your word I have placed my hope.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: You are my protection and my shield; I have waited for your word.

Hebrew Trans: Depart from me, you evildoers, that I may keep the commandments of my God.
Septuagint Trans: Depart from me, you evildoers, and I will search out the commandments of my God.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Depart from me, you evildoers, and I will search out the commandments of my God.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Depart from me, you evildoers, and I will keep the commandments of my God.

Hebrew Trans: Sustain me according to Your word, that I may live, and do not put me to shame because of my hope.
Septuagint Trans: Uphold me according to your oracle, and I shall live; and do not put me to shame in my expectation.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Uphold me according to your word, and I shall live; and do not confound me in my expectation.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Sustain me according to your word, and I shall live; and do not put me to shame in my expectation.

Hebrew Trans: Uphold me that I may be saved, and I will gaze upon Your statutes continually.
Septuagint Trans: Help me and I shall be saved, and I will meditate on your statutes continually.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Help me and I shall be saved, and I will meditate on your justifications always.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Help me, and I shall be saved, and I will delight in your precepts continually.

Hebrew Trans: You have rejected all who stray from Your statutes, for their deceit is falsehood.
Septuagint Trans: You have brought to nothing all who fall away from your statutes, for their thought is unjust.
Gallican Psalter Trans: You have despised all who depart from your statutes, for their thought is unjust.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: You have cast away all who oppose your precepts, for their thought is deceitful.

Hebrew Trans: You have made to cease like dross all the wicked of the earth; therefore I love Your testimonies.
Septuagint Trans: I reckoned as transgressors all the sinners of the earth; therefore I have loved your testimonies continually.
Gallican Psalter Trans: I have regarded all the sinners of the earth as transgressors; therefore I have loved your testimonies.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: You have reckoned all the wicked of the earth as dross; therefore I have loved your testimonies.

Hebrew Trans: My flesh trembles from fear of You, and I am afraid of Your judgments.
Septuagint Trans: Nail my flesh with the fear of you, for I have been afraid of your judgments.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Pierce my flesh with your fear, for I am afraid of your judgments.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: My flesh has shuddered with dread of you, and I have feared your judgments.

Hebrew Trans: I have done justice and righteousness; do not leave me to my oppressors.
Septuagint Trans: I have done judgment and righteousness; do not hand me over to those who wrong me.
Gallican Psalter Trans: I have done judgment and justice; do not hand me over to those who slander me.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: I have done judgment and justice; do not abandon me to those who slander me.

Hebrew Trans: Be surety for Your servant for good; let not the arrogant oppress me.
Septuagint Trans: Receive your servant for good; let not the arrogant oppress me.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Receive your servant for good; let not the proud slander me.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Be surety for your servant for good; let not the proud slander me.

Hebrew Trans: My eyes fail for Your salvation, and for the word of Your righteousness.
Septuagint Trans: My eyes have failed looking for your salvation and for the oracle of your righteousness.
Gallican Psalter Trans: My eyes have failed looking for your salvation and for the word of your justice.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: My eyes have failed looking for your salvation and for the word of your righteousness.

Hebrew Trans: Deal with Your servant according to Your lovingkindness, and teach me Your statutes.
Septuagint Trans: Deal with your servant according to your mercy, and teach me your statutes.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Deal with your servant according to your mercy, and teach me your justifications.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Deal with your servant according to your mercy, and teach me your precepts.

Hebrew Trans: I am Your servant—give me understanding, that I may know Your testimonies.
Septuagint Trans: I am your servant; give me understanding, and I shall know your testimonies.
Gallican Psalter Trans: I am your servant; give me understanding, that I may know your testimonies.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: I am your servant; instruct me, and I shall know your testimonies.

Hebrew Trans: It is time to act for the LORD; they have broken Your Torah.
Septuagint Trans: It is time for the Lord to act; they have scattered your law.
Gallican Psalter Trans: It is time to act for the Lord; they have scattered your law.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: It is time for you to act, O Lord; they have violated your law.

Hebrew Trans: Therefore I love Your commandments more than gold, even more than fine gold.
Septuagint Trans: Therefore I have loved your commandments above gold and topaz.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Therefore I have loved your commandments above gold and topaz.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Therefore I have loved your commandments above gold and topaz.

Hebrew Trans: Therefore all Your precepts concerning all things I regard as right; every false path I hate.
Septuagint Trans: Therefore I was directed toward all your commandments; every unjust way I have hated.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Therefore I was directed toward all your commandments; every wicked way I have hated.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Therefore I have directed myself by all your precepts; every path of falsehood I have hated.

Hebrew Trans: Wondrous are Your testimonies; therefore my soul keeps them.
Septuagint Trans: Wonderful are your testimonies; therefore my soul has searched them out.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Your testimonies are wonderful; therefore my soul has searched them out.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Your testimonies are wonderful; therefore my soul has kept them.

Hebrew Trans: The opening of Your words gives light, giving understanding to the simple.
Septuagint Trans: The revelation of your words gives light and gives understanding to the simple.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The revelation of your words illuminates and gives understanding to the little ones.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The opening of your words gives light; it teaches the simple.

Hebrew Trans: I opened my mouth and panted, for I longed for Your commandments.
Septuagint Trans: I opened my mouth and drew breath, for I longed for your commandments.
Gallican Psalter Trans: I opened my mouth and drew in breath, for I longed for your commandments.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: I opened my mouth and drew breath, for I longed for your commandments.

Hebrew Trans: Turn to me and be gracious to me, as is fitting for those who love Your name.
Septuagint Trans: Look upon me and have mercy on me, according to the judgment of those who love your name.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Look upon me and have mercy on me, according to the judgment due to those who love your name.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Look upon me and have mercy on me, according to the judgment due to those who love your name.

Hebrew Trans: Establish my steps in Your word, and let no iniquity have dominion over me.
Septuagint Trans: Direct my steps according to your oracle, and let no lawlessness have dominion over me.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Direct my steps according to your word, and let no iniquity have dominion over me.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Make firm my steps in your word, and let no iniquity have dominion over me.

Hebrew Trans: Redeem me from the oppression of man, and I will keep Your precepts.
Septuagint Trans: Redeem me from the oppression of men, and I will keep your commandments.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Redeem me from the slanders of men, and I will keep your commandments.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Redeem me from the slander of man, and I will keep your precepts.

Hebrew Trans: Make Your face shine upon Your servant, and teach me Your statutes.
Septuagint Trans: Make your face shine upon your servant, and teach me your statutes.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Make your face shine upon your servant, and teach me your justifications.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Show your face to your servant, and teach me your precepts.

Hebrew Trans: Streams of water run down my eyes, because they do not keep Your Torah.
Septuagint Trans: My eyes shed streams of water, because they did not keep your law.
Gallican Psalter Trans: My eyes have sent forth streams of water, because they have not kept your law.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Streams of water flowed from my eyes, because they did not keep your law.

Hebrew Trans: Righteous are You, O LORD, and upright are Your judgments.
Septuagint Trans: Righteous are you, O Lord, and upright are your judgments.
Gallican Psalter Trans: You are just, O Lord, and your judgment is right.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: You are just, O Lord, and your judgment is right.

Hebrew Trans: You have commanded Your testimonies in righteousness and in great faithfulness.
Septuagint Trans: You have commanded your testimonies in righteousness and truth exceedingly.
Gallican Psalter Trans: You have commanded justice in your testimonies and your truth exceedingly.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: You have commanded the justice of your testimony and truth exceedingly.

Hebrew Trans: My zeal has consumed me, because my adversaries have forgotten Your words.
Septuagint Trans: The zeal of your house has consumed me, for my enemies have forgotten your words.
Gallican Psalter Trans: My zeal has made me waste away, because my enemies have forgotten your words.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: My zeal has consumed me, because my enemies have forgotten your words.

Hebrew Trans: Your word is very pure, and Your servant loves it.
Septuagint Trans: Your oracle is thoroughly refined by fire, and your servant has loved it.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Your word is exceedingly refined by fire, and your servant has loved it.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Your word is tested exceedingly, and your servant has loved it.

Hebrew Trans: I am small and despised, yet Your precepts I have not forgotten.
Septuagint Trans: I am young and despised; your statutes I have not forgotten.
Gallican Psalter Trans: I am young and despised, yet I have not forgotten your justifications.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: I am small and despised, yet I have not forgotten your precepts.

Hebrew Trans: Your righteousness is an everlasting righteousness, and Your Torah is truth.
Septuagint Trans: Your righteousness is righteousness forever, and your law is truth.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Your justice is justice forever, and your law is truth.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Your justice is justice everlasting, and your law is truth.

Hebrew Trans: Trouble and anguish have found me, yet Your commandments are my delight.
Septuagint Trans: Affliction and distress found me; your commandments are my meditation.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Tribulation and anguish have found me; your commandments are my meditation.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Tribulation and anguish have found me; your commandments are my delight.

Hebrew Trans: Your testimonies are righteous forever; give me understanding and I shall live.
Septuagint Trans: Your testimonies are righteousness forever; give me understanding and I shall live.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Your testimonies are equity forever; give me understanding, and I shall live.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Your testimonies are just forever; teach me, and I shall live.

Hebrew Trans: I have called with my whole heart; answer me, O LORD; I will keep Your statutes.
Septuagint Trans: I cried out with my whole heart; hear me, O Lord! I will seek your statutes.
Gallican Psalter Trans: I cried out with my whole heart; hear me, O Lord; I will seek your justifications.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: I cried out with my whole heart: hear me, O Lord; I will keep your precepts.

Hebrew Trans: I have called upon You; save me, and I will keep Your testimonies.
Septuagint Trans: I cried out to you; save me, and I will keep your testimonies.
Gallican Psalter Trans: I cried to you; save me, and I will keep your commandments.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: I called upon you: save me, and I will keep your testimonies.

Hebrew Trans: I rose before dawn and cried for help; for Your word I hoped.
Septuagint Trans: I arose before dawn and cried out; in your words I hoped.
Gallican Psalter Trans: I arose before dawn and cried out; in your words I have hoped.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: I rose while it was still dark and cried out, waiting for your word.

Hebrew Trans: My eyes anticipate the night watches, to meditate on Your word.
Septuagint Trans: My eyes anticipated the morning watch, to meditate on your oracles.
Gallican Psalter Trans: My eyes anticipated the dawn, that I might meditate on your words.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: My eyes anticipated the night watches, that I might meditate on your words.

Hebrew Trans: Hear my voice according to Your lovingkindness; O LORD, according to Your judgment give me life.
Septuagint Trans: Hear my voice, O Lord, according to your mercy; according to your judgment give me life.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Hear my voice according to your mercy, O Lord; according to your judgment give me life.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Hear my voice according to your mercy, O Lord; according to your judgment give me life.

Hebrew Trans: Those who pursue wickedness draw near; from Your Torah they are far.
Septuagint Trans: Those who persecute me with lawlessness have drawn near, but from your law they have gone far.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Those who persecute me with iniquity have drawn near; but from your law they have gone far.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: My persecutors have drawn near to wickedness, and they have gone far from your law.

Hebrew Trans: You are near, O LORD, and all Your commandments are truth.
Septuagint Trans: You are near, O Lord, and all your commandments are truth.
Gallican Psalter Trans: You are near, O Lord, and all your ways are truth.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: You are near, O Lord, and all your commandments are truth.

Hebrew Trans: Of old I have known from Your testimonies that You have founded them forever.
Septuagint Trans: From the beginning I knew from your testimonies that you have founded them forever.
Gallican Psalter Trans: From the beginning I have known from your testimonies that you have founded them forever.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: From the beginning I have known from your testimonies that you have established them forever.

Hebrew Trans: See my affliction and deliver me, for Your Torah I have not forgotten.
Septuagint Trans: See my humiliation and deliver me, for I have not forgotten your law.
Gallican Psalter Trans: See my humility and deliver me, for I have not forgotten your law.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: See my affliction and rescue me, for I have not forgotten your law.

Hebrew Trans: Plead my cause and redeem me; according to Your word give me life.
Septuagint Trans: Judge my case and redeem me; according to your word give me life.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Judge my cause and redeem me; give me life because of your word.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Judge my cause and redeem me; give me life by your word.

Hebrew Trans: Salvation is far from the wicked, for they have not sought Your statutes.
Septuagint Trans: Salvation is far from sinners, for they have not sought out your statutes.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Salvation is far from sinners, for they have not sought your justifications.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Salvation is far from the wicked, for they have not sought your precepts.

Hebrew Trans: Your mercies are great, O LORD; according to Your judgments give me life.
Septuagint Trans: Your mercies are many, O Lord; according to your judgment give me life.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Your mercies are many, O Lord; give me life according to your judgments.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Your mercies are many, O Lord; give me life according to your judgments.

Hebrew Trans: Many are my persecutors and my adversaries, yet from Your testimonies I have not turned aside.
Septuagint Trans: Many are those who pursue me and afflict me; from your testimonies I have not turned aside.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Many are those who persecute me and afflict me, yet I have not turned aside from your testimonies.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Many are those who persecute me and afflict me, yet I have not turned aside from your testimonies.

Hebrew Trans: I have seen the treacherous and quarreled with them, because they did not keep Your word.
Septuagint Trans: I saw those who deal treacherously and I was consumed with grief, because they did not keep your oracles.
Gallican Psalter Trans: I saw the transgressors and I wasted away, because they did not keep your words.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: I saw the transgressors and I grieved, those who did not keep your word.

Hebrew Trans: See that I love Your precepts; O LORD, according to Your lovingkindness give me life.
Septuagint Trans: See that I have loved your commandments; O Lord, in your mercy give me life.
Gallican Psalter Trans: See that I have loved your commandments, O Lord; in your mercy give me life.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: See that I have loved your precepts, O Lord; give me life according to your mercy.

Hebrew Trans: The sum of Your word is truth, and every one of Your righteous judgments endures forever.
Septuagint Trans: The beginning of your words is truth, and all the judgments of your righteousness endure forever.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The beginning of your words is truth, and all the judgments of your justice endure forever.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The sum of your words is truth, and every judgment of your righteousness is everlasting.

Hebrew Trans: Princes have persecuted me without cause, but my heart stands in awe of Your word.
Septuagint Trans: Rulers persecuted me without cause, but my heart stood in awe of your words.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Princes have persecuted me without cause, but my heart has stood in awe of your words.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Princes have persecuted me without cause, but my heart has feared your words.

Hebrew Trans: I rejoice at Your word, like one who finds great spoil.
