Septuagint Trans: I will rejoice over your oracles, as one who finds great spoils.
Gallican Psalter Trans: I will rejoice over your words, like one who finds great spoils.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: I rejoice in your speech, like one who finds much spoil.

Hebrew Trans: Falsehood I hate and abhor, but Your Torah I love.
Septuagint Trans: Injustice I have hated and abhorred, but your law I have loved.
Gallican Psalter Trans: I have hated iniquity and abhorred it, but your law I have loved.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: I have hated falsehood and detested it, but your law I have loved.

Hebrew Trans: Seven times a day I praise You, because of Your righteous judgments.
Septuagint Trans: Seven times a day I have praised you for the judgments of your righteousness.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Seven times a day I have spoken praise to you, concerning the judgments of your justice.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Seven times a day I have praised you for the judgments of your righteousness.

Hebrew Trans: Great peace have those who love Your Torah, and nothing causes them to stumble.
Septuagint Trans: Great peace have those who love your law, and there is no stumbling block for them.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Great peace have those who love your law, and there is no stumbling block for them.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Great peace have those who love your law, and for them there is no stumbling block.

Hebrew Trans: I have hoped for Your salvation, O LORD, and Your commandments I have done.
Septuagint Trans: I waited for your salvation, O Lord, and I have loved your commandments.
Gallican Psalter Trans: I waited for your salvation, O Lord, and I loved your commandments.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: I have waited for your salvation, O Lord, and I have fulfilled your commandments.

Hebrew Trans: My soul keeps Your testimonies, and I love them exceedingly.
Septuagint Trans: My soul has kept your testimonies and has loved them exceedingly.
Gallican Psalter Trans: My soul has kept your testimonies, and I have loved them exceedingly.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: My soul has kept your testimonies, and I have loved them exceedingly.

Hebrew Trans: I have kept Your precepts and Your testimonies, for all my ways are before You.
Septuagint Trans: I have kept your commandments and your testimonies, for all my ways are before you, O Lord.
Gallican Psalter Trans: I have observed your commandments and your testimonies, for all my ways are in your sight.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: I have kept your precepts and your testimonies, for all my ways are in your sight.

Hebrew Trans: Let my cry come near before You, O LORD; according to Your word give me understanding.
Septuagint Trans: Let my supplication draw near before you, O Lord; according to your oracle give me understanding.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Let my supplication draw near before you, O Lord; according to your word, give me understanding.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Let my praise come before you, O Lord; teach me according to your word.

Hebrew Trans: Let my supplication come before You; according to Your word deliver me.
Septuagint Trans: Let my petition come before you; according to your oracle deliver me.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Let my petition come before you; according to your word, deliver me.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Let my supplication come before your face; deliver me according to your word.

Hebrew Trans: My lips shall utter praise, for You teach me Your statutes.
Septuagint Trans: May my lips pour forth a hymn, when you teach me your statutes.
Gallican Psalter Trans: My lips will pour forth a hymn, when you have taught me your justifications.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Let my lips pour forth a hymn, for you will teach me your precepts.

Hebrew Trans: My tongue shall respond with Your word, for all Your commandments are righteousness.
Septuagint Trans: May my tongue speak of your oracle, for all your commandments are righteousness.
Gallican Psalter Trans: My tongue will proclaim your word, for all your commandments are equity.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Let my tongue speak your word, for all your commandments are just.

Hebrew Trans: Let Your hand be ready to help me, for I have chosen Your precepts.
Septuagint Trans: Let your hand be ready to save me, for I have chosen your commandments.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Let your hand be ready to save me, for I have chosen your commandments.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Let your hand be my helper, for I have chosen your precepts.

Hebrew Trans: I have longed for Your salvation, O LORD, and Your Torah is my delight.
Septuagint Trans: I have longed for your salvation, O Lord, and your law is my meditation.
Gallican Psalter Trans: I have longed for your salvation, O Lord, and your law is my meditation.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: I have longed for your salvation, O Lord, and your law is my delight.

Hebrew Trans: Let my soul live, and it shall praise You, and let Your judgments help me.
Septuagint Trans: My soul shall live and shall praise you, and your judgments shall help me.
Gallican Psalter Trans: My soul shall live and shall praise you, and your judgments shall help me.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: My soul shall live and shall praise you, and your judgments shall help me.

Hebrew Trans: I have gone astray like a lost sheep; seek Your servant, for Your commandments I have not forgotten.
Septuagint Trans: I have gone astray like a lost sheep; seek your servant, for I have not forgotten your commandments.
Gallican Psalter Trans: I have gone astray like a sheep that is lost; seek your servant, for I have not forgotten your commandments.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: I have gone astray like a lost sheep; seek your servant, for I have not forgotten your commandments.

Hebrew Trans: A song of ascents. Unto the LORD, in my distress I called, and He answered me.
Septuagint Trans: A song of ascents. To the Lord in my distress I cried out, and he heard me.
Gallican Psalter Trans: A song of degrees. To the Lord in my tribulation I cried out, and he heard me.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: A song of ascents. To the Lord in my tribulation I cried out, and he heard me.

Hebrew Trans: O LORD, deliver my soul from lying lips, from a deceitful tongue.
Septuagint Trans: O Lord, deliver my soul from unjust lips and from a deceitful tongue.
Gallican Psalter Trans: O Lord, deliver my soul from wicked lips, from a deceitful tongue.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: O Lord, deliver my soul from the lying lip, from the deceitful tongue.

Hebrew Trans: What shall be given to you, and what shall be added to you, O deceitful tongue?
Septuagint Trans: What should be given to you, and what should be added to you, against a deceitful tongue?
Gallican Psalter Trans: What shall be given to you, and what shall be added to you, for a deceitful tongue?
Hebrew Psalter Trans: What shall be given to you, or what shall be added to you, for a deceitful tongue?

Hebrew Trans: Sharp arrows of a mighty man, with coals of broom.
Septuagint Trans: The sharpened arrows of the mighty one, with the coals of the wilderness.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Sharp arrows of the mighty, with coals of desolation.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Sharp arrows of the mighty, with coals of juniper.

Hebrew Trans: Woe is me, that I sojourned in Meshech, that I dwelt among the tents of Kedar!
Septuagint Trans: Woe is me, that my sojourning has been prolonged! I dwelt among the tents of Kedar.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Woe is me, that my sojourning is prolonged; I have dwelt with the dwellings of Cedar.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Woe to me, that my sojourning has been prolonged; I have dwelt with the tents of Cedar.

Hebrew Trans: Too long has my soul dwelt with those who hate peace.
Septuagint Trans: Long has my soul sojourned.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Long has my soul been a sojourner.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Much has my soul been a sojourner.

Hebrew Trans: I am for peace; but when I speak, they are for war.
Septuagint Trans: With those who hate peace I was peaceable; when I spoke to them, they warred against me without cause.
Gallican Psalter Trans: With those who hated peace I was peaceable; when I spoke to them, they fought against me without cause.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: With those who hated peace, I was peaceable; when I spoke to them, they were for war.

Hebrew Trans: A song of ascents. I will lift up my eyes to the mountains; from where shall my help come?
Septuagint Trans: A song of the ascents. I lifted up my eyes to the mountains; from where shall my help come?
Gallican Psalter Trans: A song of ascents. I lifted up my eyes to the mountains, from whence shall come help to me.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: A song of ascents. I lifted up my eyes to the mountains, from whence my help shall come.

Hebrew Trans: My help is from the LORD, the maker of heaven and earth.
Septuagint Trans: My help is from the Lord, who made the heaven and the earth.
Gallican Psalter Trans: My help is from the Lord, who made heaven and earth.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: My help is from the Lord, the maker of heaven and earth.

Hebrew Trans: He will not allow your foot to be moved; he who keeps you will not slumber.
Septuagint Trans: Do not give your foot to stumbling, nor let the one who guards you slumber.
Gallican Psalter Trans: May he not give your foot to be moved, nor let him slumber who guards you.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: May he not give your foot to be moved, nor let him who guards you slumber.

Hebrew Trans: Behold, he who keeps Israel shall neither slumber nor sleep.
Septuagint Trans: Behold, he who guards Israel shall neither slumber nor sleep.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Behold, he shall not slumber nor sleep, he who guards Israel.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Behold, he who guards Israel shall neither slumber nor sleep.

Hebrew Trans: The LORD is your keeper; the LORD is your shade upon your right hand.
Septuagint Trans: The Lord shall guard you; the Lord is your shelter at your right hand.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The Lord guards you; the Lord is your protection upon your right hand.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The Lord guards you; the Lord is your protection upon your right hand.

Hebrew Trans: By day the sun shall not strike you, nor the moon by night.
Septuagint Trans: By day the sun shall not burn you, nor the moon by night.
Gallican Psalter Trans: By day the sun shall not strike you, nor the moon by night.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: By day the sun shall not strike you, nor the moon by night.

Hebrew Trans: The LORD shall keep you from all evil; he shall keep your soul.
Septuagint Trans: The Lord shall guard you from all evil; he shall guard your soul.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The Lord guards you from all evil; may the Lord guard your soul.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The Lord guards you from all evil; may he guard your soul.

Hebrew Trans: The LORD shall keep your going out and your coming in from this time forth and forevermore.
Septuagint Trans: The Lord shall guard your coming in and your going out, from now and unto the age.
Gallican Psalter Trans: May the Lord guard your coming in and your going out, from this time now and even forever.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: May the Lord guard your going out and your coming in, from this time forth and even forever.

Hebrew Trans: A Song of Ascents, of David. I was glad when they said to me, "Let us go to the house of the LORD."
Septuagint Trans: A song of the ascents. I was glad at those who said to me, "Let us go to the house of the Lord."
Gallican Psalter Trans: A song of degrees, of David. I was glad in the things that were said to me: we shall go into the house of the Lord.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: A song of degrees of David. I was glad because they said to me: We shall go into the house of the Lord.

Hebrew Trans: Our feet were standing within your gates, O Jerusalem.
Septuagint Trans: Our feet were standing in your courts, O Jerusalem.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Our feet were standing in your courts, O Jerusalem.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Our feet were standing in your gates, O Jerusalem.

Hebrew Trans: Jerusalem, built as a city that is joined together as one.
Septuagint Trans: Jerusalem, built as a city whose participation is together as one.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Jerusalem, which is built as a city whose participation is in unity.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Jerusalem, which is built as a city whose participation is together as one.

Hebrew Trans: For there the tribes go up, the tribes of the LORD, as a testimony to Israel, to give thanks to the name of the LORD.
Septuagint Trans: For there the tribes went up, the tribes of the Lord, as a testimony to Israel, to give thanks to the name of the Lord.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For there the tribes went up, the tribes of the Lord, the testimony of Israel, to confess the name of the Lord.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For there the tribes went up, the tribes of the Lord, the testimony of Israel, to confess the name of the Lord.

Hebrew Trans: For there are set thrones for judgment, thrones of the house of David.
Septuagint Trans: For there were set thrones for judgment, thrones over the house of David.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For there were set seats for judgment, seats upon the house of David.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For there were set seats in judgment, seats for the house of David.

Hebrew Trans: Pray for the peace of Jerusalem; may those who love you prosper.
Septuagint Trans: Ask now for the things pertaining to the peace of Jerusalem, and prosperity to those who love you.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Ask for the things that are for the peace of Jerusalem, and abundance for those who love you.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Pray for the peace of Jerusalem: may it be well for those who love you.

Hebrew Trans: May peace be within your walls, tranquility within your palaces.
Septuagint Trans: Let peace now be within your strength, and prosperity in your towered walls.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Let peace be in your strength, and abundance in your towers.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Let there be peace in your walls, and abundance in your houses.

Hebrew Trans: For the sake of my brothers and companions, let me say now, "Peace be within you."
Septuagint Trans: For the sake of my brothers and my neighbors, I spoke now of peace concerning you.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For the sake of my brothers and my neighbors, I spoke peace concerning you.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For the sake of my brothers and my friends, I will speak peace to you.

Hebrew Trans: For the sake of the house of the LORD our God, I will seek your good.
Septuagint Trans: For the sake of the house of the Lord our God, I sought good things for you.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For the sake of the house of the Lord our God, I have sought good things for you.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For the sake of the house of the Lord our God, I will seek good things for you.

Hebrew Trans: A song of ascents. Unto you I lift up my eyes, O you who dwell in the heavens.
Septuagint Trans: A song of ascents. To you I have lifted up my eyes, to you who dwell in heaven.
Gallican Psalter Trans: A song of degrees. To you I have lifted up my eyes, you who dwell in heaven.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: A song of ascents. To you I have lifted up my eyes, you who dwell in the heavens.

Hebrew Trans: Behold, as the eyes of servants look to the hand of their masters, as the eyes of a maidservant to the hand of her mistress, so our eyes look to the LORD our God, until he has mercy upon us.
Septuagint Trans: Behold, as the eyes of servants look to the hands of their masters, as the eyes of a maidservant look to the hands of her mistress, so our eyes look to the Lord our God, until he has mercy on us.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Behold, as the eyes of servants are on the hands of their masters, as the eyes of a maidservant are on the hands of her mistress, so are our eyes unto the Lord our God, until he has mercy on us.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Behold, as the eyes of servants are upon the hand of their masters, as the eyes of a maidservant are upon the hand of her mistress, so are our eyes upon the Lord our God, until he has mercy upon us.

Hebrew Trans: Have mercy upon us, O LORD, have mercy upon us, for we are greatly filled with contempt.
Septuagint Trans: Have mercy on us, Lord, have mercy on us, for we have been greatly filled with contempt.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Have mercy on us, O Lord, have mercy on us, for we are greatly filled with contempt.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Have mercy upon us, Lord, have mercy upon us, for we are greatly filled with contempt.

Hebrew Trans: Our soul is greatly filled with the mockery of those who are at ease, and with the contempt of the proud.
Septuagint Trans: Our soul has been more than filled with the reproach of those who are at ease and the contempt of the proud.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For our soul is greatly filled with the reproach of the abundant and the contempt of the proud.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Our soul is greatly filled with the reproach of the abundant and the contempt of the proud.

Hebrew Trans: A song of ascents, of David. If it had not been the LORD who was on our side, let Israel now say—
Septuagint Trans: A song of the ascents. If the Lord had not been among us, let Israel now say—
Gallican Psalter Trans: A song of degrees, of David. Unless the Lord had been in us, let Israel now say:
Hebrew Psalter Trans: A song of degrees of David. Unless the Lord had been in us, let Israel now say:

Hebrew Trans: If it had not been the LORD who was on our side, when men rose up against us,
Septuagint Trans: If the Lord had not been among us, when men rose up against us—
Gallican Psalter Trans: Unless the Lord had been in us, when men rose up against us,
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Unless the Lord had been in us, when men rose up against us,

Hebrew Trans: Then they would have swallowed us alive, when their anger was kindled against us;
Septuagint Trans: Then they would have swallowed us alive, when their anger was kindled against us.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Perhaps they had swallowed us alive, when their fury was kindled against us;
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Perhaps they would have swallowed us alive, when their fury was angry against us;

Hebrew Trans: Then the waters would have overwhelmed us, the torrent would have gone over our soul;
Septuagint Trans: Then the water would have drowned us; our soul would have passed through a torrent.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Perhaps the water had swallowed us up; a torrent had passed over our soul;
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Perhaps the waters would have surrounded us, the torrent would have passed over our soul;

Hebrew Trans: Then the raging waters would have gone over our soul.
Septuagint Trans: Then our soul would have passed through the overwhelming water.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Perhaps our soul had passed through water unbearable.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Perhaps the proud waters would have passed over our soul.

Hebrew Trans: Blessed be the LORD, who has not given us as prey to their teeth.
Septuagint Trans: Blessed be the Lord, who has not given us as prey to their teeth.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Blessed be the Lord, who has not given us as a prey to their teeth.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Blessed be the Lord, who has not given us as prey to their teeth.

Hebrew Trans: Our soul has escaped like a bird out of the snare of the fowlers; the snare is broken, and we have escaped.
Septuagint Trans: Our soul like a sparrow has been delivered from the snare of the hunters; the snare was broken, and we were delivered.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Our soul, like a sparrow, has been snatched from the snare of the hunters; the snare is broken, and we have been set free.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Our soul, like a bird, has been snatched from the snare of the hunters; the snare has been broken, and we have been set free.

Hebrew Trans: Our help is in the name of the LORD, who made heaven and earth.
Septuagint Trans: Our help is in the name of the Lord, who made heaven and earth.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Our help is in the name of the Lord, who made heaven and earth.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Our help is in the name of the Lord, who made heaven and earth.

Hebrew Trans: A Song of Ascents. Those who trust in the LORD are like Mount Zion, which cannot be moved, but abides forever.
Septuagint Trans: A song of the ascents. Those who trust in the Lord are as Mount Zion; he who dwells in Jerusalem shall not be shaken forever.
Gallican Psalter Trans: A song of ascents. Those who trust in the Lord are like Mount Zion: he shall not be moved forever who dwells
Hebrew Psalter Trans: A song of degrees. They that trust in the Lord shall be as mount Sion: unmovable, dwelling forever.

Hebrew Trans: Jerusalem—mountains surround her, and the LORD surrounds His people, from this time forth and forevermore.
Septuagint Trans: Mountains are round about her, and the Lord is round about his people, from now and unto the age.
Gallican Psalter Trans: in Jerusalem. Mountains are round about it, and the Lord is round about his people, from this time forth and forever.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Jerusalem, the mountains are round about her: and the Lord is round about his people, from henceforth and forever.

Hebrew Trans: For the scepter of wickedness shall not rest upon the lot of the righteous, lest the righteous stretch out their hands unto iniquity.
Septuagint Trans: For he shall not leave the rod of sinners upon the lot of the righteous, lest the righteous stretch out their hands in lawlessness.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For he shall not leave the rod of sinners upon the lot of the just, so that the just may not stretch forth their hands unto iniquity.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For the rod of impiety shall not rest upon the lot of the just: so that the just may not stretch forth their hands unto iniquity.

Hebrew Trans: Do good, O LORD, to the good, and to those who are upright in their hearts.
Septuagint Trans: Do good, O Lord, to those who are good and to those who are upright in heart.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Do good, O Lord, to those who are good and upright in heart.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Do good, O Lord, to the good and to the upright of heart.

Hebrew Trans: But as for those who turn aside to their crooked ways, the LORD shall lead them away with the workers of iniquity. Peace be upon Israel.
Septuagint Trans: But those who turn aside to crooked ways the Lord shall lead away with those who work lawlessness. Peace be upon Israel.
Gallican Psalter Trans: But those who turn aside unto crooked ways, the Lord shall lead them forth with the workers of iniquity. Peace be upon Israel.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: But those who turn aside unto their own perversities, the Lord shall lead them out with those who work iniquity. Peace be upon Israel.

Hebrew Trans: A song of ascents. When the LORD restored the captivity of Zion, we were like dreamers.
Septuagint Trans: A song of the ascents. When the Lord turned back the captivity of Zion, we became as those who are comforted.
Gallican Psalter Trans: A song of degrees. When the Lord brought back the captivity of Sion, we became as those who are consoled.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: A song of degrees. When the Lord brought back the captivity of Sion, we became as those who dream.

Hebrew Trans: Then our mouth was filled with laughter, and our tongue with singing; then they said among the nations, "The LORD has done great things with these."
Septuagint Trans: Then our mouth was filled with joy and our tongue with gladness. Then they will say among the nations, "The Lord has done great things with them."
Gallican Psalter Trans: Then our mouth was filled with joy, and our tongue with exultation. Then they shall say among the nations: The Lord has done great things with them.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Then our mouth shall be filled with laughter, and our tongue with praise. Then they shall say among the nations: The Lord has done great things with them.

Hebrew Trans: The LORD has done great things with us; we were glad.
Septuagint Trans: The Lord has done great things with us; we became rejoicing.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The Lord has done great things for us; we have been made glad.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The Lord has done great things for us; we have become rejoicing.

Hebrew Trans: Restore, O LORD, our captivity, as the streams in the Negev.
Septuagint Trans: Turn back, O Lord, our captivity, as streams in the south.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Turn again, O Lord, our captivity, as a torrent in the south.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Turn back, O Lord, our captivity, as a stream in the south.

Hebrew Trans: Those who sow in tears shall reap in singing.
Septuagint Trans: Those who sow in tears shall reap in gladness.
Gallican Psalter Trans: They who sow in tears shall reap in exultation.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: They who sow in tears shall reap in exultation.

Hebrew Trans: He who goes forth weeping, bearing the trail of seed, shall surely come in with singing, bearing his sheaves.
Septuagint Trans: Going forth they went and wept, carrying their seeds; but coming back they shall come with gladness, carrying their sheaves.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Going forth they went and wept, carrying their seeds. But coming they shall come in exultation, carrying their sheaves.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: He who going forth went and wept, carrying seed for sowing, coming shall come in exultation, carrying his sheaves.

Hebrew Trans: A Song of Ascents, of Solomon. Unless the LORD builds the house, its builders labor upon it in vain. Unless the LORD guards the city, the watchman keeps vigil in vain.
Septuagint Trans: A song of ascents, for Solomon. Unless the Lord builds a house, in vain have the builders labored on it. Unless the Lord guards a city, in vain has the watchman kept vigil.
Gallican Psalter Trans: A song of degrees of Solomon. Unless the Lord has built the house, they have labored in vain who build it. Unless the Lord has guarded the city, in vain has he watched who guards it.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: A song of degrees of Solomon. Unless the Lord has built the house, they who build it have labored in vain. Unless the Lord has guarded the city, he who guards it watches in vain.

Hebrew Trans: It is vain for you to rise up early, to sit up late, eating the bread of toils; so He gives to His beloved sleep.
Septuagint Trans: It is vain for you to rise early, to get up after sitting, you who eat the bread of sorrow; when he gives sleep to his beloved ones.
Gallican Psalter Trans: It is vain for you to rise before the light, to rise after you have sat down, you who eat the bread of sorrows; when he shall give sleep to his beloved.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: It is vain for you to rise up early, to sit up late, you who eat the bread of sorrows: for so he shall give his beloved ones sleep.

Hebrew Trans: Behold, children are a heritage of the LORD, the fruit of the womb is a reward.
Septuagint Trans: Behold, the inheritance of the Lord is sons, the reward is the fruit of the womb.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Behold, the inheritance of the Lord: children; the reward: the fruit of the womb.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Behold, the inheritance of the Lord are children, the reward is the fruit of the womb.

Hebrew Trans: As arrows in the hand of a mighty man, so are the children of one's youth.
Septuagint Trans: As arrows in the hand of a mighty man, so are the sons of those who have been shaken out.
Gallican Psalter Trans: As arrows in the hand of the mighty, so are the children of those who have been cast out.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: As arrows in the hand of a mighty man, so are the children of youth.

Hebrew Trans: Happy is the man who has filled his quiver with them; they shall not be put to shame when they speak with enemies in the gate.
Septuagint Trans: Blessed is the man who shall fulfill his desire from them; they shall not be put to shame when they speak with their enemies in the gate.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Blessed is the man who shall fill his desire from them; they shall not be confounded when they speak with their enemies in the gate.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Blessed is the man who has filled his quiver with them: they shall not be confounded when they speak with their enemies in the gate.

Hebrew Trans: A song of ascents. Blessed is everyone who fears the LORD, who walks in His ways.
Septuagint Trans: A song of the ascents. Blessed are all who fear the Lord, who walk in his ways.
Gallican Psalter Trans: A song of degrees. Blessed are all who fear the Lord, who walk in his ways.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: A song of degrees. Blessed is everyone who fears the Lord, who walks in his ways.

Hebrew Trans: When you eat the labor of your hands, blessed are you, and it shall be well with you.
Septuagint Trans: You shall eat the labors of your fruits; blessed are you, and it shall be well with you.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For you shall eat the labors of your hands: blessed are you, and it shall be well with you.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: When you shall eat the labor of your hands, blessed are you, and it shall be well with you.

Hebrew Trans: Your wife shall be like a fruitful vine in the innermost parts of your house; your children like olive plants around your table.
Septuagint Trans: Your wife shall be as a fruitful vine on the sides of your house; your sons like young olive plants around your table.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Your wife shall be as a fruitful vine on the sides of your house; your children like young olive plants around your table.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Your wife shall be as a fruitful vine in the inner parts of your house; your children as olive shoots around your table.

Hebrew Trans: Behold, for thus shall the man be blessed who fears the LORD.
Septuagint Trans: Behold, thus shall the man be blessed who fears the Lord.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Behold, thus shall the man be blessed who fears the Lord.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Behold, thus shall the man be blessed who fears the Lord.

Hebrew Trans: May the LORD bless you from Zion, and may you see the good of Jerusalem all the days of your life.
Septuagint Trans: May the Lord bless you out of Zion, and may you see the good things of Jerusalem all the days of your life.
Gallican Psalter Trans: May the Lord bless you out of Zion, and may you see the good things of Jerusalem all the days of your life.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: May the Lord bless you out of Zion, and may you see the good things of Jerusalem all the days of your life.

Hebrew Trans: And may you see your children's children. Peace be upon Israel.
Septuagint Trans: And may you see your sons' sons. Peace be upon Israel.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And may you see your children's children. Peace be upon Israel.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And may you see the children of your children. Peace upon Israel.

Hebrew Trans: A Song of Ascents. Many a time have they afflicted me from my youth, let Israel now say.
Septuagint Trans: A song of the ascents. Many times they have warred against me from my youth — let Israel now say —
Gallican Psalter Trans: A song of degrees. Often they have attacked me from my youth: let Israel now say.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: A song of degrees. They have often attacked me from my youth: let Israel now say.

Hebrew Trans: Many a time have they afflicted me from my youth, yet they have not prevailed against me.
Septuagint Trans: Many times they have warred against me from my youth, yet they have not prevailed against me.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Often they have attacked me from my youth: yet they have not prevailed against me.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: They have often attacked me from my youth: but they have not prevailed against me.

Hebrew Trans: Upon my back the plowers plowed; they made long their furrows.
Septuagint Trans: Upon my back the sinners wrought; they prolonged their iniquity.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Upon my back the sinners were forging; they prolonged their iniquity.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Upon my neck the plowers plowed: they prolonged their furrow.

Hebrew Trans: The LORD is righteous; He has cut asunder the cords of the wicked.
Septuagint Trans: The Lord is righteous; he has cut the necks of sinners.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The Lord is just: he will cut the necks of sinners.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The Lord is just: he will cut the snares of the wicked.

Hebrew Trans: Let them be ashamed and turned backward, all those who hate Zion.
Septuagint Trans: Let them be put to shame and turned back, all those who hate Zion.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Let them be confounded and turned backward, all who hate Zion.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Let them be confounded and turned backward, all who hate Zion.

Hebrew Trans: Let them be as the grass upon the rooftops, which before it is plucked up withers;
Septuagint Trans: Let them become like grass on rooftops, which withered before it was plucked out,
Gallican Psalter Trans: Let them be as the grass of the housetops, which withers before it is plucked up.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Let them be as the grass of the housetops, which as soon as it has sprouted withers away.

Hebrew Trans: With which the reaper does not fill his hand, nor the binder of sheaves his bosom;
Septuagint Trans: with which the reaper has not filled his hand, nor the gatherer of sheaves his bosom.
Gallican Psalter Trans: With which the reaper does not fill his hand, nor he who gathers sheaves his bosom.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Of which the reaper shall not fill his hand, nor he who makes sheaves his bosom.

Hebrew Trans: Neither do those who pass by say, "The blessing of the LORD be upon you; we bless you in the name of the LORD."
Septuagint Trans: And those who passed by did not say, "The blessing of the Lord be upon you; we have blessed you in the name of the Lord."
Gallican Psalter Trans: And those who passed by have not said: The blessing of the Lord be upon you; we have blessed you in the name of the Lord.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Of which those passing by have not said: The blessing of the Lord be upon you; we have blessed you in the name of the Lord.

Hebrew Trans: A song of ascents. Out of the depths I have cried unto you, O LORD.
Septuagint Trans: A song of the ascents. Out of the depths I have cried to you, O Lord.
Gallican Psalter Trans: A song of ascents. Out of the depths I have cried to you, O Lord.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: A song of degrees. Out of the depths I have cried to you, O Lord.

Hebrew Trans: Lord, hear my voice; let your ears be attentive to the voice of my supplications.
Septuagint Trans: Lord, hear my voice; let your ears be attentive to the voice of my supplication.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Lord, hear my voice. Let your ears be attentive to the voice of my supplication.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Lord, hear my voice. Let your ears be attentive to the voice of my supplication.

Hebrew Trans: If you, O YAH, should mark iniquities, O Lord, who shall stand?
Septuagint Trans: If you should observe iniquities, O Lord, Lord, who shall stand?
Gallican Psalter Trans: If you will observe iniquities, Lord, Lord, who shall endure it?
Hebrew Psalter Trans: If you will observe iniquities, Lord, Lord, who shall endure it?

Hebrew Trans: For with you there is forgiveness, that you may be feared.
Septuagint Trans: For with you there is propitiation.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For with you there is propitiation.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For with you there is propitiation, though you are terrible.

Hebrew Trans: I have waited for the LORD, my soul has waited, and in his word I have hoped.
Septuagint Trans: For the sake of your law I have waited for you, O Lord; my soul has waited for your word.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Because of your law I have waited for you, O Lord. My soul has waited upon his word.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: I have waited for the Lord, my soul has waited, and in his word I have hoped.

Hebrew Trans: My soul waits for the Lord more than watchmen for the morning, watchmen for the morning.
Septuagint Trans: My soul has hoped in the Lord, from the morning watch until night; from the morning watch let Israel hope in the Lord.
Gallican Psalter Trans: My soul has hoped in the Lord, from the morning watch even until night.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: My soul waits for the Lord, from the morning watch even until the morning watch.

Hebrew Trans: Let Israel hope in the LORD, for with the LORD there is lovingkindness, and with him is abundant redemption.
Septuagint Trans: For with the Lord there is mercy, and with him there is abundant redemption.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Let Israel hope in the Lord, for with the Lord there is mercy, and with him plentiful redemption.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Let Israel hope in the Lord, for with the Lord there is mercy, and with him plentiful redemption.

Hebrew Trans: And he shall redeem Israel from all his iniquities.
Septuagint Trans: And he shall redeem Israel from all his iniquities.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And he himself shall redeem Israel from all his iniquities.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And he himself shall redeem Israel from all his iniquities.

Hebrew Trans: A Song of Ascents. Of David. O LORD, my heart is not lifted up, and my eyes are not raised high, and I have not walked in things too great and too wondrous for me.
Septuagint Trans: A song of the ascents, of David. Lord, my heart was not exalted, nor were my eyes lifted up, nor did I walk among great things, nor among wonders beyond me.
Gallican Psalter Trans: A song of degrees of David. Lord, my heart is not exalted, nor are my eyes lifted up: neither have I walked in great things, nor in wonderful things above me.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: A song of ascents. Lord, my heart is not exalted, nor are my eyes lifted up: and I have not walked in great things, nor in wonderful things above me.

Hebrew Trans: If I have not calmed and quieted my soul, like a weaned child upon its mother, like a weaned child is my soul upon me.
Septuagint Trans: If I was not humble-minded, but rather exalted my soul, like a weaned child upon its mother, so may there be recompense upon my soul.
Gallican Psalter Trans: If I did not think humbly, but exalted my soul: as a weaned child upon his mother, so is the recompense in my soul.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: If I have not set forth and made my soul to be silent, as a weaned child toward his mother, so is my soul weaned toward me.

Hebrew Trans: Let Israel hope in the LORD, from this time forth and forevermore.
Septuagint Trans: Let Israel hope in the Lord, from now and until forever.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Let Israel hope in the Lord, from this time now and forever.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Let Israel hope in the Lord, from henceforth and forever.

Hebrew Trans: A Song of Ascents. Remember, O LORD, unto David all his affliction;
Septuagint Trans: A song of ascents. Remember, O Lord, David and all his meekness.
Gallican Psalter Trans: A song of degrees. Remember, O Lord, David and all his meekness.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: A song of degrees. Remember, O Lord, David and all his affliction.

Hebrew Trans: How he swore unto the LORD, and vowed unto the Mighty One of Jacob:
Septuagint Trans: How he swore to the Lord, and vowed to the God of Jacob:
Gallican Psalter Trans: As he swore to the Lord, he vowed a vow to the God of Jacob:
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Who swore to the Lord, and vowed a vow to the God of Jacob:

Hebrew Trans: "Surely I will not come into the tent of my house, nor go up onto the bed of my couch;
Septuagint Trans: "I shall not enter into the dwelling of my house, I shall not go up upon the couch of my bed,
Gallican Psalter Trans: If I shall enter into the tabernacle of my house, if I shall ascend onto the bed of my couch,
Hebrew Psalter Trans: If I shall enter into the tabernacle of my house, if I shall sit upon the bed of my couch,

Hebrew Trans: I will not give sleep to my eyes, nor slumber to my eyelids,
Septuagint Trans: I shall not give sleep to my eyes, nor slumber to my eyelids, nor rest to my temples,
Gallican Psalter Trans: If I shall give sleep to my eyes, and slumber to my eyelids,
Hebrew Psalter Trans: If I shall give sleep to my eyes, and slumber to my eyelids,

Hebrew Trans: Until I find a place for the LORD, dwelling places for the Mighty One of Jacob."
Septuagint Trans: until I find a place for the Lord, a dwelling for the God of Jacob."
Gallican Psalter Trans: And rest to my temples, until I find a place for the Lord, a tabernacle for the God of Jacob.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Until I find a place for the Lord, tabernacles for the God of Jacob.

Hebrew Trans: Behold, we heard of it in Ephrathah; we found it in the fields of the forest.
Septuagint Trans: Behold, we heard of it in Ephrathah; we found it in the fields of the forest.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Behold, we heard of it in Ephrata; we found it in the fields of the forest.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Behold, we heard of it in Ephrata; we found it in the region of the forest.

Hebrew Trans: Let us go into his dwelling places; let us worship at his footstool.
Septuagint Trans: We shall enter into his dwellings; we shall worship at the place where his feet stood.
Gallican Psalter Trans: We shall enter into his tabernacles; we shall worship in the place where his feet have stood.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Let us enter into his tabernacles; let us worship at the footstool of his feet.

Hebrew Trans: Arise, O LORD, unto your resting place, you and the ark of your strength.
Septuagint Trans: Arise, O Lord, into your rest, you and the ark of your holiness.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Arise, O Lord, into your rest, you and the ark of your sanctification.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Arise, O Lord, into your rest, you and the ark of your strength.

Hebrew Trans: Let your priests be clothed with righteousness, and let your faithful ones sing for joy.
Septuagint Trans: Your priests shall be clothed with righteousness, and your holy ones shall rejoice.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Your priests shall be clothed with justice, and your saints shall exult.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Let your priests be clothed with justice, and let your saints praise.

Hebrew Trans: For the sake of David your servant, do not turn away the face of your anointed one.
Septuagint Trans: For the sake of David your servant, do not turn away the face of your anointed one.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For the sake of David your servant, do not turn away the face of your anointed one.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For the sake of David your servant, do not turn away the face of your anointed.

Hebrew Trans: The LORD swore unto David in truth; he will not turn back from it: "Of the fruit of your body I will set upon your throne.
Septuagint Trans: The Lord swore truth to David, and he shall by no means annul it: "From the fruit of your body I will set upon your throne.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The Lord swore truth to David, and he shall not frustrate him: Of the fruit of your womb I will set upon your throne.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The Lord swore truth to David; he will not turn from it: Of the fruit of your womb I will set upon your throne.

Hebrew Trans: If your sons keep my covenant and my testimony that I shall teach them, their sons also shall sit upon your throne forevermore."
Septuagint Trans: If your sons keep my covenant and these testimonies of mine which I shall teach them, their sons also forever shall sit upon your throne."
Gallican Psalter Trans: If your sons shall keep my testament and my testimonies, these which I shall teach them, their sons also forever shall sit upon your throne.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: If your sons shall keep my covenant and my testimony which I shall teach them, their sons also forever shall sit upon your throne.

Hebrew Trans: For the LORD has chosen Zion; he has desired it for his habitation:
Septuagint Trans: For the Lord has chosen Zion; he has desired her for a dwelling for himself.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For the Lord has chosen Zion; he has chosen it for a dwelling for himself.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For the Lord has chosen Zion; he has desired it for his dwelling.

Hebrew Trans: "This is my resting place forevermore; here I will dwell, for I have desired it.
Septuagint Trans: "This is my resting place forever and ever; here I will dwell, for I have desired her.
Gallican Psalter Trans: This is my rest forever and ever; here I will dwell, for I have chosen it.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: This is my rest forever; here I will dwell, for I have desired it.

Hebrew Trans: Her provision I will abundantly bless; her needy I will satisfy with bread.
Septuagint Trans: Her provisions I will abundantly bless; her poor I will satisfy with bread.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Blessing, I will bless her widow; her poor I will satisfy with bread.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Blessing, I will bless her provision; her poor I will satisfy with bread.

Hebrew Trans: And her priests I will clothe with salvation, and her faithful ones shall sing aloud for joy.
Septuagint Trans: Her priests I will clothe with salvation, and her holy ones shall greatly rejoice.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Her priests I will clothe with salvation, and her saints shall exult with exultation.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Her priests I will clothe with salvation, and her saints shall praise with praise.

Hebrew Trans: There I will cause a horn to sprout for David; I have prepared a lamp for my anointed one.
Septuagint Trans: There I will cause a horn to spring forth for David; I have prepared a lamp for my anointed one.
Gallican Psalter Trans: There I will bring forth a horn for David; I have prepared a lamp for my anointed one.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: There I will make a horn to spring forth for David; I have prepared a lamp for my anointed.

Hebrew Trans: His enemies I will clothe with shame, but upon him his crown shall flourish."
Septuagint Trans: His enemies I will clothe with shame, but upon him my sanctification shall blossom."
Gallican Psalter Trans: His enemies I will clothe with confusion, but upon him my sanctification shall flourish.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: His enemies I will clothe with confusion, but upon him his sanctification shall flourish.

Hebrew Trans: A song of ascents, of David. Behold, how good and how pleasant it is for brothers to dwell together in unity.
Septuagint Trans: A song of the ascents, of David. Behold now, what is good or what is pleasant but for brothers to dwell together!
Gallican Psalter Trans: A song of degrees of David. Behold, how good and how pleasant it is for brothers to dwell together in unity.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: A song of degrees of David. Behold, how good and how pleasant it is for brothers to dwell together in unity.

Hebrew Trans: It is like the fine oil upon the head, running down upon the beard, the beard of Aaron, that runs down upon the collar of his garments.
Septuagint Trans: Like ointment upon the head, running down upon the beard, the beard of Aaron, running down upon the hem of his garment.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Like the ointment upon the head, which descended into the beard, the beard of Aaron, which descended to the edge of his garment.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Like the finest ointment upon the head, which descends into the beard, the beard of Aaron, which descends upon the hem of his garments.

Hebrew Trans: Like the dew of Hermon that descends upon the mountains of Zion; for there the LORD commanded the blessing, life forevermore.
Septuagint Trans: Like the dew of Hermon, which descends upon the mountains of Zion; for there the Lord commanded the blessing, and life forevermore.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Like the dew of Hermon, which descends upon the mountains of Zion: for there the Lord commanded the blessing, and life forevermore.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Like the dew of Hermon, which descends upon the mountains of Zion: for there the Lord commanded blessing, and life forevermore.

Hebrew Trans: A song of ascents. Behold, bless the LORD, all you servants of the LORD, who stand in the house of the LORD in the nights.
Septuagint Trans: A song of the ascents. Behold now, bless the Lord, all you servants of the Lord, who stand in the house of the Lord, in the courts of the house of our God.
Gallican Psalter Trans: A song of degrees. Behold, now bless the Lord, all you servants of the Lord, who stand in the house of the Lord, in the courts of the house of our God, in the nights.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: A song of degrees: Behold, bless the Lord, all you servants of the Lord, who stand in the house of the Lord in the nights.

Hebrew Trans: Lift up your hands to the sanctuary, and bless the LORD.
Septuagint Trans: In the nights, lift up your hands to the holy places and bless the Lord.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Lift up your hands to the holy place, and bless the Lord.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Lift up your hands to the holy place, and bless the Lord.

Hebrew Trans: May the LORD bless you from Zion, the maker of heaven and earth.
Septuagint Trans: May the Lord bless you out of Zion, he who made the heaven and the earth.
Gallican Psalter Trans: May the Lord bless you out of Zion, he who made heaven and earth.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: May the Lord bless you out of Zion, the maker of heaven and earth.

Hebrew Trans: Praise the LORD! Praise the name of the LORD; praise Him, you servants of the LORD,
Septuagint Trans: Hallelujah! Praise the name of the Lord; praise, O servants, the Lord.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Alleluia! Praise the name of the Lord; praise the Lord, O servants.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Alleluia. Praise the name of the Lord; praise the Lord, you servants.

Hebrew Trans: who stand in the house of the LORD, in the courts of the house of our God.
Septuagint Trans: You who stand in the house of the Lord, in the courts of the house of our God.
Gallican Psalter Trans: You who stand in the house of the Lord, in the courts of the house of our God.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: You who stand in the house of the Lord, in the courts of the house of our God.

Hebrew Trans: Praise the LORD, for the LORD is good; sing praises to His name, for it is pleasant.
Septuagint Trans: Praise the Lord, for the Lord is good; sing psalms to his name, for it is beautiful.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Praise the Lord, for the Lord is good; sing praises to his name, for it is sweet.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Praise the Lord, for the Lord is good; sing to his name, for it is fitting.

Hebrew Trans: For the LORD has chosen Jacob for Himself, Israel as His treasured possession.
Septuagint Trans: For the Lord chose Jacob for himself, Israel as his own possession.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For the Lord has chosen Jacob for himself, Israel as his own possession.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For the Lord has chosen Jacob for himself, Israel as his own possession.

Hebrew Trans: For I know that the LORD is great, and that our Lord is above all gods.
Septuagint Trans: For I have known that the Lord is great, and our Lord is above all the gods.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For I have known that the Lord is great, and our God is above all gods.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For I know that the Lord is great, and our Lord is above all gods.

Hebrew Trans: Whatever the LORD pleases, He does, in heaven and on earth, in the seas and all the depths.
Septuagint Trans: All things whatsoever the Lord willed, he did—in heaven and on earth, in the seas and in all the depths.
Gallican Psalter Trans: All things whatsoever the Lord willed, he has done, in heaven and on earth, in the sea and in all the depths.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: All things whatsoever the Lord willed, he has done, in heaven and on earth, in the sea and in all the depths.

Hebrew Trans: He causes the clouds to rise from the ends of the earth; He makes lightning for the rain; He brings forth wind from His storehouses.
Septuagint Trans: He brings up clouds from the end of the earth; he made lightnings for the rain; he brings forth winds out of his treasuries.
Gallican Psalter Trans: He brings forth clouds from the end of the earth; he has made lightnings for the rain; he brings forth winds out of his storehouses.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Raising clouds from the ends of the earth, he made lightning into rain, bringing forth winds from his storehouses.

Hebrew Trans: He who struck the firstborn of Egypt, from man to beast.
Septuagint Trans: He who struck the firstborn of Egypt, from man to beast.
Gallican Psalter Trans: He who struck the firstborn of Egypt, from man even unto beast.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: He who struck the firstborn of Egypt, from man even to beast.

Hebrew Trans: He sent signs and wonders into your midst, O Egypt, upon Pharaoh and upon all his servants.
Septuagint Trans: He sent signs and wonders into your midst, O Egypt, upon Pharaoh and upon all his servants.
Gallican Psalter Trans: He sent signs and wonders into your midst, O Egypt, upon Pharaoh and upon all his servants.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: He sent signs and wonders into your midst, O Egypt, upon Pharaoh and upon all his servants.

Hebrew Trans: He who struck many nations and slew mighty kings:
Septuagint Trans: He who struck many nations and slew mighty kings:
Gallican Psalter Trans: He who struck many nations and slew mighty kings:
Hebrew Psalter Trans: He who struck many nations and slew mighty kings:

Hebrew Trans: Sihon, king of the Amorites, and Og, king of Bashan, and all the kingdoms of Canaan;
Septuagint Trans: Sihon, king of the Amorites, and Og, king of Bashan, and all the kingdoms of Canaan.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Sihon, king of the Amorites, and Og, king of Bashan, and all the kingdoms of Canaan;
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Sihon, king of the Amorites, and Og, king of Bashan, and all the kingdoms of Canaan;

Hebrew Trans: and He gave their land as an inheritance, an inheritance to Israel His people.
Septuagint Trans: And he gave their land as an inheritance, an inheritance to Israel his people.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And he gave their land as an inheritance, an inheritance to Israel his people.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And he gave their land as an inheritance, an inheritance to Israel his people.

Hebrew Trans: O LORD, Your name endures forever; O LORD, Your remembrance is throughout all generations.
Septuagint Trans: O Lord, your name endures forever; O Lord, your memorial endures to generation and generation.
Gallican Psalter Trans: O Lord, your name endures forever; O Lord, your memorial endures throughout all generations.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: O Lord, your name endures forever; O Lord, your memorial endures through generation and generation.

Hebrew Trans: For the LORD will judge His people, and He will have compassion on His servants.
Septuagint Trans: For the Lord will judge his people, and concerning his servants he will be comforted.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For the Lord will judge his people, and he will show compassion upon his servants.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For the Lord will judge his people, and he will be merciful toward his servants.

Hebrew Trans: The idols of the nations are silver and gold, the work of the hands of man.
Septuagint Trans: The idols of the nations are silver and gold, works of the hands of men.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The idols of the nations are silver and gold, the works of the hands of men.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The idols of the nations are silver and gold, the works of the hands of men.

Hebrew Trans: They have mouths, but they do not speak; they have eyes, but they do not see.
Septuagint Trans: They have a mouth and will not speak; they have eyes and will not see.
Gallican Psalter Trans: They have a mouth, but they shall not speak; they have eyes, but they shall not see.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: They have a mouth and they will not speak; they have eyes and they will not see.

Hebrew Trans: They have ears, but they do not hear; nor is there any breath in their mouths.
Septuagint Trans: They have ears and will not hear; they have noses and will not smell; they have hands and will not feel; they have feet and will not walk; they will not utter a sound in their throat, for neither is there breath in their mouth.
Gallican Psalter Trans: They have ears, but they shall not hear; for neither is there any breath in their mouth.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: They have ears and they will not hear; nor is there breath in their mouth.

Hebrew Trans: Those who make them will become like them, everyone who trusts in them.
Septuagint Trans: May those who make them become like them, and all who trust in them.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Let those who make them become like them, and all who trust in them.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Let those who make them become like them, and everyone who trusts in them.

Hebrew Trans: O house of Israel, bless the LORD! O house of Aaron, bless the LORD!
Septuagint Trans: O house of Israel, bless the Lord; O house of Aaron, bless the Lord.
Gallican Psalter Trans: O house of Israel, bless the Lord; O house of Aaron, bless the Lord.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: O house of Israel, bless the Lord; O house of Aaron, bless the Lord.

Hebrew Trans: O house of Levi, bless the LORD! You who fear the LORD, bless the LORD!
Septuagint Trans: O house of Levi, bless the Lord; you who fear the Lord, bless the Lord.
Gallican Psalter Trans: O house of Levi, bless the Lord; you who fear the Lord, bless the Lord.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: O house of Levi, bless the Lord; you who fear the Lord, bless the Lord.

Hebrew Trans: Blessed be the LORD from Zion, He who dwells in Jerusalem. Praise the LORD!
Septuagint Trans: Blessed be the Lord out of Zion, he who dwells in Jerusalem.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Blessed be the Lord from Zion, he who dwells in Jerusalem.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Blessed be the Lord from Zion, he who dwells in Jerusalem. Alleluia.

Hebrew Trans: Give thanks to the LORD, for He is good, for His steadfast love endures forever.
Septuagint Trans: Hallelujah! Give thanks to the Lord, for he is good, for his mercy endures forever.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Alleluia. Give thanks to the Lord, for he is good, for his mercy endures forever.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Give thanks to the Lord, for he is good: for his mercy endures forever.

Hebrew Trans: Give thanks to the God of gods, for His steadfast love endures forever.
Septuagint Trans: Give thanks to the God of gods, for his mercy endures forever.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Give thanks to the God of gods, for his mercy endures forever.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Give thanks to the God of gods: for his mercy endures forever.

Hebrew Trans: Give thanks to the Lord of lords, for His steadfast love endures forever.
Septuagint Trans: Give thanks to the Lord of lords, for his mercy endures forever.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Give thanks to the Lord of lords, for his mercy endures forever.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Give thanks to the Lord of lords: for his mercy endures forever.

Hebrew Trans: To Him who alone does great wonders, for His steadfast love endures forever.
Septuagint Trans: To him who alone does great wonders, for his mercy endures forever.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Who alone does great wonders, for his mercy endures forever.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Who alone does great wonders: for his mercy endures forever.

Hebrew Trans: To Him who made the heavens with understanding, for His steadfast love endures forever.
Septuagint Trans: To him who made the heavens with understanding, for his mercy endures forever.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Who made the heavens in understanding, for his mercy endures forever.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Who made the heavens in wisdom: for his mercy endures forever.

Hebrew Trans: To Him who spread out the earth upon the waters, for His steadfast love endures forever.
Septuagint Trans: To him who established the earth upon the waters, for his mercy endures forever.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Who established the earth upon the waters, for his mercy endures forever.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Who established the earth above the waters: for his mercy endures forever.

Hebrew Trans: To Him who made great lights, for His steadfast love endures forever.
Septuagint Trans: To him who alone made great lights, for his mercy endures forever.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Who made the great lights, for his mercy endures forever.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Who made the great lights: for his mercy endures forever.

Hebrew Trans: The sun to rule by day, for His steadfast love endures forever.
Septuagint Trans: The sun for dominion over the day, for his mercy endures forever.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The sun to rule the day, for his mercy endures forever.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The sun to rule the day: for his mercy endures forever.

Hebrew Trans: The moon and stars to rule by night, for His steadfast love endures forever.
Septuagint Trans: The moon and the stars for dominion over the night, for his mercy endures forever.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The moon and the stars to rule the night, for his mercy endures forever.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The moon and the stars to rule the night: for his mercy endures forever.

Hebrew Trans: To Him who struck Egypt through their firstborn, for His steadfast love endures forever.
Septuagint Trans: To him who struck Egypt with their firstborn, for his mercy endures forever.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Who struck Egypt with their firstborn, for his mercy endures forever.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Who struck Egypt with their firstborn: for his mercy endures forever.

Hebrew Trans: And brought out Israel from among them, for His steadfast love endures forever.
Septuagint Trans: And brought Israel out from among them, for his mercy endures forever.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Who brought Israel out from among them, for his mercy endures forever.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And brought Israel out from among them: for his mercy endures forever.

Hebrew Trans: With a strong hand and an outstretched arm, for His steadfast love endures forever.
Septuagint Trans: With a mighty hand and an uplifted arm, for his mercy endures forever.
Gallican Psalter Trans: With a mighty hand and an outstretched arm, for his mercy endures forever.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: With a strong hand and an outstretched arm: for his mercy endures forever.

Hebrew Trans: To Him who divided the Sea of Reeds into parts, for His steadfast love endures forever.
Septuagint Trans: To him who divided the Red Sea into divisions, for his mercy endures forever.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Who divided the Red Sea into divisions, for his mercy endures forever.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Who divided the Red Sea into divisions: for his mercy endures forever.

Hebrew Trans: And made Israel pass through the midst of it, for His steadfast love endures forever.
Septuagint Trans: And led Israel through the midst of it, for his mercy endures forever.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And led Israel through the midst of it, for his mercy endures forever.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And led Israel through the midst of it: for his mercy endures forever.

Hebrew Trans: And shook off Pharaoh and his host in the Sea of Reeds, for His steadfast love endures forever.
Septuagint Trans: And shook off Pharaoh and his force into the Red Sea, for his mercy endures forever.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And shook off Pharaoh and his army in the Red Sea, for his mercy endures forever.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And overthrew Pharaoh and his army in the Red Sea: for his mercy endures forever.

Hebrew Trans: To Him who led His people through the wilderness, for His steadfast love endures forever.
Septuagint Trans: To him who led his people through the wilderness, for his mercy endures forever. To him who brought water out of a sharp-cut rock, for his mercy endures forever.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Who led his people through the desert, for his mercy endures forever.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Who led his people through the desert: for his mercy endures forever.

Hebrew Trans: To Him who struck down great kings, for His steadfast love endures forever.
Septuagint Trans: To him who struck great kings, for his mercy endures forever.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Who struck down great kings, for his mercy endures forever.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Who struck down great kings: for his mercy endures forever.

Hebrew Trans: And slew mighty kings, for His steadfast love endures forever.
Septuagint Trans: And slew mighty kings, for his mercy endures forever.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And slew mighty kings, for his mercy endures forever.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And slew mighty kings: for his mercy endures forever.

Hebrew Trans: Sihon, king of the Amorites, for His steadfast love endures forever.
Septuagint Trans: Sihon, king of the Amorites, for his mercy endures forever.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Sihon, king of the Amorites, for his mercy endures forever.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Sihon, king of the Amorites: for his mercy endures forever.

Hebrew Trans: And Og, king of Bashan, for His steadfast love endures forever.
Septuagint Trans: And Og, king of Bashan, for his mercy endures forever.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And Og, king of Bashan, for his mercy endures forever.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And Og, king of Bashan: for his mercy endures forever.

Hebrew Trans: And gave their land as an inheritance, for His steadfast love endures forever.
Septuagint Trans: And gave their land as an inheritance, for his mercy endures forever.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And gave their land as an inheritance, for his mercy endures forever.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And gave their land as an inheritance: for his mercy endures forever.

Hebrew Trans: An inheritance to Israel His servant, for His steadfast love endures forever.
Septuagint Trans: An inheritance to Israel his servant, for his mercy endures forever.
Gallican Psalter Trans: An inheritance to Israel his servant, for his mercy endures forever.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: An inheritance to Israel his servant: for his mercy endures forever.

Hebrew Trans: Who remembered us in our lowliness, for His steadfast love endures forever.
Septuagint Trans: For in our humiliation the Lord remembered us, for his mercy endures forever.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For in our lowliness he was mindful of us, for his mercy endures forever.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For in our lowliness he was mindful of us: for his mercy endures forever.

Hebrew Trans: And rescued us from our adversaries, for His steadfast love endures forever.
Septuagint Trans: And redeemed us from our enemies, for his mercy endures forever.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And redeemed us from our enemies, for his mercy endures forever.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And redeemed us from our enemies: for his mercy endures forever.

Hebrew Trans: He gives food to all flesh, for His steadfast love endures forever.
Septuagint Trans: He who gives food to all flesh, for his mercy endures forever.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Who gives food to all flesh, for his mercy endures forever.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Who gives bread to all flesh: for his mercy endures forever.

Hebrew Trans: Give thanks to the God of heaven, for His steadfast love endures forever.
Septuagint Trans: Give thanks to the God of heaven, for his mercy endures forever. Give thanks to the Lord of lords, for his mercy endures forever.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Give thanks to the God of heaven, for his mercy endures forever. Give thanks to the Lord of lords, for his mercy endures forever.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Give thanks to the God of heaven: for his mercy endures forever.

Hebrew Trans: By the rivers of Babylon, there we sat down, yea, we wept, when we remembered Zion.
Septuagint Trans: Of David. By the rivers of Babylon, there we sat down and wept when we remembered Zion.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Of David, of Jeremiah. By the rivers of Babylon, there we sat and wept, when we remembered Zion.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: By the rivers of Babylon, there we sat down and wept, when we remembered Zion.

Hebrew Trans: Upon the willows in the midst thereof we hung up our harps.
Septuagint Trans: Upon the willows in the midst of it we hung up our instruments.
Gallican Psalter Trans: On the willows in the midst of it we hung up our instruments.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Upon the willows in the midst of it, we hung up our harps.

Hebrew Trans: For there our captors asked of us words of song, and our tormentors asked of us mirth: "Sing us one of the songs of Zion."
Septuagint Trans: For there those who had taken us captive asked us for words of songs, and those who had carried us away asked for a hymn: "Sing us one of the songs of Zion."
Gallican Psalter Trans: For there those who had led us captive asked us for the words of songs, and those who had carried us away said: "Sing us a hymn from the songs of Zion."
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For there those who led us captive asked of us the words of a song, and those who afflicted us asked for mirth: "Sing to us from the songs of Zion."

Hebrew Trans: How shall we sing the song of the LORD upon a foreign soil?
Septuagint Trans: How shall we sing the song of the Lord in a foreign land?
Gallican Psalter Trans: How shall we sing the song of the Lord in a foreign land?
Hebrew Psalter Trans: How shall we sing the song of the Lord in a foreign land?

Hebrew Trans: If I forget you, O Jerusalem, let my right hand forget its skill.
Septuagint Trans: If I forget you, O Jerusalem, let my right hand be forgotten.
Gallican Psalter Trans: If I forget you, O Jerusalem, let my right hand be given over to oblivion.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: If I forget you, O Jerusalem, let my right hand be forgotten.

Hebrew Trans: Let my tongue cleave to the roof of my mouth, if I do not remember you, if I do not set Jerusalem above my highest joy.
Septuagint Trans: Let my tongue cleave to my throat, if I do not remember you, if I do not set Jerusalem above the beginning of my joy.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Let my tongue cling to the roof of my mouth, if I do not remember you, if I do not place Jerusalem above the beginning of my joy.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Let my tongue cling to my palate, if I do not remember you, if I do not place Jerusalem above the beginning of my joy.

Hebrew Trans: Remember, O LORD, against the children of Edom the day of Jerusalem, who said, "Raze it, raze it, down to its foundation!"
Septuagint Trans: Remember, O Lord, the sons of Edom on the day of Jerusalem, those who said, "Raze it, raze it, down to its foundation!"
Gallican Psalter Trans: Remember, O Lord, the sons of Edom, on the day of Jerusalem, who say: "Lay it bare, lay it bare, even to its foundation!"
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Remember, O Lord, the children of Edom, in the day of Jerusalem, who said: "Raze it, raze it, even to its foundation."

Hebrew Trans: O daughter of Babylon, who are to be destroyed, happy shall he be who repays you the recompense with which you repaid us.
Septuagint Trans: O daughter of Babylon, you wretched one! Blessed is he who shall repay you the recompense with which you repaid us.
Gallican Psalter Trans: O wretched daughter of Babylon, blessed is he who shall repay to you your repayment which you repaid to us.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: O daughter of Babylon, laid waste, blessed is he who shall repay to you your recompense which you repaid to us.

Hebrew Trans: Happy shall he be who seizes and dashes your little ones against the rock.
Septuagint Trans: Blessed is he who shall seize and dash your little ones against the rock.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Blessed is he who shall seize and dash your little ones against the rock.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Blessed is he who shall seize and dash your little ones against the rock.

Hebrew Trans: Of David. I will give you thanks with all my heart; before the gods I will sing praises to you.
Septuagint Trans: Of David. I will give thanks to you, O Lord, with my whole heart, for you have heard the words of my mouth; and before angels I will sing praises to you.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Of David himself. I will confess to you, O Lord, with my whole heart, for you have heard the words of my mouth. In the sight of the angels I will sing praises to you.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Of David. I will confess to you with my whole heart; in the sight of the gods I will sing to you.

Hebrew Trans: I will bow down toward your holy temple and give thanks to your name for your lovingkindness and for your truth; for you have magnified your word above all your name.
Septuagint Trans: I will worship toward your holy temple and give thanks to your name for your mercy and your truth, for you have magnified your oracle above every name.
Gallican Psalter Trans: I will worship toward your holy temple, and I will confess your name, for your mercy and your truth: for you have magnified your holy name above all things.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: I will worship in your holy temple and I will confess to your name for your mercy and for your truth, for you have magnified your word above all your name.

Hebrew Trans: In the day that I called, you answered me; you emboldened me with strength in my soul.
Septuagint Trans: In whatever day I call upon you, quickly hear me; you will enrich me in my soul with power.
Gallican Psalter Trans: In whatever day I shall call upon you, hear me: you shall multiply strength in my soul.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: In the day I shall call upon you, and you will hear me; you will enlarge the strength of my soul.

Hebrew Trans: All the kings of the earth shall give you thanks, O LORD, for they have heard the words of your mouth.
Septuagint Trans: Let all the kings of the earth give thanks to you, O Lord, for they have heard all the words of your mouth.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Let all the kings of the earth confess to you, O Lord, for they have heard all the words of your mouth.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Let all the kings of the earth confess to you, O Lord, for they have heard the words of your mouth.

Hebrew Trans: And they shall sing of the ways of the LORD, for great is the glory of the LORD.
Septuagint Trans: And let them sing in the ways of the Lord, for great is the glory of the Lord.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And let them sing in the ways of the Lord, for great is the glory of the Lord.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And let them sing in the ways of the Lord, for great is the glory of the Lord.

Hebrew Trans: For though the LORD is high, yet he regards the lowly; but the haughty he knows from afar.
Septuagint Trans: For the Lord is exalted, yet he regards the lowly, and the lofty he knows from afar.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For the Lord is exalted, and he regards the lowly, and the lofty he knows from afar.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For the Lord is exalted, and he regards the humble, and the lofty he knows from afar.

Hebrew Trans: If I walk in the midst of trouble, you will revive me; you will stretch forth your hand against the wrath of my enemies, and your right hand shall save me.
Septuagint Trans: If I walk in the midst of affliction, you will give me life; against the wrath of my enemies you stretched out your hand, and your right hand saved me.
Gallican Psalter Trans: If I shall walk in the midst of tribulation, you will give me life; you have stretched out your hand against the wrath of my enemies, and your right hand has saved me.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: If I shall walk in the midst of tribulation, you will give me life; against the fury of my enemies you will stretch forth your hand, and your right hand shall save me.

Hebrew Trans: The LORD will accomplish that which concerns me; O LORD, your lovingkindness endures forever; do not forsake the works of your hands.
Septuagint Trans: The Lord will repay on my behalf. O Lord, your mercy endures forever; do not forsake the works of your hands.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The Lord will repay on my behalf. O Lord, your mercy endures forever; do not despise the works of your hands.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The Lord will act on my behalf. O Lord, your mercy endures forever; do not forsake the works of your hands.

Hebrew Trans: For the choirmaster. Of David. A psalm. O LORD, you have searched me and known me.
Septuagint Trans: For the end, a psalm of David. O Lord, you have tested me and known me.
Gallican Psalter Trans: To the end, a psalm of David. 138. O Lord, you have tested me and known me.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For the victory of David, a song. O Lord, you have searched me and known me.

Hebrew Trans: You know my sitting down and my rising up; you discern my thoughts from afar.
Septuagint Trans: You have known my sitting down and my rising up; you have understood my thoughts from afar.
Gallican Psalter Trans: You have known my sitting down and my rising up; you have understood my thoughts from afar.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: You have known my sitting down and my rising up; you have understood my thought from afar.

Hebrew Trans: My path and my lying down you have winnowed, and you are acquainted with all my ways.
Septuagint Trans: My path and my measuring-line you have traced out, and all my ways you have foreseen.
Gallican Psalter Trans: You have traced out my path and my lot, and you have foreseen all my ways.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: My path and my lying down you have winnowed, and all my ways you have understood.

Hebrew Trans: For there is not a word on my tongue— behold, O LORD, you know it altogether.
Septuagint Trans: For there is no word on my tongue—
Gallican Psalter Trans: For there is not a word on my tongue — behold, O Lord, you have known all things.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For there is not a word on my tongue—behold, O Lord, you know all things.

Hebrew Trans: Behind and before you have enclosed me, and you have laid your hand upon me.
Septuagint Trans: Behold, O Lord, you have known all things, the last and the first. You formed me and laid your hand upon me.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The last things and the first — you have formed me, and you have laid your hand upon me.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Behind and before you have formed me, and you have laid your hand upon me.

Hebrew Trans: Such knowledge is too wonderful for me; it is too high, I cannot attain it.
Septuagint Trans: Your knowledge was made wondrous from me; it was made strong; I cannot attain to it.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Your knowledge has become wonderful beyond me; it is strengthened, and I cannot reach it.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Such knowledge is above me and is too lofty; I cannot attain to it.

Hebrew Trans: Where shall I go from your Spirit? And where shall I flee from your presence?
Septuagint Trans: Where shall I go from your spirit? And where shall I flee from your face?
Gallican Psalter Trans: Where shall I go from your spirit? And where shall I flee from your face?
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Where shall I go from your spirit? And where shall I flee from your face?

Hebrew Trans: If I ascend to heaven, you are there; and if I make my bed in Sheol, behold, you are there.
Septuagint Trans: If I ascend into heaven, you are there; if I descend into Hades, you are present.
Gallican Psalter Trans: If I ascend into heaven, you are there; if I descend into hell, you are present.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: If I ascend into heaven, you are there; if I lie down in hell, you are present.

Hebrew Trans: If I take the wings of the dawn, if I dwell in the uttermost parts of the sea,
Septuagint Trans: If I should take up my wings at dawn and dwell in the uttermost parts of the sea,
Gallican Psalter Trans: If I take up my wings at dawn and dwell in the uttermost parts of the sea,
Hebrew Psalter Trans: If I take up the wings of the dawn, and dwell in the farthest parts of the sea,

Hebrew Trans: Even there your hand shall lead me, and your right hand shall hold me.
Septuagint Trans: Even there your hand shall guide me, and your right hand shall hold me.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Even there your hand shall lead me, and your right hand shall hold me.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Even there your hand shall lead me, and your right hand shall hold me.

Hebrew Trans: And I said, "Surely the darkness shall cover me, and the night shall be light about me."
Septuagint Trans: And I said, "Surely darkness shall trample me, and night shall be illumination in my delight."
Gallican Psalter Trans: And I said: Perhaps darkness shall trample upon me, and the night shall be illumination in my pleasures.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: If I say, "Perhaps darkness will cover me," the night also shall be light around me.

Hebrew Trans: Even the darkness is not dark to you, and the night shines as the day; the darkness is as the light.
