Septuagint Trans: For darkness shall not be darkened before you, and night shall be illuminated as day; as is its darkness, so also shall be its light.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For darkness shall not be darkened before you, and the night shall be illuminated like the day; as is its darkness, so also is its light.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Nor do the darkness hold any darkness before you, and night shines as the day; darkness and light are alike.

Hebrew Trans: For you created my inward parts; you wove me together in my mother's womb.
Septuagint Trans: For you acquired my inward parts, O Lord; you took hold of me from my mother's womb.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For you have possessed my inmost parts; you received me from the womb of my mother.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For you possessed my inmost parts; you wove me together in the womb of my mother.

Hebrew Trans: I will praise you, for I am fearfully and wonderfully made; wonderful are your works, and my soul knows it well.
Septuagint Trans: I will give thanks to you, for I was fearfully made wondrous; marvelous are your works, and my soul knows it exceedingly.
Gallican Psalter Trans: I will praise you, for you are terribly magnified; wonderful are your works, and my soul knows it exceedingly.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: I will praise you, for you have made me fearfully magnificent; wondrous are your works, and my soul knows this exceedingly.

Hebrew Trans: My frame was not hidden from you when I was made in secret, when I was woven together in the depths of the earth.
Septuagint Trans: My bone was not hidden from you, which you made in secret, and my substance in the lowest parts of the earth.
Gallican Psalter Trans: My bone was not hidden from you, which you made in secret, and my substance in the lower parts of the earth.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: My bones were not hidden from you, when I was made in secret; I was fashioned in the lowest parts of the earth.

Hebrew Trans: Your eyes saw my unformed substance, and in your book they were all written— the days that were fashioned, when as yet there was not one among them.
Septuagint Trans: Your eyes saw my unformed substance, and in your book all shall be written; days shall be formed, and no one among them.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Your eyes saw my imperfect being, and in your book all shall be written; they shall be formed in a day, and no one among them.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Your eyes saw me yet unformed, and in your book all shall be written; days were formed when not yet one of them existed.

Hebrew Trans: And to me, how precious are your thoughts, O God! How vast is the sum of them!
Septuagint Trans: But to me your friends were greatly honored, O God; greatly strengthened were their first principles.
Gallican Psalter Trans: But to me your friends are exceedingly honored, O God; their principalities are exceedingly strengthened.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: But to me, how honorable are your friends, O God! How mighty are their multitudes!

Hebrew Trans: If I should count them, they are more than the sand; when I awake, I am still with you.
Septuagint Trans: I shall count them, and they shall be multiplied beyond the sand; I have awakened, and I am still with you.
Gallican Psalter Trans: I shall count them, and they shall be multiplied beyond the sand; I have risen, and I am still with you.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: I shall count them, and they shall be more numerous than the sand; I have awakened, and I am still with you.

Hebrew Trans: If only you would slay the wicked, O God! O men of blood, depart from me!
Septuagint Trans: If you would slay sinners, O God— O men of blood, depart from me!
Gallican Psalter Trans: If you would slay sinners, O God — and you men of blood, depart from me —
Hebrew Psalter Trans: If you would slay the wicked, O God—O men of blood, depart from me—

Hebrew Trans: They who speak of you for evil purpose; your enemies take your name in vain.
Septuagint Trans: For you shall speak in deliberation; they shall take your cities in vain.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For you will say in thought: they shall receive your cities in vain.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Who will contradict you wickedly; they are lifted up in vain, your adversaries.

Hebrew Trans: Do I not hate those who hate you, O LORD? And do I not loathe those who rise up against you?
Septuagint Trans: Have I not hated those who hate you, O Lord? And have I not wasted away over your enemies?
Gallican Psalter Trans: Did I not hate those who hate you, O Lord, and waste away over your enemies?
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Do I not hate those who hate you, O Lord, and do I not loathe your adversaries?

Hebrew Trans: With perfect hatred I hate them; they have become my enemies.
Septuagint Trans: With perfect hatred I hated them; they became enemies to me.
Gallican Psalter Trans: With a perfect hatred I hated them; they have become enemies to me.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: With perfect hatred I hated them; they have become enemies to me.

Hebrew Trans: Search me, O God, and know my heart; try me and know my anxious thoughts.
Septuagint Trans: Test me, O God, and know my heart; examine me and know my paths.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Test me, O God, and know my heart; examine me and know my paths.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Search me, O God, and know my heart; test me and know my thoughts.

Hebrew Trans: And see if there be any grievous way in me, and lead me in the way everlasting.
Septuagint Trans: And see if there is a way of lawlessness in me, and guide me in the everlasting way.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And see if there is a way of iniquity in me, and lead me in the eternal way.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And see if there is any way of idolatry in me, and lead me in the everlasting way.

Hebrew Trans: For the choirmaster, a psalm of David.
Septuagint Trans: For the end, a psalm of David.
Gallican Psalter Trans: To the end, a psalm of David.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For the victory, a song of David.

Hebrew Trans: Deliver me, O LORD, from evil men; preserve me from men of violence,
Septuagint Trans: Deliver me, O Lord, from the evil man; rescue me from the unjust man.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Deliver me, O Lord, from the evil man: from the wicked man rescue me.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Deliver me, O Lord, from the evil man: from unjust men preserve me.

Hebrew Trans: who have devised evil things in their heart; every day they stir up wars.
Septuagint Trans: Who devised injustices in their heart; all the day long they arrayed themselves for wars.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Who have devised iniquities in their heart: all day long they plotted battles.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Who have devised malice in their heart; all day long they have engaged in battles.

Hebrew Trans: They have sharpened their tongue like a serpent; the venom of a viper is under their lips. Selah.
Septuagint Trans: They sharpened their tongue like a serpent's; the venom of asps is under their lips. Selah.
Gallican Psalter Trans: They have sharpened their tongues like a serpent: the venom of asps is under their lips. Diapsalma.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: They have sharpened their tongue like a serpent: the venom of the asp is always under their lips.

Hebrew Trans: Guard me, O LORD, from the hands of the wicked; preserve me from men of violence, who have purposed to push aside my steps.
Septuagint Trans: Guard me, O Lord, from the hand of the sinner; deliver me from unjust men, who have devised to trip up my steps.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Preserve me, O Lord, from the hand of the sinner: from wicked men deliver me, who have purposed to trip my steps.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Guard me, O Lord, from the hand of the wicked: from the man of iniquities preserve me, who have plotted to trip my steps.

Hebrew Trans: The proud have hidden a snare for me, and cords; they have spread a net by the side of the path; they have set traps for me. Selah.
Septuagint Trans: The proud hid a snare for me, and stretched out cords as traps for my feet; they set a stumbling block for me near the path. Selah.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The proud have hidden a snare for me, and they have stretched out cords as a trap: by the wayside they have set stumbling blocks for me. Diapsalma.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The proud have hidden a snare for me, and with cords they have spread a net beside the path; they have set stumbling blocks for me always.

Hebrew Trans: I said to the LORD, "You are my God"; give ear, O LORD, to the voice of my supplications.
Septuagint Trans: I said to the Lord, "You are my God; give ear, O Lord, to the voice of my supplication."
Gallican Psalter Trans: I said to the Lord: You are my God; hear, O Lord, the voice of my supplication.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: I said to the Lord: You are my God; hear, O Lord, the voice of my supplication.

Hebrew Trans: O LORD, my Lord, the strength of my salvation, You have covered my head in the day of battle.
Septuagint Trans: O Lord, Lord, the strength of my salvation, you have overshadowed my head in the day of war.
Gallican Psalter Trans: O Lord, Lord, the strength of my salvation: you have overshadowed my head in the day of battle.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: O Lord God, the strength of my salvation: you have sheltered my head in the day of battle.

Hebrew Trans: Do not grant, O LORD, the desires of the wicked; do not let his scheme succeed, lest they be exalted. Selah.
Septuagint Trans: Do not deliver me, O Lord, from my desire to the sinner; they have plotted against me; do not forsake me, lest they be exalted. Selah.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Do not deliver me, O Lord, from my desire to the sinner: they have plotted against me; do not forsake me, lest they be exalted. Diapsalma.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Do not grant, O Lord, the desires of the wicked; let not his schemes be poured out and exalted forever.

Hebrew Trans: As for the head of those who surround me, let the mischief of their own lips cover them.
Septuagint Trans: The head of those who encircle me—the toil of their lips shall cover them.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The head of their encirclement — the labor of their own lips shall cover them.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The bitterness of those who surround me—let the labor of their lips cover them.

Hebrew Trans: Let burning coals fall upon them; let them be cast into the fire, into deep pits, that they rise not again.
Septuagint Trans: Coals of fire shall fall upon them; you shall cast them down in miseries; they shall not stand.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Burning coals shall fall upon them; into fire you shall cast them down: in miseries they shall not stand.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Coals of fire shall fall upon them; you shall cast them down into pits, so that they shall not rise again.

Hebrew Trans: A man of evil tongue shall not be established in the earth; as for the man of violence and evil, may calamity hunt him with thrust upon thrust.
Septuagint Trans: A man of much talk shall not prosper upon the earth; evils shall hunt the unjust man to destruction.
Gallican Psalter Trans: A man of evil tongue shall not be directed upon the earth: evils shall seize the unjust man unto destruction.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: A man of evil tongue shall not be established in the earth: evil things shall seize the unjust man unto destruction.

Hebrew Trans: I know that the LORD will maintain the cause of the afflicted, and the justice of the needy.
Septuagint Trans: I know that the Lord will execute the judgment of the poor and the cause of the needy.
Gallican Psalter Trans: I know that the Lord will execute judgment for the needy, and vindication for the poor.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: I know that the Lord will take up the cause of the needy, and the judgments of the poor.

Hebrew Trans: Surely the righteous shall give thanks to Your name; the upright shall dwell in Your presence.
Septuagint Trans: Surely the righteous shall give thanks to your name, and the upright shall dwell in your presence.
Gallican Psalter Trans: But surely the just shall give thanks to your name: the upright shall dwell in your presence.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Surely the just shall give thanks to your name: the upright shall dwell with your countenance.

Hebrew Trans: A psalm of David. LORD, I call upon You; hasten to me; give ear to my voice when I call to You.
Septuagint Trans: A Psalm of David. Lord, I cried out to you; hear me. Give heed to the voice of my supplication when I cry to you.
Gallican Psalter Trans: A psalm of David. O Lord, I have cried to you: hear me. Attend to my voice when I cry to you.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: A canticle of David. Lord, I have cried to you; hasten to me. Hear my voice when I cry to you.

Hebrew Trans: Let my prayer be set forth as incense before You, the lifting up of my hands as the evening offering.
Septuagint Trans: Let my prayer be directed as incense before you, the lifting up of my hands as an evening sacrifice.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Let my prayer be directed as incense in your sight; the lifting up of my hands as an evening sacrifice.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Let my prayer be directed as incense in your sight; the lifting up of my hands as an evening sacrifice.

Hebrew Trans: Set, O LORD, a guard over my mouth; keep watch over the door of my lips.
Septuagint Trans: Set, O Lord, a guard on my mouth and a door of enclosure around my lips.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Set a guard, O Lord, over my mouth, and a door of enclosure around my lips.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Set a guard, O Lord, over my mouth; keep watch over the poverty of my lips.

Hebrew Trans: Do not incline my heart to any evil thing, to practice deeds in wickedness with men who are workers of iniquity, and let me not eat of their dainties.
Septuagint Trans: Do not incline my heart to words of wickedness, to make excuses with excuses in sins, with men who work lawlessness; and I will not partake with their chosen ones.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Do not incline my heart to words of malice, to make excuses in sins, with men who work iniquity; and I will not partake with their chosen ones.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Incline not my heart to evil words, to turn over impious thoughts with men who work iniquity; and let me not eat of their delicacies.

Hebrew Trans: Let the righteous strike me—it is a kindness; and let him reprove me—it is oil upon the head; let not my head refuse it; for yet my prayer also shall be against their evil deeds.
Septuagint Trans: A righteous man will discipline me in mercy and reprove me; but let not the oil of a sinner anoint my head, for yet even my prayer is against their pleasures.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The just man shall correct me in mercy and shall rebuke me; but let not the oil of the sinner fatten my head. For yet my prayer also shall be in their good pleasures.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Let the just man correct me in mercy and rebuke me; let not the oil of bitterness anoint my head; for my prayer also is against their wickedness.

Hebrew Trans: Their judges are thrown down by the sides of the rock, and they shall hear my words, for they are sweet.
Septuagint Trans: Their judges were swallowed up near the rock; they shall hear my words, for they are pleasant.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Their judges, joined to the rock, have been swallowed up. They shall hear my words, for they have prevailed.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Their judges have been cast down by the rock, and they shall hear my words, for they are gracious.

Hebrew Trans: As when one plows and cleaves the earth, our bones are scattered at the mouth of Sheol.
Septuagint Trans: As when the thickness of earth is broken upon the ground, our bones have been scattered at the mouth of Hades.
Gallican Psalter Trans: As a clod of earth is broken upon the ground, our bones have been scattered at the mouth of the grave.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: As a farmer when he breaks open the earth, so our bones have been scattered at the mouth of the grave.

Hebrew Trans: For to You, O LORD, my Lord, are my eyes; in You I take refuge; do not pour out my soul.
Septuagint Trans: For toward you, Lord, Lord, are my eyes; in you I have hoped. Do not take away my soul.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For to you, O Lord, Lord, are my eyes: in you I have hoped; do not take away my soul.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For to you, O Lord God, are my eyes; in you I have hoped; do not strip away my soul.

Hebrew Trans: Keep me from the hands of the snare they have laid for me, and from the traps of the workers of iniquity.
Septuagint Trans: Guard me from the snare they have set for me, and from the stumbling-blocks of those who work lawlessness.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Keep me from the snare which they have set for me, and from the stumbling blocks of those who work iniquity.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Keep me from the hands of the snare which they have set for me, and from the stumbling blocks of those who work iniquity.

Hebrew Trans: Let the wicked fall into their own nets together, while I pass by safely.
Septuagint Trans: Sinners shall fall into his net; I am alone until I pass through.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The sinners shall fall into his net: I am alone, until I pass over.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The wicked shall fall into his net together; but I shall pass beyond.

Hebrew Trans: A Maskil of David, when he was in the cave; a prayer.
Septuagint Trans: A meditation of David, when he was in the cave; a prayer.
Gallican Psalter Trans: A psalm of understanding of David, when he was in the cave: a prayer.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: A lesson of David, when he was in the cave: a prayer.

Hebrew Trans: With my voice I cry unto the LORD; with my voice I make supplication unto the LORD.
Septuagint Trans: With my voice I cried unto the Lord; with my voice I made supplication unto the Lord.
Gallican Psalter Trans: With my voice I cried out to the Lord; with my voice I made supplication to the Lord.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: With my voice I cried out to the Lord; with my voice I made supplication to the Lord.

Hebrew Trans: I pour out my complaint before him; I declare my trouble before him.
Septuagint Trans: I pour out my petition before him; I declare my affliction before him.
Gallican Psalter Trans: I pour out my supplication before him; I declare my tribulation before him.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: I pour out my speech before him; I declare my tribulation in his presence.

Hebrew Trans: When my spirit is overwhelmed within me, you know my path; in the way wherein I walk they have hidden a snare for me.
Septuagint Trans: When my spirit was failing within me, then you knew my paths. In this way in which I was walking, they hid a snare for me.
Gallican Psalter Trans: When my spirit was failing within me, you knew my paths. In this way in which I walked, they hid a snare for me.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: When my spirit was anxious within me, for you knew my path; in this way in which I walk, they have hidden a snare for me.

Hebrew Trans: Look on my right hand and see, for there is no one who acknowledges me; refuge has failed me; no one cares for my soul.
Septuagint Trans: I was looking to the right and observing, but there was no one who recognized me. Escape perished from me, and there is no one who seeks after my soul.
Gallican Psalter Trans: I looked to the right hand and saw, and there was no one who would recognize me. Flight has perished from me, and there is no one who seeks after my soul.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Look to the right hand and see, for there is no one who knows me; flight has perished from me; there is no one who seeks after my soul.

Hebrew Trans: I cried unto you, O LORD; I said, "You are my refuge, my portion in the land of the living."
Septuagint Trans: I cried unto you, O Lord; I said, "You are my hope, my portion in the land of the living."
Gallican Psalter Trans: I cried out to you, O Lord; I said: You are my hope, my portion in the land of the living.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: I cried out to you, O Lord; I said: you are my hope, my portion in the land of the living.

Hebrew Trans: Attend unto my cry, for I am brought very low; deliver me from my persecutors, for they are stronger than I.
Septuagint Trans: Attend to my supplication, for I have been brought very low. Deliver me from those who persecute me, for they have become stronger than I.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Attend to my supplication, for I have been humbled exceedingly. Deliver me from those who persecute me, for they have been strengthened over me.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Hear my supplication, for I am brought very low; deliver me from my persecutors, for they are stronger than I.

Hebrew Trans: Bring my soul out of prison, that I may give thanks unto your name; the righteous shall surround me, for you shall deal bountifully with me.
Septuagint Trans: Bring my soul out of prison, that I may give thanks to your name, O Lord. The righteous wait for me, until you recompense me.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Lead my soul out of prison, that I may confess your name. The just await me, until you reward me.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Lead my soul out of prison, that it may confess your name; the just await me, when you shall have rewarded me.

Hebrew Trans: A psalm of David. O LORD, hear my prayer, give ear to my supplications; in your faithfulness answer me, in your righteousness.
Septuagint Trans: A psalm of David, when his son was pursuing him. O Lord, hear my prayer; give ear to my supplication. In your truth, hearken to me; in your righteousness.
Gallican Psalter Trans: A psalm of David, when his son was pursuing him. O Lord, hear my prayer; with your ears perceive my supplication; in your truth hear me, in your justice.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: A song of David. O Lord, hear my prayer; listen to my supplication. In your truth, hear me; in your justice.

Hebrew Trans: And enter not into judgment with your servant, for no living being shall be justified before you.
Septuagint Trans: And do not enter into judgment with your servant, for no one living shall be justified before you.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And do not enter into judgment with your servant, for no living person shall be justified in your sight.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And do not come to judgment with your servant, for no living person shall be justified in your sight.

Hebrew Trans: For the enemy has pursued my soul; he has crushed my life to the ground; he has made me dwell in darkness, like those long dead.
Septuagint Trans: For the enemy has pursued my soul; he has humbled my life to the ground; he has made me dwell in darkness like the dead of ages past.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For the enemy has persecuted my soul; he has brought low my life to the earth; he has placed me in dark places, like the dead of ages past.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For the enemy has persecuted my soul; he has crushed my life to the ground; he has placed me in darkness, like the dead of old.

Hebrew Trans: And my spirit is overwhelmed upon me; within me my heart is desolate.
Septuagint Trans: And my spirit grew weary within me; my heart was troubled within me.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And my spirit is anxious within me; my heart within me is troubled.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And my spirit was anxious within me; in my midst my heart was troubled.

Hebrew Trans: I remember the days of old; I meditate on all your works; I muse on the work of your hands.
Septuagint Trans: I remembered the days of old, and I meditated on all your works; I meditated on the works of your hands.
Gallican Psalter Trans: I remembered the days of old; I meditated on all your works; I was meditating on the deeds of your hands.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: I remembered the days of old; I meditated on all your works; I spoke of the deeds of your hands.

Hebrew Trans: I spread out my hands to you; my soul thirsts for you like a weary land. Selah.
Septuagint Trans: I spread out my hands toward you; my soul is like a waterless land thirsting for you. Selah.
Gallican Psalter Trans: I stretched out my hands to you; my soul is like land without water before you. Selah.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: I stretched out my hands to you; my soul is like a thirsting land for you, always.

Hebrew Trans: Answer me speedily, O LORD; my spirit fails. Hide not your face from me, lest I become like those who go down into the pit.
Septuagint Trans: Hear me quickly, O Lord; my spirit has failed. Do not turn your face from me, lest I become like those who go down into the pit.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Quickly hear me, O Lord; my spirit has failed. Do not turn your face from me, lest I become like those who descend into the pit.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Quickly hear me, O Lord; my spirit has failed. Do not hide your face from me, lest I be compared to those who descend into the pit.

Hebrew Trans: Cause me to hear your lovingkindness in the morning, for in you I trust; make known to me the way in which I should walk, for I lift up my soul to you.
Septuagint Trans: Make me hear your mercy in the morning, for in you I have hoped. Make known to me, O Lord, the way in which I shall walk, for I have lifted up my soul to you.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Make me hear your mercy in the morning, for in you I have hoped. Make known to me the way in which I should walk, for to you I have lifted up my soul.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Make me hear your mercy in the morning, for in you I trust. Make known to me the way in which I should walk, for to you I have lifted up my soul.

Hebrew Trans: Deliver me from my enemies, O LORD; to you I flee for refuge.
Septuagint Trans: Deliver me from my enemies, O Lord, for I have fled to you for refuge.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Deliver me from my enemies, O Lord; to you I have fled for refuge.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Deliver me from my enemies, O Lord; by you I am protected.

Hebrew Trans: Teach me to do your will, for you are my God; let your good spirit lead me in a land of uprightness.
Septuagint Trans: Teach me to do your will, for you are my God. Your good spirit shall guide me in an upright land.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Teach me to do your will, for you are my God. Your good spirit will lead me into the upright land.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Teach me to do your will, for you are my God. Your good spirit shall lead me into the upright land.

Hebrew Trans: For your name's sake, O LORD, revive me; in your righteousness bring my soul out of trouble.
Septuagint Trans: For the sake of your name, O Lord, you shall give me life; in your righteousness you shall bring my soul out of affliction.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For the sake of your name, O Lord, you will give me life; in your equity you will bring my soul out of tribulation.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For the sake of your name, O Lord, you shall give me life; in your justice you shall bring my soul out of distress.

Hebrew Trans: And in your lovingkindness cut off my enemies, and destroy all those who afflict my soul, for I am your servant.
Septuagint Trans: And in your mercy you shall destroy my enemies, and you shall bring to ruin all those who afflict my soul, for I am your servant.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And in your mercy you will destroy my enemies, and you will bring to ruin all who afflict my soul, for I am your servant.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And in your mercy you shall scatter my enemies, and you shall destroy all those who afflict my soul, for I am your servant.

Hebrew Trans: Of David. Blessed be the LORD, my rock, who trains my hands for battle, my fingers for war.
Septuagint Trans: By David, against Goliath. Blessed be the Lord my God, who teaches my hands for battle, my fingers for war.
Gallican Psalter Trans: David against Goliath. Blessed be the Lord my God, who teaches my hands to fight, my fingers to war.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Of David. Blessed be the Lord, my strength, who teaches my hands to fight, my fingers to wage war.

Hebrew Trans: My lovingkindness and my fortress, my stronghold and my deliverer, my shield, and in whom I take refuge, who subdues my people under me.
Septuagint Trans: My mercy and my refuge, my supporter and my deliverer, my shield—and in him I have hoped—who subdues my people under me.
Gallican Psalter Trans: My mercy and my refuge, my supporter and my deliverer, my protector, and in him I have hoped, who subdues my people under me.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: My mercy and my fortress, my helper and my savior, my shield, and in him I have hoped, who has subjected peoples under me.

Hebrew Trans: O LORD, what is man that you take knowledge of him, the son of man that you think of him?
Septuagint Trans: Lord, what is man that you have made yourself known to him, or the son of man that you take account of him?
Gallican Psalter Trans: Lord, what is man, that you have made yourself known to him? Or the son of man, that you regard him?
Hebrew Psalter Trans: O Lord, what is man that you take notice of him, or the son of man that you regard him?

Hebrew Trans: Man is like a breath; his days are like a passing shadow.
Septuagint Trans: Man is likened to vanity; his days pass away like a shadow.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Man is made like to vanity: his days pass away like a shadow.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Man is likened to vanity; his days are as a shadow passing away.

Hebrew Trans: O LORD, bow your heavens and come down; touch the mountains and they shall smoke.
Septuagint Trans: Lord, bow your heavens and come down; touch the mountains and they shall smoke.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Lord, incline your heavens and descend; touch the mountains and they shall smoke.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: O Lord, bow down your heavens and descend; touch the mountains and they shall smoke.

Hebrew Trans: Flash forth lightning and scatter them; send out your arrows and confound them.
Septuagint Trans: Flash forth lightning and you shall scatter them; send out your arrows and you shall throw them into confusion.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Flash forth lightning and you shall scatter them; send out your arrows and you shall throw them into confusion.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Flash forth lightning and scatter them; send out your arrows and destroy them.

Hebrew Trans: Stretch out your hands from on high; rescue me and deliver me from great waters, from the hand of foreigners,
Septuagint Trans: Send forth your hand from on high; rescue me and deliver me from many waters, from the hand of the sons of strangers,
Gallican Psalter Trans: Send forth your hand from on high; rescue me and deliver me from many waters, from the hand of the sons of strangers,
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Stretch forth your hand from on high; deliver me and rescue me from many waters, from the hand of the sons of strangers,

Hebrew Trans: whose mouths speak falsehood, and whose right hand is a right hand of deceit.
Septuagint Trans: whose mouth has spoken vanity, and whose right hand is a right hand of unrighteousness.
Gallican Psalter Trans: whose mouth has spoken vanity, and whose right hand is a right hand of iniquity.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: whose mouth has spoken vanity, and whose right hand is a right hand of falsehood.

Hebrew Trans: O God, I will sing a new song to you; on a ten-stringed harp I will sing praises to you,
Septuagint Trans: O God, I will sing a new song to you; on a ten-stringed psaltery I will play to you,
Gallican Psalter Trans: O God, I will sing a new song to you; on a psaltery of ten strings I will sing praises to you,
Hebrew Psalter Trans: O God, I will sing a new song to you; on a psaltery of ten strings I will sing praises to you,

Hebrew Trans: who gives salvation to kings, who rescues David his servant from the evil sword.
Septuagint Trans: who gives salvation to kings, who redeems David his servant from the evil sword.
Gallican Psalter Trans: who gives salvation to kings, who redeems David his servant from the malignant sword.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: who gives salvation to kings, who rescued David his servant from the grievous sword.

Hebrew Trans: Rescue me and deliver me from the hand of foreigners, whose mouths speak falsehood, and whose right hand is a right hand of deceit.
Septuagint Trans: Rescue me and deliver me from the hand of the sons of strangers, whose mouth has spoken vanity, and whose right hand is a right hand of unrighteousness;
Gallican Psalter Trans: Rescue me and deliver me from the hand of the sons of foreigners, whose mouth has spoken vanity, and whose right hand is a right hand of iniquity;
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Deliver me and rescue me from the hand of the sons of strangers, whose mouth has spoken vanity, and whose right hand is a right hand of falsehood;

Hebrew Trans: May our sons be like plants grown tall in their youth; our daughters like corner pillars, carved in the fashion of a palace.
Septuagint Trans: whose sons are like young plants, established in their youth; whose daughters are adorned, decorated like the likeness of a temple;
Gallican Psalter Trans: whose sons are like new plants in their youth; whose daughters are adorned, decorated round about, as the likeness of a temple;
Hebrew Psalter Trans: that our sons may be as a growing plantation in their youth; that our daughters may be as corners adorned after the likeness of a temple;

Hebrew Trans: May our granaries be full, supplying every kind of produce; may our flocks be thousands and ten thousands in our fields.
Septuagint Trans: their storehouses are full, overflowing from this to that; their sheep are prolific, multiplying in their pastures;
Gallican Psalter Trans: whose storehouses are full, overflowing from this to that; whose sheep are fruitful, abounding in their goings forth;
Hebrew Psalter Trans: that our storehouses may be full, overflowing from this into that; that our flocks may be in thousands and innumerable in our open places;

Hebrew Trans: May our oxen be heavily laden; may there be no breach, no going forth, and no outcry in our streets.
Septuagint Trans: their oxen are fat; there is no breach of wall, nor passage through, nor outcry in their streets.
Gallican Psalter Trans: whose oxen are fat; there is no ruin of the wall, nor passage through, nor outcry in their streets.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: that our oxen may be fat; that there be no breach, and no going out, and no cry of anguish in our streets.

Hebrew Trans: Happy is the people for whom it is so; happy is the people whose God is the LORD.
Septuagint Trans: They called blessed the people for whom these things are so; blessed is the people whose God is the Lord.
Gallican Psalter Trans: They have called the people blessed to whom these things belong; blessed is the people whose Lord is its God.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Blessed is the people whose lot is such; blessed is the people whose Lord is their God.

Hebrew Trans: A praise of David. I will exalt you, my God, the King, and I will bless your name forever and ever.
Septuagint Trans: A praise of David. I will exalt you, my God, my King, and I will bless your name forever and to the age of ages.
Gallican Psalter Trans: A praise of David. I will exalt you, my God and King, and I will bless your name forever and ever.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: A hymn of David. I will exalt you, my God, my king, and I will bless your name forever and beyond.

Hebrew Trans: Every day I will bless you, and I will praise your name forever and ever.
Septuagint Trans: Every day I will bless you, and I will praise your name forever and to the age of ages.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Every single day I will bless you, and I will praise your name forever and ever.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Every day I will bless you, and I will praise your name forever, continually.

Hebrew Trans: Great is the LORD, and greatly to be praised, and his greatness is unsearchable.
Septuagint Trans: Great is the Lord and greatly to be praised, and of his greatness there is no end.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Great is the Lord, and greatly to be praised, and of his greatness there is no end.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Great is God and exceedingly to be praised, and of his magnificence there is no finding out.

Hebrew Trans: Generation to generation shall praise your works, and they shall declare your mighty acts.
Septuagint Trans: Generation after generation shall praise your works, and they shall declare your power.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Generation after generation shall praise your works, and they shall declare your power.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Generation to generation shall praise your works, and they shall declare your mighty deeds.

Hebrew Trans: The splendor of the glory of your majesty, and the words of your wondrous deeds, I will meditate upon.
Septuagint Trans: They shall speak of the magnificence of the glory of your holiness, and they shall recount your wonders.
Gallican Psalter Trans: They shall speak of the magnificence of the glory of your holiness, and they shall tell of your wondrous deeds.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The beauty of the glory of your greatness I shall speak of, and the words of your wondrous deeds.

Hebrew Trans: And the might of your awesome deeds they shall speak of, and your greatness I will recount.
Septuagint Trans: And they shall tell of the power of your fearful deeds, and they shall recount your greatness.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And they shall speak of the power of your terrible deeds, and they shall tell of your greatness.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And they shall speak of the strength of your terrible deeds, and I shall recount your greatnesses.

Hebrew Trans: The memory of your abundant goodness they shall pour forth, and your righteousness they shall sing of joyfully.
Septuagint Trans: They shall pour forth the memory of the abundance of your goodness, and they shall rejoice in your righteousness.
Gallican Psalter Trans: They shall pour forth the memory of the abundance of your sweetness, and they shall rejoice in your justice.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: They shall speak the memory of your abundant goodness, and they shall praise your righteousness.

Hebrew Trans: Gracious and merciful is the LORD, slow to anger and great in lovingkindness.
Septuagint Trans: The Lord is compassionate and merciful, slow to anger and abounding in mercy.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The Lord is compassionate and merciful, patient and greatly merciful.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The Lord is gracious and merciful, patient and of great compassion.

Hebrew Trans: The LORD is good to all, and his mercies are over all his works.
Septuagint Trans: The Lord is good to all, and his compassions are upon all his works.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The Lord is sweet to all, and his mercies are over all his works.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The Lord is good to all, and his mercies are upon all his works.

Hebrew Trans: All your works shall give thanks to you, O LORD, and your faithful ones shall bless you.
Septuagint Trans: Let all your works give thanks to you, O Lord, and let your holy ones bless you.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Let all your works confess to you, O Lord, and let your saints confess to you.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Let all your works confess to you, O Lord, and let your saints bless you.

Hebrew Trans: The glory of your kingdom they shall speak of, and your might they shall tell of,
Septuagint Trans: They shall speak of the glory of your kingdom, and they shall tell of your dominion,
Gallican Psalter Trans: They shall speak of the glory of your kingdom, and they shall tell of your power,
Hebrew Psalter Trans: They shall speak the glory of your kingdom, and they shall tell of your mighty deeds,

Hebrew Trans: To make known to the sons of men his mighty acts, and the glory of the splendor of his kingdom.
Septuagint Trans: To make known to the sons of men your dominion, and the glory of the magnificence of your kingdom.
Gallican Psalter Trans: That they may make known to the sons of men your power, and the glory of the magnificence of your kingdom.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: to make known to the sons of men his mighty deeds, and the glory of the beauty of his kingdom.

Hebrew Trans: Your kingdom is a kingdom of all ages, and your dominion is throughout every generation.
Septuagint Trans: Your kingdom is a kingdom of all the ages, and your sovereignty is in every generation and generation. 13a. The Lord is faithful in his words and holy in all his works.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Your kingdom is a kingdom of all ages, and your dominion is in every generation and offspring. The Lord is faithful in all his words, and holy in all his works.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Your kingdom is a kingdom of all ages, and your dominion is in every generation and generation.

Hebrew Trans: The LORD upholds all who fall, and raises up all who are bowed down.
Septuagint Trans: The Lord upholds all who are falling, and raises up all who are broken down.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The Lord lifts up all who fall, and raises up all who are cast down.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The Lord sustains all who are falling, and raises up all who are cast down.

Hebrew Trans: The eyes of all look to you in hope, and you give them their food in its season.
Septuagint Trans: The eyes of all hope in you, and you give them their food in due season.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The eyes of all hope in you, and you give them their food in due time.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Their eyes look hopefully to you, and you give them their food in its due time.

Hebrew Trans: You open your hand and satisfy every living thing with favor.
Septuagint Trans: You open your hand and fill every living thing with good pleasure.
Gallican Psalter Trans: You open your hand, and fill every living thing with blessing.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: You open your hands and fill every living thing with nourishment.

Hebrew Trans: The LORD is righteous in all his ways, and kind in all his works.
Septuagint Trans: The Lord is righteous in all his ways and holy in all his works.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The Lord is just in all his ways, and holy in all his works.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The Lord is just in all his ways, and holy in all his works.

Hebrew Trans: The LORD is near to all who call upon him, to all who call upon him in truth.
Septuagint Trans: The Lord is near to all who call upon him, to all who call upon him in truth.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The Lord is near to all who call upon him, to all who call upon him in truth.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The Lord is near to all who call upon him, to all who call upon him in truth.

Hebrew Trans: The desire of those who fear him he will fulfill, and their cry he will hear, and he will save them.
Septuagint Trans: He will fulfill the desire of those who fear him, and he will hear their supplication and save them.
Gallican Psalter Trans: He will do the will of those who fear him, and he will hear their prayer, and he will save them.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: He will fulfill the desire of those who fear him, and he will hear their cry and will save them.

Hebrew Trans: The LORD guards all who love him, and all the wicked he will destroy.
Septuagint Trans: The Lord guards all who love him, and all the sinners he will utterly destroy.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The Lord guards all who love him, and all sinners he will destroy.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The Lord guards all who love him, and all the wicked he will destroy.

Hebrew Trans: The praise of the LORD my mouth shall speak, and let all flesh bless his holy name forever and ever.
Septuagint Trans: My mouth shall speak the praise of the Lord, and let all flesh bless his holy name forever and to the age of ages.
Gallican Psalter Trans: My mouth shall speak the praise of the Lord, and let all flesh bless his holy name forever and ever.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: My mouth shall speak the praise of the Lord, and let all flesh bless his holy name forever and continually.

Hebrew Trans: Praise the LORD! Praise the LORD, O my soul!
Septuagint Trans: Hallelujah, of Haggai and Zechariah. Praise the Lord, O my soul.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Alleluia, of Haggai and Zechariah. Praise the Lord, O my soul.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Alleluia. Praise the Lord, O my soul.

Hebrew Trans: I will praise the LORD while I live; I will sing praises to my God while I have my being.
Septuagint Trans: I will praise the Lord in my life; I will sing psalms to my God while I exist.
Gallican Psalter Trans: I will praise the Lord in my life: I will sing to my God as long as I shall be.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: I will praise the Lord in my life: I will sing to my God as long as I exist.

Hebrew Trans: Do not put your trust in princes, in a son of man, in whom there is no salvation.
Septuagint Trans: Do not put your trust in princes, nor in the sons of men, in whom there is no salvation.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Do not put your trust in princes, in the sons of men, in whom there is no salvation.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Do not put your trust in princes, in the son of man, in whom there is no salvation.

Hebrew Trans: His spirit departs, he returns to his earth; in that very day his plans perish.
Septuagint Trans: His spirit shall go forth, and he shall return to his earth; in that day all their thoughts shall perish.
Gallican Psalter Trans: His spirit shall go forth, and he shall return to his earth: in that day all their thoughts shall perish.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: His spirit shall go forth, and he shall return to his earth: in that day all his thoughts shall perish.

Hebrew Trans: Happy is he whose help is the God of Jacob, whose hope is in the LORD his God,
Septuagint Trans: Blessed is he whose helper is the God of Jacob, whose hope is in the Lord his God,
Gallican Psalter Trans: Blessed is he whose helper is the God of Jacob: his hope is in the Lord his God,
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Blessed is he whose helper is the God of Jacob: his hope is in the Lord his God.

Hebrew Trans: who made heaven and earth, the sea, and all that is in them, who keeps truth forever,
Septuagint Trans: who made the heaven and the earth, the sea and all the things in them; who keeps truth forever;
Gallican Psalter Trans: who made heaven and earth, the sea and all things that are in them: who keeps truth forever,
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Who made the heavens and the earth, the sea, and all things that are in them: and who keeps truth forever.

Hebrew Trans: who executes justice for the oppressed, who gives bread to the hungry. The LORD sets free the prisoners.
Septuagint Trans: who executes judgment for those who are wronged; who gives food to the hungry. The Lord sets free those who are bound.
Gallican Psalter Trans: who executes judgment for those who suffer wrong, who gives food to the hungry. The Lord looses those who are bound in fetters.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Who executes judgment for those who suffer wrong, and gives bread to the hungry. The Lord looses the bound.

Hebrew Trans: The LORD opens the eyes of the blind; the LORD raises up those who are bowed down; the LORD loves the righteous.
Septuagint Trans: The Lord raises up those who are broken down; the Lord gives wisdom to the blind; the Lord loves the righteous.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The Lord enlightens the blind. The Lord lifts up those who are cast down. The Lord loves the just.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The Lord enlightens the blind. The Lord lifts up those who are cast down. The Lord loves the just.

Hebrew Trans: The LORD watches over the strangers; he upholds the fatherless and the widow, but the way of the wicked he turns upside down.
Septuagint Trans: The Lord watches over the strangers; he will take up the orphan and the widow, and will destroy the way of sinners.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The Lord watches over strangers; he shall support the orphan and the widow, and he shall scatter the way of sinners.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The Lord watches over the strangers; he shall support the orphan and the widow, and the way of the wicked he shall destroy.

Hebrew Trans: The LORD shall reign forever, your God, O Zion, to all generations. Praise the LORD!
Septuagint Trans: The Lord shall reign forever; your God, O Zion, to generation and generation.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The Lord shall reign forever: your God, O Zion, from generation to generation.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The Lord shall reign forever: your God, O Zion, unto generation and generation.

Hebrew Trans: Praise Yah, for it is good to sing praises to our God; for it is pleasant, and praise is fitting.
Septuagint Trans: Hallelujah. Of Haggai and Zechariah. Praise the Lord, for a psalm to our God is good; may praise be sweetened.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Alleluia, of Haggai and Zechariah. Praise the Lord, for he is good; let a psalm to our God be joyful, and let praise be fitting.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Alleluia. Praise the Lord, for a hymn to our God is good, for it is fitting; praise is beautiful.

Hebrew Trans: The LORD builds up Jerusalem; He gathers the outcasts of Israel.
Septuagint Trans: The Lord builds up Jerusalem and will gather together the dispersions of Israel.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The Lord builds up Jerusalem; he will gather together the dispersed of Israel.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The Lord will build up Jerusalem; He will gather together the outcasts of Israel.

Hebrew Trans: He heals the brokenhearted and binds up their wounds.
Septuagint Trans: He who heals the brokenhearted and binds up their wounds.
Gallican Psalter Trans: He who heals the brokenhearted and binds up their wounds.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: He who heals the broken of heart and binds up their wounds.

Hebrew Trans: He counts the number of the stars; He calls them all by names.
Septuagint Trans: He who counts the multitudes of stars and calls them all by names.
Gallican Psalter Trans: He who counts the multitude of the stars and calls them all by name.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: He who numbers the multitude of the stars and calls them all by their name.

Hebrew Trans: Great is our Lord, and abundant in power; His understanding is beyond measure.
Septuagint Trans: Great is our Lord and great is his strength, and of his understanding there is no number.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Great is our Lord, and great is his power, and of his wisdom there is no measure.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Great is our Lord and great in strength; of His wisdom there is no number.

Hebrew Trans: The LORD upholds the humble; He casts down the wicked to the ground.
Septuagint Trans: The Lord lifts up the meek, but humbles sinners to the ground.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The Lord lifts up the meek, but humbles sinners down to the ground.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The Lord lifts up the meek; He humbles the wicked down to the ground.

Hebrew Trans: Sing to the LORD with thanksgiving; make melody to our God upon the harp.
Septuagint Trans: Begin a song to the Lord with thanksgiving; sing praise to our God with the harp.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Sing to the Lord in thanksgiving; make melody to our God upon the harp.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Sing to the Lord with thanksgiving; sing to our God upon the harp.

Hebrew Trans: Who covers the heavens with clouds, who prepares rain for the earth, who makes grass to grow upon the mountains.
Septuagint Trans: To him who covers the heaven with clouds, who prepares rain for the earth, who causes grass to spring up on the mountains and green plants for the service of mankind.
Gallican Psalter Trans: He who covers the heavens with clouds and prepares rain for the earth; who brings forth grass upon the mountains and herbs for the service of men.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: He who covers the heavens with clouds and provides rain for the earth, and makes the grass to spring up on the mountains.

Hebrew Trans: He gives to the beast its food, to the young ravens which cry out.
Septuagint Trans: Who gives to the beasts their food, and to the young of the ravens that call upon him.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And gives to the beasts their food, and to the young ravens that call upon him.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: He who gives to the beasts their food, and to the young ravens that cry out.

Hebrew Trans: He delights not in the strength of the horse; He takes no pleasure in the legs of a man.
Septuagint Trans: He does not delight in the strength of the horse, nor does he take pleasure in the legs of a man.
Gallican Psalter Trans: He shall not delight in the strength of the horse, nor shall he take pleasure in the legs of a man.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: His delight is not in the strength of the horse, nor is He pleased with the legs of a man.

Hebrew Trans: The LORD takes pleasure in those who fear Him, in those who hope in His steadfast love.
Septuagint Trans: The Lord takes pleasure in those who fear him and in those who hope in his mercy.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The Lord takes pleasure in those who fear him, and in those who hope in his mercy.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The Lord is pleased with those who fear Him and with those who hope in His mercy.

Hebrew Trans: Praise the LORD, O Jerusalem; praise your God, O Zion.
Septuagint Trans: Hallelujah. Of Haggai and Zechariah. Praise the Lord, O Jerusalem; praise your God, O Zion.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Alleluia. Praise the Lord, O Jerusalem; praise your God, O Zion.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Praise the Lord, O Jerusalem; sing to your God, O Zion.

Hebrew Trans: For He has strengthened the bars of your gates; He has blessed your children within you.
Septuagint Trans: For he has strengthened the bars of your gates; he has blessed your children within you.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For he has strengthened the bars of your gates; he has blessed your children within you.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For He has strengthened the bars of your gates; He has blessed your children within you.

Hebrew Trans: He makes peace your border; He satisfies you with the finest of wheat.
Septuagint Trans: He who makes your borders peace and fills you with the fatness of wheat.
Gallican Psalter Trans: He who has established peace within your borders, and fills you with the finest of wheat.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: He who has made your borders peace; He fills you with the fat of wheat.

Hebrew Trans: He sends forth His command to the earth; His word runs very swiftly.
Septuagint Trans: He who sends forth his oracle to the earth; his word runs with great swiftness.
Gallican Psalter Trans: He who sends forth his word to the earth; his command runs swiftly.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: He who sends forth His word to the earth; His word runs swiftly.

Hebrew Trans: He gives snow like wool; He scatters frost like ashes.
Septuagint Trans: He who gives snow like wool, who scatters mist like ashes.
Gallican Psalter Trans: He who gives snow like wool; he scatters frost like ashes.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: He who gives snow like wool; He scatters frost like ashes.

Hebrew Trans: He casts forth His ice like morsels; who can stand before His cold?
Septuagint Trans: He who casts his ice like morsels; before his cold, who shall stand?
Gallican Psalter Trans: He sends forth his ice like morsels; before the face of his cold, who shall endure?
Hebrew Psalter Trans: He casts forth His ice like morsels; before the face of His cold, who shall stand?

Hebrew Trans: He sends out His word and melts them; He causes His wind to blow and the waters flow.
Septuagint Trans: He will send forth his word and melt them; he will cause his wind to blow and waters will flow.
Gallican Psalter Trans: He shall send out his word and shall melt them; his spirit shall blow and the waters shall flow.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: He shall send out His word and melt them; He shall blow with His spirit and the waters shall flow.

Hebrew Trans: He declares His word to Jacob, His statutes and His ordinances to Israel.
Septuagint Trans: He who declares his word to Jacob, his ordinances and judgments to Israel.
Gallican Psalter Trans: He who declares his word to Jacob, his statutes and his judgments to Israel.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: He who declares His word to Jacob, His statutes and His judgments to Israel.

Hebrew Trans: He has not done so for any other nation; and as for His ordinances, they have not known them. Praise Yah!
Septuagint Trans: He has not done so to every nation, and his judgments he has not made known to them.
Gallican Psalter Trans: He has not done so to every nation, and his judgments he has not made known to them.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: He has not done likewise to every nation, and His judgments they have not known. Alleluia.

Hebrew Trans: Praise the LORD! Praise the LORD from the heavens; praise Him in the heights!
Septuagint Trans: Alleluia, of Haggai and Zechariah. Praise the Lord from the heavens; praise him in the highest places.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Alleluia. Praise the Lord from the heavens; praise him in the heights.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Alleluia. Praise the Lord from the heavens; praise him in the heights.

Hebrew Trans: Praise Him, all His angels; praise Him, all His hosts!
Septuagint Trans: Praise him, all his angels; praise him, all his powers.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Praise him, all his angels; praise him, all his hosts.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Praise him, all his angels; praise him, all his hosts.

Hebrew Trans: Praise Him, sun and moon; praise Him, all stars of light!
Septuagint Trans: Praise him, sun and moon; praise him, all stars and light.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Praise him, sun and moon; praise him, all you stars and light.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Praise him, sun and moon; praise him, all you stars of light.

Hebrew Trans: Praise Him, heavens of heavens, and the waters that are above the heavens!
Septuagint Trans: Praise him, heavens of heavens, and the water that is above the heavens.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Praise him, heavens of heavens, and the water that is above the heaven.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Praise him, heavens of heavens, and you waters that are above the heavens.

Hebrew Trans: Let them praise the name of the LORD, for He commanded and they were created.
Septuagint Trans: Let them praise the name of the Lord, for he spoke and they came into being; he commanded and they were created.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Let them praise the name of the Lord, for he spoke and they were made; he commanded and they were created.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Let them praise the name of the Lord, for he commanded and they were created.

Hebrew Trans: And He established them forever and ever; He gave a decree and it shall not pass away.
Septuagint Trans: He established them forever and ever; he set a decree and it shall not pass away.
Gallican Psalter Trans: He established them forever and for ages of ages; he set a decree and it shall not pass away.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And he established them forever and ever; he gave a decree and it shall not pass away.

Hebrew Trans: Praise the LORD from the earth, sea creatures and all depths,
Septuagint Trans: Praise the Lord from the earth, you dragons and all deeps;
Gallican Psalter Trans: Praise the Lord from the earth, you dragons and all deeps.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Praise the Lord from the earth, you dragons and all deeps.

Hebrew Trans: Fire and hail, snow and vapor, stormy wind fulfilling His word,
Septuagint Trans: Fire, hail, snow, ice, stormy wind, fulfilling his word;
Gallican Psalter Trans: Fire, hail, snow, ice, stormy winds, which fulfill his word.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Fire and hail, snow and ice, stormy wind, which fulfill his word.

Hebrew Trans: The mountains and all hills, fruit trees and all cedars,
Septuagint Trans: The mountains and all the hills, fruit-bearing trees and all cedars;
Gallican Psalter Trans: Mountains and all hills, fruit-bearing trees and all cedars.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Mountains and all hills, fruit-bearing trees and all cedars.

Hebrew Trans: The wild beasts and all cattle, creeping things and winged birds,
Septuagint Trans: The beasts and all cattle, creeping things and winged birds;
Gallican Psalter Trans: Beasts and all cattle, serpents and winged birds.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Beasts and all cattle, creeping things and flying birds.

Hebrew Trans: Kings of the earth and all peoples, princes and all judges of the earth,
Septuagint Trans: Kings of the earth and all peoples, rulers and all judges of the earth;
Gallican Psalter Trans: Kings of the earth and all peoples, princes and all judges of the earth.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Kings of the earth and all peoples, princes and all judges of the earth.

Hebrew Trans: Young men and also maidens, old men together with youths—
Septuagint Trans: Young men and maidens, old men together with the young;
Gallican Psalter Trans: Young men and maidens, the old together with the younger.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Young men and maidens, old men together with children.

Hebrew Trans: Let them praise the name of the LORD, for His name alone is exalted; His splendor is above the earth and heavens.
Septuagint Trans: Let them praise the name of the Lord, for his name alone is exalted; his praise is upon earth and heaven.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Let them praise the name of the Lord, for his name alone is exalted; his praise is above heaven and earth.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Let them praise the name of the Lord, for his name alone is sublime; his glory is above heaven and earth.

Hebrew Trans: And He has raised up a horn for His people, a praise for all His faithful ones, for the children of Israel, the people near to Him. Praise the LORD!
Septuagint Trans: And he shall exalt the horn of his people; a hymn for all his holy ones, for the sons of Israel, a people drawing near to him.
Gallican Psalter Trans: And he shall exalt the horn of his people: a hymn to all his saints, to the children of Israel, the people that draws near to him.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: And he has exalted the horn of his people; praise to all his saints, to the children of Israel, a people drawing near to him. Alleluia.

Hebrew Trans: Praise Yah! Sing to the LORD a new song; His praise in the assembly of the faithful.
Septuagint Trans: Hallelujah! Sing to the Lord a new song; his praise is in the assembly of the holy ones.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Alleluia. Sing to the Lord a new song: his praise is in the assembly of the saints.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Alleluia. Sing to the Lord a new song: his praise is in the congregation of the saints.

Hebrew Trans: Let Israel rejoice in his Maker; let the children of Zion exult in their King.
Septuagint Trans: Let Israel rejoice in the one who made him, and let the sons of Zion exult in their king.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Let Israel rejoice in him who made him, and let the children of Zion exult in their king.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Let Israel rejoice in his maker: let the children of Zion exult in their king.

Hebrew Trans: Let them praise His name with dancing; with timbrel and harp let them make music to Him.
Septuagint Trans: Let them praise his name with dancing; with timbrel and psaltery let them make music to him.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Let them praise his name in the dance: let them sing to him with timbrel and psaltery.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Let them praise his name in the dance: let them sing to him with timbrel and harp.

Hebrew Trans: For the LORD takes pleasure in His people; He adorns the humble with salvation.
Septuagint Trans: For the Lord takes pleasure in his people, and he will exalt the humble with salvation.
Gallican Psalter Trans: For the Lord is well pleased with his people, and he will exalt the meek in salvation.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: For the Lord takes pleasure in his people: he will exalt the meek in Jesus.

Hebrew Trans: Let the faithful exult in glory; let them sing for joy upon their beds.
Septuagint Trans: The holy ones shall boast in glory, and they shall rejoice upon their beds.
Gallican Psalter Trans: The saints shall exult in glory: they shall rejoice in their beds.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The saints shall exult in glory: they shall praise upon their beds.

Hebrew Trans: The high praises of God in their throats, and a two-edged sword in their hands,
Septuagint Trans: The exaltations of God are in their throat, and two-edged swords are in their hands,
Gallican Psalter Trans: The exaltations of God shall be in their throats, and two-edged swords in their hands:
Hebrew Psalter Trans: The exaltations of God shall be in their throats: and two-edged swords in their hands.

Hebrew Trans: To execute vengeance upon the nations, chastisement upon the peoples,
Septuagint Trans: to execute vengeance upon the nations, rebukes upon the peoples,
Gallican Psalter Trans: To execute vengeance upon the nations, rebukes among the peoples:
Hebrew Psalter Trans: To execute vengeance upon the nations, rebukes upon the peoples.

Hebrew Trans: To bind their kings with chains, and their nobles with fetters of iron,
Septuagint Trans: to bind their kings in fetters, and their nobles in iron shackles,
Gallican Psalter Trans: To bind their kings in fetters, and their nobles in chains of iron:
Hebrew Psalter Trans: To bind their kings with chains, and their nobles with fetters of iron.

Hebrew Trans: To execute upon them the judgment written; this is the glory of all His faithful ones. Praise Yah!
Septuagint Trans: to execute upon them the written judgment. This glory belongs to all his holy ones.
Gallican Psalter Trans: To execute upon them the judgment that is written: this glory is for all his saints.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: To execute upon them the judgment that is written: this is the glory of all his saints. Alleluia.

Hebrew Trans: Praise Yah! Praise God in His sanctuary; praise Him in the firmament of His power.
Septuagint Trans: Hallelujah! Praise God in His holy places; praise Him in the firmament of His power.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Alleluia. Praise the Lord in his holy places: praise him in the firmament of his power.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Alleluia. Praise God in his holy place: praise him in the firmament of his power.

Hebrew Trans: Praise Him for His mighty acts; praise Him according to the abundance of His greatness.
Septuagint Trans: Praise Him for His mighty acts; praise Him according to the multitude of His greatness.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Praise him for his mighty acts: praise him according to the multitude of his greatness.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Praise him for his mighty acts: praise him according to the multitude of his greatness.

Hebrew Trans: Praise Him with the blast of the shofar; praise Him with the lyre and harp.
Septuagint Trans: Praise Him with the sound of the trumpet; praise Him with the psaltery and the harp.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Praise him with the sound of the trumpet: praise him with the psaltery and the harp.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Praise him with the sound of the trumpet: praise him with the psaltery and the harp.

Hebrew Trans: Praise Him with the timbrel and dance; praise Him with stringed instruments and pipe.
Septuagint Trans: Praise Him with the timbrel and dance; praise Him with strings and the pipe.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Praise him with the timbrel and the dance: praise him with strings and the organ.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Praise him with the timbrel and the chorus: praise him with strings and the organ.

Hebrew Trans: Praise Him with cymbals of sound; praise Him with cymbals of shouting.
Septuagint Trans: Praise Him with well-sounding cymbals; praise Him with cymbals of jubilation.
Gallican Psalter Trans: Praise him with cymbals sounding well: praise him with cymbals of jubilation.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Praise him with sounding cymbals: praise him with tinkling cymbals.

Hebrew Trans: Let every thing that has breath praise Yah. Halleluyah!
Septuagint Trans: Let every breath praise the Lord. Hallelujah!
Gallican Psalter Trans: Let every spirit praise the Lord.
Hebrew Psalter Trans: Let everything that breathes praise the Lord.
